# Thread: Candida
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.135/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2012-03-15T13:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.135/post-672>

I know there are alot of us combating candida... so can we have a candida chat?

I am taking nystatin, kicked dairy completely to the curb and my beloved carrots and beets and all other starches. Goodbye wine, even though I wasn't supposed to have you anyway. Its actually pretty easy to follow this diet, I am really surprised. I did keep my butter for my bulletproof coffee, but that was deemed ok according to some candida places.

Thing is, I am not having a ton of die off, or retracing. Some digestive issues though, like noisy stomach. And apparently now vaginitis, which I never had trouble with prior to this. I just really wanted this to be a process where I knew it was leaving my body and it would be gone for good. I feel like the candida is holding me back from optimal.

It has really interfered with my hormones, that's for sure. The progesterone I am taking just causes continual bloat, and according to Uzzi Reiss's book, that indicated candida issues. It has also given me trouble with resisting temptations. Not to bad foods, but just snacking after a meal because I was looking for something to satisfy that urge. (Again, we're talking a little more carrots after I ate or more spoonfuls of coconut oil because I felt I needed something to signify dessert.) I have noticed this is diminishing, so I will take that as a positive.

Anyone else care to share their experiences?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T14:54:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.135/post-702>

I'm battling Candida and parasites...

I'm working on finishing up two cleanses for the parasites and candida... using DE as well... my biggest challenge is the diary...some times its like I just have to have dairy... as I was detoxing a week or so ago - I just HAD TO HAVE BLUE CHEESE - gave in and had it... shouldn't have... those little bugs loved it...

Die off was crazy when I started treating for the parasites- w/in 6hrs of taking the meds I felt so bad - horrible reactions from both ends... Initially I was wondering if I had what the kids had (they were sick) but considering mine lasted a max of 6hrs... I chalked it up to just being a detox symptom....

How do you take DE? And how much?

### tweety — 2012-03-15T19:31:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.135/post-911>

I have considered taking the DE too. So many times it is recommended to go slow, not do too much at once. But I want to be done with it so I find it hard not to throw everything at it from the start. I suspect I have other parasites too, but am waiting for my stool test results first.

My cravings are more wine and starchy veggies, mainly the carrots and beets. Man I love beets. I was shocked how easy it was to avoid dairy. My coffee is what I won't give up. Healing by Bee says to give up coffee and teas, but thats not happening right now.

### IceBarbie — 2012-03-15T20:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.135/post-959>

I've been struggling with systemic Candida for years. Here are few things that I felt helped me the most: Colloidal Silver (high quality but not sure which ones are best today as mine was homemade from a chiro at the time it helped me). Kyolic Garlic, Now brand Candida Clear, Caprylic Acid, Oregano Oil and of course, Diflican (available at All Day Chemist for a fraction of what a script costs in the USA without insurance). I have also tried Nystatin but it did nothing. And I also read that if you don't mix up your treatments a bit, the yeast start to resist the treatment. I've read that if you take Nystatin too long, the yeast will actually start to feed on it.

I do believe it's better to KILL the yeast/fungus rather than try to starve it. When you simply cut back your fruits etc, the yeast will poke through your intestines to find sugar elsewhere which contributes to leaky gut plus it goes deeper into your body searching for sugar. Yeast is insidious and very damaging to your health.

I've also tried MMS mixed with Citric Acid but didn't do very long. It is supposed to kill yeast as well as all acidic pathogens in your body including parasites, bacteria & viruses. The taste was hard for me to overcome. I have since used it (successfully) when getting a cold using some Berry or Orange flavored liquid Stevia and it was much easier to drink. Now I just need to do the entire protocol sometime as it also helps an acid body which I still have.

About 4 years ago, I did a parasite cleanse "just in case" as I have dogs. And shockingly, yep, I did have parasites--lots of them. At that time, I was using a whole series of cleanses from Blessed Herbs. There are less spendy alternatives for this including the info from Hulda Clarke. And interestingly, parasites have a natural cycle of hatching eggs on or close to a full moon. So it's good to start your parasite cleanse shortly before a full moon to kill the adult parasites then also later kill the hatchlings. Then repeat the following month to get any you missed the first time. It is estimated that 90 percent of people have parasites. Pinworms have been described as "something you've had, something you do have, or something you will have." A pinworm egg can live for 2 weeks on your bed and easily be inhaled. Some issues such as bedwetting or grinding teeth at night are caused by untreated pinworms. Hookworms can be acquired from walking barefoot. Since learning this after my unexpected experience, I now do a parasite cleanse at least one or twice a year in the spring or fall.

I am planning to order some DE as well for myself and my dogs. Haven't tried it yet but have been reading some awesome info about it.

### Kcaliaz — 2012-03-16T01:01:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.135/post-1121>

Anybody have experience taking Candex? I just took my first capsule and within 1/2 hour I was feeling poorly. Then, it got so bad, I had to call my husband home from work to watch our 4 year old while I laid in bed wanting to just throw up or do something to make the nausea and stomach pains go away. 5 hours later, I'm starting to feel human again. Is this a normal response?? I thought there was very little, if any, "die-off" symptoms from Candex??

### IceBarbie — 2012-03-16T02:12:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.135/post-1171>

Sounds to me like it's working! But bummer on the die-off symptoms...

### rbu5@sssnet.com — 2012-03-16T02:56:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.135/post-1209>

I took some DE today for the first time. Just mixed it into some green tea. I used 1 Tablespoon and didn't notice any digestive trouble or discomfort. Googled and saw that they recommend 1 to 2 T a day. Will see how it goes tomorrow. I'll stick with the 1 T for a week or two.

Pinworms have been described as "something you've had, something you do have, or something you will have." A pinworm egg can live for 2 weeks on your bed and easily be inhaled. Some issues such as bedwetting or grinding teeth at night are caused by untreated pinworms. Hookworms can be acquired from walking barefoot. Since learning this after my unexpected experience, I now do a parasite cleanse at least one or twice a year in the spring or fall.
>
>
>
> I am planning to order some DE as well for myself and my dogs. Haven't tried it yet but have been reading some awesome info about it.

What do you recommend for the best parasite cleanse? Thanks.

### IceBarbie — 2012-03-17T00:18:28+0200

I also read to use different sources to kill the yeast. Kyolic Garlic is what I take now, and it has oregano oil in it. I also have pure oregano oil, will switch those around. >
> For all fighting the candida, how did you know when you were making progress?

When my Candida is bad, I can feel an itching sensation internally (but NOT in the V-JayJay as Oprah calls it!) as soon as I eat something sweet. I'm doing so much better since changing my diet starting a few years ago. My candida got so bad that I even had external raw sores on my body where the sun didn't shine and my skin folds harbored moisture (c-section scar & below my girls). I fixed that with MMS baths and haven't had the happen for a few years now.

### IceBarbie — 2012-03-17T00:26:47+0200

You can buy them in kits like Blessed Herbs parasite cleanse (which are spendy) or you can buy them separately which is the cheapest. # Thread: Go Lisa Go!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LisaAPB — 2012-03-15T15:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-720>

I saw my doctor last week, for the first time in 18 months. It was discouraging because the visit started with a very high reading of blood pressure, and then I had to argue to have certain non standard labs drawn. In the end, I quit arguing and decided I would go have my own labs drawn... while looking for a new doctor. You see, I had lost 40 lbs since my last visit, and no mention was made of that. She just couldn't get past the blood pressure (which I will overcome eventually!) although I did get an email from her that evening remarking on how "good" my labwork looked.

Good?! They were freakin' awesome! A year and half ago, I was diagnosed prediabetic, anemic, fatty liver disease, hypothyrod, sever sleep apnea, hypertensive (still), hypothyroid, and hypertryglyceremia. My labs now look better than they have in 12 years! Here's just a few before & after numbers:

Weight, from 208 to 168

Fasting Blood Glucose, from 120 to 80 Not diabetic

AST/SGOT from 84 to 24

ALT/SGPT from 82 to 21 No fatty liver disease

LDL, from 101 to 84

Cholesterol, from 218 to 145 No more high cholesterol

Triglycerides, from 383 to 189 No more hypertryglceremia

Unmedicated thyroid levels:

TSH from 15 to 5.4 (still a bit hypo, but better!)

Free T4 from .65 to .8 and Free T3 from 1.8 to 2.9 improving slowly

Some lab values are still not optimal, like potassium is low normal and that darn blood pressure; however, on the whole everything is so much better! and they will get better still!

I'm not sure exactly where I went off track in the past, more likely I was born off track. I tried to eat healthily (according to SAD standards). Starting around 1999 I started getting migraines, and pre-menopause symptoms. From 2001 to 2007, I was under enormous amounts of stress and steadily gained weight that refused to drop off in spring and summer like my normal pattern. From 2007 to summer of 2010, I tried everything from working out like a demon 6 days a week and limiting calories to 1000 per day, never with much success. Oh, I might lose 4 lbs, but it would come right back plus some. By autumn of 2010 I was going to liver and blood specialists because something was killing me, faster rather than slower. My red blood cells had tanked, my bilirubin was too high, liver and spleen enlarged and I felt seriously bad. After a few months of monitoring and investigating, it was discovered that I was having a severe and prolonged reaction to an antibiotic I'd taken months earlier. The antibiotic attaches to a protein in red blood cells, and my body was attacking that thinking there was an invader. It was enough to make me reexamine my life... well, I seriously thought I was dying.

In Dec. 2010 I found the hCG community. I'd tried everything else to lose weight, and though it sounded like a crazy diet, I decided to try it. I lost 20 lbs that first month and another 10 the following Spring. The information and support from the hcg community was phenomenal, and I can't say enough good things about the friends I've made there. I was ecstatic but scared to death I would gain in right back. That is when I found Paleo, and then Leptin Rx and started following it's principles. I easily maintained my weight loss and never felt deprived. A few weeks ago I decided to try hcg again, with CT this time, and I've lost another 10 lbs so far.

So that's my success' so far, but I'm not done yet! My ultimate goals are to lose another 20 lbs, get my blood pressure under control without Rx, and eliminate my sleep apnea so I can sleep without a cpap machine. It took me over 50 years to accumulate the negative effects of a SAD diet and improper thoughts following CW, so I'm being patient (sometimes that's hard!) that if I just keep moving forward everything will work out. I feel great but I'll get even better!

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-15T15:48:20+0200

Congratulations on your successes thus far! I'm sure you'll reach your goals, mama!

Also, how do you eat 50g of BAB per Leptin Rx and stay within the 500 calorie limit of hCG?

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-16T04:28:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-1264>

Thanks all for stopping by and the compliments! Dr K commented on my high blood pressure (CT6) and steered me toward renin aldersterone imbalance. I looked it up and bingo, that's me. Symptoms are high blood pressure, low potassium, high normal sodium, and purple stria on the belly. I had wondered about the purple stripes on my belly, thinking they were normal since I got stretch marks when I was pregnant a few decades ago. Well, I've lost 5 inches in my waist and the purplish color is still there! Anyway, he said if I take Vit K & D, keep eating clean and doing CT, it should fine... so I'm running with that treatment plan. I am taking a very low dose of blood pressure medicine just because it was consistently more than just a little high (160-180 over 100).

jeepifer, when I do the first phase of hcg diet, I don't do a traditional BAB. I eat 2 meals a day, each about 300 calories. The first meal may be breakfast or lunch depending on my mood. If I eat breakfast, I will drink broth for lunch. Plus I've been putting cream in my coffee twice a day, which adds another 100 calories. I go into the Leptin Rx with BAB after weight loss mode for 6 to 8 weeks, it helps me stabilize.

### caroline — 2012-03-16T09:40:16+0200

### Cron23 — 2012-03-16T19:06:44+0200

### colleencoble — 2012-03-17T20:31:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-2089>

Lisa, are you on HCL, dhea, pregnenolone? I'm concerned about your cholesterol. It's actually too low.  YOur story is incredible!!!

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-18T05:11:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-2300>

Too low now... drat. \*sigh\* I have pregnenolone, I was taking it every other day until I started my last round. What is HCL, is that the Betaine HCI? I did that for about a month, when I was having the stomach issues. I planned to do it again when I get to P3 because that's when I tend to get the stomach issues, after I add back in more fats. I have some dhea but the last time I took it I got a headache, and I got pimples on my face, so I stopped taking it. Right now I'm just taking Krill Oil, D3 and K, plus my thyroid and though I don't want to, I am taking half dosage of the high blood pressure medicine the doc prescribed. I took my blood pressure at the pharmacy last weekend and it was 160/100 so I thought I would try the meds. Today my blood pressure was 109/78 so I guess it is doing something for the symptom, but doubtful it does anything for the underlying cause which I'm pretty sure is an adrenal thing. Really, really hoping the CT helps to correct that.

Today I went to the farmer's coop and bought a 75 gallon stock tank so I can actually submerge in a tub, instead of just doing cold showers and ice packs. It's 41 degrees outside so I think the water might be too cold? I am so looking forward to summer because I will be in that tub of ice water lickety split. Guess I'm going to have to work up to the cold tub, I only made it about 10 minutes tonight and I only got the lower half of my body. I sure feel good right now though!

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-18T19:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-2426>

I cannot share a full accounting of my success without doing a comprehensive autopsy of my failures in the past. Maybe that will help me make sense of it all. So, I will use this platform to share the good, the bad, the ugly, the beautiful. Bear with me as I tend to go down side alleys exploring thoughts, sometimes leading to dead ends, but sometimes there's a gem of wisdom found along the trail.

I mentioned previously that I was under enormous amounts of stress from 2001 to 2007, but the truth is, I can remember it long before that. I was born into a large family and my parents were chronic alcoholics. Growing up in a dysfunctional family was stressful at times. I learned many things as a child. I learned to stifle things and ignore my gut feelings. I learned to try harder. I learned to separate and detach. Don't get me wrong, I had a lot of good times and a lot of love in the family too. When it was good, it was good and when it was bad, it was oh so bad. I could write a whole book on those years alone, but that story is for another platform. I'm just guessing that my early years conditioned me for a certain level of chronic stress and a feeling of helplessness. Things I have actively been trying to reverse as I've matured and learned.

The next incident on the autopsy list was the birth giving time in my life. I had no complications with my first child. His conception was planned and there was nothing but joy and happiness in abundance. I had a good job and good insurance, times were good... that was 1981. As with a lot of people in the 80's, I was laid off during the prolonged recession, losing income and insurance. When my child was 6 months old, I discovered I was pregnant again. This one was not planned, and my (now ex) husband pressured me to terminate, but I didn't want to do that. I scrimped and saved, even sold my car, to pay the hospital bill which was due in full prior to check in because I had no insurance.

As Murphy's Law would have it, there were complications, some of which I would not know the mystery of until 12 years later. It so happened that when I was in the hospital giving birth to my daughter in the beginning of 1983, that my grandfather had been admitted into same hospital dying from a stroke. The day after I went home with my newborn, all my family was at the funeral. I stayed home because I had developed a fever, and lots of pain. That day, all alone, I started passing bloodclots the size of cantelope. It was pretty scarey. Eventually, and with medication, the bleeding stopped but the pain I felt in my abdomen and back hung on for another 12 weeks. I'd been through a normal delivery and recovery and I knew something was wrong, but repeated visits to the doctor didn't help. I was put on long term antibiotics because, I think, the doctor didn't know what else to do.

By the end of that year, I was feeling almost back to normal. I'd taken advantage of programs offered to the unemployed and enrolled myself into a training program to learn about computerized business applications. I would go to school evenings while the kid's father watched them. I started getting extreme fatigue and nausea and discovered I was very anemic... and pregnant again, even though I'd been taking birth control. It happens. I went home to tell my husband, except he didn't come home that night. He didn't come home for over a week, and then it was to tell me he was leaving. He'd "fallen in love", didn't love me anymore, and didn't like the whole "family" scene. I was devastated. He cleared out our bank account, which wasn't much, took the only car. I was pregnant, alone, broke, and getting sicker every day. I ended up miscarrying, which seemed a blessing at the time. Because I'd learned as a child to try harder, I ended up finishing my schooling while being a single parent. It was a stressful time and the things that helped me through was the love for my children, and the support of my family. My siblings and I learned long ago to rely on each other in times of need, and they did not fail me then either. Eventually my husband and I reconciled and I put the past behind us because I felt strongly that our children would be best served by living with their natural parents. It wasn't until a decade later, when my mother died and I reflected on her life, that I realized that children do best when their parents, together or not, are happy and healthy.

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-18T20:21:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-2437>

My parent went in and out of rehab, alternating periods of sobriety (though not always at the same time) with periods of drunkeness. That was my growing up years. Eventually they divorced and went on to find new partners. My father sobered up for good and has reached a 40 year sobriety streak. He's survived quadruple heart surgery, colon cancer, lymphoma and a host of other issues, some related to alcohol damage (epilepsy) and some likely from SAD way of eating. I remember he used to like to eat wonder bread, butter and brown sugar sandwiches. My stepmother, bless her heart, cured him of that mistake! My mother was not as successful overcoming her alcoholism.

Fast forward to Mother's Day, 1991: the call came early in the morning. Mom was taken to the hospital with pain down her leg. We found out that day that the aorta from her heart clear down her leg had ruptured. Likely disintegrated due to tissue damage from long term Prednisone use prescribed for her asthma. Mom's health had steadily declined over the past ten years. When she and dad split, she had met and married a man who soon after died of heart failure. She was lonely. She was also in a great deal of physical pain from osteoarthritis in the spine. She still alternated between bouts of sobriety and drunk. I loved her in spite of her disease, and I wish now that she had known about the plan for Optimized Living because I do not think she would have died early and would have had more enjoyable years had she known the things I know now. I was devastated by my mothers death.

I spent some time reflecting on my mother's life and when commiserating with my younger sister in the week after her death, that I wished mom would have spent more time finding happiness. I was convinced that the years my mom and dad stuck it out with each other for the sake of the kids were miserable for her, and I wondered if I was doing my kids any good by staying with their father who was proving to be mentally and on more than one occasion, physically, abusive. That was when my sister, shared with me the secret she and my mother had been keeping from me: that my (now-ex) husband had been showing up drunk and harassing them. On one occasion he'd stolen my mother's car (returned it a day later) and he'd developed a "crush" on my sister. He was hitting on her regularly and when she told him to stop, he'd gotten irate. For some reason, they did not want to tell me about his behavior, they didn't want to be responsible for "tearing our family apart". I felt like I'd been blindsided, but really, I was in so much grief over losing my mom, that divorcing my husband was just a no-brainer. Frustrating, because he didn't want to cooperate in the split, but I had no emotional attachment to the breakup... it was a relief.

I was a single parent again, but happily. My sister and her children moved in with us and things were pretty good most of the time. The only thing I would change about that period of time is the fact that I did a lot of "stretching the food dollar" which involved feeding the kids lots of crap that I now know was unhealthy. Lots of Ramen noodles, pasta, cheap white bread and potatoes... filler food. A few years later I met another single parent, a father trying to raise his two boys. We fell in love and made plans to move in together after his kids graduated high school. We lived 50 miles apart and we had a 4 year plan... well, you know what they say about the best laid plans.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-19T04:14:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-2567>

Wow, Lisa, what hard times you've gone through--and yet you've persevered and come out better and stronger. I'm full of admiration!

On the cholesterol thing, when it's too low your body can't make the hormones you need. How much HCL were you taking? Yes that's betaine. You might have been taking too much or not enough. I had to take 3 per meal at first and now I'm down to 2.

### vlynnb — 2012-03-19T04:41:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-2591>

Lisa, Congratulations on your progress. I admire what you have done with your life in spite of the circumstances.

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-19T05:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-2602>

Thanks for stopping by! Colleen, I will add the HCL back in this week. I still have half a bottle left.

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-19T06:09:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-2630>

So, where did I leave off? It was the early 1990's, I'd lost my mom, my marriage broke up and I'd met my future husband. We were going to take it slow, since we both had kids in middle school and high school. Didn't want to upset the boat. I had joined a spring recreational coed softball team, felt strong and healthy again. Mid-way into the season, I hit a stand up double and easily ran to 2nd base, only to be nailed in the gut with the gloved hand of an over exuberant gorilla of a weekend baseball warrior. I wasn't expecting it as time had already been called, and I went down hard on my ass. Shock waves of pain went ripping through my body, but I just got up and finish the game. That night I woke up around 3 in the morning, in severe pain, and unable to move my legs. I crawled to my sister's room and she got me to the hospital. I had broken my cocyx bone and they had to go in and set it back into place. In case anyone doesn't know, the cocyx is the tailbone, and you can guess how they went in to set it. I was under anesthesia and don't have any memory of that for which I am thankful.

The following week I went to the bone & back doc and got the news that I was not to return to work for 12 weeks. I was not to lift anything over 2 lbs for 12 weeks. This was not a good thing. I had no disability insurance and very little in savings. My sister was leaving that week for a summer away with her boyfriend. I was really stuck, and in a lot of pain. The doctor commented that I must know the kind of recovery I'd be in for as this was not the first time I'd sustained such an injury. huh? ... then it clicked in my brain, this was what had happened when I gave birth to my daughter twelve years earlier! My (future) husband took it upon himself to come to my rescue. We moved up our plan, and became a blended family. This was no Brady Bunch, nor anything like the carefree Yours, Mine & Ours family. Anyone who's been there knows: becoming a blended family, especially when the kids are pre-teen or young teens is stressful. Very stressful at times those first few years, and we also found out my new mother in law had ovarian cancer during that time. Sometimes I'm amazed we made it through those years, but spouse and I are celebrating our 20th anniversary this winter so we done good! Coincidentally, I started passing enormous bloodclots again after breaking my tailbone. In 1996 tragedy struck when my 13 year old nephew broke his neck playing football. He became a quadriplegic. We sold our house, uprooted our children and moved to the area my sister was living. We knew she, her son, and her family were going to need a lot of ongoing support.

After we moved, the spouse and I looked for work closer to our new home. He had a lot of contacts in industry, and we ended up starting a business out of our garage. In 1999, my son, my firstborn, graduated from high school and went straight into the Army. By 2001, my daughter, the baby of the family was graduating and we were moving yet again onto some property that had a shop big enough to accommodate our growing business. The spouse and I had both been working outside jobs, plus our own business, but the time was drawing near that we would need to devote our time to our manufacturing business full time. It was exciting and it was stressful. Then 9-11 hit, and I did not cope with it well.

I remember being glued to the TV set, watching in disbelief as the Twin Towers went down. Watching that scene over and over again. The phone rang and it was my son, asking me if I'd seen the news, and letting me know that I might not be hearing from him for awhile. I was terrified, and for the next few years while he went on repeated tours of Iraq and Afghanistan, I was in chronic panic mode. Our business was doing well but I had decided to go back to college and get my degree, so I was only working in the shop part time. By 2006, I saw the writing on the wall, the economy was about to tank, orders were drying up and we made the decision to close our business and secure outside employment. In early 2007, my husband had secured a job with an engineering firm, which left me wrapping up the closure of the business while still taking classes part time toward a degree. To say that this was a stressful period in my life is an understatement.

As mentioned in my opening post, by then end of 2007, I had put on lots of weight I could not lose no matter how hard I tried. I'd become hypothyroid, hypertensive, fatty liver disease, sleep apnea, chronic migraines... and so on and so forth. I know the SAD diet I'd been eating full of "healthy" grains, fruit snacks, and prepared foods contributed greatly to my demise. I'm also sure that the stress triggered some imbalances that were compounded by ongoing and continuing chronic stress.

I've learned to let go of a lot of things I just can't control. I've learned to step outside of myself and evaluate how true a thing really is. I've learned that Love can carry you through when nothing feels right. I've learned to listen to my gut feelings, and trust my intuition. I know that I am on the road to recovery. Already, the lab tests show amazing improvements, and I feel so much better. I feel so good, I can't help but share it with those I love, and some of them are slowly coming around. In the last two years I've regained my health. I'm blessed with the most amazing family, and beautiful grandchildren. If I need to sit in a tub of cold water so that I can be there for them, so be it. Bring on the cold.

### Destiny — 2012-03-31T23:07:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/go-lisa-go.141/post-9378>

If I need to sit in a tub of cold water so that I can be there for them, so be it. Bring on the cold.

You are such a survivor, Lisa APB! Your story made me cry and smile. All your struggles and tragedies ended up with you winning. I am sure it will be the same outcome with CT.

Hi from the sunny UK...well it's sunny for now!

Have a few issues with high cholesterol, well my doc is having the issues! My LDL is high, HDL and Trigs are good. Meeting with a blood specialist soon to discuss, looking forward to that! I'm hoping the CT will help with that.

I have had sleep issues most of my life(45), and here's hoping the CT will help that too!

Interested in the blue light issue I've just been reading about on this site, great stuff, so much to learn.

# Thread: Howdy from Athens Georgia!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-athens-georgia.143/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Torey — 2012-03-15T15:41:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-athens-georgia.143/post-733>

Hey all,

Happy to introduce myself. My name is Torey Freeman. Im 33, married and father to my amazing 16 month old daughter. i discovered Paleo last summer after hearing Nora G on coast to coast am. I got her book and since then my life has changed immensely. My family and I have been furthering our Paleo practice and we are now fully in stride. We get ALL of our meat and veggies from local farms. I am totally into biohacking (I.F. C.T. HiiT etc) I have lost 25 lbs and maintained that weight effortlessly. I have read pretty much EVERY paleo book save 2 or 3 which I am taking down now and I have even enrolled at UGA to pursue a degree in nutrition education to teach in high school (so I can slip JERF and Paleo principles in with the modern curriculum). My wife and I are attending ASH12 in boston and my main paleo templates are: Body By Science HiiT, Cold Thermogenesis, some MOV NAT, and I utilize the following diet templates: Bulletproof Exec, Leptin Rx, Lean Gains, Paleo Solution, MDA, Primal Body Primal Mind etc.

My intuition when I started paleo was that after the initial change I thought (and still do) that it makes the most sense that your diet would be dynamic and change with the seasons and natural rhythms so the more I learned about hormone balance the more I knew my intuition was correct. I firmly believe that diet is just the tip and that lifestyle is the iceberg under the surface.

I am very excited to be part of this community, and I am eager to meet and discuss topics with you all. I also am a member of the bulletproof exec forum.

podcasts blogs that I read regularly:

chris kresser, rob wolf, dave asprey, tim ferris, jimmy moore, mark sisson, whole 9, lean gains, Doug McDuff, Jack Kruse, Bryan Davies, everyday paleo, nom nom paleo

so to sum it up: We are a young family, living very primal. My wife and I are both in education and seek to implement the primal movement and principles into our work. We raise our daughter on real food, support only local farmers who produce the cleanest and highest caliber foods. I am WAY into biohacking and utilize - I.F., C.T. HiiT, cooking methods and science and research to further my n=1 experiments. I am VERY open minded, rarely argue and try my best to follow the science, not the crowd.

Welp, thats my story. say hi sometime

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T16:31:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-athens-georgia.143/post-772>

Hi Torey, glad you are here!

### chasee33 — 2012-04-02T04:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-athens-georgia.143/post-10122>

Hey Torie I'm living in Atlanta, but I have a nutrition science degree from UGA. You're not going to learn the type of information you want, at least in my experience. However, it does provide the education to open many other doors. I'm interested in some of the local farms you are involved in. If you see this post anytime I'd appreciate a list of those farms. Congrats on raising a paleo family as well, no better way I think. Hope to hear from ya.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-03T03:27:10+0300

# Thread: Greetings from Missouri
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-missouri.145/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MartiD — 2012-03-15T16:15:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-missouri.145/post-754>

Hi, my name is Marti. I am a 43 yr old Mom with two teenage kids (Lord help me!) I have been overweight off and on for 17 years and I started last Summer with a round of HHCG. My SW was 228 and after 2 rounds I was down to 187 for my lowest drop weight. I did a sudo-LR RX between rounds and immediately went back the RX after round two. I am down to 172 this morning but more significantly I am down two pant sizes from LDW. I am now starting some toning exercising, weights, walking, etc as well as being 3 weeks into CT via Cold Baths. I have been fortunate to not have dramatic detox issues but have been dealing with an allergy issues that came out in the form of hives. I don't believe this is a result of CT because it first appeared in early January before CT, but I have been tested and have a plan of action since apparently I am allergic to everything I breathe!

My original goal in June 2011 was to get down to 185, or a comfortable size 14 which is where I was able to get to 4 years ago before a hystertomy and spinal fusion detoured my stabilization. However, after getting well below that I have readjusted my goals to 160-165. At 5' 6" with a medium frame I realize that I would be more optimal on the BMI chart at 145-155, however, I don't want to get down to a weight I can't maintain. I know I can be a comfortable size 10 at 165. Once I achieve this goal, then I may re-adjust but for now this and becoming LS and optimal are my main goals.

I am grateful for this and other forums that have kept me so focused and taught me so much about my body, foods and overall a healthy way of living. I look forward to "cyber" meeting all of you!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

This year I started a VERY STRICT Robb Wolf autoimmune paleo diet with the leptin reset and within 5 weeks I was losing again.

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T17:15:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-807>

Don't you love shopping in your own closet?  Congrats on your success, and future success!

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T17:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-825>

Actually, I have been doing less shopping and more tossing.

### MartiD — 2012-03-15T17:43:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-833>

Thanks for intro. I know what you mean about tossing...I can't tell you how many bags of clothes I have donated in the last 7 months. I told my husband I have NO summer clothes that I can wear. Last September I was in a size 16 shorts and now I am an easy 12 if not a 10 in some sizes. I think the 16's would fall right off my hips.

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T17:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-842>

Oh, we should start a clothing swap on here. I'm working my way down, hate having to buy new transitional stuff. LOL

### nonchalant — 2012-03-15T23:13:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-1067>

Nice going, Birdy! You must be adding muscle, since the weight isn't dropping much.

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T02:46:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-1196>

I hope that is what is happening.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-16T03:33:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-1237>

Well done, you are on the right track!

### caroline — 2012-03-16T09:46:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-1337>

well done on all your hard work. I am working on getting rid of all the blubber around my waist!

### WandaP — 2012-03-16T22:10:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-1677>

Hey, Birdy! This is my first post, and it's to congratulate you. I just started eating differently 3 weeks ago! I've lost 15 lbs. so far. I have about 125 more to go. I'm 63 and have been struggling with weight since I was 40, so it has been a career of it. I have fallen twice and bummed up both knees in the last 11 years, so I've gained from that, but no more excuses! I nearly cried when I was reading a message by Jack to another lady about the leaky gut. I realized that it what I have and why I have struggled without anyone to help me. I was eating just like my husband (honestly, maybe better), but he never gained and I gained 10-15 lbs. a year. I could lose 10 lbs. and would stall. Then I would get discouraged and quit. I was tired of having no answers. I feel the Leptin RX is what I need to fix the leaky gut and get back to the health I want so I can enjoy my 8 grandchildren. I have missed out on so much of their lives so far. I have been there, but never to run and play with them! In Aug. I weighed in at 290 at an emergency trip to the hospital in front of my husband (who had no idea what I weighed). I have never spoken to him about it since. Then after Thanksgiving and Christmas, I weighed 299. One morning, I hit 300 and gasped. Bear in mind, I"m only 5' 3" tall. That is when I found Jack Kruse. It makes sense, so I'm doing this. I have decided to post my weekly loss on FB for my friends as a means of accountability. I had a list of ills a mile long, but swollen, painful feet is no longer on the list.

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T23:42:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-1723>

WandaP, I am so glad things are working for you. It got so discouraging doing things the old way.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-17T01:57:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-1780>

Congrats Birdy on your successes, especially reducing the belly fat. That is so hard to get rid of.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-17T20:34:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-2092>

Terrific story, Birdy, congratulations!

### MJ* — 2012-03-18T05:19:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-2301>

Great job Birdy, thanks for sharing your story!

### Birdy — 2012-03-22T04:55:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-4241>

### MJ* — 2012-03-22T17:31:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-4438>

> **Birdy;4267 wrote:** I think the diet is on his website, if you go digging. It is just paleo minus a few things. No nightshades, dairy, grains of course, and he also doesn't include nuts and citrus, but I didn't. I did low carb, so there wasn't much citrus anyway. I also got rid of bacon and eggs. I just wanted to do it for awhile to see if it made a difference. It did! I found out that I have sensitivities to pork, lots of eggs, cashews and some nightshades. Since then I have brought back some pork, some eggs, some dairy and hot pepper. But, I am staying away from chronic cheese and nightshades.
>
> My hs-crp was pretty low but not low enough. Now that everything has settled down, I can tell right away if something bothers me, it causes an immediate change to my skin.

What dairy are you able to tolerate now Birdy? I love cheese giving that up would be so hard for me...

### CURLY-Q — 2012-05-10T23:51:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-ill-go-next.146/post-23020>

Birdy - I'm late in seeing your post. Congrats! I wanted to let you know I have a similar story. I did Atkins and lost a lot of weight in the early 2000s but gained it all back and then some. Paleo made me feel better than the SAD but I wasn't losing. It wasn't until I started the auto-immunity protocol & LR (like you) that I finally started to see the scale move. I'm officially down 16# since early March. I've wanted to start a thread that discusses the benefits of this strict protocol for the "big girls" who are having a hard time getting the scale to move.

I'm curious - what do you eat if not pork? I live on bacon & sausage for breakfast and steak and broccoli for dinner. I've been curious if pork is one of my triggers but I don't know what to eat for breakfast since I've cut out everything else.

# Thread: Birdy's Nest
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 48
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2012-03-15T17:19:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-810>

Just flew in!

I'll update more when I collect everything.

I have done the leptin reset successfully and am still losing weight slowly. I don't have much further to go, but I do not have a set goal weight in mind. Non-scale goals include getting rid of belly fat. I have no health issues.

I follow a ketogenic ancestral diet.

I do HIIT a couple of times a week, running and aerobics.

I do strength training sporadically.

I an VERY active outdoors in the garden and the farm.

I have just started CT, and right now I am doing the tub method starting with lukewarm water and then moving to cold tap water. I live in a warm, low-humidity environment with little central heating/cooling and so am able to take advantage of evaporative cooling.

I get lots of sunshine and fresh air, and real grass under my toes.

I don't take any hormones or supplements regularly.

Currently I take a multi, alpha lipoic acid, CoQ10, inositol/choline, fish oil and magnesium.

### Birdy — 2012-03-17T00:25:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-1741>

Yesterday I did HIIT, and it wasn't really that hard. I had the wrong shoes on to pound too hard. I tried taking off my shoes, but there were too many stones. Today I did my third bath, and I was really looking forward to it. I start out with a bit of lukewarm water and then add cold, and stay in the water until I start shivering too much. I love how the cooler water makes my skin feel. The first couple of times I showered in colder water it seemed hard getting my scalp clean, but now my skin feels much better without the heat. Today I lasted over 10 minutes and my feet didn't turn blue. Pink this time, and no huge bouts of shivering. I got caught up on some Garnet Hill catalogs and an issue of Archaeology magazine. Figure I might find a clue to factor X......

### Hotmail — 2012-03-17T11:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-1903>

hey Birdy, you are really brave with the cold showers too :0 hmmm maybe one day when am convinced I will try it.. one step at a time.

### Birdy — 2012-03-17T17:42:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-2014>

Just turn the temp down gradually and you will be there before you know it. The hard part is getting out and evaporating the water naturally. If you aren't convinced, just try the face dunk.

### Phoenix — 2012-03-17T17:46:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-2017>

Your method is the same as mine, Birdy. Warm water first, then turn it down. Works perfectly. I was so cold last night afterwards. OK while i was sitting in the water, freezing in the warm air. Weird. I read somewhere that this is exactly what is supposed to happen, so we're both on the right track!

Keep up the good work, neighbor!!

### Birdy — 2012-03-22T04:59:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-4247>

Today I had two CT sessions, once out in the cold, and then a cold shower later in the day. I am struggling with what to wear. Today I just put on a tank top, but my husband objected. I run into some creepy people on my errands, and don't want some of the creeps staring at my nipples. But I don't want to be too hot either.

### Birdy — 2012-03-23T19:31:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-5062>

This morning's CT session was out in the Adirondak chair. I just watched how they make the chairs on the science channel, and wanted to test for myself how easy it is to sit in it and never get out. Mine is formed out of resin, but still comfy. I set the timer at 20 minutes and instructed my husband to get me at the proper time. Was it just a minute before I heard the tap on the window? I am reading John Grisham, and there is a reason he is a best-selling author. Tomorrow I'll go longer and wear less clothing. It was around 50 degrees and foggy and I was cold only when a little wind kicked up.

### Birdy — 2012-03-24T18:30:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-5457>

Yesterday the tap water was pretty warm, so the cold soak was kind of a bust. I increased my outdoor time this morning, and then my husband forgot to get me, so I went 35 minutes. I sat on the edge of the Adirondak chair in the foggy 49-degree drizzle until a lack of circulation and the cold made my toes turn blue and then I stood up and walked around for the rest of the time. I had huge trouble keeping warm through the night, and am wondering if I am doing something wrong. During the cold soak, I amused myself with knitting until it provided too much insulation for my lap. Then I switched to reading the novel. Now I don't want to put it down. As of today I have lost 15 lbs since starting the autoimmune leptin reset at the beginning of the year. It is slow, but now a more steady loss. I keep changing if and how much I want to lose. Since doing CT, fat has poured out of the small of my back and it is very boney there. I also lost lots of fat in my armpits. Look ma! No "crotch" fat either.

### Slone07 — 2012-03-24T19:20:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-5481>

I will admit, when I first read the CT blog, I thought to myself, "What a crock" and laughed it off. I may be biased towards warmth, though, as I've been miserably frigid cold for the past few years with my thyroid stuff. As I read, it makes more and more sense. I can only hope that the LR allows me to warm up enough to consider CT. I like what it's doing for you and am eager to see the full force of it when you're done.

### Birdy — 2012-03-25T20:42:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-5928>

Today's CT was in the rain. I went outside, with my leopard print swimsuit. (How Paleo is that? The good news: It fits! I bought it at a garage sale last fall but it didn't really fit yet. Even though it is an expensive Catalina, I meant to use it for a hot tub so I wouldn't ruin my other suit.) I set up the umbrella to shield my radio and Grisham novel. I sat down in the rain, reading the novel and listening to KUSC's choral music program. My mind drifted to the pumpkins still on the patio after all winter. They looked great, so I can do this too. I went inside when my fingers turned white, right when my husband came to get me. I lasted 22 minutes. Gotta luv KUSC!

Love the idea of sitting out in the rain in a leopard print suit for CT. Oh, what a great way to initiate discussion with neighbors...lol

### Birdy — 2012-03-27T03:17:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-6723>

### Birdy — 2012-03-27T04:44:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-6761>

Today's first CT session was cut short because I overslept. I had an egg breakfast and headed out to the patio for 22 minutes. It was wet, but not raining and really not all that cold. I got to the part where Mark met his lawyer while listening to Dennis Bartel play snappy music on KUSC, and then got that too-soon tap on the window. Yesterday's session left me hungry, and I did finally warm up, but was really cold in the evening before retiring. My hands kept cold so I figured, hey, if the ice man can do it so I can I, so I willed my hands to be warmer and that worked, but the will didn't have a memory and I would wake up cold again and have to re-will things. Finally I willed myself to get up and get another blanket, and that did the trick. It was thrown off soon anyway. I am experiencing CT envy, as my husband has started CT, seems to have permanently warmed up his hands, has declared success and now he's thinking of taking up swimming. It will be easy to get a soaking pool in the backyard, but now I may have to fight for pool-time. We'll see. He'd rather go down in flames than give up his TV and evening lights.

### Birdy — 2012-03-27T18:08:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-7051>

Today's CT was out on the patio, and the sun was just coming up. I make sure to sit underneath nothing, so the cold can be even colder. I shivered the whole time (38 degrees), but wasn't that uncomfortable. Just wearing wool socks, my insulating crocs and my underwear. Gee, hope there aren't any HOA "issues"! My feet hurt yesterday. I have injured my food and have reduced sensation and swelling on a couple of toes, so I think it is a good idea to keep my feet a bit warmer. I think the leopard swim suit is better, even though there is more coverage, compression and all. I got to the part where Reggie was threatening the FBI, and the plot thickens. As I got up, I realized how warm my butt was, sitting on my robe. I'll have to fix that.

### Birdy — 2012-03-27T18:09:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-7054>

I haven't lost additional weight, but my body is looking sculpted. This is very weird.

### Birdy — 2012-03-29T01:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-8007>

Today's CT session on the patio seemed colder. I guess it was in the 30's. I lasted for 25 minutes, but the last couple of minutes I got up and moved around, just to increase circulation in my legs. I wasn't really into it that much. I just stumbled out of bed, grabbed some quick meat and fat, and was out the door. The cold did wake me up. The wool socks really help the whole process.

### Birdy — 2012-03-29T17:47:16+0300

I am taking a day off from CT, and nursing a sore foot. I decided to take antibiotics, and a bit bummed that I'll have to fix gut health all over again, but I have had this infection on and off for months, after injuring my foot. The doctor scared me into taking antibiotics. I was cold and exhausted last night, but in the early morning, I threw all the covers off and stayed sleeping that way.

### Slone07 — 2012-03-29T18:11:50+0300

Thanks Slone07! Some of the inflammation is down. It really doesn't hurt that much because I already had nerve damage. I am just trying to not re-injure it. Gotta love the barefoot exercise folks who are so rigid. ### Birdy — 2012-03-30T23:39:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-8987>

I did today's CT sort of half-a$$ed. It was too warm (though foggy soupy) and I had too many clothes with me. I didn't even really shiver. I stayed out for half an hour. Mark just met someone in the elevator, my tea got cold, and I started singing to KUSC. I read about it on the Factor x monster thread. Could it be singing? Well, it was fun to try, but I could not keep up with the Mexican classical music, and the Mexico-inspired classical pieces featured this morning. ### Birdy — 2012-04-02T03:53:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-10102>

I have been taking it easy the past couple of days, nursing my foot. Finally today I soaked my feet in tap water. Our water is cold!!!!!!!! My feet hurt for a couple of minutes, but then felt much much better, and they were pink the whole time. I think I soaked them for 20 minutes. The cold water really made the infection area more comfortable. I spent the afternoon getting in a bit of Vitamin D. I am going to have to make a couple of swimsuits, or coverings that look like coverings from my neighbor's viewpoint, but really aren't. How about a loincloth?

### Birdy — 2012-04-02T18:20:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-10339>

Today's main CT was outside, where it was cold, breezy and with a dewpoint of 33! I started with knitting, until my hands started shaking uncontrollably and I stopped. Then I moved to fidgeting. A few minutes in and the sun came out, then all these sparrows started flying around. Soon, hundreds joined them, and they were all swooping, diving and running around. I turned off the radio. A formation of geese flew by, and very low, since the seemed to have recently taken off. There was a hawk high in a lone tree watching the birdy game. 30 minutes was over before I knew it. I felt I didn't get very cold and I warmed up in no time.

### Birdy — 2012-04-03T04:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-10658>

Canada geese......its a sign

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-03T13:50:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-10725>

So glad CT is helping your foot -- I hope you feel better soon. A spiky bush penetrated my Vibrams, so I understand you on the spiky stuff.

### Birdy — 2012-04-03T18:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-10821>

Today I took off from the icy morning CT. PaleoSue, I think I just have to go with the heavy-bottomed work shoes on the farm. I just hate wearing them much because they are quite heavy.

### Birdy — 2012-04-07T04:42:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-12357>

I have been able to vacation in a colder climate this weekend. In the morning, it is in the 40's and hasn't gotten much over 60 during mid-day. I haven't done much official air CT, but I have been running around town with just a light shirt on, while others are wearing serious jackets. I don't shiver in the morning cold anymore. I have been soaking my feet with very cold tap water twice a day. My toe turned beet red on the travel day when I couldn't soak it or prop it up. Keeping it in the cold (per my doc's instructions) keeps the swelling down. It looks better, and now that is down in size, it is starting to itch and I have been told that is a good sign of healing. This CT is all I have while I am gone, and finished the antibiotics which didn't do anything. My feet stay pink in the water, except where I had started growing a bunion. The bunion gets blue first. Who knew?

### Birdy — 2012-04-08T17:39:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-12684>

Happy Easter! I am taking a day off from any cold soaks. When I get back home, I'm going to see about getting a horse tub and rearranging my backyard. Right now, I have planters, pots, seedlings, and I want to convert it to a CT oasis.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-08T17:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-12698>

Birdy what a GREAT idea! I'll have to stop off at Shipton's tomorrow and price those tubs out. Sure wish hubby hadn't sold our hot tub a couple of years ago. It would have been GREAT for CT!

### Birdy — 2012-04-16T17:47:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-15587>

After pretty much an entire week of vacationing and entertaining, I am ready to get back to a less half-a$$ed diet and CT regimen. I sort of went off the diet for Easter, and the carbs creeped back and wouldn't leave. This week I will be beating them back again, but it is a long slog uphill for a few days. Sis visited, and even though I felt a bit pudgy from the week, and am still about 10 lbs heavier than her, she thought I looked really great and much thinner. Sis was mostly thinner than I ever was, but over the years, she has been going towards osteopenia, obesity and pre-diabetes. So it's really weird that we are basically the same size and body type, only she weighs 10 pounds less and I am two sizes smaller. Last year she gave me hand-me-downs after she lost weight, and this year, the roles are switched. I saw a woman on the train last week, and couldn't believe how much she looked like sis. It was very weird and kind of troubling, since the woman was about 100 pounds overweight. That is what my sister could become. The woman had great trouble navigating through the train, due to her extra weight and size. I don't want sis to end up like that. For two days, we got up early, enjoyed a moderate low carb breakfast, and then I was able to go till about 2:30 and she was hungry after a couple of hours, and then hungry over and over again. So, she is obviously in carb-burning territory, and maybe I didn't break myself as much as I thought.

### Birdy — 2012-04-18T17:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-16209>

I think it is the beginning of the end of cheap and free air CT. Last night I had the bedroom window open and it only got down to 66. Tap water is running hotter, even in the early morning. I usually keep a couple of jars of water in the fridge to mix with tap water, but I'll be switching to ice as soon as I eat that huge bag of frozen shrimp I bought this week..... Yesterday's CT consisted of an icy foot soak, being outside with few clothes on, and a cold shower that wasn't really cold. I am trying to get my spotter to go to the beach with me, but so far no luck. I "found" a huge Rubbermaid tub today, but not the kind designed for water. I am going to try it anyway, using a pine needle base. It has these molded plastic feet which will need to be reinforced by a proper base if I want it to last. Hopefully this thing will fit in my car easily and I can have a soaking tub installed by this weekend. When it breaks, I'll have a really great planter.

### Birdy — 2012-04-19T07:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-16519>

Now we are vying for who gets to take a shower first. We used to have to warm up the water first. I used to go first, then DH would go after the water was heated and then also turn on a portable heater. Now, the first person in gets the colder water. Sooo.......I "installed" the cold tub today, and covered it with an old window to keep the leaves out. It is in a shady spot, and sort of looks like something you might find in Restoration Hardware. And that would be appropriate.

### Birdy — 2012-04-19T21:40:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-16707>

Today was the first day with the outdoor cold tub, using just hose water. It really didn't seem that cold, though over an hour later I am still not all warmed up. My sleep hasn't been too great the past few nights, but after this CT, I feel like going into a deep sleep. My new tub is a bit sub-optimal. I washed it several times, and with bleach, but it still had a smell and I found that even after a post-soak shower, I still smelled a bit like the plastic, and it made my stomach a bit queasy. Anyway, DH is heavily invested in the whole process, having wonderful success with a variety of issues that people usually think are age- and injury-related. Now he wants the outdoor tub, too. He thinks we should use chlorine or something. We are going to find a tub big enough for him, and he likes the kind with the drain hole. I hope we can find something we are both happy with. Otherwise, we may end up like this (without the pharma, of course, not necessary ) [http://www.schadenfreude.net/2009/01/19/why-the-double-bathtub.php](http://http://www.schadenfreude.net/2009/01/19/why-the-double-bathtub.php)

### Birdy — 2012-04-27T03:39:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-19209>

I have been tubbing it for the last week, and looking forward to my first full-body swim this weekend, and my first beach visit. I am still finishing up my day-glo swim cap. I am still reading my book. Mark just ordered the pizzas.

### Birdy — 2012-05-02T03:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-20579>

Recently I did several days of full-body immersion in an unheated pool or in the ocean. It was easier than I thought it would be, maybe because I was chillin' in the sun. I got a bit of a suntan this weekend, too. Today's backyard CT was in the misty rain, and I went for 25 minutes. Mark is in the courtroom hearing. It was pretty easy for the first half, and then I got goosebumps but never lots of shivering until I got out of the tub. It is taking me longer to warm up today. I think I am ready for an iced tub, and so am scouting around for a thermometer. Next weekend I'll be getting to the beach again, not sure which beach. Hopefully I'll be able to catch it at lower tide.

### Birdy — 2012-05-05T05:18:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-21517>

Just some air-cooling today, and the beach for tomorrow. I am looking forward to it even though it probably won't be that cold.

### Birdy — 2012-05-09T18:16:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-22584>

The beach swim was fun, but I need to get into some serious conditioning before I feel comfy with lots of cold-water swimming. My new orange swim hat worked really well. This week I have filled up the backyard ct tub as high as it will go, and the tap water was pretty cold. Unfortunately, covering it with an old window traps in heat, and the next day I used it, my skin outside the water was colder than underneath the water. We had some really dry winds, and though the outside temp was warm, I got some serious cold on getting out of the tub. It is warm, and in order to continue serious CT in the summer, I'll have to step up my game.

### Birdy — 2012-05-09T18:18:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-22586>

Oh, and Mark is back in jail and eating pizza. I needed to put that book down because all that fighting has seemed like real life during these past few days. I got a book about Uri Geller instead. He came to my campus years ago, and I didn't know what to make of it, but we were out of water that night, a water main break in town. They say it happens all the time. Interesting stuff. I'll keep everyone here posted.

### Birdy — 2012-05-11T21:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-23238>

Today's CT was for 50 minutes in water in the low 60's. It has taken me over an hour to warm up. This week marks my first week with extended times in the tub, and for every day. The dry skin on my legs has returned, but the dry heels are getting better and better.

### Birdy — 2012-06-06T17:21:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-29720>

It was cold this morning, and I felt cold and still couldn't warm up. Paradise lost! Looking forward to the change of the seasons. I can't believe two more weeks and it is summer. I used to feel kind of sad at that time of the year, as it was all downhill from there. This year I am looking at it from a different perspective.

### MJ* — 2012-06-09T05:34:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-30244>

> **Birdy;29822 wrote:** It was cold this morning, and I felt cold and still couldn't warm up. Paradise lost! Looking forward to the change of the seasons. I can't believe two more weeks and it is summer. I used to feel kind of sad at that time of the year, as it was all downhill from there. This year I am looking at it from a different perspective.

Good for you Birdy!! It looks like you're doing great!

### Birdy — 2012-07-26T04:53:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-41151>

I am hobbling through this endless summer, doing cool CT about every week or so. I got a big increase in hormone levels without any supplementation whatsoever, but then adding in a few too many carbs caused everything to get out of balance again, with breast tenderness and pms-type symptoms. So I am back to lower carb. My hormone levels are overall higher, but balance has been restored. I have been sleeping well, and am careful about the evening lights. Keeping a light mask on all night allows me to leave the windows open and keep everything cool.

### Birdy — 2013-01-21T20:14:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-72167>

Hey folks! I am back on the leptin reset today. I got my labs, bought a can of clams and I am going to the beach tomorrow. I am rushing around this afternoon trying to get some things done before lights out.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-21T22:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-72228>

well you are getting ready for some eye opening stuff.......be ready on Jan 24th. And no.......I am not kidding.

### Birdy — 2013-02-27T02:50:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-77936>

Oh, it has been awhile. I am still on the LR, but I haven't lose any weight for a long time. I do deep ocean ct at least once a week, but I think all the cold is helping me gain weight. I have started making changes to minimize emf, mostly turning equipment off when not using it.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-27T02:56:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/birdys-nest.148/post-77938>

Birdy it's a tough road, isn't it. I've been on the reset for over a year with no weight loss in sight. Of course, during my journey I found out that I have too high an RT3/T3 ratio and THAT'S why I have had so much trouble with my weight all these years. I try to climb in the tub every other day. Sure wish hubby hadn't sold our hot tub several years ago. Now I'm looking at getting a new one.

Hang in there. It works ... just look in the "success stories" forum.

# Thread: Hi from St. Louis!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-st-louis.149/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vwaggs — 2012-03-15T17:22:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-st-louis.149/post-812>

I am a 40 yr old mom to two great kids and a very supportive husband. I work in Chihuahua rescue and teach 5th grade. I have always been healthy and involved in sports but right after I had kids I had a hard time taking off the baby weight and keeping it off. Atkins worked for awhile but then I found hCG. I did the homeopathic first and was always hungry so I ordered the Rx stuff but did it subligually. I was always hungry so that was horrible but I lost 30 lbs and hit goal in 2010 and kept 20 of it off. I just tried another round to get the extra 10 lbs off and I was miserable. Nothing else worked so I felt hCG and starving was my only option. Until now. I am finally losing again on the reset and CT without hunger. I am very excited about the overall health benefits! I am finally, after 3 weeks of baths every other night, getting used to the cold and warming up quicker. I love reading the stories of others and their health successes! This has so many possibilites for helping others and I am so grateful that I found Dr. Kruse and his work. Looking forward to having this one place to talk about all these great things and not going back and forth to all the forums.

### lisa32989 — 2012-03-24T16:51:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-st-louis.149/post-5422>

Hi! I just joined. I'm also in St Louis. I started the reset on Feb 7. I'm slowly feeling better. I think its time for some labs to see what's going on!

# Thread: I'm thinking of building a cooling tub

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2012-03-15T17:27:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-thinking-of-building-a-cooling-tub.150/post-819>

...figuring all I need is a vinyl pool liner and a bunch of urbanite, and a siphon hose when I want to clean it. If I just stack the urbanite up a few feet, will it hold the water, or do I need additional reinforcement? I figure I can get enough urbanite to buttress it all around if that is necessary.

### Dextery — 2012-03-15T23:30:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-thinking-of-building-a-cooling-tub.150/post-1073>

@monte on the CT6 comments said he bought a free standing tub and plumbed it to the cold water fawcett of the washing machine and then plumbed the incoming water through a aquarium chiller so he could have water as cold as 37 degrees F. Google aquarium chillers and look on ebay and craigs list.

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T23:35:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-thinking-of-building-a-cooling-tub.150/post-1080>

Wow!! I just checked tub prices! I was thinking something more free, so I could blow my limited budget on more seafood.

### JC2K — 2012-03-16T00:08:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-thinking-of-building-a-cooling-tub.150/post-1100>

Water's pretty heavy stuff. You'd have to be careful with the urbanite that it stays secure and doesn't slip on you.

Alternatively, look into children's wading pools. Not so expensive, but not sure how well they'd last.

### Continuum Fitness — 2012-03-16T01:07:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-thinking-of-building-a-cooling-tub.150/post-1124>

check out habitat for humanity stores, they may have old tubs for cheap. or even the for sale ads

### Birdy — 2012-03-16T01:39:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-thinking-of-building-a-cooling-tub.150/post-1150>

# Thread: Omega 3 supplementation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 67
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T18:02:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-848>

I'd love to hear from folks here about what brands/types of oils they're using to get their Omega 3s into their diet. I understand foods are the best form of nutrition, but I'm asking about supplementation at this point.

I'm looking at the most bang for my buck - fish oil in capsule or liquid (liquid seems easier when given the amounts needed to ingest to reduce inflammation from 4 decades of a CW diet). I've swallowed fish oil capsules by the handful and then switched to cod liver oil with an omega 3 of 1.4g/tsp. A few tsps and I'm done - much preferred. But then I've run across oils and brands that boast 2-3/omega 3s per serving, etc. Some have even resorted to buying bulk pet food salmon oil (not sure I can take that palatability yet).

I'm getting overwhelmed with the choices. I'm willing to spend a little $ to get a good quality product, but my goal is to get the most Omega 3s with the least amount of capsules/spoonfuls. Go with a good fish oil with high omega 3 concentration (and expense), or will cod liver oil suffice? Any brand recommendations?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T18:16:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-863>

I've gone w/the Jarrow Formulas Balanced EPA-DHA... when compared to some of the bigger name brands like nordic naturals - its a better deal and has a better EPA-DHA ratio...

Dex and Quelson have use human grade fish oils that you can buy at natural/holistic pet stores and have had good success..

### Dextery — 2012-03-15T18:20:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-865>

I would suggest you start with a 12 oz bottle of Life Line human grade Salmon Oil but sold into the Pet Food market. A 1 gal bottle frt prepaid is $75 but see if you can find it locally for a small amount first to see if you can take the taste. Just tastes like fish to me but I can eat most anything. One person said they use cold green tea as a chaser after each tablespoon. I take 3-4 Tablespoons a day. My ratio is 2.3 to 1 Dr. K says you do want some omega 6 in your diet because he could never operate on people. Omega 6 has clotting properties.

[http://www.wildalaskansalmonoil.com/contact.html](http://http://www.wildalaskansalmonoil.com/contact.html) If you buy the gallon, call Ted direct

Wild Alaskan Salmon Oil

Our fish oil contains ONLY pure Wild Alaskan Salmon Oil. Each 4650 mg teaspoon provides approximately 1395 mg of total omega-3 fatty acids, including 558 mg of EPA and 651 mg of DHA. In addition, laboratory analysis reveals an amazing 32 distinct additional fatty acid molecules you're unlikely to find in highly processed "purified" or "de-scented" alternatives.

There are 768 teaspoons/

256 tablespoons in one gallon for $75

Dex,

Thanks for the advice. I got the salmon oil you right about and started in on 4-5 tablespoons/day, about 2 days ago. Last night.....oops, I got the runs...and it was horrible.

Better today.

Without knowing my numbers, any estimate on how long before my ratio could be in the right range? What has been your experience? For me, 40 years of SAD, with the past year of paleo. I'm really hoping it gets there soon, because I have NOT cold adapted yet, after starting right in in the middle of Feb. I'm tired of being cold all the time on this, and I'm hoping that getting the ratio right will make the difference.

Also, once your ratio is in the right place, do you continue to take that much fish oil or do you taper back to something a lot smaller.

thanks from Big Sky Country.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T20:35:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-966>

> **Big Bobcat;924 wrote:** Dex,
>
> Thanks for the advice. I got the salmon oil you right about and started in on 4-5 tablespoons/day, about 2 days ago. Last night.....oops, I got the runs...and it was horrible.
>
> Better today.

What's been your experience with this as far as taste and ease of getting it down? I've been able to take cod liver oil (albeit flavored) without any problems. I'm afraid the salmon oil is going to taste/feel horrible and am putting off that option out of fear.

### blindpig — 2012-03-16T00:42:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-1112>

Clabbergirl,

I just started taking this a few days ago after seeing Dexter writing about it for so long. I had been taking CLO and having no problem with that taste at all. The salmon oil, well, it tastes like oily salmon, and I won't lie and say I'm enjoying it. But unless you have a really sensitive gag reflex, i can't imagine that you wouldn't be able to get it down. I am taking two regular size teaspoons full twice a day and so far no potty problems. I am willing to choke down a lot in the name of health, but if this stuff was too nasty I would not do it. I say give it a try! Just close your eyes and do it! (Do get a small quantity at first...I bought a half gallon, but I'm sharing with a dog and a bunch of cats, and may share with the chickens, too. Maybe DH, too, if he gets on board with the rest of the program!)

### Lyndra — 2012-03-16T01:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-1149>

I like Carlson's fish oil. The taste is very mild and comes in in lemon or orange flavor. It's sold at room temperature but needs to be refrigerated after opening. I prefer oil over capsules because your nose will help you know if oil has gone bad.
