I did not know my O6/O3 ratio before starting on Pure Line Salmon Oil but had it tested after two months of 5 tablespoons a day and the ratio was 2.3 to 1. Just remember that you do not want to overwhelm the omega 6 as it assists in the clotting mechanism of the body. I have backed down to 3 tablespoons a day now for maintenance.

The fact that you got the runs indicates leaky gut and leptin resistance. You need to marry the Leptin reset protocol with the leaky gut prescription along with the cold thermogenesis protocol. This will turn you into a fat burning machine instead of a carb/surgar burning machine. It takes the digestive system time to convert over.

Stay with it and you will be surprised at how well you will feel.

Got married in Missoula 41 years ago.

Dextery,

Thanks.

I am on the Leptin reset (started at the same time as CT, about middle of Feb). I will look into the Leaky Gut protocol...I hadn't thought about that.

Another question - last night I had to miss dinner and miss my after dinner CT. Late yesterday afternoon, I started to feel warm....very warm. I hadn't felt this warm without external heat in a long time. My hands were "burning". Could this be a sign that my pilot light is turning on? I have been doing dinner and after dinner CT since middle of Feb, and this was the first real day that I missed it. Is this a good sign? Should I keep on keeping on, or shift to something different.

Thanks again for your help. Missoula is a great town, but as you can probably tell from my username, I'm partial to the school in Bozeman. GO CATS

### JoeBranca — 2012-03-16T20:43:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-1645>

Any idea on the rough shelf life of the gallon of salmon oil, if refrigerated? If one were to do only 1 TBsp / day it could get finished before going bad?

### Dextery — 2012-03-16T22:04:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-1673>

> **JoeBranca;1647 wrote:** Any idea on the rough shelf life of the gallon of salmon oil, if refrigerated? If one were to do only 1 TBsp / day it could get finished before going bad?

The 1 gallon costs $75 freight free.

There are 256 tablespoons in one gallon. They do have half gallons for around $50 [http://www.ebay.com/itm/FRESH-Wild-Alaskan-Salmon-Oil-Omega3-DHA-pet-1-2-gallon-/120628238083?pt=LH\_DefaultDomain\_0&hash=item1c1600d703](http://http://www.ebay.com/itm/FRESH-Wild-Alaskan-Salmon-Oil-Omega3-DHA-pet-1-2-gallon-/120628238083?pt=LH_DefaultDomain_0&hash=item1c1600d703)

This is their Ebay store. Call them direct and save them the ebay commission. They will do the same deal.

My gallon lasted around 2 months in the fridge and I never detected it going bad. And that is the reason I like buying the fresh product. One can smell, taste, look at the salmon oil to determine if it has gone rancid...unlike the product than is encapsulated in the gel tabs.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-19T06:46:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-2642>

Jan (of the monster MDA thread) posted this on the CT 6 recent blog comments. I'd be interested in thoughts from you Dex et al:

What is the O6/O3 ratio is a good question.

Using dr Kruse advice, I went to horses mouth on that topic, dr Patricia Kane

What I found there is very helpful to me,

and

at the same time highly disturbing.

For years I was managing actively my Fatty Acids (with help of professionals),

and was doing it wrong way.

If what I found there is correct, then most people who are using

fish oils, krill oils, EPA/DHA pills are doing it all wrong,

and ass backward.

It was said there that

supplementing EPA/DHA is only half way right,

which (when you know better way) means that it is all wrong.

Conclusion,

EPA/DHA (may) have its place in therapy, in small doses,

only as finishing touches, guided by test results, otherwise do not use it.

Q.What actually is meant when by Omega6/Omega3 ratio?

A.Omega6=ALA, Omega3=LA

Omega6/Omega3=ALA/LA

That ratio is optimal when is 4:1

On a cheap, Hemp Oil is 3.8:1

Going first class BodyBio (own by dr Kane) makes 4:1 blend

Important

Omega6 can easily be suppressed by excess of Omega3

Omega3 is not suppressed by excess of Omega6

Supplementing by EPA/DHA creates at least two problems.

Suppresses Omega6

Suppresses ALA

suppressed Omega6 is missing (super important and highly misunderstood) AA Arachidonic Acid

ALA is not only used as material to produce EPA & DHA but, in this case more importantly,

ALA gives its own benefits (if is present)

Also mathematics of O6/O3 ratio are totally distorted.

Dr Kruse is saying that optimal ratio O6/O3 is 4:1 but good range is (2:1) to (6:1)

In this context, I am assuming that he is after ratio of totals.

(Total Omega6)=(LA+GLA+DGLA+DTA+Eicosadienoic)

(Total Omega3)=(ALA+EPA+DPA+DHA)

—————

What happened in my case

Analysis done at Genova)

(Total Omega6)/(Total Omega3)=2.8(3.4-9.4)

LA/ALA=(12.1/0.1)=121 not even close to desired 4

All this with help of “professionals”

Note that Genova have different O6/O3 range

I am hoping to get some enlightenment from dr Kruse on this.

Right now I am thinking winter/summer, hibernation/(active life) is affecting those numbers.

Note

dr Kane is dealing with warm people,

lets hear from dr Kruse what cold does to all this.

————

What to do:

There is a procedure that describes the process.

Practically it is.

Drop all fish, krill, EPA/DHA supplementation.

Supplement either with correct oil (4:1) from BodyBio or Hemp oil.

Protection of (vital)(AA) is achieved by use of Evening Primrose Oil (GLA).

Couple blood analysis, every 3 months, plus dose corrections could bring all this process to a close to perfect

(O6/O3=ALA/LA=4)

————-

I have made notes to myself here:

[http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171945#post171945](http://http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171945#post171945)

### Dextery — 2012-03-19T18:14:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-2788>

Regarding supplementation with Omega 3s:

I respect JanZ's research into Dr Patricia Kane's work and Dr Kruse has praised her work. He has encouraged Jan to go to the conference she is heading up in NJ in April.

Dr Kane is marketing an oil that she says is the perfect oil to bring the O6/O3 ratio to 4 to 1. It is made from cold pressed flax seeds and cold pressed sunflower oil.

My question is did our ancient pathways utilize these oils to get us through a million years? We do know that part of the ancient pathway utilized omega 3s from the sea...fish and kelp...but not plant omega products until much later.

Recently, Dr K has encouraged the consumption of seafood and seaweed along with cold thermogenesis. But I have seen Dr K downplay the use of flaxseed oil.

I guess you can consume flax and sunflower oil....but the real question is should you?

The writings of Dr Kane's husband offers a lot of proof that the 4:1 ratio is critical: [http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-4to1Oil.pdf](http://http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-4to1Oil.pdf)

I don't pretend to understand it all. And the anecdotal stories of assistance with Lyme, schizophrenia, CFS, MS, MD, Autism are impressive.

However, if one eats sufficient seafood and seaweed...or eats fresh wild salmon oil as I do...is this better than the seed oils that are blended together to 4:1?

How much of a mismatch is consuming these seed oils with our ancient pathways in pursuit of the optimal 4:1 omega 6/omega3 ratio?

Remember Dr K has said that if it weren't for Omega 6 FA, he could not operate on patients because there would be no clotting agent. And our ancesters would not have survived without Omega 6s.

For those that need a link to the Salmon Oil I take: [http://www.ebay.com/itm/FRESH-Wild-Alaskan-Salmon-Oil-Omega3-DHA-pet-1-2-gallon?item=120628238083&cmd=ViewItem&\_trksid=p5197.m7&\_trkparms=algo%3DLVI%26itu%3DUCI%26otn%3D1%26po%3DLVI%26ps%3D63%26clkid%3D7112681576122795935](http://http://www.ebay.com/itm/FRESH-Wild-Alaskan-Salmon-Oil-Omega3-DHA-pet-1-2-gallon?item=120628238083&cmd=ViewItem&_trksid=p5197.m7&_trkparms=algo%3DLVI%26itu%3DUCI%26otn%3D1%26po%3DLVI%26ps%3D63%26clkid%3D7112681576122795935)

Scroll down to see the analysis of EPA and DHA Omega 3s.

And as Jan said, Dr Kane is operating in a warm adapted population. If one becomes cold adapted and you have lengthened your telomeres, does Dr Kane's work apply?

### healthnut — 2012-03-25T02:12:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-5628>

My 0-6 to 0-3 ratio is 2.7 to 1. I can't take a lot of fish oil being Apo E4 E4, so I use a lot of chia seed, flax seeds. I take two caps of krill per day (240mg 0-3 dialy). It improved my ratio vs taking high dose fish oil (was formerly taking 3,000mg 0-3 from F.O.). I suspect switching from ethyl ester form of fish oil to the triglyceride form made the smaller dose of omega 3 more absorbable.

[http://www.langeeyecare.com/article\_fish\_oil\_triglycerides\_vs\_ethyl\_esters\_main.htm](http://http://www.langeeyecare.com/article_fish_oil_triglycerides_vs_ethyl_esters_main.htm)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-25T05:33:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-5677>

JanSz, I had a look at some of Patricia Kane's work. IN a bulletin about oils and correct functioning, she quotes the following (which is from Yehuda's research): Infancy and aging are two sensitive and critical periods, where adequate EFA and PUFA bioavailability is crucial for proper functioning of the brain. In addition, linoleic and alpha-linolenic acids per se have an effect on the neuronal membrane ﬂuidity index. They are able to decrease the cholesterol level in the neuronal membrane, which would otherwise decrease membrane ﬂuidity, which in turn would make it difﬁcult for the cell to carry out its

normal functions and increase the cellÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s susceptibility to injury and death. Symptoms of essential fatty acid deﬁciency include fatigue, dermatological problems, immune problems, weakness, gastrointestinal disorders, heart and circulatory problems, growth retardation,

and sterility --- this list is not all-inclusive.Ã¢â‚¬Â￾

She then goes on to summarise by saying: "For the majority, the 4:1 oil should be a staple, with the dietary addition of eggs,

meat, and dairy for the n-6s, and ﬁsh or ﬁsh oil for the n-3s, which should ﬁ ll in our higher EFA requirements." The 4:1 oil is the blend you mentioned, which is made from sunflower and flax.

She seems to be quite clear that, on its own, 03 supplementation with fish oil is highly problematic, will suppress o6 in the body and lead to a lack of membrane fluidity, as well as a host of problems typically associated in mainstream with 'high fat' diets. Yet she seems to indicate that fish oil, in combination with plant oils and the right diet, is the way to go.

Does this seem right to anyone else? Dr Kruse? Care to weigh in?

Saying Omega-6 or Omega-3 is not descriptive.

There are many omega6 type of fatty acids.

It all starts with "parent" omega6 that is LA

There are many omega-3 types of fatty acids.

It all starts wit "parent" which is ALA.

One can make ratio, and they do that and report on test report:

(Sum of all Omega6)/(Sum of all Omega-3)

but dr Patricia Kane holds that good ratio is:

LA/ALA=4

About both of these ratios can be said that they are O6/O3 ratio.

Which one is yours?

The whole discussion on oils is about this type of situations and advertising EPA & DHA (out of context).

You are not missing anything, this topic should be better addressed by dr Kruse.

It is clear to me that even laboratories (Genova Diagnostics and Spectracell) do not share (or do not know about) this concern.

I did my blood analysis there, within 2 weeks span. Individual Fatty Acids results were about the same.

Spectracell had given me outright pass on my results.

They just said HS Index=8.8% desirable is 8% you are good to go.

Genova have given me much more detailed report.

[http://img705.imageshack.us/img705/5059/image163x.jpg](http://http://img705.imageshack.us/img705/5059/image163x.jpg)

They were concerned about all kind of individual levels and ratios.

But they do not have upper cut off levels for any individual Omega3 component.

So, when I loaded up on EPA & DHA and consequently suppressed LA and AA that was not clearly seen as bad input vs bad output.

I really like Genova's way of reporting, very visually friendly.

If I could change there something, I would add

upper ranges for EPA & DHA

and

ALA/LA ratio.

I will send PM to dr Kruse.

Hopefully he will say something.

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/member.php?353-Jack-Kruse](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/member.php?353-Jack-Kruse)

### Dextery — 2012-03-26T04:09:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-6188>

> **healthnut;6141 wrote:** I didn't see that they distinguished either. From what I understant....the triglyceride form is what is found naturally in fish oil that has not undergone the mollecular microdistillation process.

This from the bottle of Life Line

"Most commercially available fish oils have rather mysterious origins. This is because many of them begin as a low grade by-product of rendering plants, requiring excessive processing before they are fit for human consumption. This process results in fish oil that is anything but natural. While molecular distillation is often advertised as a plus, and indeed does reduce contaminants, some researchers believe it also denatures the oil and lowers its beneficial value."

"In contrast, Life Line wild salmon oil is 100% natural. It is produced in a dedicated processing facility in Alaska from freshly caught, sustainably harvested salmon utilizing a “cold” extraction method that ensures the viability of our oil. Immediately after extraction natural mixed tocopherols (Vitamin E) are added to maintain freshness. The oil is immediately deep chilled and stored in airtight, lightproof containers until filtered and bottled. This thorough filtration process results in a pure, unadulterated extra virgin salmon oil rich in health-promoting omega-3s as well as a naturally balanced “formula” of over 30 other distinct fatty acid molecules."

### freshveggies — 2012-03-30T01:08:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-supplementation.153/post-8502>

Here is some info about krill oil. So it looks like if you have a good ratio that krill oil would be the way to supplement.

A krill, in profile

Krill oil, like fish oil, contains both of the omega-3 fats eicosapentanoic acid (EPA) and docosahexanoic acid (DHA), but hooked together in a different form. In fish oil these omega-3 fatty acids are found in the triglyceride form whereas in krill oil they are hooked up in a double chain phospholipid structure. (The fats in our own cell walls are in the phospholipid form.) Attached to the EPA leg of the phospholipid is a molecule of astaxanthin, an extremely potent anti-oxidant. The phospholipid structure of the EPA and DHA in krill oil makes them much more absorbable and allows for a much easier entrance into the mitochondria and the cellular nucleus. In addition to EPA and DHA krill oil contains a complex phospholipid profile including phosphatidylcholine, a potent source of reductive-stress-reducing choline, which also acts as a natural emulsifier.

Krill oil contains vitamin E, vitamin A, vitamin D and canthaxanthin, which is, like astaxanthin, a potent anti-oxidant. The anti-oxidant potency of krill oil is such that when compared to fish oil in tems of ORAC (Oxygen radical absorptance capacity) values it was found to be 48 times more potent than fish oil.

The astaxanthin found in krill oil provides excellent protection against ultravoilet light and UV-induced skin damage. It was for this reason that I started taking krill oil to begin with–I only discovered its other virtues later on.

### HeyDianne — 2012-03-15T18:03:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-locations-etc.154/post-849>

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T18:15:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-locations-etc.154/post-862>

In the places you want to lose the most fat.

### HeyDianne — 2012-03-15T18:25:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-locations-etc.154/post-871>

I have been doing mostly stomach at work (thighs at home as it is really awkward) but didn't know if the back of the shoulder area would be good. Just drawing on something that I read years ago that I believe said that most BAT is located in that area. Any thoughts about that?

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-15T19:05:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-locations-etc.154/post-894>

> **HeyDianne;872 wrote:** I have been doing mostly stomach at work (thighs at home as it is really awkward) but didn't know if the back of the shoulder area would be good. Just drawing on something that I read years ago that I believe said that most BAT is located in that area. Any thoughts about that?

Several people on the MDA forums have spot-iced on the upper back area between the shoulder blades because of BAT. I've done it also simply because it was easy to place a pack there when laying down, etc. Even if it doesn't help brown fat, it's still cooling surface area temps. I see it as a win-win.

### grammasmitty@hotmail.com — 2012-03-16T04:35:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-locations-etc.154/post-1267>

I started by putting an ice pack on my belly for several hours and in my first 6 days lost 2" from just my belly! I use one on my back when I am getting ready to take my CB. It makes it so much easier to lie down in that very cool water when my back is already cold. LOL!!

### Dan in Utah — 2012-06-21T01:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-pack-locations-etc.154/post-32541>

Here's a link to interesting study on BAT in adults, with accompanying scans showing the most common BAT area. Based on this, I'm going to start icing my neck and upper torso for awhile. [http://ajpendo.physiology.org/content/293/2/E444.long](http://http://ajpendo.physiology.org/content/293/2/E444.long)

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-05T18:14:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-11766>

You are not getting enough protein at breakfast. Try using fit day.com as it will help you calculate how much protein you're actually getting.

My BAB (which is about 50g) is usually 5-6oz of grass fed ground chuck, 3 eggs with coconut milk (from the carton, not the can), and a couple of slices of nitrate free bacon.

Bullet proof coffee is great ... an excuse to add more dietary fat is always welcome  so why are you ditching it? cook your eggs in CO. Now you have both.

OK I'm done with the "lecture." ;P

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-07T23:46:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-12535>

According to whom? Jack sez:

> 2\. To regain Leptin Sensitivity (LS) follow a strict Paleolithic diet as outlined in The Paleo Solution by Robb Wolf or The Primal Blueprint by Mark Sisson.
>
> The type of fuel you eat is important in eliminating the foods that cause Leptin receptors to become nonfunctional. These two books clearly outline a solid reference point to achieve this.
>
>
>
> A. Try to eat as soon as possible upon rising in the AM, ideally within 30 minutes of waking. Make sure that breakfast has little to no carbs (less than 50 grams), and has a lot of protein and fat. I use as a general rule 50-75 grams of protein with most patients. Some patients can use less and some need more. The key point of knowing how much is right for you is your hunger later in the day. If you remain ravenous throughout the day, you need to eat more protein in the AM. If you can hold off eating until dinner you probably are at homeostasis for you. If you can skip both meals you likely are overdoing it at breakfast. As for sources, I suggest pastured or organic eggs first, served with left over dinner scraps of grass fed meats, poultry, or fish. A third option, although less ideal, would be whey protein or protein shakes.
>
>
>
> B. Try to limit carb intake to 25 grams if you are overweight by more than 30 lbs. If you are fit and have a small amount of weight to lose, (less than 30 lbs.) you can titrate up your carb loads. Even then, I do not advocate potatoes or rice as some Paleo diets allow for. You will be able to eat them eventually, but try to avoid starches until you have mastered your cravings and hunger. Do not count calories; it is not needed at this point. Any time I eat carbs I use liberal amounts of butter, heavy cream, coconut or palm oil. I do not recommend other oils initially such as olive oils or industrial seed oils. I would also avoid nut oils at the initial stages. My personal favorite is coconut oil because of the great metabolic effects of MCT, and how it helps heal the guts of LR folks.

Are you eating carbs? THEN add in lots butter, cream, etc as he states above. This is what I'm trying to do ... HIS leptin Rx, not how others seem to be tweaking it.

Just my opinion and worth exactly what you paid for it.

### penguinmom718@yahoo.com — 2012-04-08T00:28:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-12537>

Interesting. I read this

> Make sure that breakfast has little to no carbs (less than 50 grams), and has **a lot of protein and fat**.

To mean we need a lot of both at breakfast, even though Dr Kruse only gave amounts for protein.

I find that while enough-for-me breakfast protein keeps me from nighttime cravings, I need a lot of fat also to keep from getting hungry during the day/before lunch. I have done the following at breakfast.

enough protein + enough fat - I can last to dinner or almost there; no night cravings

enough protein + not enough fat ( example my meat was fairly lean like pork loin) hungry before/by lunch. This was back when I thought Dr Kruse meant protein was the only item that mattered.

And one day when I couldn't stand the thought of another protein breakfast, I did

carbs+ little to no protein + enough-for-me fat - I was not hungry until dinner BUT I did get night cravings.

Interesting to me, I was back to being fine with protein and fat and no/low carb breakfast the next day.

### penguinmom718@yahoo.com — 2012-04-08T00:32:58+0300

> **ealachan;12601 wrote:** I thought I was supposed to be at 70%+ calories from fat...did I dream that up? I wouldn't put it past me, frankly...

I think you are correct as I remember reading at least 60% by calories from fat, with something like 20-30% protein and up to 10-20% carbs. You can keep carbs even lower, and raise the fat. You don't want "too much for you" or all protein.

### ealachan — 2012-04-12T03:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-14018>

I got in a cold tub for the first time tonight. HOLY MOLEY, people. The water was 70 degrees, and it actually wasn't hard for me to get in at all, but I started shivering about 2 minutes in. I was shocked, as spot-icing never makes me shiver, walking outside in the cold (like 30 degrees) with no coat doesn't make me shiver, walking around naked in a 60 degree house doesn't make me shiver!

I spent 10 minutes in the water, then got out. My skin where it had been under water was reading at 71 degrees when I got out. I've still got kind of a sub-shiver (just feels like slight muscle tenseness without any actual shaking) thing going on 10 minutes later. I have just a hint of that lovely leaden-legged cold-tiredness I used to get as a kid when I'd spend literally hours every day in the pool or lake.

My biggest problem is that we have a really weird bathtub and there's nothing to help haul my fat butt out of it once I get down in it. I basically have to rely on my pretty measly upper body strength to propel myself up out of the tub when I'm done and that doesn't work so well. Sooo...I think it's back to icing for me, at least until I can start getting into the lake or my mom opens her pool up.

### ealachan — 2012-04-13T17:01:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-14714>

**Leptin Reset, Day 44**

b: 5oz beef, two eggs, BP coffee w/ CO, KG and 2 tsp cocoa

l: not planning on any

d: big salad with a little cheese and some chicken thighs, olive oil and sherry vinegar. Ate early - 3:30ish, because I was home and husband was bugging me about fixing something to eat.

\- No CT last night because I had a couple drinks while I was out. Will make up for it this weekend, though.

\- slept like a rock from about 10:30 - 12:15, then from 12:45 - 7am. It got down to 25 degrees last night and husband was semi-horrified that I left the window open a couple inches, but I really do sleep SO, SO, SO much better (deeper?) when the bedroom is cold.

It's interesting - I used to sleep with my window open a little at night as a kid all the time, but growing up seems to have made me forget that. I used to swim in cold water all the time as a kid...but growing up made me forget about that, too. Makes me wonder what else growing up has made me forget.

### ealachan — 2012-04-14T17:51:25+0300

L: -

D: 6oz steak with some mushrooms, one grilled Roma tomato, half a cup of steamed broccoli, and a few buffalo chicken wings around 3pm.

\- Noticed something interesting this morning. After not having eaten for about 18 hours (not intentionally - I just ate dinner early last night and breakfast late this morning), I tested for ketosis and only registered a "trace". It seems like if I hadn't eaten for a long time, my body should be burning fat to keep me going, and thus producing ketones, right? So why did I not register darker on the test strip?

ETA: I tested again about an hour or so after eating breakfast, and my strip was dark purple! So...my body only produced ketones when I've eaten? I'm clearly missing something, here...

### ealachan — 2012-04-17T17:02:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-15885>

**Leptin Reset, Day 48**

b: two eggs, two slices of bacon, green tea (I ran out of coffee, waaaah!)

L: -

D: 200g of potato, 6ish oz t-bone steak

\- Only ate a little kielbasa and a low-carb ice cream bar (I KNOW) for dinner last night. It was way too hot, I was miserable and exhausted, and I just plain didn't damn well care. Still wasn't hugely hungry this morning, so had a smaller breakfast than usual.

\- TMI WARNING: guts are still crampy. It's not my stomach, it's down in my intestines, all on the left side, mostly up near my waist but also down along the side sometimes. It still feels very much like I'm full of gas or poo or something, but very little of anything actually comes out when I go to the bathroom, for the most part. I did have a BM earlier that wasn't runs, but it was certainly not normal. I was passing clear mucus last night as well...yuck. I also keep getting random waves of cramping that seem to travel across my gut from right to left....those REALLY hurt, but they don't last long - maybe 30 seconds or so. I don't think i was actually running a fever last night...I think I was just overheated because it was so beastly hot in our apartment last night. I'm going to try to give the guts another day to work themselves out, but if they're not better tomorrow morning, I'm going to go to the doctor.

\- slept better last night, though I did wake up with cramps a few times. Was mostly able to get back to sleep with no problems. Not being utterly exhausted this morning has helped my outlook tremendously...LOL I tend to get kind of hysterical when I get really tired, especially when I don't feel well at the same time.

### ealachan — 2012-04-17T23:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-15991>

Feeling better this afternoon (knock on wood), but I'm still going to go to the doctor tomorrow morning to get checked out, as I still have an ache and heavy feeling in my lower left pelvis and I'm still passing kind of mini-stools every time I go to the bathroom. I have a cyst on my left ovary and I want to rule out torsion, among other things. If I didn't need the ovaries for the hormones, I'd just have 'em yanked and be done with it, since I don't want kids anyway.

### ColdBren — 2012-04-18T00:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-15998>

I had similar gastroint. symptoms, and I have read tons of people having the loose stools. (there are entire threads on it) So, I actually think they are great signs. It seems like the quickest way to get rid of the junk and fat is through the stool...and so I am assuming loose stools during CT is straight fat loss. As for the bloating feeling, probiotics+enzmes have cleared that for me, I think it takes both. I know Dr. K drinks Kevita (from whole foods) which has good probiotics...tastes horrible the first time you try it (due to the appe cider vinegar) but after a few of them, I love them! I had an ovarian cyst actually rupture on me soon after starting CT so keep an eye on the pain....but to be honest, that cyst exploding was the best thing that happened. I have since had no ovulation pain (which had been worse than period cramps for YEARS) I think you are going through some serious detox.....sorry its so annoying, but you seem really tough!

### ealachan — 2012-04-18T03:00:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-16082>

> **ColdBren;16068 wrote:** I had similar gastroint. symptoms, and I have read tons of people having the loose stools. (there are entire threads on it) So, I actually think they are great signs. It seems like the quickest way to get rid of the junk and fat is through the stool...and so I am assuming loose stools during CT is straight fat loss. As for the bloating feeling, probiotics+enzmes have cleared that for me, I think it takes both. I know Dr. K drinks Kevita (from whole foods) which has good probiotics...tastes horrible the first time you try it (due to the appe cider vinegar) but after a few of them, I love them! I had an ovarian cyst actually rupture on me soon after starting CT so keep an eye on the pain....but to be honest, that cyst exploding was the best thing that happened. I have since had no ovulation pain (which had been worse than period cramps for YEARS) I think you are going through some serious detox.....sorry its so annoying, but you seem really tough!

Yep, if I get checked out tomorrow and they can't find anything acutely wrong with me, I'm going to go with the assumption that this is some crazy detox and just keep on keepin' on. The loose stools themselves don't worry me...it's the weird constant cramping I've had since Sunday. I'm PRETTY sure it's nothing scary, but I just want to double check. The clinic I go to is pretty progressive and my PCP and the nurse practitioner I see are both open-minded. I don't think it's going to end up being a case of "let's see what we can diagnose you with", which is what some doctors certainly seem to like to do!

I'll have to see if I can find Kevita and some probiotics. I already take a crapload of supplements...I might as well add another one to the mix.

eta: thank you for the post, by the way!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-18T07:48:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-16158>

Hang in there! I hope the doc gave you the all-clear. FWIW, I had some awful GI issues about 3 weeks into CT. It lasted a couple of days and I was bloody miserable. As no one else in my family got sick and nothing had changed in my diet, I'm pretty sure it was detox. I definitely recommend probiotics ( supplements or food sources); and re your mag supp, probably make sure it's cheated and not mag citrate.

### ealachan — 2012-04-18T17:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-16208>

**Leptin Reset, Day 48**

B: two sausages (19g protein each), two eggs, black coffee w/ no CO or KG because I was disorganized and in a hurry.

L: -

D: chicken leg and a bit of breast, half a potato (about 200g), 3/4 cup steamed broccoli

\- feeling a little better this morning. Slept somewhat crappily last night, which I think is mostly down to mistakenly drinking half of a fancy vitamin-water drink that I didn't realize had caffiene in it...arg! I've been better about being able to handle caffiene lately, but it still keeps me awake at night if I have it after about 3pm...and this was about 6 that I drank that stupid thing. I read the ingredients, too...just not close enough. At least I only drank half. If I had downed the whole thing, it would have been ugly!

\- saw the doctor this morning. She pressed and prodded and listened and thumped and decided that she thinks the pain I'm having is likely related to my ovarian cysts. She says one may have ruptured, or it may just be irritated from hormone fluctuations. She said that the weird constipation-to-diahrea swing can be caused by hormone fluctuations as well. In the grand scheme of things, it actually jives pretty well with the idea that this is all detox, since CT'ing has probably liberated a bunch of estrogen, which in turn has started to screw things up with the lady-bits. She advised to take advil for the pain if I thought I needed to (which, I'm tough, so I probably won't), and I'm under orders to be seen again ASAP if I develop nausea, vomiting, bleeding, or fever, as those are all possible signs that something more emergent is going on. I'll take that with a grain of salt, of course.

\- had a charlie horse in my leg for the first time in AGES last night, even with having taken my magnesium before bed. Also had a cramp in my foot later on in the night that woke me up, so I think my electrolytes are probably out of whack. I know for a fact I'm not getting anywhere near 3500mg of potassium a day. I'm pretty sure my sodium is ok and I would think my magnesium is too. I can't seem to find a multivitamin that has potassium in it...is that normal?!

\- somewhat bummed out by the fact that my scale at home seems to be about 20lbs kinder to me than the one at the doctor's office. I was weighing about 348 this morning at home, then went to the doctor's and weighed 367! So by their total, I've only lost 8lbs since February (the 375 starting weight was from their scale). I know it's not supposed to be about the pounds at all, and I DO both look and feel far different than I did when I started this, but the scale thing is inherently depressing none the less. Meh.

### ealachan — 2012-04-19T17:50:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-16599>

**Leptin Reset Day 49**

B: two sausages, two eggs, BP coffee w/ CO, KG and 1 tsp cocoa

L: -

D: haddock pan-fried in a mixture of CO and a little bacon fat, 2 servings of spinach sauteed with a little butter and sherry vinegar. Ate a total of over half a pound of fish. Could have happily eaten the whole pound. It was SO GOOD.

\- no CT last night. Hubs was home and I just wanted to relax and unwind with him after a stressful first part of the week of not feeling well. I must have been even more exhausted than I thought, because we shut all the lights off after dinner to watch some TV and by 9:15 I was getting dozey in my chair. I was in bed by about 9:30, and I bet I didn't lay there 10 minutes before I zonked out. I woke up a couple times that I was aware of, but just rolled over and passed back out again until about 7 this morning.

\- Have been noticing that, once I actually get up out of bed, I'm ready to go. I used to feel like crap for an hour or so after I got up - groggy and grumpy...to the point where I have an epic reputation with my family as "not a morning person". But lately, once I'm up, I just feel normal. Awake, happy, ready to do stuff. And that's BEFORE coffee!

\- switched to regular coffee (was drinking decaff) and tried cold-brewing. It's friggin' AMAZING how different the coffee tastes!! I don't know if cold brewing cuts down on the caffiene in the finished coffee too or not, but I don't feel jittery from it like I normally would. Very happy about that. I just need to find a big French press so I don't have to fart around with filters and pouring coffee into different jars and all the hoopla i went through this morning. It was a hassle, but that's what I get for trying things on a whim without being prepared. Story of my life...hah!

\- guts are feeling much better today. Still not 100%, but less pain than yesterday and MUCH less than earlier in the week.

\- ate half a potato (about 200g) and 3/4 cup of broccoli with dinner last night and didn't have any problems with muscle cramps. I found Dr. K's "eat a paleo diet and you should be covered" answer to my potassium question kind of frustrating, as it seems nearly impossible to stick to the 25g of carbs or less rule and still manage to eat enough veg to get 3500mg of potassium a day, unless I'm missing some big magical non-fruit, non-starchy source of potassium somewhere...which, frankly, could be quite possible.

\- noticed a weird little bruise on my arm this morning, though I can't remember what I might have banged into that would have caused it. The general area it's in is kind of twinge-y as well, so I'm sure my klutzy ass must have banged into something at some point.

\- skin is SO dry and itchy...ugh. It's really aggravating.

\- should be able to resume CT tonight, as husband will be at work.

### ealachan — 2012-04-20T17:34:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-17073>

**Leptin Reset, Day 50**

b: 1.5 sausages, 3 eggs, BP coffee w/ KG and CO, 1 tbsp cocoa

l: -

d: [chicken piccata](http://http://onegirlcooks.blogspot.com/2012/04/chicken-piccata.html) with asparagus, which came out awesome!

\- no CT last night. Got in late and then got distracted with housework.

\- skin feels like sandpaper in places, even after lotion. Seem to notice it most on my lower legs, especially a patch right above the front of my ankle on both legs.

\- TMI ALERT: guts are feeling mostly better...cramping / soreness are almost all gone, but haven't had a solid poo since Wednesday morning. This morning has been particularly...abundant and forceful. And weird. This morning there were several 3-4inch long strands of white-ish material that I noticed before I flushed (sorry, told you it was TMI, but I find this stuff oddly interesting), and I haven't eaten anything with that texture. Then, later on when I got to work I had another ass-plosion and it had a bunch of what looked like last night's spinach, undigested, in it. Guessing I didn't absorb much potassium from the spinach, then...LOL

\- Kind of tired this morning. Had a hard time getting to sleep last night, but I screwed around on the computer way after dark, so not terribly surprised.

\- Up to 10k mg of D3 a day, but not sure if it's doing me much good if my guts are still messed up and things aren't getting absorbed.

### ealachan — 2012-04-21T16:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-17403>

**Leptin Reset, Day 51**

B: 6.5oz beef, BP coffee w/ CO and KG

L: -

D: meatloaf, roasted red potatoes, 1/4 cup of green peas

\- woke up this morning with a messed-up eye. It looks ok, but my sight is very slightly blurry through that eye. It's annoying. It's my non-dominant eye, but it's still noticeable. It was fine at 4.30 this morning when I was taking hubs to work, but apparently it decided to go weird sometime between 4:45 when I went back to bed and 8am when I got back up. So, that's fun.

\- forgot to start my coffee last night, so I had hot-brew this morning rather than cold brew, and the difference is quite stunning. I definitely prefer cold brew.

\- lots of post-nasal drip yesterday and today. The pollen is really heavy around here right now, and we haven't had any rain, so that's been making it a lot worse. Even so, I've got nothing like the allergies I've always gotten in the spring before, which is great! It's still a little early, but I'm hopeful the trend will continue.

### ealachan — 2012-04-21T18:01:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-17430>

15 min of legs in 60 degree water. Water felt actually warm after about 3 minutes...very odd, but nifty.

Diahrrea again this morning, about 10 minutes after CT, but no cramping or anything else with it, so not going to worry too much about it.

OMG- the exact same thing happened to me! I think the CT is making major changes in the gut biome! I had that nasty dia. and then proceeded to come down with a nasty head and chest cold, to take care! I haven't done a cold bath since but continue to spot ice. Still get gurgly tummy sounds from tummy icing. Probiotics stopped the dia. for me.

I also got the ovulation pain as well, so you're not alone.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-04-22T01:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-17576>

Hiya Ealachan! THanks for helping earlier with my gall bladder issue. I feel much better, saw GI doc, he recommended removing all caffeine, drinking 1 oz aloe vera juice 2x day, and gave me scrip for nexium (2 outta 3 aint bad) Did a scan of GB, spleen, pancreas etc, he is cool with my being paleo, he outright said "carbs are what messes up the GI tract".

I also have had weird GI issues, post nasal drip (that wakes me up in the middle of the night-argh!) and crazy itchy dry skin, especially a patch on the front of my right ankle, and left lower shin, they are both super rough. When I do cold baths, I alternate days: 1 day sitting up with legs/feet in; next day reclining back with head on bath pillow and my feet out, to give my dry ankles and shins a break.

"several 3-4inch long strands of white-ish material that I noticed before I flushed" Parasites?!

Good journal

Hi Teenie - glad you're feeling better! I wasn't even remotely Paleo back when mine went bad, so I'm sure all the whole wheat and brown rice and oatmeal I was eating all the time to be "healthy" really did a number on it.

re: parasites - I don't know...seems like if I had a tapeworm, I'd be skinnier...hahaha! Seriously, though...I'm not sure what it was. Haven't had it since. Hopefully won't have it again!

### Kiera2 — 2012-04-22T10:49:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-17681>

I'm glad I took a moment to stop by here ealachan, I had exactly the same pain/poop response when I started CT. From day 6 to 8, and again from about day 13 to 16. Exactly the same pain as you described, down the left side of my abdomen. I imagined that peristalsis was jumping from forward to reverse, it was so painful. It was followed by mucous and odd 'fluffy' poop. Even drinking water would trigger another bout. As far as I know I haven't ovulated for quite some time, and no ovarian cysts, so mine was just a bowel detox. I haven't had the white stuff you describe, but I know a lot of people following the GAPS diet have. Another sign of detox. So, hopefully, you're through that nasty episode now.

Hi Coriander - thanks for telling me that. It's reassuring to know that others have had the same experience. It's weird to me that the whole time I was spot-icing, I never had any bowel issues...but as soon as I started doing cold-water soaks, I got hit with major detox. Guess that means it's really working?  I do feel much better now - no more lingering soreness, and although my bowels are still loose, I'm back to my "regular" time of day, rather than constantly feeling like I need to go and not having anything happen. Whew!

### ealachan — 2012-04-22T18:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-17775>

**Leptin Reset, Day 52**

B: 7oz beef, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: probably leftover meatloaf

\- Noticed the last couple times I've eaten that about an hour afterward, the skin down my spine seems to heat up from the inside. I only notice it on my back, and the skin doesn't actually feel any warmer than the rest of my body to the touch, but it feels hot just under the skin. Also noticed the same feeling in the same area last night while in bed. I was warm enough that I had to kick the quilt off, even though it couldn't have been more than 60 degrees in our room and the window was open (it got down to about 40 deg last night).

\- Ate more carbs than I should have last night, but still stayed in ketosis. I've been running in the two darkest purple categories on ketostix for the past 5 days or so.

\- TMI WARNING: I ate some roasted red-skinned potatoes last night as part of dinner, and quite a lot of the skins didn't digest at all, by the looks of my poo this morning. Really need to get some probiotics and see if it slows things down inside a little.

\- no CT today, as I'm trying to ease back into it to avoid the epic detox thing again. I say "no CT", but I still am wandering around in my house in a tshirt with no socks on when it's 60 degrees in here.

### ealachan — 2012-04-23T00:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-17906>

Got some krill oil supps today - 300mg each. I should probably be taking two at a time, yeah?

Also got some probiotics, which are NOT cheap. If they help they'll be well worth it, though.

Picked up some different magnesium as well. This stuff is magnesium oxide with chelated zinc added. I'm hoping I get more out of it than I've been getting from the magnesium stearate I've been taking.

I swore I saw Beatine Hcl at the store before, but I couldn't find it this time. I have another local store I want to check before I buy it online.

\- not enough protein with breakfast, I know. I'm sure I'll start feeling that about 2pm. Gotta get better organized for mornings.

\- went to bed around 10:15 last night, and I don't think I laid there 10 minutes before I zonked out. Woke up a couple times briefly but went right back to sleep. Got up a little after 7 and felt good, although it was dark and rainy and I really just wanted to stay home and veg all day.

\- had a lot of ovulation pain last night (no question that's what it was, as I've had this particular pain for years)...enough so that I popped a couple Advil around 9pm. Seems fine so far this morning.

### ealachan — 2012-04-24T17:10:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-18360>

**Leptin Reset, Day 54**

b: slightly less than 5oz beef (steak, with accompanying fat), two eggs, BP coffee

l: half a pound of deli roast beef (it was all I could find at the time), V8 juice, a little dark chocolate

d: probably more steak

\- Dinner last night ended up being egg-drop soup with a ton of shredded chicken breast in it. I ate about 1.5 chicken breasts' worth of meat, plus the actual soup part which is comprised of chicken stock, an egg, scallions and sesame seed oil. Interestingly, it brought me out of ketosis. I'm guessing because there was minimal fat involved (only what was in the skinless chicken plus the eggs)? Would too much protein all at once like that bring me out of ketosis?

\- Woke up this morning with an effed-up neck. Really limited range of motion. I must have slept funny last night or something, but it's weird because I'm using the same pillows I've been using for months and haven't had a problem before.

\- Probiotics are the best thing ever!!! Normal poo this morning! HUZZAH!!

\- was looking at the krill oil supplement I bought and realized that it actually has 1/10th of the O3's I was getting from the fish oil I was taking. Crap! I really can't afford to feed both me and my husband huge doses of krill oil in addition to everything else. \*sigh\* Guess I need to learn to love sardines.

### ealachan — 2012-04-25T17:13:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-18701>

**Leptin Reset, Day 55**

B: two and a half sausages (14g protein each, so 35g total there), two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: taco salad - ground beef, spices, spring greens mix, salsa, black olives, cheese, greek yogurt...plus some cocoa crack for dessert

\- slept pretty well last night, except that birds chirping this morning woke me up really early. I really ought to just get my ass to bed early at night and get up when the birds wake me up in the morning.

\- have been noticing a lot of dry mouth feeling lately. I usually drink 2L of water during my 8 hour work day, plus all the liquid I drink at home (coffee plus a few glasses of water most days). Apparently I need to drink even more?!

\- little bit of intestinal weirdness this morning. Hoping that if it's detox kicking in again, it's not as bad as last time.

\- I noticed last night on the way to bed that one of my toes had turned white, a couple hours after CT! Never had that problem before, but I guess it makes sense given that I recently switched from fish oil to krill oil and am getting significantly less O3. I upped the krill oil by a pill (300mg each) this morning. I need to invest in some higher-dosage ones. Part of why I was taking so much fish oil (1200mg a day) was because it was really helping with my depression / anxiety issues. Being on a lower dose even for the past few days, I'm starting to feel the difference and it's not good.

\- tomorrow will be day 56, which will be 8 weeks I've been doing the reset! I can honestly say that, while it's gotten easier to do than it was at the beginning, I'm really looking forward to eventually moving on. I feel like I'm kind of in a holding pattern lately and it's getting old.

### ealachan — 2012-04-25T19:14:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-18767>

I've gone without my normal antiperspirant / deodorant for about a week because I realized it's probably not very good for me, and had been gloating to myself about how I haven't stunk since giving it up, blah blah blah...well. I was just in the bathroom, reached up to mess with my hair, and the B.O. coming from my armpit damn near knocked me over. Where the hell did THAT come from?! It's not even been hot...I'm not sweating, but I sure as hell stink. I had to take some paper towels, douse them in warm water and antibacterial hand soap, and scrub my pits. LOL

This too shall pass, right? I hope? Please?

### ealachan — 2012-04-26T17:07:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-19024>

**Leptin Reset, Day 56**

_HAPPY 8 WEEKS TO ME_!

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, handful of shredded cheese, BP coffee

l: -

d: big steak, brussels sprouts roasted with bacon, 2tbsp cocoa crack stuff

\- Fell asleep fine last night, had to get up at midnight to go get hubs but was only up for about half an hour, went right back to sleep when I got home. Slept great until 6am when the dog started stomping all over me and the birds started chirping.

\- feeling kind of heartburny this morning, which is annoying. It may be from the cheese?

\- back to CT tonight, since I'll have the house to myself. I've been thinking about going over to the lake and wading around in it for CT, but then I'd have to do it before dinner (not having eaten since about 8am), and I don't know if that's a great idea.

### ealachan — 2012-04-26T17:18:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-19029>

Also, in the "New Things I've Tried In The Name of Paleo" files - I had Braunschweiger last night. It tasted like very salty, bacony hot dog...kind of. It wasn't bad at all, though I can see how having crackers or bread to spread it on would make it easier to eat. I'm pretty sure that the amount of salt and nitrites in it probably trump the nutritional value of the liver content...but it was an interesting experience none the less.

I bought a can of water-packed sardines as well. Still working up the ovarian fortitude to try those.

### quelsen — 2012-04-26T20:28:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-19095>

So we need a then and now point to point comparison for such a milestone

### ealachan — 2012-04-26T21:53:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-19121>

> **quelsen;19168 wrote:** So we need a then and now point to point comparison for such a milestone

I did take "before" pictures, but they're for my own personal edification and shan't be seeing the light of the interwebs...at least, not any time soon.

Here are my original measurements, taken on Day 7:

Weight: 375lbs (doctor's office scale a few days before)

Bust: didn't actually measure this

Ribs under bust: 48"

Belly above waist: 56"

Waist: 49.75" (I was up to 52" earlier in the winter)

Hips / spare tire: 65". Again...not as big as it used to be. Pop your eyes back in their sockets, kthanks.

Thighs: 34"

Calves: 22"

Ankles: 11.5"

Upper Arms: 22.5"

Neck: 15.75"

Here are today's measurements, as of 8:30 this morning:

Weight: 352 (home scale, post-BAB)

Bust: didn't actually measure this, but my bra cups have extra room in them, so I know it has gotten smaller

Ribs under bust: 46"

Belly above waist: 54" (was icing this 4-5 times a week up until two weeks ago when I started doing cold water instead)

Waist: 49"

Hips / spare tire: 64.75"

Thighs: 33"

Calves: 22"

Ankles: 11"

Upper Arms: 20.5"

Neck: 15.25"

So, as you can see, not HUGE drops anywhere (which is fine with me, slow and steady)...but there's lots of stuff I'm noticing that's not really measure-able (and these are all with no exercise outside of my normal mile or so of walking every day), ie:

\- my calves now have definition

\- my knees are COMPLETELY differently shaped now (which is such a weird thing to notice, but a fat girl clings to what she can...LOL)

\- thighs are firmer even though they haven't shrunk much, fat looks less cellulite-y on them

\- starting to get muscle definition on tops of upper arms, fat wings on bottoms of underarms don't go all the way into my armpits anymore (hard to describe, but trust me, they're different)

\- I have actual armPITS now...as in, concave areas under my arms. Used to be flat/ slightly protruding under there.

\- starting to see the first hints of collarbone and shoulder definition

\- Booty is still epic and round, but is starting to tighten up, fat looks smoother there, too

\- TMI: body hair (pits, pubes, legs) feels more silky now, where it used to be really scratchy / wirey

\- second chin is dissipating, more cheekbone and jaw definition starting to show up

There's also a lot of other stuff going on:

\- better / deeper sleep

\- weird random foot and leg pain I used to get almost every day (regardless of whether I'd walked or not) hasn't shown up in weeks

\- singing voice is better (I actually rolled my eyes when I was first reading MamaGrok's claim about this on MDA way back in the day (sorry, MG!), but it's totally legit. I don't have constant post-nasal issues anymore, so my throat actually works like it's supposed to again. Plus, I can breathe better again, which is important when you're singing!)

\- cold tolerance is much better. I do still get cold, but now it's like "meh...chilly." rather than "CHILLY! MUST PUT A SWEATER ON!"

\- get winded a little less easily

\- can eat a bite of chocolate or ice cream and then walk away. Could never, EVER do that before. Ice cream especially would send me into binge mode

\- joints don't seem to randomly hurt anymore

\- ADD-type tendancies (never diagnosed, but heavily suspected) have stabilized somewhat

\- blood pressure has been trending downward, as has pulse.

\- Aside from the detox weirdness from CT, I just generally feel...better.

So, yeah. There's that. I know I need to start doing some testing so I can quantify my internal changes better, but I have really \*\*\*\*ty insurance that basically covers no bloodwork, so I'm saving up / waiting for my FSA account to re-fund in June. I've always had admirable cholesterol numbers in the past (even at my heaviest), so I'm fairly interested to see how much that has changed. Also interested to see my thyroid numbers, as I know for a fact that my doctor has only ever had my TSH tested, and we know how useful THAT is.

### ealachan — 2012-04-27T02:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-19198>

20 min, 50 degree water (it was coming out of the tap at 47), legs only.

Skin was 85 degrees going in, 53 coming out. 10 minutes later, skin still feels very cold to the touch but I have no sensation of my legs being cold, if that makes sense.

Oral temp before I went in: 98.6

Oral temp after 12 minutes in water: 99.1

Oral temp at end of 20 min: 99.1

HOLY MOTHER OF SNOWBALLS! Coldest I've done so far. It felt like a million pins and needles stabbing my skin, especially my feet, the whole time. My skin turned lobster-red within 5 minutes. About 10 minutes post-soak, skin is mostly back to normal color even though it's still very cold to the touch.

Felt really tired getting out of the tub - like I'd just climbed a mountain and done a bazillion sit-ups.

### ealachan — 2012-04-27T17:11:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-19330>

**Leptin Reset, Day 57**

b: one burger (28g protein), two eggs (6 each = 12g protein), BP coffee with 2 T each CO and KG (to try and make up for the slight shortage of protein)

l: -

d: shrimp scampi made with CO and KG butter, broccoli

\- have been drinking a glass of water with 1-2tsp of apple cider vinegar in it before breakfast the past few days and it seems to help me not become belchy and too-full feeling after my BAB. I'm sure it's not as good as betaine or similar, but it's what I've got for the moment.

\- was in bed by 10:15 last night and probably asleep by 10:30. Was up for half an hour around 12:15 to 12:45, then zonked right back out. Dog started barking at stupid-o'clock (5ish?), so I slept kind of fitfully from then until about 7 when I finally got up. When I was allowed to sleep, I slept well!

\- made a scrub out of some sugar and some coconut oil and slathered myself with it in the shower this morning to try and slough off some dead skin / moisturize the rough, dry spots. It's really odd, because it worked great in places, but there are other patches that still feel really lizard-y even after the scrub and CO application. They happen to be the spots on the fronts of my ankles / shins that started to get really lizard-y feeling right after I started immersion CT. Maybe I'm just bound to have lizard-shins!

### ealachan — 2012-04-29T00:18:33+0300

Nothing I or anyone else does is good enough, I don't want anything to do with other people at all, and I think I seriously offended my husband by turning down his advances repeatedly last night and today. I haven't had much of a sex drive for a couple years now, but sometimes it gets to the point where the idea of having sex just...pisses me off, for lack of a better descriptor?! It's not my poor husband's fault, but it snowballs really quickly when I'm in a mood like this because he takes it REALLY personally, which pisses me off even more, and...yeah. Butt-hurt all around. ### ealachan — 2012-04-29T03:18:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-19706>

Holy suffering SeaMonkeys!! Fat girl not only got her fat butt INTO the tub tonight, but she GOT IT OUT. Awwww yeah. Victory points for me! For those who don't recall, the last time I tried sitting in the tub for CT, I had a really hard time getting out because, frankly, I'm too damn heavy to hoist myself back up without grab bars, pulleys, blocks and tackles, a team of draft horses and a suspension of the gravitational force itself. But tonight? TONIGHT...I just gave it the ol' heave-ho at the end of my 20 minutes in the water, and had WAY less struggle than last time. It's not like I weigh significantly less, so I'mma chalk that up to a strength gain and be overjoyed about it.

Anyway...the pertinents from tonight:

Water temp: 52 deg F Duration: 20 min, though it took me a while to actually ease down into the water, so I'll call it 15 minutes of actually sitting in the water fully.

Before CT: oral temp 97.7, skin temp 82

After CT: oral temp 99.0, skin temp 60 on lower legs, 70 on butt where I was touching the sides of the tub / didn't have good water circulation

Notes: Think my O6:O3 is still out of whack because my toe went white again. Just one toe, doesn't hurt, just turns waxy white. It looks pretty zombie-ish still, 15 min later. I did try to keep my toes out of the water once I got settled, but it didn't seem to matter. Tons of goosebumps while I was in the water, but no actual shivering while I was in. Slight amount of shivering started about 10 min post-CT, but not actually noticeable shaking...just muscles feeling tense. Felt like I'd climbed a mountain again (all in my legs) when I got out of the bath.

20 minutes post-bath, oral temp is 98.7...WHEEEEEEEEE!

### ealachan — 2012-04-29T03:20:24+0300

### ealachan — 2012-04-29T16:50:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-19771>

**Leptin Reset, Day 59**

B: three eggs, some kielbasa (didn't measure...it was a lot but it's pretty low-protein), BP coffee

l: broccoli soup, two pork ribs with too-sugary sauce

d: bangers and mash with onion gravy (that's the plan anyway)

\- felt really fidgety (and SO! ITCHY!) and like I couldn't get warm (even though I wasn't really shivering) about an hour after CT last night. My toe stayed white and slightly numb for a good hour or so, even after I put socks on. I upped my krill oil to 900mg to see if it helps with that. I had been taking 1200mg of fish oil before I switched to krill oil and never had any problems with toes going cold / numb!

\- slept like a rock from 10:30 to 12:15, went to get hubs from work, was zonked back out by about 1 AM since the dog was being kind of a little \*\*\*\*. Wicked vivid dreams again. Woke up around 6:45, got up and walked the dog in the sunshine (and cold!) for about 20 minutes, then came in and had breakfast.

### ealachan — 2012-04-30T17:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20068>

**Letpin Reset, Day 60**

B: three eggs, 7oz (give or take) ground beef / pork / veal mix, BP coffee

L: -

D: T-bone steak...YEE HAW!

\- Yesterday I made some cream of broccoli soup that ended up having WAY too much cream in it. I ate a cup of it and them dumped the rest of the pot because it wasn't fix-able. I also ate two leftover pork ribs with the home-made BBQ sauce I baked them in. They tasted great, but the sauce was just way too sweet for my system (molasses was the only sweetener I used, but there was a lot in there). About half an hour after eating it, I got all heart-poundy and shakey and I got a weird flush on my face - just the underside of my chin and a little bit on both cheeks along the side of my nose. WTF? It went away after about 20 minutes or so.

\- didn't get to sleep until around 11 last night, but slept like a rock until just before my alarm went off at 6:40. I love nights that I don't have to get up and go pick up my husband! Wish he worked more days...or would get his driver's license...LOL

\- BP was 134/82 this morning, but I was literally doing a bunch of stuff and then sat down and immediately took it, so it probably wouldn't have been as high if I'd sat there for a few minutes first. Either way, not horrible!

### ealachan — 2012-04-30T22:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20177>

I have T-bone steaks waiting for me at home for dinner. I am unreasonably excited by this. They're beautiful. I can't wait to sear them off, let them rest, and then DESTROY them. Well, I'll only destroy one, but still. Mmmmmm, meat. I think I'll quick-braise some kale to go with it. Mmmmm, kale with coconut oil and crispy bacon. ### ealachan — 2012-05-01T03:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20264>

I didn't want to CT tonight, but I sucked it up and did it. And you know what? It. BLEW. GOATS. It was COLD, I didn't get used to the water like I normally do, it made my toes ache like a mo-fo...it was just balls. But it's done with now, so that's a plus.

Another plus - my toes didn't turn white at all! I'm up to 900mg of krill oil, and I was getting white-toe problems @ 600mg, so now at least I have an idea of my lower threshold...or something? I don't even really know what that means, I guess. HAH!

Water temp: 48 degrees (damn you, cold nights lately!)

oral temp before: 97.7

skin temp before: 85

duration: 25 minutes (working my way up)

oral temp after: 99.3

skin temp after: 52

skin definitely got lobster-red!

### ealachan — 2012-05-01T17:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20413>

**Leptin Reset, Day 61**

B: burger (28g protein), 3 eggs scrambled with leftover kale, BP coffee

L: -

D: t-bone-gasm, v. 2.0! Now with added spinach, mushrooms and onions!

\- had some dark chocolate on the way to work...but to my credit, I stood there in front of a rack of freshly made donuts and muffins and just mentally said "meh...don't want" and walked away. Two months ago, I absolutely could NOT have done that. Victory point for me.

\- Was cold and slightly shivery for hours after CT last night. It was bad enough that it actually made my sleep pretty fitful, which was annoying. I almost got up and put a pair of socks on in the middle of the night, but I figured that would be counter-productive.

\- Shower this morning was about 65 degrees by the time I finished. I started at 72 and worked my way down as I went along.

\- According to my calendar, yesterday was 28 days from the end of my last period, so I'm expecting Shark Week pretty much any time now. Will be interesting to see if it gets whacked out from CT like some people have experienced.

\- waist was down to 48" this morning, but hips and belly were both up an inch...weird.

### ealachan — 2012-05-01T22:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20521>

My chin is all flushed again. It happened Sunday and yesterday right around the same time of day. On sunday, I had eaten a meal that included some pretty sweet sauce, so I was blaming it on that. Yesterday, it was warm in my office all afternoon, so I blamed it on THAT. Today, I've neither eaten anything nor is it overly warm in here...so WTF?! It's my chin and the skin directly under it (wattle? second chin?), and I'm willing to bet that it will eventually be along the sides of my nose a little bit as well.

For what it's worth, I do tend to flush when I get over-tired, but I don't think I've ever flushed in such specific spots for three days running like this. Very weird. Last thing I ate was that chocolate at around 9 this morning, but I didn't have that yesterday and got the same deal around the same time of day. All I've had since the chocolate has been water.

Waaaaaah!

### ealachan — 2012-05-02T02:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20560>

I had steak for supper again tonight. Even after all this time, I still feel bad / worried if I eat beef two meals in a row. Some habits die hard, I guess.

So, the steak I ate was .93 of a pound before I cooked it, including bones. That's 14.88 oz before cooking. I weighed the bones that were left after I inhaled the meat, and they were 1.8oz...so I effectively ate 13-ish oz of T-bone for supper. Plus 2 cups of spinach, about a cup of mushrooms, and probably half a cup of onion, all sauteed in coconut oil. Pretty sure I ate more than I actually needed to, but my god was it good.

Trying to decide if I want to do CT again tonight. Hmmmmm.

### ealachan — 2012-05-02T16:36:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20654>

**Leptin Reset, Day 62**

b: 4 oz burger, two eggs, sauteed spinach, mushroom and onion, BP coffee

L: -

D: ham and something. I baked an enormous ham last night, so I'm not going to be able to get away from it for a few days.

\- woke up all snotty and sneezy this morning...fun! Maybe because I've been sleeping with the window open and have been getting pollen'ed? I've never had super bad allergies, but I've definitely always had bouts of sneezy congestion every spring. Snuffling seems to have abated almost completely now - it was really only bad for about an hour after I got up.

\- ate WAAAAAAAAY too much last night. First time I've been uncomfortably full in ages, and I think I've learned my lesson. I was still picking at stuff as late as 8.30 last night and consequently had a really hard time falling asleep. I think I've identified what put me into munch-mode last night - I had a BIG glass of red wine with dinner. I tend to be a munchy drunk, and while the one big glass (probably 8oz, so two servings) certainly didn't make me DRUNK, I had a little buzz going. I think the wine makes my inhibitions drop just enough that I forget it's not ok to just keep eating. Either that, or aliens took over my brain. It's 50/50, really.

\- One relative down-side to losing weight, I noticed this morning, is that my varicose and spider veins are a lot more noticeable now...presumably because they're closer to the surface of my skin because I don't have as much retained fluid and/or inflammation between them and the skin now. Makes for some fairly grisly-looking thighs. :

\- speaking of veins, the big cluster of spider veins and general grisly weirdness that's been on the side of my right thigh for ages is weirdly achy today. It hasn't hurt in eons, so I dunno what's going on there. Hopefully it's healing, and not getting worse. I know that sitting all day long likely aggravates stuff like that, but I think probably standing all day would, too. Since I can't switch my work station back and forth between sitting and standing every 90 minutes all day long, I have to pick one. Sitting is currently it.

\- TMI WARNING: still no Shark Week arrival. Breasts have been tender for the last two days, but certainly nowhere near as sore as they used to get pre-Shark Week. I'm taking that as a good sign. Not really feeling like Bitchy McKillersons either...yet. In the past I've always had a very marked surge of hostile attitude the week before my period. If that's really gone away, it would likely be to the great relief of everyone around me.

\- Also, totally TMI: I had the most amazing poo this morning. It was nearly effortless, and when I stood up to survey my work, I was shocked to see just how enormous and perfectly formed it was. Veggies and probiotics FOR THE WIN!

### chocolate — 2012-05-02T16:43:57+0300

I used one of the myofascial release rollers and it made my veins not visible....I don't know what happened to them, but you can't see them. Maybe it was both. shrug...I never expected anything, it just happened, before CT even.

### ealachan — 2012-05-03T00:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20833>

Welllllll, I just blew $80 on supplements...good thing I had an Amazon gift card, holy hell!  I got Neuro-mag, DIM, and more krill oil, as those seem to be the most important things for me right now. I guess. I'm not entirely sure. It seems like I take SO much stuff already, but I think a lot of that is because I have low dosages of stuff like D3 and krill oil currently, so I have to take like 3-5 pills just of those things to get the dosage I want. Hoping that as I run out of stuff, I can phase up to higher dosages and hopefully not feel like such a pill-popping fiend in the mornings.

For the record, I'm currently taking:

Multivitamin

Antioxidant blend (NatureMade) x1

K2 (Naturemade) - 45mcg of K2 + 2000 IU of D3 in one capsule

D3 (Naturemade) - 2000 IU x 4 (which makes 10k IU total)

CoQ10 (Naturemade) - 200mg x1

Krill Oil (Naturemade) - 300mg x 3

probiotic (can't remember the brand) - 2 huge pills

my one actual prescription, which is the diuretic I take for my blood pressure - HCTZ 25mg

I also take 400mg of magnesium at night, but it's oxide (which I went out and bought because it was on the Jack Recommends page and then two days later he was posting telling someone to avoid oxide...LOL! At least it was cheap), so I'm getting the Neuro-mag to replace that.

Anybody see anything I could axe, right off the bat? I'm still trying to stay below 25g of carbs daily if at all possible, so it kinda limits the variety / amount of veg I can eat, which I feel like I need to make up for with supps...?

### ealachan — 2012-05-03T17:22:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-20987>

**Leptin Reset, Day 63**

B: three eggs scrambled in CO, a bunch of ham (had to have been at least 4oz, likely closer to 6oz), BP coffee

L: -

D: some kind of salad from the local Italian place, since it's card-geek night and we'll be out

\- Got to bed around 10:30 last night and was zonked before 11. Got to sleep all night with no interruptions...HUZZAH! Slept like a rock, too. Woke up literally two seconds before the alarm went off.

\- kicking myself for having bought that stupid ham. I should know better. It's SO salty, and I'm apparently really sodium-sensitive for some reason. I love ham, but it makes me feel like a friggin' toad, ugh. And of course I ate more of it for breakfast this morning. I think I might chop the rest up tonight and freeze it for future use, because I don't think I can eat any more of it for a while.

\- more weird random aches last night and this morning. Right leg is still intermittantly achey around where my clusters of messed-up veins and vessels are. Ahh, the joys of being a fat-ass.

\- Picked up some epsom salts and some aloe vera gel last night. I couldn't find any aloe vera gel that didn't have a bunch of other \*\*\*\* added to it, unfortunately...but I decided to give it a go on my lizard-skin none the less. It seemed to help make my skin feel smoother, but it's still pretty flakey. I also added some epsom salts to the tub this morning, filled the tub about halfway with warmish water, then stood in that while I took my normal cool shower. Not sure with what frequency I should be doing epsom salt soaks...anybody have any suggestions?

\- My nails are growing much faster than they used to. I'm a nail-biter, so I haven't really noticed that my nails have gotten any stronger...but they're certainly growing faster. I can't really judge if my hair is growing faster, as it grows ridiculously fast anyway.

\- I've noticed the past week or so that I seem to be working better mentally, which was a pleasant surprise. I seem to be having less trouble searching for words when I'm speaking or writing, and I seem to be able to self-edit more effectively (though you wouldn't likely know it from reading this journal...hah!).

Edited to add: I got SO friggin' warm about 20-30 minutes after eating, and it lasted about 45 minutes or so. ### ealachan — 2012-05-04T17:30:48+0300

**Leptin Reset, Day 40523094...I mean, 64**

b: two eggs, some ham, BP coffee

l: -

d: going out...not sure

\- Last night's dinner was a salad with some buffalo-style chicken on it. I also ate a small piece of bread, which was stupid...but \*\*\*\* happens and I'm not going to stress about it.

\- Spent all evening under nasty flourescent lighting...ugh. The only up-side is that it was effing COLD where we were, and I was just in a thin shirt and jeans, so I think I got some inadvertant CT in. I spent several hours on the verge of shivering. Finally dragged my butt up at 7:15 after mistakenly shutting the alarm clock completely off when it went off at 6:30...oops.

\- Butt issue from yesterday is gone, everyone will be happy to hear, I'm sure.

### ealachan — 2012-05-04T20:48:41+0300
