1:47 and I'm not at all hungry, so we're doing better than yesterday! I think I actually ate less protein for breakfast this morning than I did yesterday, but I'm pretty sure I had more CO and butter in my coffee today.

edited to add:

People, it's 4:18 and I'm still not hungry!! My stomach is usually talking to me (and everyone else in my office) by this time of day. What the...? I really didn't have anything different for breakfast this morning, and I certainly didn't have a LOT more CO or butter in my coffee.

WEIRD.

### ealachan — 2012-05-05T17:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-21612>

**Leptin Reset, Day 65**

b (9am): a couple ounces of kielbasa, an ounce or so of liverwurst, BP coffee. Really not hungry this morning.

l: probably something, since i didn't eat much breakfast

d: dunno yet

\- Went out last night for a birthday party. Veered off the rails, diet-wise. A couple sugary cocktails, a small wheat tortilla (part of the appetizer), a baked potato (not my worst transgression of the night by any means, but still), and...the real killer...a rather obscene amount of ice cream with hot fudge sauce. On the UPSIDE, though...I got my seafood servings for the week in easily, as I had an entree that consisted of haddock, scallops, shrimp and lobster in a cream sauce. YUM!

\- didn't finish dinner until nearly 8:30pm. Wondering if maybe that's why I'm not even remotely hungry this morning, even though I slept in until 8:30am (didn't get to sleep til like midnight and then had to get up at 4 to take hubs in to work. Got home, went back to bed, slept till 8:30).

\- Sitting here in a tank top in the living room with the windows wide open...it's like 60 degrees in here...perfectly comfortable. Seems so weird to be able to do that.

\- have been reading a bunch of stuff about iodine lately and beginning to wonder if supplementing that would help me, as I have a LOT of the deficiency symptoms....but with all the warnings I keep reading about horrible detox from iodine supplementation, I'm kind of gun-shy about it. I'm scared I might do myself more harm than good.

\- trying to motivate myself for a quick CT session before I have to go pick hubs up. I actually had a really vivid dream last night about CT'ing with Dr. K. It was very odd and it made me laugh out loud to recall it when I woke up this morning. Brains are weird.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-05T19:32:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-21634>

Just my 2cents and worth every penny  If you're using iodized salt you're getting all the iodine you need. From what I've read it's very easy to overdo it.

I was using sea salt for probably 10 years before I realized that it had NO iodine in it. I found one that HAS been iodized and have switched to that. I had started taking a kelp tab daily to help but quit when I switched salts.

On the wheat thing ... I promise that if you read Davis's "Wheat Belly" you'll never eat it again.

### ealachan — 2012-05-06T05:02:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-21725>

> **ColdBren;21773 wrote:** man thanks for your honesty.....I can't bring myself to admit things on my own journal....which is why its pretty blank because I mess up all the time....okay, I am going to do better...thanks!

Part of why I'm brutally honest on here is because if I put something in writing, I can't deny it. I've found over the years that it's super easy for me to just pretend stuff (mistakes, bad experiences, what have you) didn't happen. My brain seems to have an endless capacity for self-delusion. I have learned to side-step that rather unhelpful quirk by writing things down. If it's here in writing, I can't "forget" that it happened. It's right there staring me in the face the next time I go to post. I am forced to acknowledge it and either learn from it, or keep repeating it until I DO learn from it.

### ealachan — 2012-05-06T05:10:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-21726>

Lunch today was hotdogs (nitrate-free, at least!) and coleslaw (with home-made olive oil mayo...yay for being a kitchen geek). Dinner was ribs - I did a garlic / paprika / onion dry-rub and baked them for a few hours at 250. They came out good, but a little salty. I had a little sauce on mine, and a side-salad of spring mix dressed with red wine vinegar.

Gonna try not to sleep in so long tomorrow morning. Today I've felt like everything was on about a 3-hour delay - didn't get hungry til lunch time, didn't feel like doing much until late afternoon, not really tired now that it's bed time, etc. I don't want to get into that pattern on weekends. I'd rather try to stay up after I've taken my hubs to work at 4.30 am and just be ready to go to bed super early, I think.

Also, going to order some blue-blocker glasses for both me and him on Monday. He's on the laptop constantly when he's home and has trouble getting to sleep / sleeping enough. Hoping if I can get him to try the blue-blockers and they work, that he'll be willing to try some of the other crazy stuff I do, too...

\- picked up some 5000mg D3 supps that have resveretrol (sp?) added...200mg, I believe. Figured it certainly can't hurt, anyway.

### ealachan — 2012-05-07T17:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-21925>

**Leptin Reset, Day 67**

B: two sausages (30g protein total), 3 eggs (18g), BP coffee

L: -

D: sirloin tips with pan sauce made of ramps (wild leeks), mushroom, splash of red wine and butter, along with braised kale and some chocolate for dessert.

\- Well, the period that I've been waiting to start for a week finally kicked in yesterday. Minimum of cramping and related horridness, which is super as I normally spend the first day of my period popping Advil and sequestering myself so as not to kill anyone. I've also had a minimum of pre-menstrual heart palpitations this month, which is another big plus. My doctor just kind of pooh-pooh'ed me a year or so ago when I asked if the palpitations I was getting could be hormone-related since they definitely seem to flare during certain parts of my cycle.

\- Hung a towel up over the window last night because there was a bright light shining in our window from outside (which is not normally the case). Got to bed around 10:30, slept like a rock until the alarm went off at 6:40. I actually woke up about 10 minutes before the alarm, but I was comfortable and didn't want to get up. Feel pretty tired this morning even though I slept well last night, but that tends to be my M.O. during Shark Week anyway, so not gonna worry about it too much.

\- I've been avoiding CT lately. Need to suck it up and do it tonight. It's going to be warm here again today, so hopefully by 7pm I'll be ready for a cool dip anyway.

### ealachan — 2012-05-08T17:47:10+0300

b: 5.5 oz steak, two eggs, a cup or so of kale, one sausage, BP coffee

l: if I need lunch after all that, I'mma get checked for tape worms

d: burgers, green salad dressed with bacon, a little olive oil and vinegar, cauliflower mashed with butter and a little cheddar cheese

\- Ate a lot at breakfast, but didn't really feel overly stuffed at the end of it. When I can remember to drink a big glass of water with a TBSP of apple cider vinegar in it before eating breakfast, it really seems to help me not feel like a toad for hours afterward.

\- was asleep (or nearly asleep) by 10:15 last night. Had to get up at 12:15 and pick up hubs, but I can actually barely remember anything about having done so, so I'm guessing I didn't wake up too much. Certainly had no problem going right back to sleep when I got back. Didn't hear a thing until the alarm went off at 6:40 this morning. It's really dark and rainy, so there wasn't much light coming in the window and it doesn't surprise me that I didn't wake up on my own.

\- TMI - menstrual flow doesn't seem to be as heavy this month as I'm used to, and minimal cramping. So that's a plus.

**Leptin Reset, Day 69**

B: some steak (at least 3oz, probably 4+), some leftover burger (another 2-3oz), leftover cauliflower mash, two eggs

L: -

D: dunno yet

\- stayed up way too late last night. I got wrapped up in a program I was watching on TV, and the next thing I knew it was 11:15. Ugh. I was going to have to get up at midnight and go pick hubs up anyway, so I just didn't bother going to bed until I'd gone to pick him up. Had a bitch of a time waking up this morning, and still feel like I could curl up and go right back to sleep for hours.

\- feel like I've stalled. Scale hasn't moved in days, measurements are all holding steady, and I just feel like I'm not progressing anymore. I have a lot less patience for all the hoops I have to jump through to do the reset when I'm not seeing actual results. I'm pretty much the most impatient person in the world.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-09T18:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-22600>

I guess that makes me +3.

Did you get your blu blockers yet? I do not know how I lived without 'em.

I was lights out at 11:15 last night (reading a book by one of my fave authors ... it's a silly bit 'o fluff series about a witch ... and couldn't stop). I was afraid I wouldn't fall asleep right away. HAHAHAHA. Shut my eyes and bang I was gone.

I also realized somewhere in the last day or so that my body has a long road to go re:healing. I have seriously damaged myself over basically my entire life and I have to stop demanding that it fix itself overnight. This is a process that is going to take more time.

I suppose it would help if I starting doing cold baths. The shower is still as far as I've gotten. And forget the bag 'o ice ... haven't done it for 2 weeks.

So. Let's the 3 of us link arms and skip down this yellow brick road because we all know where we're headed. (humming a bit of "We're off to see the wizard."

Haven't gotten my orange goggles yet, no. Amazon is saying they should be here Monday. That's what I get for being a cheapskate and going for the free shipping, I guess. \*grumble\* I should be getting my Neuro-mag stuff on Monday, too. Have you started taking that yet? I know you said you'd ordered some...

I can't get into a cold shower. It just doesn't work. I normally only take tepid showers anyway, so I can get into my normal-temp shower and then slowly work the temp down as I go along, but trying to make myself step into an already-cold shower spray? No can do. Point being - if you can stand cold showers, I bet you could do a cold bath pretty easily! It only really sucks for the first few seconds when you first get in, but then it's fine...for me, at least. I kind of lower myself down into the water in stages, because I'm afraid if I get in all at once I'll give myself a heart attack...LOL! I sit on the edge of the tub with my lower legs in, then slowly lower my butt down into the water with knees still bent, then stretch out one leg so it's totally submerged, then do the other one. I swear a lot during the whole process, too. That definitely helps.  Usually I bring my iPod in with me and play Bejeweled while I'm in there, but last time I actually brought a book and the time went faster, once I was able to concentrate on the words.

### ealachan — 2012-05-10T01:43:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-22699>

Strangest dinner ever, tonight - tin of water-packed sardines, two slices of braunschweiger with some nice hot mustard, four squares of chocolate. Quick and oddly satisfying. The crunchiness of the sardines takes a little getting used to, but the taste is more tuna-like than anything, really. I can see how they'd be good with hot sauce. The next ones I eat, I might try with Sriracha. Sriracha is like bacon...it makes everything better!

Gonna let my dinner settle a little then do some CT. I have to be up at stupid-o-clock tomorrow and I need to get up in the middle of the night to pick hubs up, so it's early to bed for me.

### ealachan — 2012-05-10T03:28:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-22712>

CT: 25 minutes in 53-degree water. Skin was 82 deg going in, 60 coming out. Goosebumps the whole time, started to get shivery at about the 20 minute mark. Continually amazed at how relatively easy it now is for me to haul my fat ass up out of the tub. I know I haven't gotten significantly lighter in a while, so I must have gotten stronger. Without actually doing anything. COOL!

### ealachan — 2012-05-10T15:09:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-22801>

**Leptin Reset, Day 70**

B: (6:30am) two chicken sausages, two eggs, BP coffee

L: maybe tuna, depending on if/when I get hungry

D: dunno yet

\- Well apparently all I needed to do was complain about stalling out and mope for a day. I thought I looked different in the mirror this morning so I busted out the measuring tape, and lo! I'm down almost an inch on my waist and half an inch on my belly! Dunno if it's maybe just bloat from Shark Week evaporating or what...but I'll take it. Irritatingly, my hip measurement still will not budge. That's where I'm biggest - around my hips / below-waist spare tire. Really hoping that diligent CT will start to melt that, because as much as I (and my husband) actually like my big booty / wide hips, it seems to be the one thing that truly impedes me in terms of being able to do things I want. Currently I don't fit comfortably into most seats with attached arm-rests - ie: those at cinemas, theaters, ball parks, on airplanes, many restaurants, lawn furniture...you get the drift. Makes going out and doing fun stuff kind of...not fun. We only get one go-round here, and I'm sick of sitting at home thinking "gee, I wish we could go see that movie, but I don't fit in the seats" or similar. I don't ever need to be a size 6 to be happy (at nearly 6 feet tall, I don't think I could healthily be a size 6 anyway), I just want to be a reasonable size that allows me to do what I want to do and be relatively healthy.

Wow, rant-tastic. That's what happens when I get up way too early. Moving on.

\- Went to bed at 9:15 last night. Had a little trouble dropping off to sleep, but once I did I was fine until I got the hubs-pickup call. Zonked right back out after coming home from that, and only had to hit the snooze button once when the alarm went off at 6am. I'm a little worried that I'm going to get really hungry early this afternoon, since I ate breakfast about an hour and a half earlier than I normally do, but I have a packet of tuna if I really need it.

\- Chest has been sore the last few days. Not markedly, just annoyingly. I had a bad bout of costochondritis (swelling of the cartiledge in the sternum) about 6 years ago, and sometimes it seems to kind of resurface. The pain is nowhere near what it was when I first had it (it sent me to the ER, thinking I was having a heart attack at 25 years old), but it can get fairly annoying at times.

\- I want to source a few of those gel-packs for the freezer - the flexible ones. I want to start putting those on my neck / collarbone area while I'm watching TV or in the bath. I tried it with regular ice, but I can't get them to stay. I figure with the flexible ones, they should stay better because they'd bend to the shape of my neck where as the ice bag just falls off.

### ealachan — 2012-05-10T19:23:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-22908>

Yay, Neuro-mag is here! Amazon was telling me I wasn't going to get it til Monday next! I will say, the company it came from isn't very subtle with their labeling on the outside of the package. It says "NEURO MAG CAP" in huge letters across the bottom of the shipping label. If it was coming to my home, it wouldn't matter...but I had it shipped to me at work and there's a SUPER nosey woman who sorts the mail. She kept quizzing me about what it was and why it said "neuro" on it. She was like "DOESN'T NEURO MEAN BRAIN?!?!" I finally said "Yep, you figured me out. I'm taking supplements to help develop my super powers." That got me a total deer-in-the-headlights look, which I enjoyed heartily as I walked away.

### ealachan — 2012-05-10T20:17:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-22935>

Just to torture myself a little...

> 5\. Signs that you are becoming Leptin Sensitive (LS) again
>
>
>
> A. Men will notice quick weight loss.
>
>
> Not applicable.
>
>
>
>
> > B. Women will notice mood changes first (calmer/sleepy) and their sleep will improve. Their clothes will fit differently but weight may not change drastically initially because of effects on the pituitary. This will change too if they continue moving forward
> >
> >
> > Calmer, yes. Sleepier, yes. Sleep improved? yes. Clothes fit differently? yes.
> >
> >
> >
> >
> > > C. You will notice a change in your sweating pattern.
> > >
> > >
> > > Not sure how to judge this, since I haven't really had cause to sweat, given that I'm not supposed to exercise and it hasn't been that hot here yet. Does he mean we'll start to sweat in different places, or at different times, or...?
> > >
> > >
> > >
> > > D. You will notice you have better recovery from exercise and your energy levels seem to have risen.
> >
> >
> >
> > I DO notice that I don't get winded as easily as I did 6-8 weeks ago, and my heart rate seems to go back to normal more quickly after I've been for a walk or climbed steep stairs. Does that count?
> >
> >
> >
> > E. Your hunger is gone and so are your cravings.
>
>
>
> I only get actually, physically hungry once a day: in the late afternoon once my BAB has worn off. It's generally 6-7 hours after my BAB that I start to get hungry. Cravings-wise, I certainly don't crave carbs anymore, and I can pick up some of my old junk-food favorites, think about how they'd taste, and then put them right back down again and walk away. This is magical to me, still. I used to be a donut-a-day girl, and often far worse than that. I would go into the store just on auto-pilot and buy a donut or a pastry just because it was there. Often I didn't even taste it as I was eating it, but I was compelled to have it none the less. Being free of that is pretty awesome.
>
>
>
> F. When you awaken you will feel very refreshed like you slept well.

I've gotten so that I usually wake up right before my alarm goes off, and I certainly have an easier time getting up than I used to even a few weeks ago...but I wouldn't say I feel "very refreshed" when I wake up. I don't think I've EVER felt "very refreshed" upon waking, but then again i've always been fat and unhealthy, soooo...yeah.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-11T19:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-23211>

The powder is real easy. I put a scoop of it in a glass, add about 1/2 cup HOT water (usually left in the tea kettle from making my Americano). then I add in a tsp of MSM powder, stir really well and add about another 1/2 cup of cold water. I sip it as I eat breakfast. Flavor isn't too bad. They added a bit of citric to it. I'm taking so darned many supplements now I just didn't want to add another freaking pill.

### ealachan — 2012-05-14T17:09:29+0300

**Leptin Reset, Day 73**

b: omlette with broccoli and swiss cheese, two sausage patties, a few pieces of grilled home-fries, regular coffee (I was out for brunch with my mom and nana)

l: couple bites of kielbasa while I was cooking it for hubs, piece of liverwurst

d: taco salad - lettuce, spiced ground turkey, salsa, a little cheese, a little greek yogurt

\- Went out to brunch with the fam for Mother's Day. My meal came with toast on the plate even though I specifically said I didn't want it. In the past, if that had happened I'd have eaten at least one piece of it anyway. I didn't even touch it, other than to move it off my plate yesterday. So proud of myself! It sounds trivial, but it's definitely a big deal to me.

### ealachan — 2012-05-14T17:15:30+0300

I couldn't get comfortable, felt like I was fighting the husband and the dog for blankets all night, and just generally didn't sleep well. > Maybe the gas is from the addition of dairy???

The annoying thing is, it's not like any gas is actually coming OUT. It just feels like a big gas bubble that moves around in there but won't come out. In other words, if I would just fart and get it over with, life would be grand...but it ain't happenin'. I had something similar a month or so ago and it literally lasted a friggin' WEEK. I had alternating constipation and diahrea to go along with it, just for funsies. Last time I even went to the doctor to rule out stuff like appendix and ovary issues, because it got so it really hurt. Doctor deemed it general residual gut weirdness left over from the stomach flu I'd had a couple weeks before. I started taking probiotics at that point and everything got a lot better...until last night. I'm going to go back and look, but I don't think I'd had a ton of dairy last time when this kicked in. I do remember it being right after my period ended last time, too. I wonder if it's got something to do with hormone fluctuation? I've definitely been subject to bouts of pre-period runs in the past, but constipation / weird gassiness has never been a recurring issue. Interesting. Must investigate further, Watson!

### ealachan — 2012-05-14T20:45:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-23951>

Ok, so oddly enough, it's been almost a month to the day since I had the last bout of bad GI stuff. That hit on 4/15. I started feeling icky yesterday evening which was 5/13. Also, both times it's happened, it has started on Sunday evenings. WEIRD. Last time I ended up chalking it up to detox, but I haven't done CT since Wednesday or Thursday of last week this time. I really am starting to think it's either some kind of hormonal fluctuation, or it's just straight-up something I'm eating over the weekend that isn't agreeing with me. I can't see any major dairy episodes in my journal last month like I had this month. So odd.

Also, last time it started 13 days after the end of my period, BUT I had a cycle that was 10 days longer than my normal (which I blamed on CT). This time, it started 3 days after the end of my period.

Hmmmmmm.

L: -

\- gut issue seems to be starting to ease off today, knock on wood. Pretty sick of feeling like I got kicked in the lower abdomen.

\- slept like a rock from about 9:30 to 12:15, then wore my orange glasses out on the drive to go pick up hubs, got home and zonked right back out again within 5 minutes of laying down. Slept right straight through until 6 this morning. Woke up, went to the bathroom, decided I didn't want to be up that early, went back to bed, fell asleep promptly again, and then struggled to wake up when the alarm went off at 7. How weird. I was totally awake at 6. Should have just stayed up.

\- picked up my new car today, so THAT stressor is finally over and done with. Been agonizing about car crap for like 3 months now. I was a little sad to see the old car go because I get attached to things, but the new car has a lot better gas milage and is brand new!

\- I don't think it was the DIM giving me a headache over the weekend. I skipped it for a day and have taken it the last couple days and haven't had the same headache, so I think it was just pollen or something. I seem to be having WAY less of a reaction to the pollen this year than I have in previous years, but I do still have a little bit of sinus pressure and a little post-nasal drip.

Yeah, I've only been married two and a half years, so the "wait til it's his idea" concept is just starting to dawn on me. Problem is, I'm impatient. If I wait til something is his idea, I might be old and grey by the time stuff starts to happen...LOL He tried the glasses last night when he got home from work. I haven't had a chance to quiz him on if he slept better or not.

re: triceps - I just noticed last night that I actually HAVE triceps. I was reaching up to scratch my arm, and there are definitely muscles there that I've certainly never felt before, even when I was "skinny" (read: 300lbs). I definitely still have major hanging fat on my upper arms, but it looks a lot different now, which is weirdly exciting. I feel like a mad scientist in front of the mirror, poking and prodding my fat, comparing it to how it used to look, saying things like "yes, yessss...it's WORKING, Precious!"

Because, you know. mad scientists always talk like Gollum. Trufax.

Weight is trending downward again. I seem to go in fits and starts. Lose some, stay the same for a while, lose some more, stay the same again. I'm curious to see if backing off on my dinners will make me lose faster, or make me stall out.

### ealachan — 2012-05-17T19:07:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-24941>

**Leptin Reset, Day 77**

_Holy \*\*\*\*, happy 11 weeks to me!_

B: two chicken sausages (14g protein each), three eggs scrambled, coffee with JUST butter because I ran out of coconut oil, damn it!

L: -

D: going out, so not sure. It will likely involve at least one margarita, though.

\- Ok, convinced of the mini-miracle of the blue-blocking glasses after just a couple nights using them. I shut off all the lights at 8:30 last night, put on the glasses and turned on a documentary about China (I'm a geek. I love documentaries and nature programs), and I was feeling actually drowsy by about 9pm. I stayed up and finished the hour-long show, but then I went to bed and slept like a rock. I woke up at 12:20, right before hubs called for his pick-up. Wore the glasses on the drive out to get him and back, and then went right back to bed. I had a little trouble getting back to sleep but once I did, I was OUT until 6:30 this morning. Alarm doesn't go off til 7, mind you. Got up, walked the dog (normally I would have waited until my husband got up and let HIM walk the dog), got a little sunshine, came in and made breakfast. So chipper it was DISGUSTING, I tell you.

\- gut thing has eased off a lot so far today, which is good. My ACV in a glass of water trick before breakfast is still helping to keep me from getting heartburn / ucky full feeling after breakfast as well, so that's a plus.

\- out of coconut oil...SAD PANDA! I thought I had another jar, but I don't, and the nearest place that carries it is like half an hour away, so I didn't have time to make it up there tonight. Maybe this evening before we go out for dinner. If not, I'll HAVE to go tomorrow night, because I'm going to be cleaning someone's house all day Saturday (ugh).

\- co-workers are currently all chowing down on pizza and it's all I can smell, but I am resisting. Y'all should be proud of me. Pizza has always been pretty much my favorite S.A.D. junkfood besides donuts. I have harnessed the pizza chi and I am not running into the other room to stuff my face with it. HOLLAH!

\- weight was down to around 346 today. I'm not going to get too excited about that until a) I've gotten that reading (or lower) for a couple weeks in a row, and b) this gut thing is over and done with. It was nice to see none the less, though!

\- also, as mentioned in the post above, I haz triceps. WTF? Never been able to feel the actual muscles before, but I can now!

Woohoo on the 11 weeks plus the tricepts!!! I had triceps once in my life, when I was a cocktail waitress at a very busy sports bar that used glass beer mugs. Lots of sore arms til I worked up to that. Bet it's cause of the CTing, which is awesome!

You definitely help me to keep going bc although I'm already seeing progress, resisting pizza at work and getting tricepts is awesome stuff.

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

### ealachan — 2012-05-17T20:36:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-24959>

> **skywaykate;25033 wrote:** Woohoo on the 11 weeks plus the tricepts!!! I had triceps once in my life, when I was a cocktail waitress at a very busy sports bar that used glass beer mugs. Lots of sore arms til I worked up to that. Bet it's cause of the CTing, which is awesome!
>
>
>
> You definitely help me to keep going bc although I'm already seeing progress, resisting pizza at work and getting tricepts is awesome stuff.
>
>
>
> sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

I have to admit, the ability to just say "that's ok, I don't want any pizza (or fill in the blank)" is a far bigger deal to me than any physical attribute I might gain or lose from all this. I feel like I'm actually in control of myself, for once. I was a fat kid, a fat teenager, and of course a fat adult. I can remember even as a kid, eating and feeling like I _couldn't_ stop - just one more bite, just one more bite. Eating until I felt ill, even, and totally not understanding why. As a teenager and young adult, I started to get into the blaming myself cycle. I'd eat stuff I knew I shouldn't, then beat myself up over the fact that I ate bad stuff, then I'd be upset and I'd eat again to comfort myself. When I realized I was doing it, it was super frustrating...and when I did it WITHOUT realizing it until way after the fact, it was downright scary. I started blaming my eating habits on my depression and anxiety...but now I realize that my depression and anxiety, at least to a large degree, was actually being CAUSED by what I was eating, which was then making me eat even more of it! HOLY CIRCLE OF DOOM, BATMAN!

So, yeah. Even if I never drop 150lbs and can't go waltzing into any store in the mall and not have to shop in the "plus-sized" section, I'm still eternaly grateful that I've been able to gain way more control over what I eat and why!

Not really, no. I mean, not in the sense that I sit here going "OMG, I need something sweet!" like I used to sometimes do. I haven't craves anything lately, really. The last real pronounced craving I had was a few weeks ago, for shrimp. It was like ALL I could think about one afternoon, which was really odd. In fact, I actually kind of find cooking more difficult now than I used to, because I used to sit there at work thinking about food all day and end up planning out a whole meal in my head by the end of the day, so I'd know exactly what I was going to make. Now I don't seem to have that kind of attachment / preoccupation with food anymore, so I tend to come home at night, open the fridge, and not know what the hell to cook for dinner. Thankfully, my husband hasn't gotten sick of steak yet...

re: margaritas - I should have known better. The tequila and the lime juice themselves aren't too bad, but the sour mix and / or triple sec are super sugary. I DID know better, but I just sort of switched off the voice of reason for a while there. Hah!

### ealachan — 2012-05-20T00:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-25583>

**Leptin Reset, Day 79**

b: turkey sausage, two eggs, BP coffee with CO but no butter (out of THAT, now. Can't win...LOL)

L: salad of romaine lettuce, chicken, parmesan, caeser dressing

d: steak, probably

\- ate lunch today because I'd been busting ass since 9am and I was hungry by noon (even though I had plenty of fat and protein for breakfast, so I'm guessing I just burnt through all my fuel and needed more).

\- ended up cleaning for about 5 hours total. It was pretty hot and stuffy in the house I was working in, but I really didn't sweat much...only my armpits and my scalp / back of my neck. Last summer I remember being soaked with sweat after a few hours doing the same work in similar weather...so that's interesting.

\- starting to wonder if the cramping I'm having might be a UTI. I get crampy-feeling way down low in my pelvis (lower than menstrual cramps even, really), and it seems to ease off considerably after I've peed. No burning, no weird colored urine, no kidney pain or flank pain...do UTI's ever feel like this? I can't remember ever having had one, so I can't really say for sure. I think this is different from the gut issue I was having earlier in the week, for what it's worth. Still no clue WTF that is, and hoping it doesn't come back again.

\- really tired. It's been a long day and I didn't get a ton of sleep last night. Would like to curl up and nap, but I have to make some dinner and then get ready and go to a party, at least for a little bit. Blah.

### ealachan — 2012-05-20T19:28:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-25745>

**Leptin Reset, Day 80**

B: two eggs, five strips of bacon, coffee w/ coconut oil

l: -

d: steak and salad, probably

\- ended up eating a sandwich minus most of the bread for dinner last night. Went to a party, had two jello-shots and three beers. Got in at 11:30pm, had to get up at 4am to take hubs to work. Went back to bed til about 8:30 this morning. Woke up feeling almost like I had a cold, but then it cleared up pretty quickly. We were outside for the party, so thinking the pollen in combination with being around a lot of smoking people probably didn't help. Plus, you know, beer and jello shots. Heh. The upside is that I don't plan on going to that kind of a party again for the forseeable future. This was someone's 30th birthday and it really was a last hurrah party-wise for a lot of us.

\- pretty sore from all the heavy cleaning I did yesterday. Noticed something interesting, though - I used to get just generally sore, like my back or arms or legs...but now I can feel the actual muscles in my back that are sore, if that makes sense. It definitely feels different than it used to.

\- kind of an emotional mess today, for no good reason that I can come up with. I certainly haven't ever had this kind of reaction to having had alcohol before, so I don't think it's that. Today is the anniversary of my grandfather dying, but that was back in 1991, so it's not like it's a fresh wound. There's some other stuff going on, but again, it's really nothing new...so I don't know. I just hope I've gotten the crying jags out of my system this morning because I don't want to be a mess when my husband comes home from work.

\- gotta get up and get some stuff done. It's supposed to be 90 degrees this afternoon...yuck. I wish I lived somewhere where it topped out at about 75 in the summer time. I hate being hot. Always have.

### ealachan — 2012-05-21T17:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-25957>

**Leptin Reset, Day 81**

b: two eggs, steak, red onion, grape tomatoes, cheese, coffee w/ CO.

l: -

d: dunno yet

\- ended up making a really good steak salad last night for dinner - spring mix tossed in a horseradish-mustard vinaigrette (home-made of course) and topped with slices of seared NY Strip steak, grape tomatoes, red onions, gorgonzola cheese and some crumbled bacon just to gild the lily.  I was full like 3/4 of the way through my plate but I ate it all anyway...oops. We had the leftover steak (it was a pack of 3) for breakfast. Yum. Also had two beers. Fun fact - beer now gives me killer flatulence. Never used to have a problem with it before, but now...watch out. Good thing I don't drink it much anymore.

\- gut issue and cramping appear to be cleared up again. No friggin' clue what's going on there. Hoping it doesn't come back, though.

\- still a little sore from epic house-cleaning job on Saturday, mostly in my traps and obliques. I was doing a lot of reaching up over my head and twisting to reach stuff, wash windows, etc...so I guess it makes sense.

\- didn't get to bed til 11 last night, but slept like a rock until 4:30 when the birds woke me up. Got back to sleep after about 20 minutes and then slept until after 7. I need to just learn to stay up when I wake up early, because when I go back to sleep, I wake up later feeling like crap. It's annoying.

\- It was HOT yesterday, but it's supposed to be mid-70's and sunny all week while I'm stuck inside at work, of course. Hmph.

\- need to think about CT'ing again, now that the gut thing has resolved. Have been thinking about going over to the lake to try it out, but I need to find my bathing suit. My parents have their pool uncovered now as well, but I don't think it's been cleaned at all. Maybe if I volunteer to clean it, they'll let me get in it while it's still cold.

### ealachan — 2012-05-22T17:49:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-26340>

**Leptin Reset, Day 82**

B: two scrambled eggs, 1.5 italian sausages (crumbled), leftover zucchini and red pepper from dinner, BP coffee with CO, butter, and cinnamon

L: -

D: dunno yet

\- Dinner last night was kielbasa, sauteed red peppers, onions and zucchini, and a side-salad of spring mix, grape tomatoes and gorgonzola cheese with a vinaigrette. Quick, easy, and nummy, and the leftover veggies made for good breakfast filler this morning.

\- got to bed a little late, but slept well until the dog started being a pissant at about 4:30. Managed to fight him off and sleep fitfully until about 7.

\- been getting really tired legs lately, which seems weird because one of the first things I noticed when I started doing the Reset was that my legs didn't feel like lead anymore. Wonder why it has come back the last couple weeks?

\- still waking up every morning lately with mildly sore throat and semi-stuffed-up nose. Like I said before, it's way less trouble than my allergies USED to give me, but it's still kind of annoying.

\- have to get up at midnight to pick up hubs for the next four nights in a row. Yaaaaaaay (sarcasm).

### ealachan — 2012-05-23T17:56:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-26684>

**Leptin Reset, Day 83**

b: two eggs, 1.5 italian sausages, BP coffee

l: -

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was salad tossed in vinaigrette and topped with chicken, bacon, avocado, tomatoes and the last of the gorgonzola cheese. I picked at the bacon and the chicken the whole time I was cooking, so I wasn't even that hungry by the time the salad was made, but of course I ate it anyway and then felt like a toad. No one is even home to roll their eyes at me if I don't eat a "proper" dinner, so I don't know why I feel like I need to force myself to do it.

\- heartburn this morning, bad. Not sure if maybe the cheese is starting to catch up with me? I love cheese...why must it hate me in return?! Wondering if NAC and MSM would help, especially given that I don't have a gallbladder...but I'm REALLY loathe to buy and take more supplements at this point. Ugh.

\- painful gas / bowel episode this morning. Felt just like all the issues I had last week, except with no lower-left cramping. I just want to have normal guts again. The irony of changing my diet all around to help "heal" my gut and then having nothing but eff'ed up gastric issues for weeks and weeks is not lost on me, trust me.

\- didn't get to sleep til about 1am, and was up at 6:30. It's gonna be a long day.

I do eat right around 50g of protein every morning. For example, today's breakfast was two chicken sausages @ 14g each = 28g, plus 3 eggs @ 7g each, makes 49g. Days I skimp, I definitely feel it about 3pm, so I've mostly learned not to do that.

Haha, I assure you, I'm not your co-worker! That's an old picture from last summer, after a haircut I liked particularly well. Haven't had a haircut SINCE then, in fact...hah! My hair is long enough to touch my shoulders again, so it needs to get chopped off for the summer.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-24T22:10:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-27154>

> **justjoan060;27201 wrote:** Do you know why it's taking you that long to become LS?

I've been listening to Jack's interview on the JoAnne Unleashed podcast via Jack's media page, and in that he talks about that if you've got a lot of inflammation, it'll take longer. 6-8 weeks is for the average person. Doesn't necessarily have to do with being fat, but that's likely a big contributor. It almost seemed to me that he hinted that a low CRP test would indicate LS but it's not an exact correlations. Once inflammation goes down, you'll be LS.

### ealachan — 2012-05-24T22:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-27156>

Once inflammation goes down, you'll be LS.

I can tell that my inflammation has gone down a lot already - my skin is way better, I sleep much better, my joints don't bother me anymore, etc...but yeah. I figure because I've been so fat (and therefore inflamed) for so long, that it will take a while. Once my FSA account re-fills with money at the end of June, I'll have an HS-CRP and some other blood tests down. For now, I just have to assume I'm still broken I guess.

### ealachan — 2012-05-25T17:24:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-27340>

**Leptin Reset, Day Eleventy Billion (85)**

b: three eggs, kielbasa (that's what happens when I let hubs go to the store for breakfast stuff), BP coffee

l: -

d: dunno yet

\- Well. I had a thought last night (several, actually...but only one is applicable here). You know how I keep moaning about these weird gas-like cramps? I realized last night that it's really only in the mornings that I have them. Hmmmm. What do I do at night that could cause cramps in the early morning? I narrowed it down to things: I eat dinner, and I take my NeuroMag. Now, I'm not eating anything different for dinner than I've been eating right along for months and months...but the NeuroMag is relatively new. I decided to skip it last night - I took mag oxide instead (because I had it sitting around) and did an Epsom Salt soak for good measure. This morning? No weird cramps. I'm going to continue to lay off the NeuroMag for a few more days and see how I feel. If the constipation resolves itself too, I'll have my answer.

\- Had chicken for dinner again last night. It's finally gone now. Hoping to cook up some shrimp or maybe some mussels tonight, depending on how hot it is. We live in an upstairs apartment with no A/C (currently, anyway), so when it gets really hot my interest in cooking declines sharply.

\- TMI AHEAD: sex drive seems to be slowly creeping back? YIPPEE!

\- hoping to start swimming in the lake after work soon. I have kind of a lake-pond phobia because I panic when I can't see the bottom or see what's around my legs, but I'm going to try to fight it and see how it goes.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-26T04:04:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-27539>

> **skywaykate;27252 wrote:** I've been listening to Jack's interview on the JoAnne Unleashed podcast via Jack's media page, and in that he talks about that if you've got a lot of inflammation, it'll take longer. 6-8 weeks is for the average person. Doesn't necessarily have to do with being fat, but that's likely a big contributor. It almost seemed to me that he hinted that a low CRP test would indicate LS but it's not an exact correlations. Once inflammation goes down, you'll be LS.

I would have to say not necessarily on this based on N-1, just got my HR-CRP back, I was rockin a .9! Woot. However, I'm pretty sure I'm not LS. I still fight the urge to snack, and when I DO snack or don't have my BAB, my eating gets out of control quick.

I can keep it underwraps, but only by doing the reset plan with keto.

I think the sheer number of fat cells you have in your body are material to your ability to be LS, I think as long as you are significantly overweight there are not just leptin signals at issue, but hormonal ones as well.

I've come to believe that there is an added layer of factors to take into account and deal with for fat women that men just don't have. Also age is a factor. Lastly, it's a different scenario on what needs "repair" in your body if you have been obese since childhood vs gained weight after puberty.

That's my $.02. As much as I would like to get away from the BAB, it's better than being "starving" all the time while consuming massive amounts of bad foods.

### ealachan — 2012-05-30T16:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-28347>

**Leptin Reset, DAY 90**

90 days and no end in sight...

B: turkey burger, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: dunno yet.

\- last night's dinner was "meatza" with mushroom, onion, yellow pepper, pepperoni, olives and a little cheese on top. It was...ok, but I don't think I'll bother making it again. First of all, all the grease that accumulated in the pan when I was baking the "crust" kind of grossed me out, and secondly, it's just...not pizza. I will accept novelty substitutions of many things (I'm looking at YOU, spaghetti squash), but pizza is apparently not one of them.

\- anti-virals have thus far been uneventful. Hopefully they'll stay that way. Shingles isn't any worse this morning and the pain isn't keeping me up at night, so that's a plus.

\- took 12,000 IU of D3 this morning, 1000 mg (plus whatever is in my multivitamin) of C, and 10,000 IU (plus whatever's in the multi) of A. I'm going to take another 1000mg of C when I get home tonight as well, and work my way up to bowel tolerance.

\- forgot to wear my blue-blockers last night, but was in bed by 10pm and slept like a rock none the less. I've been splitting my NeuroMag dose lately - two in the morning, and then one at night combined with some standard mag oxide. It seems to have remedied my intermittant constipation issues, which improves my mood somewhat, certainly!

\- Forgot to mention - yesterday at the doctor's, I was 4lbs lighter than the last time I got weighed there. It's still 10-15lbs more than what my home scale is telling me, but either way it's going down and that's good!

### ealachan — 2012-05-31T17:40:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-28603>

**Leptin Reset, Day 91**

B: omlette made of: 3 eggs, sauteed mushrooms, yellow pepper, onion, a little shredded cheese, 5 slices of smoked ham

L: -

D: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was less than optimal - I was quite hungry on the way home at 5:30, but I needed to run some errands that couldn't wait, so I stopped at the store and grabbed a couple hard-boiled eggs and ate those on the go. When I got done errands, it was almost 7pm and I didn't want to get into cooking something and end up not eating til 8, so I grabbed some roast beef from the deli and munched on that. I ended up eating half a pound of it...oops. Then I ate some (ok, a LOT of) ice cream. Not so smart, but surprisingly it didn't give me heartburn. It DID give me a fairly urgent case of the runs, though. I think it was from the sugar, as I don't have a history of lactose intolerance.

\- was in bed by 10 and zonked out by 10:30 last night. Slept like a rock, even though I had to get up at midnight to go get Hubs. I was back asleep within minutes of hitting the pillow when I got back. Woke up around 6:30 before my alarm went off. The dog decided that since I was awake it was time to get up, so I took him out for a walk (usually hubs walks him in the morning). It was nice, because the mist was rising up off the fields and the sun was really bright, but it wasn't hot yet. If only I could get my ass out of bed in the mornings, I could really get used to that.

\- got my Betaine HCl and NAC in the mail yesterday. Figured I'd give myself a week or so to get used to the Betaine, then start on the NAC. First dose of Betaine this morning was uneventful. I did notice that I didn't seem to feel quite so stuffed and burpy after breakfast, but that could be because I didn't have as much protein as usual. Speaking of which, I don't know how long the breakfast I had this morning will sustain me. Will be interesting to see how it goes.

\- shingles still hurt, though it seems to be just a skin-hurt now, not the deep ache that it was before. They're not weepy, so that's a definite plus. The idea of weeping sores on my scalp really makes me gag....yuck. Do not want.

### ealachan — 2012-05-31T20:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-28680>

Just took a walk (about a mile). My fingers started to swell about halfway through the walk and are still somewhat swollen after the fact. I've always gotten this when walking for exercise. It eventually goes away, and it's just my fingers, not even my whole hands. No pitting...my fingers just feel tight and weird for a while and my rings are hard to get off, then it goes away.

Anybody know why that happens and/or what I can do about it? The last doctor I asked about it told me that it was because I was eating too much salt even though I was, at that point, on a strict 1500mg a day sodium limit.

### ealachan — 2012-05-31T21:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-28703>

Emergency packet of tuna has been cracked, as of 2:08pm EST. This morning's omlette officially did not have enough protein to keep me going. I pretty much knew that was going to happen. Just thankful I was prepared!

### Lanie — 2012-05-31T21:25:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-28710>

> **ealachan;28779 wrote:** Just took a walk (about a mile). My fingers started to swell about halfway through the walk and are still somewhat swollen after the fact. I've always gotten this when walking for exercise. It eventually goes away, and it's just my fingers, not even my whole hands. No pitting...my fingers just feel tight and weird for a while and my rings are hard to get off, then it goes away.
>
>
>
> Anybody know why that happens and/or what I can do about it?

I think this is caused by centrifugal force - when you swing your arms your blood pools in your fingers. I used to hate this too - now I'm usually pushing a stroller instead of swinging my arms so it doesnt happen anymore. Try holding something while you walk, light light weights or something...or keep your hands in your pockets...?

### ealachan — 2012-05-31T22:24:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-28725>

huh...I never even considered that. That's interesting. I don't swing my arms wildly, but I do definitely move them. I can't walk with my hands in my pockets for very far...it just doesn't feel right.

### skline@peak.org — 2012-06-01T21:45:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-28924>

> **chocolate;28996 wrote:** I think it takes two weeks to get over "bad" food. I hope the shingles go away quickly.
>
> I had cheese yesterday after requesting none on a lettuce wrapped burger. I didn't ditch it because I wanted to sleep and didn't pick it off because I didn't want scunge under my nails.... my face is puffy. I think I'm just going to keep putting coconut fat on tea and not eat until it goes away.

If you want the shingles to depart as fast as possible, take lysine on an empty stomach. It really works.

Your food transgressions that one day seem innocuous enough, except the rhubarb. It's notoriously high in oxalates. I wonder if that might have given you some problems -- some people are very bad at handling oxalates. Other main offenders: spinach, beets, chard, nuts, chocolate. Tea, carrots, potatoes, and sweet potatoes are also high. It can be bad enough that people who start having a daily "healthy spinach salad" have given themselves kidney stones within a year or two.

It's either a problem or it's no problem ... YMMV.

### ealachan — 2012-06-01T22:09:15+0300

I wonder if that might have given you some problems -- some people are very bad at handling oxalates. Other main offenders: spinach, beets, chard, nuts, chocolate. Tea, carrots, potatoes, and sweet potatoes are also high. It can be bad enough that people who start having a daily "healthy spinach salad" have given themselves kidney stones within a year or two.
>
>
>
> It's either a problem or it's no problem ... YMMV.

Yep, someone else mentioned lysine earlier, too. I'll look for some this weekend.

Oxalates have never given me grief in the past. I don't think the shingles was food-related, frankly. I think it was likely my immune system being in the toilet from a week of nowhere near enough sleep. I was thinking about the possibility of it being a detox effect too, but apparently the VZ virus doesn't live in fat, it lives in the ganglia even after it's gone dormant. Therefore it seems unlikely that any fat cells I may have somehow managed to kill off would have revived said virus.

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-05T06:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-29497>

> **Lanie;28809 wrote:** I think this is caused by centrifugal force - when you swing your arms your blood pools in your fingers. I used to hate this too - now I'm usually pushing a stroller instead of swinging my arms so it doesnt happen anymore. Try holding something while you walk, light light weights or something...or keep your hands in your pockets...?

Ah this is quite common. Back when I trained for (walking) marathons....(before I knew to avoid endurance exercise!!!)! We would teach our walkers to bend their elbows at 90 degree angles and keep their hands UP to minimize this. Otherwise, the dreaded "sausage fingers" would appear and persist for a while. Even so, around mile 5 of a half marathon I had a hard time dialing my phone, manual dexterity goes in the toilet on prolonged walks even if you don't get sausage fingers.

My understanding is it is a lymph thing. Your feet also swell, and that is lymph too. They sense they are taking a pounding and send a nice cushion of fluid to protect your feet. All the fluid circulating goes to other extremities too. Early In the 'season' it would make my feet SOOO tender but as training progrssed over time and mileage increased, it would take serious miles for them to get tender as my body stopped getting so worked up over a little exercise.

Just.... Bend those elbows baby!!!!  you'll be fine!!

\- had leftover soup for dinner last night. And ice cream. Need to get ice cream the hell out of the house and remind hubs that there was a damn good reason we weren't eating it. For a while, I was able to control myself around it, but it seems like the more often I have it, the less I'm able to control myself around it, and now I'm back to picking at it whenever he's not around to catch me. The ice cream itself doesn't make me feel crappy per se, but mentally I feel like such a failure for having lost that control over myself. Meh.

\- have been losing rather a lot of hair the past few days. Not sure what's up with that.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-06T17:54:18+0300

sounds like being properly prepared is an issue here.... I just make sure my freezer is always stocked with meat and fish. Frozen shrimp can be done in 15 minutes or so, a lb of ground meat can defrost in 20 min in a bowl of water, just like most frozen fish. After that, there's always canned sardines, kippered herring, oysters, tuna... works for me in a pinch. If you like sausage, maybe try liverwurst or braunschweiger from US wellness meats?

I don't think I could do your sleep schedule... that would set me up for failure.

### ealachan — 2012-06-07T18:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-29915>

**Leptin Reset, Day 98**

B: 6.5oz steak, two eggs, BP coffee w/ a square of dark chocolate in it

L: we don't need no stinkin' lunch!

D: not sure yet

\- Dinner last night was shrimp scampi (shrimp, olive oil, coconut oil, butter, lemon juice, garlic, onion, white wine, parsley). I ate 26 big shrimp...dunno what that translates into serving-size wise, but it was a pretty big pile and it was yummy! I also had two beers (Guinness, no less...) and a bunch of chocolate...whoops. The beer was definitely a stress response. I had a rough afternoon at work and by the time I got home all I wanted was a beer. Not a good habit, I know...but at least it wasn't ice cream?

\- I've found that a beatine dose in the morning before breakfast doesn't bother me and seems to help digestion, but if I take one in the evening before dinner, I get a weird, slightly uncomfortable heartburn-esque feeling about half an hour or so after I eat. I'm assuming this means I don't need that second dose?

\- TMI AHEAD: have noticed that my stool seems consistently darker in color since starting the beatine (not just when I've eaten a bunch of chocolate the day before, haha). Wonder if this means my body is breaking down my food better / extracting more out of it? Hope so!

\- Have noticed that the NAC is definitely making breathing a bit easier. I still get winded, but I feel like I might be recovering more quickly. Also, if I remember to take the night-time dose, I don't wake up with so much crap in my throat in the mornings. My husband has pretty bad seasonal allergies...wondering if the NAC would help ease some of his congestion.

### ealachan — 2012-06-08T17:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-30133>

**Leptin Reset, Day 99**

B: steak - probably 5oz, didn't weigh - 2 eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: planning on beef bulgogi

\- dinner last night was some shaved steak sauteed with onion, pepper and mushroom, topped with a little cheese. Steak bomb with no bread!  It was tasty, but the green peppers gave me pretty marked heartburn, even with remembering to take my betaine before I ate. I also had a little piece of braunschweiger.

\- TMI AHEAD: another bout of weird, foul, fatty and kind of stringy poo this morning. Haven't actually had a solid poo since I started taking the betaine. Not sure if that's normal or not? It's not exactly diahrea, but it's consistantly quite loose.

\- pretty tired today. Slept well while I was allowed to sleep, but the dog is really adamant that we get up at like 5am lately, which isn't fun. Wondering if a black-out blind on our bedroom window would make him less apt to start in at dawn...

\- Long, LONG day planned for tomorrow. Have to be on the road by 5am, drive 3 hours up to Montreal, do stuff all day, drive back, not looking at getting back much before about 1am Sunday. UGH. I need to boil some eggs and cook some meat up tonight so that I have something to eat in the morning, otherwise I'll end up getting fast food and that's not a good idea.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-08T20:59:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-30176>

> \- TMI AHEAD: another bout of weird, foul, fatty and kind of stringy poo this morning. Haven't actually had a solid poo since I started taking the betaine. Not sure if that's normal or not? It's not exactly diahrea, but it's consistantly quite loose.

Yep, did this for me too. I think over time it wasn't as bad but for someone who was chronically constipated, it was great to finally be going no matter how it came out. I never get the fattiness thing though. Sounds like the little burn at night is telling you that you might not need them then.

Are you taking enzymes? Might want to consider something with amylase and lipase for the proteins and fats. I take them before BAB, 2 hrs later, with lunch, 2 hours later, with supper and before bed. This is how my NP doc told me to take them. She said I needed them all the time not just when I ate. I don't take as many as I had been but I still take a lot. CT'ing has really helped me with my digestion. Crazy but true!!!

Hope you have safe travels this weekend.

### Lynnetnb@frii.com — 2012-06-08T22:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-30192>

On the zinc question, you can get zinc indicator solution. Just hold a teaspoon in your mouth for 10 seconds. If it tastes metallic, you have enough zinc. If it tastes like water, you are short of it. I've been doing this for about 6 months, after I realized the NAC depletes zinc. I've been taking NAC for a couple of years. I've been taking 3 zinc pills a day, one of them with the copper in it. The solution tasted just like water 6 months ago. I tested again in a couple of months, faint metallic taste. Yesterday, a distinct but

not unpleasant metallic taste. I Really like supps that are self-diagnosing!

Lynnet

Lynnet

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-11T22:33:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-30592>

OK, ladies, what is betaine? It sounds like I've found a couple more constipated people!! Probiotics alone aren't helping me so far. I use Smooth Move tea which usually works, but not without a stomach ache. Stool softeners......please!! I've seen people on here using mag for that purpose, you're talking about betaine (whatever that is)....what does Dr. K recommend, or does it depend? Also, what the heck causes constipation if you're eating a salad every day, drinking plenty of water? I've always eaten a lot of cheese and lately haven't been eating hardly any, which is killing me, but doesn't seem to make any difference in the constipation. Thanks for any advice!

### ealachan — 2012-06-11T23:00:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-30595>

> **justjoan060;30696 wrote:** OK, ladies, what is betaine? It sounds like I've found a couple more constipated people!! Probiotics alone aren't helping me so far. I use Smooth Move tea which usually works, but not without a stomach ache. Stool softeners......please!! I've seen people on here using mag for that purpose, you're talking about betaine (whatever that is)....what does Dr. K recommend, or does it depend? Also, what the heck causes constipation if you're eating a salad every day, drinking plenty of water? I've always eaten a lot of cheese and lately haven't been eating hardly any, which is killing me, but doesn't seem to make any difference in the constipation. Thanks for any advice!

Betaine Hcl is derived from beets. It helps your stomach create enough acid to break down the food we eat - especially the protein. I started taking it after reading Dr. K's blog about the gallbladder and what happens if you don't have one (because mine got taken out before I understood what made it sick in the first place).

[Here](http://http://www.modernherbalist.com/betaine.html) is a link with way more info. Also, if you have not already read Dr. K's blogs about gallbladder and leaky gut, you should do so. Constipation can be caused by all kinds of things, including whacked-out hormones. I think the "cheese binds you up" thing has actually been proven to be a myth...but I'm not 100% sure on that, so don't go and eat a wheel of gouda and then come back and kill me over the internet...

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-14T17:43:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-31125>

No gallbladder? Bile acid factors is what you need and I mean that. I tried the betaine. sort of worked ok. BUT you need bile acids to help deal with all the fat. The BAF really do a great job AND the stuff I'm using (from Jarrow) does have betaine in it so it's a 1 - 2 punch. When I ran out I tried using digestive enzymes. What a waste of money (for me, anyway). The BAF works like a charm.

And to answer JustJoan's plea about constipation ... just a thought but have you considered you might have food allergies? I battled contipation for several years and in this past winter it was just awful. That's when I started using the mag. then I'd have bouts of diarrhea but if I cut back on the mag the constipation was right back.

Now I know what the problem is/was. Food allergies. If I eat a trigger food I can count on having diarrhea the next day.

\- stayed up too late reading (albeit with blue-blockers on). Feeling really under the weather again this morning - lots of coughing, post-nasal drip, generally feeling like dog poo, even with regular doses of cough medicine. Suspect I might have an ear infection, as that's what sinus stuff usually morphs into with me. I have big tonsils and oddly tilted Eustacian tubes, and that's not a winning combination, trust me.

\- Dinner last night was mince curry with cauliflower in it. Super yummy.

\- Trying to get back into CT. Iced my stomach for an hour last night. I used a gel icepack thing (over my shirt, not on skin), and it only really made the skin super cold for like the first 25 minutes, then it thawed out really quickly. I have two of the packs, so I think next time I ice, I'll do half an hour with one, then switch and do half an hour with the other one.

\- 1500mg of vitamin C this morning and will repeat tonight. Not that it's particularly easy to tell if I've reached bowel tolerance when I've already got the runs...: Doubling my D3 dose again for now, too - up to 12k IU a day until I kick this sickness. I need to get some more vitamin A...right now all I'm getting is what's in my multivitamin and that's not a huge amount.

Chocolate, are you using pantothenic acid in lieu of something like betaine or bile acid factors? Or in conjunction with them? I have no gallbladder, so I want to find the best possible solution to deal with that.

### chocolate — 2012-06-15T18:58:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-31366>

Well, I suspect I have a pathetic gall bladder and pancreas. I tried the betaine, but really it just made stuff blow out the exhaust hard and fast. I take this for the adrenals, like I used to take vitamin c for the adrenals, but decided to back off because of the iron hoarding. For some reason I can't feel good. This helped. It made my tummy tighten and made me stand taller. Everything doesn't feel like it doesn't fit now. I was terrified to go keto, I have had violent fat problems before. This stuff helps break up fat and digest acetyl co A. Seems like everything I'm doing is about fat and acetyl co A. I get high sugar easily. The potassium iodide busts up fat, too.

L: -

D: dunno yet

\- FBG this morning was 69?! 45 minutes after eating, it was 82.

\- Still coughing my fool head off. It only seems productive first thing in the morning...then it's just incessant hacking with nothing coming up. Don't feel terribly congested or anything else...just wish I could stop coughing. It seems to get worse in the evenings, but it pretty much goes away when I lay down to go to sleep.

\- TMI: Bad, bad guts this morning. Not sure if it was the mega-doses of vitamin C (I got up to 5k mg yesterday) or dietary choices gone awry over the weekend (we went out for dinner last night and I had a lovely eggplant dish...but in retrospect, it was really loaded with fiber - tons of broccoli, the eggplant, pesto, and polenta...all things my guts aren't used to). l: -

D: dunno yet. probably not much as it's supposed to be super hot.

\- forgot to check my FBG this morning. Whoops.

\- slept kind of crappily last night, as I kept coughing, even with cough medicine in my system. Think it might be time to go to the doctor, as I feel like complete and utter ass. Worn out, coughing, crap in my sinuses. I've been trying to mega-dose on vitamin C to get rid of it, but all that seems to be doing is giving me poo that feels like liquid fire when it comes out. Do not want.

\- stocked up on cans of wild salmon, mackerel and sardines last night. Now just have to make myself eat them.

\- supposed to be ridiculously hot through the rest of the week here, and my boss hasn't had the A/C units installed yet, so I'm going to fry for 8 hours a day for the next 4 days. I work in an old converted farmhouse, in an upstairs office on the south side of the building. It gets very hot, very quickly. Not looking forward to this, especially with already feeling like crap.

\- picked up some MSM last night. Didn't look at the ingredients until I got home, and of course it's full of magnesium stearate. Awesome.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-19T18:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-32161>

Sorry to hear you're sick but especially sorry you have to work in the heat without A/C. I get \*seriously\* cranky when I'm stuck inside in the heat. I take it "the boss" either doesn't work there or he's not affected by the heat. Urghhh!

### ealachan — 2012-06-19T19:01:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-32171>

> **paulalynn;32274 wrote:** Sorry to hear you're sick but especially sorry you have to work in the heat without A/C. I get \*seriously\* cranky when I'm stuck inside in the heat. I take it "the boss" either doesn't work there or he's not affected by the heat. Urghhh!

Oh no, he works here, and he hates the heat. He's just completely incapable of thinking ahead for any reason. This is my 5th summer working here, and we've gone through this process EVERY summer that I've been here. It gets hot, everyone complains that we need A/C, nothing gets done, it gets hotter, we complain more, HE complains himself about how hot it is in HIS office, he doesn't call the maintenance guy to get the A/C installed, it gets hot enough that I or one of my co-workers seriously starts to feel like we're going to pass out and take an afternoon off because it's seriously way too hot to function in here, then about a week later he makes a big show of getting the A/C installed.

The problem is that he's the owner of the building as well as my boss, so a lot of his decisions when it comes to the building are self-serving. A/C costs him a lot of money, so he doesn't want to run it until he absolutely has to. Plus he has to pay the guy to install the units, and he doesn't want to do that either. That's pretty much where his thought process ends. I actually like the guy as a person, but he's really not great to work for sometimes.

I'm seriously considering taking tomorrow off, just so I don't have to be up here when it hits 95 in here.

### ealachan — 2012-06-20T18:00:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-32423>

**Leptin Reset, Day 111**

B: 7ish oz steak, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was a can of salmon mashed up with the juice of a lemon, a little extra-virgin olive oil, some chopped red onion, all plopped on a bed of spring mix and baby spinach. I'd never had canned salmon outside of my mom's horrendous Salmon Pea Wiggle back when I was a kid, so I was totally shocked at how GOOD this tasted. The salmon has an almost buttery quality to it, and if you mash it up well with a fork, the bones and skin just incorporate right into the mix and you don't even know they're there! WIN!  Oh, I also drank a beer and had a 1-oz serving of 85% dark chocolate.

\- slept ok last night. Need to look into getting a blind for the bedroom window. NEED MOAR DARK! And an A/C unit. Sweet gallopping apes, do we need an A/C unit!

\- have added MSM to my mitt-full of supplements the last few days, and definitely feel a bit more perky. Interesting.

\- guts have improved since cutting back on vitamin C (was up to 5k mg a day split into morning and night, now down to 2k, split), but still a definite diahhrea response to too much fat. I've purchased some bile acid factors and some digestive enzymes, and am hopeful that the combination will help. My plan is to get my gut back to a point where it can actually absorb what it needs out of the food I eat, then start to axe some of the vitamin supps I'm taking (since I should be able to get a lot of that stuff from my food once my gut is breaking it down properly).

\- went to the doctor about my week-and-a-half cough, and she said it's a worse than usual case of seasonal allergies. She gave me some Allegra to try, and also a nasal spray, but the understanding was that I don't have to take the nasal spray unless the Allegra doesn't work well on its own.

\- BP was 118 / 79 this morning, and FBG was 75 at 7:15am.

\- kicked up enough fuss that boss put one of the A/C units in this morning, so hopefully I won't turn into a grease spot in my office chair this afternoon...

### ealachan — 2012-06-21T17:08:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-32699>

**Leptin Reset, Day 112**

B: burger (big...didn't weigh it), two eggs, bp coffee with a lot less CO than I've been using

L: -

D: dunno yet

\- Dinner last night was half a pound of shrimp. It was too hot to make anything else. I had a little chocolate for dessert too, but it was kind of even too hot for that. It's way too early to be having this kind of oppressive heat and humidity around here! The warm spell is supposed to break tomorrow, thankfully!

\- backed off on the CO in my coffee this morning, and didn't have a bile-dump issue...HUZZAH! My guts went a little churny and it felt like I was going to, but it didn't happen. Going to go lighter on the CO until I get my bile acid factors and enzymes and figure the dosing out for those.

\- took two MSM caps this morning instead of one. Hopefully it won't make me explode in a sulfury cloud of...sulfur. the bottle says to take three times a day with meals, but I don't eat lunch and I'm hesitant to take it before bed because I've read that it keeps some people awake. Will experiment with it this weekend.

\- slept like a rock last night from about 10pm to 12:15, then from 12:45 to 6:30ish. I was so far gone that when my husband called to tell me he was ready to be picked up from work, I said "Ok, you want me to pick you up in the parking lot?" and he was like "Umm...yes? That's kind of a weird question to ask. You always pick me up at the parking lot outside the store." Then I woke up all at once and realized how out of it I had been...LOL

\- iced my stomach for 45 minutes last night. It felt SOOOOOOO good to have ice packs on me in the 90 degree heat! I took a cold shower around 9pm as well. I can't sleep when I'm all sticky and sweaty, so cold showers at night are a must in the summer time for me.

\- have to go downtown tonight to play cards, so dinner will likely be a big chef salad.

### ealachan — 2012-06-22T17:06:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-32943>

**Leptin Reset, Day 113**

b: burger, 7oz shrimp, two eggs, bp coffee w/ just butter in it (forgot the CO)

l: -

d: not sure yet

\- last night went a little sideways. I started out having a Cobb salad with chicken, which was ok...but then I had some fries with it...and then I had some chocolate soft-serve. And THEN...it was the weirdest thing...even though I was full and KNEW I was full, I kept driving by fast-food places and thinking "ooh, I could really go for a (fill in the blank)!" and actually contemplating pulling in to get one. Totally weird. I fought through it, though!

\- slept from 10:30 straight through to about 4:30am (when the dog woke me up very briefly...basically just long enough to tell him to hush), then went right back to sleep til about 6:45.

\- Little cooler outside today, thankfully. Still semi-beastly in my office, but I figure I can use it as an excuse to pop out for a walk later on. I need to start taking lunch breaks again, if only to get the hell out of here for a little while.

\- yesterday's breakfast wasn't enough protein...I was hungry by 1pm! Figured I'd try to stem that off by finishing off the shrimp this morning. Everything seemed to settle ok, and no bile-dumping...possibly because I forgot to put CO in my coffee. Really hoping the bile factors and enzymes come in the mail today so I can start that little experiment.

\- Allegra this morning for my allergies. It's working really well so far...huzzah!

\- 2 MSM caps with all my other supps this morning.

Hehehe, Not EXACTLY a random weird thing. French Fries and Soft Serve lead to fast food binge?? Whaaa, can't possibly be!

### ealachan — 2012-06-23T17:36:47+0300
