I don't usually get "evening desires", though...(much to my husband's disappointment? HAH! I won't go there.) I see what you're saying, but normally I come home, eat dinner, and I'm done for the night. I haven't felt snacky in the the evenings in a very long time. I don't think it was the fries that set me off...I think it was the soft-serve ice cream, honestly. I have ice cream issues anyway, and I should have known better.

Yes, I definitely felt proud of myself for NOT pulling into Dunkin' Donuts and getting a donut or three, for sure! I felt like I had fought the good fight and won. Now to just write myself a big sticky note to attach to the dash of my car that says "NO ICE CREAM, EVER!" and I should be all set.

### ealachan — 2012-06-25T17:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-33444>

**Leptin Reset, Day 116**

B: 7oz ham, three scrambled eggs, half a tomato, BP coffee

L: -

D: not sure yet.

FBG this morning: 94

\- Dinner last night was a can of sardines and some caprese salad (tomato, mozzarella, basil, drizzled with balsamic and olive oil). Weird combo, but I got it down ok. Tomatoes have come into season at the local farm-stand, and I couldn't pass them up. We get such crappy tomatoes around here most of the year. When the local ones come into season, it's an EVENT for me. I love them. Same with strawberries, which is why I have a quart basket of those sitting on my counter at home now, too. Gotta get the good stuff while you can, right?

\- Had kind of an up-and-down weekend eating-wise. There were transgressions aplenty on Friday, as hubs and I had "date night" - went out to dinner (which actually wasn't that bad itself - I had ribs and broccoli salad and garlic mashed potatoes (which were MIND-BLOWING), but the giant beer I washed it down with didn't help matters any), and then went to the drive-in to watch a couple movies. That involved more beer, and snacks. I paid the gastrointestinal price for my snacking and beer consumption, though. Lesson re-learned yet again. For now. Saturday was pretty good eats-wise, but yesterday I was dumb and got an ice cream. It actually gave me a stomach ache, which it's never done to me before. Interesting.

\- Had a nice long walk with hubs and the dog in the sunshine yesterday.

\- spot-iced my stomach for about an hour last night while watching TV. I need to go back to regular ice packs. The gel things I've been using just plain don't get my skin cold enough. With normal ice, I go numb in about 25 minutes and stay numb for a long time afterward. With the gel packs, I'm barely numb after an hour and I warm right back up in about 30 minutes. So much for trying to find a reusable solution.

\- Slept well last night - was in bed and zonked out by about 10:30. Woke up at 11:30 when the dog jumped on me, but went right back to sleep. Had to pick hubs up at 12:30, went right back to sleep when I got home and didn't resurface until 6:30 this morning.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-06-25T20:55:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-33493>

> **skywaykate;33627 wrote:** I've been wondering the same thing, bc I have far too many interrupted nights courtesy of my beautiful daughters, and it IS killing me.
>
>
>
> sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

I have a theory on this. It developed as my mini me had major sleep issues. He COULD.NOT.LAY.DOWN. So, for around 18 months me and my husband had to try to sleep while sitting up holding the baby. I truly have an understanding for sleep deprivation. I'm damn sure it's a major contributing factor to health issues now 4.5 yrs later.

Anywhoo - I think the "how the \*\*\*\* does mommy sleep and take care of a baby" issue is nature making sure that we only stay alive long enough to rear our offspring and then die to make room for the next fresh generation.

### ealachan — 2012-06-25T21:33:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-33501>

> **vkiernan;33625 wrote:** How do you keep up with your crazy sleep schedule or should I say lack thereof? That would drive me 6 ft. under for sure.

Funnily enough, the having to get up at 12:30am thing doesn't screw me up NEARLY as much as when he works an open and I have to get up at 4am to take him in. I could go to bed at 8pm, sleep straight through til 4am, and still be pretty much worthless all day... but to get to sleep somewhere between 9:30-10:30, sleep til 12:30, then get back to sleep by 1am and sleep through til 6:30 or 7 doesn't seem to bother me much. It USED to, because I used to be a chronic laptop abuser. I'd sit around all evening playing video games, absorbing all that blue light, and then wonder why I couldn't get to sleep before midnight. Once I weaned myself off the laptop, things started getting better. Blue-blocker glasses have helped even more. I'm usually asleep within about 10 minutes of hitting the pillow now, where as I used to lay there staring at the ceiling for hours before.

Routine is a big part of the picture, too. I've found I have to set limits for myself - no laptop after 9pm. Tv MUST be off by 10:30 at the absolute latest, can't hit the snooze button on the alarm more than twice. If I stick to those, I do ok. If I ignore them, things get a lot more difficult.

### ealachan — 2012-06-25T21:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-33504>

> **skywaykate;33627 wrote:** I've been wondering the same thing, bc I have far too many interrupted nights courtesy of my beautiful daughters, and it IS killing me.
>
>
>
> sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

I think interruptions from kids are likely a lot harder to deal with than the kind of interruption I deal with. All I get is a call around 12:30 to wake me up, at which point I put on my glasses and blue-blockers, some flip-flops, grab the car keys and drive 8 minutes to my husband's work, then 8 minutes back home, kick off the shoes, take off the glasses, go right back to bed. He knows to not try and engage me in any kind of involved conversation in the middle of the night, and he's certainly not asking for anything or being needy (well, other than needing a ride home) like a kid would be. I'm awake MAYBE a total of a half hour tops, and I can pretty much auto-pilot the whole thing, where as I'm not confident that I could if a child was involved.

Knowing that I like my sleep and don't deal well with trying to function in the middle of the night was in fact one of several key deciding factors in my choice to not have children.

### ealachan — 2012-06-25T21:42:59+0300

I went and got some lunch - some sliced roast beef from the deli and some cottage cheese. I was going to get a salad, but all their salads looked really manky.

### skywaykate — 2012-06-25T21:44:41+0300

So, for around 18 months me and my husband had to try to sleep while sitting up holding the baby. I truly have an understanding for sleep deprivation. I'm damn sure it's a major contributing factor to health issues now 4.5 yrs later.
>

Lol! Definitely seems plausible. Saw you wrote that on another thread and just couldn't imagine. We did start our girls w pillows in their cribs pretty young because I figured out they were so used to them in our bed w us and it helped them sleep better after I did that. One of my twins has loved to sleep w a full crib, 16 stuffed animals of varying sizes and two pillows and at least one blanket firmly tucked in around her feet, legs and waist. Often she'll bury herself under it all, which may actually be a sensory processing coping mechanism. I have been meaning to look into weighted blankets, or at least old-fashioned quilts for them. Can't believe she hasn't had any in bedw except an occasional random one since we returned from vacation a week ago--but that's great because we have spent the last 48 hours cleaning out from that scourge that still exists in modern day life with ineffective and yet dangerous drugs to treat it (ah, wait, that's still most diseases), lice.

### ealachan — 2012-06-26T17:05:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-33663>

**Leptin Reset, Day 117**

B: 8oz (weight before cooking) home-made pork sausage, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: dunno yet

FBG this morning: 91

\- Dinner last night was about 8oz of NY Strip steak (a really good one, at that!), a little less than half a head of cauliflower, and about half a cup of sauteed onions and mushrooms (sauteed in Kerrygold, a little CO, and steak juices). I also had a little over half a cup of strawberries with 1/4 cup or so of whipped cream for dessert. I was STUFFED. Could have definitely eaten less steak and been just fine.

\- got my digestive enzymes yesterday - I got the "Rainbow Light" brand because it had the highest reviews. Took one before dinner last night and didn't notice much, but I took one before breakfast this morning and...NO BILE DUMP! YAHOO! I didn't even get any kind of gastric stirrings at all, and that was WITH a big cup of BP coffee that had butter and CO in it. I'm going to try the bile acid factors when they show up, as well (I bought Nutricology brand. OMG, their shipping takes frickin' FOREVER, and I even paid for faster shipping. Not stoked about that), but wondering if I haven't solved my problem already with the enzymes.

\- kept waking up last night, but it was mostly because the dog kept sticking his cold, wet nose on my butt... I can sleep through construction right outside my window, but let the dog nose my butt and I wake up like someone has dumped a bucket of cold water on me...LOL

### ealachan — 2012-06-27T17:50:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-33945>

**Leptin Reset, Day 118**

B: 8oz or so of home-made sausage (beef/veal/pork blend this time), two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: dunno yet

FBG this morning: 84

\- dinner last night was salmon cakes and asparagus with a lemony yogurt sauce. It came out really well, and was very quick and easy to make. I DID use some gluten-free flour in them, so it wasn't as low-carb as it could have been, but I'm pretty sure I still didn't top 50g for the day. For dessert, I had the rest of the heavy whipping cream (1/4 cup or so), whipped with some cocoa powder to make a bittersweet chocolate mousse. I got out of work early yesterday, so I was all done with supper by 5:45 last night (an hour earlier than usual), which was nice.

\- stayed up too late (11:30) reading last night. I'm totally addicted to the Hunger Games series - just finished the 2nd book last night, and will get a good start on the 3rd tonight. Thank goodness there are only three, or I might never get another decent night of sleep.

\- TMI AHEAD: Have been diligent with the digestive enzymes before every meal since I got them, and my bowels seem SO much better already. I had a totally awesome normal poo this morning and everything. It sounds really silly, but I don't think I realized how crappy (no pun intended) having constant loose bowels was making me feel until it started to go away.

\- need to ice tonight. If I'm going to meet my goal of fitting into a plane seat by mid-august, I think I need to start icing the sides of my thighs and hips rather than my belly. I actually don't have a problem getting a seatbelt on, on most airplanes, but I usually need to have the arm-rest up because my butt overflows the seat some. This is ok when I'm sitting next to my husband, but I had a really humiliating experience last fall when we were coming back from Ireland: the seats we had reserved for the return flight had a fixed arm-rest between them, and we didn't realize it til we went to board. There was NO way my ass was fitting into that space, especially not for a 6 hour flight. I had to pull a flight attendant aside and tell her the problem, and she had to move me to another seat way back in the plane, between a stranger and an empty seat. I spent 6 hours scooted as far over to the empty-seat side as I could with the armrest up as far as it would go, so that I wouldn't touch the stranger (who seemed pretty repulsed to have to sit next to me even though I wasn't touching her, which made things that much harder). Granted, I did come home and look up the airline we flew and found out it has pretty much the smallest coach-class seats of all international flights...but still. I don't ever, EVER want to have to do that again. Not fun.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-06-27T19:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-33984>

I used to put anything and everything over my lap to hide when the staff went by. I'd pull the seatbelt up so it showed on the sides and I would sit there praying to ever damn god that ever was that they wouldn't call me on it - and we would have to do the unpleasant "seatbelt extender" routine.

What doesn't kill us makes us stronger. Or crazier, whatever.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-06-27T19:43:07+0300

### ealachan — 2012-06-27T20:15:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-34001>

> **Cavemam;34124 wrote:** Penzy's Spices Rocks for spices for making sausage!!!

I know, I gotta stock up again! The last two times, I've used a tweaked version of the recipe on the MDA blog, and it's quite tasty. For a pound of ground pork, I use:

1.5 Tbsp red wine vinegar

1 scant Tbsp fennel seed

1 tsp allspice

1/2 tsp each of garlic powder and onion powder

1 tsp salt

1/4 tsp black pepper

I squish it all up and have at it. It's better if it sits overnight in the fridge, but it's still pretty damn tasty made fresh and browned up. I don't even bother to make patties - I just crumble it all up as it browns, scoop it onto plates, and slide our eggs right on top of it. NOM!

### ealachan — 2012-06-28T17:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-34158>

**Leptin Reset, Day 119**

B: 9oz (cooked weight) leftover sirloin tips, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: dunno yet

FBG: 98

\- FIRST of all, I know why my FBG was high this morning. I made the mistake of buying some Jennie's Coconut Macaroons - they're gluten-free, but they're sure as hell not sugar-free. Predictably, I could not eat just one serving. I ate one...and then another...and then another...aaaaand then another. It was a disturbing step back into my auto-pilot sweet-eating days, and I got really pissed at myself about it afterward, and again this morning when I saw my FBG (even though I knew it was coming). Hubs is going to eat some of the macaroons today and throw away the rest so that I don't have to face them again.

\- Expecting Shark Week to kick in pretty much any day now, which explains not only the "omg must eat all the sweet things" feeling, but also the epic tiredness the last few days. Granted, poor sleep is not helping the tiredness issue, but there's also the PMS-tired feeling in the background, which feels different and very distinctive.

\- Speaking of poor sleep...slept like \*\*\*\* last night. Everything kept waking me up. It was like I couldn't ever drop down into deep sleep...felt like I was just dozing all night. The dog moving around woke me up, my husband coming to bed woke me up, my husband's nose squeaking as he slept woke me up, birds outside my window woke me up, being pissed off about not being able to sleep woke me up, and then eventually the alarm woke me up, even though I tried to beat it into submission several times. Meh.

\- Iced last night - sat in the recliner and wedged an ice block between the arm of the chair and my hip on each side, then laid the gel freezer pack thingies over the tops of my thighs. Sat like that for an hour. At the end of the hour, I measured skin temps - where the ice blocks had been, I was at 54 degrees and the skin was nicely pink and numb. Where the gel-packs had been, the skin was not pink at all, not numb at all, and only got down to 75 degrees. Clearly the gel-packs are only good for using in the picnic cooler from now on.

\- switched from the NatureMade MSM I bought at WalMart to the NOW Foods brand, as I noticed on the back of the NatureMade bottle that out of a 1000mg caplet, only 350ish mg is actually sulfur, whereas the NOW brand says it's 97% pure.

\- started on milk thistle this morning, too. Hoping it helps my liver do its livery things more effectively. GO GO GADGET LIVER!

### ealachan — 2012-06-28T17:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-34174>

> **paulalynn;34328 wrote:** Awesome! Where do you all find the time to do these things? Blogs, cooking, etc. I'm really hoping I'll be motivated and \*feel\* like doing this kind of stuff when I'm optimal

Well, I'm a crappy housekeeper, for starters. When you don't clean house, you're afforded lots more free time... I'm only half-kidding there, btw...haha! Also, I don't have kids, so there's even more free time. And lastly, cooking is just something I love to do. Always have. Cooking and knitting are like meditation to me. I can lose myself in them, and they make me happy.

The blogging came about because I started taking pictures of my food and posting them up on Facebook for my friends to see. People started asking for recipes or just generally asking questions about the food, so I started doing little write-ups on Facebook, too. Then I figured, that's basically just micro-blogging anyway, so why not just do a blog? One Girl Cooks was born! The "One Girl" thing is a nod to my previous, totally-not-about-cooking blog, which had "One Girl" in the title. I'm just one girl in a sea of people, you know?

OGC has a Facebook fan page you can join to get automatic updates when I post, if you're interested. You should be able to find it by just searching for One Girl Cooks.

### ealachan — 2012-06-29T17:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-34366>

**Leptin Reset, Day 120**

B: 7ish oz ham, 1 egg, BP coffee

L: -

D: dunno yet

\- I learned two valuable lessons yesterday: 1 - the NOW Foods MSM is definitely more potent than the NatureMade stuff I'd been taking, and 2 - it IS possible to over-sulfur. I took one MSM cap in the morning as normal, then I took one in the evening as I've been doing the past week, as well. Except, I forgot that I was taking a stronger version. Then I decided an Epsom salt footbath sounded like a nice idea. I'm sure you can see where this is going. I soaked my feet in warm water and about two cups of Epsom salts for a good half hour, right after I took the MSM caplet. Just as I was finishing my foot soak, I had a bout of the most epic and SMELLY gas I think I've ever experienced...I mean, it was seriously eye-watering. About five minutes later, I high-tailed it to the bathroom and...yeah. If you've seen the movie Dumb and Dumber, it was quite a lot like the bathroom scene in that...LOL So, no more second dose of MSM in the evening if I'm going to do a foot soak...yowzah!

\- had a nice long (for us, anyway....20 min or so) walk with the dog at dusk last night. I live on the edge of a mini-golf course and driving range, and once it's closed for the evening, we can wander across it as we please. I kicked off my shoes and walked barefoot through the grass the whole time. The dew had already fallen, so the grass was nice and cool and damp. It made me feel very happy and peaceful, even with having to swat mosquitos away now and then...

\- As Dr. K has said yoga is ok on the reset, I'm going to look into getting back into the yoga class I used to take a year or so ago. I really enjoyed it, but I stopped going because of scheduling conflicts at the time. It's actually kind of a hybrid yoga / Pilates class, and it was certainly challenging and built up some muscles for me at the time, and that was when I was basically eating SAD. Curious to see how it goes whilst eating paleo.

\- slept pretty crappily again last night. My right forearm was really sore and it was keeping me awake until I finally took some ibuprofen. Not sure if I've got the beginnings of carpal tunnel or what. Wouldn't surprise me, since I type and mouse for 8 hours straight, 5 days a week.

### ealachan — 2012-06-30T02:44:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-34508>

Supper was garlic-roasted shrimp over a salad of organic baby spinach, half a tomato, some chopped kalamata olives, a little feta cheese, and dressed with tzatziki sauce (Greek yogurt-based sauce with cucumber, dill, lemon, and shallot). It was pretty quick and easy (once I got done peeling the ever-loving shrimp). Ended up eating half a pound of shrimp total, plus the veggies / dressing.

I really need to work on not munching the whole time I'm cooking, though. I had three pieces of summer sausage (locally made, no bad stuff) while I was cooking, plus I kept nibbling on bits of feta and olives. I only had one beer, though! And a couple coconut macaroons, but all before 7pm.

**Leptin Reset, Day 121**

B: 7.75 oz beef (very lean), BP coffee

L: -

D: not sure yet

FBG: 91

\- slept like crap yet again last night, ugh! I went to bed at 9:45, and I swear I laid there for like an hour before I dropped off, and it felt like just dozing sleep again for most of the night. Had to get up at 4ish to take hubs to work. Felt REALLY awake at 4:30, but figured I'd better go back to bed for a couple hours because I slept so crappily all night. Laid there for what felt like ages before dropping off and dozing again. Slept on and off until 7:15. Something tells me it's gonna be a long, hot day.

\- started the Nutricology Ox Bile this morning - they're just tiny caplets, 125mg. Figured I'd start with one per meal and see how I reacted. The digestive enzymes have already been helping, so I don't know if the ox bile will be overkill, maybe? It certainly didn't seem to do anything crazy / funky this morning.

\- noticed I've started to get a little bit of a tan...woo! When I was a kid, I used to get SO brown and hardly ever burnt, but I was outside constantly (usually in the pool...I was part fish, and I want that part back!).

### skywaykate — 2012-06-30T22:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-34674>

Thanks for your reply to my message before. Best way was to email [support@jackkruse.com](mailto:support@jackkruse.com).

Also, forgot to mention I make a shrimp w leeks scampi based on the one on your blog, soon after I started "following" you, and my family loves it. I put it over cauliflower by steaming it after cutting it up pretty small, til al dente-ish, then mash by hand w masher and tons of butter, to make it about the size of orzo. Season w salt, pepper and cayenne or chili powder. Highly recommend you try it out, and it's great for absorbing all that great liquid from the shrimp, too!

**Leptin Reset, Day 122**

B: 10ish oz very lean steak, 2oz summer sausage (finished it up...not buying any more!), BP coffee

L: not sure

D: not sure

\- You can tell it's a quite weekend around my house when I have a chance to update my thread both days!  Slept like crap last night, stayed up when I had to take hubs to work at 4:30 this morning. I was just...awake. I got home and looked at the bed and was like "there's not much point", so I made some breakfast instead. Hopefully if nothing else, this will ensure that I sleep tonight!

\- Ended up having ice cream last night around 5:30 (which is likely why I couldn't sleep). We went out for it, though...so the remainders aren't sitting in the freezer taunting me. I learn slowly, but I do learn!

\- Thought my belly looked/felt a little different this morning so I busted out the tape measure for the first time in a couple weeks. I've lost about an inch off my waist, and my whole belly is looking and feeling more squishy and deflated, though not actually measuring smaller?! Arm fat is starting to squish up/get smaller, too...noticed it last night as I was getting into the shower. Stay the path, stay the path, lay off the ice cream, we can do this!

### ealachan — 2012-07-02T16:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-35018>

**Leptin Reset, Day 123**

B: 7oz ham (gotta get hubs off the ham kick, getting really sick of it!), two eggs, cup of kale, BP coffee

L: not planning on any

D: considering making asian chicken lettuce wraps, but something else might come up. Company for supper tonight.

\- was up from 4am to 10:30pm yesterday. Slept like THE DEAD when I finally got my head down. It could have been sheer exuastion, but it might have also had something to do with the mind-blowing nookie...ahem. Went to sleep with a biiiiig smile on my face...hehe! ANYWHO...slept really well! YAY! Woke up a couple times just for a second or three, then zonked right back out til 6:45 this morning.

\- Shark Week started this morning...bleh. Right on time as usual, though. I've been using the "Fertility Friend" app to track my period and symptoms for a few months now. It's really funny, because I started using it the month that my period was a week and a half late, so the thing INSISTS that I have a 35-day cycle, even though I've been 28 or 29 days like clockwork every month except that one month, for years. Silly computers...

\- ox bile seems to be going well - no adverse effects, that I've noticed. Not sure if it's actually doing anything spectacular for me though, since the digestive enzymes had already made a big difference. Might notice a difference in the long-term? Not sure.

\- my parents got a new pool ( one of the small soft-sided ones) and set it up yesterday, so hopefully I'll be a dog-paddling fool for the rest of the summer! It won't be terribly cold, but it will be better than nothing!

### ealachan — 2012-07-03T03:12:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-35187>

Ended up eating a late lunch / early dinner around 3:30 - had a hot dog with awesome home-made (not by me) sauerkraut and a few fries. I did also have a soda (perils of letting hubs order for me), but it was truly awful in its sugary-ness, and I couldn't drink more than about half of it. Ugh. I've never been much of a soda person anyway, and even when I did drink it, it was diet Coke or diet Ginger Ale if I ever did drink it, so having the high-test stuff today was a palate-shocker. Blech. At least it was small-batch local stuff made with real sugar, rather than HFCS.

Had ice cream IN MY HAND at the grocery store, and I put it back down and walked away. High five to me!

\- slept really well last night, from about 10:30 right through to 6:30, with only a couple of very quick wake-ups where I just rolled over and went back to sleep instantly. I read with my blue-blockers on for about half an hour before bed last night - that makes me even more tired than watching TV with them on. I was really surprised.

\- A/C unit being put in today...HUZZAH!! So excited to sleep in a nice, cool room again! Happy dance, happy dance!

\- checked the pool last night, and it's filled but not actually hooked up yet. Maybe by the weekend. Fingers crossed!

### chocolate — 2012-07-03T18:46:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-35299>

> \- have to spend all day tomorrow out in the heat and humidity, helping with the big 4th of July barbecue in town. Bleh. I get free chicken and beer, though...

My son drinks beer and he says there is no wheat in it. Classic LA carboholic... no carbs, unless its beer. I try to get him to roll over to vodka but people bring beer to our house, he drinks two kinds: cheap and free. Is it the taste or the buzz?

### ealachan — 2012-07-03T18:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-35303>

> **chocolate;35459 wrote:** My son drinks beer and he says there is no wheat in it. Classic LA carboholic... no carbs, unless its beer. I try to get him to roll over to vodka but people bring beer to our house, he drinks two kinds: cheap and free. Is it the taste or the buzz?

The taste, mostly. I average three a week, maybe? More in the summer, but far less in the winter. Tend to drink it more if we're out or socializing. I've tried gluten-free beer and it tastes awful to me.

I like wine, too (pinot noir, pino gris, chardonnay, merlot, cab sauv to a lesser extent). I actually like hard liquor just fine, but it and I are not a good combination.

### chocolate — 2012-07-03T21:05:51+0300

### ealachan — 2012-07-03T21:53:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-35333>

> **chocolate;35480 wrote:** I get drunk on half a glass and won't drive for 24 hours after... I feel that impaired.

Cheap date...LOL I tend to get a headache from beer long before it gets me drunk. Wine, it takes 2-3 4oz glasses to get a buzz. Hard liquor doesn't take much to get me buzzed - maybe two shots' worth.

I'm much better than I used to be: 8-10 years ago I used to routinely drink through a 750ml bottle of wine a night, 4-5 nights a week. Usually with a sensible dinner and then a pint of Ben & Jerry's for dessert. How I'm not actually diabetic at this point must be some kind of miracle, honestly.

### ealachan — 2012-07-05T17:03:04+0300

\- Slept like a rock from 10:30 to about 6:30. God bless the A/C!

\- Pretty sure I didn't eat enough for breakfast, but it was all I had at the time. Gotta grocery shop tonight.

\- yesterday was kind of an Omega-6 fest - I ate quite a lot of grilled chicken with skin. Successfully avoided all the many pasta salads, chips and crackers that were at the family barbecue, though! I actually ate almost entirely meat and veg all day long, except for dessert. And beer. Back on the wagon today.

\- was out in the heat from 9am to 3pm yesterday. It was 85-90 and very muggy. I was sweaty, but I noticed a couple things: a) it was mostly around my hairline and scalp, b) for as hot as it was, it didn't seem like I was sweating nearly as much as I have in the past, and c) I was still able to function mentally and physically. I was carrying heavy stuff, walking back and forth to do different stuff, and actually jogged about 50 yards over to my car at one point. It was a pleasant surprise.

### ealachan — 2012-07-06T17:16:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-35848>

**Leptin Reset, Day 127**

b: 8oz home-made pork sausage, 2 eggs, BP coffee

L: -

d: dunno yet

\- was pleasantly surprised that I didn't need lunch yesterday. I started getting hungry around 3ish, but it was totally manageable until I got home and could cook dinner. I was figuring I'd be starving since I didn't eat nearly as much for breakfast as usual, but I was ok. This seems like a good sign.

\- as mentioned before, dinner last night was almost a full pound of cod, topped with an olive / tomato tapenade (olives, grape tomatoes, capers, olive oil, shallots, vinegar...very basic and easy to do!). I bought a big bag of gorgeouns Bing cherries, intending to have some for dessert, but by the time I finished dinner it was 7pm, so I didn't eat any. Was tempted to bring a bag of them to work with me to munch on too, but I knew better and did not.

\- iced last night - 1 hour with an ice block on each hip, then I did an hour with the new gel cold-packs on my neck (one on each side) when I went to bed. I couldn't quite fall all the way asleep with the cold packs on my neck, though I did doze on and off. I finally decided I needed to actually sleep, so I took them off and zonked right out. That was at 11:30. Had to get up at 12:30 to get hubs. Was back in bed by 1am and asleep about 10 minutes after that. Didn't hear a thing until 7am. The dog and husband getting up for a walk didn't even wake me up, which usually does.

\- wrist and part of hand is sore today - feels like a tendon thing, probably from too much typing. Gotta see about getting that wrist-rest!

\- noticed yesterday that my hair has grown a ridiculous amount lately. It's always grown fast, but it's now growing in thicker and heavier and even faster. And so, so shiny! Nails are growing like crazy, too. Elbows (which always used to be rough even with moisturizer) are silky, and my heels are getting better, too. Bumps on upper arms are going away in patches. Facial skin is the best it's been in years. YAY, sulfur and coconut oil!

\- TMI: really loose guts this morning. Too much fiber? Fair amount of undigested tomato skin.

### chocolate — 2012-07-06T17:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-35862>

Wrist and hand sore thingy...I had a wrist guard with a thumb stabilizer I slept in and took off and shoved under my pillow when it was too obnoxious. It really seems to make a difference sleeping in it. The thumb is key. Maybe that healing in your sleep stuff is real. I 'd still get the computer wrist rest, though.

### ealachan — 2012-07-09T17:27:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-36321>

**Leptin Reset, Day 130**

b: two chicken sausages (no MSG, 14g protein per), 2 eggs, coffee w/ CO and a little HWC (because I need to use it up, mostly)

l: -

d: dunno yet

\- as mentioned yesterday, dinner last night was mussels. They were very good! I also ate a bunch of cherries for dessert, and I'm paying for that this morning. Holy bathroom blitz, Batman!

\- sat out in the sun for an hour yesterday morning because it was so nice out. Got fairly pink on my back and it was a little stingy / burny last night, but by this morning it was tan. I should have been more careful, given that my back hasn't had ANY exposure yet this summer, but it turned out ok. I've got a lot more freckles where I've been getting sun this year, too. Not sure if that's good, bad, or otherwise.

\- skipped sleeping with the ice packs on Saturday night, but missed them and went back to them last night. I use two - wrap each in an old tshirt and lay one on each side of my neck. It's interesting - it makes me much more aware of my heart slowing down as I relax and start to fall asleep. I did the one-on-each-side thing from 10:30 to 12:15, then went out to get hubs, came back and put a fresh ice pack on just the left side of my neck (I sleep on my right side) and zonked right back out. Didn't hear a peep until the dog jumped on me at around 6:45. Felt pretty good when I first got up, but feeling kind of tired now. Having to go to work might have something to do with it...this place makes me mentally tired. LOL

\- out of blood glucose test strips...need to order some more.

### ealachan — 2012-07-10T17:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-36547>

**Leptin Reset, Day 131****

b: two chicken sausages (no MSG, 14g protein per), two eggs, coffee with CO and a little HWC

l: -

d: planning on mini-meatloaves, but we'll see how hot it is when I get home.

\- dinner last night was an effing GLORIOUS T-bone steak, some sauteed mushrooms and onions (a little butter, a little white wine, and the accumulated juices from the resting steaks), and half a sweet potato. I had a couple glasses of red wine, as well. NO BEER**!

\- slept with ice pack on my neck last night. Had no trouble falling asleep with it, which was good. I went to bed around 10:15, woke up around 11:45 and ditched the ice pack because it was no longer very cold. Zonked right back out and slept until about 5:30am. Woke up from a REALLY terrible dream and kind of tossed and turned until 7ish, when I finally got up.

\- think I might have lost some more weight recently. Clothes are fitting a little differently, and things just FEEL different. If icing my neck when I sleep and not eating carbs was the solution to being a fat ass all along, I'm going to be pretty excited, I can't lie...

\- have to do a crapload of baking the next two nights for a work function that I said I'd make some stuff for. It will be fun because I love to bake, but it will totally suck because it's stuff I normally try not to eat (fancy cupcakes). So that will be...interesting.

\- need to get together a list of blood tests I want done and start working on my doctor about them. I have a history of being a "frequent flier" at the clinic I go to, so I'm kind of bracing for the "there's nothing wrong with you" conversation that's likely to ensue. I like my doctor a lot, but I think sometimes she just wants to reach over, pat me on the head, and say "you're totally bonkers".

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-36593>

Are your meat loaves on your blog? I was doing turkey with a little oats and were they ever handy, but not optimal.

EDIT:I found your meatloaves and couldn't delete. Illiteracy issues, apparently.

### ealachan — 2012-07-10T20:41:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-36607>

> **chocolate;36759 wrote:** Are your meat loaves on your blog? I was doing turkey with a little oats and were they ever handy, but not optimal.
>
>
>
> EDIT:I found your meatloaves and couldn't delete. Illiteracy issues, apparently.

I think the last meatloaf entry I did was an older version of the recipe. I've tweaked it a couple times since then.

I've been using chopped mushrooms in my meatloaf rather than bread crumbs or cracker crumbs, and it has worked well. I like to mince the mushrooms up fine and saute them with a little bit of chopped-up bacon and some garlic and herbs (parsley and thyme, usually) for a few minutes. I let it cool a little and incorporate that into my meat mixture along with a beaten egg, grated onion, sometimes some grated carrot, salt, pepper. Then I form the little loaves, give them a quick sear in a hot pan like burgers, then bake them off. I usually use a mixture of ground beef and pork, but I've thrown in everything from venison to turkey to veal before and had good results. If I'm using leaner meat, I add more bacon into the mix to help keep things moist.

### ealachan — 2012-07-11T15:33:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-36799>

**Leptin Reset, Day 132**

b: 6.5oz shaved steak, two eggs, coffee w/ CO and a little HWC

l: -

d: leftover meatloaf, probably

\- dinner last night ended up being mostly bacon, but not by design. I was making meatloaf, and I kept munching on the bacon I cooked for it as I was prepping...and by the time the meatloaves were actually done, I just wasn't hungry. I did also have a couple ounces of the meatloaf mix cooked off (I usually make a little "tester" burger of the mix to make sure my seasoning is good), so it wasn't ALL bacon...but still. The up-side is that we now have plenty of meatloaf for supper tonight!

\- cooked a dozen cupcakes (only about 1/3 done with that project...the rest get done tonight), and managed to not eat a single one. HIGH FIVE FOR ME! I did taste the batter a couple times, as well as the lemon curd I made, but didn't eat any significant amount of it. Very proud of myself.

\- had an exercise in fragmented sleep last night: went to bed at 10:30, woke up at midnight to go get Hubs, got back to bed around 12:45, then had to get up at 4am to take him BACK to work (his boss quit at 11pm last night, so Hubs had to do a turn-around close-open...NOT fun), came back home and was back in bed by 4:30, slept til 7. I've been up an hour and a half and I already want to go back to bed. Not good. Hopefully the regional manager will be able to sort out an employee from another store to sub in for either the opens or closes so that Hubs doesn't have to do more turn-arounds like that, because I don't think either he or I will last long if those continue...ugh!

\- ice packs on my neck (both sides) last night from 10:30 to midnight.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-11T18:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-36844>

I give you strength today to battle those cupcakes! You are a brave woman. You know, they say willpower gets exhausted. . .so beware of willpower fatigue.

Your food choices seem to be heading in a positive direction. . .directionally accurate if you will. Yay for you! I'm impressed that you realized you were getting filled up on the bacon and didn't eat the nice meatloafs that were hot/fresh just for the fun of it.

### ealachan — 2012-07-11T19:27:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-36871>

> **Cavemam;37010 wrote:** I give you strength today to battle those cupcakes! You are a brave woman. You know, they say willpower gets exhausted. . .so beware of willpower fatigue.
>
>
>
> Your food choices seem to be heading in a positive direction. . .directionally accurate if you will. Yay for you! I'm impressed that you realized you were getting filled up on the bacon and didn't eat the nice meatloafs that were hot/fresh just for the fun of it.

Thanks for the strength, lovely! xx Tonight is chocolate cupcakes and filling / frosting, so I may well need it.

Yeah, I've been a lot better lately about gauging when I'm actually full, and not just eating because it's there / because it's "time" to. And honestly, last night with the cupcakes, it didn't really feel like a huge willpower struggle for me, which was AWESOME. I tasted the batter and the lemon curd both to make sure they were the way I wanted them, but then I didn't have any problem just putting the spoon down. The old me would have likely eaten at least a cupcake's worth of batter raw, and then a cooked cupcake, too...maybe more, if I had extra. I like the feeling of self-control that comes with just being able to say "you don't need that". I just ate indiscriminately without regard for actual hunger or nutritional needs for so long that now it seems like a miracle to not do that...to not only be aware of what I'm eating, but to feel like I have a choice in the matter...if that makes sense.

### ealachan — 2012-07-12T17:21:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-37167>

**Leptin Reset, Day 133**

B: 5ish oz shaved steak plus a little chopped-up bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ CO and HWC

L: company BBQ - will stick to meat and veg!

D: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was a mini meatloaf (which wasn't that mini, really...haha), half a potato with butter and a side-salad of spring mix dressed with a little olive oil and sherry vinegar. It was good, I didn't feel stuffed afterwards, and I was able to get on with all the baking I had to do without feeling like I needed to eat all the sugary stuff I was making.

\- baked a bunch of cupcakes, and didn't eat a one!  Every time I got tempted, I thought "No, I told CaveMam I wouldn't..." and it worked like a charm. The only real slip-up I had was with the cherry brandy that the recipe called for. I had a sip to taste it before I put it in the batter, then I had another sip before I added it to the filling, then I had kind of a big sip because I was still trying to decide if I liked it or if it tasted like cough medicine. It didn't give me an alcohol buzz, but it gave me a sugar rush which was NOT pleasant. My heart beat really hard and fast for about 10 minutes, then it just went away and I was fine...but it really sucked for those 10 minutes or so. Lesson learned on the cherry brandy!

\- Hubs did NOT have to do another close-open turnaround, so that was a relief. Didn't get a ton of sleep last night, though. I was working in the kitchen until about 9:45, then went to bed and he woke up and wanted to mess around...and since the oxytocin is good for us, I oblidged of course...haha...so I didn't get to sleep until 11ish. Had to get up at 4 to take Hubs to work, was back in bed by 4:30. Woke up at 7 because the dog started in, but dozed on and off until 8. Pretty glad I don't have anything to do tonight and can just crash out early!

\- slept with ice pack on neck until some time after midnight when I woke up and it wasn't cold anymore, so I tossed it on the floor.

### ealachan — 2012-07-13T18:47:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-37502>

**Leptin Reset, Day 134**

b: went to the diner: eggs with veggies (onion, pepper, mushroom) and cheese, two sausage patties, some grilled potatoes, coffee w/ half and half

l: -

d: not sure yet. Something light, as it's going to be super hot.

\- DAY OFF TODAY! Huzzah!  Had to do some running around this morning and hubs was fasting for blood work, so we skipped breakfast until after his draw. Ended up finishing breakfast around 10:45. I was HUNGRY! I totally forgot to take my enzymes before we went out to eat though, and I paid for it. The upside is that I got a little extra exercise while I was BOLTING for the toilet...hahaha

\- went out for dinner last night - had a really good tex-mex steak salad - greens dressed with a lime vinaigrette, some fresh jalapenos, seared steak, pico de gallo and some queso fresco. I had a couple margaritas, too...but they were too sweet. Next time I'm going to just ask for tequila and lime juice, I think.

\- Epic oxytocin dosage last night, ifyouknowwhatimean andithinkthatyoudo... Iced my neck and slept like the dead from about 10:45 until 7 this morning. Hubs is working opens all weekend, so that means getting up at 4am tomorrow, Sunday and Monday. I'm going to try to stay up in the mornings rather than going back to bed. I'd like to try to train myself into a pattern of going to bed earlier and getting up earlier.

\- I get to hang out with a couple friends and their kids and play some mini-golf late this afternoon. Pretty psyched for that, although it's going to be like 90 degrees and muggy. Hoping I can at least get some sun.

### ealachan — 2012-07-14T14:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-37717>

**Leptin Reset, Day 135**

b (4:45am...ugh!): three eggs, some bacon ends, coffee w/ co and hwc

L: not sure?

d: dunno

\- pretty sure I'm going to need lunch, as I highly doubt there was enough protein and fat in breakfast to keep me going until dinner.

\- dinner last night was a rootbeer float. I KNOW. Kept me up half the damn night, too. Won't be repeating that poor choice any time soon.

\- had a ball mini-golfing with the kids yesterday afternoon. Got plenty of late-afternoon sun, and managed to not feel like I was going to die even though it was like 90 in the shade. We horsed around and were just generally loud and raucous and it was a good time. My inner 8-year-old was happy to hang out with some real 8-year-olds for a while.

\- iced neck last night from 8:45 to around midnight. Had trouble getting to sleep, then had bizarre dreams all night and kept waking up. No nookie before bed...that probably didn't help, but hubs was tired. We were up at 4am to get him to work, and I stayed up rather than going back to bed (which was an internal struggle, I tell you what). Not being productive, but at least I'm up.

### ealachan — 2012-07-14T17:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-37760>

> **Cavemam;37895 wrote:** I admire that you are plowing forth and not using crazy schedules as a free pass. I think when I was your age, I would have used that as an excuse. Yay for you. Lay down a negative pathway on the dinner choice! Glad it made you feel bad!

re: schedules - I'm trying to train myself to be less lazy. Phase One is trying to get up earlier in the morning, whether it's a work day or not. Phase Two is going to be getting at least one household thing done a day. Phase Three will hopefully involve cleaning out the spare bedroom that is currently packed with all manner of \*\*\*\* because my husband is a \*\*\*\*ing pack-rat and is as lazy as I am. He keeps talking about wanting to buy a house, but I don't want to buy a house and then just be lazy and irresponsible and let it go to hell like we have done with our apartment since we moved in. I'm 32 years old, and he's 37. Time to grow up a little bit.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-15T07:08:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-37991>

> **ealachan;37927 wrote:** re: schedules - I'm trying to train myself to be less lazy. Phase One is trying to get up earlier in the morning, whether it's a work day or not. Phase Two is going to be getting at least one household thing done a day. Phase Three will hopefully involve cleaning out the spare bedroom that is currently packed with all manner of \*\*\*\* because my husband is a \*\*\*\*ing pack-rat and is as lazy as I am. He keeps talking about wanting to buy a house, but I don't want to buy a house and then just be lazy and irresponsible and let it go to hell like we have done with our apartment since we moved in. I'm 32 years old, and he's 37. Time to grow up a little bit.

Let go that way of thinking, You are stating Lazy as a negative personality issue. If there is stuff you would do if you had more energy then it's an energy issue not a character issue. F-ing pack rat is a whole other matter. I've run screaming from one of those. My hubs had hoarder/collector tendancies, but he also was A-OK letting me rein that in for a peaceful life with a happy wife.

Now I must put on my real estate hat: Now is an A W E S O M E time to purchase a 1st home. super low interest rates and still lots of low downpayment options.

Of course I have a cleaning lady and super fabulous house. . . so who am I to dispense advice in these matters. The cleaning lady is a requirement for my happy marriage since my husband is blind to all manner of disorganization and dirty.

Ok, 32. I want you to have a baby no later than 35 so cut back on the margaritas.

### ealachan — 2012-07-15T12:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38013>

> **Cavemam;38159 wrote:** Let go that way of thinking, You are stating Lazy as a negative personality issue. If there is stuff you would do if you had more energy then it's an energy issue not a character issue. F-ing pack rat is a whole other matter. I've run screaming from one of those. My hubs had hoarder/collector tendancies, but he also was A-OK letting me rein that in for a peaceful life with a happy wife.
>
>
>
> Now I must put on my real estate hat: Now is an A W E S O M E time to purchase a 1st home. super low interest rates and still lots of low downpayment options.
>
>
>
> Of course I have a cleaning lady and super fabulous house. . . so who am I to dispense advice in these matters. The cleaning lady is a requirement for my happy marriage since my husband is blind to all manner of disorganization and dirty.
>
>
>
> Ok, 32. I want you to have a baby no later than 35 so cut back on the margaritas.

It's not that I don't have the energy to do stuff around the house so much as I'm just not a home-maker type. My house-wife tendencies come to a screeching halt outside of the kitchen. I've just come to realize in the 10 years I've lived on my own that I'd way rather spend my time doing things that interest me (reading, knitting, farting around on the computer, cooking) than things that don't (cleaning, organizing). I'm not beating myself up over it...I'm just saying that's how I roll. The only time I get pissy about it is when my parents come over, because my mom notices stuff like cobwebs and dustbunnies (whereas none of my friends do, or if they do, they couldn't care less).

re: pack-rat - he's not a hoarder, per se. He's just scatterbrained, and will collect a bunch of stuff meaning to do something with it, but then move on to the next thing and kind of forget all about what it was he was going to do with the stuff. It takes me eventually poking him and saying "hey, you've got a big pile of X, Y, and Z on the table...are you going to do something with it?" to remind him, then he'll either throw it away or sort it out.

re: house - I'd love to have one, but I'm scared \*\*\*\*less of the money aspect. We don't have anything resembling a down-payment, for one thing. For another, I've still got fairly sketchy credit (though it's getting better...still paying for my mis-spent youth), and husband has NO credit because he's only been in the States for a little less than three years and only working for about a year and a half. We're working on that too, but it's slow going. Also, I can't get past the fact that if I'm renting and the hot water heater breaks, I can call someone else and they have to get it fixed...whereas if I own a house and the hot water heater breaks, I have to figure out how the hell to pay for it.

re: baby - no thank you, ma'am. I love kids, as long as they go home with their parents at the end of the day.  I have no desire to create offspring. I am not emotionally cut out to be a momma, and I'm absolutely fine with that. I'm quite happy just being the crazy auntie to my friends' and cousins' little ones.

So there's your early-morning edition of Things That Eala Thinks...LOL

### ealachan — 2012-07-15T13:06:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38016>

**Leptin Reset, Day 136**

b (4:45am): three eggs, some bacon ends, coffee w/ co and hwc

l: will likely need it, but don't know what yet

d: dunno / may skip

\- Ended up eating breakfast at 4:45am yesterday and then didn't eat lunch until like 2:30ish (had a couple bratwurst and some onions and peppers, and a beer). Was full after that and didn't want to eat after 7, so skipped dinner even though I got kind of hungry later on. Will probably do something similar today, though husband gets out of work slightly earlier today, so may eat lunch early enough to be able to have a bit of dinner, too.

\- Not sure if it was being somewhat hungry that made me restless all night, but I had a lot of trouble dropping off to sleep even though I was exhausted. Ice pack on my neck felt way too cold and annoying, A/C sounded too loud, dog couldn't get comfortable, kept hearing creepy things, etc. It was all just conspiring against me. By the time 4am rolled around, I felt like I had just gotten to sleep...so today's going to be a long, hot day. Seriously considering just holing up in the A/C'ed bedroom and watching NetFlix all day once the laundry is done. The long stretch of hot weather is finally wearing on me, mentally. Totally sick of it. Ready to move to Greenland.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-15T23:14:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38120>

hehe sista, that was my speech right up until the day I saw the ultrasound! If nature wins, give me a call - don't panic.

My now husband found out because the very first thing I did after seeing the stick was look up planned parent hood and I left it on the computer in my freaked-out-panic.

Long story short, I was 16 weeks preggy before I knew it!! Since my entire life was either gaining or losing, during that time frame I was gaining, so thought nothing of those extra pounds. I also thought all the "feelings" stuff was because I was so upset at gaining weight.

Just sayin, never know what life will bring. Besides - with all this oxytocin I keep hearing about along with all this good health you got going on, nature may win yet.

What up with the beer? Is that Vermont thing? Everyone I know from up that way is drinking beer all the damn time!

### ealachan — 2012-07-16T12:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38320>

> **Cavemam;38289 wrote:** hehe sista, that was my speech right up until the day I saw the ultrasound! If nature wins, give me a call - don't panic.
>

> My now husband found out because the very first thing I did after seeing the stick was look up planned parent hood and I left it on the computer in my freaked-out-panic.
>
>
>
> Long story short, I was 16 weeks preggy before I knew it!! Since my entire life was either gaining or losing, during that time frame I was gaining, so thought nothing of those extra pounds. I also thought all the "feelings" stuff was because I was so upset at gaining weight.
>
>
>
>
>
> Just sayin, never know what life will bring. Besides - with all this oxytocin I keep hearing about along with all this good health you got going on, nature may win yet.
>
>
>
> What up with the beer? Is that Vermont thing? Everyone I know from up that way is drinking beer all the damn time!

I grew up with a mom who, while not abusive per se, wasn't very good at hiding the fact that she kind of resented having to give up a lot of what she had going on in order to have me and take care of me. Without getting into the whole therapy side of things, let's just say that I recognize a lot of her personality traits in myself and I'd rather not bring a kid up like she brought me up. I love my mom, don't get me wrong...and she did the best she could with what she had...but there's a lot of stuff going on there that colors my opinion of my personal reproduction goals. If I DID somehow mysteriously end up pregnant, I'm sure I'd suck it up and deal...but everyone (with the exception of my mother-in-law, who is baby-obsessed but also lives 3500 miles away in the UK) would be a lot happier if that didn't happen.

re: beer - I just really like it! Yes, it's certainly popular in Vermont. New England has a lot of really amazing micro- and nano-brewers, craft beers, etc. I think part of my beer affinity is linked to my foodie-ness, and my general curious nature. I like to try new things. New things that don't involve liquified fish heads, anyway.

### ealachan — 2012-07-16T13:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38321>

**Leptin Reset, Day 137**

b (4:45am): steak (7ish oz), coffee w/ CO and HWC

L: hoping I can skip it

d: probably better eat some fish.

\- Ate late lunch / early dinner (drunch? Linner?) around 3:30 yesterday - we had burgers (no buns, obviously) and salad (mostly lettuce, a few grape tomatoes, little bit of onion and carrot) dressed with olive oil and sherry vinegar. Also had a beer (Honker's Ale! So good!) and a little bit of ice cream. I've come to the realization that I just plain hate normal lettuce-based salad. I had to force myself to eat it last night. I can handle it better if it's made with spring mix because it's lots of different greens mixed together, but your normal run of the mill iceberg lettuce based salad? Gross. No wonder people drown that \*\*\*\* in ranch dressing! And no, I didn't voluntarily make such a salad. It was a pre-made one that hubs picked up.

\- still super stupidly hot and muggy here. Did laundry early yesterday morning (and put it all away! ADULT HIGH FIVE!), then mostly just mooched around all day. Took a cold shower around 5:30pm, then sat in the A/C and read for a while. Got in a bit of "oxytocin therapy" around 8pm, was zonked out with ice packs on my neck by 9:30. I only remember waking up once and it couldn't have been for more than a couple seconds, but for some reason 4am really kicked my ass today. I stayed up, but I have to go to work and I'm not looking forward to it. I'm hoping that if I get in there by 7, I can talk Boss into letting me go home at 3, because I don't think I can last until 5 without a nap.

\- have noticed a weird sensation the last couple days where, when I eat beef that's fatty, the fat seems to cling to the roof of my mouth for a while. It's kind of gross. I haven't noticed it with pork fat or coconut oil...just beef fat from the burgers last night and the steak this morning. Dunno if it's something to do with a chemistry change in my saliva or if I'm just being a spaz, or what.

### ealachan — 2012-07-16T16:28:38+0300

### ealachan — 2012-07-16T19:51:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38409>

I've come to the realization that it's a really good thing this asian pear and ginger-flavored kombucha is like $3.99 a bottle, because otherwise I'd be drinking it constantly. SO FIZZY AND GOOD.

### ealachan — 2012-07-17T16:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38709>

**Leptin Reset, Day 138**

B: 8ish oz shaved steak, little bit of onion and red pepper, slice of cheese, coffee w/ CO and HWC

L: -

D: possibly going out for a burger, not sure yet.

\- was in bed with ice packs on neck by 9:15 last night. Woke up once for a minute or so when hubs came to bed around 10pm, then maybe one other time in the night when I threw the no-longer-cold ice packs off, but otherwise slept like a rock until my alarm went off at 6:15. Only hit snooze once, then got up! That's a big deal for me, trust me. I have a long history of being a snooze-button abuser (which is silly because most clocks only have like a 10 minute snooze, so it's not like you're getting any more actual sleep at that point...you're just dozing. Kind of pointless...). FURTHER - I not only got up, but I leashed up and walked the dog. Usually hubs walks the dog first thing in the AM unless he's at work. Go me, being motivated.

\- had what I believe was ovulation pain this morning (timing would be right), but nowhere near as hurty as it has been for years now, so that's interesting.

\- need to remember to pick up some BG monitor strips this weekend while I'm in town. Curious to see what my FBG has been doing since I started icing my neck every night.

\- kicking myself for not being on the mailing list and seeing the offer for the $99 email consult with Dr. K!! I would have jumped on that, dang it! It's going to be a while yet before I've got enough saved up for an hour consult, but an email consult would have at least got me pointed more in the right direction. Oh, well. On the mailing list now, so hopefully I won't miss the next one.

### janagram — 2012-07-17T18:20:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38737>

your shrimp sounds great...

icing your neck and FBG? interesting...I missed the connection...would you explain it to me?

### ealachan — 2012-07-17T19:08:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38762>

> **janagram;38915 wrote:** your shrimp sounds great...
>
> icing your neck and FBG? interesting...I missed the connection...would you explain it to me?

It's kind of an experiment I'm conducting. There was a thread a couple weeks back where Dr. K and several others mentioned icing the carotid artery while sleeping because it cools the blood going into the brain and should therefore have an impact on the hormonal processes that go on while we sleep (or at least that's what I extrapolated out of the conversation). I figured hey, what have I got to lose, so I started doing it. I haven't tested my FBG since I started doing it (because I ran out of strips), so I'm now curious to see if it has had any effect. My FBG has not historicly been a problem so long as I'm not eating a load of carbs before bed, but I'm interested to see if there's any difference, regardless.

I WILL say that, while it took a little while to get used to falling asleep with the ice packs on my neck (I ice both sides, not just the left side where the carotid runs), I now really enjoy the sensation. It seems to be doing SOMETHING, because I've felt calmer and more focused lately, and my sex drive appears to be returning full-tilt (TMI, sorry...haha).

### ealachan — 2012-07-18T16:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-38991>

**Leptin Reset, Day 139**

B: 9ish oz shaved steak, slice of cheese, onions, red peppers, coffee w/ CO and the LAST of the heavy whipping cream (thank goodness)

L: -

D: not sure yet - probably fish, since hubs is working

\- did end up going out for dinner last night - had a burger w/ lettuce, tomato, bacon, mushrooms and cheese and a side of house-made chips, and a beer. Just one beer, even though husband offered to get me another and then begged to buy more when we were at the store. I said no to the beer, but I couldn't win the ice cream battle, so we ended up having a little bit of ice cream, too. At least I've got him talked down to just buying pints of it now, rather than half gallons...

\- Had to tack a blanket up over my bedroom window because not only did the dumbass teenagers who work downstairs leave the huge floodlight on when they left, but the fairgrounds are right next to us and they're setting up stuff for the fair this weekend, so there were tons of neon lights from the rides, shining directly into my window! Looks like the blanket-curtain is going to be a design feature for the next few days.  Was in bed by 9pm. Read for a while with ice on my neck, got up and switched out my ice packs for fresh ones at around 9:45 and was asleep by about 10pm. I don't remember waking up once, even though we had some bad thunderstorms and Hubs said the dog had him up and down a lot in the night. Got right up when the alarm went off at 6:10 this morning...woo!

\- bought another round of Jarrow MK-7 vitamin K2. I looked all over for MK-4, but the only one I could find was a 5g dosage! I didn't want to make a jump in dosage like that without knowing what I actually need, so I just re-ordered the less-optimal MK-7. Also bought some more NeuroMag and some more DIM. Hoping that after I get my blood tests done, I can start to drop some of these supplements. Getting pretty sick of taking a mitt-full of pills every morning.

### ealachan — 2012-07-19T01:41:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39186>

Well, \*\*\*\*.

Just got my husband's blood work back from his physical, and his FBG was 222!  His mom and two of her brothers are diabetic, so I've been really trying to push him to eat better, but I guess I gotta start pushing a hell of a lot harder now. His HDL was low and his trigs were high, as well. Not super duper high, but definitely higher than I know they should be.

This is going to be a long, hard slog. He has no real interest in any of this stuff and isn't science-minded at all, so this is going to be a whole lot of me saying "you can't have that, eat this instead", him saying "why?" and me trying to explain it while his eyes glaze over.

### ealachan — 2012-07-19T03:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39233>

He's very good about eating what I make and not complaining (I think because I'm a good cook and I make even things he thought he didn't like taste good, thank gods). It's when he's at work (in a convenience store, ugh) or left to his own devices to get lunch when I'm not home that \*\*\*\* goes sideways. He's a LOT better than he used to be. It used to be sandwiches and full-sugar sodas all the way, plus candy and chips and...yeah. Now I've got him down to diet soda (which he still drinks too much of, but at least it's not pure sugar), and he'll usually have a chef salad for lunch...but he eats horrible stuff when he's at work. Work is really the big issue. I can figure out how to get more organized so that there's lunch stuff at home for him, but I think if I don't want him to eat \*\*\*\* at work, I'm going to have to basically make him a meal ahead to take with him, or take a meal to him while he's there. It's not super far away from home...it's do-able.

I just have to keep telling myself this. It's do-able. We can do this. We can get him better. It's just going to take some time.

I don't want my husband to be sick...

### Lanie — 2012-07-19T13:51:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39312>

Cavemam is right...he's got to want to change (and I know you know that). Sorry...delurking for a moment. I can tell how scared you are seeing those numbers.

There's no way he feels good with blood sugar that high. Maybe you could ask him how he feels during the day - lots of highs and lows, lots of cravings, maybe not thinking clearly...? Just listen and remind him there's a way for him to feel better. Also remind him of the consequences of continuing on as he is. I'm sure you know the risks of untreated diabetes.

Maybe it's time for him to start looking for a different job? It's kind of like an alcoholic working in a bar.

I hope your conversation with him went well. He may surprise you. My husband has been surprising me lately with willingness to get on board the paleo train. Didn't see that coming. What motivates him is the possibility of a brighter future for himself. Maybe you can help your hubs see one for himself.

This really sucks but like you said it is doable. Baby steps and you will get him there. Once you start implementing things, over time they become natural just like everything we do out here. The soda will be a tough one but you will figure something out. You could always do real lemonade with stevia or come up with some other creative concoction. You sound like you are very creative in the kitchen.

It is sad, but this will probably be my dh down the road. His trigs are really bad and his D & T is low but I have been making him take D, A and K. I am guessing he will end up on bs med and bp med before too long. Stressful life and BAD(SAD) diet make for a deadly combination. Keep us informed of the changes you are able to implement. I will need ideas I'm sure. I am always working on how I can get him to change but like CM said, they have to want to.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-19T15:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39329>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39331>

Yes, I have a BG monitor. I was going to pick some more strips up this weekend. ### ealachan — 2012-07-19T16:23:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39334>

Thanks for the support, guys. I talked to him a little bit about it this morning, and it went well. I said, basically, "with these numbers, you're diabetic. If we don't get it fixed, you'll end up like your step-dad, having to inject yourself before each meal, or worse...you'll end up like your uncle, losing your eyesight and eventually a leg. This is not something to eff around with." He said he understood, and he'd do whatever I told him to do in order to get healthy. He tends to be fairly paranoid about health stuff like I am, so I'm thinking that the numbers may have actually scared him onto the straight and narrow. We'll see. I realize that I can't change him if he doesn't want to change...trust me, I've got that life lesson down pat from several bad experiences in the past 10 years (not having to do with him)!

I'm a little bit perturbed because of course the doctor sent a note with the labs, saying she wanted to see him again to discuss stuff, and so he's all gung-ho about seeing her again. I already know what she's going to tell him, which is the same thing I told him - lay off the sugar and starch - but she's also pushing a low-saturated-fat diet, and that's the opposite of what we do. He tends to be very "doctor knows best", so I'm kind of worried that the low-fat thing might become a sticking point...but we'll see. I've looked around for other more Paleo-friendly doctors in our area, but we've basically got one huge hospital that almost all the doctors around are affiliated with, and Paleo is very much not their line of thinking.

In the meantime, he has strict instructions for no soda, candy or bread with lunch today, and we'll work on the rest step by step.

### ealachan — 2012-07-19T16:51:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39340>

**Leptin Reset, Day 140**

20 weeks! That's 5 months! Holy crap!

B: 8ish oz home-made sugar-free pork sausage, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: sardines and kale, likely

\- dinner last night was about a cup of kale sauteed in KG butter and bacon fat (if you've never tried kale cooked with bacon or in bacon fat, you should...SO delicious!), and half a pound of shrimp cooked in KG butter, a splash of white wine, some garlic and some lemon juice. I dumped it all in a bowl and went to town on it. YUM! I only wish I'd had more shrimp. I did try to eat the tails / shells on some of the shrimp, but they were weird and didn't really break down much when I chewed them...it was like chewing on a piece of plastic. Had some dark chocolate for dessert. Haven't had chocolate in a couple weeks, at least...and didn't really miss it much, honestly. Not missing chocolate, for me, is earth-shaking, trust me!!

\- got to bed around 9:45 but had trouble falling asleep (oh hai, chocolate consumption much?!). Probably was awake until 10:30 or so. Slept with ice packs on neck until 12:30, then got up to pick up husband. Back to bed with fresh ice packs by 1am, slept well for a while but then got tossy-turny as it started to get light out. Finally got up at 6:30, walked dog in the sunshine.

\- chest and shoulders ache a lot lately. Not sure if it's due to changing my keyboard at work (and typing a lot more at work lately) or the way I'm sleeping or what, but it's annoying. Our mattress is really old and crappy and we need a new one, but haven't had the money. Hopefully this fall we can sort something out.

\- I stopped chewing my nails last week! They've grown almost out to the tips of my fingers already. I've been a chewer on and off all my life (my dad is, too), but I've managed to stop a few times and grow my nails out. I'm hoping this time they'll grow out less bendy. I've never had a problem length-wise, but strength-wise they've always been pretty lacking.

\- Hair is growing like crazy, too. I haven't had a hair cut in a year, and I've gained a good 6-7 inches of length since then, but a lot of it has been in the last 6 months. It has started to grow in thicker, too. Yippee! When I was a kid and teenager, I had a pretty impressive head of hair - down to my waist and my ponytail was as thick around as a banana. Wouldn't mind having that back again.

\- weighed myself out of curiousity this morning. Got readings of 344, 345 and 342. w00t!

### ealachan — 2012-07-19T17:27:12+0300

I don't! I stand very still! That's actually one of the smallest 3-in-a-row swings I've ever had with that scale. Usually the swing is like 10-12lbs. That's why I started doing 3-in-a-rows and averaging them in the first place, because I'd get on the scale, see the number and think "the \*\*\*\*? that can't be right!", then step off and get on again, only to show a wildly different number!

Stupid scale is stupid. Good thing I don't pay much attention to it anymore.

### ealachan — 2012-07-20T16:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39665>

**Leptin Reset, Day 141**

B: 8oz homemade pork sausage, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: not sure yet

\- forgot to mention yesterday that there was a cookout here at work at lunch time. I had a burger w/ no bun and some salad. I also had a diet Coke, which I haven't had in EONS, and I couldn't even finish it. Blech.

\- got to bed around 10 last night, was asleep with ice packs on neck by 10:30. Woke up at 12:30 to go get hubs, had a MASSIVE charlie-horse in my right calf as I was getting out of bed. I haven't had one in a long, long time and it effing HURT. The muscle was all knotted up and it took a couple minutes of walking around the bedroom to get it to loosen up. Crazy. I've been well-hydrated and taking all my supps and eating plenty of salt, so not sure where the hell that came from!

\- Didn't sleep all that well after getting back to bed last night. Everything kept waking me up and I was just really tossy-turny again. The dog started in whining and jumping on me around 6:15, and I finally gave up and got up at 6:30. I love my dog, but he's kind of a little tyrant sometimes...

\- got some new clothes in the mail yesterday. I ordered my "old" size for everying, since I'm paranoid about getting smaller sizes without trying them on first. Happy to report that all the shirts could easily go down at least a size and still fit just fine, and same for the skirt I bought. Everything is still wearable, so I'm not going to return any of it...but I was chuffed to find that everything was a bit loose.

\- stopped for iced coffee the last couple mornings on the way to work. Need to stop doing that immediately. Not only is it more caffiene and dairy than I need, but I know that I'll eventually end up buying some kind of baked-good nonsense as well if I keep it up. DO NOT WANT!

### ealachan — 2012-07-21T12:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-39962>

**Leptin Reset, Day 142**

b (4:45am): 8ish oz ribeye steak, bp coffee

l: hoping i dont need it!

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was [bahn mi salad](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/banh-mi-salad/#axzz21FNLI5A6) from the recipe on the MDA blog. It's basically very lean pork marinated in fish sauce and black pepper, then seared off and cut into pieces and served with a salad of cabbage, carrot, cukes, cilantro, green onion, and I added some radish as well. The dressing is just mayo and rice vinegar / lime juice - super simple. I should have made my own mayo but I cheated and used store-bought olive-oil mayo. The whole thing tasted fantastic and took like 15 minutes to make (gods bless pre-shredded cabbage! lol). I never would have thought to marinate pork in fish sauce, but it totally works. So yummy. I had a small glass of pinot grigio with it, just for the sake of full disclosure...

\- got some oxytocin therapy in last night, but then I got up because I had to walk the dog and I wanted to watch the fireworks at the fairgrounds next door. Walked the dog on the driving range in the near-dark at about 9pm. Barefoot, nice soft slightly damp grass, stars just coming out...very nice. I stayed up too late watching the fireworks, though - didn't get to bed til around 10:15 and had to be up at 4am. On the up-side, had no trouble sleeping last night, nor am I likely to tonight!

### ealachan — 2012-07-22T01:37:24+0300

ate lunch around 2:30 - can of sardines smashed up with some lemon juice and green onion. It was not one of my finer creations. Next time I'll just eat the sardines straight.

Dinner was too early at 6pm but hubs was hungry so I made it and ate with him. Had rainbow chard, half a sweet potato each (mashed with lots of KG), and pork chops. He has specifically asked for cod for dinner again soon, so that was encouraging!
