Haven't had that happen in a long time, so I dunno if I maybe just drank a lot more water than normal yesterday or what. Stayed up rather than going back to bed, since we have three 4am wakeups in a row this week.

### ealachan — 2012-07-23T12:27:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-40368>

**leptin reset, day 144**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, bp coffee

l: -

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was a scrambled eggs with onion, red pepper and mushroom, and a couple slices of ham. ate late and then had to go to bed early, but it didn't keep me awake at all, so that's a plus at least.

\- was in bed by 8.30 last night and asleep by 9ish. Didn't hear a thing until I woke up at 2am needing a pee (gotta stop drinking a bunch of water right before bed). Went right back to sleep after that and woke up at 4am to take hubs to work. I had every intention of going to do the laundry this morning at 6 when the laundromat opens, but realized I don't have any cash and I'm broke until I get paid on Wednesday, so that plan got scuppered.

\- feeling mentally messed-up lately. lots of thought weirdness and just general unhappiness. So that's fun.../sarcasm....

### ealachan — 2012-07-23T15:45:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-40384>

Someone that I love posting on facebook about doing stupid Herbal-life liquid diets pissed me right off this morning. Long walk with the doggie and a cold, cold shower this morning improved my mood some. Funny how that works.

I decided on my walk that I want to try to be this new person that I've been fumbling around at being for the past couple weeks. I want to be someone who gets up early every day, not just the days that I HAVE to. I want to be someone who takes the dog for a long walk first thing in the morning. I want to be someone who can get stuff done and still have some time left for herself. I don't want to waste the majority of every day sitting thinking "if only I could do X, Y and Z, then I'd be happy". X, Y and Z might never materialize, and then what? Why not just decide to be happy with what I have, when I have it?

I need to keep reminding myself that anybody who claims to be perfect is spewing total bull\*\*\*\*, because none of us are or ever will be. I need to stop using other peoples' lives as the yardstick against which I measure my own.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-24T08:05:27+0300

>
>
>
> I need to keep reminding myself that anybody who claims to be perfect is spewing total bull\*\*\*\*, because none of us are or ever will be. I need to stop using other peoples' lives as the yardstick against which I measure my own.

Amen, sister! Took a life-threatening illness for me to wake up to this one. Love that you've got the wisdom now.

### ealachan — 2012-07-24T12:48:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-40621>

> **KiwiLauren;40803 wrote:** Amen, sister! Took a life-threatening illness for me to wake up to this one. Love that you've got the wisdom now.

I think the real work may be not so much in realizing the wisdom, but actually applying it to one's life and not letting one's self slip back into that comparing-to-others mode. My therapist (who I haven't seen in a long time...hmmm) and I used to talk a LOT about how learning how to live the life you want to live can sometimes be your life's work. I'm an incredibly impatient person at times, and I often feel like if I'm not good at something right off the bat, there's no point in continuing to do it.

I need to practice patience...but it takes patience to practice...

### ealachan — 2012-07-24T13:14:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-40624>

**Leptin Reset, Day 145**

B (4:45am): taco omlette - used up leftover taco-spiced ground beef from last night's dinner, some cheese, and salsa, folded into an omlette. bp coffee.

L: ?

D: dunno yet.

\- did end up eating lunch yesterday. Not sure why I thought I could go 13 hours (4:45am to 5:45pm) without eating anything at all...that was kind of silly. I know other people do it, and I might be able to someday too, but right now 9 or 10 hours without food is pretty much my limit. Plus, I needed to get out of the office for a little while yesterday and lunch was a good excuse. I had some sliced pastrami and too much chocolate. Today I'm going to bring some sardines with me and eat those instead, because I know if I go to the store I'll just get things I don't need.

\- dinner last night was taco salads - spiced ground beef, lettuce, cheese, avocado, salsa, black olives, onion. It was good and pretty quick, but for some reason it made hub's sugar spike! Maybe it was too much protein / fat all at once and his body converted it to glucose? He also had a yogurt after dinner, but it was only like 18g of carbs...would that have done it? We were so happy before dinner when he tested, because he was down to 112, but then an hour or so late, it was up to 192, and was still in the 170's this morning. I need to remind myself that we're only four days into this, and that it will take time for things to settle down and even out. His system is likely still recovering from all the crap he was eating this time last week.

\- noticed yesterday that my tightest pair of jeans in current rotation (don't all women have a bunch of different sizes of pants, and rotate smaller or larger ones in and out as necessary? lol) is actually not tight at all anymore - not on the waist, not in the hips (where everything is ALWAYS tight on me because that's where I'm biggest), not even in the thighs. That was a rather pleasant revelation! Also, someone has stolen rather a substantial amount of my boob fat. hard to describe, but they're different, trust me. Getting some flabby loose skin on my sides and the tops of my thighs, now. Measurements are not drastically different, but things look and feel different, for sure.

\- weird little patch of rash on the inside of my right wrist...showed up yesterday morning. Can't figure out what it might be. It's just a dozen or so tiny little raised red bumps, scattered across about a 2" square patch. Vaguely itchy if I touch them, but otherwise don't even really know they're there. It's actually the exact spot on my wrist that rests on the laptop when I'm typing on it, I just noticed. Hmmmmm...suddenly allergic to the laptop?! Doesn't make much sense.

\- Was in bed by 9 last night, but was up several times before I finally got settled and got to sleep, as we had a whopper of a storm system come through and the wind knocked a bunch of stuff off my book shelves and table, and scared the crap out of the dog. Slept pretty well once I did get to sleep, and was up at 4am without too much trouble. May see about getting out of work a little bit early today, though. This eating dinner at 6pm and going to bed at 8:30 crap is not ideal.

\- have been fantasizing about swimming a lot more than usual lately. Maybe from the heat and humidity, but it's gone way past "gee, I'd like to take a swim to cool off". It's more like full-on lusting for big cold bodies of water. I see a river or lake as I'm driving and I start looking for possible swimming areas. I think I need to find my bathing suit and go jump in some water before I drive myself crazy...lol

I keep trying to be a cheerleader and tell him how good he's doing and how much better he will eventually feel. I just hope I'm eventually proven right!

### ealachan — 2012-07-24T19:52:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-40748>

Tin of smoked herring for lunch. 6.7oz tin contained:

385 cal

28g fat (3.5g saturated)

122.5mg cholesterol

805g sodium

0g carbs, of course

38.5g protein

Ingredients: wild caught sustainable harvested naturally wood smoked herring fillets, cracked black pepper, salt

They're my favorite brand - Bar Harbor Foods (barharborfoods.com), and they're available even in WalMart around here. If you see them, try them...they're really good! I've decided I definitely prefer herring to sardines. Not so helpful in the short-term because I have 3 tins of sardines left to use up...but still!

### ealachan — 2012-07-25T12:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-40953>

**Leptin Reset, Day 146**

B: 2 chicken sausages, three eggs, bp coffee

L: feeeeeeesh!

D: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was beef rib-eye, sweet potato mash w/ butter, and some sauteed mushrooms and onions. Yum! Also had a serving of 70% dark chocolate for dessert.

\- stayed up a bit too late for the webinar, but it was totally worth it. Got to bed around 10:15, zonked out pretty quickly, slept like a log until Hubs spoke to me at 4am to wake me up. It's interesting - a few months ago, when I'd have to get up at 4am, Hubs would have to come in and wake me up two or three times before I'd finally get up, and then I'd be stumbling around for a while. Nowadays, he comes in, touches my arm or side and just quietly says "time to get up", and I yawn, stretch, put my glasses on and get right up! I don't necessarily feel like running any 5K's at 4 in the morning, but I'm not stumbling around incoherently anymore like I used to. It's kind of awesome.

\- another interesting thing - yesterday I ate that tin of fish for lunch, and I felt way less tired all afternoon / evening. I was still hungry by 5:30 (I ate around 12:30), but I didn't spend all afternoon staring at my computer screen thinking "sweet fancy Moses, I just want to go home and sleep" like i have in the past.

\- webinar was very informative, and very glad I invested in it. Now to invest in some sea veg! I like nori, but I don't have much experience with other sea veg, so this should be interesting. Also really need to start making bone broth regularly. Going to have a sniff around locally and see if I can score a used pressure cooker for cheap.

\- we had several more days of oppressive heat and humidity, but a front came through late yesterday evening, and this morning is beautiful - clear skies, light breeze, nice dry air and slightly chilly temp! Can't wait to leash the doggie up and have a traipse around the driving range.

### quelsen — 2012-07-25T16:31:41+0300

i think it has to do with the way that they toxins release and the manner in which your cells die off.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-25T20:43:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41051>

I'm curious, did you get a chance to reread both blogs? Are you going to try to do the LS way of things now? I'm sure it would be a scary thought to change what is working now but Jack says you will have amazing results when you flip over if you are becoming LS. You could also try some HIIT to see how your muscles react and to know if T3 is getting into the cells. If you are overwhelmingly sore, than go back, but if not push forward.

### ealachan — 2012-07-25T21:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41058>

> **vkiernan;41266 wrote:** I'm curious, did you get a chance to reread both blogs? Are you going to try to do the LS way of things now? I'm sure it would be a scary thought to change what is working now but Jack says you will have amazing results when you flip over if you are becoming LS. You could also try some HIIT to see how your muscles react and to know if T3 is getting into the cells. If you are overwhelmingly sore, than go back, but if not push forward.

I did go back and re-read, yes. I'm still iffy on the whole thing, because he's quite clear in both the Rx blog and the FAQ blog that if you're overweight, you've got some level of leptin resistance....but aside from that, I don't see where he says anywhere that you can't exercise if you're not 100% leptin-sensitive. He does mention that one shouldn't IF until one is definitely leptin-sensitive, though.

The funny thing is, I think I pretty much already eat like the Post-Rx blog post is laid out! I eat a breakfast with a lot of fat (and protein right now), I hardly ever eat lunch (and on days I do, it's generally small), and dinner is always protein-heavy. I'm usually below 50g of carbs a day, and I'm ALWAYS below 100g unless I've really gone off the rails (which certainly happens, but I've never felt like I needed to start all over again, if you see what I mean. I've always been able to just get right back to plan). Pretty much the only Post-Rx part I'm not currently doing is lifting / HIIT. I do walk a little bit every day - usually about an hour's worth total (according to my pedometer), and it's strolling with the dog or walking around at work, not power-walking. I usually do 15-20 minutes with the dog in the morning, then another 10-20 min with him in the evening, and the rest of my walking is moving around normally at home or work. I average higher on the weekends when I'm not stuck at a desk...

Anyway...I don't know. I'm torn. I want to run off and lift weights and do push-ups and be Super Woman, but on the other hand, this is the best I've felt in like 3 or 4 years, and I'm kind of scared to screw that up. I can't imagine just a few sessions of lifting or HIIT would send me back to the dark ages, though.

I wish the effing lab wasn't an hour away. I need to get my \*\*\*\* together and get some labs done, which should answer a lot of these questions.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-25T22:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41067>

> I did go back and re-read, yes. I'm still iffy on the whole thing, because he's quite clear in both the Rx blog and the FAQ blog that if you're overweight, you've got some level of leptin resistance.

This is before you go on the reset. That does not mean after you start the reset and become LS. I think a lot of people confuse this statement. I have read a lot of the same comments by others just as you stated.

### skywaykate — 2012-07-25T22:40:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41071>

I was chatting w the librarian while my girls played at our ah-mazing library and eventually Paleo came up and we talked about it (among other things, I was inquiring about finding out if they were going to get Practical Paleo when it comes out bc it looks like a really good resource, but I need to save the money right now) and she said that she just found out she was almost diabetic and said she really liked this book that she showed me, and as she flipped thru it, it had a lot about practical things, real life, re-working your thought patterns and being mindful of eating kinds of things, it looked good. I have no idea if any of the diet advice is actually good, and from the title probably not all of it is, but the vast majority was not "diet" advice...I thought of how your husband has to rethink his food choices and so I snapped a pic for you (otherwise I wouldn't remember the name wo a lot of work, lol!).

Honestly, the subheadings in the book seemed like a lot of the tools I was taught/expanded on in my time working w the dietician at the eating disorder program, and that stuff has helped me have a better relationship w food and overeat less, etc. Might be worth checking out.

### ealachan — 2012-07-26T16:49:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41196>

**Leptin Reset, Day 147**

b (6:30am): 7ish oz steak, two eggs, BP coffee

l: -

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was shrimp and cauliflower in thai red curry sauce (red curry paste, coconut milk, fish sauce, water, cilantro). I ate about half a pound of shrimp, all told. It was delicious, although I got a particularly young / strong batch of cilantro and the taste was kind of over-powering at times.

\- Excellent oxytocin therapy session last night! Was zonked out with my ice packs by 9:15. Slept great, woke up about 10 minutes before the alarm was set to go off (6am), got right up like a champ and took the dog out even though my husband offered to do it himself. Adult points for me!

\- hoping I won't need lunch today, since I didn't eat breakfast until 6:30.

\- very excited to report that husband's FBG was in the 130's this morning. That's the lowest it's been since we started on this odyssey, so we're feeling quite encouraged that we're headed down the right path!

\- third day in a row of not just cool but COLD shower in the morning. Tap turned all the way to cold, and I let the water beat directly on my stomach for like 5 minutes straight. Skin was pinky and down to 68 degrees when I got out, and it felt AMAZING. I was covered in goosebumps and raring to go! Cranked the A/C up in the car on the way to work, too. The cold makes me feel almost giddy. It's wonderful.

\- tiny mystery rash on inside of my wrist has faded a lot since I stopped using the old manky wrist-rest on my desk while mousing at work! Don't know why that would make me break out, but the correlation seems pretty direct to me.

This is good that you asked as others will now guage themselves against your progress. Hope you aren't mad at me for pushing you. I'm really surprised by his answer.

Not mad at all, no!

He kind of lost me a little bit with " ...you know what needs to be done to show it in 3 D". I'm guessing he's talking about tightening up my diet more in order to drop more weight, or possibly he's talking about more CT. His last sentence about wanting to know what's going on in the Ferarri is his way of telling me to get the damn labs done...THAT I get...hahaha!

I wonder if running up the stairs at work multiple times counts as HIIT. It's certainly a lot easier than it used to be - they're STEEP and I used to feel like I was going to keel over when I got to the top just going normal speed...couldn't even say more than good morning to my co-workers for a couple minutes after I'd climbed them -but now I can scurry up them pretty quickly and only be slightly out of breath for not even a minute after. But that's after just one trip. I'm pretty sure I'd find multiple trips one right after the other to be challenging!

### ealachan — 2012-07-27T16:27:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41521>

**Leptin Reset, Day 148**

b: 7ish oz steak (which is now thankfully gone), two eggs, bp coffee

L: -

D: fish, goddamnit!

\- didn't get to bed until 10pm last night. Slept really well (although BIZARRE dreams...holy crap) until 12:30 when I had to go get Hubs. Was back in bed w/ ice packs on neck by 1am and was asleep within 10 minutes or so. More weird-ass dreams (my boss and his snotty teenagers at my house drinking my iced tea...WTF?!), woke up at 6:30. Was a little slow this morning (very grey and rainy the last couple days), but got up and walked the doggie rather than letting hubs do it.

\- woke up with a weird crick in my right shoulder. Super annoying, because I'm right-handed and I mouse and type all day long at work. Wish I'd have thought to bring an ice pack with me.

\- got a couple new shirts in the mail yesteday. Got my "normal" size - 26/28 - and two of the three shirts are big. One is verging on TOO big, but I think that's just the style of the shirt. The other is just comfortably roomy. The third fits fine. Losing weight is bad for my checkbook, because I really like new clothes...especially when I can get them on sale...

\- hubs got his schedule for next week and it's all open shifts! Good thing I've been getting more used to getting up early...eeek!

\- beef and broccoli that I made for dinner last night made his blood sugar go up / stay up more than it has been doing lately. I think it might have been from the cornstarch I put into the sauce without even thinking about it...whoops. It was only a tablespoon divided over 4 servings, but that's the only thing I can think of that would have done it...?

### ealachan — 2012-07-27T19:33:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41537>

I was just in the bathroom and happened to catch my reflection in the mirror and realized that I have noticebly less of a double-chin. I may have done a happy dance right there in the bathroom at work when I noticed it...

Also, I have muscles in my feet. I can SEE them. And my right ankle, which has had an annoying puffy pad of fat or fluid or something on it for ages, has started to shrink.

HOT DAMN, PEOPLE!

### ealachan — 2012-07-28T04:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41623>

### ealachan — 2012-07-28T15:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41659>

**Leptin Reset, Day 149**

_In which our heroine has fish for breakfast and likes it..._

b: big bowl of last night's leftover fish soup, bp coffee

l: maybe a salad...not sure yet. We're out at a card event today.

d: not sure yet

\- The fish soup was even better for breakfast than it was for dinner last night. I definitely have to make that again. Next time I'll be better prepared and it should go more smoothly, hopefully. It was just a fly-by-the-seat-of-my-pants thing last night that happened to work out well. It could have very easily turned into a $30-worth-of-seafood disaster! But sometimes that's the thrilling part...

\- stayed up a bit too late watching the Olympics. Got to bed around 10:30, slept hard until 12:30, went to get Hubs, was back in bed with ice packs on neck by 1am. Went right back to sleep, and slept hard again (more really bizarre dreams...this is apparently the week for them?) until the dog woke me up around 6:45 this morning. Hubs was about to leash the dog up and walk him, but I sent him back to bed and did it myself.

\- Had a strange low-back-of-the-skull headache on and off all day yesterday, and my ears were aching intermittently as well. Not sure if it was all due to tiredness or impending Shark Week issues or what, but it wasn't pleasant. I've upped my K2 supplement this week...could that have caused it? I went from 90mcg to 180mcg, as I've been reading a lot about K2 and fatty liver disease (which i don't have, but I also don't want).

\- laying in bed last night, I had my hand on my stomach and I noticed that it feels smaller, deflated. below-waist belly has the same deflated feeling. Maybe from the week or so of letting cold water beat on it every morning?

### ealachan — 2012-07-30T18:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-41946>

**Leptin Reset, Day 151**

b (4:45am): can of salmon mixed into three scrambled eggs, some hot sauce (Sriracha...nectar of the gods!), bp coffee

l: chicken sausage and some raw green beans from my dad's garden

d: not sure yet.

\- yesterday was fairly uneventful - had eggs and homemade pork sausage for breakfast at 4:45am, had some shaved steak with lots of mushrooms and some onion and green pepper for lunch, and a couple of uncured organic hot dogs (seems like an oxymoron, I know) and a big green salad for dinner. Oh, and a low-sugar ice cream bar. Not a huge fan of those.

\- oxytocin therapy before bed last night...w00t! Zonked out with ice packs on neck by 9ish. Slept hard until about 3am, then woke up because I was laying funny and my hand had gone numb...lol Spent a confused 15-20 min trying to thaw out my hand and get comfortable again, which was annoying. Then I had to get up at 4 anyway. Was able to get up and get going with no problem, but feeling kind of tired now.

\- Pretty sure I've lost about a cup size from my boobs so far. They look kind of sad, but I keep telling myself it's for the best.  Have also hit the annoying in-between-jeans-sizes stage, where the jeans I'm used to wearing are now too big, but the smaller-sized jeans I have in stock don't fit yet.

\- I feel like we're starting to get a better grip on Hubs' blood sugar, finally. He was below 120 every time he tested yesterday, for the first time since he started testing. Doctor's appt is tomorrow. Hoping we can show her that we're getting it under control with diet and see if we can avoid meds for now. He's been really good about sticking to plan and I'm proud of him. We're both going to have a hard time when we're on vacation, but we're going into it with a can-do attitude, which should hopefully help.

\- Got some Nutiva coconut oil yesterday. It was $20 for the jar, but I can see why people pay that price for it, now. SO YUMMY!

### ealachan — 2012-07-31T18:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42058>

**Leptin Reset, Day 152**

_In which Shark Week rears its ugly head..._

b: 7ish oz steak, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was SO not optimal. I had a weird hankering for curried chicken salad, so I bought a plain rotisserie chicken, stripped the skin off, chopped up the meat, added some chopped shallots and red pepper, a bunch of curry powder, and...this is where \*\*\*\* starts to go downhill fast: mayo. Not even home-made mayo, but the \*\*\*\*ty store-bought stuff. I knew, I KNEW...that it wasn't going to end well, but I was tired and hungry and hot and just did it anyway. I mixed it all up and plunked it on big beds of baby spinach, and it actually tasted pretty ok...but oh, it was not ok in my guts. Holy hell. It sat like a curried chicken BRICK in my stomach for hours and just made me feel GROSS. I was not a happy bunny. LESSON LEARNED. Blech.

\- was in bed by 9ish with ice packs, no oxytocin therapy last night because Shark Week started. Boo. Had to get up and take some advil around 12:30am because cramps woke me up. Slept until about 6:15ish this morning. No doggie walk for me this morning because Hubs had already gotten up and done it when I got up.

\- went to the doctor with Hubs this morning for his diabetes follow-up. She looked at the testing numbers he's been getting, her eyes went big, and she said "well I'm not putting you on medication with these numbers. What ever you've been doing for the past week and a half, keep doing it." She wants him back in 3 months to check his cholesterol because his HDL was low on the last round of labs, but other than that she was pretty happy with us. She looked a bit aghast when she asked what he'd had for breakfast and he told her "steak and eggs", but whatever. Quite pleased that we were able to get out of there with no medication, and with a big pat on the back for taking control of the situation.  I am however a little peeved that the "Living With Diabetes" literature that she gave Hubs talks about how it's ok to have snack-sized candy bars and diet soda. WTF?! He was like "look, this says I can have these things!" and I said "we're not listening to what most of that crap says." He was fairly disappointed. He's craving sweets pretty bad lately. I need to figure out something to do or give him that will help.

\- Doctor's office scale says both Hubs and I are down 6lbs from last time...woo!

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-31T19:01:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42072>

Hmmm. Sweet snack ... how about cococrack?

I use Tropical Traditions coconut oil because it is beyond yummy. I get a gallon tub for about $70 but it lasts a long time. It's STRAIGHT oil. website is tropicaltraditions.com -- read their story, I think you'll like it and them.

ANYway. Mix melted coconut oil with unsweetened, melted chocolate or your choice of dark cocoa to taste. It won't take much ... about 1 cup of oil and couple of squares of chocolate or 1/4 cup cocoa powder. Stir in your choice of sweetener (except of course SUGAR). drop by small spoonfuls onto waxed paper and chill or use one of those mini cupcake pans (I prefer the silicon ones because it's much easier to remove the little gobbets of chocolate). Word of warning, if use use the chocolate BAR you won't be able to get the sweetener to blend in (you know, oil and water yadda yadda). You can add some chopped macadamias if you want or a handful of TT's coconut flakes. mmmm tasty. Once you have it you'll know why it's called cococrack.

Tasty and good for you.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-31T19:16:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42077>

[http://www.amazon.com/Dr-Bernsteins-Diabetes-Solution-Achieving/dp/0316182699/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1343751278&sr=8-1&keywords=dr+bernstein+diabetes+solution](http://http://www.amazon.com/Dr-Bernsteins-Diabetes-Solution-Achieving/dp/0316182699/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1343751278&sr=8-1&keywords=dr+bernstein+diabetes+solution)

It doesn't come off as a "low carb" and a few Diabetics I know that have gone paleo etc think this is da bomb. It's more Krusy.

Sounds good! I used to make a similar thing with coconut oil, cocoa, cinnamon and coconut flakes, but no sweetener, and it was pretty good. I might mix up a batch of this with some stevia and see if he'll eat it. I'm generally not a fan of stevia because most of the kinds I've tried have had a horrible weird chemical taste to them, but the Stevia In The Raw stuff I got recently doesn't seem to have the additive that all the other kinds have, and it's not too bad.

### ealachan — 2012-08-01T16:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42245>

**Leptin Reset, Day 153**

_In which much fish is eaten..._

B: can of salmon, three scrambled eggs, Sriracha sauce, BP coffee, piece of cocoa crack

L: will try to go without

D: dunno yet.

\- dinner last night was half a pound of baked cod and some sweet potato mashed with butter and a little salt. I made up a batch of cocoa crack (3oz unsweetened Baker's chocolate and 6 tablespoons of coconut oil melted together, added 1 tsp cinnamon, a packet or so of Stevia In The Raw, an ounce of chopped-up macadamia nuts and an ounce or so of shredded unsweetened coconut) in the hopes that it would sate Hubs' dessert cravings, but he was not a fan. It didn't help that the stevia all seemed to settle in the bottom rather than dissolving in the mixture. I still like it and will eat it, but it's a no-go for him. Back to the drawing board.

\- got to bed around 9 last night, ice packs on neck, went right to sleep. I don't think I laid there even 5 minutes before I drifted off. LOVE THAT!  Woke up a couple times briefly in the night but only for a couple minutes each time, then zonked right back out. Was up at 4am like a champ.

\- walked on the driving range with the doggie for 15 min or so this morning. It was nice and sunny and they'd just cut the grass yesterday, so we didn't get soaked with dew. I did a sprint, too! I did a 50-yard sprint (the driving range is conveniently marked off in yards, woo!) on the flattest part of the range, and I lived to tell the tale! But then I realized I'm not supposed to be exercising until evening, so I didn't sprint anymore. May go out and try it in the driveway tonight, though.

\- have been noticing the last few days that I'm shedding hair like crazy. It's growing really fast and looks / feels really healthy, but it also appears to be falling out a lot all of a sudden. WTF? I know it's normal to shed up to 100 hairs a day...wondering if this is just a seasonal thing maybe, and it looks /seems like a lot more because I'm not used to it? I counted at least 25 hairs this morning, not counting what was in the drain in the shower when I got out. It doesn't seem like it's coming out in clumps, either...I just keep coming up with hair wound around my fingers every time I run my fingers through my hair or comb it.

\- feel fat and bloated today, even though nothing has really changed. Stupid hormones.

### ealachan — 2012-08-02T15:47:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42488>

**Leptin Reset, Day 154**

_In which our heroine...runs?!_

B: taco omlette - 3 eggs, leftover taco-spiced ground beef (I make my own spice mix, can't stand the packaged stuff!), cheese, salsa...and BP coffee

L: probably a salad. Have to go out for lunch for a work thing. Not thrilled.

D: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was taco salad, and it spiked the hell out of Hubs' blood sugar again even though I was careful to read all the labels, measure out all the portions, etc. I think it's just got to be something to do with the combination of everything together that's setting him off, because none of the individual components are high in carbs at all. So we won't be having that again, now that we know for sure it's going to spike him.

\- may have solved the dessert dilemma in the short-term with sugar-free Jello, but won't know until he tries it tonight. I'm sure it's probably not good for us, but right now I just need to help him get past the "OMG SO DEPRIVED NEED SUGAR" phase of this process.

\- was in bed by 8 last night...LOL! Read for about 20 minutes with the blue-blockers on and then literally couldn't keep my eyes open, so I gave up and went to sleep. I was probably out cold by about 8:45. Slept with ice packs on neck (they don't feel that cold anymore even though they're frozen...waaaah! I think I'm just used to them now), woke up once in the middle of the night to drop the warmed-up packs on the floor, then slept hard again until 4am when it was time to get up.

\- abbreviated walk with the pup this morning because I screwed around and left it too late to get going (I was dicking around on the internet...oops). We jogged halfway back to the house (maybe 100 yards?) just for funsies and I didn't fall over dead, so that was good. Also, I did 20 wall-push-ups in the shower, randomly. The water was wicked cold and I just felt hyper and like doing something, so I did some wall-push-ups. Rar!

\- measured my neck this morning and it's down to 15". It was 15.5" for months and months, but the nightly icing seems to have shrunk it a bit.

\- Belly and waist both look and feel smaller, but are measuring exactly the same as before, which means my mind is playing tricks on me. ANNOYING.

### ealachan — 2012-08-03T16:38:15+0300

I like AM dog walking now way more than I did a month ago... Yes, I was very pleased with the work schedule thing. ### ealachan — 2012-08-03T16:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42671>

> **Cavemam;42871 wrote:** My A/C is broken! Tell me what this head/neck CT for sleep thing is you are doing? Bag of ice? Gel pack? Make it something I can whip up for tonight! I'll take my cold bath right before bed which will help - but I need my icy cave for sleep!

Sorry, I didn't have the magical Internets box on after I posted last night, so didn't see this 'til this morning! What I use are [these](http://http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000FEFJ2A/ref=oh_details_o04_s00_i00). They're 7.95 for a pack of two, and they work REALLY well, so far. They're not super big, but they seem like the perfect size for my neck. I use two at a time - one on each side. They're flexible, so you can mold them to fit right against the curve of your neck, which is nice. I have an old T-shirt that I wrap them in so that they're not directly on the skin. They stay quite cold for a good 30-45 minutes, then they're just kind of cool for another half hour or so...but that's also in my air-conditioned (68-72 average) bedroom. They would probably not last as long in a hotter room.

I can't friggin' wait for winter. I always sleep way better in the winter...and now I understand why!

### ealachan — 2012-08-03T16:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42676>

**Leptin Reset, Day 155**

_In which our heroine appreciates that getting to sleep in until 6am now feels like a luxury..._

B: two MSG-free organic pork sausages (19g protein each), two eggs, bp coffee with too! much! butter!

L: -

d: not sure yet

\- - Had a pretty naughty day yesterday. Friend took me out for lunch and I had a sandwich...and actually ate some of the bread. Then I had dessert. Then, the little food truck that's right on my way home was doing meatloaf as a special for dinner last night and it was too hot to cook anyway, so I picked up two orders of their meatloaf and mashed potatoes for dinner. I will say this about my naughtiness: the lunch? Totally not worth it...but the dinner? SO WORTH IT. It was really only the mashed potatoes that were a big issue, and they DID send Hubs' sugar up pretty bad...but it was a once-in-a-great-while treat for us, and hey...it had to be better than ice cream and beer, right?

\- Speaking of which...another week with no beer! Last beer was on 7/17, so it's been 2.5 weeks. Go me!

\- didn't go to bed til 8:45 last night, and read for half an hour or so, then zonked out with ice packs. Don't remember waking up at all in the night. Dog woke me up at 6am (day off for Hubs today) and I got right up and took him out, enjoyed the sun coming up over the ridge, and just generally had a good walk. The driving range was really wet so we didn't for a long walk this morning, though.

\- need to think of something fishy and not too heat-intensive to have for supper tonight. It's supposed to be 95 degrees here today, and my kitchen isn't air conditioned. Maybe I should have saved the cheat-day for today...LOL

### ealachan — 2012-08-03T23:58:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42767>

Hopefully starting yoga class again on Tuesday, wheeee! I haven't been for about a year and a half. It was a good group back then - fun, not super serious, but still a good workout. I wonder if I'll find it more difficult now or easier. I'm about the same weight now as when I stopped going last time (because I was super sick with bronchitis...not this past winter, but the winter before).

### ealachan — 2012-08-04T16:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-42835>

**Leptin Reset, Day 156**

B: one sausage (no msg, 19g protein), two eggs, bp coffee w/ a little hwc because I ran out of butter

L: dunno yet - may try to skip

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night ended up NOT being fish. Fail. We had take-out salads instead - greens, tomato, onion, bacon, grilled chicken, a little cheese, nasty dressing. The salads themselves were quite good but the dressing was ick. We split half a bar of Godiva 72% dark chocolate for dessert.

\- Was in bed by 8:15ish and read until close to 9 with ice packs on neck. Got up and switched the packs out for fresh ones before I went to sleep. Slept well until about 2am, when I woke up thinking it was 4. Checked the time, then tossed and turned for a while. Woke up at 4 feeling more groggy than I have in a month or so, so I guess the combination of staying up too late and waking up early wasn't the best. At least I don't have to be at work doing any critical thinking today.

\- good walk around the driving range with the pup, although the grass was really wet and we got soaked! plus, it was really misty, so we didn't even get any sun-in-the-eyes benefit. Kind of a rip-off...lol

\- mouth and throat have felt really dry the last few days. I think it's maybe from all the air conditioning at home, work and in the car, since it's been so damn hot.

\- headed into "town" (which is actually 3 towns away) this afternoon to run some errands, and hoping to hit one of the bigger grocery stores to check out the fish selection. I'm all out of canned salmon and frozen shrimp...need to stock up! Plus I wanted to see if I could find some roe. I've never had it, but I figure worst case scenario, I can take spoonfuls of it like medicine daily.

### ealachan — 2012-08-05T13:47:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43024>

**Leptin Reset, Day 157**

b: one beef burger with a little cheese, two eggs, bp coffee w/ a little hwc instead of butter because I'm out

l: dunno

d: dunno

\- ended up going out for lunch yesterday. We were really good, though! Hubs had baked cod, and I had a caeser salad with salmon on it. We skipped appetizers AND dessert....go us!  For dinner we had burgers (1 each with a bit of cheese) and some steamed summer squash with butter on it. Sugar-free Jello with some unsweetened whipped cream for dessert.

\- Was in bed by 8:15. Going to bed early really cuts down on the need for the blue-blocker glasses...LOL I read for 20 min or so with the glasses on, then zonked out. Ice packs on. The dog got a little barky at some point in the night and woke me up, but I went right back to sleep after that and don't remember hearing anything else until 3:45 when DH's alarm went off. Dozed back off for about 20 minutes after that, then woke right up at 4:05 like someone flipped a switch. Weird.

\- Crappy weather today, even at 6am, ugh. Muggy and buggy...my two lease favorite things when being outside. Fought through the humidity and the bugs around the driving range with the pup, but we were both fed up about halfway through our normal route and so we cut it a little short. Still got a good 10 minutes or so in, though. Getting dark now and looks like it might storm. I wouldn't mind a quiet rainy Sunday, to be honest.

### ealachan — 2012-08-06T12:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43171>

**Leptin Reset, Day 158**

b: burger (no cheese!), three eggs, bp coffee (with butter! I restocked...hehe)

L: going to try to go without

d: dunno yet

\- ended up eating lunch yesterday - a salad with some sliced deli turkey and dressing made of greek yogurt with a little onion powder and garlic powder added (it's essentially french onion dip, but thinner and not from a packet). Dinner was hot dogs (the uncured organic ones, at least!), steamed summer squash with butter, and some spinach sauteed with garlic.

\- didn't amount to much yesterday aside from a couple of dog walks and an hour of house cleaning. I am NOT a domestic goddess in the least, so actually putting the laptop down, setting the timer for one hour, and spending that hour cleaning was like Super Adult Time for me. I got a lot done, too! I need to start doing this regularly. It's not like we live in filth or anything...there's just a lot of clutter usually, and I always feel better when the clutter is picked up / put away. It's calming to have a clean house.

\- took a long cold shower last night because it was so stinking hot in here. Did 20 wall push-ups while in the shower. It's really funny because I can be so miserable and hot and tired and gross-feeling, but after a few minutes in the cold water, I get a surge of energy. I routinely dance around in the shower when it's cold, goofing off like a little kid, making faces and giggling to myself.

\- went to bed around 7:30 because the bedroom is air conditioned and I was sick of being hot! Read for a while, had some oxytocin therapy (yaaaaaay!), probably got to sleep around 9:30ish. Didn't wake up on my own this morning, hubs had to come in and speak to me, and I was feeling pretty groggy for a few minutes there but perked up after that.

\- My boss is on vacation this week, so my stress level is about to go wayyyyyyyy down.  I only have to be around him for a day when he gets back, then I go on vacation. Couldn't have planned that better if I tried...

### ealachan — 2012-08-06T17:05:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43207>

Two 50-yard sprints this morning, btw! I figured doing them in the morning is better than not doing them at all, which is currently my other option. No clue if I'm doing them effectively or not, honestly. I walk a few minutes around the outside of the driving range with the dog, then we double-back and cut across the middle to the 150-yard sign, and run from there to the 100-yard sign. Then I walked from the 100-yard sign to the 50-yard sign and then ran from that to the end of the range. It certainly leaves me panting, but it's not like I have to stop moving completely to catch my breath, if that makes sense. I'm doing it barefoot, as well. I've never been a runner, even as a kid, so I'm sure my form leaves a lot to be desired...

Interestingly, I checked my blood sugar before I went out and then after I came back in, and it had gone UP 10 points. Is that normal, or is that weird?

### ealachan — 2012-08-07T17:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43426>

**Leptin Reset, Day 159**

B: leftover shrimp and cauli mash from last night's dinner (a dozen or so large shrimp and less than a quarter cup of mash, plus the buttery fatty sauce), along with two eggs and some kielbasa (was for the husband...I have grown to hate it), BP coffee

L: -

D: not sure yet

\- as mentioned up-thread, dinner last night was super good and full of shrimp. Om nom nom!

\- went to bed around 9:15 last night, read for a while, got to sleep around 10ish. Slept like a rock until 4am, at which point I woke right up and then realized I didn't have to be up for another couple hours. I slept kind of fitfully from 4am to 6:15ish, mostly because the dog was antsy and wanted to get up.

\- good walk with the dog this morning - no sunshine as it was still misty out, but it got down to about 50 degrees last night for the first time in ages, so it was nice and chilly out! The grass was very wet and cold on my bare feet. Pup got quite sodden and chilled about halfway through and demanded we take a short-cut across the range rather than around it, but he only weighs 10lbs so I can't really fault him for getting cold easily.

\- Cold, cold shower after chilly walk...eeeep! I kept saying to myself "suck it up, don't be a pussy, this is good for you", and of course I felt fantastic afterwards.

\- have noticed the last few days a couple of changes: I'm starting to see muscle in my chest (under my boobs...lol) and upper abs when I flex. Not actual definition, because I've still got a several-inch-thick layer of fat to work through, but things move now that didn't used to move. Also, my lower belly fat (I carry a lot of weight below the belly button in a big pooch, and also around my hips and butt) is changing shape. It's hard to describe without seeing it, and I won't subject you to pictures of it...but it's definitely changing.

\- I think I'm losing weight from the top down. Shoulders are more defined, starting to see just a hint of the possibility of collar-bones, neck is leaner, boobs have shrunk at least a cup size...but everything else is pretty much status-quo. Just have to keep telling myself that it will happen eventually...just need to be patient.

### ealachan — 2012-08-08T12:49:34+0300

**Leptin Reset, Day 160**

b: half a can of salmon, three slices of bacon, two eggs, bp coffee

L: -

d: not sure yet

\- was able to skip lunch yesterday, even with the smaller-than-usual breakfast...yay!

\- dinner last night was cod with tomato olive tapenade, on a bed of steamed spinach. It's SO GOOD. I had to use frozen cod because my grocery store didn't have any fresh, and the texture was a bit rubbery...but it was still very tasty. Also had half a glass of wine.

\- stayed up a little too late last night - didn't get to bed til almost 9 and had a little trouble dropping off to sleep, but slept like a log after I did finally zonk out. Was up at 4am. I think the reason I had trouble falling asleep last night was because I didn't wake up until 6:15 yesterday. I think my body is getting used to the 4am routine enough that when I sleep in a little, it throws things off.

\- trying to get stuff ready to go on vacation, and it's kind of stressy...we're going to Indianapolis for a big gaming convention next week. It will be good in that I'll get to see a lot of people I only see once every year or two, and there will be PLENTY of walking, but it's going to suck in terms of throwing off my sleep, my eating, and my stress levels. It's not a relaxing vacation. I have told Hubs that next year for vacation, I want to rent a house on the beach in Maine and just sit my ass on the beach eating lobster for a whole week. THAT is relaxing...

### vkiernan — 2012-08-08T14:58:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43628>

Yeah, more progress!!!! You may not be LS yet, but are on your way. Remember that while on vacation so it might help you stay in control just a bit more. I hope you have a fantastic time.

### ealachan — 2012-08-08T15:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43641>

> **vkiernan;43919 wrote:** Yeah, more progress!!!! You may not be LS yet, but are on your way. Remember that while on vacation so it might help you stay in control just a bit more. I hope you have a fantastic time.

Yeah, reminding myself that I've come a long way has really helped with motivation lately, honestly.

Then again, I measured this morning and I've somehow gained like 2" on my belly since the other day. That pissed me off...but I remember reading that some people have had bloat and bowel issues when first switching over to a fish-heavy diet, so I'm hoping that's all it is. I haven't actually gained any weight according to the scale...just see to have ballooned in the belly over the past couple days.

I'm workin' on it!  I only measure because I get excited because I feel so good, and I think "geez, I must have lost more weight", and then I want to quantify that. I need to work on letting go of my desires to quantify and to compare.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-08T16:34:51+0300

This is so exciting!!! Sounds like you are naturally adjusting your eating, and your tastes are changing. Clap Clap!!! Good luck on Vaca next week. I hesitate to ask what a "gaming" convention is. I think I know, but in case you don't want to ADMIT to being a furry

Are you flying?

### ealachan — 2012-08-08T16:52:40+0300

ROFL...I AM NOT A FURRY THANK YOU VERY MUCH! The gaming convention we're going to is GenCon. There's every game you can possibly think of there (and yes, probably some furries too), but we go for a card game that we play, and I also play some boardgames. It's a big social thing, too - lots of friends from the UK, Ireland and Europe usually come over, so it's a really good time.

Yes, we're flying. That's why my plane-seat goal is 8/14. I'm not going to fit the way I'd LIKE to fit, but I'll fit well enough to get out there and back. It's only a 2.5 hour flight, as well. Being squashed for that long isn't so bad. It's the 6-7 hour flights to the UK that being squashed really \*\*\*\*ing sucks.

### ealachan — 2012-08-09T15:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43867>

**Leptin Reset, Day 160**

b: half a can of salmon cooked in bacon fat, 3 strips of bacon, two eggs, BP coffee

L: -

D: not sure yet...fly by the seat of my pants, as always...

\- Ended up getting pretty hungry around 3pm yesterday and ate a couple hard-boiled eggs and drank a bottle of kombucha. As such, I decided to up my fat at breakfast this morning to see if it could get me through until dinner time. I added a little more CO and butter to my coffee, and I cooked my salmon in the bacon fat accumulated from frying the bacon. Fingers crossed!

\- dinner last night was rib-eye steak (my favorite!), steamed broccoli, and some sauteed onion, green pepper and mushrooms. An interesting thing happened: a) Instead of buying two individual steaks, I found a lovely one that was just over a pound and split it between the two of us. In the past, I have been quite able to sit down and inhale a pound of steak in a sitting easily, but last night the 7ish oz (after cooking) steak was quite enough. I was happily fully after supper, but not stuffed. I had no trouble just...being done. It was a nice feeling!

\- Got to sleep LATE (for me, anyway...like 10ish. I remember when 10 was the earliest I'd go to bed. Weird!), but it was for a good cause (oxytocin therapy), so I won't complain. At least, not right now. I might be complaining this afternoon when I'm fighting to stay awake...lol Anyway, slept like a log and woke up around 5:15. Hubs was home and walked the dog while I was in the bathroom, so I guess I'll have to make up for that later on. Was rushed to get breakfast and get ready to go, as hubs had to be to work at 7 and my whole routine was just kind of thrown off.

\- the 2+ inches of bloat that I observed yesterday morning were gone this morning. Crazy. Hopefully it stays away...not that I will continue measuring daily...ahem.

### ealachan — 2012-08-09T21:11:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43941>

Ate some beef jerky and drank a bottle of kombucha about and hour ago and now my heart is racing / pounding. Must have been something in the beef jerky, because the kombucha is the same kind I've had a few times before and it has never bothered me.

Guess it serves me right for snacking when I wasn't hungry, but I was so \*\*\*\*ing bored at work that I was ready to scream, so I had to walk over to the store for a change of scenery.

I've noticed something lately. The better my brain starts to work, the less interested in my job I become. It's incredibly boring. It doesn't require any brainpower at all. I could do SO much more than this, except that I can't afford to not work here. Lovely.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-09T21:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43943>

> **ealachan;44236 wrote:** Ate some beef jerky and drank a bottle of kombucha about and hour ago and now my heart is racing / pounding. Must have been something in the beef jerky, because the kombucha is the same kind I've had a few times before and it has never bothered me.
>
>
>
> Guess it serves me right for snacking when I wasn't hungry, but I was so \*\*\*\*ing bored at work that I was ready to scream, so I had to walk over to the store for a change of scenery.
>
>
>
> I've noticed something lately. The better my brain starts to work, the less interested in my job I become. It's incredibly boring. It doesn't require any brainpower at all. I could do SO much more than this, except that I can't afford to not work here. Lovely.

A M E N sista. The best thing I ever did was work for myself. Of course, I did get married and my hubby was able to support us during the first couple of years (in RE those years are a negative income - you PAY for the honor).

Start using that time to daydream about what you REALLLLLLLY want to be doing. I know a lot of people on here will disagree, but no way could I have done what I do now when I was more overweight - the world doesn't like working with fat people. Of course there are examples of the contrary, and it's certainly changing - but nothing can beat the enthusiasm and go-get-it vibe a newly good looking person has! They just ooooze confidence and competence. So start thinking and start planning - because one day in the not to distant future you can put on the power suit and go-get-em.

Once you quit "going to work", the world is a different place.

### ealachan — 2012-08-09T21:46:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43951>

> **Cavemam;44238 wrote:** A M E N sista. The best thing I ever did was work for myself. Of course, I did get married and my hubby was able to support us during the first couple of years (in RE those years are a negative income - you PAY for the honor).
>
>
>
> Start using that time to daydream about what you REALLLLLLLY want to be doing. I know a lot of people on here will disagree, but no way could I have done what I do now when I was more overweight - the world doesn't like working with fat people. Of course there are examples of the contrary, and it's certainly changing - but nothing can beat the enthusiasm and go-get-it vibe a newly good looking person has! They just ooooze confidence and competence. So start thinking and start planning - because one day in the not to distant future you can put on the power suit and go-get-em.
>
>
>
> Once you quit "going to work", the world is a different place.

I'm not a power-suit kind of girl. I'm more of a dirty-apron girl. I have no desire to participate in Corporate America, which is a lot of the problem that I have with my current job. I already know that I can do anything I put my mind to - mama didn't raise no shrinking violet - my main problem is that I still don't know what I want to be when I grow up. I thought I wanted to be a music teacher in high school. Turns out I hate teaching more than I love music (which, if you knew me, is A LOT). I'm very good at tinkering - fixing things, creating things, learning the mechanics of things. I learn by doing. Someone could hand me a technical manual and tell me to learn how to use a program from it, and I'd stare at it for days and not retain a word, but you sit me down in front of a computer with a program and show me the very basics, and I will have that \*\*\*\* mastered in a matter of hours. I'm good with languages, too - computer programming languages, spoken languages, codes. What I'm supposed to DO with all of that...I have no \*\*\*\*ing clue.

### chocolate — 2012-08-09T22:01:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43957>

> I've noticed something lately. The better my brain starts to work, the less interested in my job I become. It's incredibly boring. It doesn't require any brainpower at all. I could do SO much more than this, except that I can't afford to not work here. Lovely.

You're on a roll. Just bide your time and take care of yourself. And get some dark glasses for when you have that far away look in your eye. You can't let anyone know you're bored. They just pile on more work. Which, i ain't agin' working hard.... but something tells me that you are already doing the work of two.

### ealachan — 2012-08-09T22:15:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-43959>

> **chocolate;44252 wrote:** You're on a roll. Just bide your time and take care of yourself. And get some dark glasses for when you have that far away look in your eye. You can't let anyone know you're bored. They just pile on more work. Which, i ain't agin' working hard.... but something tells me that you are already doing the work of two.

Haha, good point. I actually like when I'm given projects to do, because it's something different than the norm. If my boss would actually teach me something NEW now and then, I'd be friggin' ecstatic, but he's...not the type. I'll just leave it at that...

I grew up helping my dad do sheetrocking and house painting. You do that for 8 hours a day and you can see what you've accomplished by the end. What I get now after 8 hours is a pile of paper moved from one side of my desk to the other. My ancestors (for the last 300 years, anyway) have mostly been farmers. I sometimes wonder if it's something in my blood deep down somewhere that's rebelling against the 40 hour a week desk routine because of that.

Yeahhhh...I'm gonna need you to go ahead and come in to work on Saturdayyyyyyyy...love that movie.

I went to college with big designs to be a music teacher, but I only lasted a year before I bombed out. I just wasn't ready to be there, socially or academically. My M.O. all through high school was to do only enough to scrape by, and rely on my charm to bull\*\*\*\* my teachers into letting me get away with things they shouldn't have. That didn't work in college, and it sent me into a tail-spin. The whole college experience really \*\*\*\*ed me up for a long time, because I wasn't used to failing and not being able to fast-talk my way out of stuff, and didn't really know how to handle it.

So, yeah. Back to those TPS reports, I guess.

### ealachan — 2012-08-10T18:07:36+0300

I've not tried Adsense on the food blog lately - I was using it for a while but I wasn't getting enough traffic so it wasn't really doing anything.

### ealachan — 2012-08-10T18:20:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-44143>

**Leptin Reset, Day 161**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, bp coffee

L: -

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was shrimp tikka masala. Tikka masala typically has cream in it, but I use coconut milk instead because I like the taste better. I went a little heavy on the coriander (and added in some turmeric!) in the garam masala, but it turned out well regardless. I put mushrooms and cauliflower into the curry. Have never had mushrooms in curry before so was a bit skeptical, but they were very tasty so will be doing that again!

\- got to bed around 9:30 last night, slept well until about 4am (again, that's when my body just wants to get up now, I think), then went right back to sleep until about 6:45.

\- headache this morning. I hardly ever get headaches, so not sure what the deal is. I did drink a shot of tequila last night, but I certainly shouldn't have a hangover from that. This doesn't feel hangover-y anyway, it feels sinus-y. We've had some pretty extreme weather changes the past day or so...maybe that has something to do with it.

\- trying to figure out how I'm gonna ice my neck at night while on vacation. I think maybe just some big gallon-sized ziplock freezer bags, and I'll have to double or triple bag so that it doesn't leak. Even if I could bring my cold-packs, there's no freezer to put them in, but there's ice machines in the hotel.

### ealachan — 2012-08-12T12:59:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-44495>

> **Anna K.;44664 wrote:** Ealachan, do you do cold baths regularly? I've read some of your journal and it seems that you started doing it, but not sure how often and for how long? thanks.

I stopped doing them for a while, but I've been taking daily cold showers for the past month or so - cold enough to get my skin down to 65 degrees, usually. I know showers aren't as good as baths, but right now that's what I'm doing. Also, I sleep with ice packs on my neck. Strange and wonderful things started to happen right after I started doing that!

### ealachan — 2012-08-12T13:13:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-44497>

**Leptin Reset, Day 162 (yesterday)**

b: 2.5 beef sliders (2ish oz each before cooking?), three slices bacon, two eggs, bp coffee

L: handful of mixed nuts, few pieces of raw veg (carrot, pea pod, some broccoli) - this was not really by design!

d: salad of mixed greens, couple oz each of lean roast beef and sliced turkey, sprinkle of cheese

\- friday night dinner was burgers, cauliflower and half a sweet potato, glass of wine. At breakfast, I actually had 3 sliders on my plate but I was full after 2.5 so I gave the rest to hubs. It was weird - I didn't feel stuffed to sickness or anything...it was just like a switch flipped in my brain suddenly. It was like, eat, eat, eat, ok DONE...and then I was done. Magic!

\- got to bed later than usual - 9:30ish? Got up around 6am. Spent the morning putzing around and then went to visit friends an hour and a half away to play cards for a while, so lots of sitting on my ass yesterday. Went from 7am to 7:30pm with no food other than the aforementioned nuts and veggies, and didn't actually get hungry until about 6:30ish. Shouldn't have eaten so late, I know, but it's hard to convince myself to just go to bed when tummy is rumbling.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-12T19:15:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-44551>

Just catching up and had to laugh. Way back when the internets was just taking off I was in an online group of wimmin who had the hots for Mitch Pileggi from the Xfiles (OMG did you see those SHOULDERS!?). What that man did to a white dress shirt was just shy of obscene.

ANYWAY. Everybody kept flappin' their lips about WRITING something but never did so one night I whipped out a little short story entitled The Red Dress. After everyone had finally cooled off (about a week if I recall), a whole spate of "red dress" stories began to appear in the group.

I eventually wound up selling it to PlayGirl who's editors made a few minor changes. I've often wondered if Red Dress press got it's name from that short short story. I think that's what they call themselves.

don't ask. I don't think I kept a copy. I've been through several computers since then and many MANY iterations of Mac OS's. It might be languishing on the HD on my old Mac (pre-iMac) but I can't be sure. And I don't think I kept a copy of the issue of Playgirl. Sorry. too bad cause it was smokin'. You'd NEED a cold bath after you read that one, LOL.

### ealachan — 2012-08-13T12:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-44657>

**Leptin Reset, Day 164**

b: three eggs, three slices of ham, sprinkle of cheddar cheese, bp coffee w/ 1 tbsp cocoa powder

L: ?

d: not sure yet

\- ended up eating "dinner" around 3pm yesterday - had a big salad of greens, mushroom, broccoli (raw), red pepper, tomato and green onion topped with seared steak and some crumbled bacon. Hubs called that lunch and said he wanted dinner around 5:30, so I made him another salad with the leftovers, but I didn't want any so I was fine with not eating. We were watching TV anyway, so it's not like I was sitting there watching him eat...lol

\- was crabby and hot and exhausted all afternoon yesterday, even though it wasn't THAT hot. I just felt totally snarly about everything. I always get kind of pissy right before vacation because my husband and I are VERY different when it comes to trip planning and getting things ready to go. He's very detail-oriented and gets really antsy about little things that just don't matter to me, but I pick up on his stress none the less. Oh well...frantic laundry and packing tonight and tomorrow morning and then we'll be on our way.

\- went to bed at 8 last night, read for awhile, settled down with my ice packs around 8:45. Had no trouble dropping right off to sleep, and slept straight through until hubs got up to go to the loo at some point in the wee hours (no pun intended). Dropped back off immediately, and woke up at 4am. Realized this morning that it's so much nicer to not be woken by an alarm. I've fought my alarm clock my whole life...always hated it. Now that I don't have it screeching in my ear every morning, waking up doesn't suck nearly as much! It was like waking up to that sound was instantly putting me in a bad mood every morning before my feet even hit the floor.

\- Ate a bit less at breakfast again this morning, and it filled me right up. Not sure how far it will carry me today, but we'll cross that bridge when we come to it.

\- left foot started hurting yesterday afternoon (right before vacation in which I will need to walk miles every day...awesome timing!) - I think it might be due to my cheapo flip-flops breaking down, but not positive. Going to wear my sneakers today (and while on vacation) and hope that it goes away on its own.

### ealachan — 2012-08-15T02:11:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-44969>

**Leptin Reset, Day 165**

B: three eggs, two sausages, mushrooms and green peppers, bp coffee w/ 1tbsp cocoa

L: baked haddock and lobster in some kind of sauce (probably had flour in it), cole slaw, steamed broccoli and zucchini

D: terrible badness: a Gyro (I didn't eat the bread, but still), and onion rings.

\- First leg of journey (2.5 hour bus ride to Boston) down. I hate the bus. It's like a plane but without anything fun to look at. We have to get up early for our flight, so I'll be crashing out early tonight even without my blue-blockers. Tomorrow is going to be a whole lot of hurry-up-and-wait once we get to where we're going, so not sure when I'll get a chance to update again...but don't worry, just like The Terminator, Ah'll Be Baaaahhhck!

### ealachan — 2012-08-21T17:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46042>

Alright, I'm back. Everyone can stop holding their collective breath now.  And yes, for anyone wondering, I did fit into the plane seats just fine! Time for a new goal, I guess. I'll have to think on that.

Not gonna beat around the bush, here. I went totally off-plan for most of vacation. I drank beer, I ate things I shouldn't have (though surprisingly, not nearly as much as I expected I would. I've become pretty decent at navigating restaurant menus and finding things that are the least of all the evils, and then actually ordering those things. I pleasantly surprised myself in that capacity), and the biggie...I was not sleeping nearly enough. I spent like 5 whole days under flourescent lighting, but I think I was only on the laptop or iPod a total of maybe an hour for the whole week, so that was good. And I walked. So, so, so much walking. I clocked 6 miles one day, and three other days I had 5+ miles. Holy hell. For me, who is normally doesn't even hit 2 miles a day, that's pretty epic.

Apparently hotels don't believe in cold water...WTF? I had the tap turned all the way to cold every morning in the shower and I don't think the water got below 80 degrees. My cold shower at home this morning felt soooooo good. I didn't realize just how much I'd missed it.

Alsso, to my credit, most days I was able to get in a really big protein-and-fat breakfast first thing in the morning and then go straight through until dinner without eating anything else. That was nice, and it saved money too, as convention food is expensive.

So, yeah. I think that rather than try and figure out what day of the reset I'd be on at this point, I'm just going to pick up where I left off, seeing as how I wasn't really compliant while I was gone anyway. Having said that, here we go:

**Leptin Reset, Day 166**

b: two eggs, two chicken sausages, BP coffee w/ a little HWC

l: -

d: something fishy. I missed my fish.

\- slept with ice packs on neck for the first time in a week, last night. Oh how I missed them! The combination of being tired already, the ice packs, and sleeping in my own bed with the A/C blasting made for a very deep-sleeping night. I was asleep by 10 and woke up at 7 when my husband's alarm went off. I actually woke up around 5, but rolled over and went right back to sleep.

### ColdBren — 2012-08-22T05:31:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46223>

Yeaahhh! New goal time....I have to admit that goal was stressful....even for me so I am glad its over LOL. Can you believe you missed cold shower....that is so funny. Sounds like you had fun and even though you went off some, sounds like you are totally in a different place. That is how I feel. I am nowhere near perfect, but I am so different than before. Loved the update!

### ealachan — 2012-08-22T12:21:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46252>

> **ColdBren;46608 wrote:** Yeaahhh! New goal time....I have to admit that goal was stressful....even for me so I am glad its over LOL. Can you believe you missed cold shower....that is so funny. Sounds like you had fun and even though you went off some, sounds like you are totally in a different place. That is how I feel. I am nowhere near perfect, but I am so different than before. Loved the update!

Yep, definitely missed the cold shower, and my ice packs! We did have a blast, yes. Indianapolis is a good walking city - everything is laid out in a perfect grid and stuff is really easy to find, plus the downtown area is very clean and pretty. It's hotter than Hades this time of year, usually...but we lucked out and got a few really nice (upper 70's lower 80's) days in a row while we were there.

Still thinking on the new goal. I've got a pair of jeans that I wore back in 2009 that I can get pulled on but by no means can button or zip up. Maybe getting into those will be my new goal. They're an Old Navy size 22, and they're the smallest pair of jeans I've ever been able to wear as an adult. I bought them way back in '05 or '06 when I lost almost 100lbs (which I then gained back, with interest, haha).

### ealachan — 2012-08-22T12:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46253>

**Leptin Reset, Day 167**

B: two eggs, two chicken sausages, bp coffee w/out butter because I ran out

L: not planning on it

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was baked cod topped with bacon, mushrooms and shallots all cooked together. Steamed broccoli smeared with coconut oil on the side. I had half a bar of dark chocolate for dessert, and a glass of pinot grigio. I forgot to take my enzymes before dinner and about an hour later I was running for the toilet, which sucked. It seemed different than my normal bile-dump reaction, though...usually I get that within about 20-30 minutes of eating, and once it's done, it's done...but this was an ongoing thing for about an hour last night. I did eat a LOT of cod...like 3/4 of a pound. Maybe after not having fish for a week, my system just kind of freaked out? lol

\- got to bed around 9:15 last night, slept with ice packs. I woke up a couple times in the night but was able to go right back to sleep each time. Hubs kept doing the same thing. Guess we were both paranoid about missing the alarm since this morning was our first early morning since getting back from vacation.

\- sitting for 8 hours straight at work yesterday after being constantly on the move for a whole week kind of sucked. I didn't miss that crap while I was gone.

Vermont is going to be a ridiculous place to live circadian-rhythm-wise come this winter, man. In January the sun doesn't come up til 7:30, and is setting again by 4pm. Oy.

### ealachan — 2012-08-23T12:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46500>

Good morning, lovelies! It's 5:41am EST and the sky has turned from black to steel blue. Orange glasses off in T minus 20 minutes...hope we're not socked in with fog, as I'd like to see the sun this morning!

**Leptin Reset, Day 168**

B: three eggs scrambled in CO with 4 strips of bacon, 2 servings of water-pack sardines, a handful of mushrooms, and one scallion. BP coffee w/ CO and a little HWC. still out of butter...oops.

L: -

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was a big salad of organic spring mix, green onion, grape tomatoes, tossed with home-made balsamic vinaigrette and topped with seared sirloin steak tips, sauteed mushrooms and a sprinkle of gorgonzola. Yuuuuuum. Had a small glass of pinot grigio and half a bar of dark chocolate for dessert.

\- this morning's breakfast concoction might sound a bit odd, but it was super tasty. Will happily be making that again, though I've learned the hard way that my cast iron pan is not seasoned enough to cook eggs in yet. It's ok, the scrubbing is probably good for my arms anyway...

\- orange glasses went on at sunset last night. Watched a little tv, was in bed with my ice packs by 8:30. I zonked right out...bet I didn't even lay there 10 minutes before I drifted off. Woke up once around 3.30 when the dog whined, then again at 4 when it was time to get up. Orange glasses on as soon as I woke up...10 circadian points for me!  I was a little worried that the glasses would make me stay sleepy when I got up (because I usually get sleepy not long after putting them on at night), but they didn't, so it's all good. Just waiting for the sun to come up now so that I can take the pup out.

\- got a definite HOT sensation on my upper back right after finishing breakfast this morning. Haven't had that in a while...hopefully it's a good thing.

### ealachan — 2012-08-23T16:02:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46523>

Cold! I walked the dog barefoot in the chilly wet grass, then came in and took a cold, cold shower, got dressed and got in the car, cranked up the A/C, drank a big container of ice water on the ride in...I've still got goosebumps almost half an hour after getting out of the A/C...\*giggles\*

### chocolate — 2012-08-23T17:39:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46535>

> \- got a definite HOT sensation on my upper back right after finishing breakfast this morning. Haven't had that in a while...hopefully it's a good thing.

I hope so. The doc's last blog mentioned that as a sign of candida. Not the heat, but the spot. Nonchalant was asking about it in the comments. I had a cold adapted experience where that part wasn't cold adapted until I was submerged.

### ealachan — 2012-08-23T17:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46542>
