I did see that, yes. It's not pain or pressure at all...it feels like heat radiating off my upper back and a little bit on the backs of my arms. Almost like a mild sunburn, but it feels like it's under the skin rather than on it...if that makes any sense at all.

### chocolate — 2012-08-23T18:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46548>

I keep thinking its my brown fat activating, but who knows? I have a few heatful looking spots. Doc knows. He has the road map to these nerves.

### ealachan — 2012-08-24T17:03:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46716>

**Leptin Reset, Day 169**

b: pork sausage (organic, locally made at my favorite farm, no funny stuff) - abt half a pound before cooking, two eggs, mushrooms, green onion, bp coffee with CO and raw cream from same above-mentioned farm

L: do not want

d: shrimp curry, most likely.

\- went out for dinner last night. Had two margaritas (but no beer! I'll take my bright side where I can find it...lol) and a lovely salad of greens dressed with a strawberry-chipotle vinaigrette (sounds weird, tastes SO GOOD), goat cheese, toasted almonds, a few sliced strawberries and grilled salmon. I would like to point out as well that my other options were chicken enchiladas, a pulled-pork quesadilla, flank steak tacos...all of which I've had before and know are excellent...but I went with the fish. FISH!

\- heard parts of the webinar last night, but frustratingly, I missed a total of about 20 minutes of it in 3-4 minute chunks because the audio kept dropping out. Pretty bummed, given that $38 is not pocket change for me. Also somewhat bummed that, of the webinar I DID get to hear, a lot of it was about membership changes, not actual circadian stuff. I know that there's a lot of members who need / want to know that stuff, but it seemed like it could have been done via email or a forum post just as easily.

\- was in bed by 9:30ish last night, asleep shortly after. Woke up at 2:30 and stumbled to the bathroom in complete darkness (note to self: put all dog toys AWAY before bed every night from now on...lol), went right back to sleep until about 5 when the dog woke me up. Calmed the dog down and zonked out again until 6:30. Got up, went right outside with the dog, got my daylight fix. It was foggy, but any daylight is better than fake light, according to Dr. K.

\- now that vacation is out of the way, I need to suck it up and talk to my doctor about ordering labs. I hate having to explain myself in order to get what I want, and I have a feeling that she's going to want to know the why's and wherefore's of all this lab stuff. She's pretty skeptical about Paleo and low-carb in general. Nice lady, but not terribly progressive.

### ealachan — 2012-08-24T19:24:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46750>

Forgot to mention - my BP has been really good (like 110's over low 70's) for a couple weeks, so yesterday I decided I'd start halving my dose of HCTZ and keeping close track of the BP to see how it goes. So far, so good - yesterday I had a reading of 114/76 with pulse 70 at 6:50am right after my walk with the dog, 122/77 with pulse 73 at 5:52pm, and this morning it was 112/83 with pulse 72 about 20 minutes post prandial. If I can keep up numbers like that for a couple weeks straight, I'll try going without it entirely and see what happens. My BP was never SUPER high to begin with (hence only being on HCTZ), so I'm not too concerned with fiddling with the medication on my own, especially when I'm keeping an eye on the BP a couple times a day anyway.

### chocolate — 2012-08-24T20:16:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46769>

I just started pycnogenol and a little arginine here and there for my blood pressure. I really think its working and its an anti-oxidant. If you wanted an alternative, there are lots.

### ealachan — 2012-08-24T21:08:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46783>

> **chocolate;47179 wrote:** I just started pycnogenol and a little arginine here and there for my blood pressure. I really think its working and its an anti-oxidant. If you wanted an alternative, there are lots.

My goal is more to not NEED an alternative... I started to get pretty skeptical of the whole high blood pressure thing when I started supplementing 10k IU of D3 a day back last fall and my BP dropped like 10 points and STAYED DOWN. My doctor hadn't even tested my vitamin D level before putting me on the diuretic and giving me the lecture about how I would end up just like my mom (having a stroke before 50) if I didn't lose a bunch of weight ASAP. I don't debate the needing to lose weight one bit because my fat has and will continue to make me sick in worse and worse fashion as I age, mind you - but I have a hard time swallowing that medication should always be the first line of attack. Even more-so, after having hung out around here for a while and become reletively well-edumacated on How Stuff Works.

### ealachan — 2012-08-25T15:39:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46991>

**Leptin Reset, Day 170**

b: leftover seafood curry, bp coffee w/ co and raw cream

L: -

d: grass-fed burgers, I think.

\- dinner last night was Thai red curry with cauliflower, mushrooms, shrimp and mussels. I remembered about saturated fat blocking DHA absorption about halfway through my bowl of coconut-milk-laden curry...oops. So I probably didn't get much brain-goodness out of the seafood, but it was still tasty. AND...bonus points: I got my husband to try mussels for the first time and he decided they weren't so bad. woo hoo! He's never had clams or oysters, either. Mussels are a good gateway shellfish, in my mind - no bellies to deal with like with clams, and not quite as snotty a texture as oysters...

\- got to sleep a little late last night (10ish), but it was for a good cause: epic oxytocin therapy. Yeah buddy.  Zonked out around 10, woke up briefly around 2:30, then got up at 4am and took hubs to work. I actually went back to bed when I got home from taking him to work this morning, which I haven't been doing, but I was mindful of the lack of sleep during the night and the fact that Dr. K said any sleep is good sleep. So, I unpinned the covering from the window so that the light would come in when the sun came up, and I went back to sleep around 4:45. Combination of sun and dog woke me up at 6:45. Walked the pup (it's his 3rd birthday today!) and came in to have breakfast.

\- Breakfast seems to have done kind of a number on me - not sure why. It agreed with me fine last night for dinner, but this morning it sent me running to the bathroom actually thinking I was going to barf for a few minutes. I didn't, but it was nip and tuck for a little bit there. Weird.

\- really nice out this morning - sunny but cool. Thinking about maybe taking a book outside and getting an hour of sun before it starts getting busy around here. Not sure if maybe it's too early to get much benefit from the sun, though.

\- BP was 122/82 with pulse 68 right after walking the dog this morning. Not too shabby.

\- need to get back to writing. I started a writing whirlwind right before we left for vacation, and I'm having trouble getting back into the habit.

\- Hubs is working mostly 9-to-5's next week, which is good. His boss is basically trying to get him onto just 2 opens (4:30am starts) a week and the rest 9-to-5ish shifts. This would be pretty ideal, so I hope it happens!

### Martin — 2012-08-25T16:07:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-46996>

Good stuff.

Yeah, mussels are a gateway drug. I wish I lived on a rocky coast somewhere.

Yes, you need to write more. We need our fix out here.

### ealachan — 2012-08-25T18:54:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47040>

> **Martin;47420 wrote:** Good stuff.
>
>
>
> Yeah, mussels are a gateway drug. I wish I lived on a rocky coast somewhere.
>
>
>
> Yes, you need to write more. We need our fix out here.

I am luckily about 2 hours from both the Maine and the Massachusetts seacoasts, so we tend to have access to excellent clams, mussels and lobster around here. I learned a good lesson with the mussels last night, too: I bought them at the local fish place and paid like $3.29 a pound for them, which I was scoffing over at the time, because the grocery store has them for .99 a pound...but the quality difference was truly marked. I only had four duds (three that were dead and one broken) in a bag of almost 2lbs from the fish place, whereas the last batch I got from the grocery store, almost half were duds! Also, most of the fish place ones were already de-bearded and had very clean shells, which saves a lot of prep time. And finally, the last batch I got from the grocery store had a TON of grit in them even though I washed them really thoroughly...they must have just been in a really muddy area when they were growing. The fish place ones were clean as a whistle. I didn't even have to strain the cooking liquid before I used it as the base for my curry broth! Definitely worth the extra money. I'm considering going back and getting some oysters tonight, but I don't know if shellfish two nights in a row might send Hubs for a loop...lol

### ealachan — 2012-08-25T19:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47043>

Also, I did end up going out to sit in the sun for about 45 minutes this morning, from 9 to 9:45ish. Pretty sure I didn't get any color (though my skin got warm), but it sure felt nice. I noticed something interesting while outside, too: I used to not be able to sit on a flat surface (ground, floor) with my legs straight out in front of me for more than 5-10 minutes without one or both legs falling asleep, but today I sat like that for the full 45 minutes and didn't get even the slightest hint of pins and needles, numbness or pain. COOL!

### ealachan — 2012-08-26T15:27:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47206>

**Leptin Reset, Day 171**

b: burger, two eggs, coffee with co, butter and hwc added

l: -

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was burgers. I served them with lettuce, tomato, red onion, mushrooms sauteed in the burger fat, crumbled bacon, gorgonzola cheese and avocado slices. They were, if I do say so myself, pretty \*\*\*\*ing epic. I don't know what it is about gorgonzola, bacon and mushrooms, but I'm pretty sure that combination would make a dog turd taste good, even. I better watch my mouth, or Dr. K will be telling us next that we need to go out and get good bacteria from dog poo... Anyway, I had a couple glasses of merlot (my favorite) and a little lemon-meringue pudding cup thing that my husband bought for dessert. I made the mistake of not checking the label on the meringue things until we'd already eaten them. It was only 17g of sugar, but it was definitely full of all kinds of fake stuff. Blech.

\- got to bed around 8:30 last night, oxytocin'ed up and was asleep by probably 9:30. I slept great for a few hours, then I think the wine caught up with me because I woke up and was really tossy-turny for quite a while. Lesson re-learned there. Got up at 4 to take Hubs to work, came back home and went back to bed again. Woke up just before my alarm went off at 7am with the sun beating in on my face... Pup and I had a nice sunlit stroll, then I came in and had breakfast.

\- slacked off all day yesterday, so need to go into CLEAN ALL THE THINGS mode today...bleh. Also need to actually plan food ahead for the week somewhat, as hubs will need to take lunches to work this week (or end up eating crappy canned soup or even crappier pre-made salad at work). I was happily surprised yesterday when he came home from work at 2:30pm and said "I haven't eaten since 5am, but I think I can make it through til dinner time." He's always been an "eat three meals a day even if you arent hungry" type. He's somehow convinced himself that if he doesn't eat three times a day, it gives him bad guts. Cutting out all the soda and sugary crap and carbs he was eating has vastly improved his guts already just in the few weeks he's been on the wagon, and he's finally started admitting that maaaaaaaybe it was all the junk he was eating that was making him sick all the time, rather than when his meals were or whether or not he got in 3 meals a day. \*smirks\* I never waltz around the house singing "I was right, I was right, I was riiiiiiight"...at least, not out loud...

### ealachan — 2012-08-26T17:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47240>

I just replaced my two overhead kitchen light bulbs, the living room lamp and the bedroom lamp with yellow "bug light" light bulbs. I predict much husband eye-rolling tonight when it starts to get dark and he starts switching lights on left and right...hehehehe. There's more than one way to skin a cat! If he won't leave the lights OFF, I'll just make them all as un-damaging as possible. I put f.lux on the laptop the other day too, and turned it down to 3400k permanently (rather than just at night). I didn't tell him I did it, so yesterday he was puttzing on the computer and he goes "man, this laptop screen is going to hell, I think." I said "no, I messed with it. Less blue light!" He muttered something under his breath, but seeing as how he's just using the laptop for internet stuff and not anything where color actually MATTERS, I don't care much. He can deal.

### vkiernan — 2012-08-26T18:04:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47258>

You are a really good wife. He will appreciate your efforts some day!

### ealachan — 2012-08-26T18:40:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47270>

> **vkiernan;47697 wrote:** You are a really good wife. He will appreciate your efforts some day!

He appreciates me now...he just takes a lot longer to buy into the changes I'm making because he has zero interest in science and doesn't want to know the "why" behind anything. Which is fine, I can completely respect that...not everyone is as bat\*\*\*\* crazy with the why why why of everything like I always have been - I was the little kid who took apart anything I could get my hands on in order to figure out how it worked. After I broke a toaster trying to get the heating filaments to ignite when I was like 8, my parents started hiding all the screwdrivers in the basement because they knew I hated the basement and wouldn't go down there looking for them. That's when I realized that a butter knife works pretty well as a screwdriver in a pinch... The more I think about what a weird little kid I was, the more I wonder how my parents didn't kill me or ship me off to military school...LOL

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-26T21:26:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47309>

They are selling lights now that are the old fashioned tungsten kind. They have the filament that gets bright when it heats up. In my mind, this equates candles.

They are about $11 a bulb. Pricey, but perhaps a good nighttime use for bathrooms and stuff or bedside reading when you don't want to burn the house down.

Lights out now, when dark comes at 8:30 isn't a big deal - but when it's dark at 5:00 p.m., I bet I will have to come to some kind of method that's practical. . .and my hope is to use those tungsten lights.

### ealachan — 2012-08-27T16:42:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47481>

**Leptin Reset, Day 172**

_In which Shark Week looms and makes me kind of hate life a little bit..._

B: some ham, three eggs, coffee w/ co and butter

L: hoping I won't need it

D: steak tips and...something. Not sure what yet.

\- dinner last night was a strange but tasty salad of lettuce, tomato, avocado, red onion, grassfed ground beef cooked in bacon fat, mushrooms, and a little crumbled bacon. Something about avocados and bacon together just give me a big fat (literally!) food happy. I loved them. Also had a glass of merlot and the last of the lemon meringue pudding thingies that my husband is not allowed to buy any more of.

\- went to bed around 9, no nookie, slept like crap. Hubs was up reading for a while, and the yellow lightbulb I put in the bedside lamp is actually brighter (albeit much less blue-colored) than the CFL bulb that was in there before, and I couldn't fall asleep. It was so. annoying. Clearly need a lower-wattage bulb and/or a sleep mask. I tossed and turned all night, kept waking up, nothing was comfortable...I was too hot, then I was too cold, then the pillow was squashed in the wrong postion, then the dog wanted to cuddle, then the blankets were choking me, blah blah blah. Just all-around not a good night's sleep. You know, I just realized something - the last two nights in a row, I've taken an MSM cap with my magnesium caps an hour before bed, and then slept like \*\*\*\*. I wonder if THAT is something to do with it? Hmmmm.

\- TOTM (aka: shark week) is due to strike in the next day or so, which would explain why I've gained two inches of waist, I'm constantly tired and my boobs are sore. I will say this, though...and if you're male and don't want to hear about "women's problems", you should back away slowly now...you gone? Ok. As I was saying re: Shark Week - I used to get very tender breasts starting like two weeks before my period. Sometimes they were so sore that it hurt to put on and take off a bra, even. Nowadays, they get a little achey a couple days before the main event, and that's it! Huzzah! Also, I've never had super bad cramping (usually just cramps the first day of bleeding, then nothing the rest of the time), but the last two TOTM's have been even less crampy, which is certainly nice.

\- BP was 112/77 with pulse of 70 this morning about 30 minutes after breakfast. Woo! This makes me very happy.

\- still losing hair every time I get in the shower, and yet my hair looks fuller and it feels like there's more of it lately...wtf?! Crazy.

\- hoping to maybe make my triumphant (har har) return to yoga class tomorrow night, but we'll see. If Shark Week hits, I will likely skip it, because...no.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-27T20:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47512>

I wish I had some confidence that what I was buying was worthwhile. It's a lot of money for light bulbs! But when I think how much I'm spending trying to get hormones fixed out, it's a paltry amount. We save a lot of money by eating out very little and these days we waste almost zero food so overall things are evening out. It's interesting how my feelings towards food have changed - now I feel an almost sacred connection and really feel compelled to use it all and not let it go bad. Truly living off the land and the animals you raise must create really intense feelings about what you're consuming. Oh, just realized I'm going off on \*your\* blog .

Oh no, feel free!  I'm with you, except for the fact that I still like to go out to eat...

### Lyndra — 2012-08-27T21:18:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47526>

> **ealachan;47966 wrote:** Oh no, feel free!  I'm with you, except for the fact that I still like to go out to eat...

Going out to eat used to be one of my favorite activities, but I'm so picky now about what I eat that much of the fun is gone. I have to check the menu online first, try to figure out whether they are too snooty to allow substitutions, and whether they likely to use good or crappy ingredients. At least now I don't have bread cravings, so it's no big deal to forego the bread. One place we thought was good turns out to use flour in all their soups (even the broth-based). I'm avoiding nightshades, don't eat cheese, don't like raw onions, so a salad turns out to be just lettuce. Boring. Going out to lunch seems to be the worst - I pay $15 for a salad, omitting half the ingredients and get dried out chicken with it. Or maybe salmon for another $2-4. Paying $20 for lunch just makes me cringe. I'd rather have my sardines and avocado.

### ealachan — 2012-08-27T21:34:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47532>

I have to check the menu online first, try to figure out whether they are too snooty to allow substitutions, and whether they likely to use good or crappy ingredients. At least now I don't have bread cravings, so it's no big deal to forego the bread. One place we thought was good turns out to use flour in all their soups (even the broth-based). I'm avoiding nightshades, don't eat cheese, don't like raw onions, so a salad turns out to be just lettuce. Boring. Going out to lunch seems to be the worst - I pay $15 for a salad, omitting half the ingredients and get dried out chicken with it. Or maybe salmon for another $2-4. Paying $20 for lunch just makes me cringe. I'd rather have my sardines and avocado.

Woo, I can see how that would take the fun right out of it then, yes. I'm not currently avoiding nightshades or dairy, so that opens up quite a few options for me. I'm actually pretty picky too, but I'm very lucky in that our few "regular" spots to eat are pretty progressive and open to substitutions and special restrictions. Plus, our favorite place is one that's got a revolving seasonal menu and is very into using local and high-quality ingredients. I know the chef and the owner, and I know it's important to them to not feed people crap. We pay a little more when we go there, but it's totally worth it to know that you're getting the good stuff.

### ealachan — 2012-08-28T17:24:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47780>

**Leptin Reset, Day 173**

b: two eggs, some ham (didn't weigh), coffee w/ butter, hwc and co

l: -

d: salmon and...something.

\- dinner last night was sirloin steak tips, spinach and mushrooms sauteed in a little bacon fat with a bit of onion and garlic, half a tomato, and three squares of dark chocolate. Also, a glass of merlot and half a glass of chardonnay.

\- got to bed around 9:15ish, quick oxytocin therapy session (heh), was probably asleep by 10pm. I did NOT take any MSM before bed, just my NeuroMag, and I had no tossing and turning problems, so I think that must have been what was doing it the last couple nights. Slept like a log...only remember waking up for a minute or two once in the night when I pulled the ice packs off my neck. Went right back to sleep until the light woke me up at 5:56am. Got up and did my morning bathroom stuff without turning any lights on, then leashed the dog up and went out to absorb what light we could from the fog-socked-in sky.

\- This morning was the first time I've felt actually WOKEN UP by being out in the light first thing in the morning. I felt a little sleepy getting out of bed, but within a minute of being outside in the light, I really did feel my head clear right out and by the time I got back to the house (like 5 minutes, tops), I was fully awake and ready to roll. It was pretty cool!

\- have been re-jigging my supplements a little - I added PQQ and Curcumin, and I've stoped taking Krill oil for now. So here is a list of what I'm taking right now:

Morning:

Rainbow Light enzymes - 1 pill before breakfast

Nutricology ox bile 125mg - 2 pills before breakfast

multivitamin (Centrum adult formula)

HCTZ (diuretic that I'm trying to get off of - taking 1/2 dose of 12.5mg now)

MSM 2,000 mg

D3 5,000 IU

Antioxident blend (mostly vitamin E, but some selenium and stuff as well)

Curcumin (Jarrow brand - 1 pill)

PQQ 20mg

NeuroMag - 1 pill

Jarrow K2 90mcg x 2 pills = 180mcg

Evening: 2 NeuroMag an hour before bed.

I recently dropped the probiotic I was taking because it was fairly expensive and was probably just amounting to a drop in the bacterial bucket in the long run. I'd rather spend that $30 a month on fermented foods and hopefully get more / a larger variety of bacterial bang for my buck.

Also, dropped DIM because I'm not doing serious CT so it seemed like kind of a waste, and I dropped Krill oil because I've been eating upwards of 2g of omega3's via fish almost every day lately (last couple days notwithstanding). I'm going to add in some CoQ10 in the next few days, then give it a few weeks to see how things go.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-28T19:45:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47808>

> **Lyndra;48243 wrote:** I tested this theory with the special $4 bulb I got from lowbluelights.com. The blue flash is definitely not there with that bulb but present with a regular incandescent. Next step - buy one of the orange party lights and see what happens with that.

What is this blue flash thing? I want to try it on my yuppie overprivleged princess bulbs!

### ealachan — 2012-08-28T20:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47834>

> **Cavemam;48259 wrote:** What is this blue flash thing? I want to try it on my yuppie overprivleged princess bulbs!

It's when you take a CD or DVD and hold it shiny-side up under a light and tilt it to see what colors it reflects. To the naked eye under "normal" light, it should flash all the colors of the rainbow, ROYGBV, because "normal" light contains all those colors. When you stand under the same light and put your blue-blockers on, theoretically the blue will disappear from the "flash" or the reflection on the CD. Also, the green will look more yellowish and the violet will look more reddish, because theoretically the blue component of both colors is being blocked from your eyes. This test was outlined somewhere in one of the threads about blue-blocking glasses fairly early on, I believe. I tried it with my orange Uvex saftey glasses of extreme sexiness, and it worked...pretty cool.

Anyway - Lyndra and I were wondering if the same method works to gauge whether a lightbulb is throwing off blue light or not. Hence the Bill Nye-esque home experiments... My yellow bug-bulbs DO create some blue flash on the CD, but it's somewhat less than what I get under my standard white incandescent bulbs. We would be very interested to know if your Special Snowflake bulbs do the same thing!

### Lyndra — 2012-08-28T21:13:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-47863>

> **Cavemam;48259 wrote:** What is this blue flash thing? I want to try it on my yuppie overprivleged princess bulbs!

Take a CD and flip it to the data side. Tip it from side to side in a bright light and you'll see a rainbow of colors. Now put on your blue blocker glasses, and the blue will disappear from the rainbow. Do the same thing under your princess bulb and see whether the blue is there or not.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-29T01:13:06+0300

Ok, bought the "party light" CFL at Home Despot (oops, is that a typo?) and ran it through the CD test. Passed with no blue flying colors. I snuck one into a lamp we have on in the evening, will see if the hubs notices. He's on board these days, though, even putting on the glasses before I do so I think he'll like it.

### ealachan — 2012-08-29T17:19:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-48084>

**Leptin Reset, Day 174**

_In which our heroine is trapped inside on a gorgeous late-summer day...\*whine\*_

B: ham (sick of it, blech), two eggs, coffee with co, hwc, butter, and a tablespoon or so of cocoa

l: -

d: not sure yet.

\- dinner last night ended up being kind of a surf'n'turf thing - I had some leftover steak tips to use up, plus the salmon. I pan-seared the salmon in a little bacon fat - got the skin nice and crispy, YUM! I warmed up the steak (it was already cooked), steamed some asparagus, and then hit the salmon with a few spoonfuls of chimmichurri. Chimmichurri, if you're not familiar with it, is a sauce made of fresh parsley and cilantro, with raw garlic, some green onion, olive oil and vinegar. Some people also put cumin and/or hot pepper in it, but I like mine more on the mild side. It's lovely and bright, and the vinegar cut the fattiness of the salmon very nicely. Definitely a good combination. Had some chocolate after dinner as well...and NO WINE!

\- didn't get to bed until 10 last night, but I was out like a light within 10 minutes of laying down, and I slept AMAZINGLY. I woke up once very early (3? 4, maybe? didn't look) and needed to get up for a pee (stupid asparagus...lol), but I went right back to sleep after that and woke up at 6:30 when my husband opened the curtain to let the sun shine in on me. I'm going to attribute the awesome night's sleep to something weird, here: I put the duvet back on the bed. We haven't used it all summer long, and we've had the A/C blasting (I have it set to 65 when I go to bed at night. I don't know what possessed me to put the duvet back on the bed last night and leave the A/C going, but it was like magic. I woke up this morning feeling like a million bucks!! I wonder if maybe the A/C was making me too cold in the night before and that's what was making me wake up, however briefly. If I hadn't had to wake up to pee this morning, I'm quite certain I'd have slept straight through the night.

\- It is unadulteratedly (is that a word? It is now!) gorgeous here today - perfect late-summer Vermont weather: dry, light breeze, not a cloud in the sky, should top out around 75 degrees. Achingly beautiful. I cannot put into words how much I do not want to be inside today. It was a major struggle not to call in sick this morning, and I'm still entertaining the thought of developing a "migraine" this afternoon. I likely won't, because I'm terribly responsible, but I can still fantasize.

Life is short!!! Make it an afternoon to remember! That sounds amazing - don't pass tis up. I never thought it was fair that well people couldn't use their sick days for whatever the hell they wanted. I don't call using sick days you earn for whatever your needs are as being shenannigans. Perhaps you take one to go on a long hike, and it triggers something in you for wellness. Then you've healed your body a little. Who's to say the starting point on someone else's journey is more or less deserving of time than yours?

So the person who smokes has a sick day because they are always catching some cold. . .why shouldn't you take your sick time to deal with your issues as they are. Go do some of that Yoga (note the lack of judgement in my tone  or hike or walk or just stare into the sun and pick a flower. . That's improving your health no?

### ealachan — 2012-08-30T15:05:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-48341>

**Leptin Reset, Day 175**

B: last of the ham (thankfully!), two and a half eggs, coffee w/ hwc, co and butter, 1tbsp cocoa

L: -

d: something fishy

\- dinner last night was...naughty, by any standard. We went out. There was beer, there was bratwurst, there was sauerkraut...and there was spaetzle. Probably if I'd have avoided the spaetzle, it wouldn't have been so bad...but I ate it. And some flourless chocolate cake. And did I mention the beer? It was oatmeal stout. I didn't just fall off the wagon, I did a \*\*\*\*ing nose-dive...LOL I'm getting up and dusting myself off and moving on, though. \*\*\*\* happens...dwelling on it doesn't fix anything.

\- paid for my carby transgressions with fairly poor sleep last night - fell asleep quickly but was pretty tossy-turny as the night went on. Bad dreams, too. Had to get up at 5:30 to get ready for work, but the upside is I get to go home early today.

\- After a few days on the PQQ (20mg), even without the CoQ10, I have to say...my number recall at work seems a lot better (I do a lot of stuff where I have to look at a 5 to 7 digit number on one screen and be able to remember it to input it on another screen, over and over again, with a lot of numbers that look very close to one another) - I find myself not having to go back and check that I got the right numbers so much, which is nice. Also, I seem to have less instances of getting distracted and going off-task. This is both good and bad, because it means I'm getting stuff done more quickly, which means I'm going to have to either pefect the art of looking busy when I'm not, or I'm gonna start getting saddled with more work...

\- TMI AHEAD: Souldanzer brought up an interesting point in the EpiPaleo Diet thread that made me realize - my period started yesterday, and I've not had a single cramp! I normally get moderate cramping on the first day, but I've had nothing at all yet. Knocking on wood. My cycle was also 29.5 days long this time, and my period is basically coinciding with the full moon. Interesting.

d: something shrimpy

\- dinner last night was pork chops and steamed broccoli. And ice cream. \*\*\*\*! I had a coming-to-Jesus session with hubs about the constant begging for ice cream, and hopefully it will now stop. I know that I should be able to just not eat it if he buys it, but it doesn't work that way for me. I could resist cookies, cake, any kind of candy...but for some reason, ice cream is still a major issue for me. I can't have it in the house.

\- slept well last night - in bed by 8:30, oxytocin therapy session, asleep by 9:30 (if not before). slept straight through (super weird dreams all night) until 4am when I had to take hubs to work. Came home from that, zonked right back out by like 4:45, and woke up at 6:30 to go do stuff with my mom.

\- found some REALLY orange lightbulbs...ugh. They're definitely way more orange than the bug light bulbs, and I totally hate them...so I have a feeling the lights are going to start staying off one way or another...lol I left the 60watt bug lights in the kitchen because I need to be able to see to cook (damn apartment with no bathroom or kitchen windows). I switched the bathroom light to a bug light, and the hallway light as well. The bedroom reading lamp is now orange, as is the living room lamp. It's funny...now when I open the fridge, the light from it looks SO cold and blue compared to the yellow kitchen light.

\- shrimp for supper tonight - probably curry, but a tikka masala type curry rather than a thai curry, since the thai curry is coconut-milk-based and that negates any DHA goodness we might get from the shrimp! See, I do learn sometimes...

### ealachan — 2012-09-03T15:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49170>

**Leptin Reset, Day 179**

b: two eggs, 4ish oz roast beef, coffee w/ co, hwc, and cocoa

l: not planning on it

d: cook-out @ my mom's - burgers, steak, grilled veg

\- dinner last night was shrimp tikka masala and it was very tasty. lots of leftovers for hubs to have for lunch. Had a small glass of wine and a little chocolate for dessert.

\- was in bed by 8 last night. Read for a while - the orange light in the bedroom isn't so bad, I guess. It makes everything look exactly the same as wearing orange glasses (literally cannot tell a difference between glasses on or off under the orange bulb) so that's a good sign, I suppose. We got in a little oxytocin therapy and I was asleep by 9-ish. Slept well at first but then kept waking up. It was chilly and we'd taken the duvet off the bed due to it being warmer earlier in the week...I think that might be what did it. Got up at 4 to take hubs to work, felt really tired and crappy for the first time in a long time. Went back to bed when I got home, got up around 7:15. Still socked in with clouds / fog here this morning, so that's a bummer.

\- have had a sore throat since mid-afternoon yesterday. It's only on the left side, and my throat isn't actually swollen up at all (I have huge tonsils and usually if something irritates my throat, they swell up), it just feels scratchy and annoying on the one side, and my nose on that side feels a bit runny / post-nasal-drippy too. The only reason for this that I can come up with is that I deep-cleaned the bathroom yesterday, which included sucking the accumulated dust out of the vents on the exhaust fan. My face was right up next to it the whole time, and I'm sure I probably breathed some of that \*\*\*\* in - it was likely moldy / mildewy too, just by virtue of being the shower exhaust fan. So maybe I'm having a bit of a reaction to that? Or maybe aliens have taken over my throat. Could go either way, really.

### ealachan — 2012-09-04T18:30:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49365>

**Leptin Reset, Day 180**

_6 months, roughly! Still trying to decide what I have to show for it...lol_

b: steak (didn't weigh, had to be at least 6oz), two eggs, coffee w/ CO and HWC

L: -

d: not sure yet...likely the rest of the steak, since I have to use it up.

\- dinner yesterday was cook-out stuff - I had a pork rib (no sauce), a chicken thigh (no skin, no sauce), a couple small pieces of kielbasa, some roasted potatoes, roasted pepper salad with garlic, grilled zucchini and summer squash, a few deviled eggs (mom made them, so they likely had sketchy stuff in them) and a couple pieces of celery stuffed with olives and cream cheese. I also drank two beers. Serving of dark chocolate for dessert.

\- slept like CRAP last night. Some kind of allergy thing kicked in on Sunday afternoon - sore throat like I mentioned before - but then yesterday I started feeling really tired and out of it, and had wicked body aches along with sinus pain. Also weird hot-and-then-cold sensations. Felt for all the world like a fever (body aches tend to be the tell-tale sign of a fever for me), but my temp never got above 99.8, so I don't know. Is there such a thing as a mini-fever? LOL I finally had to get up and take some Tylenol at around 10:30pm because I was so achey that I couldn't sleep and was just laying there in misery. Today I don't have body aches and temp was at around 98.4 this morning, but my sinuses feel like lead and are really achey. So that's fun. Hopefully whatever bloomed that set this off will die off quickly!

### ealachan — 2012-09-05T15:53:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49577>

**Leptin Reset, Day 181**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, four cherry tomatoes, coffee w/ hwc, co and butter and a tablespoon of unsweetened cocoa powder

l: -

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was ribeyes, sweet potato, and organic mushrooms and leeks sauteed in butter and the steak run-off. Tasty, but the meal sent DH's blood sugar up like a rocket. Whoopsies. Guess we won't have a huge slab of meat AND sweet potato AND a bunch of onions all at the same time again. Heh. I also had a couple glasses of wine.

\- went to bed around 9:30, passed out within about 10 minutes, slept well for a while but then got tossy-turny in the middle of the night. Sinuses still really full, so it was making it hard to breathe, which of course makes it hard to sleep. Had to get up at 6am, which felt like a bit of a struggle, but once I was up I was ok. Very dark and gloomy morning again today...makes it hard to get up / wake up.

\- allergies (or whatever this is) still kicking my ass, though my face doesn't hurt as badly as it did yesterday (knock on wood). Mostly just faucet-nose and post-nasal drip now. Ugh.

\- started CoQ10 this morning. interested to see if it makes any difference with the PQQ I've been taking. I don't think I'm really taking a big enough dose of the PQQ to do a whole lot (it's 20mg, and that \*\*\*\* is EXPENSIVE!), but we'll see.

\- noticed loose skin in weird places this morning - wrinkles around my elbows that never used to be there, sides of my stomach are getting wrinkled too. Also, I really wish I'd have taken "before" pictures of my thighs, because the difference is pretty astounding. My thighs aren't a whole lot SMALLER than 6 months ago, but the difference in cellulite is amazing. I used to have wicked cottage-cheese thighs...like REALLY deep dimples, all over. Now, I still have some dimples on the tops and sides, but way less, and nowhere near as deep...and the BACKS of my thighs seriously look like someone else's legs. It's crazy. I have hardly any cellulite there anymore! And it's not like I'm sitting in a cold bath every night, either. I haven't sat in a cold bath since like May. I do take cold showers, and I sleep with ice packs on my neck and the a/c cranked up, but that's really it in terms of CT for me lately. Crazy.

### ealachan — 2012-09-06T14:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49719>

**Leptin Reset, Day 182**

b: three eggs, some sausage (didn't weigh, at least two large links' worth), coffee w/ co, hwc and butter

L: we laugh in the face of lunch, haaaaaa hahaha!

d: not sure yet - company coming over tonight.

\- dinner last night was my new favorite standby: roasted cod with tomato olive tappenade. My tappenade was made with cherry tomatoes from the farmer's market that were seriously amazing...so yummy. I had quick-braised kale with it, which I have decided I like even more than the spinach I usually have with it. Kale is more work, but it tastes better and is probably healthier anyway.

\- got some butter and some mozzarella cheese both made from milk from a herd of local grass-fed Devon cows last night. The butter is salted, which I didn't realize until it was too late. Still decent, though. The mozzarella is very nice...would happily nom way too much of that.

\- allergies still kicking my ass. \*sigh\* Went to bed around 9:30 last night, oxytocin'ed up, zonked out. Slept well for a few hours, then woke up tossy-turny again. Annoying. I'm hoping that this is just something to do with being congested, and not my new sleep pattern.

\- had to be up at 5:15 this morning. Tomorrow is a 4am day, and I think I'll try to go back to bed for a bit after I drop hubs off at work, as 4:30 is just way too early for me to go in.

\- hubs stayed up to watch football last night...with his blue-blockers on and the obnoxious orange lightbulb in the lamp. This morning he gets up and goes "wow, I must have been really tired last night...I couldn't even stay up for the whole football game. By 10pm my eyes just wouldn't stay open!" Hee hee hee. In the past, he'd have had the lamp, TV and laptop all going and would have been up until gone midnight, at least. Score one for blocking blue light.

### ealachan — 2012-09-07T02:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-49884>

People. I am HOT. Not even hot to trot (although...\*eyes husband from across the room\*)...I mean I'm like, overheating. The thing is, it's not that hot out. I don't have a fever, I'm not flushed...I'm just FECKING HOT. Is this some kind of crazy detox my body is doing or something? I'm too young to be having peri-menopausal hot flashes, aren't I?

This has been going on for the last few days. I just can't seem to get comfortable unless I'm sitting in front of an a/c unit. It's super annoying.

### ealachan — 2012-09-07T17:55:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-50019>

**Leptin Reset, Day 183**

b (7am): two chicken sausages, two eggs, coffee with hwc / butter / co, 1tbsp cocoa

L: nyet

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was zucchini stuffed with a mixture of ground beef, leeks, cherry tomatoes, mushrooms, some herbs, and topped with a bit of local raw-milk mozzarella. Side salad of greens dressed with balsamic and olive oil. YUM. Had one beer for dessert.

\- went to bed around 9pm, zonked right out, woke up some time in the night for a pee, then zonked out again. Had to get up at 4am, which kinda sucked, but I put my blue-blockers on straight away and went right back to bed around 4:30. Slept like a log until 7am, then got up and took the doggie out for what little light exposure we could get through the thick fog (ahh, fall in Vermont...so foggy!).

\- allergies / cold / whatever this is feels better this morning - sinuses don't hurt so much and ears aren't as crackly, and I don't feel dead on my feet today like I have the last two days, so that's good. Wondering if the overheating thing I've had for the past few days has been my body fighting off whatever I've had going on, since said overheating started like 24 hours after the cold-like symptoms set in. Hmm.

### ealachan — 2012-09-10T17:12:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-50719>

**Leptin Reset, Day 184**

_In which we skip over the weekend because not much positive happened...heh..._

B: can o' salmon, three slices of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ hwc, co, butter and 1 tbsp cocoa

L: nyerhe

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was t-bones (they were on sale), sauteed kale, and portobello mushrooms that I stuffed with a crab meat / cream cheese mixture. The mushrooms were good, but recipe needs to be re-worked to not use cream cheese, because...hello, cream cheese. Also, I over-garlic'ed the filling so you couldn't really taste much crab. AND...I had no idea crab was fat-free. That means no DHA, right? Which means I don't need to bother buying any more of it...lol

\- took a cold shower just as it was getting dark last night, then put my blue-blockers on and stayed up to watch a little football. I went to bed around 9:30, oxytocin therapy, zonked out with ice packs on neck by 10pm. Slept like a frickin' ROCK. I woke up once in the night because I was coughing ( tail end of cold / allergies clearing out), but other than that it was zonk-out city. Woke up this morning when hubs uncovered the window at 7am. A couple minutes of sun in the eyes and I perked right up. It's nice to not feel like a complete zombie for an hour after getting up in the morning nowadays...

\- I did wake up with an annoying split in my bottom lip, which I think is from mouth-breathing due to congestion. It's right on the edge so it really hurts when I smile. Guess I'll just have to pretend to be grumpy today...haha!

\- weekend was fun - we went away for a nerdy card tournament (Which my husband won...aww yeah!). We got to see lots of folks we don't see too often, and it's nice to get out and be social with them. The downside was that diet and circadian rhythms went right in the toilet for the day. Ah, well. You only live once. Might as well have a good time when you can. It's not like we eat pizza and drink beer and stay up til midnight regularly any more.

\- cold shower in the evening last night was an attempt to start working my way up to cold baths again. Tap water was around 65 last night. I figure if I can start working my way into it again now, then I'll be well on my way by the time the tap water gets down to like 45-50 degrees this winter.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-11T02:56:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-50858>

### ealachan — 2012-09-11T17:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-50950>

**Leptin Reset, Day 185**

b: two strips of bacon, tin of water-packed sardines, two eggs, coffee w/ hwc, butter, co and 1tbsp cocoa

l: nyet

d: leftover chili

\- dinner last night was Quick and Dirty Chili, so named because it doesn't cook all day like my normal chili does. I have a couple tricks I use to cram as much flavor as I can into the mix in a short amount of time - first being that I use a little chopped bacon to saute the chopped veggies in...that gives a nice smokey base to the chili right off the bat. The other trick is to add all my spices to the cooked meat / veg and then let it cook without adding any liquid for a few minutes. This helps take the rawness off the spices, makes them bloom, kind of. It's the same concept as when you toast spices before adding them to a curry. Anyway - chili consisted of ground beef and ground pork, a little bacon, onion, garlic, green pepper, mushroom, carrot, canned tomatoes, spices...and then I topped the servings with chopped avocado and a little cheddar cheese. It was good stuff, and we have enough leftovers for dinner tonight as well. Chili is one thing that hubs never minds eating two nights in a row!

\- drank too much wine last night and was tossy-turny in the middle of the night. Also had a weird headache when I woke up, which I never get from wine normally, but this wine was not my normal brand nor my preferred variety...so I'm going to blame that. Good thing it's gone now.

\- gorgeous sunrise this morning, but I forgot to take a picture. Nice to finally see the sun first thing, after so many foggy mornings in a row! Looking at the sun this morning was like flipping a switch in my head again - I went from bleary-eyed and groggy to wide awake and ready to go in literally the space of about 60 seconds. SO COOL.

\- totally off blood pressure med for a week now, and things are holding steady in the 115/75 average range...WOO HOO!

\- stomach fat feels different lately - looser, more fluid. I've definitely been shrinking, but in odd places where people don't usually measure, if that makes sense...lol I can see my knees again - like, the actual kneecap and stuff. My knees have just been vauge round things for a really long time. Calf definition is getting a lot better, which, considering I was on a diuretic before and I used to get swollen lower legs at the drop of a hat, I take this as a really good sign! Upper arms aren't getting smaller yet, but fat distribution is definitely changing. Sides of stomach have wrinkles of loose skin now when I "suck it in". Also, I now have a slightly indented line from sternum to belly button, just like what people with washboard stomachs have, except of course mine is on fat, not muscle. It's fun and new none the less, though...

\- I just feel GOOD. Life's not perfect, work is not ideal, my husband still leaves wet towels on the bathroom floor, but none of that bothers me nearly as much as it did even a month ago. Maybe it's just a product of where I am in my cycle? Maybe my husband is secretly spiking my coffee with happy drugs. Don't know...but as long as it doesn't go away, I'm totally ok with it.

### ealachan — 2012-09-12T16:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51193>

**Leptin Reset, Day 186**

b: can o' salmon, three strips o' bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ hwc, butter and co, last of the cocoa...\*pout\*

l: -

d: not sure yet

\- dinner last night was left-over chili with some cheddar cheese, and a glass of wine. Was in bed by 8:15 last night, dropped off to sleep after about 15 min or so. Woke up right before hubs' alarm went off at 3:45, then dozed unti he woke me up at 4. Took him to work, came back home and went back to bed. Oxytocin'ed up, then slept like a rock until 7.

\- wearing a skirt to work today that just barely fit but wasn't comfortable when I bought it a month or so ago. It now fits perfectly and is totally comfortable. I went for literally YEARS without wearing skirts in public because I hated my legs so much. A combination of maturing (ie: getting to that "who \*\*\*\*ing cares if anyone doesn't like my legs?!" stage of a 30-something) and my legs starting to look better has broken me out of that habit. Yay! Now I just have to remember to conduct myself like a lady when I have a skirt on. THAT is the really hard part for me...LOL Always been kind of a tomboy.

\- A friend of mine who had either gastric bypass or a lap band (can't remember which) about a year ago is now on Facebook complaining that even though she's eating 1400 calories a day and doing 30 min of cardio a day, she's gaining weight. I feel bad for her, but my few suggestions that she try something totally different (ie: epi-paleo) have been met with responses that it's a little too far out for her. It makes me sad. I feel so much better personally, and I really wish people were open-minded enough to give this stuff a try themselves. Oh well...can't save 'em all, I guess.

> b: can o' salmon, three strips o' bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ hwc, butter and co, last of the cocoa...\*pout\*
>
> l: -
>
> d: not sure yet
>
>
>
> \- dinner last night was left-over chili with some cheddar cheese, and a glass of wine. Was in bed by 8:15 last night, dropped off to sleep after about 15 min or so. Woke up right before hubs' alarm went off at 3:45, then dozed unti he woke me up at 4. Took him to work, came back home and went back to bed. Oxytocin'ed up, then slept like a rock until 7.
>
>
>
> \- wearing a skirt to work today that just barely fit but wasn't comfortable when I bought it a month or so ago. It now fits perfectly and is totally comfortable. I went for literally YEARS without wearing skirts in public because I hated my legs so much. A combination of maturing (ie: getting to that "who \*\*\*\*ing cares if anyone doesn't like my legs?!" stage of a 30-something) and my legs starting to look better has broken me out of that habit. Yay! Now I just have to remember to conduct myself like a lady when I have a skirt on. THAT is the really hard part for me...LOL Always been kind of a tomboy.
>
>
>
> \- A friend of mine who had either gastric bypass or a lap band (can't remember which) about a year ago is now on Facebook complaining that even though she's eating 1400 calories a day and doing 30 min of cardio a day, she's gaining weight. I feel bad for her, but my few suggestions that she try something totally different (ie: epi-paleo) have been met with responses that it's a little too far out for her. It makes me sad. I feel so much better personally, and I really wish people were open-minded enough to give this stuff a try themselves. Oh well...can't save 'em all, I guess.

Yay on the skirt! Good for you princess!

I've been there on the gastric bypass! Just wait till her vagus nerve re-wires and the uncontrollable eating starts back up!

### ealachan — 2012-09-13T16:03:51+0300

**Leptin Reset, Day 187**

B: can of sardines, two eggs, strip of bacon, coffee with all the fat (which I just realized is probably cancelling out the fish I'm eating...whoops)

L: probably something

d: not sure

\- dinner last night was steak, sauteed kale with mushrooms and onions, and some steamed asparagus. Also some chocolate. No booze, woo!

\- was in bed by 10ish, oxytocin therapy, slept like a rock until 7:30 this morning.

\- Day off today - waiting for the plumber to come fix the kitchen sink, then we're going to the fair! woo, fair! Unreasonably excited for the fair.  We...may eat bad things there. I can't lie. It will be fun, though. YAY FAIR!!

### ealachan — 2012-09-14T17:07:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51640>

**Leptin Reset, Day 188**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, coffee w/ all the fats, cup of green tea on the way to work

L: -

d: dunno yet

\- ate some horrible things at the fair yesterday, but managed to not get any cotton candy, and talked myself right out of fried dough, which I was very proud of. It was still a pretty bad food day, though. And fun. So much fun.  Walked a couple miles, got lots of sun and fresh air, got to pet lots of cows and goats and sheep, and just generally had a really good day out with my husband. We took the long way back home and it was a lovely scenic drive, and a very nice ending to the day.

\- got new shoes yesterday! My sneakers died a stinky and painful death when we were on vacation a month or so ago, so Hubs took me to get some new ones yesterday. They're Keens and they're super comfy, even for my big paddle-feet.

\- lots to do at work and not really motivated to do it. I just want to go out and lay in the sun.

### ealachan — 2012-09-15T17:22:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51812>

**Leptin Reset, Day 189**

b: two chicken sausages, two eggs, coffee w/ all the fats

l: -

d: not sure yet. thinking something fishy.

\- dinner last night was not optimal, but we were pressed for time. I got a rotisserie chicken from the deli and we each had a leg plus some salad. It supposedly didn't have any MSG in it, but I got a pretty banging headache later on in the evening, so I'm suspicious. I'll let hubs eat the rest of the chicken for lunch today and be done with that. We used to eat that crap all the time...hard to believe nowadays.

\- got to bed around 9:30ish, fell asleep quickly, woke up around 3:30 when hubs' alarm went off, laid there half-awake for 20 minutes until he finally got up. Managed to get my blue-blockers on before he started turning lights on...haha! Took him to work, got back home at 4:30 and went back to bed, oxytocin'ed up, zonked out and didn't wake up until 8:30!! Went right out and got some sun first thing, which is good because its gone behind a cloud now...boo.

\- weather is supposed to be super nice tomorrow. I want to get up in the morning and do something fun outside, but I'm at a loss as to what. We live in an apartment, so we don't really have any outside space to ourselves that I could work in, and I don't really want to go for a hike by myself (hubs has to work in the morning again tomorrow). I don't know. Maybe I'll just take the dog over to the lake and walk around over there for a while. He's not very good walking on the road, so that idea doesn't appeal to me hugely, but we'll see.

\- not sure if I'm imagining it, but it seems a weird thing to imagine: my eyebrows are growing in thicker lately? They've always been pretty sparse, but they do seem more substantial lately. interesting.

### ealachan — 2012-09-15T21:37:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51855>

I'm on a mission to get oysters for tonight. They're all I've been able to think about for a week now. Must have.

### ealachan — 2012-09-16T17:00:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51923>

**Leptin Reset, Day 190**

b: three eggs, sauteed kale, squirt of Sriracha, fat coffee

l: -

d: fish goddamnit!

\- last night's dinner ended up not being fish at all. I made meatballs and tomato sauce and had it over roasted spaghetti squash. Chocolate and macadamia nuts for dessert. NO BOOZE!

\- wasn't feeling the BAB this morning for some reason, so I listened to my body and had sauteed kale and eggs instead. It wasn't that I wasn't hungry, I just couldn't muster my normal enthusiasm for meaty breakfast things. Kale and eggs with fat coffee hit the spot. Will be interested to see how long it lasts me before I get hungry again.

\- went to bed around 8 last night, oxytocin'ed up, was zonked out by 9ish. Slept fine for a while, then got tossy-turny later on in the night. Got up at 4 to take hubs to work, went back to bed when I got home, oxytocin'ed up again (I am a machine...lol), zonked out again, woke up at 8. Sunlight right away, perked right up. Went out to walk the dog in shorts and a tank top, came back in and saw the weather-bug on the computer claiming that it was 45 degrees out. WTF?! I wasn't the least bit chilly while I was outside. The sun was nice and warm on my skin, there was a cool breeze, but I was by no means cold. If this is cold adaptation, I'll take it! Been sitting here with all the windows open, still in my shorts and tank top, with a breeze blowing in and goosebumps on my legs, but I don't actually feel cold at all. craziness.

\- currently having a conundrum re: breakfast - right now, I have a big cup of coffee w/ fat added, along with some eggs and then either meat or fish. My problem is that the sat fat in the coffee and the eggs is likely cancelling out any good the DHA in the fish would be doing me. But...the fat is what keeps me full all day long. Do I ditch the fat coffee and eggs and just eat fish for breakfast, and start eating lunch again? Or do I keep on with what's worked really well fullness-wise for me for months now, and just try harder to get my fish in at other times of the day? This would certainly be easier if I DID eat lunch, but I normally don't WANT lunch...so I don't really know what to do.

### chocolate — 2012-09-16T17:21:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51925>

Can't you do olive oil with fish some way? I'm so limited with what I can eat. I use lettuce and olive oil to get the oil down. And I went back to the odd fish oil with cooked fish. Life Extension just posted a dissection of the fish oil study.

### ealachan — 2012-09-16T17:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-51930>

> **chocolate;52540 wrote:** Can't you do olive oil with fish some way? I'm so limited with what I can eat. I use lettuce and olive oil to get the oil down. And I went back to the odd fish oil with cooked fish. Life Extension just posted a dissection of the fish oil study.

I love olive oil...I can drink it straight. I don't want to put it in my coffee, though...

My conundrum is more an issue of whether I should stick with the sat-fat-heavy breakfast / protein-heavy dinner template that's been working pretty well for me hunger-wise, or if I should try switching to less fat in the mornings so that I want lunch, at which point I could eat fish without having to worry about combining it with too much sat-fat.

I know if I ask Jack, he'll tell me I need tests to decide...which is fine, I know I do, but I can't afford them quite yet.

### ealachan — 2012-09-17T16:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-52102>

**Leptin Reset, Day 191**

b: two pork sausages, two eggs, coffee w/ co and butter (trying to lay off the HWC for a while)

l: can of olive-oil packed tonno (tuna)

d: piiiiiiiiiig. There's been a pork butt in the crock pot since bedtime last night. OMG, THE SMELL. It was hard to not get into it for breakfast this morning.

\- dinner last night is documented above. I noticed something last night - I eat WAY slower now than I used to, and way, WAY slower than my husband does. It's almost a little disturbing. His plate is clean when I'm like halfway done. I wonder if he even tastes half of what he eats sometimes. He says it's because he's always really hungry, and because my cooking is really good. I'm going to try not to notice it so much, because it's one of those things that truly doesn't matter one bit, and yet I fixate...lol

\- went to bed around 8:45 last night, read for a while. No oxytocin therapy, as Hubs was zonked out from having been up at 4am. Had a hard time falling asleep, but finally dropped off maybe half an hour after I shut the light off. Must have slept in a weird position all night because I woke up with a really stiff neck. Boo. Also, I love my dog to bits, but he needs to learn to sleep at the end of the bed rather than in between Hubs and I. He only weighs like 13lbs, but you'd think he was a full-sized cinder block when he's weighing the blankets down on the bed!

\- going to try eating lunch this week even though I'm likely not going to be hungry. I figure if I can force some fish down every day at lunch, it's better for me in the long run than skipping lunch entirely and then running the risk of not getting enough fish in.

\- my nails are growing super fast and my hair seems to be as well, but I still have all these annoying short broken hairs around my temples and widows' peak that look really bad. I wish they'd hurry up and grow in so that I don't look like quite so much of a nutter. I'm not sure how I managed to break the hair off like that because I don't style it - the most I ever do is pull it back in a ponytail. I HAVE been washing it every day (with regular shampoo) all summer long...maybe it's dried out from that? It doesn't look / feel dry at all, but maybe the shampoo has made it fragile or something. I need to start doing every-other-day washes to prepare for this winter anyway. If I wash it every day during the winter, it turns into static city, and that's no good.

### ealachan — 2012-09-18T17:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-52350>

**Leptin Reset, Day 192**

b: one each pork sausage and chicken sausage (using stuff up), two eggs, coffee w/ co and butter, handful of wakame

\- green tea w/ nothing added, mid-morning -

l: tin o' smoked herring

d: something with leftover pork

\- dinner last night was pulled pork. I used the recipe from Nomnompaleo.com, which is just a pork butt (shoulder), three strips of bacon, and some red Hawaiian salt. Stick it all in a crockpot for like 16 hours, then remove, shred and enjoy. It was really good - we made Asian-style lettuce wraps with it: lettuce, pork, a little hoisin sauce, squeeze of lime juice, scallions, red pepper, shredded carrot, cilantro. Pick up and eat like a taco. Yum! The pork was pretty fatty and rich, though...I ate too much, and felt a bit ill a few hours later, like it was having trouble leaving my stomach or something. I remembered my enzymes before we ate, so thinking I just ate too much flat out. Been a while since that's happened!

\- got to bed around 9:45 last night after a round of oxytocin therapy. Dropped off to sleep ok but then woke up and was kind of tossy-turny on and off all night. Again, think it may be a product of having eaten too much, because I was pretty indigestion-y while tossing and turning as well. Got up around 5:45 to pee, went back to bed and slept until 7. Dark dreary morning...bleh. Woke up with kind of a halfway-stuffed nose, as well. Annoying.

\- cold shower this morning - skin got down to 67 in about 5 minutes. Need to suck it up and start filling the tub and getting in it tonight. I know it won't be that bad once I actually DO it, I just...keep finding other things to do instead...lol

### ealachan — 2012-09-19T17:12:13+0300

b: two eggs, two pork sausages, guacamole (1/4 cup, maybe?), salsa (couple tablespoons), coffee w/ CO and butter

l: can o' olive-oil packed tonno

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was the left-over pork on a big bed of lettuce with some chopped green and red pepper, salsa and home-made guacamole. I'm pretty sure I could happily eat guacamole every day for a week and never get sick of it. Had some dark chocolate chips and macadamia nuts for dessert.

\- got to bed around 9:30-ish. Had no problems dropping off, slept hard until 2:15 when I woke up thinking I could smell something weird. I got up and investigated but it must have just been a dream. I laid there maybe 15-20 minutes before I fell back to sleep. Woke up once more around what was probably 5ish, then slept until 6:45.

### ealachan — 2012-09-20T17:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-52902>

**Leptin Reset, Day 194**

_In which our heroine is sitting at her desk shivering...and loving it._

b: two eggs, two pork sausages, coffee w/ co and butter and half an ounce of unsweetened baker's chocolate

l: can o' tonno and some decaff green tea

d: not sure yet - early night tonight, so needs to be something fast. probably steak.

\- dinner last night was SO GOOD: mushrooms, garlic, shrimp, linquica sausage, olive oil, asparagus, parsley, sherry, a little butter, juice of half a lemon. I did it all in one big pan, then spooned it into bowls and we went to town on it. It was quick, easy, and SO GOOD. Gotta make it again and write it up for the blog.

\- got to bed kind of late last night - 10:15ish. Couldn't seem to get comfy or settled down. Finally fell asleep, woke up in the middle of the night all tossy-turny again. I'm hoping that this is a passing phase in my personal evolution and isn't going to become a chronic thing.

\- cold shower this morning (as every morning!) - skin was 90 degrees before I got in, 68 when I got out. Took my oral temp as 98.2 before shower, 98.8 when I got out...yee hah! Sitting here at work now with all the windows open, shivering and goose-bumpy, with my co-workers wearing sweaters... Bring it! Bring on the cold! I love it. It makes me feel alive.

\- batteries in the scales at home are dead. Gotta remember to get some more. I've lost a couple inches off my belly in the last couple weeks, so really curious to see if I've dropped any pounds.

\- I SWEAR I can see better. I'm due to get my eyes checked anyway, and I'm REALLY curious to see how it goes. These glasses are two years old and scratched to \*\*\*\*, but I can see far-away stuff much clearer than I could even a few months ago, I swear it. If it's some kind of optical illusion or something, then it's a damn impressive one.

### ealachan — 2012-09-21T15:36:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53152>

> **Lyndra;53661 wrote:** Kilts and headbanging. There must be a story in there...

My husband is Welsh and very proud of his Celtic heritage. When we were planning our wedding, he said he wanted to wear a kilt for the wedding, as that's quite traditional in Celtic families. His best man wanted to match, so he wore one too...and they looked quite spiffy, if I do say so myself.  The headbanging was just a product of good music and a fair amount of booze at the reception...LOL We're into rock and heavy metal, so it was kind of inevitable that the DJ would play something we'd head-bang and play air guitar to...

This was me and the hubs, before the headbanging...haha! It's hard to see the pattern on his kilt because it's dark, but it's the Pride of Wales tartan, which is blue, black and green with a thin red stripe running through. Also, I knit that lace shrug that I have on in the picture :

### ealachan — 2012-09-21T15:42:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-53154>

I had no idea just how many working parts there are to a dress kilt set-up until the rentals showed up in huge boxes a couple days before the wedding. Shirt, vest, kilt, hose, flags, sporran, those crazy shoes, jacket, kilt pin, jacket pin, tartan thingy to pin to the shoulder...I think my husband probably had a longer / harder time getting dressed for the wedding than I did. All I had to do was get my hair did and put a big poofy dress on!
