### ealachan — 2012-10-26T17:50:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60318>

Nope, I don't do DHEA, 7keto or otherwise. Dunno what the hair loss thing was, but I was losing a fair amount daily all summer long. Adding seaweed to my diet SEEMS to have slowed it down after a few weeks, so I'm guessing it was thyroid-related? I'm planning on getting my doctor to order hormone tests (and thyroid tests) when I see her next, but that's not until December.

### ealachan — 2012-10-26T17:58:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60319>

**EP Journal, Day 225**

b: 1.5 sausages, 2 strips bacon, 2 eggs, sauteed mushrooms, half a tomato, black tea

l: not gonna need it, pretty sure.

d: dunno yet

\- hubs had to go get his blood drawn at stupid-oclock this morning and had to be fasting, so we didnt have breakfast until we got home. We were pretty hungry, so I did a home version of the "full english" breakfast...minus the puddings, beans and fried bread of course.  It was good, but I'm pretty sure I'm not going to be hungry for lunch now!

\- stayed up way too late last night as hubs had to work a close and I didn't want to go to sleep and not hear the phone...so I was up until almost 1am. The up-side was that I slept like a baby until 6:30 this morning. Have a feeling going to bed early tonight won't be a problem.

\- annoying post-nasal drip thing making me cough and clear my throat a lot today. Gotta love allergies. My house is pretty dusty...that may not be helping.

\- thinking about trying quickly-seared heart for dinner rather than making it all into stew. Hubs isn't going to be around to see my gross-out face if things go awry, so maybe this weekend I should try liver, as well...? hehehe

\- wanted to get the nurse at the clinic to weigh me after she did the blood draw, but it was the bitchy nurse that I dislike, so I didn't bother. She would have made it into a big thing and had to make an appoiintment out of it, which isn't worth it to me, frankly. I don't care that much. LOL

### ealachan — 2012-10-27T17:17:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60443>

**EP Journal, Day 226**

b: leftover ground beef cooked with onion, garlic, fresh thyme, and some kalamata olives, two eggs, coffee w/ CO, a little hwc and 1 tbsp cocoa

l: dunno...may skip it

d: probably beef stew, unless I get some other genius stroke of inspiration

\- dinner last night was ground beef cooked as above. Didn't eat til 7ish...just wasn't feeling it. Went to bed around 9:30, had no problem dropping off, but the dog woke me up around 12:30 and then again at 5:45. Went back to sleep with no trouble each time, though I did wake up really hot and sweaty at 12:30 and actually turned the a/c on. Slept deeply and had sad dreams about people long gone from my life. Finally got up around 8, took Mr. Doggie for his walk, had some breakfast and have been sitting on my arse ever since. It's grey and misty out...not great for motivational purposes. I'm solar-powered...I do better when it's sunny out!

\- back on the ox bile as of this morning...hopefully my guts will thank me.

\- lots of housework to do, got some beef bones to make broth with (so time to learn how to use the pressure cooker...whee!), got some pumpkin I want to make into crust-less pie, and I'm meant to be having my parents up for dinner tonight.

### ealachan — 2012-10-28T04:46:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60512>

Did half an hour of CT this morning in the tub - 57 degree water. Oral temp was 98.8 to start, 99.2 at finish. Skin temp got down to 60 degrees. No shivers, no goosebumps, and NO "aftercold", which is a first.

Made pork roast stuffed with spinach, mushrooms, garlic, feta cheese and sundried tomato pesto for dinner, along with roasted Brussels and potatoes. It was good, but missing my fish. Can't wait for payday. May have to celebrate with oysters...lol

Back is hurting...not sure what I did to it.

### ealachan — 2012-10-28T17:59:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60577>

**EP Journal, Day 227**

b: two eggs, two sausage patties, some potato homefries, coffee w/ half and half, one half of one slice of rye toast with marmalade

l: probably some bone broth and the leftover pork from last night

d: heart!

\- went to bed around 11 last night. Woke up at 3.30am with the realization that....TMI...shark week had hit, and HARD. Ugh. Super heavy out of nowhere. I knew it was coming and I had been spotting lightly all day, but this was just...flood proportions. Yuck. ANYWAY - went back to bed and woke up at 7:15 when my phone went off...parents asking me to go to breakfast with them. Had a kind of crappy diner breakfast, then went shopping with my mom. Back home now and relaxing with the pup.

### ealachan — 2012-10-29T18:09:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60730>

**EP Journal, Day 228**

b: two eggs, two pieces of bacon, coffee w/ CO and cocoa

l: roast beef sandwich - had a lunch meeting and it was all that was around.

d: not sure yet...depends if there's power or not.

\- went to bed at 9:30 last night. Slept well until 5ish, when I woke up needing the bathroom. Went back to sleep until about 6:45, then got up and walked the dog. No sunshine yet again...we're waiting on the stupid hurricane to hurry up and slam us. They've been talking about it since middle of last week. I'm all for making sure everyone is informed, but this is the most drawn-out pre-hurricane media blitz I've ever seen.

\- TMI ALERT: super heavy flow this morning again, ugh. Really hoping it lets up soon as this is getting mighty old. Really curious as to what would make it suddenly be so much heavier than I'm used to...? If anyone has any insight into that, please feel free to share.

\- Hoping my boss sends us home early due to the weather...assuming the weather ever actually shows up...

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-29T18:12:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60731>

Additional CT is releasing old and bastardized estrogen stores from fat cells. Causing havoc with the lady stuff. Expect a really long week. . .going into 2 weeks. What fun!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-29T18:14:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60732>

Also don't be completely alarmed if you have a change in normal clotting patterns - and by change I mean you could throw clots that make you think you just had a miscarriage.

### diane — 2012-10-29T19:04:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60746>

Wow - Cavemam your response was a mixed blessing. One - I read it while I was eating lunch  but two - that's exactly what happened to me last TOM. Mine was almost 2 weeks, and just as you described. I was starting to worry - but if it's old stuff releasing, that's a good thing! Thanks for that!

### ealachan — 2012-10-29T19:04:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60747>

> **Cavemam;62427 wrote:** Also don't be completely alarmed if you have a change in normal clotting patterns - and by change I mean you could throw clots that make you think you just had a miscarriage.

That's pretty much been my norm for a few years now, clot-wise. Barf-tastic. I really hope it doesn't last any longer than normal. Just flush out and be done with it, uterus! I'd like to get on with my life.

### ealachan — 2012-10-29T19:21:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60750>

What ISN'T listed somewhere as a sign of iodine deficiency? LOL

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-29T19:41:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60753>

It was, for me, a sign that the kilo's worth of fibroids I'd been growing were now doing the bleeding. You may want to rule that out just in case.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-29T19:42:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60755>

> **diane;62442 wrote:** Wow - Cavemam your response was a mixed blessing. One - I read it while I was eating lunch  but two - that's exactly what happened to me last TOM. Mine was almost 2 weeks, and just as you described. I was starting to worry - but if it's old stuff releasing, that's a good thing! Thanks for that!

I think I was an early adapter on the fat-lady-ct rollercoaster. I went in guns freezing - and thought I was dying of fibroids or something. Quit the CT then had a MAJOR workup by lady-doc, lady parts had nothing wrong - doc even said they looked amazing for my age. AND it quit when I quit the ct for the summer. Only took one cycle to get back to non-heart-stopping. In fact, after it went to a perfect 28 day cycle with only 2 days of real bleeding!

### ealachan — 2012-10-29T19:48:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60757>

I don't have fibroids. They checked me for that when I had some ovarian cysts checked last summer. Also, I was told that my cysts were all of normal size / function and were not PCOS.

### diane — 2012-10-29T20:25:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60766>

Now I want to do more CT to see what else can get screwed up to get fixed! LOL.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-29T21:00:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60772>

It does give you some wierd sense of accomplishment. The other really "freaky" thing for me was in a few days time I busted out with skin tags all over my shoulders and side of my neck. Big red ones. Those went away after awhile, but no like!

### ealachan — 2012-10-29T21:17:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60774>

> **Cavemam;62468 wrote:** It does give you some wierd sense of accomplishment. The other really "freaky" thing for me was in a few days time I busted out with skin tags all over my shoulders and side of my neck. Big red ones. Those went away after awhile, but no like!

I have thankfully not gotten those, but I'm not going as cold as you were, either. I do have a monster zit brewing on my chin, though. I think I'm going to name it Sandy, after the bitchy hurricane that I'm sick of hearing about.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-29T23:58:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60810>

### ealachan — 2012-10-30T16:04:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60914>

> **Jack Kruse;62506 wrote:** I have my own hurricane Sandy I live with everyday.......She really lowers my "pressure in millibars" and causes tsunami's on my beach

### ealachan — 2012-10-30T16:15:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-60921>

**EP Journal, Day 229**

b: 1 chicken sausauge, 2 eggs, coffee w/ CO and cocoa, a cup of pumpkin custard that I did not need, more coffee w/ half and half on the way to work.

l: can of tuna. Forgot my dulse at home.

d: not sure yet.

\- went to bed around 8:30 last night, read for half an hour, then gave my husband his birthday present...ahem. Slept well, aside from being woken up by the howling wind a couple times in the night. Woke up around 6:30 without an alarm even though the window was covered up. The only time I seem to have trouble waking up in the mornings is the two or three days right before my period starts, when I feel like there's not enough hours in the day that I could sleep. Interesting.

\- TMI ahead - the red flood of Shark Week doom dissipated quite a bit last night, thankfully. It's acting much more like my normal period now. I only had one bout of mild cramps for about 5 minutes when the whole thing kicked off over the weekend. Pretty sweet. I used to get cramps that made me hurt from my knees right up through to my waist. Haven't had cramps like that in months now, and I sure don't miss them!

\- glad to report that we got nothing more than a bit of rain and some gusty wind from what was left of Hurricane Sandy. Parts of my state are still trying to recover from Irene last year, so I'm glad Sandy petered out before she got to us. I feel bad for the folks in NYC and Jersey, though.

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T14:12:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-61056>

**EP Journal, Day 230**

b: two eggs, 1 chicken sausage, coffee w/ CO and cocoa, more coffee w/ half and half on the way to work

l: tuna

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was steak and onions, mushrooms and green peppers cooked in olive oil and steak juice. I could happily eat this every day. I actually cooked us a sweet potato too, but when I went to mash it, it was a really sketchy color so I tossed it.

\- went to bed around 9:30 last night, zonked right out. I woke up once when some thunder rolled through, but went right back to sleep and slept deeply until 5:30 when the alarm went off. Only early morning this week, so I can't complain too much I guess.

\- sitting here in my super awesome witch hat, Elvira shirt and far too much black eye-makeup. I love Halloween.  I tend to do the nerdy Halloween stuff at work during the day, then get my Samhain on at night.

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T14:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-61063>

### ealachan — 2012-11-01T16:48:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-61284>

Thanks for the compliments, everyone! Nice ego-boost to start off my work day...

**EP Journal, Day 231**

b: two eggs, two strips of bacon, half a can of salmon (oh breakfast salmon how I missed you!), coffee w/ CO and cocoa

l: can of tuna

d: going to a birthday party - planned menu is lasagna made with GF noodles, plus salad and some GF bread that I'll skip. I"m making an antipasti platter for appetizers.

\- went out for dinner last night and went way off the rails, diet-wise...had pasta AND beer AND a piece of bread. No cheesecake though, so I get points for that, right?  I am paying for my wheaty transgressions today with bloat and gas, and I've re-learned my wheat lesson for a while now...ugh.

\- pulled something in my back yesterday evening. Still a bit sore today, but last night it literally hurt to breathe for a while there. I iced it but it didn't help much, so I did end up taking half a valium (5mg of a 10mg tablet) before bed as well to see if I could get it to unclench. It must have helped a little or at least made me not give a \*\*\*\*, because I slept from 10:30 right through until 6:30 this morning. Interesting, I didn't feel hung over when I woke up, which even half a valium tablet USED to do to me, back when I took it regularly. That doesn't mean I'm going to go start popping it regularly of course...it was just an interesting observation, to me at least.

\- doubled my CoQ10 dose from 100mg to 200mg, as I realized that 100mg really isn't that much. Still keeping my PQQ at 20mg, though. That \*\*\*\* is EXPENSIVE. I'm curious to know if PQQ makes a bigger difference to weight loss for the truly fat or if resveratrol does. I'd like to basically drop one and double up on the other, once I figure out which one will help me more.

\- I don't know if it's my hormones, my husband's hormones, a combination of the two, or the eye makeup I had on yesterday, but Hubs has been Mr. GrabbyHands lately, BIGTIME. It's a good thing I don't mind being randomly groped, because dude can't seem to keep his hands off me, which I find hilarious.

### ealachan — 2012-11-02T17:54:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-61560>

**EP Journal, Day 232**

_In which I am naughty...so naughty...\*grins\*_

B: half a can of salmon, two eggs, two slices bacon, coffee w/ co and cocoa

l: can of tuna

d: something fishy, now that I have money in the bank account again

\- last night I was a bad, bad girl. I went to a birthday dinner party. Dinner was gluten-free lasagna and salad, with gelato for dessert. The badness started, however, with margaritas. I had 4 of them. Now, before you get your knickers all in a twist, hear me out: they were made with low-sugar mix, which was then further diluted with real lime juice. And tequila. Kind of a lot of tequila. I didn't get WASTED, but...I got a damn good buzz on, and had a lot of fun. I also smoked three cigarettes. Whoopsies. The last remaining vestige of my hard-partying past. It was a good time, but needless to say, I won't be running out and doing it again any time soon. I think it's perfectly human and acceptable to let loose and make bad choices once in a while, and I certainly checked that box last night...haha!

\- got to bed around 10:45, and had...I will not lie to you here...a pretty mind-blowing oxytocin therapy session. Downside is that I didn't get to sleep until probably close to midnight, but it was WORTH it. Yeah buddy.

\- Hubs had a follow-up with our doctor about the blood labs they drew earlier in the week - we got gold stars across the board! HDL was up, LDL and trigs were down, he's lost 11lbs since we were there 3 months ago, his blood pressure was great, and we got a resounding "whatever you're doing, keep doing it!" from the doctor. YAY, EPI-PALEO!  He goes back in 3 months to have his A1C checked again, but he's checking his BG regularly at home and it's pretty steady for the most part, so we're not expecting anything but improvement with the A1C when the time comes. Really hoping I get as good of results when I have my labs done in December.

\- CT tonight. I haven't been tubbing because I've been on my period, but that's done now so I have no excuse. Tub! Tub! Tub! Tub!

\- doubled my PQQ this morning, so I'm up to 40mg. I feel good...like extra awake, but not in a jittery way. I feel like I'm firing on all cylinders. Vroom, vroom!

### ealachan — 2012-11-03T16:33:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-61771>

**EP Journal, Day 233**

b: three strips of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ co and cocoa, two squares of chocolate

l: not sure - may skip

d: not sure but likely won't be good for me

\- ended up making soup for dinner last night, and had some dark chocolate and a glass of wine. Got to bed around 9ish, had to get up and take hubs to work at 4am. Didn't go back to bed, unfortunately. Long story, won't bore you with it.

\- am at a games convention all day. We don't have a ton of people for our event, so hoping it will be quick and relatively painless. I'm not gonna lie to you...there may be beer later.

### ealachan — 2012-11-04T03:33:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-61904>

So, I restrained myself to two beers which, given the company I was with, was quite an accomplishment. We went out for dinner and I had some kind of chicken and sausage thing with risotto, which was kinda "meh", especially for what it cost. Also had dessert - cheesecake. I'm sure I'll pay for that tomorrow. My guts are already not happy with me.

### ealachan — 2012-11-05T01:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62101>

**EP Journal, Day 234**

b: two eggs, a chicken sausage, black tea with nothing added to it

l: burger with mushrooms, bacon and feta

d: super ridiculous meatloaf, roasted butternut squash

\- my meatloaf is in the oven, and it's truly epic. It's got spinach, parsley, carrot, celery, mushrooms, a little bacon, ground beef, spices, and it's wrapped in bacon. It's seriously a thing of beauty. Pretty excited to eat it.

\- did half an hour of CT in 56 degree water this afternoon. It was pretty non-exciting, but my husband straight-up JUMPED on me when I got out of the tub...yeee haw! I won't get into the nitty gritty details, but let's just say I've been a REALLY happy camper all afternoon since then, and I keep hearing about how round two is coming up after dinner...lol

### ealachan — 2012-11-05T14:55:43+0200

First snow of the season! I don't think it's cold enough to stick, but it's certainly coming down in a serious-looking manner. YAY SNOW!

### ealachan — 2012-11-05T15:10:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62196>

**EP Journal, Day 235**

b: half a can of salmon, two strips of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ butter and cocoa, two pieces of dark chocolate with mac nuts

l: tuna, maybe seaweed if I can remember to take some with me

d: possibly leftover meatloaf if hubs doesn't destroy it for lunch

\- dinner last night was really good. I used to HATE butternut squash because my mom only ever made it into mush and I just hated the texture so much. Now that I've figured out you can pan-roast it like potatoes and it gets nicely crispy on the edges, I'm kind of addicted. I roasted it with cumin last night. Next time I'm going to try sprinkling it with cinnamon, or maybe curry powder.

\- went to bed at 8pm, as we had to be up early. Had about half an hour of oxytocin therapy, then slept like the dead (happy, happy dead!) until 4:15 this morning. Took hubs to work, came home and went back to bed from 4:45 to 6:45. It was weird - I felt totally awake when I went back to bed, but I was out like a light within 5 minutes of laying down.

\- Bright sunrise this morning as the clouds hadn't moved in yet. Walked the pup in the sunshine and 35 degree weather with just tshirt and yoga pants and mittens on. Felt shivery the whole time, then I realized I had forgotten my hat. Seems like with hat and mittens on, I can be out there in a tank top and be perfectly fine, but if I forget the hat or mittens, I just want to go inside ASAP.

### ealachan — 2012-11-06T18:42:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62373>

**EP Journal, Day 236**

b: half a can of salmon, three strips of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ butter and cocoa

l: can of tuna, dulse, half an avocado, gelatin

d: crock-pot pig

\- last night we had leftover meatloaf along with some mashed sweet potato and some mushroom and onion gravy (home-made). Dessert was chocolate with mac nuts. Also had a glass (ok it was a coffee mug so I should probably call it two glasses) of wine.

\- got to bed around 10pm. Had a self-administered oxytocin therapy session (hubs was watching football...heh), zonked right out and slept like the dead until about 6:15 when it started getting light and the dog started whining. Got up at 6:30.

\- something weird - brushed my teeth with just baking soda for the first time, and it made my gums bleed! My gums haven't bled in ages, so I'm wondering if the baking soda alone is too abrasive or if I brush too hard or something? My teeth feel nice and clean, but it definitely ripped my gums up a bit...eek!

\- decided the other day on a whim that I should add some gelatin to my diet, as I can't seem to make myself drink bone broth regularly no matter how hard I try. I found an interesting idea in "Practical Paleo" where they take unflavored gelatin and make it with herbal tea instead of plain hot water. I tried it with chammomile tea, and the results are...interesting. It's funny to try and eat because it tastes like chammomile tea but my brain associates that taste with hot liquid, so when I get that taste from a cold solid, everything kind of goes "BWUHHHH?!" I think next time I'll use just a smidgen of honey, but all in all it seems pretty palatable. It's easy to just chuck a couple tablespoons of it into a baggie and eat at lunch time, too. I wonder if the gelatin would set properly if I used green tea, or if the tannins would mess it up. Hmmmm.

\-

### diane — 2012-11-06T19:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62383>

I'm so making your meatloaf this weekend! Yum!

I love bone broth. I bought a thermos so I can heat it up in the morning and bring it to work. Seems to be the best way for me to remember to drink it. I did put it in my travel mug for my morning commute, but it made the mug really greasy. I LOVE coffee - but it just puts me so out of whack. So now on to green tea.

### ealachan — 2012-11-06T19:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62388>

> **diane;64195 wrote:** I'm so making your meatloaf this weekend! Yum!
>
>
>
> I love bone broth. I bought a thermos so I can heat it up in the morning and bring it to work. Seems to be the best way for me to remember to drink it. I did put it in my travel mug for my morning commute, but it made the mug really greasy. I LOVE coffee - but it just puts me so out of whack. So now on to green tea.

The meatloaf is DENSE, for sure, but it's tasty. The mushrooms and spinach in the meat mix keep it nice and moist. Dry meatloaf is a sin against humanity!

re: broth - do you use just one kind of bones (ie: beef, chicken, pork), or do you chuck them all in together? I think mine might be severely lacking in salt, which makes it just not taste like much of anything. It's also a pain in the ass for me to store - I need to get some jars.

re: coffee - I love, love, love it. Nothing to do with the caffiene, even...I just love the taste of it. I like all kinds of teas too, and I drink lots of plain water...but there's nothing like a good cup of coffee first thing in the morning, for me. It makes me happy.

### diane — 2012-11-06T19:48:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62391>

I will let you know how the meatloaf turns out! I'm so excited. Which you can probably tell - I've only commented on it in 3 different places.

I use mainly beef for the broth - have thrown in some lamb too. Salt is key - I use celtic sea salt. I don't get fancy - no veggies, juts the bones, water, salt and a bay leaf. The batch I just made was a little bland - gonna need to add salt as I heat it up.

I don't store it in glass jars either. Once it's cool, I measure out into 1 cup portions, and put into freezer bags, then into the freezer it goes. That way I can keep it longer and not have to worry about it going bad. And most recipes call for a cup - so if I'm cooking, I just defrost a bag. It's pretty easy.

I despair every week about the coffee LOL. I give in on the weekends, then take 2 days during the week to shake the headaches. Which means it REALLY isn't good for me. C'est la vie!

> I don't store it in glass jars either. Once it's cool, I measure out into 1 cup portions, and put into freezer bags, then into the freezer it goes. That way I can keep it longer and not have to worry about it going bad. And most recipes call for a cup - so if I'm cooking, I just defrost a bag. It's pretty easy.
>

> I despair every week about the coffee LOL. I give in on the weekends, then take 2 days during the week to shake the headaches. Which means it REALLY isn't good for me. C'est la vie!

AHA! The freezer bag thing is brilliant! Will try that this weekend. The last batch I made didn't come out all gelatinous, but I used marrow bones and not knuckle bones, so I don't think there was much connective tissue to be had there.

oooh, yeah caffiene headaches aren't good. I have a 2-cup-per-morning coffee habit (it's actually one mug, but it holds 2 cups worth), and I usually drink a cup of decaff on the way to work. Anything more than that and I get all bouncy-off-the-walls, which is not good given my profession.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-06T20:36:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62400>

What IS your profession, btw? Professional trouble maker?  I don't know if you mentioned it anywhere and I'm inherently curious so ... .

### ealachan — 2012-11-06T21:08:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62407>

> **DarleenMB;64214 wrote:** What IS your profession, btw? Professional trouble maker?  I don't know if you mentioned it anywhere and I'm inherently curious so ... .

If I could figure out how to make money causing trouble then I'd sure as hell be doing it! Maybe I need to get into politics, in fact...

I am actually an accountant. Not a CPA, though...I thought about going to school for that but the descriptions of all the classes in the course catalog just bored me to \*\*\*\*ing tears, so I didn't bother...LOL! I do all of the accounts receivable functions (in other words, accounting for and applying in-coming monies to customer accounts) for a multi-million dollar company that re-sells computer equipment to the government. I also do some invoicing, and a lot of collections (because the govt doesn't always like to pay their bills, WHODA THUNK?!). Most of our customers are Dept of Defense related, so I do a whole lot of trawling through complicated and boring contracts, making sure all our I's are dotted and our T's are crossed. I also do some technical writing - tutorials on specific functions in our highly customized piece of crap software system, mostly.

In other words, I have the most boring job on the planet. It keeps me in canned salmon and PQQ supps though, so I shouldn't complain.

### ealachan — 2012-11-07T17:05:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62528>

> **janagram;64281 wrote:** pardon my nosiness, but what does ealachan come from? (Armageddon would be a cool username)

Oh I don't mind a bit! The word "ealachan" is actually an old Scots Gaelic word for "swan" (the modern word is just "eala"). It's roughly pronounced as "AY-lah-kan", emphasis on the first syllable. The "ch" sound in Gaelic is more gutteral than just a "k" sound, though...kind of like clearing crap out of your throat...LOL

I was studying old Scots Gaelic like 12-13 years ago, and that was one of the words that I learned. Long story made short, I started using it as my online user name because I liked the idea of reminding myself that I am something beautiful (the proverbial swan) even if no one understands it (literally, in the case of the word I was using, and figuratively in the case of my experiences with people in real life). Keep in mind I was 19 at the time, so it was all very deep and feely at the time.

It's also a good user name in a practical sense because no one else ever uses it, so it's never taken when I sign up for a website...

Hahaha...it's actually quite similar in Vermont. Interestingly, you're deemed a horrible filthy ignorant redneck in VT if you don't recycle, but most towns don't actually have recycling facilities with reasonable hours. And no curbside pick-up at all unless you live in a city. So basically you're stuck with trying to remember to haul your crap to the dump between the hours of 8-9:30am every third Saturday, unless it's a holiday in which case the open hours MIGHT be re-scheduled for like 3pm on a Wednesday afternoon...LOL That's semi-hyperbolic of course, but you see what I mean.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T17:32:30+0200

Penn and Teller have the most awesome show on recycling. Recycling = following a CW diet.

When I first moved here, I told the lady on the phone at the city "No, we don't need a recycle can -it takes up too much room in the garage". A heated debate ensued. I ended with "I'm from Texas. We don't recycle." I went on some list no doubt. But I've learned to keep my arse firmly hidden in the closet with that one.

Son goes to a super well meaning montessori school. Let's just say woo-woo runs deep there. They are indoctrinating him just fine into harming the world through unintended well meaning consequences. I pay them a lot for that service so I can be a crankly old lady looking out for the black helicopters.

### ealachan — 2012-11-07T17:41:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62546>

**EP Journal, Day 237**

b: two strips of bacon, 1 chicken sausage, two eggs, coffee w/ butter and cocoa, another cup of coffee on the way to work

l: tuna, half an avocado, dulse, gelatin

d: something involving the pork butt I cooked - maybe chili

\- dinner last night was mashed sweet potato and pulled pork (I did a pork butt in the crock pot). We had ice cream as well. Hubs keeps asking for sweet potatoes, like 3-4 times a week. I know they're good for us, but I'm starting to wonder if he's just craving them because they're sweet, and if they're messing up his blood sugar more than we realize. I'm still trying to feel out the balance.

\- went to bed around 10, went right to sleep. Slept soundly until about 6:30, got up at 6:45. I don't think I woke up once all night long, now that I think about it! WOO!

\- sinuses were kind of crappy this morning. Pretty sure it was the ice cream.

### ealachan — 2012-11-07T17:47:15+0200

We don't recycle." I went on some list no doubt. But I've learned to keep my arse firmly hidden in the closet with that one.
>

They are indoctrinating him just fine into harming the world through unintended well meaning consequences. I pay them a lot for that service so I can be a crankly old lady looking out for the black helicopters.

We used to recycle when we also did the recycling for my boss here at work weekly, because it was easy to just throw ours in with the load we were taking over anyway. Now we don't do that any more (hubs was doing it for extra money before he found full-time employment), and our town has a totally messed-up dump schedule for recycling, so we don't do it. I DO use re-useable shopping bags and I DO try to buy stuff with minimal packaging whenever possible (which, honestly? When you're not buying those boxes of 100-calorie cookie packs and other CW \*\*\*\*, it's pretty easy). I figure that way, we're creating a lot less trash anyway, and that's probably more meaningful in a Save The World way than recycling would be, anyway.

### ealachan — 2012-11-07T17:59:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62557>

His sugar isn't actually terrible though, that's the thing. It's not like squash and potato are spiking him astronomically. He stays right around 110-120 most of the time, which I realize is not IDEAL, but for an unmedicated diabetic, it seems decent? He hardly ever has big spikes unless he has a couple beers or something really sugary like ice cream.

### ealachan — 2012-11-07T18:05:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62561>

> **Anna K.;64390 wrote:** Just to help me understand, are you guys saying that if recycling was easy (like for example in my condo it's a separate bin next to the trash dump), you would do it? It's only because it's so difficult that you don't?
>
>
>
> Or you just don't believe in recycling, period?

We originally stopped doing it because we couldn't get to the facility when it was open, so yes, it was originally an ease-of-use thing. It's not that I don't BELIEVE in recycling, it's that I'm not entirely convinced that it has much of a positive impact on the environment when you start factoring in all the greenhouse gasses produced by the processing, transportation, and everything else involved. Also, I think to a certain degree that recycling has been used as an excuse to keep creating things in packaging that we don't need. I think I would rather see money be put into educating people to buy in bulk, re-use containers they already have, and choose to buy products with less packaging / biodegradable packaging. I think NO packaging has far more impact on the environment than RECYCLED packaging.

Also, I don't test his sugar, he does. He's a 38 year old man.

### ealachan — 2012-11-08T17:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62900>

**EP Journal, Day 238**

b: three strips of bacon, two eggs, salmon, coffee w/ cocoa and butter, piece of dark chocolate w/ macadamia nuts

l: tuna, dulse, half an avocado, gelatin

d: dunno yet

\- dinner last night was chili made from the leftover pork butt I did in the crock pot. It's just pork, a couple cans of diced tomatoes, some onion and carrot sauteed in bacon fat, and a crapload of spices: cumin, chili powder, cocoa, cinnamon, coriander, garlic, salt, oregano. I did also dump a beer in there.  I had it with half an avocado on top...yum! I drank a beer with it as well, and had some chocolate for dessert.

\- got Hershey's Special Dark chocolate chips because the grocery was out of my normal Ghiradelli ones. Blech. I mean, as blech as chocolate ever gets, anyway. It claims to be 60% dark, which is what Ghiradelli is, but it tastes much sweeter. I've never been fond of Hershey's chocolate - it's got an odd, burn-the-back-of-your-throat quality that I think might come from using cheaper beans. Anyway - I want to trawl around on Amazon today and see if I can find a source of 70% or higher chips. I like to melt them down, add my mac nuts, then spread it in a pan and let it harden and break it into pieces. We seem to be able to control our consumption better when we do it this way versus just keeping a bag of chocolate chips around and eating them out of hand.

\- went to bed around 10 last night. Slept deeply, woke up once in the night when hubs got up to use the bathroom, but went right back to sleep and didn't wake up until it started to get light in our room around 6:30. Had bizarre dreams about Dr. K coming to play cards with us and eating all the chocolate in my house! Had to laugh at that one when I woke up.

\- really feeling like I need a creative outlet lately. I knit, but that's more of a meditative thing...it doesn't really allow for a whole lot of soul-purging...LOL Maybe I need to get back into writing dodgey poetry. I used to do that quite a lot. Most of what I wrote was pretty \*\*\*\*-tastic, but I had a few pieces published when I was in college.

\- hoping to get some CT in tonight, as hubs will either be at work for a late shift, or will be curled up sleeping off the pain meds he's on for his toothache. He's got a molar that broke off years ago and he never had the remains of it pulled. It's now hurting him pretty badly, so he's waiting to hear back from the dentist about getting it yanked.

### ealachan — 2012-11-08T18:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62927>

I just had a thought while looking for chocolate on the internets, and now you're going to be subjected to it:

Paleo and Epi-paleo eating have been an interesting experience for me in that all the things I was never "allowed" to have growing up and on most CW diets (ie: steak! bacon! chocolate! butter! cream!), I am now allowed and even sometimes encouraged to eat. At first it felt SO NAUGHTY, like "oooh, I'm putting butter in my coffee, I HOPE THE DIET POLICE DON'T FIND OUT!"...but now it's just...normal. I think taking all of the naughty allure of those foods away, making them just every-day things for me, has worked some kind of magic in mah brains in a psychological sense. I don't feel bad when I eat a handful of dark chocolate chips anymore. I don't feel guilty when I eat three strips of bacon every morning for breakfast. I'm running out of things to mentally kick myself over...and that feels really foreign to me, but it also feels pretty good.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-08T19:22:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62938>

### ealachan — 2012-11-08T19:29:44+0200

### Lyndra — 2012-11-08T21:48:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-62977>

I spent my 20s and 30s eating virtually no fat so mentally getting used to eating fat has taken some time. Unfortunately even my with-it doctors are skeptical about eating saturated fat and worry about "high cholesterol" (mine is 225) being grounds for statins. I take the mostly-good I get from them and discard what doesn't make sense.

### ealachan — 2012-11-08T22:29:00+0200

Unfortunately even my with-it doctors are skeptical about eating saturated fat and worry about "high cholesterol" (mine is 225) being grounds for statins. I take the mostly-good I get from them and discard what doesn't make sense.

I'm insanely curious to see what my cholesterol numbers are in a couple months when I get my blood work done. Even when I weighed 400+ lbs, my cholesterol, trigs, and blood sugar were all very good. I had a (really bitchy and SUPER skinny) PA tell me once that I had no business having cholesterol numbers better than hers at my size.

### ealachan — 2012-11-11T15:42:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-63584>

**EP Journal, Day 240**

b: salmon, 6 strips of bacon (I was hungry), two eggs, coffee w/ butter and cocoa

l: not sure

d: also not sure yet

\- dinner last night was rapini with lots of garlic and italian sausage, a glass of wine and some chocolate. We ate pretty early (like 4:30), because a) we'd both skipped lunch and b) we were going to bed early anyway. It worked out well, although something we ate made both our guts sing really strange songs for like hours last night...which was amusing, until my husband started farting.

\- got to sleep around 9. Slept pretty deeply, though woke up a couple times to the dog whining. Woke up at 4 with no alarm, took husband to work (wearing my blue blockers, of course), came home and went back to bed. Weird sensation when I went back to bed - felt like I couldn't get warm for the longest time. It wasn't even that cold in here, nor was it terribly cold outside. I wasn't shivering, either...just felt chilly, even under the blankets. Weird. Woke up again around 7am with a dog in my face. We went for a quick walk, then came in and had breakfast. Not much in the way of sunshine this morning as it's socked in with clouds, but still nice at about 35 degrees.

\- Noticed last night that my belly looked a little different in the mirror so I busted out the tape...and I've lost 2" around my hips / lower gut in like a week, apparently?! I'm going to label it a fluke until it stays that way for a week, I think...hehe.

\- have a bit of a headache this morning. About to go fill up the bathtub and sit a spell, now that my big fat breakfast has had some time to settle.

### ealachan — 2012-11-11T17:13:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-63607>

Just got out of the tub. Cold as \*\*\*\*. Here is my data:

oral temp before: 98.1

beg water temp: 54.5

beg skin temp: 88

28 min in, oral temp was 98.7

duration: 35 min

ending oral temp: 99.2

water temp at end: 55

skin temp: 58

oral temp 5 min post: 98.8

Something new and different, possibly because of the slightly colder water: my feet and lower legs went numb about 8-10 min into my soak. Not NUMB like, couldn't feel them, but...numb. Hard to describe. It was sort of like everything got really tight, but not like muscle-cramp tight. Like wearing compression hose kind of tight, if that makes sense. It was odd. It went away a little bit when I started moving my legs around in the water. Also, my lower legs felt really tired when I first got out of the tub, wobbly, like I'd just climbed a steep hill.

I found it interesting that while the water is technically only 2 degrees colder than the last time I did CT, it felt so! much! colder! when I first got in. Not stabby-cold, but just "OMFG THIS SUUUUUCKS" cold for a few minutes, then I was ok. There was a lot of F-word yelling this morning, since no one was home...lol The poor dog sat there in the bathroom doorway looking at me like "bitch, you crazy." and I was somewhat inclined to agree.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-11T18:26:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-63617>

You can TALK while you're in the tub? All I can do is gasp for air until I numb up. And our tub is not anywhere near big enough to get all my parts in simultaneously. I can either sit with legs extended or lie back with legs out of the tub. And forget the front parts. The water only comes around my sides leaving the rest out in the air. Lovely mind picture.

I did find yesterday I can't take a lot of cold on my legs and esp not my feet so tomorrow I will wear socks. BUT on my back? meh. No big deal. Must be from all those cold showers I've been taking but I was quite comfy once I got scootched down. Tomorrow I'll try going 10 minutes with legs and feet submerged before I start on my back.

### ealachan — 2012-11-11T19:30:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-63629>

> **DarleenMB;65518 wrote:** You can TALK while you're in the tub? All I can do is gasp for air until I numb up. And our tub is not anywhere near big enough to get all my parts in simultaneously. I can either sit with legs extended or lie back with legs out of the tub. And forget the front parts. The water only comes around my sides leaving the rest out in the air. Lovely mind picture.
>
>
>
> I did find yesterday I can't take a lot of cold on my legs and esp not my feet so tomorrow I will wear socks. BUT on my back? meh. No big deal. Must be from all those cold showers I've been taking but I was quite comfy once I got scootched down. Tomorrow I'll try going 10 minutes with legs and feet submerged before I start on my back.

Our tub is the same way. It isn't huge /deep, and I'm both tall AND fat, so it's pretty much impossible to get a whole lot of me under the water. I just sit with my legs stretched out and lean back as much as I can. I will say, though - the first few times I sat in the tub, the water didn't even cover the tops of my thighs when the tub was full. Now it covers the tops of my thighs almost completely and I get some on my belly, too! Progress!  The cold bothers my feet, too. Sometimes one of my toes goes white and numb, but it always comes back eventually so I figure it's not too big of a deal...just more annoying than anything.

I've also figured out a neat trick - rather than keep having to turn the water on and off as I lose water to the overflow drain, I can just keep the tap running and flip the drain open with my toe as necessary if the tub gets too full.

### ealachan — 2012-11-12T17:15:06+0200

Hubs has decided that the 85% is not too bitter for him, so I'm psyched and I'm going to buy a whole bunch when I get paid, so that he doesn't have an excuse to go back to the 60% stuff!

I cooked some mushrooms and onions in the pan after I seared the meat, so they got a dose of spices, too. Very yummy. Bit of dark chocolate for dessert.

Hubs walked the dog, then dragged me back in the bedroom for pre-breakfast oxytocin! Apparently sleeping on it didn't solve the problem for him.

\- Out of a bunch of stuff I need - good butter, salmon, coconut oil, etc. Glad I get paid tomorrow.

\- runny nose I had yesterday had basically disappeared by mid-afternoon yesterday, and hasn't come back...hooray! Right ankle is still kind of swollen / weird, though...not sure where that came from. Boo.

### ealachan — 2012-11-14T16:54:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-64251>

**EP Journal, Day 243**

b: 4 strips of bacon, two eggs, coffee w/ hwc and cocoa

l: tuna with lemon

d: not sure yet, having company for dinner

\- dinner last night was pork chops (just spiced and baked, nothing fancy), kale sauteed with bacon, and some mushrooms stuffed with a little cheese because they caught my eye at the store. Also had a couple big glasses of wine - one on an empty stomach, hoo boy - and dark chocolate for dessert. Very proud of myself because my husband stood at the Ben & Jerrys cooler pointing out all the new flavors they had in stock and begging to buy some, and I said no, no, no, NO. He said "you're a strong woman" as we were walking away from the ice cream, and I was thinking "buddy, you have NO \*\*\*\*ing idea".

\- Went to bed around 9:15, slept soundly until 2ish when the dog had a retching spell that woke me up and made me check for barf on the bed...LOL There wasn't any, thankfully. Got up and had a pee, went back to sleep and slept pretty soundly again until just before 7am.

\- kind of sneezey again this morning, but nothing like I was over the weekend, thankfully. Hubs works in retail and comes into contact with all sorts of peoples' germs all day long, so the longer we manage to stay cold-free at our house, the more of a victory I see it as.

\- been having some mental struggles lately that I don't particularly feel like getting into here. I think I need to get back into doing some actual pen-to-paper journalling to see if I can purge some of this crap and/or make sense of it, otherwise it's just going to keep bothering me.

### ealachan — 2012-11-15T17:00:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-64485>

**EP Journal, Day 244**

b: half a can of salmon, 4 strips bacon, 2 eggs, coffee w/ hwc and cocoa

l: tuna, maybe?

d: something involving flank steak

\- ended up skipping lunch yesterday - didn't get hungry at all until around 4pm. Dinner last night was a really tasty (if I do say so myself!) pork stir-fry that I need to make again so I can take a picture and blog the recipe. I marinated a bunch of bits of pork in tamari, a little vinegar, lots of grated fresh garlic and ginger, some allspice, ground cloves, black pepper and a little bit of crushed red pepper. Stir-fried that in some olive oil, then stir-fried a whole mess of mushrooms, onions, red and yellow bell pepper, broccoli and some snow peas, added the meat and juices back in, added some beef stock with the same spices as before added, and gave it a couple minutes to mingle in the pan. Super yummy. I had too much wine and too much chocolate as well.

\- went to bed around 9:30ish, had no trouble falling asleep, but woke up sometime in the night with heart RACING. Guessing it was the combo of too much chocolate and too much wine finally catching up with me. I got back to sleep fairly quickly, but still. Going to lay off the wine for a few days, methinks. Woke up at 6:45 with a dog jumping all over me. I got up and took him out - 15 minutes outside in 20 degree air with just tshirt, yoga pants, hat and mittens on, wheeee!  I like 20-degree mornings. I like the crunch of the frosted grass, I like the way everything sparkles a little bit in the sunshine, I like STILL cold air a lot. It's when the wind kicks up that I tend to go into "get inside ASAP" mode.

### ealachan — 2012-11-16T17:40:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-64743>

**EP Journal, Day 245**

b: half a can of salmon, 4 strips bacon, coffee w/ butter

l: still have this can of tuna hanging around...

d: not sure yet

\- ended up not eating lunch as such again yesterday, although I did slip up and eat a piece of office birthday cake...which was too sweet but still pretty tasty. I also ate some dark chocolate during the day. Surely the two offset each other...

\- dinner last night was "fajitas" - flank steak spiced with cumin, chili, salt, pepper, garlic, lime, seared and cut into strips, served with sauteed onions, mushrooms and a yellow bell pepper I had hanging around. Also made quacamole - just avocado, lime juice and raw garlic - to go with it. I drank a beer as well.

\- was in bed by 10pm last night, oxy'd up, slept like a rock, woke up for a minute or so sometime in the night, woke up at 6:45 this morning via a combination of dog whining and sun in my eyes. Weird dreams again - I dreamt I was working for a tech company and one of my husband's friends worked there too, and he convinced the CEO of the company to basically rob the company via bank technology stuff, but they held us all hostage while they did it...very odd.

### ealachan — 2012-11-17T15:49:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-64933>

**EP Journal, Day 246**

b: salmon, 2 strips bacon, 2 eggs, coffee w/ butter and cocoa

l: dunno

d: dunno

\- skipped lunch again yesterday, just wasn't hungry. Dinner was cod with tomato olive tapenade and some mashed sweet potatoes. And ice cream. No booze though, so that's a small plus.

\- back started hurting yesterday afternoon. I think it's likely due to poor posture at work, combined with a crappy office chair. I took 800mg of ibuprofen last night and it dulled it enough that I could sleep, but I was tossy-turny all night (except that it hurt to move so I couldn't really toss and turn, I'd just keep waking up and grumbling...lol) and didn't get much quality sleep, I don't think. Still in pain this morning. Took some more ibuprofen with breakfast and am now sitting with a couple ice packs on my back. I'd like to get into the cold bath, but I'm pretty sure that I wouldn't be able to get out if I could get in. Pretty bummed, because I had a lot I wanted to do today.

### ealachan — 2012-11-18T17:48:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65147>

Took it easy all day yesterday - mostly sat around knitting and watching movies with the hubs, which was nice. Back is still a little sore, but not can't-move-at-all sore like it was Friday night and yesterday morning. Ibuprofen and ice has been helping.

Darleen - I've never been to a chiro, no. The idea kind of freaks me out a little, although I know they help a lot of people. It just seems like once you start going to one, you have to keep going, you know? Some of the ones around here seem to operate like that, anyway.

Got all my T-day shopping done this morning, so that's out of the way, thankfully! Hubs is working late on Wednesday night, so it should work out pretty well, as I'll be able to do all my prep ahead of time without feeling bad for spending all evening in the kitchen.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-18T18:36:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65157>

Re the chiro ... no you do NOT have to "keep going" any more than you have to keep going to the massage therapist. It's a good idea to get an occasional tune up but not necessary.

Our chiro (who is so danged busy it's a 6 week wait to get in to see him) also has a masters in sports medicine. He is FANTASTIC. He's so good and so well educated that DH's doc at the VA gave him permission to continue to treat Joe after his back and then neck surgery.

BTW it was the chiro who insisted that they do an MRI on Joe after the neck surgery. He had started having headaches which began right after the surgery and became so bad that he couldn't get out of bed AT ALL. The doc at the VA had decided he just had a sinus infection. I was at my wit's end because you know, men do not listen to their women. We don't know anything.

They scheduled the MRI for that day and found he had a hematoma that filled 1/4 of his skull. I am not kidding. I saw the images. The upper left quadrant of his head was full of blood. Good thing it was a slow bleed or he'd have died.

Anyway. Jim, our chiro, is the absolute BEST in my book. Besides saving DH's life, he helped his shoulder pain which they finally figured out was caused by the way he (hubby) was hanging off the train cars at work. He'd thought he was going to need surgery but Jim fixed it.  Jim uses a lot of additional therapies including deep tissue massage (OW!!!), heat, muscle stimulation with some kinda hoodoo electrical thingy (that's the technical term for it, btw). And when he's done he tells you to go home and ICE whatever was bothering you.

So ask around. I'd think you'd have a good chiro somewhere in the vicinity. We swear by 'em.

### LinD — 2012-11-18T19:51:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65173>

I didn't believe in chiros until back in late 2004/early 2005, I ended up with a C4/C5 issue (herniation). The therapy at the orthopedic office was not helping, and I chickened out on the cortisone injection. So I asked for prayer in Sunday school class when a fellow member told me his story. So I proceeded that route and after 5 weeks of therapy (3 days per), I was in tons better shape. Also, I recommend a book by McKenzie, "7 Steps to a Pain-Free Life: How to Rapidly Relieve Back and Neck Pain."

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/0452282772](http://http://www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/0452282772)

I still do the exercises occasionally, plus pay attention to my neck position all the time. I'm sure there is the same issue with backs.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-19T05:21:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65266>

Since it's your doing that I am now pretty consistently doing CT on a daily basis I thought I'd ask YOU .... are you just doing cold water or have you also added ice? I've been going over the protocol post again because I was thinking that after my 2nd week of playing ice queen I'd need to actually add some ice. Since I can do the big bag of ice on my bare belly when I'm not IN the tub I'm hoping it will be fine when I'm actually in the cold water. I hope.

### ealachan — 2012-11-19T17:19:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65350>

> **DarleenMB;67273 wrote:** Since it's your doing that I am now pretty consistently doing CT on a daily basis I thought I'd ask YOU .... are you just doing cold water or have you also added ice? I've been going over the protocol post again because I was thinking that after my 2nd week of playing ice queen I'd need to actually add some ice. Since I can do the big bag of ice on my bare belly when I'm not IN the tub I'm hoping it will be fine when I'm actually in the cold water. I hope.

I don't do ice because I'm a lazy \*\*\*\*, basically...LOL I don't see the point of schlepping re-usable ice containers back and forth every time for just a few extra degrees' difference, and I'm certainly not going to shell out for pre-made ice every time I want to take a bath. My tap water is currently coming out at about 52 degrees, and my last bath in that got my skin down pretty close to the "magic" 55 degree mark after 30 minutes, so I'm ok with it. I am hesitant to go super-hardcore-cold because I'd rather detox more gradually, at least for now. I have like 30 years' worth of bad \*\*\*\* floating around in my fat and I don't really want it all hitting my system in a short amount of time, since I have to actually function and work 40 hours a week and stuff. If I could take a couple months off and do nothing but CT and detox, then I'd be hitting it as hard as I could...but I have to balance this whole craziness with an actual LIFE, you know?

### ealachan — 2012-11-19T17:41:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65357>

**EP Journal, Day 247**

_In which our heroine's back STILL \*\*\*\*ing hurts, ugh!_

b: 3 strips bacon, 6-ish oz venison, two eggs, coffee w/ butter, hwc, cocoa

l: probably not going to need any

d: not sure yet

\- skipped dinner last night because we had a late lunch of sausage and spinach crustless quiche (blog post coming on that...it was wicked yummy) and neither of us were hungry by dinner time. Did have one beer, some dark choc and some walnuts around 6pm.

\- had quite a lot of oxytocin yesterday evening...heh. Went to bed at 10, fell asleep pretty quickly, slept fine until it started to get light out, then I woke up and couldn't get all the way back to sleep. Got up and walked the pup at 7am - it was 22 degrees out. Was out in tshirt, yoga pants, hat and mittens for about 15 min or so.

\- back STILL hurts. It seemed fine most of yesterday. I don't know if the semi-energetic boinking last night caused it to regress or what, but it's back to me not really being able to lean, twist or reach to the left much without causing pain. Also, I think I figured out what caused it. Friday afternoon at work, the water cooler was empty and I hoisted a full 5-gallon water jug onto the machine to refill it. Back didn't hurt right then and there, but it definitely started hurting soon after, and left was the direction I had to turn to get the bottle onto the machine. I've done that same lift and movement before and not had an issue, but maybe I was just postioned slightly differently or something this last time. Joy.

\- Adding insult to literal injury, I woke up with a tiny zit right on the end of my nose.  I'm trying to leave it alone. Trying. Not entirely succeeding.

\- fed my husband venison for breakfast. I didn't tell him what it was until it was cooked and on the plate, so he couldn't really argue. He didn't NOT eat it, so I guess it wasn't too offensive to him... He's a city boy, and stuff like wild game makes him recoil a little bit, but I grew up eating venison and really like it once in a while.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-19T18:24:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65372>

Venison ... yum.

sorry your back is still bothering you. There's also the alternative of seeing a massage therapist or maybe even an acupuncturist. I know it can be expensive but if that's what you need to fix it then my suggestion (unasked for I know) would be to fix it.

OTHO that snarky jealous part of me sez that's what you get for all that enthusiastic boinking. pfbbbbt.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-19T19:14:21+0200

We have a stash of vicodin from various things. Happy to mail you some! I try to remain open minded about chiro/massage as a therapy. The rate of resolution of back pain due to a specific injury without treatment and with treatment are the same.

Seems like those that are of the mind to go to see a chiro/massage person, also seem to be the type to benefit from the placebo effect and spiritual related well-being.

So I try those, yet come back to my original conclusion - that for me, since I'm missing the spiritual/placebo/benefit from the bonding kind of person -it's not gonna help.

### ealachan — 2012-11-19T19:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65385>

I have a stash of vicodin too, but I'm always so afraid of it making me barf that I hardly ever end up taking it. I actually have a pretty high pain tolerance for the most part, and I try not to take medication unless I really need it. That having been said, hubs' Rx 800mg ibuprofens were my best friend all weekend. Wish I'd have remembered to take one before I left for work this morning!

The thing about chiros that really scares me is this: All it takes is one snap to throw something even MORE out of whack. My dad has sworn by his chiro for years, but I've always seen him come back from there limping just as much as when he went in, and taking like three days to get back to feeling good anyway. That, to me, seems not really worth it. Also, I know several people whose chiros have them on a "schedule". They go in every 3 months (or every month, in one case) to get "adjusted", whether anything is out of whack or not. That kind of thing always makes me a little suspicious. I know not all chiros operate like that of course, but it just sends up kind of a red flag for me in general.

### Lyndra — 2012-11-19T19:50:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65388>

I like rolfing (or maybe I should say, I like the effects). The process is less than fun but it helps me a lot (haha - I first typed that as "hurts a lot." Freudian slip). Some rolers may be trained to do high velocity moves (e.g. the "cracking" that chiros do) but I wouldn't let someone do it unless they do it a lot. Like many times a day. Neck adjustments cause strokes at a rate of 1 in a million, which doesn't sound bad until you're that one (as was a friend's sister). Hope your back gets better - I know how much that ruins my mood.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-19T20:32:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65394>

I can verify, and so can hubby, that there is no placebo effect going on. He came home from work one time and he looked exactly like the crooked man. It took about 3 visits to the chiro to get him straightened out.

My first visit was because I had developed a horrible pain in my lower back right near my hip. THe doc couldn't figure out what was wrong and the nurse told me (after he'd left) to go see the chiro. He got rid of the pain and he was (and still is) the only one who can fix my neck. I will suffer for weeks, sometimes months, it never corrects itself. I don't know what it is that he does but it works.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-19T21:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65401>

> **DarleenMB;67412 wrote:** I can verify, and so can hubby, that there is no placebo effect going on. He came home from work one time and he looked exactly like the crooked man. It took about 3 visits to the chiro to get him straightened out.
>
>
>
> My first visit was because I had developed a horrible pain in my lower back right near my hip. THe doc couldn't figure out what was wrong and the nurse told me (after he'd left) to go see the chiro. He got rid of the pain and he was (and still is) the only one who can fix my neck. I will suffer for weeks, sometimes months, it never corrects itself. I don't know what it is that he does but it works.

When I say "placebo effect", I don't mean it's imaginary and subsequently full of bollocks. I am not putting it down! The great part about it (they should rename it now that we know more about it) is that in the correct setting, with the correct expectations - "placebo" inclined patients can actually generate physical changes that improve their condition. The demeanor of the practitioner is key - I think it's related to that weird "catching the mood of others positive thing". (on that note, I sorry if you catch my mood today!). It isn't that people simply PERCEIVE changes, they actually have concrete and observable changes in a significant way compared to a double blind control.

I need a new word beside placebo - I think people have a pre-idea that it means "imaginary", which is not what I mean.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-19T21:34:55+0200

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-19T21:45:13+0200

I didn't think of it like that - but what you say sounds spot on. I disagree in one big way with Jack on this though, I don't think it is something that applies across the board to all people. I think this ability is part of the hand we are dealt when our genetic deck is shuffled, and constantly telling the crabby, unenlightened, hopeless and scroogesqe folks to "just meditate", "just give it up to Jesus" is the same as the lifelong athlete and gymrat telling the super obese "you just need to push away from the table and jazzercise".

### ealachan — 2012-11-19T22:34:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ealachans-leptin-reset-saga.155/post-65431>

It just means you have to keep digging to find what DOES work for you. There are no silver bullets or golden arrows or platinum...spears? Whatever. You know what I mean. One size shouldn't fit all.

I normally only take tepid showers anyway, so I can get into my normal-temp shower and then slowly work the temp down as I go along, but trying to make myself step into an already-cold shower spray? No can do. Point being - if you can stand cold showers, I bet you could do a cold bath pretty easily! It only really sucks for the first few seconds when you first get in, but then it's fine...for me, at least. I kind of lower myself down into the water in stages, because I'm afraid if I get in all at once I'll give myself a heart attack...LOL! I sit on the edge of the tub with my lower legs in, then slowly lower my butt down into the water with knees still bent, then stretch out one leg so it's totally submerged, then do the other one. I swear a lot during the whole process, too. That definitely helps.  Usually I bring my iPod in with me and play Bejeweled while I'm in there, but last time I actually brought a book and the time went faster, once I was able to concentrate on the words.

Can I please ask what goggles you are using?

Cal

# Thread: Healing my child's gut and more
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### donkjellberg — 2012-03-15T18:07:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-854>

My son is sensitive to gluten and pretty much any food with flour in it. He had bad intermittent stomach aches and sinus problems at an early age (3-5 years). I cut out gluten, wheat, and other similar foods. Now I give him paleo as much as we can. I also give him raw grass fed dairy such as my homemade kefir to improve his gut flora. I was pleased to find that raw, grass fed milk has an omega 6:3 ratio of 1:1 to 2:1 and many healthy bacteria when fermented. That is a ratio similar to salmon. I just hope he is not sensitive to milk proteins. I want to improve his health with whole foods and not supplements as I believe this is the most natural approach.

He is doing much better, gets sick less often, and doesn't have any more stomach aches. Infrequently, he suffers from sinus allergy symptoms. When he does, it is at night when he goes to bed.

Any tips or advice would be appreciated. We even started homeschooling him to get more control of his food, and of course, his learning and environment.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T18:30:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-874>

GAPS...lots of bone broths....most likely ds has a leaky gut - look at the leaky gut rx and leverage that.... If he's having allergy symptoms...you might try going w/o dairy for a a week or to to see if it improves... the trees are in full bloom her in NoVA - so both my kids have xtra runny noses right now...

there's a GAPS thread on MDA [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread27963.html](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread27963.html)

there are other GAPS threads as well.. there too

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-15T19:00:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-890>

I am having a hard time finding good bones where I live, but I like a challenge. I want to find bones from naturally raised animals. I have tried grasslandbeef.com but they never have any in stock when I make my regular purchases. Hopefully I can find a resource near Los Angelos. Driving an hour is not a problem. I have gotten so far away from feed lot animals that I don't want to have anything to do with them. I will check out the thread you posted. Thanks

Edit: My mom is in Fresno and is picking up lamb and beef bones for me. Both pasture raised, woo hoo! Now to make tasty broth/soups.

### Mom — 2012-03-16T03:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-1224>

My kids also have a lot of food allergies/reactions and I am also working on healing leaky guts for two of them. The way I understand it, gluten (grain protein) and casein (milk protein) have a very similar structure. I find that easy to believe since gluten makes dough soft and doughy, and casein is what makes cheese melt nicely (gooey). Because of that, the body tends to react to them similarly. So, if you have a problem with one, you are more likely to hav a problem with both. My kids can't have either, plus many other foods. We also are homeschooling, and one reason is because it is so hard to control their exposure to foods and allergens in craft products when they are at school.

It's great that you are seeing improvements. Good for you. We also are blown away by the difference it's made. We are 2 1/2 years into the special diet. It continues to be a lot of hard work, but the payoff is huge us keeps us going.

### tweety — 2012-03-16T23:28:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-1711>

I have cooked everything in bone broth that I possibly could, and the kids drink it as well as eating fermented foods. But is that enough? They eat potatoes and other starches, though no grains.

What about healing candida for kids and parasites? I have my other thread but I know I have read that parents usually pass the yeast and fungi on to their kids. Is the slow and steady enough to do the trick or do I need to get serious for them too?

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-18T21:43:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-2447>

My son appears to have fungal infection. It is in both his small toenails. I had it 12 years ago in one. I applied topical antifungals to no avail. When I took an oral antifungal it wiped it out permanently. I want to get this treatment for my son as it is pretty clear he has a systemic fungal problem. I do not like to use antibiotics due to their impact on the gut and immunity. Any ideas?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-19T15:55:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-2724>

> **donkjellberg;2454 wrote:** My son appears to have fungal infection. It is in both his small toenails. I had it 12 years ago in one. I applied topical antifungals to no avail. When I took an oral antifungal it wiped it out permanently. I want to get this treatment for my son as it is pretty clear he has a systemic fungal problem. I do not like to use antibiotics due to their impact on the gut and immunity. Any ideas?

Coconut oil! you can look at a candida cleanse for your son... also young children can take diflucan for fungal/yeast infections as well.

If you focus on gaps, and eliminate diary, and focus on establishing good pro-biotics it can help establish healing... as for the toes itself - try a mix of CO, w/tea tree oil and Grapefruit seed extract daily on the toes.... it will wipe out any fungi!

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-20T09:40:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-3168>

Thanks Shijin13. I will give the mix of CO a try. We are cutting out dairy even though all he has is raw, organic, grass fed kefir and infrequent cheese. Kefir is one of the best probiotics IMHO. That is what is frustrating. The good/bad dichotomy.

### cantweight — 2012-03-27T17:36:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-my-childs-gut-and-more.156/post-7029>

I am treating 3 children with systemic candida and parasites.

Paratosin and Parastat from Premier Research Labs are the herbals we use. My kids are also on prescriptions.

FNG or Candida Plus are homeopathic drops for yeast. Dr Ohira probiotics, Sacromyces Bouliardi (sp?) excellent for gut flora along with kefir. If you want to avoid dairy (excellent suggestion) try coconut milk kefir.

Must remove all sugar...fructose and lactose included. Hard to deny children fruit....berries, green apples, and grapefruit are lowest in sugar to my knowledge.

My one son (6yrs old) developed 2 motor tics and one verbal...tourettes like syndrome some would call it. I removed all grains from his diet....(he was already generally gluten free) within a few weeks all tics were gone and the recurring stutter he's had his whole life was gone along with his bloated belly. It was amazing! Gluten free is just not enough for some of these kiddies with gut issues....grain free was definitely a turning point. Plus all grains feed candida and parasites. I do have issues with die off though....mostly unpleasant behavior is the fall out, but it is easy to see the discomfort prompting it.

Good luck....its not easy being that "wacky family" that does all the weird stuff

**

Have you contacted you local Weston A Price Foundation** leader? They will be able to help with local sources of nourishing traditional foods. I know that there has been legal problems with Rawsome and Victoria Bloch so maybe other contact would be better:

[http://www.westonaprice.org/local-chapters/find-local-chapter#ca](http://http://www.westonaprice.org/local-chapters/find-local-chapter#ca)

Los Angeles-West & Santa Monica: Victoria Bloch (310) 447-4527, [victoria@wisetraditions-la.com](mailto:victoria@wisetraditions-la.com) & Shirley Scheker-Young [Shirley@wisetraditions-la.com](mailto:Shirley@wisetraditions-la.com), [http://www.meetup/Los-Angeles-Whole-Food-Nutrition-Meetup-Group/](http://http://www.meetup/Los-Angeles-Whole-Food-Nutrition-Meetup-Group/)

The best way to get bones is to buy the whole animal and get a butcher to process the meat the way you like it. Get everything from the butcher even the cuts of meat you don't know how to cook. Here are some suggestions:

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/weston-a-price-foundation-shopping-guide-for-canada/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/weston-a-price-foundation-shopping-guide-for-canada/)

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/where-to-start-limited-time-and-budget/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/where-to-start-limited-time-and-budget/)
