# Thread: a new Sandy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 37
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sandy — 2012-03-15T18:08:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-856>

I am not good at journalling. I tend to be gung ho on a plan and post and then fall off plan and quit journalling.

It will be different this time!

I am not a 2nd attempt at LR/CT. This is my 2nd day

yesterday I had my BAB, went for a walk, then did a 30 min cold bath.

I'd like to lose about 80#

I have been trying to low carb for years. Lose a bit, then fall off the wagon into a bowl of sugar. I have a horrible sweet tooth. My best success was in 2009 when I went zero carb. Lost about 25#, but again fell off the wagon. The only positive I can say from all my failures is that I learn a little something new each time.

In addition to losing weight, I am hoping the reset will help with the issues I have surrounding lack of motivation, laziness, low energy. Carb cravings

I was laid off in 2003, and never went back to work. At 1st it was because of the kids, but now they are in high school and don't need me for much. Even with that, my life is high stress, mainly due to the antics and attitudes of my 2 high school boys. Relationship with husband sucks. We kind of just put up with each other- barely. I don't have much of a social life either. I've dealt with depression for years. In 2010, I felt like I was losing my mind, so I went to the doc and he put me on lexapro. It made me less depressed but also made me sleep 12 hrs a day. I got off of that and switched to magnesium, and that has been a miracle for me. I still have some depression, but deal with it much better than I was.

I have not had any blood work done. I hate to spend all that money. I may have it done at some point, but right now I would like to try the baths to see if I get any improvement.

I take D, reversterol, B, glutamine, krill, K vitamins

I am a diet soda junkie, and at some point I know I need to drop it, but right now the diet and baths are enough for me - I don't want to overwhelm myself.

I eat VLC- mainly meat and a bit of veggies.

hope you can all support me to the finish line!

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T22:54:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-1063>

go Sandy!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

spent too much time on the computer

went for a 30 min walk. it was a little chilly with short sleeves. Lots of pain in my achilles heel for the 1st 5 min, almost no pain after 15 min

wasted more time on the computer

took a cold bath. 30 minutes. got my feet in a bit - hoping this will help my heel

lunch was a protein shake(whey/eggs/co cream)

dinner was......having brain fart- oh yeah. liversausage on coconut flour pancakes

I did get some cleaning done, but not enough!

couldn't figure out what to eat because of the nausea. Had a bit of coconut manna

may go lay down for awhile and see if I feel better

food has been ok. Been snacking a bit but at least its low carb. No walks or baths yesterday or today. Hope I feel better tomorrow!

Have my corned beef in the crock pot- YUM

### sandy — 2012-03-20T04:40:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-3108>

spent most of the weekend feeling not so great

blew the diet yesterday

having a great day today tho

ate leftover corned beef for bab- was not hungry for like 7 hrs

got hub to take a walk. my heel hurt much less walking later in the day- I must have stretched it out walking during the day

then i did a 30 min cold tub

got a bit hungry- had some coconut manna

still peckish but trying to ignore it

hopefully will get a good nights sleep tonight

### sandy — 2012-03-21T15:01:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-3849>

yesterday- had the last of my corned beef for breakfast. I'm gonna miss it

got some organizing done while I watched Made of Honor(just ok), and then Tuesdays are reruns of Criminal Minds on Ion. My fave show and I never watched the original, so most of them are new to me

3 italians with gr pepper/onion and tom sauce around 3

went for a walk

got hungry too late, but wanted to take a cold bath, so I had an apple with peanut butter

took my bath

had trouble falling asleep- probably not til 1 am

then I woke at 6. I do not like it when I don't get enough sleep

if I did not have to pee, I could probably sleep longer- hopefully that will go away

had 2 italians for breakfast. probably should have had more, but being tired makes me lazy

### sandy — 2012-03-22T18:57:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-4506>

yesterday- 2 italians for BF. really should have had more because I was snacky all day

chick breast tai style for lunch. again not enough

2 sausages for supper. also snacked on a fage with rasberries, 3 apples. I ate supper way too late

still having trouble sleeping. really annoying. waking up stuffed up

did not walk or take bath yesterday, but i did some spot ct

not a great day for me emotionally today. its my beautiful baby boy's 15th bday today. he has gone from my buddy and a sweetheart of a kid to someone who can't stand me. It hurts so much but I just try to stuff it down. The worst part is he blames me for it all. I am not mother of the year but never thought i was that bad. I am tempted to go sit and look thru his baby pictures but I know it will only turn me into a bawling mess

### sandy — 2012-04-02T19:53:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-10395>

yesterday, ate corned beef for breakfast around 10. This lasted me until about 330, when I had another small piece of CB. Then, I finished it off at around 8. Total was about 1.9# of corned beef, but alot of that was fat which I did not eat

wt today 225 no shoes, so about the same

spent yesterday watching hockey so no walk or bath for me

got up around 8 today and had corned beef. took 3 HCl and had lots of loose stools

trying to clean

### sandy — 2012-04-03T16:21:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-10757>

yesterday was a good day, ate about 2# corned beef during the day. breakfast, lunch, and then dinner(but later than I should have- got hungry) The Betaine seemed to help when I went up to 4 pills. will keep working on this

got some cleaning done which always makes me happy because I hate cleaning

talked to my sis and daughter about going to Florida for a visit next month. was on the phone too long so no bath for me

scale is around 223# no shoes

slept like crap last night- only about 5 hours

no meat so I had a protein shake for breakfast- woke up hungry

### sandy — 2012-04-04T17:45:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-11295>

not a good day yesterday. did not have any meat ready for breakfast so I had a shake. that did not fill me up. had an early lunch of a small steak, still not satisfied. Had pork roast. still hungry. made son a pizza and ate 1/4 of it. from then it was all downhill. icecream and chips. had a headache almost all day(even before the junk food)

woke up really early today. HAD to finish the ice cream. idiot. still no walking or baths

today is going to be a bad day. found the sons pot again and took it. last time i did that he went mental and broke a bunch of stuff. ran away in the middle of the night when it was 20 degrees out. I had to call the cops and it was just shitty : (

too bad for me his back hurts and he stayed home from school today. I am sick to my stomach thinking about what is in store for me when he wakes up and sees it is gone. I toyed with the idea of giving him a sleeping pill when i gave him some advil, bad mama

i have always loved being a mom. I always wanted to be an amazing mom but have fell short in so many ways. these teenage years are killing me tho, and if I knew then what I know now.....I don't know if I could do it again. Days like this I wonder if I will mentally survive the next few years until they are gone and I don't have to worry anymore. add to that the fact that my husband is a major ahole and I am a hermit and my life pretty much sucks. Makes it hard to be motivated to be optimal when I hate myself and my life.

I have meat ready for later or tomorrow. whenever I get my ass back in line.

right now, I am just scared. high cortisol levels anyone?

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-04T18:38:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-11331>

Sandy, sorry for the problems. But persevere! You ARE a good mom. Just keep telling yourself that.

As to falling off the wagon ... it was the wheat. If you haven't read "Wheat Belly" it should be on your list (sorry for "shoulding" on you). Wheat is LITERALLY as addictive as heroin. If you stay away from it you'll be fine.

We're all here rootin' for you! New day, new direction. You can do it!

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-04T18:41:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-11332>

Sandy, I just saw you are eating a LOT of corned beef. Most of that stuff is loaded with MSG which could explain a lot. Try to stick to unprocessed meats as much as possible.

My breakfast is usually about 5-6 oz of good ground chuck and a couple of eggs and 2 pieces of uncured, nitrate free, bacon. That will carry me through most of the day.

### sandy — 2012-04-05T20:21:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-11811>

yesterday was pretty bad. I spent the day in fear of my son discovering I had taken his stash and going crazy. He slept til 315, and then decided he could not go to work because you are not supposed to work if you don't go to school. I highly doubt the school calls their employer! then he would not go to lacrosse practice for the same reason. Then yelled at me because he missed hockey(I was not sure he wanted to go so I did not tell him in time). Told my husband he had to come home right after work because I did not want to be alone with son, and he got disgusted with me. To top it off I felt like crap all day and just could not deal with it

In the end, all of my fears were for nothing. My son either has not realized it is gone(although he has his bag with him- I wonder if he is dumb enough to take it to school) or has decided to not bring it up. I hope its the latter. The last time this happened he told me the stuff belonged to someone else, and that it was worth like $240 plus the kids pipe and grinder were another $80. So, I gave him back the pipe and grinder to give to the kid and the money to cover the pot because I thought it was someone elses- well, that was in his bag yesterday so he was obviously lying about the whole thing. He has also told me that he quit which must be another lie.

at least he is at school today so I can breathe. and I think he is going out tonight so I won't have to worry except for the time in between. Next week is Easter vacation so that should be fun. NOT. I miss when they were younger and we would go to Tenn for break. Go to the smokies, and dollywood, the aquarium or zoo. they went white water rafting last time we went. That was in 09. Now they won't go anymore because they want to hang with their friends : (

back on track today. had corned beef for breakfast. I know you don't think its good Darleen, but I can eat it in the morning and not be hungry or want to eat all day. It was that shake that screwed me up. I will have to check the package for msg.

thanks for the visits Darleen and lina : )

### sandy — 2012-04-08T18:21:18+0300

managed to get a cool, not cold bath in yesterday

stuck with the corned beef. also picked up some marscapone cheese. add a little stevia and that stuff is wonderful

still a bit of loose stools but much better than before. going to up the hcl to 5 pills today and see if that helps

going to lunch with mom and sis, then to nieces to see my favorite girls. they always make me smile

sadly, my own boys won't be with me. one has to work and the other is mad at me again

### sandy — 2012-04-10T18:56:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-13387>

easter- ate some corned beef and masc cheese for breakfast

lunch- was not really hungry but mom was taking me out for my birthday/easter. ordered crab legs, broccoli and salad. ate the salad and brocc. tried to eat crab legs but they were so hard to get out of the shell that I quit trying. was pretty full but that did not last long. went to my nieces for easter and had some sausage. tried to play capture the flag with the kids but its pretty hard for a 220# woman to outrun little kids! it was fun tho. I love them. they make me feel great compared to my own family

I think i may have had some corned beef later on that night- don't really remember

monday- corned beef for breakfast. took the little kids out for ice cream and had a cheeseburger no bun

had some corned beef for dinner. i am still getting hungry later at night because i dont eat enought during the day. went for an hour walk in short sleeves- it was pretty chilly- in the 40's and dark out

today- scale was at about 218

had corned beef for breakfast

### skline@peak.org — 2012-04-15T22:53:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-15385>

Good luck to you, Sandy, and all your good work.

You said back at the beginning that you are a diet soda junkie -- have you got rid of that?

The MSG (if any) in the corned beef and the aspartame (if it's that instead of splenda) in the diet soda are similar and both do very bad things to people -- carb cravings, sugar instability, emotional problems.

Perhaps a different order of operations -- ditch the "excitogens" first, and then move into the cold thermogenesis?

### sandy — 2012-04-16T18:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-15612>

have not gotten rid of the diet soda. I know I should. I am just not mentally ready yet. I am probably going to get a soda stream and sweeten with stevia

felt like I hardly ate yesterday. just some corned beef and a few pieces of bacon. went for a 56 min walk. it was so hot I came home and iced myself while I watched criminal minds(my other addiction). did not have time for a bath

### skline@peak.org — 2012-04-16T23:50:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-15723>

That stuff that you "love" will do in everything else you try till you get on top of the problem.

Suggestion: What kind of "diet soda" are you in love with? If it's aspartame (nutrasweet), then change to splenda. Then go to soda water with some kind of real sugar such as honey ("bee puke", as doc calls it). Then ease off the sugar.

The whole "diet soda" idea is the problem. Any sweet taste will get your brain expecting sugar. If you then don't give it any sugar, it goes sort of crazy wanting this and that, looking for the energy supply the taste promised it.

Check your corned beef and be sure it has no MSG in it. It screws with your neurotransmitters.

They say giving up aspartame is as bad as going off some hard drugs, but it can be done.

People who say they could never POSSIBLY give up their bread (or other baked goods) are in a similar pickle -- the wheat has "opioids" in it which are addictive.

Be courageous. Move on, don't keep that monkey on your back. You'll be rewarded.

### sandy — 2012-04-17T18:16:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-15901>

PDL- love your tough love and I know you are right. I've just been doing this for so many years(been low carbing since 1998) that I know what my triggers are. Right now I can basically eat .. fatty meat. Veggies sometimes trigger, creamy dairy can trigger, fruit is definitely out. I don't like seafood, and Dr K says fowl is fowl. I have been living on corned beef, and honestly, I don't even really like meat. I'm just not ready to give up my pacifier. But, I have progressed to the point where I know the soda needs to go eventually. In the past I thought there was absolutely nothing wrong with it. I love the fizziness. My throat tends to get gooked up and water just does not cut it. I've tried sparkling water and its so bitter. I honestly can get a little panicky when I know I am running out of soda in the house.

I've been able to quit smoking in the past(I currently use an ecig because of stress with my kids), I quit drinking(and I used to be a pretty heavy drinker), I could care less about wheat, bread pasta. But the sugar and diet soda are hard to crack for me!

My corned beef does not have MSG(yay)

yesterday was good. only ate corned beef. still getting hungry at night because I can't get enough meat down during the day

went for a walk yesterday. 1:05. It was so cold my hands turned purple and then got red spots on them

no cold bath

scale was 215 today. happy about that

### sandy — 2012-04-18T21:03:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-sandy.157/post-16306>

started out yesterday with CB. Then on to tackle the dreaded taxes. I am such a procrastinator.

Did the stress get me? I don't know. suddenly there was a demand for carbs. ate the rest of a bag of chips and 2 choc easter eggs. followed up later with crackers and peanut butter. sigh

no walk or bath. between the taxes and yesterday being criminal minds marathon on ION, I did not get them in

back to it today. CB for brekkie. going for a walk soon. maybe follow up with a bath

I am so unmotivated. I wake up every day around 7, but lay there and daydream for an hour or two. Guess I need to daydream myself out of my \*\*\*\*ty life

got into it last night with my husband. He want to let our 17 year old go to Mexico with his friend. This kid has been smoking pot in our house, brought pot on a hockey trip I took him on with the intent to sell(well that was his story anyhow), had it in the car where it stunk up the car and I was afraid I would get pulled over. When he discovered that I took it, he went mental, pushed me, broke a 46 in flat screen, poured bleach on my carpet in 2 rooms, and broke my steam cleaner. Then he took $200 from my purse. plus made me pay for the rest of the pot under the story that it belonged to someone else and he had to pay them back. He has also put holes in 2 doors, broken a coat tree, my laptop, a regular tv, and more. This kid calls me a bitch, cunt, fat whore. I can barely have a conversation with him without him going mental. oh yeah, he broke both car windows of my car. and has thrown a 6 pack of water bottles into the car door causing a dent. The night that he broke everything, he ran away in the middle of the night. It was about 20 degrees and he was sending me messages about how I would be happy when he was dead. I had to call the cops and we were out all night looking for him. My husband has never seen a need to discipline him in any way for any of this(I can't because I am afraid of the kid), and now he is mad because he thinks we should let him go to Mexico. I think he is nuts and am furious with him. we don't see eye to eye on how to raise the kids at all. I guess this is why I daydream so much. when I do, I have a perfect life, LOL

my precious corned beef is gone. Hope I can find more now that st pattys is over with. had chicken sausage for breakfast- a little over 40 grams of protein.

# Thread: Not looking to lose weight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### canuck — 2012-03-15T18:09:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-857>

Hey Everyone,

I am not looking to lose weight, but to balance out my hormones and get some energy back. 14 months of amenorrhea, overtraining has actually caused a weight loss (but increased bf % due to muscle loss). Low progesterone, am on prometrium, also have a prescription for estrogen but dont want to take it. My question is, should I be stuffing my face with calories or follow my appetite and possibly be around the 1000-1300 cal mark. I am 5'3 and 115lbs, i am up from an all time low of 108 which terrified me so i ate a lot of fruit and paleo carbs, probably 3-4000 calories some days. Any similiar stories or advice out there?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-16T02:52:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-1203>

Canuk, I'm right there with you. I'm 24, 5'3" and 113 lbs. I finished a round of hcg in Sept. at 120, dropped to 117 on the leptin reset (keto-paleo style) and since adding CT in February, I've dropped four more. The thing is I'm dropping it in places I don't need to and the love handles remain! Clearly, this is an indication of hormonal issues. I have adrenal fatigue and hashimotos that I'm struggling with.

I stick to 1200-1400 calories most days, but then I can pound it back other days, easily reaching that 3,000 cal mark. I would also like advice on this topic. I really need my body to detox extrogen (got off birth control in Feb. too) and get a handle on my low thyroid issues.

Let's continue to exchange notes. I appreciate your post on this. I think just listening to your body and not denying it food when it needs it is key...but it's hard with decreased appetite as a result of CT.

### canuck — 2012-03-16T16:49:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-1473>

May I ask why you did the HCG diet? Seems like you didnt need to lose weight, was it an attempt at balancing hormones?

I have low DHEA but still within range that my doctors dont see it as a problem (go figure). I am in Canada and you cannot buy DHEA without a prescription, but I have a small supply that I snuck across the boarder but my naturopath said we should get my progesterone numbers up first. Took me 6 months to get an appt with an endocrinologist and her advice was for me to gain 10lbs, but she also gave me a 6 month supply of prometrium and estrogen (I am not taking the estrogen). I know its not a weight thing since I lost my period when I was my heaviest about 127, but was probably my most muscular at this weight as well. I also have a lot of digestive issues and since everything is controlled via hormones, its probably the source of most of problems (again docs are not convinced of that connection). I too am seeing weight loss in the wrong places and have seen more weight around the love handles.

Trying everything, getting colonics to help with constipation, which has helped quite a bit. Osteopath for getting colon functioning which is a new thing but my osteopath is a paleo supporter and has noticed a few lesions around my descending colon which we are working out and acupuncture. I am also going to start seeing a psychologist. I dont feel overly stressed or depressed but might as well see if it works. Glad I have good benefits.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-16T17:54:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-1532>

Canuk, I've struggled with my weight my whole life. My highest weight was 177.7. I got down to 138 for my wedding in 2010, but within a month or two I had gained 10 lbs. back. I was fat and frustrated, and I ran into hcg in the health store. I gave it a whirl, and the rest is history. I believe if I hadn't found hcg, I wouldn't have found paleo and then Dr. K. So I'm very grateful.

I'm down another pound this morning to 112.4, and it's starting to freak me out. I have NEVER before had to worry about losing too much weight. I have ALWAYS been the fat girl. I plan to continue keto-paleo, but I'm going to increase cals and see how it goes.

Sounds like we are struggling with the same battles. For constipation, I take Natural Calm with Calcium in the evenings. I also have added DE to the mix, which has really helped. Betaine hcl helps me digest all the proteins I'm taking in, and I eat plenty of CO, too, which helps.

Have you tried Dr. K's leaky gut protocol? In addition to thyroid and adrenal issues, I have leaky gut and candida (I'm guessing), so I follow a pretty strict diet. I haven't been tested for DHEA, but I plan to get a full panel done when I see a doc on March 28. Currently, I'm on a low dose of cytomel, t3 therapy, which has seemed to help. Granted, I started it at the same time as CT, so who knows what is actually working?

I hope the psychologist helps. Sometimes the emotional burdens we carry are as toxic as the yucky stuff we carry inside.

I really want to balance out my figure, regain my period and get my hormones in check. I'm praying CT is the answer.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-17T01:25:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-1765>

Canuk, I had one of my 3,000 calorie days. It's amazing how I go from being not hungry at all to ravenous! The weight loss is starting to freak me out though, so even though I'm pretty full, I'm taking the urge to eat as a good thing right now.

Dr. K says if you're hungry while doing CT, your hormones are out of whack though...so who knows what's good and bad?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-17T01:26:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-1766>

Wait, I forgot to ask, are you following the leptin rx and ct?

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-18T00:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-2152>

I have this problem as well. I don't want to lose any more weight and have limited my CT as a result, but really want to delve into it full force. My hormonal issues emerge rapidly when I have too low body fat. I wonder if Jack has found any differences in the way women react to CT as opposed to men?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-19T01:44:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-2513>

I think we just have to eat more to compensate. Frankly, I don't want to give up the other benefits of CT; it's too important. But, at the same time, I don't want to be a skeleton either.

### hellojtm — 2012-04-12T03:26:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-14035>

Would be great if we could get someone with access to a DEXA to just follow their appetite while doing CT and see what's going on. Preferrably they would already be lean so that we can figure out if they are just becoming even leaner or if they are losing muscle by eating to satiety. I'm in the same boat as you all except I'm male. I lost 4lbs doing CT and for the most part I believe it was body fat even thought I was already lean ~12%, I'm ok as long as I don't go below like 7% body fat and for women I'm assuming you're looking at maybe 13-14%... I hadn't read that hunger during CT implies hormone problems, but it \*kinda\* makes sense. For me I have been using it (the fact that I'm hungry) as an indication that I'm not cold adapted yet. But now that is getting kind of silly as I've started to exhibit all the outwards signs of being cold adapted like ... cold tolerance ... darker skin.

jesse

### hellojtm — 2012-04-19T01:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-16383>

> **Anna K.;16086 wrote:** wow, Is your skin actually getting darker because of CT? not sunbathing?
>
>
>
> I've been doing CT for about 4 weeks and I's say that my hunger levels fluctuate. some days more, some days much less, but never out of control.

Yeah I have been avoiding the sun as much as possible while trying to adapt, so it's either become hypersensitive to the 2-3 minutes I was forced to get or else CT darkened my skin. This is all just me going off my memory, so feel free to toss it completely. And now that it's past April 15th I've officially transitioned out of my self-imposed winter and am getting sunlight freely (and there's plenty of it too!)

jesse

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-19T05:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-looking-to-lose-weight.158/post-16485>

alpha MSH makes our skin darker.....and it is an evolutionary adaptation due to the ancient pathway.

# Thread: low carb= loose stools for me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sandy — 2012-03-15T18:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-864>

I am always amazed at people who get constipated on this diet.

for me, its the total opposite.

yesterday was my first day, and already this morning the loose stools have started. within an hour of eating

warning, this will get graphic

in the past, I have had it so bad, its almost like "peeing out of my butt"

it comes on with no warning, so if I am not near a bathroom, I am in real trouble.

sometimes, its so frequent that it happens 5 times an hour and I get sore from going so much

I eat mainly meat. veggies are just there to help the meat go down. I eat some dairy(but not yesterday or today). I eat plenty of fat

So, am I not digesting the meat or fat? am I overdigesting it? If I am not digesting it, shouldn't I be losing weight like crazy?

ox bile, HCL, digestive enzymes?

I would really be interesting in hearing from someone who has had this problem and how you resolved it

### Dineen — 2012-03-15T18:55:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-885>

Going paleo completely relieved a lifetime of constipation for me. From things I've read, people who get constipated on a low carb diet are those who were "addicted" to fiber. You may have a corrollary issue with fiber, needing it to "bulk up" or slow down your digestion in the large bowel.

Your particular 5 times an hour and very liquid stool, may be other issues with side effects of over supplementation (frequent issues with too much glutamine, Vitamin C, or magnesium, for instance). That was the past, so stick with what's going on now.

Fat also is a great help for those with constipation issues, so you may want to adjust your fat consumption down just a bit.

You can learn a lot about what normal stool patterns are from gutsense.org. There is a great image of the Bristol stool scale with explanations of normal there.

Liquid is not good. Soft is good; daily and even after every meal is good.

I find that if I eat too many fibrous veggies (more than a cup of grean beans at a time, for instance) I don't have a daily bowel movement and feel bloated or "backed up". My stools are still soft to pass though. So you may want to eat more veggies and more fibrous ones rather than just lettuce, for instance.

### sandy — 2012-03-15T19:05:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-892>

thanks

I don't take vit C, the loose stools happened before I took mag(and I take a chelated form), only take 1 glutamine, but not consistent with that, although the loose stools are.

I think more veggies would help, but, one, they increase my cravings, and two, I get the feeling Dr K does not think we should be eating many veggies(esp during the winter) and that we should try to eat Ketogenic and as low carb as possible

### Croak — 2012-03-15T19:27:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-909>

Start with some HCL, and realize that you need time to adapt to high fat/high protein intake and the loss of carbs. Could take weeks, and loose movements will be something you'll be dealing with for some time. And there's usually other factors in play (toxin release, overgrowth die-off) that will keep you paying attention to the quality of your toilet tissue.

It eventually settles down.

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T19:47:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-924>

Second the HCl, Now Brand makes a good digestive enzyme in general and it's inexpensive on Amazon.

Do you still have a gallbladder? I've had issues like you describe, but I don't have a gallbladder anymore, so I have to ramp up the fat gradually or have veggies to help mitigate it.

### sandy — 2012-03-15T20:09:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-940>

this does not seem to go away with time for me. I have been ZC for 2+ months and it was still bad after all that time. Now that I remember, it also ends up smelling awful after a time

I will try the HCL. any thoughts on ox bile?

### sandy — 2012-03-18T00:01:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-2148>

> **shilohman;1506 wrote:** Sandy, if you haven't already read the 'Leaky Gut Prescription' you need to. The best way to test for LG is the VAP test.

I have read it(and many other of his blogs). Sadly, much of it goes over my head. I need to get myself into a mindset for leaky gut. It just makes me sad because I already feel so limited on what food I can eat(that I enjoy)

### Phoenix — 2012-03-18T00:28:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-2153>

Even though your problem started before you started taking magnesium, I'd still cut it out. And cut out all supplements for that matter. Also remove dairy and cut back on fat and remove nightshades. Remove all starches too. Be especially careful that no gluten gets into your diet.

Then see where that leaves you after a week or so. If your diarrhea is better, then start adding back one by one to find what the problem was. Wait at least a week between each addition. If it isn't better, then go the route of adding in bile or HCL.

Not telling you what to do ... that's just what I would do.

Good luck! Hope you figure it out!

> I eat mainly meat. veggies are just there to help the meat go down. I eat some dairy(but not yesterday or today). I eat plenty of fat
>
>
>
> So, am I not digesting the meat or fat? am I overdigesting it? If I am not digesting it, shouldn't I be losing weight like crazy?
>
>
>
> ox bile, HCL, digestive enzymes?
>
>
>
> I would really be interesting in hearing from someone who has had this problem and how you resolved it

I have this problem when I forget my digestive enzymes, esp after eating the big ass breakfast.

FWIW, I don't have a gall bladder anymore so DUH I obviously have a hard time digesting fat.

I have not been losing on leptin reset but I think maybe I had too many carbs, even when I was doing a low

amount. Now I've cut out almost everything. It's only been two days and still not losing (yes I'm one of those

awful people that weighs everyday)

thanks for the ideas. I can't give up my Mag(it is chelated so it should not be a problem). The mag has really helped my depression. My mental health depends on it!

I hear you on the other stuff. I often read how dairy and nightshades are bad for you. Although, in my 2.5 month stint on ZC, I did not have either(except a bit of cheese when I ate a burger- which was not often because I really only like burgers on buns)

I suspect it could be fat. My problem with lowering it is that when I eat lower fat meat, I am not satisfied, get hungry quickly, and it has set off binges.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-18T18:59:26+0200

I asked Dr. K about this in the comments at one time because, according to my VAP, I shouldn't have leaky gut, but I definitely deal with constipation and seem to have some food intolerances too. Sometimes I wonder though if my gut issues aren't more related to my whacked out hormonal deficiencies.

Shilohman - how do you use the VAP to indicate leaky gut? Any other ways to determine if leaky gut is truly the culprit?

### agatha — 2012-03-26T23:51:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-6603>

Hi Sandy

Have you tried probiotics - could be a gut flora problem. Or it could be that you are experiencing a detox reaction - the liver dumps fat soluble toxins into the bile which then creates diarrhoea further down the gut. If you are unlucky, your gut will then reabsorb some of the toxins and so the cycle repeats itself. I have had success with low carb diarrhoea using activated charcoal after breakfast. Don't do this for more than a few days though - the charcoal will absorb some of the nutrients.

Hope you get it fixed.

### janagram — 2012-03-27T00:04:25+0300

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-27T21:36:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-7302>

I just wanted to say you are not alone. I'm in my 4th week now, and I am having this issue. It is FRUSTRATING!!!

I lowered the fat intake because I know I have fat malabsorption, and then I was wondering if I was eating too much protein because I wasn't eating any vegs yet (green vegetables make me seriously ill--that's how messed up my digestive tract is). I'm not eating any fruit, in addition to it not being on the leptin RS, but also because I suspect fructose malaborption also.

I take Now Super Enzymes (5 capsules) with every meal.

It is a slow and frustrating process.

Something like the SCD diet or GAPS might be helpful also. I am going completely dairy-free now and adding in small amounts of easily digestible veggies to see if it helps my issue. My next step will be to stop all supplementation and see what happens. I take an adrenal supplement, magnesium, Vitamin D, Vitamin K, Vitamin C, B Vitamins and glutamine.

If you'd like to PM back and forth, please let me know. I could use support on this issue, too. It is tough to stick with something that feels like it is making my digestion worse. But I am trying to keep in mind that in the long run, it will be better. I can feel the health effects already, so I know that it is a good change.

I think on the leaky gut post Dr. K mentioned supplementing with colostrum, and I believe Robb Wolf has cleared that for "short-term use" before as well. I may look into that next.

Hi PaleoSue

would love to PM. It sounds like you are farther in the process of fixing yourself than I am. I can barely stay on this diet. I restarted today with some corned beef. I took 2 betaine HCl with it and had loose stools within the hour. Next time I eat I will try 3.

what are the super enzymes? have they helped at all? have you tried the HCl?

### Glamazon — 2012-04-01T20:28:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-9877>

This always happens to me when I switch from a carb based diet to a protein/fat based diet and vice versa. It lasts about two weeks for me and I just have to tough it out. It takes awhile to adapt. I do so without adding any supplements. I do drinks a lot of water to compensate for the liquid stools. I get very thirsty during that time. Yep, it is like I am peeing out my butt.

Use coconut oil or desitin diaper rash cream (has vit a and zinc oxide in it) on the ouchy parts till it passes.

### Owl — 2012-04-01T20:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-9889>

I have this same problem. It started when I went on the hcg diet a few years ago. I was eating no grains or starches, and 500 calories per day. I would get loose stools/diarrhea while I was on the diet, and it would clear up when I added grains and starches back in.

After my last round, I had trouble keeping the weight off, so I kept with the low carbs and also kept the loose stools. Probiotics didn't do anything for me.

I thought that's how it was going to be, but since going paleo, I find that other people actually have constipation problems!

I just did the GI Effects stool test and some other blood work. No bad bacteria, no candida, no h.pylori or anything like that. Most of my good bacteria levels are good. I do have a non-human parasite. But they say it should be transient and resolve on it's own and usually doesn't cause many symptoms, and to work on other problems before trying to eradicate that. But that's something you might investigate yourself. I don't think the parasite is my problem, since it's been going on for 2 years and seems to correlate with starchy carb intake.

Anyway, I've decided to switch my chelated magnesium malate, which was causing my stools to be looser, to chelated mag glycinate. I bought this brand:[http://www.amazon.com/Albion-Chelated-Magnesium-200mg-VegCap/dp/B002TU58QA](http://http://www.amazon.com/Albion-Chelated-Magnesium-200mg-VegCap/dp/B002TU58QA)

I've been taking it for a week, and have worked up to 1000mg/day. These are loose powder in capsules, unlike most magnesium which is pressed pills. It seems to be much better than the malate on my bowels. I think I'm absorbing it better before it has a chance to get to my bowels.

For the past few days, I have also been taking Now brand Betaine HCL with pepsin. I'm up to 4 capsules/meal. I don't know if this will help my stools, but it's worth a try.

### sandy — 2012-04-02T19:50:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-10390>

I did 2 caps of betaine hcl with my meal yesterday(corned beef). still had alot of problems. I was at the store and barely made it to the bathroom

today I took 3 caps with my corned beef, still had many trips to the bathroom. will try 4 with the next meal

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-02T20:30:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-carb-loose-stools-for-me.159/post-10412>

Hi, Sandy. I take 5 Now Super Food enzymes with HCL with each meal, and still have issues although not as badly as you're describing -- especially with the higher-fat meals. I am hoping this eases over the months but it is frustrating, isn't it?

# Thread: Detox or Food Intolerance??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-or-food-intolerance.161/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-15T18:36:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-or-food-intolerance.161/post-878>

I'm really struggling to figure out if I've developed a new food intolerance or if what I'm experiencing is just typical detox from the Rx and CT. What kinds of detox reactions is everyone having?

I find myself getting very weak - legs and upper body/shoulders/neck - about 2-3 hours after breakfast and lunch. Strangely, it doesn't seem to happen after dinner. It also seems to be worse the 3 days a week I have to go to work. I don't know if that's because I'm sitting so much and just get stiff/sore or what it could be.

The only new food I've really introduced in the last few weeks has been seafood, but I've never had a problem with it in the past. I've been eating sardines, salmon, scallops, crab, etc. I'm careful to get BPA-free canned products and fresh when possible.

I'm also finding myself getting nauseous again several hours after eating - even after the weakness happens. This happened when I first started the Rx too and I thought it was due to the HCL I was taking, so I've since stopped that and it did go away. Now it's back!

The weakness is just terrible. My limbs feel so heavy and it's like I just can't get them to work. Putting one leg in front of another to walk is truly hard. I don't know what to think. I also feel much more tired and find myself just mentally zoning out.

Anyone experience symptoms like this just from detoxing? I've been on GAPS for a year and a half and didn't really expect much detoxing on the Rx or CT.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-15T19:18:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-or-food-intolerance.161/post-905>

I started a thread on this topic in the CT forum. Here's the link. Lots of people posting in that one, so hopefully you will find it useful:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?49-quot-Die-Off-quot-Symptoms](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?49-quot-Die-Off-quot-Symptoms)

### mycrazycartoons@gmail.com — 2012-03-15T20:11:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detox-or-food-intolerance.161/post-943>

> **BJK77;879 wrote:** I'm really struggling to figure out if I've developed a new food intolerance or if what I'm experiencing is just typical detox from the Rx and CT. What kinds of detox reactions is everyone having?
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> The weakness is just terrible. My limbs feel so heavy and it's like I just can't get them to work. Putting one leg in front of another to walk is truly hard. I don't know what to think. I also feel much more tired and find myself just mentally zoning out.
>
>
>
> Anyone experience symptoms like this just from detoxing?

I experienced the same feeling and also ended up with hives..but I think it was from a newly discovered egg sensitivity. I don't eat eggs now and I don't have the problem anymore.

# Thread: Greetings from Virginia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-virginia.162/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-15T18:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-virginia.162/post-881>

same simple three letters, still me here and on MDA, and Robb's website

I'm 47 year old mother of 5 ages 21, 20, 19, 9, & 6.

French, Italian, German and Irish in ancestory, so we can image what epigenitic soup I'm in.

I'm one of those devout Catholics - so where Dr. K talks mindfulness, I insert prayer. It also means that I'm pretty rooted in natural law theory - the earth itself is a revelation of God, and we should be working with it, not against it.

Originally from upstate NY, but haven't been there since I joined the Marine Corps many moons ago and married a now retired Marine. So my health care perspective is military health care and I have quite a bit of experience with it, using MTF's as well as networked providers for primary care. Our 9 year old has a genetic disorder (that's a thread/case study on Robb's forum), so I've been round the specialty provider networks alot too.

I've been a crossfitter since Sept 2008, although I had an ankle injury and surgical repair, so the last year has been working back up to things.

I have the classic symptoms of middle age womanhood. Weight that sticks, sleep that sucks, etcetera, etcetera, etcetera. Basal cell carcinoma has taken some big bites out of my scalp in the last year.

I found Dr. Kruse from a comment on Robb's website, but I had been curious about Leptin for a while from Steve G's blog.

So here I am, happily going LS from LR and saying goodbye to many health issues (current plague going through NoVa excepted)

Short goals - get back to USMC ht/wt standards, have awesome hormone profiles, and see my hair dresser more than my Mohs surgeon this year.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-15T18:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-virginia.162/post-886>

good to see you over here!

# Thread: Hello from North Texas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-texas.166/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### blindpig — 2012-03-15T19:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-texas.166/post-904>

Y'all finally broke me down and you're making me post. I think I made an introductory post over on the monster thread at MDA a while back, but I can never keep up with reading so I never posted again. By the time I read something I want to respond to, it's so far in the past, it's irrelevant!

My name is Robin and I discovered Dr. K last October. I started the Leptin Reset on November 2. It has been lifechanging. I have a lot of "issues" (mainly crazy hormones and Hep C) so I haven't moved into post-reset, but I'm not in a hurry because things are working so well for me right now. I have just recently started doing some spot-icing and face-dunking, and as soon as I get a kiddie pool will be doing some cold soaks outside since I don't have a tub.

I finally pinned my husband down and made him listen to Dr. K's interview on Underground Wellness a few nights ago and now have his full support, so I think it will be full steam ahead now.

I want to thank all of you regulars over on the MDA thread. It has been through reading all of your questions and answers that I have learned how to fine-tune things, and you have all inspired me daily. (And I am sure you all know how I feel about Dr. K because you all feel the same...thank you Dr. K for changing my life!)

I have a feeling that my cortisols are screwed up. I am notiicing that in the a.m. I use to be sluggish, but that is getting better, but in the evening, 9:30 I am ready for bed, my eyes are tired, body too. But....as soon as I lay down, my mind is going, all kinds of random thoughts..nothing serious.

Anyone have any ideas or thoughts on this...(not like I need more thoughts lol), but I will listen to yours

# Thread: Looking for Supplements w/o Stearic Acid, Magnesium Stearate

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-supplements-w-o-stearic-acid-magnesium-stearate.169/post-984>

I am looking for sources of supplements w/o the additives stearic acid, magnesium stearate.

In particular, 7-Keto DHEA.

### MonteD — 2012-03-16T17:21:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-supplements-w-o-stearic-acid-magnesium-stearate.169/post-1497>

You have a few options.

[http://nutrigold.com/7-Keto-DHEA](http://http://nutrigold.com/7-Keto-DHEA) You can get it off of Amazon.

If that doesn't work then search for "7 keto soft gels". NOW brand makes a good one. There are a few lesser known brands that have soft gels as well. Sometimes they use titanium dioxide but you can find one without that in it as well.

### ASHO — 2012-03-16T17:53:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-supplements-w-o-stearic-acid-magnesium-stearate.169/post-1530>

>
> [http://nutrigold.com/7-Keto-DHEA](http://http://nutrigold.com/7-Keto-DHEA) You can get it off of Amazon.
>
>
>
> If that doesn't work then search for "7 keto soft gels". NOW brand makes a good one. There are a few lesser known brands that have soft gels as well. Sometimes they use titanium dioxide but you can find one without that in it as well.

100 mg is a high does for me. I did find this Twinlab MaxiLIFE 7-Keto -- 25 mg - 60 Capsules at Amazon.

[http://www.amazon.com/Twinlab-MaxiLIFE-7-Keto----25/dp/B00139MH94/ref=sr\_1\_cc\_2?s=aps&ie=UTF8&qid=1331913107&sr=1-2-catcorr](http://http://www.amazon.com/Twinlab-MaxiLIFE-7-Keto----25/dp/B00139MH94/ref=sr_1_cc_2?s=aps&ie=UTF8&qid=1331913107&sr=1-2-catcorr)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-supplements-w-o-stearic-acid-magnesium-stearate.169/post-2158>

### hazyjane — 2012-03-19T04:41:10+0200

Apex Energetics makes a regular DHEA in liquid form and they make all their supplements without excipients (they cater to autoimmune people). Premier Labs is another excipient-free company.

### Hotmail — 2012-03-15T21:27:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-1005>

Hello World !!

I do like documenting my experiments ... so glad I found a journal.

Started this on Monday, started at 145.6 lbs, this morning I was 142lbs .

I ate too much beef this morning , about 1lb, made into burgers.

OK will go and do more reading now.

Wish me luck

### MaggieC — 2012-03-16T01:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-1161>

Welcome to the journals!

### Hotmail — 2012-03-16T11:05:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-1348>

Thanks everyone.

I will add the notes from Monday that I have documented later today when I get a chance.

This morning no change in my weight from yesterday- 142lb

Yesterday I had 1lb of beef burgers for breakfast

Had to go out with work for lunch, I ordered carpaccio, which was great, unfortunately the chef drizzled some olive oil on the beef slices..

I wasn't hungry for dinner, but had a handful of walnuts, and decaf with soya milk. Not great I know.

### Hotmail — 2012-03-16T11:50:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-1350>

For got to add, yesterday and today I have been feeling extremely tired, really really tiered.

### Hotmail — 2012-03-17T11:40:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-1909>

Yesterday started great, but didn't end on the same note.

Had Thai chicken curry for breakfast which was home made. I only added the curry paste which was ZC and 2 T of organic desiccated coconuts. I was still full at lunch time, and decided to go swimming for my lunch break, even though it wasn't a sunny warm day. The pool water was quite warm, I was a bit disappointed because I thought it will be a good start to some CT, but hey, I really enjoyed the swim.

I usually eat my second and last meal between 3 to 4pm... but yesterday I decided I will wait until I go home to eat the rest of the Thai curry. I think that was a mistake, as I was too hungry by the time I got home. I had the Thai chicken curry...

Unfortunately I had a call with some bad news relating to my son, not incredibly serious, but it made me feel very anxious, and lost a bit of judgement, so had quite a bit of almonds and walnut.

I thought I shouldn't weigh this morning so I wont get upset of the increase, but then again, I told myself if I am going to be honest with myself and with this journal I should really record it... so this morning I weigh 142.6, this is a 0.6lb increase.

I wont give up, and i wont be too hard on myself, even though I should.... but today, I am determined to put that behind me.

### Phoenix — 2012-03-17T17:43:18+0200

### sandy — 2012-03-18T00:34:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-2155>

Hi - thanks for popping by! Hope you are having a great weekend

### Hotmail — 2012-03-18T12:10:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-2331>

No change in weight, still 142.6, I am getting discouraged, when I didn't have a large breakfast I lost better, then again it could be the decaf and the small amount of soya milk in it... thing is I hate black decaf, and trying to avoid all dairy, so can't have cream. Today I will try with no decaf, not sure about the green tea either.

### Phoenix — 2012-03-18T16:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-2380>

> **Hotmail;2338 wrote:** No change in weight, still 142.6, I am getting discouraged, when I didn't have a large breakfast I lost better, then again it could be the decaf and the small amount of soya milk in it... thing is I hate black decaf, and trying to avoid all dairy, so can't have cream. Today I will try with no decaf, not sure about the green tea either.

You know, I've been doing Paleo/Leptin RX for about 8 weeks and haven't really lost much at all. Probably about 3-4 pounds. But the difference between then and now is that my calorie count has practically doubled. And I'm not gaining weight. That's like a miracle for me. I used to gain weight if I ate any more than 1200 calories. In order to lose I had to cut back to 500 per day.

So I really haven't considered this a weight loss diet, although I would be screaming "Hallelujah" from the rooftops if I did start losing. The fact that I can now eat until I'm full, and not count calories or amounts is so freeing. And not gain? That's insanely wonderful for me.

I know what it's like to feel uncomfortable at a certain weight, and to want to lose it. So I'm not lecturing you or saying that you should just enjoy not gaining. But maybe instead of getting frustrated at not losing, you can at least tell yourself that you're healing your metabolism and hormones and inflammed body, etc. It's hard though, I know.

Also, I'm like you. I love creamer in my decaf. So I just gave it up and I buy the best organic heavy cream from pastured cows. And I use about 2-4 tablespoons per day in my coffee. I also buy the best decaf I can find. With that little tweak, I don't feel deprived. And I've been doing that for a few weeks and haven't seen any weight gain or difference in the way I feel. So for me it's been OK. I know it's not protocol, but it keeps me in line with all of the other changes that I've made to my life over the last few months.

Hope you keep it up, and that you don't get too discouraged. I'm rooting for you!

### Hotmail — 2012-03-18T19:06:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-2413>

Phoenix, thanks for the above, I can't have dairy due to skin eczema, so can't do cream.

I think I really should quit the decaf, I need to make up my mind to do that, we'll see how it goes.

### Phoenix — 2012-03-19T00:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-2484>

> **Hotmail;2420 wrote:** Phoenix, thanks for the above, I can't have dairy due to skin eczema, so can't do cream.
>
>
>
> I think I really should quit the decaf, I need to make up my mind to do that, we'll see how it goes.

Wow, skin eczema. You know that's an autoimmune disease, right? That's why I started on paleo. Not eczema, but autoimmune disease (celiac, hashimoto's, diabetes type 1, amyloidosis all run in my family). You're definitely on the right track! And now I understand why you won't go near dairy.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-19T05:33:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hotmail-journal.172/post-2616>

Hey Hotmail, don't be discouraged, so many reasons the scale is not moving but give it time stay tight and it will come. Come out swinging get a BAB in tomorrow and crush Monday. # Thread: An ER Doc from California
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 69
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RobH — 2012-03-15T21:29:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-1007>

I'm an ER doc living in Northern California. Have plenty of weight to lose myself, and have struggled with it for many many years -- lots of exercise, nearly every dieting trick out there. Have lost it and gained it back mostly. Got a great heart rate monitor app for my iPhone and have done like 89 workouts in the last 4 months, burned several hundred thousand calories, tried to eat "healthy" following the Zone type diet, all while losing about 6 pounds.

Discovered (why the heck did it take me so long? Robb Wolf was just down the road in Chico) a month and a half ago, and Jack Kruse 2 weeks or while watching the Paleo Summit. Haven't felt better in 10 years since I started Paleo and now the Leptin Rx and CT (cold swimming pool this time of year) and the weight is melting off -- 23 pounds so far. Love it and can't wait for my next cold soak. I have not felt this good or this optimistic about ANYTHING in years. I have three little boys and a wonderful wife and want to be the healthiest fittest person (OPTIMAL!) I can for them and everybody in my life.

Also so important to me: Every day at work I take care of so many people suffering from the effects of chronic low-level inflammation and it is frankly mindblowing -- so many young people disabled from chronic pain, obesity, even MIs (heart attacks) in 30 years olds. So many elderly and middle aged people so tragically decrepit from every neolithic disease. So much suffering from what is so preventable and so many people living with it. I've started talking to patients about gluten free diets, recommending paleo books and really trying to hone in on interventions like that that I think will be helpful -- a little odd for an ER doc to be doing. I've read incessantly on Paleo diets and looked at some of the research -- and I am very convinced of the validity of the approach (as I'm sure you all are) but I had to be on sound footing to recommend it to my patients.

Jack, I cannot thank you enough for what you are doing. I can't wait to see your PaleoFX speech and hope to help you change the way we deliver HEALTHcare (not disease care) going forward. I know you're doing it not for the awards, but I'm thinking Nobel Prize in Medicine...

Thanks so much for what you are doing and how you are doing it.

\-RobH

### Birdy — 2012-03-15T21:31:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-1008>

Hi Doc!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### Paradox — 2012-03-15T21:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-1028>

Welcome RobH, ER Doc. Paleo eating has nearly cured my chronic RA of 35+years. The meds for it over the years have taken their toll, a big one, and now I have other damage from the meds. Paleo eating has helped tremendously but the CT is starting to kick in and I have never felt better in decades. This is not a joke, it is real. I wish you all the best and APPLAUD you LOUD AND LONG for paying it forward with your patients. it is all we can do, touch those around us and do the best for them.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-16T04:11:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-1258>

Hey RobH,

I'm glad you're having success. That is killer!

I've started drawing out the biochemistry of leptin on the back of people's lab sheets. I make a leptin sensitive column: build bone, build muscle, increase sex hormones, better sleep, lower stress, exercise tolerance, decreased appetite, easy weight loss, Hugh Jackman body, etc. Then I show was happens when you have a broken leptin receptor: lousy bone, no muscle, low sex hormones, crappy sleep, high stress, exercise intolerance, high appetite, impossible weight loss, Oprah body, etc. Then everyone asks me, how to become leptin sensitive again. Then we really get somewhere.

### grammasmitty@hotmail.com — 2012-03-16T04:29:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-1265>

So glad you found Dr. Jack! This is amazing! Hope others follow in your footsteps. It is exciting to see other doctors learn so they can help their patients become healthy. My son wants to be a PA and I am trying to get him to read here, too.

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-16T04:42:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-1270>

Hi Doc, so glad it's working for you. And I'm glad you are spreading the word!

### caroline — 2014-03-01T03:00:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-112770>

Thanks Rob for sharing this ....... now we need an update ......please!

### sooperb — 2014-03-01T12:23:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-112816>

Welcome Rob, it's so refreshing to hear from a doctor who has opened his mind to a new chapter in health care, so many refuse to acknowledge that what they learned at Med school 30 years ago or more could possibly be outdated or just plain wrong. It will be interesting to see how you fare where you work, other Doctors honing in on your advice and disagreeing or arguing, maybe even some patients being cross because they got a lecture not a pill lol. I really hope this approach works for you but I fear you're going to have to be ready to fight your corner.

I'm pleased you are doing well on your journey too, not just for your family but for yourself, you're important too

### persistence — 2014-03-09T05:53:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-114250>

RobH, great to see that there are high quality members here. Having gone through Paleo for the last 2.5 years, it is a great diet, but I would post one caution. Followed strictly, Paleo is really a Trojan Horse ketogenic diet. It's quite \*possible\* to comply with Paleo rules and end up eating only vegetables and no starchy carbs. That ends up being effectively a no carb diet (I will explain below). I followed such a diet and really crashed badly.

I would strongly urge you to go read Paul Jaminet's Perfect Health Diet, which I personally think is one of the most important nutrition books of the last 20 years. It doesn't say anything new, but does a brilliant job of summarizing the literature. One of the PHD key tenets is that the body does best with a certain minimum amount of dietary glucose from starchy carbs. It isn't much, maybe 50 grams per day minimum. They recommend 20% to 30% of diet from starchy carbs, which I find from careful measurement is NOT very much food. I get my starchy carbs from tubers (yams and taro) because of food absorption problems, and 30 grams of carbs from taro is about half of one fist ful of taro.

A key PHD insight is that vegetables that are not starchy carbs have effectively zero carbohydrate. Because the amount of energy it takes to digest them equals their caloric content, they are effectively zero net carb contributors. Eat vegetables for micronutrition, not for macronutrient carb intake.

### persistence — 2014-03-09T06:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-114255>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=114252)
>
> ^^^^^ not good.

Jack, did I screw up somewhere?  What was not good?

### caroline — 2014-03-09T07:42:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-114259>

High quality members??? Ouch...

### persistence — 2014-03-09T08:39:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-114270>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=114259)
>
> High quality members??? Ouch...

Caroline, I'm brand new here. So when I open up the very first thread in a forum and see that the poster is an emergency room doctor, yes that is a pretty high quality poster. It tells me people in the medical profession are here reading these forums and Jack's blog. That's not a put down of anyone. That's a thumbs up that the content is such high quality that it attracts people whose life work is medicine.

I've generally noticed that the best nutrition sites are ones that attract both people in health fields (practitioners or researchers) and motivated patients. I'll describe "motivated patients" as people with either above-average interest in nutritional science or serious health issues that traditional medicine doesn't address effectively. The worst sites are the ones that are made of up people who are caught up in diet fads. I'm really excited to have found this resource. I'm reading through some of Jack's articles on the main site and they fit my case perfectly. I think I can learn a lot here.

### RobH — 2014-03-09T19:51:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-114343>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=112770)
>
> Thanks Rob for sharing this ....... now we need an update ......please!

Ok, brief update ... more to come

From Feb 20th 2012 or so when I started CT / Paleo (before Epi-Paleo was revealed to the world) / Leptin Rx, without even being "perfect" as my odd job still demands some night hours, fairly high stress, occasional stupid eating mistakes etc, until August 1, 2012. I lost 78 pounds following Jack's protocols. I felt incredible. Like I was 20 years younger. My energy skyrocketed. My friends and family were shocked and amazed (as was I). A whole bunch of them started "following Paleo" and many had varying degrees of success, although none quite like mine, but many weren't as sick as I was to start with (I didn't have any conventional illnesses, only obesity, which we doctors are pretty good about ignoring as an illness or sign of disease). I pointed many to JKs website, and tried to explain that it seemed to be more than diet -- I was rewiring my brain to be more sensitive to Leptin, I was trying to follow circadian biology and light / dark cycles, and most importantly, I was getting cold. Frequently. And enjoying it. I tried in my limited way to explain the benefits I saw from CT and tried to introduce a diabetic friend to it -- he declined -- said he couldn't stand the cold. I tried to get him to start slowly, showed him the CT protocol (dunk your face, etc.) but he never could wrap his mind around it and he continues to rapidly decline from his disease.

Most people took from me that it was the diet. I made a handout and gave it to a bunch of my patients that started to introduce them to the concept of changing diet to reduce inflammation. I pointed them to some of the Paleo sites and Jack's site as the ultimate resource. You must understand this is pretty unusual behavior for an ER doctor (as was wearing funny orange safety glasses during evening and night shifts... I have to wear contacts to do so and generally prefer not to though) so many of my physician colleagues thought I was a little crazy, while at the same time asking me just what i was up to and how I was getting so much better so much faster. But when I told them they chuckled and went back to work.

Found out from some labs (ZRT) that my cortisol levels were very high and so worked hard on reducing them. Started using the Heartmath device, and stopped coffee for a couple of months.

During that time my father passed away. I flew out to visit him on my birthday in April 2012. He had been diagnosed with Stage 4 Colorectal cancer about 5 years before and had availed himself of every bit that modern medicine had to offer. He made it well past his doctor's initial predictions of his survival but that was after an aggressive surgery to resect metastases from his liver, and multiple rounds of truly awful chemotherapy. It was heartbreaking to see my well-educated, rational, wonderful father waste away in front of my eyes. I saw him in March and planned to visit again in May, but got the call in April from his oncologist telling me that he didn't have anything else to offer, and also the call from my mom that she was having a hard time taking care of him. I was supposed to have my first educational consult with Jack that day but emailed him from the airport on the way to take care of my dad, asking him to postpone it a few days.

I had tried during the last month or so of my life to get my dad to change his eating habits and try CT (even though I wasn't even sure it would be good for him but based on Jack's early CT blogs thought it could help, that and some allusions he had made to treating cancer patients with it)

When I got to Arizona to visit my dad I was shocked to see how much he had declined in the last month since I'd seen him. I won't go into extensive detail on the events of the following days but they were pretty tough.

I did have an educational consult with Jack during the next few days. I had asked him on my email to postpone to tell me what I could do to help my dad. I was amazed at he torrent of information he let loose and took 7 pages of notes which I still have. What astounded me was that he had taken my email request very seriously and clearly spent a tremendous amount of time researching some very specific ideas to help with the cancer. And all of them were based on studies and science and reasonable and rational ideas mixed with his unique viewpoint and understanding of medicine, biology, and now I know physics. He came up with a cocktail of supplements that I could try, and suggested some other measures.

This was literally 4 days before my dad passed away and at that point there was no going back and the doctor in me knew that but the son in me wouldn't give up. I took my niece and we went to the local health food store and bought all the supplements. We mixed them up into a smoothie tried very hard to get my dad to take a few sips -- he could barely lift his head but we tried anyway, and I guess trying provided us some solace and helped pass the time during a tremendously difficult week. I know that had I known what I know now (or had I met Jack) 5 years before maybe I could have made a difference in the quality (and quantity) of his life but that is not how the world worked. My dad had the best "conventional" medical care had to offer but it wasn't enough.

I sat by his bedside and administered pain medications until he took his last breath. Fortunately my family was able to fly out to join us and he died a peaceful and dignified death at home, surrounded by loved ones, not in some awful ICU. As a doctor I see death too frequently in the way that nobody should have to undergo it and I am so grateful that my dad wasn't in that setting.

My dad had a PhD in Organic Chemistry and was a professor at a local college. He stayed up late nearly every night working on his lecture notes and grading papers. He sat at his desk next to this ominously humming fluorescent work light which he had had for many years. He stayed up late and then drank hot chocolate right before bed in the winter. He was fascinated by computers and spent many hours in the computer lab with the big mainframes learning to program with punch cards (and introduced me to the same). We were the first people I knew to have a personal computer and he and I spent a lot of time working on it (although it was a green CRT not a nice blue light LED display) together. He always complained that he never slept well.

He came from a long-lived family (his great grandfather died at 102, grandfather at 93, father at 91 or so (see the pattern) so why he got and epithelial cancer at 67 or so is a bit of a mystery... well maybe not so much when you look at some of the information above....

I'll say this again if you are reading this Jack: thank you. You showed a tremendous amount of compassion and you have helped me at that time more than I can possibly express.

I think I will stop now. More to come at some later time.

Thanks for asking.

Best

RobH

### persistence — 2014-03-10T00:14:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-114395>

> [RobH said:](/goto/post?id=114344)
>
> I'm not sure I qualify as high quality yet. Working on it though!
>
> RobH

Hi Rob. I lost my father a few years ago as well, and my journey with that was not much different than yours. Now that I have learned so much about nutrition and food intolerance issues, I have come to realize that my father (and grandfather, who also died of intestinal cancer) suffered from very similar problems to the ones I have: wheat intolerance, prediabetes. Both of them in later life developed heart disease, which I suspect was directly related to high insulin / high fasting glucose. I feel bad that I was not able to see their symptoms correctly and intervene when there was still time to affect the outcome.

Having watched two relatives die now, I am familiar with the extremely fragile state of someone in their last month of life. It's heart-breaking to go through this. I understand exactly your sense of desperation to attempt anything possible to extend life. I feel particularly bad for those people whose loved-ones get to a stage where intubation might extend life a few weeks, but usually under horrific conditions for everyone, and without any hope of a different outcome.

I share your opinion about Jack. To me he is exactly what physicians should aspire to be. He is trying his best to help people avoid chronic diseases in the future by paying attention to warning signs in early stage of the disease and taking action while you still can.

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-10T13:11:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-er-doc-from-california.173/post-114516>

> [Optimalbound said:](/goto/post?id=114514)
>
> Jack has several times called bullshit on Jaminet's musings about "safe starches".
> [http://jackkruse.com/the-rewarding-feeling-of-safe-starches/](http://jackkruse.com/the-rewarding-feeling-of-safe-starches/)

I heard Jimmy Moore say to Professor Cordain once that Paul Jaminet had said that his (Cordain's) diet had given him scurvy and that he'd said that starches this and starches that ...

Professor Cordain basically said, "I don't know of him" (as much as to say, he may move in your world but not in mine) and "My diet can't give you scurvy." A little _de haut en bas_ but fair comment given the provocation I thought. As for starches, Cordain went though the stuff on glycemic load for Mr. Moore.

I don't think it's true to say that "Paleo is really a ... ketogenic diet", Pone. If you look at Cordain's books, and journal papers, he actually recommends quite a lot of carbohydrate. He's not offering just a twist on what, say, Phinney, Volek and Westman are offering. He also reckons that the macronutrient ratios he recommends reflect what current groups of hunter-gatherers actually do. Since Cordain doesn't recommend cereal grains or legumes, principally because of the antinutrients in them, (and doesn't recommend sugar/HFCS either), and fights shy of potatoes because of their glycemic index, he achieves those macronutrient ratios on fruit.

There's nothing, absolutely nothing, in _The Paleo Diet_ or _The Paleo Answer_ on ketosis. The topic isn't even mentioned.

Jack, of course, would not have you eat fruit out of season. Standard Paleo has never talked much about seasonal eating.
