### AKMan — 2012-04-05T01:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-thyoid-connection.218/post-11523>

I read through the 1300+ responses on CT-6, and nobody seemed to question these claims about the thyroid. There were no cites that jumped at me saying 'thyroid studies', and nothing I have ever read on the thyroid indicates you can survive more than a few months without T3 somewhere in your system. Would love to see some discussion on this!

From CT-6:

"""THYROID TOO?

ALL REUNITE (MORE GEEKY THOUGH): THE WARM ADAPTED STORY of metabolism and biochemistry YOU HEAR REGURGITATED EVERYWHERE ON THE NET:

Once leptin enters and binds to its receptors, it effects the lateral hypothalamic tracts to immediately send a second messenger signal to the thyroid to signal it to up-regulate thyroid function and efficiency. See whne we are warm adapted it require help to partially access this pathway from T3 hormone. If you are LR you never can access this pathway. This specifically is how we can raise our basal metabolic rate when we are leptin sensitive. These coupled events, matched with leptin’s actions peripherally in muscles, occur at the UCP3 sites……to burn fat as we sleep at a higher basal metabolic rate. This means electron chain transport does not make ATP as usual. When leptin allows this uncoupling to occur we make heat and not energy from normal metabolism. This means we will burn off our excess calories as pure heat. This is one reason why calories in and calories out argument makes no biologic sense once you understand how leptin works. Humans are built to burn fat at night as we sleep to loose excess weight we don’t need. This is our modern day equivalent of hibernation. Out big fat amazing human brain got rid of winter sleep and took over a two hour window during sleep to replace it. CT-7 covers the gorgeous symphony of human circadian biology. Since it shrunk our risk of mismatches has risen exponentially. This is how your brain or feelings, can undercut you, when you are unaware of what a sped up epigenetics meant to human mammalian biochemistry. SKEPTICS: You wanted data, and now I am killing you with it.

THE COLD ADAPTED HUMAN THYROID FXN: does not bother with T3 at all. Why? When you are supremely LS by cold you go straight the the source, the hypothalamus and make TRH from the brain. The brain controls all thyroid function in cold…….forget the Moose thyroid.

SKEPTIC BOMB: You bypass all hormones and TSH too. TRH drives the whole show. The brain is completely in control and it up regualtes fat burning everywhere. This is how the Ancient Pathway lights your pilot light. The warm adapted human always complains about the cold and always feels cold……the cold adapted on is always pink to cherry in cold radiating heat like a furnace. You can thank TRH for this. This is does not even require a thyroid gland either. Is not life grand in the cold, folks?"""

I read through the 1300+ responses, and nobody seemed to question these claims about the thyroid. There were no cites that jumped at me saying 'thyroid studies', and nothing I have ever read on the thyroid indicates you can survive more than a few months without T3 somewhere in your system. Would love to see some discussion on this!

### AKMan — 2012-04-05T01:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-thyoid-connection.218/post-11524>

From Wikipedia:

"The (thyroid hormones, T3/T4) act on nearly every cell in the body. They act to increase the basal metabolic rate, affect protein synthesis, help regulate long bone growth (synergy with growth hormone) and neuronal maturation, and increase the body's sensitivity to catecholamines (such as adrenaline) by permissiveness. The thyroid hormones are essential to proper development and differentiation of all cells of the human body. These hormones also regulate protein, fat, and carbohydrate metabolism, affecting how human cells use energetic compounds. They also stimulate vitamin metabolism. Numerous physiological and pathological stimuli influence thyroid hormone synthesis.

Thyroid hormone leads to heat generation in humans. However, the thyronamines function via some unknown mechanism to inhibit neuronal activity; this plays an important role in the hibernation cycles of mammals and the moulting behaviour of birds. One effect of administering the thyronamines is a severe drop in body temperature."

So, how can CT bypass all this?

I'm too new at this to tell you if that's normal or not, unfortunately. # Thread: How are you getting the water cold enough? It's spring already.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### itsybitsy — 2012-03-17T01:37:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1772>

The warmer temps mean warmer tap water and it is more of a challenge to practically and economically get the temp down. Out of the tap, mine is in the 60's.

My fridge only makes so much ice and it melts before the water gets cold enough.

How are you doing it?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-17T02:10:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1786>

> **itsybitsy;1774 wrote:** The warmer temps mean warmer tap water and it is more of a challenge to practically and economically get the temp down. Out of the tap, mine is in the 60's.
>
>
>
> My fridge only makes so much ice and it melts before the water gets cold enough.
>
>
>
> How are you doing it?

I use reusable ice blocks that I keep in ziplock baggies and all the ice my fridge makes. My water is about 60-62 coming right out of the tap. I get the water to 55-58. I need more ice blocks but have run out of room in my freezer!

### vwaggs — 2012-03-17T02:16:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1791>

I am putting water from the sink into big tupperware like containers so that I don't use up my filtered ice and freezing it in the freezer.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-17T02:42:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1802>

Freeze tap water in emptied 1/2 gal milk jugs or other tall, slender things if you're struggling with freezer space. Then lay them in the tub and let them chill that water on down. Easy to rinse off and refreeze when done, and free.

### mshobbs — 2012-03-17T07:47:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1878>

> **Clabbergirl;1805 wrote:** Freeze tap water in emptied 1/2 gal milk jugs or other tall, slender things if you're struggling with freezer space. Then lay them in the tub and let them chill that water on down. Easy to rinse off and refreeze when done, and free.

+1 for the milk jugs and the tupperware etc. Here in OZ, the water temp is around 80 so it takes a sh!tload of ice to get to anywhere near 60F. I have 12 x 2L milk bottles cut in half and frozen along with a couple of tupperware containers filled with water. I have a 90L upright freezer that is used exclusively for the production of ice!

We are starting to come into autumn so hears hoping the water starts to cool down shortly.

### quelsen — 2012-03-17T09:19:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1888>

Old Fage containers and large ziploc containers so far two and two. my tap water is currently 51F

### Glamorama — 2012-03-17T10:37:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1895>

> **mshobbs;1881 wrote:** +1 for the milk jugs and the tupperware etc. Here in OZ, the water temp is around 80 so it takes a sh!tload of ice to get to anywhere near 60F. I have 12 x 2L milk bottles cut in half and frozen along with a couple of tupperware containers filled with water. I have a 90L upright freezer that is used exclusively for the production of ice!
>
>
>
> We are starting to come into autumn so hears hoping the water starts to cool down shortly.

In Melbourne the tap-water is about 60-65 degrees. I throw in ice bricks and throw a frozen gel-pack over my belly.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-17T11:21:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1901>

> **Glamorama;1900 wrote:** In Melbourne the tap-water is about 60-65 degrees. I throw in ice bricks and throw a frozen gel-pack over my belly.

What brand/type of gel pad? I'd like to use something like that on my belly too.

### sd13 — 2012-03-17T16:03:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1973>

I filled and froze 11 one liter bottles and I use three homemade gel packs. Plus keeping the water level in the tub a wee bit lower aids in getting the tap temp down from 60 degrees to 50. I use the gel packs on areas that aren't direcetly covered with water, plus I rotate--lay on my back at start, flip to stomach etc. In addition the bottles can be placed under arms, under the chin...you get the idea. I got the gel pack idea from someone on line--ziplock bag with a ratio of 1 part rubbing alcohol to 2 parts water--but frankly I don't measure. The bags are very cold and you should use caution at the start by placing some sort of materital (towel etc) between bag and skin. The bottles don't have that issue being placed directly on the skin.

### JC2K — 2012-03-17T17:02:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-1997>

I use two liter soda bottles. The 1 liter bottles sd13 mentioned might be better though if you've got a small tub. Annoying thing (to me) is the bumping against the sides of the tubs. Seems to sound like a drum. I might try using the alcohol/water in the bottles (instead of zip lock bags). Might get it colder than just ice.

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T18:16:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-2031>

Ya'll are so creative with your ideas! I just pick up a 20-lb bag of ice at Kroger. ;-) Siphon off some of the ice into ziploc bags for my torso; the rest goes into the water. Keeping up with the ice is kind of a pain ... this is going to a $100 / month expense if I keep buying the 20-lb bags! I'm gonna work on some of your ideas, see what Jack does, too.

### Bee — 2012-03-17T18:56:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-2051>

These ideas are so much better than buying bags of ice! Off to find the rubbing alcohol and some ziplocks, and grab those empty milk containers from the recycling box!

### tellmisty — 2012-03-19T06:14:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-2632>

Tonight for my cold bath, two huge slabs of ice - frozen into containers about a foot in diameter. I think they worked just as well as the 20-lb bags as far as water temps. Yeah, save the dough and it's so much more convenient, too!

### nonchalant — 2012-03-19T17:44:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-2768>

Water getting warmer, yes. I used up my frozen bottles of water and all my ice cubes, then remembered the quarts of lard I have in the freezer. They did the trick.  I'll try putting alcohol in those bottles, maybe that will help as the water warms even more.

Not looking forward to our warm tap water summers. I've learned that every summer I need refill my water glass after I brush my teeth and let it sit. Then when I brush my teeth again, at least the water will have cooled down to room temp. Guess I can do that with the tub as well.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-19T17:47:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-2769>

my icemaker got jammed up this weekend because of the overload from CT. So I was forced to create my own large blocks of ice in the freezer. But lucky for me, my well water is usually 50 or below if I run it long enough.

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-19T19:37:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-2833>

I'm bought some 1 gallon buckets with lids. I'm going to try and add rubbing alcohol to see if they will get colder. How much alcohol do you normally add?

### kathylu — 2012-03-19T21:04:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-2886>

I'm using 1 qt soda water bottles (frozen of course). It takes 8 of them to keep the water between 53-55 degrees. Then I pop them back in the freezer.

### Adriana — 2012-03-21T08:30:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-3808>

I like the idea of 4 gallons of reusable frozen containers. Is it possible to refreeze gallons daily? In my case the freezer is downstairs and the tub is up, hauling them up and back downstairs adds weight training .

Reusable frozen containers also allows you to use less water as this raises the water level by displacement. It also allows recycling the tub water, although it might require additional ice since the standing water will warm up to ambient temperatures which are higher than the temperature of the water coming out of the spigot.

### Jillm — 2012-05-07T11:30:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-21896>

You are doing well, mshobbs

### fitness@home — 2012-05-07T19:59:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-21973>

I feel blessed to have found a new local business that is selling 20lb bags of ice for $1 each. It is a self-serve ice trailer set up in a shopping plaza. Insert your $ or card, choose bagged or loose, and wait for it to drop down one of the chutes. (Great idea for a niche business in Houston. Wish we had thought of it...)

Two bags thrown into my bathwater 30 minutes before my bath cools it perfectly. There is plenty of ice left in the bags to place directly on my abdomen/chest.

I do ice baths 2-3 x per week and ice packs on the other nights. So, even at 3x per week it is only $24 per month. We have a large freezer so storage isn't an issue.

### Drew — 2012-05-10T10:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-are-you-getting-the-water-cold-enough-its-spring-already.220/post-22790>

I guess I'm lucky in that I live close to the Cascade Mountains. The tap water is pretty cold year-round (45 degrees or so), due to melting snow.

# Thread: curious about leptin/cortisol effects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-leptin-cortisol-effects.222/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gmel1084 — 2012-03-17T03:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-leptin-cortisol-effects.222/post-1808>

Hello everyone,

Im relatively new to the subject of hormone optimization but am eager to learn.

I have have been athletic my whole life, but despite being very active (I'm on my feet all day work, I have been training intensely in martial arts for years, and also do CrossFit up to 3x/ week), I just can't get to the point with my body where I'm very lean. Coming across the topic of hormone optimization on Mike Mahler's website, I began to wonder if it is Leptin/ Cortisol related. Upon more research, it definitely seems to be the case. With my activity level, I wouldn't be surprised if I'm experiencing adrenal fatigue, HIGH CORTISOL, and LR, IR.

My diet for the most part is good, but I definitely cheat a lot. The last body fat test I got said I was around 13%, but I store most of it in my lower stomach and love handles. For a very active person for years this is frustrating.

I'm 27 years old, 5'7, and 155lbs. Obviously not a bad thing but I'm intrigued by hormone optimization and its role in the fitness industry.

After coming across Dr. Kruse's Leptin RX info I knew I was on the right track.

I try to space out my meals as much as I can, but with the amount of workout volume I do, its hard when you want to optimize your post workout recovery with shakes, and make sure that you're getting enough calories.

Any help is appreciated!

Greg

### Owl — 2012-03-17T08:07:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-leptin-cortisol-effects.222/post-1883>

Adrenal fatigue is actually what happens when you've had high cortisol for a long time, and then the adrenals start producing less and less of it. Then your levels can fluctuate during the day from too low to too high. Over time, cortisol levels drift downward more and more, until they are all too low.

I have high cortisol, although it's much better now since I've been working on reducing it. My symptoms were being a night owl, not feeling tired at night, staying up till 1am. I didn't sleep well. Also waking up at 3 or 4 am or so. When I got up in the morning, I was not rested. I needed caffeine to get through the day, because my sleep was so bad.

I tested my cortisol with an adrenal saliva test. My night time and morning cortisol was through the roof, no wonder I wasn't sleeping well.

Dr. K would tell you to cut out the Crossfit. He thinks chronic cardio and/or very intense workouts like that will shorten your telomeres and therefore your life. He'd also tell you the shakes after workouts are not necessary.

I started on the leptin reset last summer, and am eating post leptin now. I used to snack all day, because I was hungry all day. With the leptin reset, you have to limit yourself to 3 meals a day, no snacking. Eventually, you get to 2 meals a day, because you're just not that hungry. And it really works. I am not hungry in between meals anymore. I feel much better about that, not having to always carry snacks with me to keep from getting ravenous.

According to Dr. K, you can spot reduce fat by using cold packs on those areas. There's a medical device called Zeltiq, which operates on that principle. Research showed that frostbite victims had fat cells permanently destroyed, and Zeltiq was designed around that. With the cold packs, you're not trying for frostbite, just red/pink skin. It takes longer than Zeltiq, but it works on the same principle. It sounds ideal for you. Check out the Cold Thermagenesis (CT) threads.

### gmel1084 — 2012-03-17T21:28:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-leptin-cortisol-effects.222/post-2106>

Thank you for the response.

I'm definitely going to look into CT. Im looking forward to it.

what adrenal saliva test did you use?

I just came across this site yesterday and there's so much info to go through. Whats the best to start with in regards to Dr. K's Leptin, CT, Cortisol thoughts, etc?

### Owl — 2012-03-18T04:54:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-leptin-cortisol-effects.222/post-2296>

For cortisol: [http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/](http://http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/)

He's done 6 or 7 blogs on CT, but here's the first, where he tells how to do it: [http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

You really do need to read the rest too, though.

Make sure you read the comments after each one, sometimes there's even better info there.

### gmel1084 — 2012-03-18T05:02:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-leptin-cortisol-effects.222/post-2298>

Thanks a lot!

I'm looking forward to delving into all of this info.

There's so much information out there and this is a great site.

I'm going to start with cutting out the post workout shakes and just getting in a clean paleo meal within a couple hours post workout.

And I'm also going to spread my meals out to 3x per day with protein and fat in the morning and see how it goes.

My workouts are mainly in the morning so I have to time it properly.

### Owl — 2012-03-18T09:26:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-leptin-cortisol-effects.222/post-2318>

You're welcome, tell me how you it goes for you. It was tough for me with no snacking for the first week or so. I was eating 3 huge meals a day, and was still hungry in between. Breakfast was 5 eggs and 1/2lb of ground beef! (and I'm female, 5'7", and at that time 147lb) But then it got much easier, and I wasn't hungry at all in between.

At your weight and height, you may not be leptin resistant. I know I was, because I had just lost 75lb with hcg, and was having trouble keeping it off, had gained back 15lb. (I then lost 13 of those pounds on the reset.) The leptin reset is designed to confuse your hypothalamus so it reroutes your leptin signalling to an older way of accounting for calories we all used in utero and up to age 1 -2. If you're leptin sensitive, this will be a waste of time, because your hypothalamus is working fine. So you may want to follow the eating plan of the post leptin rx, for people who are leptin sensitive: [http://jackkruse.com/so-you-completed-the-leptin-rx-what-is-next/](http://http://jackkruse.com/so-you-completed-the-leptin-rx-what-is-next/)

# Thread: Phoenix!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Phoenix — 2012-03-17T03:44:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-1839>

Freeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeezingggggggggggggggg!

Got out of the tub about 15 mins ago. This was my first time all the way down to 55 degrees!! I just figured I'd be acclimating to the cold water for about a year. But it's only been a week!

Woo Hoo! Yay, me!!

I stayed in for a little over 20 minutes too. So big accomplishment for someone who thought cold showers were intolerable. Not so anymore.

Fifty five degrees in a tub for 20 minutes!! I can't believe I'm there!

### Hotmail — 2012-03-17T11:21:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-1900>

You are so brave, I can't even bare to think of CT now

### Phoenix — 2012-03-17T17:40:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-2013>

Sure you can @Hotmail! I love heat and despise cold. After last night I'm hoping to tip the scales in the other direction. It wasn't bad at all. I started with warm water about 3 inches deep. Then turned on mostly cold with a little hot and laid back in the tub. When it got to be about 8 inches deep, and I knew I could tolerate that I turned on 100% cold and let it fill up. It was about 65 degrees at that point. Then I let about half of the water out while continuing to fill with 100% cold. That bought it down to 55 degrees and I just laid there and read a magazine for 20 minutes. It was easy.

The hardest thing about it was after it was over. Seems like the cold hit me at that point. I wasn't shivering, but I was cold and it took me a long time to warm up. You'd think that when you come out of the tub and into room temperature that it would feel warm. It's just the opposite. Very weird phenomenon. I understand that's common though.

### sandy — 2012-03-18T00:37:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-2159>

The CT is not nearly as bad as I expected. Nothing I look forward to, but tolerable. I am a warm loving person. Love warm baths, love crawling under my huge down blanket.

hope you are having a great weekend!

### Phoenix — 2012-03-18T01:33:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-2175>

> **sandy;2165 wrote:** The CT is not nearly as bad as I expected. Nothing I look forward to, but tolerable. I am a warm loving person. Love warm baths, love crawling under my huge down blanket.

I agree. It's really not. I just took my second one. It was not at all uncomfortable. My husband came in and stuck his hand in and got this look of horror on his face. He's all for me trying CT. I'm hoping he'll do the same. This morning there was a scream from the bathroom. It was him. At the end of his shower he turned down to water to the 60 degree mark just to give it a shot. So funny!

You have a great weekend too! Stay cool!

### ginastarke — 2012-03-18T02:04:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-2186>

I started out hating cold too. After reading up on CT, I started enjoying the thought of killing fat cells instead of just shrinking the little suckers like CW says is the ONLY thing you can do to them. Now when I I'm outside without a jacket and shivering I grin, thinking "DIE, fat cells, die!"

### Phoenix — 2012-03-18T04:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-2276>

> **ginastarke;2192 wrote:** "DIE, fat cells, die!"

I LOVE it! I'm going to have to remember that.

### Phoenix — 2012-03-19T00:39:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-2486>

OK, I'm seriously thinking about getting in the tub, but keep making excuses ...

So let's see if this works: "Die, fat cells, die!"

### Phoenix — 2012-03-19T15:11:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phoenix.225/post-2708>

"Die, fat cells, die!" worked, and I got into the cold tub. It was a very uncomfortable session, though. Very cold and I was uninterested in staying there. I perservered for 30 mins though. I never got the "warm up" that I got on my previous tub dunk.

And I seem to be suffering for it today. Sore throat, sore knee, sore back and just feeling kind of blah. I think this is the detox that comes with CT initially.

### Hotmail — 2012-03-19T16:15:50+0200

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 96
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Riversedge — 2012-03-17T06:54:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-1872>

Ok, started this Leptin Rx on march 2nd. I was 312 lbs weighed in today march 16th at 296 lbs. This is going so great its a little scary. I'm not craving anything although until today I was making and eating everyday a paleo chili i made up, but did not know tomato's were a no no even though the list was in the matrix I have.

So today was a great day I did some CT for the last 3 days and its going well just showers but the water here is almost 40 degrees so it does the job I'm up to about 5 minutes taking a deep breathe works for the shock although the shock is fading.

I still have not started working out (which makes my weight loss even greater) because of a dislocated shoulder and clavicle which is the reason or excuse for the weight gain of 60 lbs in 4 months. Surgery in a month unreal how the ins. company delays.

I will stay on this path for the rest of my life.

Mike

### Hotmail — 2012-03-17T11:19:10+0200

### Riversedge — 2012-03-18T05:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-2305>

Thanks Hotmail...

Another good day today. 3 eggs and chicken sausage patties for breakfast. Had a late lunch I wasnt hungry until 4 so my late lunch was also dinner. I had Sirloin steak about 14 oz and a bunch of asparagus W/coconut oil and garlic sauce.

My diet Rockstar addiction still lives on i will slowly wean myself off it (mostly for the caffeine) soon. I had my first craving and it was huge, for the grapes in the fridge so i had a few bites of chicken and 2 huges glasses of water. No CT today i will get back to it in the morning.

Well until next time folks i wish you all the best stay strong and chilled.

### adam_ — 2012-03-18T22:12:56+0200

Great to see that there are other guys making progress with these approaches - well done.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-19T05:20:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-2607>

Adam,

Thanks for the compliment. The Matrix is on Robbwolf.com unfortunately tomato's are a nightshade and Dr. Kruse does not use them and I want his results so I will follow it to a T. Adam also a great book to get is Paleo Solution by Robb Wolf i have read it cover to cover at least three times.

I did paleo for six months last year leading up to my Triathlon. Unfortunately I fell at work and dislocated my shoulder and Clavicle a month before. I was devastated and went into my six month funk. I think Dr Kruse has th perfect approach for me. I first heard him on the Paleo Summit and it seemed as if he was speaking right to me. Now here I am trying to get through all the blogs forums, studying and understanding the quilt.

I had another great day on the RX. Steak for breakfast, the last of my paleo chili (which is off the menu because of the tomato's) for lunch and leftover steak and sausage patties for dinner. I did pick up 3 pounds of haddock with every intention of having it for dinner but time was not on my side. CT was 6 minutes in 45 deg shower hoping to get in the ocean soon its a half mile down the street but I just don't think I'm ready for that yet.

until next time Stay frosty friends.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-19T20:50:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-2876>

Well, I took my before Pictures I forgot to take at the start. I have already lost 17 lbs and good thing I did'nt take them 3 weeks ago because I am totally disgusted with myself. I cant believe how bad I look. Not another piece of bread, pizza, grain or carb of any kind until I look like I used too. CT for sure tonight

Stay Frosty friends

### Riversedge — 2012-03-21T03:43:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-3737>

Today was a great day for my reset. I don't weigh myself everyday, just every Friday. today's menu was 5 egg breakfast, 1lb of steak tips for lunch and the lb of haddock is cooking on the grill. I did CT this morning and I will do it before bed, I'm still on the showers the water out of the tap was 48 so my skin is numb coming out.

The other adventure I had today was my MRI on the Shoulder with dye shot into the joint. very painful but hopefully will give the Surgeons a better look at the damage so I can move on with surgery or get back into working out.

I hope everyone had a great day today.

Stay frosty friends

Another great day on this Pathway. The CT is getting easier. My muscles are getting bigger, even though I'm losing weight and can't workout because of an injury. This is all strange to me I am only eating twice a day already, not forced just not hungry in the afternoon. I did have a craving Saturday night when I got my son a pizza but it passed. I have been 100% since 3/2 and I'm feeling It is every aspect. Had some London Broil for Breakfast (I still need some work on cooking it). ### Riversedge — 2012-03-28T04:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-7526>

Well, today went ok i stayed strict even tried to kick my diet energy drink habit I did cut it out for most of the day but what a caffeine headache could not pick my head off the pillow in the late afternoon so I broke down and had a Rockstar damn it. I will wean off instead of cold turkey. I dont like coffee so this is what I have used for my little boost over so many years. The headache prevented me from going to the butcher today for some fresh meat. So I had leftover london broil and sausages for breakfast, My Chili for lunch and also my chili and 2 sausages for dinner. I will get a better variety tomorrow some fish for sure and free range chicken. I had an awesome CT session this morning over 20 minutes for the first time. ### Jim — 2012-03-28T05:46:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-7576>

Mike,

You are doing great. I used to drink lots of Diet Coke for the caffeine too. I weaned my self down to two drinks per day over a few weeks. Then after a while on the diet I totally lost the craving for it. One day I just stopped. Stay on the diet and stay away from all artificial sweeteners. One day you'll no longer feel the need and just stop too.

Good luck.

### Riversedge — 2012-03-29T22:23:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-8452>

Thanks Jim I appreciate the support. I had a busy day yesterday stayed on the good path. Rotisserie chicken on salad with skin of course. Dinner I had a piece of sirloin and prime rib i made for the first time. I had an awesome CT session yesterday.

Today is a different story... I had a great breakfast 4 eggs and 5 slices of thick cut bacon. Lunch I had pineapple ugh. I wasn't going to eat but not sure what came over me the same old tired mind games with myself. I did squash that "well its over now" thinking and let it go.

I'm going to have seafood tonight just hope I can cook it better this time (I hate seafood). I will get a CT session in today at some point the tap water here is back under 50. I ordered all the supplements last night and I will start them when delivered. I have been taking lots of D, but seeing I don't like seafood I need to get the fish oils in.

Stay Frosty my friends

### Riversedge — 2012-03-29T23:25:17+0300

### Riversedge — 2012-03-31T05:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-9093>

Happy Friday all. Today was a good day lunch with my daughter. The University food is a disappointment I had to resort to pork loin and greens. I will write a letter telling them my concerns. I think the meal plan was $2700 this year and in no way was it worth it. Pizza, tacos, subs a great salad bar but NO bacon or eggs really. I hope dinner is set up better who knows. Anyway I had chicken wings for dinner I was going to have a Nice big Porter house but the gas on the grill ran out.

I had The best CT session yet this morning! Granted I'm still just doing the showers but our water is at 47 so it does the trick. I stayed in for at least 20 solid minutes no flinching or ill effects. It didn't end there I came up with an Idea to open up the window (second floor as not to scar the neighbors) and not dry off while shaving and brushing my teeth. It was as if I was still in the shower at 34 degree wind hitting me. That is the first time I actually shivered since starting CT.

Stay Frosty Friends

### Riversedge — 2012-04-02T04:01:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-10107>

I hope you all had a great weekend....I know I did. I had great CT sessions this weekend for the first time I stayed cold for hours after. I dont know if that is a good thing but I was just billowing heat all weekend. The water dropped back to about 48 this weekend and one session it was 47 a few degrees makes a huge difference it seems. The eating was great. Saturday I had eggs and Bacon for breakfast, flank steak for lunch and steak tip on greens for dinner with some chicken. Today was similar breakfast Eggs and bacon, no lunch and Paleo Chili for dinner.

My one month Leptin RX weigh in is Tuesday! I know the number doesn't matter, as I am seeing progress in the mirror big time. I still '"Do Not have any Cravings" this is the FIRST time in my life I can say that, it just makes me smile. ~I'm Krusing~

Stay Frosty Friends

### Riversedge — 2012-04-04T05:03:17+0300

Had a great CT session again today. I have been spot on w/leptin rx the past few days. Today I'm tired and seem to be getting cold or flu like symptoms. I haven't been sick in years. Is it toxin dump or is my immune system breaking down? Heading to bed

Stay Frosty Friends

### Riversedge — 2012-04-10T02:04:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-13180>

Wow, its been six days since my last forum post. The flu like symptoms vanished the next day, toxic dump? I had a great weekend as far as staying 100%. I had ham yesterday. I had a slight craving my Dad's apple pie but I didn't touch it. Never mind the manicotti and homemade sauce with his made from scratch meatball's. The Diet has been solid and 100% but I lost 2 pounds this week I am always hoping for more as do most of us but I was good with that.

The CT is going Great the water runs between 47 and 51 here out of the tap. I can stand there all day it seems. I am going to take it up a notch with the CT.

I finally have my surgery date April 19th. I will start today with a more intense CT and see if it makes the surgery go a little easier.

The next thing I need to attack is my sleep, its all over the place. I have the room pitch black and still lay there waiting to fall asleep. I started reading the Monk that sold his Ferrari last night and fell into a deep sleep after about 40 pages so I will do the same tonight only much earlier.

Stay Frosty friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-289 ~4/6/12

### Riversedge — 2012-04-14T03:31:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-14976>

Thanks Kiwi and Lyndra, I had another great week. I cant Believe I have not touched a Carb in almost 6 weeks! No cravings like I said thats a first for me. I wish I was working out but that will have to what for another 6 months or until I get the OK after rehab.

The sleep is all over the place but the past few nights I had to knock myself out with a few Benedryl's I'm hoping that will get me back on track. I think I'm a little anxious about the surgery also so that's not helping.

The CT is going great I now enjoy it and I'm not bothered by the freezing water. I actually look forward too it. I do think the shower is better I don't really feel the bath. I have heard people say if your still in the bath your body creates a barrier of the heated water who knows. All I know is the constant flow of 48 degree water is awesome and freezing. I do let the tub fill during so I'm up to half the calf my feet being cold seems to make the body colder.

My Diet has been spot on (except the diet rockstar) I still seem to need the caffeine. I had my weekly weigh in today and I'm down 4#'s this week I love it.

Stay Frosty my friends

CW-285 ~4/13/12

Thanks again Lyndra, Hope you had a great weekend. I had another great weekend stayed strict and haven't had a carb (minus the pineapple a few weeks ago) since 3/2. I still need to get the seafood up. I had some Thursday in Maine broiled haddock much better when other people make It.

The CT is amazing 25 minutes in the water at 49 degree's. Spot CTing the shoulder to get it ready for surgery. It will be 88 here tomorrow I have my pre-Op and hoping to walk down to the beach and try the 46 degree water.

The Boston Marathon is tomorrow and the are asking people to sit it out. I thought they were the healthy ones? Maybe they should start a winter one. I'm sure when I get home, I'll here how many people dropped or Unfortunately died. They will need lots of ambulance's on heart break hill.

Thank you all. Hitting the rack now. I have everything covered for the recovery. I will keep in touch here when I can. I do have high expectations for a quick and smooth recovery.

The other day I smacked my arm on the door knob when the door bounced back. I've done CT multiple times a day since getting the two huge bruises, one on the bicep and one on the forearm. It absolutely amazed me that after about two and a half days and about 7 CT sessions the bruising is completely gone! I cant even figure out where they were originally. CT is simply amazing me with every discovery.

### Riversedge — 2012-04-21T04:56:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-17315>

Hey everyone, thanks for the nice thoughts very much appreciated.

Well just a quick note here. Surgery went well pain yesterday post was a little harsh. The Pain block that i didn't really want but after talking to many folks I had. The problem was They said I have strange anatomy? They could not pinpoint the nerves so they just injected where they thought it would be. Needless to say they found the wrong nerves even as of today part of my neck, trap and chest are still numb. So no block on the shoulder area, I now know = pain.

The surgery went as expected, except the damage was less then thought from my contrasted MRI three weeks ago. The fraying bicep was minor, the tear in the supraspinatus and labrum were aaaaahhhhh gone! So CTing for the past 6 weeks and i cranked up the CT for the weeks leading up to surgery. I will ask more info on the damage seen in the MRI and what was seen when he went in. I didnt have a chance to chat with him after I was real out of it.

I left with Oxycodone made me sick so I was giving Vicodin later at night. I had extreme pain after i got home and for about 10 hours on. I took the Vicodin at 10 pm and 2 am with motrin in the in between hours. Today I felt incredibly good upon waking. I got up at 5:30am had lots of chicken for breakfast and only have had Motrin today and my last one was 8 hours ago. I had a great CT session today. I had the shower water directly on the shoulder for at least 10 of the 25 min at 48\*. I was actually colder than usually and light headed in this session not sure why maybe all the garbage pumped in me.

The list of meds yesterday was unreal General anesthesia, Fentnyl, versette and morphin yikes. One of the Incisions was left open I guess for drainage. The bone chips and spurs were taken care of and I think was the main reason for the pain. I will know more on April 30th. I cant help but think that the CT had an major part in the healing. I cant explain the tears not being there i hope my surgeon can. I will tell him about what I have been doing. I told the nurse all about it yesterday before surgery, but I think she was just listen and pacifying me. You can lead a horse.

Well, I'm off to sleep four recliners no waiting. I can't believe How much I miss my bed.

Stay Frosty Friends

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-21T06:07:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-17328>

CT heals muscle......and its reprodible in humans. I use it all the time in spine surgery

### Riversedge — 2012-04-22T04:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-17627>

Well, My shoulder feels pretty good. The rest of me is a mess. I'm pretty sure all the meds on the day of the surgery are messing with my system. I didnt sleep more than 2 hours last night. I'm agitated, back is sore, my neck is sore not sure what they did to me. My stomach is the worst, haven't had much to eat i'm getting some food in. The constipation has finally ended.

My CT session was great today 30 minutes and I was freezing. The ice has never left my shoulder since the surgery for more than a half hour. I did the Pendulum exercises today and that went well. I did a little stretching. Hoping for a better day on Sunday.

Stay Frosty Friends

### Riversedge — 2012-04-23T02:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-17932>

Thanks Lyndra, Its been a long weekend but productive.

Let me start by saying I'm CTing the rest of my life. I can not believe the healing process CT brings about. The movement I have in my Shoulder and arm is amazing. I haven't taken any narcotics since early yesterday morning. I took 1 Motrin 800 today. I have also been spot CTing all day, the shoulder wrap with straps is awesome. I'm thinking of starting a company with a hot tub that has a cooling system, I'll call it a Jackruzzi.

They told me at least 6 months of PT. I'm so much more optimistic about a fast recovery. Thanks to Jack Kruse, the Malibu Triathlon in September just may see me compete.

### Kathy — 2012-04-23T02:24:23+0300

### Riversedge — 2012-04-25T04:16:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-18592>

Thanks Kathy, I've had some good and bad days. The past few have been tough. I have started doing a lot more movement which of course brings with it pain. I have taken some Vicodin the past few days. The CT today was good but didnt feel as great as it has maybe the healing process. I haven't been eating a lot a few days just one meal but paleo. I will be making my Paleo Chili tomorrow, I need some nutrients and I load the chili with kale and spinach.

I lost a 1Lb this week well last week I didnt expect to lose much but I'll take it. I have had problems sleeping also. I have always slept on the surgery side so it will a while I guess.

### Riversedge — 2012-04-25T07:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-18626>

### Riversedge — 2012-04-27T05:22:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-19253>

Well, its been a rough week but I'm making progress. I have had a great week of CT and LRX doing at least 2 sessions of CT a day the Shoulder getting the most attention. I have gone back and forth with the pain Meds, when I do have pain if possible I hit the cold water.

I went on my Surgeons website (which is very cool) and found out exactly what he did. Three Procedures, most being bone removal and burring down the AC and Clavicle removal of the (bursa sac?) and tendon removal and reattachment. I will ask more questions Monday as to why the Supraspanatis was not fixed, I'm thinking the tear was not more than 50% and if so what kind of improvement i had since the contrasted MRI. I do have some great range of motion already hand can already go above my shoulder and I can touch my lower back (sounds easy) with my hand. I do get waves of pain and I'm pretty sure its the bone healing I have broken over 20 bones in my life and the pain is similar. This surgery is harder than the knee in some aspects.

My LRX has been great but with full disclosure I did have a steak and cheese sub the night after the surgery. The only other problem this week is not eating after waking. Sometimes I would not eat until almost noon but that has changed back to BAB. I blame the drugs mostly, as it has messed up my stomach and elimination.

WHEW, that's all for now. My weigh in day is tomorrow, I'm hoping to break the 30lb's lost mark.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-284 ~4/20/12

GW-201

### Riversedge — 2012-04-28T04:53:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-19515>

Well, today was an ok day. I did not sleep more than 2 hours last night today was needless to say a lay around lazy day. The shoulder acted up a lot today.

The good news today is I'm officially down 30lbs in eight weeks with just a few cravings (and not acting on them) the whole time. I had an awesome CT session. Even though I had a bad day, CT made it better. I will go to sleep early tonight and rest up.

LRX went great later in the week. Earlier in the week was a nightmare late bab and didnt eat enough. I need to get out of this house!

Have a great weekend all.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-282 ~4/27/12

GW-201

### Riversedge — 2012-05-03T02:48:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-20864>

Hey All, Thanks for the great encouragement and thoughts.

Everything is going well in every aspect of my recovery. I have had a great week with Leptin reset and CT. I even have my son doing CT now which is great. I am also trying to get him into the Paleo lifestyle, he is 19 and said he will try his best. I bought him a TRX last year and he is in great shape but wants to see his abs pop. I am just interested in him being healthy, but he is 19 and as you know we were all invincible then or so we thought.

I will be hitting the ocean on Friday (the water is at 52) after all its at the end of the street and the shower water is starting to warm (bummer) it was 56 today. I still spot CT all day with the ice on my shoulder it helps with the pain.

I received my Blue blocker glasses and started wearing them at night. Also I have the F.lux app on my laptop now, Thanks to Kevin Cottrells guest blog on CT11. What an amazing story and results, he is truly inspiring.

I hope this note finds everyone well and Optimal.

I very much appreciate all of you and the support here.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-282 ~4/27/12

GW-201

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-03T02:54:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-20866>

We appreciate you too, Mike. Thanks for the update. You're doing so well. My surgery is scheduled for the 29th, or earlier if a spot opens up.

I've ordered blue blocker glasses too. I have the F.lux. It's so fun to see my screen switch to pink.

Onward to optimal health!

### kcorcoran@comcast.net — 2012-05-03T03:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-20867>

Well done, keep up the good work!

### Riversedge — 2012-05-05T04:42:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-21500>

Thank you.

It's been a good week some ups and downs with the shoulder. The cold seems to be the only thing that really helps the pain. The Vicodin does not seem to do as well. I did take a few more but have noticed the difference when I do the CT. Yesterday and today were terrible, because I had my first PT session yesterday and she killed me. She did get my arm all the way straight over my head which is good in a recovery way but painful.

CT was awesome today. I was in the shower today it was 55 and I had three ice blocks (frozen water jugs) in the tub as it filled. After a 15 minute shower I slipped into the bath for 45 minutes. I had 2 of the blocks on my chest and stomach (w/compression shirt) that's the first time I've done that and now I'm hooked. I was going in the ocean today but the weather was terrible. I will be out of town this weekend so I will try for the ocean monday.

The Leptin reset is going very well. More fish needs to come into my diet. I'll have to start hitting the fish restaurants here they are very good and god knows I still cant make it taste good. I did get more greens in this week. I'm down another 3lbs this week and noticing more changes in my body. I am getting more muscular which still is a trip for me because I haven't done any moving at all just in the CT and out. I don't fully understand it but I love it. I think my hormones (for once in my life) are starting to align.

UPDATE: Wooo Hoo I will be out of town but i was just informed I will have a huge JACKRUZZI in the room with ice down the hall. That = as many great CT sessions as i can get in. I (while she is at the Rick Springfield concert) will be totally chilling out...Literally!

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-279 ~5/4/12

GW-201

### Riversedge — 2012-05-08T04:13:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-22075>

I hope everyone had a great weekend I know I did. The CT sessions in the jacuzzi tub were great. The water was at an even 50 and I was in for two 30 minute sessions on Saturday. Sunday we slept in so I was not able to do another. I did a shower CT at home on Sunday the water was 55 and 40 minutes. Today was 35 min at 55 degrees.

The Leptin reset was good but again full disclosure I cheated Saturday night.Johhny Rockets Double Cheddar Cheeseburger and a chocolate shake. I know I waont do this again the bun and choc shake killed my stomach all night. Scary how my body has changed those used to be a staple binge food for me. I was back to it Sunday and today.

Rain for the next three days so I will shoot for the ocean Thursday its 51.2 today.

I will be rejoining the gym tomorrow legs legs legs, until my shoulder is ready and the Dr. gives me the OK.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW-312 ~3/2/12

CW-279 ~5/4/12

GW-201

### Lyndra — 2012-05-08T06:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-22123>

I just experienced a similar lesson myself after ingesting some wheat. Won't do it again. That's what life's lessons teach us. It is remarkable how what used to be tolerated seemingly without problem now appears to be quite toxic. Keep at it with the CT.

### Riversedge — 2012-05-09T01:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-22410>

Lyndra, That was a tough lesson for me also.

I am happy not to be one of the folks here with the leaky gut, Diabetes or any other chronic problems. I have looked at my past BG levels and I am shocked at some of the numbers I see. I didnt realize or simple didnt know the implications of such high numbers. I did like the decline in BG numbers to the 80's, 90's and once 75. So not only has Jacks RX bringing me closer to an LS life. Obviously I was pre Diabetic from the numbers I see in front of me (thats scares the \*\*\*\* out of me). I witnessed one of my favorite people when i was young (my great uncle Mike) lose toes then feet then legs and finally pass away from this destructive disease. Looks like I found Jack just in time. I think what saved me over the years was a job that had extreme physical demands.

To anyone who is Interested, I have done Paleo before (last spring and summer) and it worked well. The major observation and difference between Paleo and the Leptin RX With CT (and that is crucial) are the cravings. On the Paleo alone I did lose weight but I always had a plateau I could not break, the 255 mark. The cravings were extreme and I think the CT has crushed them.

People can attack Dr. Kruse all they want. I know what my results are. Will they work for them? I don't know. You will never know if you just attack him without actual data of your own. I have told Jack many times that I was drawn here by the summit and I just related to his story. I think his paragraph about people who have never been Obese, thinking they know the journey or the psychological stressors we have to navigate are simply delusional.

I'm done ranting sorry. I re-joined the gym today and as you may or may not know I can only do legs. I can say I was not ready for the 8 month lay off weakness.

I do have to tell you I have never blogged before (and it probably shows). I only started blogging because I felt it would help me with this journey, and it truly. One byproduct i hope for is it helps just one person here. Whether it be to simple start the pathway or some sort of Inspiration to keep plugging away. I am thankful for the people here that have inspired me many times.

So I have a favor to ask. I have always loved the notion of "Pay it forward" and do so in anyway I can. I remember reading here in the trenches of this web site. Someones story of how they are following the pathway but eating cans of tuna everyday and struggling to make ends meet. I would like to offer a delivery of Us Wellness meats to be delivered to them. I am not sure how to go about it or even if they would except it. It would be my small way of paying forward and I owe that not only to them but to myself.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 279 ~ 5/4/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T18:47:15+0300

### Riversedge — 2012-05-13T09:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-23624>

Thanks Lyndra. Jack I keep an open mind with everyone but quickly become a skeptic when I hear the same old things ive heard for years.

I didnt lose any weight this week not sure why but thats ok its been falling every week for two months.

CT is going fantastically ice bath with blocks of ice today .

It has been a tough weekend Boston University lost three incredible kids yesterday. My daughter is very upset and I told her we are fragile and to live everyday like its your last. I think I'll take my own advice. As you can see I'm still up at 3am I just hate my mind racing I know she is in her dorm finishing her paper but I wish she was here in her room.

Stay Frosty Friends

### nonchalant — 2012-05-14T02:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-23778>

Mike, I know exactly how you feel. My son is in Afghanistan. You have to let them live their lives.

### Riversedge — 2012-05-15T22:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-24338>

Hey Nonchalant, I do let them live their own lives even though I tweak there thinking now and again. Your son is in our thoughts we thank him for his service to our Nation.

I had a little set back with the shoulder at PT. We are not sure what happened but I think she just bent it to far back to early and pop. I don't know what it was hoping just inflammation the next few visit will tell the story. I came homein serious pain and went to my trusted friend CT. I also followed that with the Vicodin I haven't taken for a while. Seems better today.

Last week I did not lose any weight I didn't gain any either. I will be going to the gym every other day now but still only legs and Ab's. I could do the other bicep, tricep and shoulder but I'm not sure about throwing off my symmetry and balance.

Any Thoughts on this anyone?

My eating remains the same Leptin reset I did have berries W/whey smoothie one morning but that was it for carb's besides Veggies. My FBG still remains optimal. I still never get a Ketone reading any higher than trace or Negative. I will be making a yearly physical appointment for the beginning of June with a request of all the pertinent test's. I know she will be happy with the weight loss but she doesn't know about the shoulder yet. I will let her in on the Protocol I have been following

Well, here's to another great week. I WILL make it in the ocean by the end of the week. I can still smell low tide I will wait for high tide for sure. I am allergic to seaweed and the last time it hit me in the face I looked like Rocky Balboa after the first fight.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 279 ~ 5/11/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Lyndra — 2012-05-15T22:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-24344>

So sorry to hear about the setback. CT will be your ally with that.

I think you'll be fine just doing lower-body workouts for a while. I broke my wrist last December and did mostly lower body but also did what I could with upper body. I found a study that said that your injured arm will gain in strength even when it idly watches while your other does a workout. So much strength gain is neurological so that makes sense to me. Plus it's good mental stimulation trying to figure out how to adapt your routine.

I hope you get better soon. Keep hanging in there.

### Riversedge — 2012-05-29T17:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-28178>

Hey all, I took some time off from posting but I'm back. My shoulder is slightly improved except for the muscle that runs to my mid-back from my rear delt. I will know more monday at my follow up with the surgeon.

I have been eating well trying to tweak a few things. I am still (3 weeks in a row) at 279lbs. The only thing I changed is doing heavy leg workouts twice a week so maybe inflammation, muscle gain or just a systemic adjustment.

CT is going very well I have been in the ocean almost everyday this past week and weekend. The ocean temp runs between 52-56. I have an infrared temp gun of course with the sun the closer to shore the warmer, so I tread out at least 100 yards first. I have been staying in for an hour or so one day 90 minutes. I also have gone in for 45 and came out read for a bit then back in for another session.

I hope to connect with the groups going for Ocean swims in Rhode Island, NY and California It would be great to meet some fellow Cter's.

I hope you all had a great long weekend and are looking forward to a great summer.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 279 ~ 5/25/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Lyndra — 2012-05-29T18:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-28182>

Good to hear from you again! I wish we had an ocean nearby - salt water just has its own feel. Let us know how the follow-up goes.

### Riversedge — 2012-06-03T17:44:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-29214>

Thanks Lyndra, I see the surgeon Monday morning but it has not improved much unfortunately. I have been doing my CT in the ocean and I love it. So much better then standing in a shower or laying in the tub. I did get seaweed in my eyes and of course that night my eyes swelled shut. Up at 3am for benadryl that was Thursday finally today it's almost gone. The eating is going the same I am trying to incorporate more fish but. I just can't stand it, I will keep trying.

The Weight has finally started moving again. Down 2lbs this week so the plateau has been broken! Movement will be ramped up this week training needs to start for the Triathlon in september. Whether I can compete or not is still in the air. The swim training will have to wait until the shoulder is fully healed. The one positive is I am a very strong swimmer and will need very little training. I will do breathing exercises all summer in the ocean which will help a lot.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 277 ~ 6/1/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-04T02:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-29284>

For water temps I just bring a meat thermometer.... Simple! Best of luck on your recovery

### Riversedge — 2012-06-06T03:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-29650>

The Surgeon visit went well. I was concerned with the pop but he wasn't and said it was an adhesion/scar tissue. I got him to give me another Ice wrap which I love, now I have two. I am progressing well, he told me I need patience with this surgery. The typical recovery is 6 months its been 6 weeks. I guess I'm just anxious to get back into life. I still spot CT my shoulder all day long, no drug has helped with the pain like CT.

I had a great workout today legs and Ab's of course. I "tried" the use the left side upper body and the right will neurologically keep up, but It throws off my back and neck way to much so I will stick with legs. I will try to get on the bike this week the problem of course is the shoulder I will use my full suspension on the smooth bike path and ride with one hand if need be.

The CT has been awesome! I enjoy the ocean good thing the weather will be better going forward. I don't care if its raining its the nasty surf and undertow i worry about not having use of both arms. I am heading to Cape Cod for a few days the ocean will be nice and cold. I will also eat only fish while I'm away.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 277 ~ 6/1/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-06T03:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-29651>

Great update Mike.....

### Riversedge — 2012-06-17T03:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-31679>

Hey All, It has been a very rough few weeks for me. I lost one of the greatest people I knew. My auntie Honey was like a second mother to me when I was young and she will be missed very much.

The Clavicle is acting up along with the shoulder. The Clavicle needs to be addressed by the Surgeon we shall see where that leads.

The Nutrition has been great, I did have black berries and cherries Tuesday but wow did it mess up my stomach. Ease back into fruit if you are going to, I think I will leave them out. I have lost a pound a week the past two weeks not much but heading in the right direction. I have been doing leg workouts for a few weeks and have gained muscle so we will go with that as the reason....

The CT.....Well, simply amazing. Nothing beats the ocean for it! I was in Connecticut (rocky point beach) earlier this week and the people thought I was insane. I stayed in for over an hour at 61\*. The pool is open and I have spent a few days in there at 67\* but today it was up to 70 so down the street I went. The ocean water was 60\* I was in for 50 minutes and yes the only one in the water. The insane looks continue. I stayed clear of the seaweed (allergic) as much as I could but the sea was angry my friends we shall see tonight.

Hope everyone is doing well and enjoying the Summer. I hope I can run into some folks out in Southern Cal I will be there for 3 weeks on Vacation.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 275 ~ 6/15/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Lyndra — 2012-06-18T00:43:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-31832>

Glad to see you checking in. I'm very sorry to hear about losing your aunt. Please accept my sympathies. That makes everything so hard.

Cool that you are getting into the ocean for your CT. Keep up all the good work.

### Riversedge — 2012-07-02T23:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-35128>

Thank you Lydra for the kind words.

I'm doing great the shoulder is finally making progress again. The clavicle has good days and bad we shall see. The CT has been great. I hit the ocean as much as possible which has been everyday unless we have thunder and lightning. The water runs around 60 give or take a few degree's I stay in for an hour if possible never less than a half hour. I also have the pool sometimes I journal the pool but often I'm in it for a few hours a day. The water temp is all over the place but right around 70.

I did see someone mention somewhere on here they are no longer sensitive to the sun with CT. I also am not burning and have nice even tan no peeling.

Paleo has been going as planned. I did try (last Saturday night) the cheat meal route. I had Chinese food and oh my god never again. I think it was the MSG and Carb combo but I thought my heart was going to beat out of my chest I was up all night. I cant believe I used to eat that a few times a month. I did drink at my nieces wedding but just vodka on the rocks no mixers and didnt take much two drinks and I was done.

The workouts are going great still just legs and abs with easy cardio but I have to ramp that up soon. The triathlon is still questionable although even if my shoulder is not 100% I'm a strong swimmer and have no fear I will just train my lungs in the water until then.

I lost two more pounds since last logging on. I seem to be down to losing a pound a week consistently and I'm fine with that. I am sure when I can start swinging the Kettlebell and get back to heavy lifting it will step up.

I finally found my journal I was 320 on Feb. 15th two weeks before starting this pathway. I was following Paleo but not the leptin RX.

I hope you are all enjoying your summer.

Stay Frosty Friends

SW ~ 312 ~ 3/2/12

CW ~ 273 ~ 6/29/12

GW ~ 201 ~ ?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-02T23:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-35133>

Mike I referenced you in a consult today.......somebody had an ortho issue and I told them to come to this thread for your experience.

### Riversedge — 2012-07-09T22:10:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-36381>

Jack, I hope my experience can help. The results are amazing. I had my visit with the surgeon Friday, ten weeks out from surgery and my results are ahead of schedule. I have learned so much just having this Injury and following all the protocols. I actually think t5he recovery would have been even faster (in my layman's opinion) had the Sternoclavicular Joint been addressed earlier in my recovery which has been an excruciatingly long 10 months. I am sure it is messing with the mechanics of the shoulder and its stability. I will finally be getting an Ultrasound guided injection.

The eating has been great I finally tried IFing it went well. I had imagined I would pass out without food for 24 hours but with not eating carbs and still 0 cravings it was easy.

I am down 40 pounds since starting the Leptin reset 48 total.

CT has been great as always, the Ocean is amazing and will be integral part of the rest of my life. The side benefit is the golden tan I have and how my skin adapted to the sun on its own, without the chemically laden sunscreens.

I will be heading out to California for my yearly 3 week summer vacation. You better believe most of it will be in the Pacific Ocean from Malibu all the way down to the border at Imperial beach.

I hope you are all having an Excellent summer I know I am (minus the shoulder Injury). Off to the beach

Stay Frosty Friends

CW ~ 272 ~ 7/6/12

I slacked a little on the CT this week, I finally got my first tattoo. It is to honor my Mom. I cant go into Salt or Chlorinated water until next week.

My weight is the same as last week, so I am going to do a little tweaking again with foods and moving more. I will keep my eye on the SO-CAL CTer's and hopefully meet up with them at some point.

### Riversedge — 2012-09-07T10:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-matarazzo.228/post-49940>

Hello all, well it's been a hell of a month literally. I have discovered through N=1 that CT for a fact in my experience curbs carb cravings. I made the mistake of stopping CT and caffeine at the same time to see where it would lead. The good news is I only gained 3 pounds which is monumental considering what I ate.

The caffeine addiction I had (sugar free Rockstar I don't like coffee) was brutal. The withdrawal was unbearable at times as I was up to 4 16oz cans a day. The headaches were extreme and took 3 weeks to improve, the fatigue is still an issue. I'm just hoping I didn't permenantly damage my adrenals.

That's it for now as you can sure see my sleep cycles are haywire also. On the upside My shoulder is almost fully healed 2months ahead of schedule thanks to CT.

Stay frosty friends

Riversedge

Ps: I'm off to California for another two weeks vacation on September 11th to watch the tri (shoulder could do it) but my body is not trained for it so I will be cheering them on from the sidelines.

### Riversedge — 2012-10-03T02:43:33+0300

Well, it's back to the primal ways. I went way off track had a nasty run in with the seaweed again and followed a devastating sad diet for three weeks. The damage 12 pounds and bloating, gas returned and I now know I can't drink alcohol any more.

The good news I have been cleared to work and I'm very excited. I need to do four weeks of work hardening. I am way ahead of them I started lifting weights again and boy does it feel good to be sore again, it's a great sore. The shoulder feels great some minor pain in certain positions but nothing I even notice. The shoulder held up well with military presses, lat pull downs side laterals but bench press still a little rough. I did some Kettlebell swings and they are awesome. I've never done them before but it will be a staple in my workouts.

I will be making great progress with this lifestyle now that I can put it all together. CT will be a daily ritual I cannot wait to see the results of ct and working out. I will stay 100% as I have in the past today is day two with the return of the pathway in my life.

Stay Frosty Friends

# Thread: Sleep Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-question.230/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-17T11:47:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-question.230/post-1910>

The time change has really screwed my sleep up!

So, I'm going to bed a little later than I used to, mostly because I'm doing CT after I get my son to sleep, and I'm waking up an hour earlier because of the time change. My bio clock hasn't figured out that the external clocks have shifted. So, before CT, I was sleeping 8-9 hours, and now it's closer to 7-7 1/2. I wake up refreshed and my energy throughout the day is good. Do you think the less sleep is okay, or should I be trying to rework things to get more sleep?

### marthabob2u — 2012-03-17T15:24:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-question.230/post-1955>

> **Evo Mama;1915 wrote:** The time change has really screwed my sleep up!
>
>
>
> So, I'm going to bed a little later than I used to, mostly because I'm doing CT after I get my son to sleep, and I'm waking up an hour earlier because of the time change. My bio clock hasn't figured out that the external clocks have shifted. So, before CT, I was sleeping 8-9 hours, and now it's closer to 7-7 1/2. I wake up refreshed and my energy throughout the day is good. Do you think the less sleep is okay, or should I be trying to rework things to get more sleep?

I'd say that f you are feeling good during the day the amount of sleep you are getting is fine. Your body will likely let you know if you should be catching more winks. It usually takes a few days to get the rhythm going again after a time change.

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T18:01:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-question.230/post-2026>

I find that after CTing (and time change), my nighttime sleep seems to be dropping to 7 hours, too; might be normal to get less if your sleep is better ...

I'm a little unclear on one thing after reading all of the CT blog posts(and comments). Does eating carbohydrates(veg, sweet potato, fruit) during the summer, while trying to become cold adapted create a "biological mismatch" and defeat the purpose? Jake
