# Thread: G'day
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.233/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lennyaus — 2012-03-17T14:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.233/post-1927>

G'day,

I am Australian. Living in Melbourne at the moment.

I used to be a heavy drinker - like most good old Aussie blokes. I have also had problems with my weight, which in my ignorance never thought that it could have something to do with the effect alcohol had on the liver, which is a vital organ of course, but like most parts of the body are taken for granted. Hell, I even counted calories when I was drinking.

I am 29 going on 30. For as long as I have remembered it has been an issue. Perhaps there was something wrong with me as I remember not being thin ever. However looking back at my year 12 photos... I actually was thin. I was 182 cms and 82 kilograms. For the yanks - that's a shade under 6 foot (I'll claim it) and 180 pounds. But after high school, something happened. Freedom. Liberation from academic studies (not that I was a scholar) and I gained 15+ kilos in the next year. I am currently 111 kilograms (244 pounds) and according to these scales, 29% body fat.

I have never been totally sedentary. I played sports a number of times during the week. Gotta burn those calories you know? Counted calories (even got into "negative" some days on the treadmill). I have tried cleanses. Fasting. Been a member of a number of gyms - and never went. I think I can count 4 memberships of the top of my head. I did use one of the gyms regularly - and I did managed to get down to 92 kilograms from about 105 - but that weights back again (gained it back when I met my fiancÃƒÂ©e. She is worth more than that). I have tried low carbohydrate. I have tried protein modified fasts and also intermittent fasting and now I am trying the leptin reset.

I can't say that I have lost any weight yet. I feel a bit better. I had to cut my nails for the first time in fifteen years (habitual biter, my girl reckons I have an oral fixation). I have been having cold water baths each night under the pretence of recovery aid from sport (train two nights a week and play a game each weekend) as due to being heavy I often pull up sore. I need this to work. I believe in it. I know the original prescription suggested no exercise, however I feel obligated too for the social aspect.

My fiancÃƒÂ©e doesn't believe in any of the nutritional research I have read. You name it and I have probably read it or at least have heard reference too. She doesn't believe me because I am fat. A guy who has read almost everything and is still fat. But she has issues too. OCD. Full blown OCD, not like those annoying people who because they like things in a line, they have self diagnosed themselves with OCD. Anxiety and Panic Attacks brought on by broken routines OCD. She also complains of stomach issues a lot (right after eating sandwiches or stuff - her mums a coeliac but testing has not shown she is - so she will continue eating them and not listen to me. Because I am still fat). Migraines - which incidentally have gone down since she did follow some advice about drinking more water. She is on anti-depressants and also takes the birth control pill. I feel a better diet than what she currently eats (bread, processed soup, pasta, coca cola) would help her immensely. But she won't listen. Because I am still fat. She however is not, she is just under 5'11 and 147 pounds. So I need to make the change that I have been wanting to make for ten years now in order to help the one I love and will marry next year.

I am also playing Australian Rules Football and my enjoyment of which suffers because of my size. Sure, it has its advantages in some ways (falling on people hurts them for example and you can inflict some pain on others) - but I don't train or play football for that reason. I want to actually "play". My size is holding me back here and instead of being a fun social activity with mates from my team, it's a source of anxiety and trepidation. Oh that, anxiety. I do suffer from some forms of that too and my loving fiancÃƒÂ©e want's to talk me into taking anti-depressants and doesn't see why I would not want to. I am hoping my depressed moods (which may be hormone related) and feelings of low self worth are a part of my own personal quilt which will be fixed with time.

Anyway, this has been quite therapeutic and I apologise for making this my post more like a blog post that is the pathetic venting of man who is being undermined by his neolithic thought processes. It gives me hope to see people who have benefited from the information that Dr. Kruse has provided freely from this website and blog. At first I was dismissive when reading, but the avalanche of positive results made it too hard to refuse. Listening to all podcasts he has been on was also illuminating. He did not sound like some crackpot, snake oil salesman. He sounded assured, informed and passionate about what he is doing. That has sold me and I hope I am one of the many who will benefit from his wisdom.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Gained weight after signs of LS?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T14:44:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1940>

Hi all,

I'm making my debut.  I've been reaping so much info from you all, and finally decided to post.

I started the Leptin Reset the week of Christmas, (immediately after finishing a round \[my first, and last round\] of hcg injections) and began seeing all the signs of being Leptin Sensitive at the beginning of February. I stabilized at my last injection weight of 116.4 throughout this whole time. (and aside from discovering a pork sensitivity, felt really great!)

In the past 4 weeks or so, I've started including HIIT 1 or 2X a week, along with the LHT program from MDA.

Now in the past couple weeks I've started to 'look bigger' in the mirror, and now I realized the scale is up 5 lbs!

The only things I've changed in the past couple weeks are :

\- the continued workouts, as described.

\- eating more butter

\- eating a slight amount of seeds and nuts (like a mini-handful, but most days of the week)

Now I think that I may have a leaky gut, since having 10 years of experience with anorexia/buliemia during my teen years (I'm 29 yrs now), and also a whole host of allergies (most are gone now!)

Does anyone have any clue as to what is going on? Or has anyone experienced anything similar, as far as the weight gain?

I have yet to switch over to the post-leptin eating plan, although I am often times just eating 2 meals a day, and my breakfasts have gotten a bit smaller 'on their own'.

And I have cut my afternoon coffee since a couple days ago, and the seeds and nuts. But still eating vlc (only a few green veggies, fruit only a couple times a week)

Sorry about the novel, and thank you in advance for any feedback you have!!

### tweety — 2012-03-17T14:59:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1944>

Some people are sensitive enough to fruits and nuts that it can definitely stall weight loss, if not lead to bloating or weight gain. On the other hand, are you sure its fat gain? IOW it could be muscle gain from the workouts. How do your clothes fit? Some things to consider here, but I am sure you will get other good advice from the more seasoned resetters.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T15:07:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1945>

Thank you, tweety, for your response!

I haven't taken measurements in the past few days, and my clothes are fitting alright, although I do see my belly sticking out a bit. It almost seems like bloat, since I used to be able to see definition in my arms, but now they just seem bigger (like the muscle is being masked right now!)

I appreciate your feedback, and I will continue to cut the nuts and also really watch the fruit....funny thing is, I don't usually even have a 'taste' for the fruit, which is GREAT in my eyes!

Thanks again

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T15:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1947>

I also just seem to be bigger all over.....does this seem like a sign of 'bloat', or is it more a sign of fat gain?

### L8F — 2012-03-17T15:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1958>

Hmmm, wondering if it is the HIIT--too often? And or nut/seed issue causing bloating? How is your gut? Have you had any testing?

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T15:46:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1965>

> **L8F;1963 wrote:** Hmmm, wondering if it is the HIIT--too often? And or nut/seed issue causing bloating? How is your gut? Have you had any testing?

I have not had any testing...

My gut is better than it was, but still not optimal. Have been constipated lately. (I had a long history of IBS along with food allergies, and as soon as I cut the grains this dramatically improved)

I may try to cut the HIIT for now....it just feels so good to move! Would you say that the LHT is okay to keep? I'd like to try to continue doing something, as long as it isn't screwing me up in the long run.

I would have never imagined that nuts/seeds could cause this much drama in my body....but maybe it's possible?

Ideally I would have liked to lose another couple pounds down from my 116.4, but now that I am up the 5 lbs, the 116 sounds so ideal!

Thank you for your feedback, L8H!

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T16:02:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1971>

Are you doing CT / ice baths, JoyfulJoyful? I'd do the leaky guy prescription stat and add in the ice baths (I think CT is what ultimately resolves most things and melts the fat). Ask Jack if you should stop doing HIIT ... I'm not sure that will serve you in the long run ...

I don't know if Jack has his bone broth recipe on that prescription, but he told me to do it for my leaky gut - a cup day for a week, then three times weekly for the next 2 months. It's organic beef broth (I got mine from Trader Joes) and grass-fed beef bones (got from Whole Foods) with a splash of apple cider vinegar, cooked for 20 hours on low in the crock pot. You can add in other "soup" ingredients if you want (onions, garlic, veggies, beef, etc). Broth is good in the fridge for 4 days; I had to freeze some.

### L8F — 2012-03-17T16:37:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1988>

Agree with bone broth.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T17:13:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-1999>

> **tellmisty;1976 wrote:** Are you doing CT / ice baths, JoyfulJoyful? I'd do the leaky guy prescription stat and add in the ice baths (I think CT is what ultimately resolves most things and melts the fat). Ask Jack if you should stop doing HIIT ... I'm not sure that will serve you in the long run ...
>
>
>
> I don't know if Jack has his bone broth recipe on that prescription, but he told me to do it for my leaky gut - a cup day for a week, then three times weekly for the next 2 months. It's organic beef broth (I got mine from Trader Joes) and grass-fed beef bones (got from Whole Foods) with a splash of apple cider vinegar, cooked for 20 hours on low in the crock pot. You can add in other "soup" ingredients if you want (onions, garlic, veggies, beef, etc). Broth is good in the fridge for 4 days; I had to freeze some.

Thanks for your advice, tellmisty!

I just started slowly doing CT. I haven't done the face dunks, but I started with soaking my feet with cold tap water (about 58 degrees from the tap) for a couple days, and then doing ice packs on my belly for 30+ minutes for a couple days. I just did my first cold bath today, straight cold tap water (no ice), for about 10 minutes. Then I tried a colder than usual shower. I got hives all over my legs! I will persevere though, it is really inspiring to hear all the success stories.....I take it that you have done CT?

Thank you very much for the bone broth recipe....Can you tell me what amount of each ingredient? (how much beef broth and what quantity of bones?)

I will try that right away!

And thanks, L8F for seconding the bone broth!

I really appreciate all the advice!!

### tellmisty — 2012-03-17T18:46:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2046>

I asked Jack for amounts, and he seemed to think it didn't matter. I fill the crock pot with broth (to get as many cups as possible) and just toss in a bone. LOL! A friend told me to add the apple cider vinegar, as it pulls micro-nutrients from the bone.

Jack told me to follow a Paleo diet, no nightshades (peppers, tomato, eggplants ... anything else?), no dairy (except eggs and ghee), and no gluten for Leaky Gut. I'm not sure how universal his recommendations were; I followed his instructions to a T. Seems the last round of labs had indications that my gut was better and thyroid was healing; yay!!!

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-17T19:52:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2074>

> **tellmisty;2052 wrote:** I asked Jack for amounts, and he seemed to think it didn't matter. I fill the crock pot with broth (to get as many cups as possible) and just toss in a bone. LOL! A friend told me to add the apple cider vinegar, as it pulls micro-nutrients from the bone.

> Jack told me to follow a Paleo diet, no nightshades (peppers, tomato, eggplants ... anything else?), no dairy (except eggs and ghee), and no gluten for Leaky Gut. I'm not sure how universal his recommendations were; I followed his instructions to a T. Seems the last round of labs had indications that my gut was better and thyroid was healing; yay!!!

Thank you, tellmisty! I will follow these directions....yay! I have a Trader Joes right by me (was just there yesterday ) and a Whole Foods not too far away, which I've never been to, and this gives me an excuse to go! (double yay!) And I'm so happy to hear that your labs are coming back with improvement!! I'm guessing that you are feeling well too, which is a GREAT thing!

I'm following all the rest of the recommendations that he gave you, for the most part. I'm sensitive to eggs so those are out for me.....Although I am putting heavy whipping cream in my morning coffee, and I use butter to cook chicken, or to put on veggies, etc......do you think that these also are a 'no-no'?

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-18T03:39:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2235>

This sounds like inflammation from the nuts, possibly. The exact same things happen to me even if I consume a small handful of nuts a few days in a row- all over size gain and water retention, distended belly, sometimes accompanied by acne. However, as soon as I stop the nuts the bloating and size go away in about 3-4 days. Unfortunately I love nuts and sometimes eat them anyway, lol!

### TheKid — 2012-03-18T09:17:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2317>

You know, I've been wondering the same thing about gaining and stalling out. I myself ahve been on the Leptin Rx for 6 weeks. Dropped a couple pounds max, but generally stayed within the same average weight range. I stopped all workouts exept riding my bike to work. I was really puzzled at the lack of continued weight loss.

I started CT two weeks ago and suddenly got crazy carb cravings. They got so intense couple of days ago that I succumbed and had a 4 donuts and large latte. Doubled back the next day with 2 donuts and latte.

Almost IMMEDIATELY I bloated. Pants fit tighter. Little more noticeable belly fat. It was awful.

What puzzled me was that I had previously lost cravings altogether and in fact was to the point where I frequently felt OK to skip lunch and just eat dinner. Other signs of LS were not really there yet; but I thought I was further along in the process. Can't figure out why the cravings came and why the binge had such a profound impact. Back to the drawing board.

### caroline — 2012-03-18T11:08:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2323>

> **TheKid;2324 wrote:** You know, I've been wondering the same thing about gaining and stalling out. I myself ahve been on the Leptin Rx for 6 weeks. Dropped a couple pounds max, but generally stayed within the same average weight range. I stopped all workouts exept riding my bike to work. I was really puzzled at the lack of continued weight loss.
>
>
>
> I started CT two weeks ago and suddenly got crazy carb cravings. They got so intense couple of days ago that I succumbed and had a 4 donuts and large latte. Doubled back the next day with 2 donuts and latte.
>
>
>
> Almost IMMEDIATELY I bloated. Pants fit tighter. Little more noticeable belly fat. It was awful.
>
>
>
> What puzzled me was that I had previously lost cravings altogether and in fact was to the point where I frequently felt OK to skip lunch and just eat dinner. Other signs of LS were not really there yet; but I thought I was further along in the process. Can't figure out why the cravings came and why the binge had such a profound impact. Back to the drawing board.

I had the same thing happen last week. I had a bunch of fruit \[wonderful peaches at a friend's\] and then couldn't stop eating for 2 days - it was as if a switch had been flipped and I had no control at all. quite disturbing.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-18T17:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2388>

> **TheKid;2324 wrote:** Can't figure out why the cravings came and why the binge had such a profound impact. Back to the drawing board.

it\`s what you chose to binge on that had that effect: Wheat, vegetable oil trans fats and sugar and plenty of each. to that you added a side helping of alpha-casein through the latte which is often a cross reactant to those who are gluten intolerant.

the bad news is you were bloated and felt crappy and are starting again from step 1. the good news is you discovered you are gluten intolerant and likely should avoid dairy so that may make the rest of the journey easier for you as now you know you can\`t have these foods without doing your body harm.

my suggestion is grieve the loss of these foods, seriously until you truly accept that you cannot have gluten again you will keep doing this to yourself, and move on.

I find for myself the best way to drop the cravings is to eat 80+% fat for a day or two. also the next time you do crave carbs go with less harmful versions such as sweet potato drenched in butter, frozen berries with coconut milk (makes a hell of a sherbert), 99% chocolate, a latte made with really good, organic, single estate, wet process coffee and coconut milk. any or all of these are far better for you than the donuts and latte

### David — 2012-03-18T17:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2389>

I had been experiencing a steady one-to-two pound weight loss each week, until I started CT a couple of weeks ago. Not only has the weight loss stopped, but my scale shows a net gain of four pounds over the past few days. I have not noticed any particular cravings and my meals are unaltered, still < 25 carbs per day.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-18T17:26:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2390>

> **David;2396 wrote:** I had been experiencing a steady one-to-two pound weight loss each week, until I started CT a couple of weeks ago. Not only has the weight loss stopped, but my scale shows a net gain of four pounds over the past few days. I have not noticed any particular cravings and my meals are unaltered, still < 25 carbs per day.

hormones, if you are gaining while using a diet that caused you to lose or maintain before then the new factor is hormones

did you get tested before or have any idea what needs fixing

### tellmisty — 2012-03-19T06:11:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2631>

> **joyfuljoyful;2080 wrote:** Thank you, tellmisty! I will follow these directions....yay! I have a Trader Joes right by me (was just there yesterday ) and a Whole Foods not too far away, which I've never been to, and this gives me an excuse to go! (double yay!) And I'm so happy to hear that your labs are coming back with improvement!! I'm guessing that you are feeling well too, which is a GREAT thing!
>
>
>
> I'm following all the rest of the recommendations that he gave you, for the most part. I'm sensitive to eggs so those are out for me.....Although I am putting heavy whipping cream in my morning coffee, and I use butter to cook chicken, or to put on veggies, etc......do you think that these also are a 'no-no'?
>
>
>
> I really appreciate the advice and I am very excited to implement it all!!

If you have leaky gut, Jack advises no dairy except ghee (pasturized butter) and eggs. Since you are sensitive to eggs, leave them off, and that might be confirmation that diary is no-no. I think he'd recommend coconut milk instead of heavy whipping cream.

Once your leaky gut heals, I believe you should be able to try adding these items back in to see how your body handles it ... should be okay!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-19T08:19:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2655>

> **ColleenM;2397 wrote:** hormones, if you are gaining while using a diet that caused you to lose or maintain before then the new factor is hormones
>
> did you get tested before or have any idea what needs fixing

Colleen, do you think it's coincidence that the hormones issue surfaces when starting CT? I've also noticed 'old' cravings since starting CT that I hadn't had for months. I haven't given in to them (have upped my fats when I feel them) but I find it tricky that I'm doing a bit of 'white knuckling' again re: diet. (I haven't gained weight, just the cravings.)

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-19T17:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2752>

Nope, don't think it's a coincidence at all. I've definitely noticed cravings for things I haven't wanted in months as well. my guess is that the toxin release is putting those substances in our systems which is triggering the cravings.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-19T18:13:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2787>

> **Grottenolm;2241 wrote:** This sounds like inflammation from the nuts, possibly. The exact same things happen to me even if I consume a small handful of nuts a few days in a row- all over size gain and water retention, distended belly, sometimes accompanied by acne. However, as soon as I stop the nuts the bloating and size go away in about 3-4 days. Unfortunately I love nuts and sometimes eat them anyway, lol!

Thanks for your input, Grottenolm...I cut out the nuts 2 days ago, and after the first day the scale went \*up\* .2 lbs, but today it is down 1.6 lbs. I know what you mean, I would hate to have to give up nuts! But if its necessary then I will do it, since if they are causing inflammation then that would mean that my body really doesnt like them! I want to deal with the leaky gut so that hopefully someday soon I can eat all the things that I am forbidden right now!

Thanks for sharing your experience....and it's great that you know what causes you the inflammation. It is so hard when you cant pinpoint what is going on!

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-19T18:25:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2790>

> **TheKid;2324 wrote:** You know, I've been wondering the same thing about gaining and stalling out. I myself ahve been on the Leptin Rx for 6 weeks. Dropped a couple pounds max, but generally stayed within the same average weight range. I stopped all workouts exept riding my bike to work. I was really puzzled at the lack of continued weight loss.
>
>
>
> I started CT two weeks ago and suddenly got crazy carb cravings. They got so intense couple of days ago that I succumbed and had a 4 donuts and large latte. Doubled back the next day with 2 donuts and latte.
>
>
>
> Almost IMMEDIATELY I bloated. Pants fit tighter. Little more noticeable belly fat. It was awful.
>
>
>
> What puzzled me was that I had previously lost cravings altogether and in fact was to the point where I frequently felt OK to skip lunch and just eat dinner. Other signs of LS were not really there yet; but I thought I was further along in the process. Can't figure out why the cravings came and why the binge had such a profound impact. Back to the drawing board.

Try not to be discouraged.....the way I look at it is this: at least you have confirmation now that gluten is no good for you, along with the other questionable ingredients in the donuts, as ColleenM said. I had some bad reactions to eggs and pork earlier (and have had more than my share of awful experiences with gluten, which I willingly subjected myself to!) and it was hard to feel the discomfort and see the scale go up (and my clothes fit so tight), but I really learned then that I shouldn't be touching that stuff. It strengthened my willpower in the end.

I have also wondered why the CT causes the cravings, and I guess we should be encouraged to know that it IS doing something, in getting all this junk stirred up and out of our bodies!

Keep your chin up! Youre doing a good thing!

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-19T18:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2794>

> **tellmisty;2638 wrote:** If you have leaky gut, Jack advises no dairy except ghee (pasturized butter) and eggs. Since you are sensitive to eggs, leave them off, and that might be confirmation that diary is no-no. I think he'd recommend coconut milk instead of heavy whipping cream.
>
>
>
> Once your leaky gut heals, I believe you should be able to try adding these items back in to see how your body handles it ... should be okay!

Tellmisty, thanks for your advice!

I did cut out the heavy whipping cream starting yesterday, and WOW I can't believe how tasty the coconut milk is!!! I would love to eat the whole can!  I don't feel deprived with it in my coffee, and I looked forward to my morning coffee this morning, knowing that I could add that in, AND that it wasn't bad for me! (i hope!?)

I also cut out the butter, and bought some ghee yesterday.

I never thought that since I was sensitive to eggs (which was also a shock for me to discover....I ate so many eggs previously! Like 5 a day while pregnant with my second son...And the interesting thing is that I had chronic back pain for years, and once the eggs were gone, I've since been fine!! Thank GOD!!) that it could also indicate a dairy sensitivity. I really appreciate you sharing your tips and input. And it sounds like you are very driven and applied to doing things right to get healthy. it is very encouraging and inspirational, to hear that you did things 'to a T'. And how awesome to hear that you are feeling better and seeing positive results in your labs. That is so cool. I also want to do things right and not waste any more time going 'half-way' and never seeing any results! I'm in this and already seeing SO many benefits. I'm so grateful!!

Thanks for sharing what Jack shared with you also, and I believe that I will benefit from the advice! I'm hoping to make the bone broth tonight...yay!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-19T20:04:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2853>

> **ColleenM;2760 wrote:** Nope, don't think it's a coincidence at all. I've definitely noticed cravings for things I haven't wanted in months as well. my guess is that the toxin release is putting those substances in our systems which is triggering the cravings.

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-19T21:32:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gained-weight-after-signs-of-ls.234/post-2907>

> **tellmisty;2052 wrote:** I asked Jack for amounts, and he seemed to think it didn't matter. I fill the crock pot with broth (to get as many cups as possible) and just toss in a bone. LOL! A friend told me to add the apple cider vinegar, as it pulls micro-nutrients from the bone.
>
>
>
> Jack told me to follow a Paleo diet, no nightshades (peppers, tomato, eggplants ... anything else?), no dairy (except eggs and ghee), and no gluten for Leaky Gut. I'm not sure how universal his recommendations were; I followed his instructions to a T. Seems the last round of labs had indications that my gut was better and thyroid was healing; yay!!!

How did you tell that your thyroid was healing? Are you on any thyroid meds? I am  and I'd love OFF! (I'm still by habit following more or less reset protocol + icy tubbing CT)

# Thread: Will CT make living in a hot, humid place worse?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-ct-make-living-in-a-hot-humid-place-worse.235/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### miper70 — 2012-03-17T15:21:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-ct-make-living-in-a-hot-humid-place-worse.235/post-1952>

I live in Georgia so it's pretty hot and humid here during the summer. I have always had a problem with heat, so I'm worried that doing CT will make going out even worse than it is now. Since Dr. K. lives not too far from me, wondering how he deals with the hot summer months. Let me guess......more ice....lol.

Michele

### marthabob2u — 2012-03-17T15:27:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-ct-make-living-in-a-hot-humid-place-worse.235/post-1956>

We are having super hot weather here in Mexico right now- Mother Nature is having a hot flash!  I have been doing CT in the tub and am finding it really refreshing. I use a collection of ice blocks to cool the tub water down and then refreeze them for the next time.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-17T15:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-ct-make-living-in-a-hot-humid-place-worse.235/post-1960>

> **miper70;1957 wrote:** I live in Georgia so it's pretty hot and humid here during the summer. I have always had a problem with heat, so I'm worried that doing CT will make going out even worse than it is now. Since Dr. K. lives not too far from me, wondering how he deals with the hot summer months. Let me guess......more ice....lol.
>
>
>
> Michele

No it won't. Basics law of thermodynamics as you use CT to become cold adapted your body's energy systems will become more efficient, making it easier to cool you in warm weather, and warm you in cold weather...

CT will make your boy more efficient which will = greater temperature tolerances.

### miper70 — 2012-03-17T15:45:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-ct-make-living-in-a-hot-humid-place-worse.235/post-1961>

Nice, maybe I'll actually be able to go out with no problems this summer.

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-17T15:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-ct-make-living-in-a-hot-humid-place-worse.235/post-1968>

I so hope you're right  It isn't even summer yet and I was sweating horribly yesterday. I'll continue with CT and hopefully things will get better or I'll be wearing an ice vest all summer here in Texas.

> **Shijin13;1965 wrote:**
>
>
>
> CT will make your boy more efficient which will = greater temperature tolerances.

# Thread: What is CT? What is the prescription?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-ct-what-is-the-prescription.236/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### adam_ — 2012-03-17T15:23:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-ct-what-is-the-prescription.236/post-1954>

I'm loving Kruse's insights, and completed a leptin reset last year, but I can't seem to find a summary of what CT is, and what the guidelines are?

Could somebody please point me in the right direction, as this sounds really interesting.

Many thanks,

Adam

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-17T15:58:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-ct-what-is-the-prescription.236/post-1969>

Start here: [http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

### marthabob2u — 2012-03-17T16:09:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-ct-what-is-the-prescription.236/post-1974>

> **adam\_;1959 wrote:** I'm loving Kruse's insights, and completed a leptin reset last year, but I can't seem to find a summary of what CT is, and what the guidelines are?
>
>
>
> Could somebody please point me in the right direction, as this sounds really interesting.
>
>
>
> Many thanks,
>
>
>
> Adam

Go read this: [http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-two/](http://http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-two/)

I love this quote- _When cold is perceived by skin cold receptors over two weeks leptin is liberated from fat cells in massive quantities. Cold empties fats stores like like a fire empties a movie theater._

Start out by immersing your face in cold water (50- 55F) and then progress to cold showers and cold baths. There is a lot of info in all the forum threads'

Read, read read!

### Phoenix — 2012-03-17T18:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-ct-what-is-the-prescription.236/post-2033>

In a nutshell you need to work your way up to 50-55 degree baths. 30 to 40 minutes in the cold water bath is optimal. Dr. Kruse does up to 3 hours. I read somewhere that 15 minutes at 55 degrees is the equivalent of running for 60 minutes.

It sounds a lot harder than it is. I'm a wimp, and I worked my way up to 20 mins at 55 degrees in about 10 days. The link that @JeffieJoe gave you will explain it a lot better than I did.

### adam_ — 2012-03-17T21:21:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-ct-what-is-the-prescription.236/post-2102>

Ok, I've read the links and I'm now working through the comments, as I think there cold be some gems hidden away in there.

But one question, there's a lot of discussion omega 3 and 6; do I need to increase the 6:3 ratio,,or increase the omega 3 ratio as is more generally supported with paleo?

Thanks

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-18T14:05:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-ct-what-is-the-prescription.236/post-2346>

I totally agree!

# Thread: What LHT do you follow?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-03-17T16:43:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-1992>

So what LHT program do you follow?

PBS fitness? Starting strength?, Body by science? Convict conditioning? Or something completely different?

For the past yr and change I've been doing PB fitness....now I'm transitioning it more toward BbS

What are you doing for your LHT?

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-17T20:22:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2086>

I have been doing a more advanced version of SS, called Texas Method (TM) for 4 wks. I train 3 x week, basically: 1 moderately heavy, 1 very heavy, 1 moderately light. And pull ups. No conditioning, just some walking here and there. I'm a 40 yo female, not a novice and not young, so SS would not be for me.

### SimonM — 2012-03-17T22:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2117>

OK guys, I \*thought\* I was up on this stuff, but can you explain LHT, PB, SS...?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-18T03:46:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2239>

> **SimonM;2123 wrote:** OK guys, I \*thought\* I was up on this stuff, but can you explain LHT, PB, SS...?
>
>
>

LHT = lift heavy things

SS = starting Strenght

BBS = body by science

CC = convict conditioning

The purpose of the above is 3 things , get strong and fit & LGN (look good naked)

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T05:21:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2608>

Gretchen, I started out doing a lot of Jillian Michaels, but now that my focus is on LHT and not HIIT, I'm loosely following the Starting Strengh-schedule/plans, as well as the PBF stuff.

### fabbecky — 2012-03-19T14:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2691>

I have started kettlebell training recently and love it! I am also going to start training for a half marathon. I figure my actual race will be lots of walking and some sprinting.

### Croak — 2012-03-19T17:58:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-2779>

My HT is **me**. You'd have to put a gun to my head to get me lifting weights again, let alone setting foot in a gym. Been there, done that to excess in my youth, don't want any part of that culture these days. This is my journey, and it doesn't require membership fees, lockers, or lycra.

And I know that Grok didn't have kettleballs, barbells, or a row of Nautilus machines.

### hazyjane — 2012-03-21T01:01:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-3609>

I really like kettlebells and I do bodyweight stuff, too, like planks and regular/reverse pushups.

I also love certain isometric moves from Bar Method and certain moves from T-Tapp (not a tap dance workout, lol!). T-Tapp is totally dorky, but Teresa Tapp really knows muscle mechanics and her small moves produce results. She's in fantastic shape.

I like moves that give you the most results in the least amount of time

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-22T19:12:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-4521>

I am looking into kettlebells for a lifting workout. Was a gym rat for years...even had a person trainer for 7 of them. I kinda want to stay away from the gym and maybe just do some things at home. Any suggestions on DVDs or internet programs for how to kettlebell it?

I've heard a lot about T-Tapp too. Since you mentioned it hazyjane which of her DVDs do you recommend?

Thanks!

### MouseBandit — 2019-04-13T23:40:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-268019>

I'd be very interested in what JK has to say about Teresa Tapp and her theories and workout. I'm no fitness expert, but having read a lot of the different fitness books and theories over the years, hers makes, by far, the most sense to me, and it seems to be especially excellent for women. I did HIIT for a long time in my 20s and like Croak, I don't want any part of the gym culture or weight-lifting world (which seems so often to lead to the bodybuilding cult). I think bodyweight exercises and LHT are natural, normal and effective, and I think T-Tapp is both effective and therapeutic in many regards, including electrical flow throughout the body.

### Sun Disciple — 2019-04-14T17:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-lht-do-you-follow.240/post-268043>

> [MouseBandit said:](/goto/post?id=268019)
>
> I'd be very interested in what JK has to say about Teresa Tapp and her theories and workout. I'm no fitness expert, but having read a lot of the different fitness books and theories over the years, hers makes, by far, the most sense to me, and it seems to be especially excellent for women. I did HIIT for a long time in my 20s and like Croak, I don't want any part of the gym culture or weight-lifting world (which seems so often to lead to the bodybuilding cult). I think bodyweight exercises and LHT are natural, normal and effective, and I think T-Tapp is both effective and therapeutic in many regards, including electrical flow throughout the body.

Whatever you do. do it outside. So far I have messed with bodyweight programs (onnit), functional movement like movenat or ido portal, kettlebells (pavel tasouline} Yoga, Barefoot hiking, Hill sprints and now Im messing with the X3 bar outside for heavier resistance training. I can throw it in a duffle bag and take it outside anywhere. Id also like to try gymnastic rings and set them up outside.

# Thread: Hello From Wyoming

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WyoHenMomma — 2012-03-17T17:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-wyoming.241/post-2020>

Good Morning all and Happy St. Patty's Day to ya!

I am in central Wyoming. I have been following Dr. Kruse now for over a year and am a regular blog reader. I have slowly changed my diet from a vlc to a paleo and now have my DH onboard too. It is a work in progress and I find I feel better everyday. Excited to participate in this board. Looking forward to browsing the threads and reading and sharing.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-17T23:34:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2136>

I have some big reservations about this. Fat is prohibited in the protocol because it derails the pathway to the hypothalamus. I'm one of the moderators on the humongous international hcg forum (25,000+ members) and in the 3 1/2 years I've been on there, the ones who tend not to stabilize are the ones who do only a 23 day round or who had fat in their diet.

Now will CT fix that pathway that the fat messes up? I don't know. But you can be our guinea pig  I suspect you would have had this result without the hcg, using only ice though. Because WITH hcg you should have lost 20 pounds at least while doing the CT because Doc says it would step up the losses. So if you'd done the hcg protocol with CT you might have lost 30 pounds in those 4 weeks. Even if you were to the last few pounds. Contrary to what we read in Pounds and Inches you CAN lose more than is healthy because I've seen it several times on the forum.

So Trish I'm not sure it would be worth doing hcg with the CT. I really think the CT alone would do it if you only want reshaping and to lose a few pounds.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-18T02:14:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2189>

I might do a round of hcg with CT to see what it does.  I tend to be a slow loser, about 20 pounds in 40 days. It would be interesting to see if the CT increases the losses. I really don't think you need hcg in your case though since you didn't need to lose much. I think CT did the trick for you and would have done so without the hcg. I suspect the hcg has had nothing to do with how you're reshaping and losing inches. Lucky girl!  The reason I say this is because I've talked to others who have eaten a paleo diet with hcg and didn't lose much. But they didn't know about CT.

I actually love the protocol myself. I've lost 75 pounds and kept it off. I don't mind the restrictive diet. I think it helps a lot of people break out of the grip of certain foods. One little bit of fat added and I either gained or stalled in a round. So do most others which is why Simeon told us how important it was to avoid it. Yet as soon as the diet portion was over, fats are encouraged. So I know fat has an impact. I've seen the evidence on thousands of people too much to disbelieve it. The reason people can regain is grains. Americans love their grains. It's very hard to get people to realize how damaging grains are to our health. And of course wheat is in EVERYTHING.  Time and again we see people complain because they gain 4 pounds on pizza. They want to eat "normally." Well duh. They need to learn that their bodies can't run on junk.

We sure face a battle with educating people!

### caroline — 2012-03-18T03:26:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2228>

Hi Colleen! I have been following you for so long on the HCG forums. You are one busy lady!! I have done some cold showers and a couple of baths and lots of spot icing - twice a day for at least an hour each time. I am having big trouble sleeping now. I do my last spot icing between 8pm and 10pm. I just did one this morning and I won't do another tonite and see if that helps. I don't seem to have much in the way of detoxing like others report except for the sleep issue. I am wide awake for hours when I should be sleeping! The weird thing is I feel so much calmer.

Just a note of interest - I am going to Tibet in May - can't wait to meet a Sherpa and drink yak butter tea! I am so excited.

### Claudia — 2012-03-18T03:37:01+0200

> **caroline;2221 wrote:** That is so me - skinny fat and that elusive last 10 lbs!! I so want the scale to pleeeease go down!

@Caroline and PattyCakes, I am in the same boat as you. I have lost these 10 lbs on hcg last year and I gained it back in the last 7 months, it's hormonal gain with me because I went to paleo/keto on phase 3 when I finished my round. I eat a very clean diet and I gain 2-3 lbs with each TOM and it doesn't come back off again.

I am considering another hcg round with CT. I started CT a week or so ago and got cold urticaria when I added ice to my cold bath. Then, I took about 5 days off to heal from the hives and jumped back in again with face dunks, cold showers, and cold baths. I am up to 20 minutes cold bath at 53 degrees. So far I'm ok but I am staying away from ice for now, too scared of the hives. Tonight I didn't feel up to CT..too tired. I have been super fatigued with CT, which is weird. Even shopping today was tiring, and that is very unlike me.

Anyway given my strange hormonal gains, I am unsure whether to do hcg again, but I am so uncomfortable in this body. My body composition is actually worse now with this new gain than pre-hcg. I can't fit in my clothes, the 10 lbs makes a huge difference on me. I am just puffy.

ChimpChick, can you share with me also where you get your hcg? Perhaps I need a different kind. I did hcg sublingual Rx. Can you share more about how you are combining Leptin with hcg? ### colleencoble — 2012-03-18T03:42:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2236>

Oh my goodness! What a fabulous trip. I can't wait to hear all about it, Caroline! Why are you going to Tibet? Any special reason?

On the hhcg, some of the companies say they just changed what the label says but haven't changed the formula. I don't know if they are telling the truth or not though.

RBee, I really think a lot of the time the trouble people would have with stabilizing was that we all had been taught that eating every 2-3 hours was the thing we should do. Now we know better and I'm seeing that some who have had all kinds of trouble ease into much more stability with going 5 hours or more between meals. But yes if your hhcg wasn't doing what it should, you would have been losing on a starvation diet and it would have messed up your metabolism.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-18T06:02:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2308>

Suzie, some doctors think hcg is damaged by b12 so I don't mix with it. I did for one round but that was it. I mix with bacteriostatic water. You'll have to order that online. All the brands are good. I've used hucog, many like corion. But they are all good.

This trip will be thru very primitive areas in Tibet with very basic lodging and food - so no chance of pigging out on donuts or anything!! I hope I am not too much of a princess for this trip - I have never been camping or anything remotely like that and never wanted to either - so this will be interesting. Apparently there is a 4 or 5 day period where we probably can't get a shower and will be sleeping in dorm type accommodations. My biggest concern is my hair!! a girl has gotta look good right?? We are going to the Mount Everest base camp. I can't wait to see the Sherpas in action!

About the hhcg - I don't think I believe them when they say they haven't change the formula - in fact - I am just remembering - they said they have now added an appetite suppressant. ### caroline — 2012-03-18T12:03:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2329>

I think so many of us had trouble stabilizing after hcg. Dr. K talks about that. Doesn't he say the pathway isn't complete? It isn't our fault - he says we really need CT. I personally have to eat very few calories to loose. I never did well on Atkins either where so many people have - probably mostly men. At least Dr. K says hcg does work whereas so many think it is quackery. I never told anyone I was on it - they would have thought I lost my mind!

I really get being so uncomfortable in your body - that was always me. not even so much how I looked but I felt terrible.

I loved the leptin RX - for the first time in my life I wasn't hungry all the time and no cravings at all. Right from the very start with 50 to 60 grams of protein for breakfast I wouldn't be hungry until dinner. It was like a miracle. I didn't loose weight on the reset tho - I put on a couple of pounds and when I started it I was already up a couple of pounds so I really want to get down about 6 pounds. I feel so much better at that weight and not all puffy. good luck to all of us on this crazy journey. I loved the leptin RX - for the first time in my life I wasn't hungry all the time and no cravings at all. Right from the very start with 50 to 60 grams of protein for breakfast I wouldn't be hungry until dinner. It was like a miracle. I didn't loose weight on the reset tho - I put on a couple of pounds and when I started it I was already up a couple of pounds so I really want to get down about 6 pounds. I feel so much better at that weight and not all puffy. good luck to all of us on this crazy journey. I wish Dr. Kruse would weight in on how his wife did on this protocol - obviously it is such a different journey for women

### JoanneA — 2012-03-19T01:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2508>

There can be reasons for not stabilizing, cheating off protocol, adding things to the protocol (MOP) its actually important to either pick one or the other to do HCG or LRX, not a good idea to mix them, although i will be adding CT to my hcg round only, it has been noted that someone who does not use their tbsp of milk, as well as at least one melba have issues srabilizing with nuts and dairy, i recomend Robin Woodall for the HCGers as she has written a second hypothisis on the hcg protocol, its very important not to mess with tge protocol of even upping the calories as 500/less work in conjunction with your hormone system, there can be problems with your thyroid later, yes HCG can fix thyroid problems but not if one has not been faithful to the protocol, if you want to do paleo with HCG your risking your hormones, as HCG is not a diet, people are fooled by the fact that they are still losing weigh, but truth be known are left wondering why they are not stabilizing or having now other issues, its a sad fact that people only have weightloss information about HCG, but nothing else, in the end i believe these ignorant people may be proponents to speak against hcg......... (weightloss Apocalypse, emotional eating rehab through the HCG Protocol)

### colleencoble — 2012-03-19T04:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2562>

The hcg protocol time is a perfect time to do what is called a food challenge so I don't believe it's harmful to eliminate the milk and breadsticks. Quite the contrary. Quite often the base of our weight problem is a food intolerance that is causing inflammation. When you take out the gluten and the milk for 6 weeks, you can clearly see if they are a problem for you or not. Time and time again I've seen people take those things out and their weight loss immediately is better. That's not because of the calories. It's because the source of inflammation is gone. That's why most of us have trouble losing weight--we keep eating the inflammatory foods.

thanks ChimpChick,

right now i don't think my body can handle another round. i did so much detoxing, my hormones got so out of whack, etc. that am just concentrating on nutrition and getting my biochemistry right. i was so perplexed at how bad i felt after going from 163 to 129 that i knew i had to do some digging. long story short: found out i have been a life-long pyroluric (blood disorder where zinc and B6 are severely depleted) and have hashimotos/hypothyroid, with the hypo probably since my early 20s.

i have been icing my thyroid, but haven't jumped in the tub yet... hope to this week... maybe another HCG round is in my future. i think the people who do best on it have their hormones and minerals in good balance. i hope to be there one day!

hi Claudia,

well neither endos i saw would do the the full thyroid panel or ANY of the hormone tests. the first endo did not even want to treat the hashi's! EPIC FAILURE. i am working with Alexander Haskell who wrote the book Hope for Hashimotos long distance. I got my labs thru him. i found out @ the pyro from reading Nora Gedgaudas' book.... and did my own testing. I am now working with a functional medicine center near me and just sent in a round of stool, urine and blood samples. Also jsut found out i have H. pylori, so that explains why the leaky gut was not getting much better with just paleo and leptin reset.

my LIW was 129, on the reset i got to 138 and stayed there for a while. then i got the 2 diagnoses early this year and i was an emotional mess. i cried for 2 days straight about the hashimotos. now i am 143... when i was really stressed i delved into some GF bread and some other carbs, and it definitely "stuck". I know I should really start CT, but honestly have been chicken. i will do it this week!

i did not put the ice directly on my thyroid, i wrapped the ziplock bag in a thin kitchen towel and tied it behind my neck... good thing i live alone!

if your doc won;t do a full thyroid and hormone panel, i suggest you sign up with Canary Club and do it thru them. Go to Stop the Thyroid Madness website and find out what a full thyroid panel is and what other tests they recommend, like ferritin, etc.

The CT is pretty tough. I am up to 25 minutes in 53 degree cold bath. I am cold for hours after. I had a cup of tea tonight...and then, was worried that it would cancel out the CT I did. ### colleencoble — 2012-03-19T22:43:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2940>

Some over on the hcg forum have been trying the leptin reset idea with their hcg. They've been eating one meal for breakfast then another for dinner instead of no breakfast then lunch and dinner. And they've been doing no fruit and replacing it with protein. Their losses don't tend to be better but I believe they will stabilize better. It's not been long enough to see for sure but it makes sense that the timing would help with the leptin signaling. I think that's why I've been stable. I never ate every 2-3 hours like many of them do. Many break up their food and eat every few hours and I think that's a mistake.

Advantages. Well, hcg gets off the weight FAST. I haven't seen CTing by itself drop a lot of weight quickly, though the weight tends to start dropping slowly if you need to lose. Average weight loss for a woman in 6 weeks with hcg is about 25-28 pounds. I'm a slow loser so I lose 20 pounds a round. If I knew then what I know now, I would have cut out the fruit and upped my losses to at least the average of 25 pounds. But that's a regular hcg round. I'm not sure how fast the losses would be with adding keto to the mix. Traditionally fats derail the pathway to the hypothalamus. Doc thinks the CT will fix that but I don't know whether the losses will be better, the same or worse. And what works for one person doesn't always work for someone else. We've had people on the forum add coconut oil and still lose but they tend to be the younger ones with better metabolisms. And then they often didn't stabilize. Hopefully CT will change that but I don't have enough data to say it will yet.

If your main concern isn't dropping weight fast then adding hcg to the mix isn't going to be helpful anyway. It's not going to aid in any transition. You can order it from overseas pharmacy places. The main one right now is ReliableRX.

### JoanneA — 2012-03-20T01:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-awesomeness.242/post-2995>

Stabilizing is dependant on strict protocol, although Dr.S info is outdated Robin Woodall has more to tell about Leptin and the protocol, the fact that people add in things and still lose or lose more isnt a win situation, because hcg is simply not a diet (i could scream this from the roof top) hcg works in conjunction with the strict protocol as is 500 being max eating along with hunger (leptin) even under 500 cals for most. Following strict protocol is not relative at all, its the same for each patient unless theres allergies to some of the foods. You simply dont treat hcg as a diet and do what works for you unless your at the end of losing all your weight and your leptin is signaling hunger more often, which happens, if you want a good diet get off hcg and switch, if you want hcg then there are rules that apply. I did a 36 day round losing 28 pounds and 23 inches, i went into maintenance (the fats phase) and ate all kinds of fatty foods and stabilized well no gain! I did not cheat and followed through, im on my second round day 5 feel great and im down 7 lbs already, hcg is temporary treatment, it helps far beyond our shallow ideas of what appearance is about and should be combined with emo eating rehab as well as how to live later, correcting hormones with Hcg works, and loss of fat is a great benifit!!!!

# Thread: Biotoxin Illness Thread
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 71
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2012-03-17T18:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-2045>

Since 24% of the population has a genetic susceptibility to react to biotoxins - Lyme, mold, certain algae - I suspect there are quite a number of folks here who can share what to do and how they're doing.

From one of the leaders in this field, Dr Ritchie Shoemaker:

"It all starts when a person is exposed to a biotoxin. In most people, the biotoxin is 'tagged' and identified by the body's immune system and is broken down and removed from the blood by the liver. However, some individuals do not have the immune response genes (HLA-DR genes) that are required to eventually form an antibody to a given foreign antigen. In these cases the biotoxins are not 'tagged' and remain in the body indefinitely, free to circulate and wreak havoc.

Once present in the body, the biotoxins begin to set off a complex cascade of biochemical events. The biotoxin binds to surface receptors (Toll receptors and many more) in nearly every kind of cell in the body. This recognition and binding of the biotoxin causes a continual upregulation of multiple inflammatory pathways, including production of cytokines, split product of complement, and TGF Beta-1. Biotoxins also directly affect nerve cell function, which is one of the reasons that the symptoms and visual contrast sensitivity (VCS) test are so useful in diagnosis.

[http://www.survivingmold.com/](http://http://www.survivingmold.com/)

### Valorie33 — 2012-03-17T23:32:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-2134>

This is scary stuff !!

I have been working in a building with mold issues for at least 2 years, recently a new source is about 20 ft from my office space. MY HS-CRP is 6.9 now, and I was diagnosed with Fibromyalgia a couple years ago. I am just sitting here trying to tie in the dates of the first leak and when I got so very sick.

### Martin — 2012-03-18T13:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-2343>

Mine is 3.2.

Dr Kruse says he gets antzy when it gets above 3.

Doing HS CRP was an inexpensive ($40) wakeup call that showed me I had inflammation.

I have to do some biotoxin clearing with cholestyramine before CT because the release of toxins from the fat would make me feel much worse than I already do. I found a great doc in Atlanta who follows Shoemaker and appointment is in 3 weeks!

Martin

> About the Marshall Protocol
>
>
>
> The Marshall Protocol is a medical treatment being used by physicians worldwide to treat a variety of chronic inflammatory and autoimmune diseases including (but not limited to) sarcoidosis, Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, fibromyalgia, Crohn’s Disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. While other treatments for chronic disease use palliative medications in an effort to cover up symptoms, the Marshall Protocol is a curative treatment, which strives to address the root cause of the disease process.
>
>
>
> Information about the treatment can be found at the study site, marshallprotocol.com and also at autoimmunityresearch.org. The site is run by the staff of the Autoimmunity Research Foundation, a California-based non-profit agency. Over 200 health professionals are members of the site, and discussions are moderated by volunteer moderators. There is no charge to use the website or the treatment and all patients are welcome to participate.
>
>
>
> **The FDA has already granted orphan product designations for two of Autoimmunity Research Foundation’s six applications – Sarcoidosis, and Post Treatment Lyme Disease Syndrome (PTLDS, commonly known as chronic Lyme). The Foundation continues to work with the FDA to make effective therapies available in an even wider array of chronic diagnoses.**

No endurance, libdo, hands and feet hurt constantly, severe cramps in legs and feet if I stretch, low blood pressure, and some other things I can't remember... oh yeah, lousy memory.

I have not improved much since going on cholestyramine. That is the main drug for biotoxin exposure. I have had some good half-days here and there in the last three years, but something changed radically when I got sick those three years ago. I _felt_ it.

My consultation with Dr Kruse was a breakthru in perhaps finding an underlying condition besides mold exposure. Even though I have tested positive for a mold-susceptible gene and low aMSH, I have not seen much improvement and have wondered if something else is amiss. I did not have a clue what to test but Dr. Kruse has such a brilliant diagnosing skills, he was able to tell me what work-up to get. I should know in a few weeks if I am a brain-damaged guy. Can't wait.

I listened to Dr Mercola's great interview with Dr Richie Shoemaker on the subject of Biotoxin Illness and heard this about sterol use:

**DM: Do you think that part of the reason that they have this adverse reaction to that (biotoxin) exposure is the impairment of the adrenal system?

RS: No. We see dysregulation of adrenals being under control of MSH. And as inflammation is corrected, this adrenal ACTH cortisol ratio will start to improve. We can see low ACTH-low cortisol, high ACTH-high cortisol, and in any kind of parameter in between, but itâ€™s subject â€“ like all the known cortin hormones â€“ to MSH regulation. If you think you have adrenal fatigue, if you think you have adrenal problem, check MSH before you use any kind of sterol. If you suppress ACTH, you actually can take away the last anti-inflammatory pathway these patients have.

I am thinking that if MSH is low, steroids should be avoided?

I'm also considering mega-dosing omega 3 in the form of krill oil to help my low 37 (31-86 pg/ml) VEGF**. My 03/06 ratio is 4:1 so I'm not sure if this is not warranted, but it seems like a safe shot at getting some relief.

RS: "As we go forward in this kind of pyramid of treatment, we fix hormonal problems and autoimmune problems very early on. Then we want to increase oxygen-delivering capillary beds. Hereâ€™s where VEGF comes in. Vascular endothelial growth factor has abnormal distribution in these patients. Some itâ€™s quite high; we worry about cancer there. Most people, itâ€™s quite low. Itâ€™s actually protecting against cancer in some peopleâ€™s opinions. But low VEGF is not letting adequate oxygen be delivered in capillary beds.

We need to drive up VEGF to help people with brain fog, shortness of breath, and any cognitive issues as well. We can do that using either the omega-3s in very high doses, or a drug called Pioglitazone or Actos. We canâ€™t use quite as much now that when we used to because of the question about bladder problems and all, but Actos is a good one to drive up VEGF."**

### Martin — 2012-09-10T23:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-50807>

Hi elibby1878, thanks for posting here. I found this at [http://www.chronicneurotoxins.com/](http://http://www.chronicneurotoxins.com/)

"Upon completing the VCS test, viewers receive a message indicating that biotoxins are (positive) or are not (negative) likely to be involved in their illness. The criteria for getting a "positive" VCS result is set high to avoid false positive results. This occasionally results in a false negative result; some cases of chronic-biotoxin induced illness may pass the VCS test a some times. VCS can be measured during treatment to monitor recovery."

I am under the care of a mold doc so I have not bothered to learn to interpret the numbers in the score, just whether it was positive or negative. Like you, I'd be willing to guess with a higher overall score you are becoming more loaded up and it's showing in the numbers. Do you have a doc in mind? What part of the country are you in? I can possibly hook you up with a good doc.

Check out Mycometrics, Dr Shoemaker's choice for mold testing. Pricey, but it is the best. Your health is worth it!

Have you had gene haplotype tested? A first choice for anyone wondering if they have a mold/Lyme/biotoxin illness going on. Remember, 25% of the population is susceptible, so stop wondering and get the HLA DRB DRBQ test from Labcorp # 012542. I got mine for $260 at a local lab that does self-pay for a reduced rate. Busted my case wide open! I never looked back from there as I had a place to start moving on getting well.

### Martin — 2012-09-14T14:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-51621>

HLA DRB DRBQ test from Labcorp # 012542 is the one you want. There are many HLA tests and don't make the mistake that I made and order just the HLA DR test! It is NOT the one you need! I wasted $260 of self-payin' cash on that error!

You can get decent info about other aspects of your health picture by those other tests your doc ordered. Search around this site and pick up info on how to interpret the results. Many may be normal but you still feel lousy. Common with biotoxin illness folks. What is goofing up with us is shown by very specific tests that Dr Shoemaker has researched.

The HLA DRB DRBQ test also shows if you are genetically susceptible to Lyme. I'm not sure what Lyme test your doc ordered, but often they are inconclusive, and worse - misleading.

Keep on it, and be your own advocate. Keep us posted.

### Martin — 2012-09-27T18:07:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-54753>

Thanks, glad to contribute here in the best way I can.

Cleaning can be the toughest part of this whole deal. I just got this from my mold doc: (emphasis mine)

**From one doc to mine:

"I have a patient who did ERMI testing on her office which was positive. Her

\> landlords consultants insist that air sampling is the "gold standard" and

\> plans to test with that. The landlord was surprised that she could have a

\> genetic uniqueness to mold. She was a 4,3,53 & 15,6, 51. Anyone have

\> references to the superiority of ERMI to air sampling, and the percentages

\> of the HLA's."

**My doc's reply:**

"I have faced a similar situation with my patients and it's difficult to overcome.

The main argument is that for every spore there are about 320 spore fragments. 80% of the inflammagen load of mold are located in the fragments**. Fragments can not be seen by light microscopy which is how air testing works. The ERMI is the only test which can detect these fragments.

Another study showed that one needs to run 3-9 air tests per room to truly rule out mold amplification.

Another study did split sample air testing. The air traps were sent to 9 different labs and they got 9 different results.

The best article may be the 4th one located [https://www.survivingmold.com/diagnosis/ermi-testing/articles](http://https://www.survivingmold.com/diagnosis/ermi-testing/articles)

An article by Greg Weatherman can be found at [https://www.survivingmold.com/legal-resources/environmental/whats-missing](http://https://www.survivingmold.com/legal-resources/environmental/whats-missing)

Starting on page 21 of the Policyholders of America consensus statement, Dr. Jack Thrasher talks about the different methodologies at [https://www.survivingmold.com/legal-resources/publications](http://https://www.survivingmold.com/legal-resources/publications)

My notes from one of Shoemaker's DVDs says that 4-3-53 makes up 1% of the population. The relative risk of a mold illness with 4-3-53 is 4 times higher than a non-susceptible haplotype. The relative risk data can be found at [https://www.survivingmold.com/legal-resources/library](http://https://www.survivingmold.com/legal-resources/library) Go to the 5th file, slide 41."

\>

### Martin — 2013-05-17T15:27:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-86305>

My mold doc, Dr Dave Ou, posted this on his blog:

**The genetics of Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome from biotoxins**

In the vast majority of my patients, I check a genetic test called an HLA DR/DQ from Labcorp (test #012542) I learned about this test from Dr. Ritchie Shoemaker. This gene is located on chromosome number 6 and it encodes a protein called an MHC Class II protein. Each person has two copies: one from the father and one from the mother. This test tells me if a person is susceptible to biotoxin illnesses and I’ve found that 98% of my patients are.

To give some background on this gene, when the immune system first encounters a foreign object such as a bacteria, the first immune cells to arrive on the scene are called APCs (Antigen Presenting Cells). The APC “eats” the foreign object then attaches it to a MHC Class II protein. It’s like taking a piece of the bacteria and and putting it in a picture frame. The APC then moves the foreign object and the MHC protein (the picture frame) to the surface of the cell so that the rest of the immune system (specifically T- cells) can study and learn how to defend against the foreign object. The APC essentially tells the rest of the immune system, “Look what I found! I put it in a picture frame!”

As an analogy, imagine that a crime was committed. The first police officer on the scene was able to take a picture of the criminal before he ran away. The policeman then shows a picture of the suspect to the rest of the police force. The MHC II protein does the same thing as the first police officer: to show a picture of the suspect to the rest of the immune system.

What if the police officer’s camera didn’t work well or he had trouble developing the picture? He wouldn’t be able to show the picture to the rest of the police department so they wouldn’t know who to look for. The criminal is able to run around the city to commit more crimes. This is what happens in Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome. The MHC II protein is unable to show a picture of the foreign invader to the rest of the immune system.

Ritchie Shoemaker, MD, has identified 10 types of MHC II proteins that are unable to show small biotoxins to the rest of the immune system. Biotoxins are poisons released by a variety of organisms. Examples include snake venom, bee venom, and jellyfish stingers. Bacteria and mold also make biotoxins. Some of the smallest biotoxins are those made from indoor mold and Lyme Disease. If one of these 10 types of MHC II proteins run into the small biotoxins from indoor mold or Lyme, they can’t “see” the biotoxins so the biotoxins are able to run around the body causing damage. There are about 50 other types of MHC II proteins that are able to recognize the small biotoxins from mold or Lyme so people with these don’t get biotoxin illneses very easily.

A list of the MHC II proteins / HLA DR/DQ that can’t recognize mold or Lyme biotoxins can be found at [https://www.survivingmold.com/diagnosis/lab-tests](https://www.survivingmold.com/diagnosis/lab-tests). A more complete list can be found in his book Surviving Mold

4-3-53: susceptible to both biotoxins from mold and Lyme

11/12-3-52B: susceptible to both biotoxins from mold and Lyme

14-5-52B: susceptible to both biotoxins from mold and Lyme

7-2-53: susceptible to biotoxins from mold

13-6-52A, B, or C: susceptible to biotoxins from mold

17-2-52A: susceptible to biotoxins from mold

18-4-52A: susceptible to biotoxins from mold

15-6-51: susceptible to biotoxins from Lyme

16-5-51: susceptible to biotoxins from Lyme

1-5: susceptible to low MSH which makes one susceptible to biotoxins from mold and Lyme

About 25% of the world’s population have one of the 10 susceptible types of HLA DR/DQ gene. As of Nov 27, 2012 in my practice, I have tested 227 patients. Out of those 227 patients, 222 have one of the 10 types. That’s an astounding 98% of all of my patients who are vulnerable to the biotoxins from indoor mold and Lyme! Another way of putting it is nearly all of my patients come from only 25% of the population. I have spoken to my colleagues across the country and they’ve had very similar findings to mine. I sometimes joke that the HLA DR/DQ gene predicts if someone will get frustrated with conventional medicine and seek out alternative care.

I’ve written about some of the symptoms and diseases that are associated with these genes in my previous post. Unfortunately, there are only several hundred doctors in the world who are aware of this gene. Therefore, the vast majority of doctors know nothing about this gene so that countless patients are not diagnosed properly. I suspect there has been an increase in the number of doctors who are aware of this gene as a result of a talk given at this year’s ILADS conference and hopefully this important knowledge will continue to grow.
