### Martin — 2013-12-07T18:00:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-102726>

Here's an interesting article I ran across while searching for the repair of hypothalamus receptors:

[http://invigorating-acupuncture.blogspot.com/2012/04/review-of-works-of-dr-ritchie-shoemaker.html](http://invigorating-acupuncture.blogspot.com/2012/04/review-of-works-of-dr-ritchie-shoemaker.html)

Here are some excerpts with my thoughts and notes **in bold**:

"If cytokines enter the capillaries, they will invite White Blood Cells into the area. As the capillaries are small blood vessels, they can only support so much traffic and the area will soon be clogged **(and here come those horrible leg cramps when you stretch in the middle of the night).** Cytokines regulate Hypoxia Inducible Factors (HIF) \[20\] which will result in constricted blood flow and a lack of oxygen to the cells, otherwise known as capillary hypoperfusion.\* HIF stimulates **TGFβ-1 (the test for this is what my mold doc uses to monitor progress while he treats biotin illness because over 90% of his patients test high)**, which furthers the inflammation cycle and alters the cell structure, and, Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor (VEGF)."

"By signaling the HIF, cytokines are preventing oxygen from getting to the cells. If the cells do not have enough oxygen then only 5% of their available energy, in the form of glycogen, can be used.\* The rest of the glycogen gets burned up in the form of lactic acid, hence the muscle cramps\[23\]."

"The body can overcome these limitations by burning fat for energy, however once the fat source runs out, and the people with the 6 haplotypes do have some genetic predispositions to be limber (many of these individuals according to Dr. Shoemaker have hyper mobile joints\[24\]) and athletic, then the body will find another fuel source to burn. Since these individuals may not have a lot of stored fat to begin with, or simply run out of fat to burn, the body will start to burn the protein of the lean muscle mass. Leptin is released after the fat stores are depleted **(I believe this should read, "_while_ the fat storesâ€¦)** and the body is signaled to start holding on to fatty acids. Once the body starts to cannibalize itself and eat the lean muscle, it figures, **for the time being**, it will pay the price because the problem will resolve and then the normal stores of energy will be replenished.

This restoration never occurs in a CIRS individual, because the original problem of the foreign antigens was not resolved which means all the other processes are still in play. It takes a minimum of two days of rest for the body to rebuild its glycogen stores\[25\] and most people with CFS, as soon as they get energy, burn it and do not pace themselves **(_hello?!_ Martin, are you effing _listening?!_).** At the same time the body is gaining more fatty acids which become fat weight, and the Leptin release signal never shuts off\[26\]."

"Moving on, this release of Leptin will act on the hypothalamus\[27\]. According to Shoemaker, the increase of cytokines will eventually damage the Leptin receptors on the hypothalamus, which will inhibit the binding ability of Leptin. Dr. Shoemaker amended this statement in IAQ radio episode 136 to state that the receptors for Leptin are not the only receptors which are damaged by this process.\* Since this binding does not take place, the hypothalamus does not produce enough MSH to regulate the inflammatory agents present in the body. Even if Shoemaker is incorrect on this statement in regards to the cytokine effect on Leptin receptors, which I suspect he is not, the demand from Leptin for more MSH would eventually lead to reduced MSH if the antigens were not removed, simply because the MSH would be tapped out." **(time to CT!)**

"From my own clinical experience and what I have seen and heard working with Dr. Rea, I believe that Dr. Shoemaker has potentially come across an important piece of the puzzle. While I am not sold on all that he says about Leptin, particularly trying to link it to obesity in CFS or EI/CS patients (as most of the ones I have seen have been, if anything, underweight) **(Author is not aware of Leptin Resistance in thin people - think Body Composition!)** I do believe that he makes a good case for himself. The lack of oxygen to the cells would cause the symptoms described and supports Dr. Reaâ€™s use of oxygen therapy, and potentially supports the use of Tai Chi, and Qigong breathing exercises among other disciplines. Leptin, while it may not result in weight gain in the patients described, may be the key to a number of other symptoms and the reason why some of Dr. Reaâ€™s overweight patients, and others, have a hard time loosing weight. Obesity is such a multifaceted issue that assuming there is a singular answer is, at this point, suspect at best **(Now we're talking)**. It is also possible that his more recent discovery of more receptors being damaged could be the cause of some of the symptoms."

**So, can the receptors be repaired? Does the Leptin Reset accomplish this damage that are due to cytokines?**

From the good Doc,:
"@Dan **At this point my speculation is that we can retrain the hypothalamus**. I feel I have done this to myself and many of my current patients but no one else is doing this. I have contacted Amgen about this angle and their synthetic leptin trial data but my dealings with them lead me to believe they have other plans for their synthetic leptin. I have spoken to several people in SD who were in their trial and they all had their synthetic leptin discontinued and they opted for belt lipectomies and liposuction to reduce their adipocyte numbers to control their weight. Most plastic surgeons wont do the surgery for this indication because they dont believe it is a weight loss strategy, but most plastic surgeons also think the number of fat cells we have is fixed at birth which is also old school CW. This is a research topic that probably will never get studied until synthetic leptin is FDA approved which I dont see happening anytime soon."

"When someone is not responsive to the receptor signaling of leptin in the hypothalamus, they become unable to burn their excess energy off as pure heat so they remain overweight. The reason is simple. They do not have any excess energy to burn as you will find out. They find it very difficult to lose the weight, even when they restrict calories to starvation levels. This will change when they become leptin sensitive once again. Some obese people appear to do tremendous damage to their leptin receptors and need synthetic leptin to continue to lose or maintain their weight loss. We realized this in the results of Amgen leptin trials data. Moreover, some of these people have found that removing adipocytes surgically also helps them maintain their weight better as well. Long term studies need to be done on these people to see if surgical removal of fat is really necessary for long term weight control, or can long term neuroplastic training be done while the hypothalamus undergoes neuroplastic repair over time. In my opinion, I think if we use timing of meals and couple it to the day light cycles as they adjust daily, we can facilitate that neuroplasticity. This is really the science behind the Leptin Rx. I have read extensively about the work of neurosurgeon and neuroscientists Penfield, Paul Bach y Rita, and Merzenich and their experiments have proved that the brain is very plastic, and can be retaught how to work optimally again if we give it a â€œnew wayâ€ to perceive a stimulus once damage to another part of the brain has occurred.

"We can achieve leptin sensitivity after the hypothalamus is damaged, if we teach the brain how to use our older evolutionary non-leptin neural circuits. Yoking eating to light and day, and making timing to sleep and wakefulness and to the visceral sensations of distention of the gut via the vagus nerve are precisely how the Leptin Rx is designed to work. Taking full advantage of how certain macro-nutirents are tied to light cycle further helps reset hypothalamus in two to three months. The more inflammation that is present, the longer the reset may take. Instead of relying on optimal functioning of the hypothalamic leptin receptor, we can re-teach the hypothalamus to pay attention to the light levels, awakening time, time we sleep and face dark, when our gut is distended and filled with food and when it is not. We can also load the diet with protein and fat at certain times, to take full advantage of the existing working neural circuits in the brainstem and hypothalamus that accounts for carbohydrates, to our advantage in resetting how the brain can more fully perceive our energy status when the leptin receptor is not functioning."

**Hell, yeah.**

Big

I got sulfate test strips from Quantofix in USA, but they are manufactured in Germany by Macherey-Nagel. Urinate on a strip, wait two minutes, compare with chart on container. Consistency helps establish accuracy - do it first thing in morning. Sulfur foods include eggs, broccoli, cabbage, pork, various amounts. After a few months of avoiding sulfurous foods (all while continuing to follow light, emf avoidance, and mindfulness,etc.,) the level went down to around 400 from a consistent high of 1,600. So I went back to eating sulphur foods and now I occasionally test and the levels have remained around 400. Yay! I loves me some pork and broccoli.

### prime1 — 2016-10-26T01:06:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biotoxin-illness-thread.243/post-203316>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=2045)
>
> Since 24% of the population has a genetic susceptibility to react to biotoxins - Lyme, mold, certain algae - I suspect there are quite a number of folks here who can share what to do and how they're doing.
>
>
>
> From one of the leaders in this field, Dr Ritchie Shoemaker:
>
>
>
> "It all starts when a person is exposed to a biotoxin. In most people, the biotoxin is 'tagged' and identified by the body's immune system and is broken down and removed from the blood by the liver. However, some individuals do not have the immune response genes (HLA-DR genes) that are required to eventually form an antibody to a given foreign antigen. In these cases the biotoxins are not 'tagged' and remain in the body indefinitely, free to circulate and wreak havoc.
>
>
>
> Once present in the body, the biotoxins begin to set off a complex cascade of biochemical events. The biotoxin binds to surface receptors (Toll receptors and many more) in nearly every kind of cell in the body. This recognition and binding of the biotoxin causes a continual upregulation of multiple inflammatory pathways, including production of cytokines, split product of complement, and TGF Beta-1. Biotoxins also directly affect nerve cell function, which is one of the reasons that the symptoms and visual contrast sensitivity (VCS) test are so useful in diagnosis.
>
>
>
> [http://www.survivingmold.com/](http://http://www.survivingmold.com/)

Sunlight may have an ability to kill mold - however mold isn't the problem. What it produces is the problem - called Mycotoxins. Mycotoxins are resistant to UV light. Can't radicate the mycotoxins with sunlight. I"m going to dig deeper into this

.

### Bee — 2012-03-17T19:23:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2063>

Anyone know what Dr. K thinks of skin brushing to stimulate the lymph system and get rid of toxins? Is it okay? If not, anyone know why?

### Garfield — 2012-03-17T19:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2068>

The only thing I have seen is that Dr. K told someone else **not** to brush their skin before CT.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-18T02:16:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2190>

I've been brushing my skin 2-3 times weekly before normal showers since July.

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-18T03:51:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2243>

Yes, I heard dr k say that you should not do skin brushing before a ct session. I have been skin brushing since July, before showers, and it has done amazing things for my skin! Less dry, nice and smooth, no need to rub much coconut oil on it anymore.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-18T03:52:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2244>

I've brushed off and on over the past 6 yrs. but I love it when I get to go to the Korean day spa b/c for $80 I get a full body scrub down, and full body massage which is approximately 3hrs, and use of the facilities, pools/ showers, tatami mat rooms. Pure bliss

### JC2K — 2012-03-18T04:26:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2279>

I thought it was AFTER CT that Dr K was talking about. Which is more understandable than before. Essentially, it's a bad idea to vigorously rub down the skin after cold swims/hypothermic conditions because of "after drop". What happens is the cold blood from your extremities goes to your core and causes your core temp to drop further. A bad idea, because we don't really want the core temperature to drop a lot, just the skin temperature.

### Mom — 2012-03-18T05:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2306>

I had started skin brushing a week or two before trying CT...to try and firm up skin after losing 40lbs. on HHCG. But I have red dots all over my skin now...some look like acne, some are just red spots. I assume it's my skin detoxing, but not sure. I did dry brush before CT bath last night and now I have more spots. If its from releasing toxins, that's good news. But I'm not sure if it's that. Has anyone else had breakouts from dry brushing?

JC2K, I hope you are right and it's alright to do it before CT. I stopped brushing when I started CT, but then thought it made sense to combine it, and I would like to continue both.

### JC2K — 2012-03-19T17:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2751>

Hrm, did find where Dr K was answering a question on it, and it was in re. prior to CT. Not sure on the effect of it beforehand. Maybe getting extra circulation into the skin and then getting it cold causes problems?

I don't think there's any problem with you doing skin brushing, just don't do it right before or after the CT.

### Mom — 2012-03-19T23:26:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-brushing.245/post-2953>

> **JC2K;2759 wrote:** Hrm, did find where Dr K was answering a question on it, and it was in re. prior to CT. Not sure on the effect of it beforehand. Maybe getting extra circulation into the skin and then getting it cold causes problems?
>
>
>
> I don't think there's any problem with you doing skin brushing, just don't do it right before or after the CT.

OK, good to know. I am curious why though. I will keep brushing seperate from CT from now on.

# Thread: Is this frostbite??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-03-17T20:01:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2079>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-17T21:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2107>

> **Souldanzer;2085 wrote:** My left leg fell asleep twice during CT without me noticing (I was working on my laptop in a funny position, reaching out of the tub  ).
>
>
>
> Now I have an area next to my shin that is slightly red and rather painful.
>
>
>
> I'm thinking if your leg falls asleep there's reduced blood flow and so this could happen even if otherwise you would've been just fine? I've been CT for a few weeks now and this has never happened before.

Hi Souldanzer, I had this happen to the outside of my thigh early on. When I asked Dr K his question was 'was your leg up against the side of the tub?" It sounds like yours was? I think the cold plus the extra pressure could created lower than needed skin temps and therefore frostbite. Mine was pressed against an ice pack simultaneously (I didn't notice either until after I got out.)

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-17T22:05:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2114>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-18T01:00:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2166>

> **Souldanzer;2120 wrote:** can't quite remember... other than that I was leaning over so this could have totally happened.
>
> I kept the area out of the water for now when I CT. I did add ice blocks last night
>
> Gotta be more careful. Thanks, now I know what to watch for.... the falling asleep thing probably didn't help the matter, either...

I also have a strong feeling that the ice packs you put in coolers get colder than normal ice and also warm slower. I have found that while I e next to my skin starts to melt immediately, the ice packs do not. I think they are a danger for frostbite when compressed. But I can't be sure (though I'm playing it safe from now on...)

### MonteD — 2012-03-18T01:41:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-frostbite.246/post-2178>

> **Souldanzer;2085 wrote:** My left leg fell asleep twice during CT without me noticing (I was working on my laptop in a funny position, reaching out of the tub  ).
>
>
>
> Now I have an area next to my shin that is slightly red and rather painful.
>
>
>
> I'm thinking if your leg falls asleep there's reduced blood flow and so this could happen even if otherwise you would've been just fine? I've been CT for a few weeks now and this has never happened before.

Personally I think that ice packs directly against bare skin for prolonged periods are a bit dangerous. Frostbite begins to happen at around 32 degrees. There isn't much of a margin for error at this point. However, what your describing sounds less like frostbite and more like frostnip.

# Thread: Hello from LI, NY

### gmel1084 — 2012-03-17T21:50:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-li-ny.247/post-2111>

Hello everyone,

Im relatively new to the subject of hormone optimization but am eager to learn.

Came across this site yesterday.

I have have been athletic my whole life, but despite being very active (I'm on my feet all day work, I have been training intensely in martial arts for years, and also do CrossFit up to 3x/ week), I just can't get to the point with my body where I'm very lean. Coming across the topic of hormone optimization on Mike Mahler's website, I began to wonder if it is Leptin/ Cortisol related. Upon more research, it definitely seems to be the case. With my activity level, I wouldn't be surprised if I'm experiencing adrenal fatigue, HIGH CORTISOL, and LR, IR.

My diet for the most part is good, but I definitely cheat a lot. The last body fat test I got said I was around 13%, but I store most of it in my lower stomach and love handles. For a very active person for years this is frustrating.

I'm 27 years old, 5'7, and 155lbs. Obviously not a bad thing but I'm intrigued by hormone optimization and its role in the fitness industry.

After coming across Dr. Kruse's Leptin RX info I knew I was on the right track.

I try to space out my meals as much as I can, but with the amount of workout volume I do, its hard when you want to optimize your post workout recovery with shakes, and make sure that you're getting enough calories.

Any help is appreciated!

Greg

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-03-17T22:00:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-2113>

Hi, I've been doing a variation of low carb off and on over the last several years. have done two rounds of HCG over the last year. discovered this website in Jan 2012. It was just in time to end my last HCG session. I went on the ketogenic paleo per Dr. K and am pleased to report that I've kept the weight off and even lost a little more.

Here is how I ended my latest HCG session. About a week before the end I increased my protein by 4 oz daily. Then because I have had some digestive difficulties I slowly increased the fat portion 10 gms or so every several days depending on how digestion was going. I'm currently up to over 100 gms per day. I kept the carb portions the same and eventually decreased it a little. My weight has stayed stable and I even lost 3 more lbs.

I have had problems in the past with eating too little, slowing the metabolism and moderate weight gain. So I have been keeping track of my macronutrients and have set a minimum calories that I meet. Not everyone, of course, has to do this. this is just my own physical issues I must deal with.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-03-21T00:07:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-3565>

Wanted to continue my post. The HCG diet is a very difficult diet to do and complete. The ads would have you believe that you only have to take the drops and 'the lbs melt off.' Sixty years ago only rich people did this diet. They traveled to Italy, got injected, ate the meals served to them, did site seeing around town or napped in their rooms. Someone prepared and served their meals, cleaned their rooms and at home the servants took care of the home and family. Contrast that with today, cooking maybe two different meals, cleaning up after ones self along with the drastic drop in energy as the lbs 'melt off.' I can't even imagine trying to work at a job doing this. Mind you the diet wants you to do this for as long as 6 weeks. So if you are going to do it be prepared and make sure you do not gain the weight back after. Go right to the paleo ketogenic per Dr.K. I don't think the homeopathic drops give the same results. I used them first and the real thing the second time. That time i saw the weight come off the targeted areas. It was very obvious.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-04-03T06:35:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-10685>

Here are some thoughts on altitude adjustment. Dr K made a comment on the sherpas and their CT. I realized they also had to adjust to the altitude. Then I realized I was about 20 minute drive from a 10,000 foot mountain easily accessible by tram. I live at 5200 feet here in NM. I took a first trip up paying the full price of $17, yikes. Then I noticed a seasons pass which brought the price down to $2 a trip if I went up twice a week. Much better. I've lived in NM for 15 years and never really adjusted to the altitude. I's still hard to do even a fast walk for any length of time. So I've never been able to enjoy the physical fitness aspects of this area and in fact have developed a negative attitude where NM is involved. That first trip was exhilarating. The trees, the air, watching people skiing and snowboarding was wonderful. Lots of snow there and I was able to do a CT in a snowbank! I was weak, dizzy and got a headache but it was glorious up there. Even though I was eating what I thought was enough food twice a day I got hungry and had a burger, chips and salsa down below.

The second trip up I got so hungry I could have eaten the bark off the trees so I ate chips and cheese dip up on top. I sat and read my book for a half hour after that. I got up went out side the restaurant walked about 20 steps and almost passed out. Luckily I knew to lower my head and didn't embarrass myself.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-04-03T06:50:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-10688>

My third trip up the mountain I decided to take my pulse. I strolled slowly about, enjoyed the scenery and after half an hour my pulse was 125. A half hour after that still walking about I took my pulse again and it was 100. After one hour I rested up there,took my pulse again and it was 78. interesting workout just by strolling about.

I read about the benefits of altitude. One is it increases your metabolism, it increases red blood cells, it improves the oxygenation of ones tissues. At spas in Europe and on expensive cruises, $10,000 per, they have these little machines that mimic the oxygen level at 10,000 feet. You can get a 60 to 90 minute treatment. So that is why I have my sessions at this same time span. The major difference is that when one is actually up on the 10,000 foot level the oxygen pressure is felt on the whole body. The skin does some breathing as well. The machine treatments don't have that. They even recommend this treatment for elite athletes.

It's amusing to me to see the bike riders racing about like mad around the tram parking lot and back out on the road. They don't know they could include a 90 minute treatment at 10,000 feet. Now I know some of them do go up the back road all the way to the top, but they don't stay long enough to take advantage of the treatment.

Thanks for the interest. I'm being sidetracked with a contract job for a few weeks. They shut down the tram for 2 weeks for maintenance and I've been traveling heavily since then so I have only been up the mtn once since april 20 12. I noticed I kept my conditioning and used my breath technique to keep the o2 levels up. I got an oximeter. You can by them at Walgreens of all places. With all the traveling I haven't been able to do the CT or mtn visits. So I have been thinking about what is optimal for me. The idea of 6 pack abs leaves me cold (pun intended). Since I turned 70 my interests have changed. I'm still contemplating and will complete my thoughts another time. it's true though there is a spa in my back yard and I intend to make use of it.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-04T12:22:02+0300

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-09T04:33:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-22457>

Aah, but Jack you don't have the 110 mile view, the air, the mountain wind rustling through the pines with a machine! lol On a serious note I think there is something to be said for the overall effect of the difference in pressure on the entire body. But I agree the results seem to be very good. I almost immediately noticed an increase in my energy level when I came down to 5200 feet from the 10.378. I've been up a total of 9 times since march 14,2012. All I've done is stroll around, sit and breath, talk with the tourists, and find a secluded spot to practice my singing. yet when I'm back at my home level I'm doing several activities per day and any rest periods I do take actually feel restful.

Visit 9 was May 5 Sat am. After a BAB heavy on the fat I went up via the tram. This time I wore my hiking boots and took my hiking stick. I spent my first hour as usual moving. Thirty minutes sitting and chatting. This time I tried taking both north and south trails at the top part way to find out if they were passable yet. My goal at the end of the summer is to hike the trail from 6500 feet to the top. I hear it is a 4 hour hike. Right now I am very happy that I don't get nauseated and overly dizzy anymore. In addition, right now I want to stay at 10,378 feet as much as possible. When I felt like I might be getting dizzy I used my breath technique two or three times. I felt no hunger and drank my 3 cups of water. It is very dry up there. somewhere around 16% humidity I would guess. At least that is what my gauge said outside my apt. Til next time.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-09T05:52:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-22481>

A couple years ago we flew from almost sea level in Texas to Denver, and then drove up Pike's Peak that same day. LOL! I walked a little bit at the summit, then found I had to stay very still to keep from passing out! I recovered, but had to move slowly! That was before low carb/paleo/leptin reset/CT. I wonder how I would react now, since my asthma is gone, and my pulse rate is lower.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-09T06:38:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-22491>

Hi Nonchalant, i think there is a certain amount of adaptation that must be undergone by everyone. Some don't take long to adapt and some take quite a while. my son was almost hospitalized when he was a teen and visited relatives at altitude. I think 8,000 feet. he was trying to bike ride I think. A friend of mine in her 60s at the time was happily climbing around Durango Co within a day or two at 7500 feet. I, who was 10 years her junior and had been living there for a year already was still struggling. There really isn't any research that I can find that measures anything for those of us who are untrained and older. I did find the Army manual for altitude and CT. Can we develop more RBCs? Can our blood O2 levels adjust properly and stay there so we don't pass out. What is a good blood O2 level for us anyway. I don't know how old you are but I'm talking about over 60 years. I think the low carb paleo, leptin sens. is good especially if you have made sure you are getting the proper amount of protein and fat to keep your health up. They say to come to the altitude and give yourself a few days to adjust before doing anything strenuous even if in good health. I have to make sure I get enough macro-nutrients because it's very easy for me to go into starvation mode and that sure doesn't help my health and stamina any. I think a good protein and fat meal and a stiff dose of bullet proof coffee would be a good idea if you do that again!

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-12T07:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-23350>

Yow, Pikes Peak is over 14,000 feet. No wonder it was hard. so far studies say 10,000 is good enough.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-24T08:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-26986>

last trip up the mtn was 5-5-12, 17 days later 5-23-12 today I made another (10th) trip up. Job interfered. About 3/4 of the way up I began to be concerned that I would be cold because I hadn't taken a jacket. the wind was strong, temp was down 30 points and i began to feel cold. Shortly after this I could feel the spread of warmth throughout my body. When I got off the tram at the top I was quite comfortable. I was delighted. I seem to remember recently in this blog someone mention that ones body became warm in anticipation of the treatment. The HR still goes up to 118 on exertion, but the dizziness is controlled with the breath and is less ever all. O2 goes from 87 to 96 and is affected by the breath technique.

i took off my shoes and socks and got some free Vit D from the knees down. I took off my sunglasses for about 20 minutes. My D level was 99 last I checked so I am pretty well protected I feel.

i've been able to think about my buy-in to this including and especially the CT. I'm not particularly interested in a 6 pack, nor in climbing MT Everest on my 74th BD (in 4 years) so I can be the oldest lady to do so. My children all have their own set ways and probably think I'm extreme. I'm done with playing Charley Brown to Lucy's grabbing the football from him. If people ask me how I do it. I have been answering them. I've had people start babbling senselessly. My sweet Catholic nun neighbor even jumped down my throat shouting, "What are you trying to do, kill me?" to my innocent dietary suggestion that might help the pain she always complains about. Well, at least she doesn't c/o pain to me any more but it hurts me to see her hobble so. I've done years of health education and dietary counseling and am done with being attacked for it.

Here is what I've come up with so far. I want to be able to get up from the floor without using my hands to help me. I want to wash my car windows and get into my car seat, putting my purse on the seat beside me without gasping in pain from my shoulders. I want to have energy to do at least 6 hours of various kinds of movement during the day and then go out for a couple of hours in the evening. When I retired 6 years ago people would tell me that I would get bored. I said no i wouldn't because i had a long list of things I still wanted to do. Well, that list still waits for me as I've floundered around with health issues. A lot of it has to do with art, poetry, music etc that i started out with as a young person. An optimal life for me is to have that vibrancy and joi de vivre that I had as a young person. All the better to write a song lyric or a story. It's not the hard muscles I'm after. I'm after the strong supple sound of my singing voice and not the wavering quaver of a decrepit elder. Can I have the stamina to jam with a group on my keyboard once again or play an energetic song on my flute? So far so good. I can do the face dunks now.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-26T00:25:17+0300

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-26T01:05:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-27488>

On 5-24 was my 11th trip up the mtn. It was quite windy and we were told to stay close by. I had fun climbing around on the rocks and adjusting my ankles and knees to my hiking boots. 02 levels were about 88-96. No dizziness and only one bout of nausea noted. Later I had some chips and cheese dip. Afterward walking was a little difficult and no dizziness or blackout potential noted.

One 5-25 was my 12th trip up the Mtn and 3rd day in a row. This time it was a tad more windy and we swayed into the restraining bar about 4 inches from the extreme mark. Yikes. As soon as we got there they announced no one else would go up and they began to take people down. I watched the first group go down and thought if the gondola did a loop de loop I was gonna walk down! Actually it was very safe. They put a lot of extra water ballast and the ride was very safe. I amused my self while waiting for the last ride by checking out how my new sock combo and how tighter lacing of the shoes in a certain spot would hold my feet better. I was so interested in checking out the boots I didn't realize how much I was walking and doing just fine. No nausea, gasping, or potential blackout issues. I paused once on the last set of stairs and that was all. In addition I did several loads of clothes and went to my PT appointment before I left. What a difference in my energy from 2 months ago. Last year when I finished my 3 month job I was practically on bed rest for a month. This year I kept right on going. Interesting note is that I am not doing anything more up on the mtn than slowly strolling around for an hour and then sitting and looking at the scenery and chatting with the tourists for a half hour or so. This is my kind of exercise!

### vkiernan — 2012-05-26T05:22:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-27573>

> **TerryF;27573 wrote:** Hi Vkiernan, thanks for the interest. What is your optimal?

That's a loaded question for sure. My optimal is to get rid of my adrenal fatigue, fix my gut, balance my hormones and have my thyroid back on track. A lot to work on for sure. I believe I have a fairly long road ahead of me but on a positive note at least I know most of what needs fixing. You give me hope for sure. You just keep on a chuggin'. I want to be able to play with my grandaughters and not be overwhemed with exhaustion and stress. They are 5 and 7 and just like my own. I love those babes so much. It is hard right not cuz they wear me out and then I am done for the day and have nothing left. I also want to not be cranky but all that ails me makes me that way. I have made a lot progress but there is more to be gained. My optimal really is to wake up happy and want to smile. That will be a grea day for sure. When that happens, I will know everything else is making progress and I will be able to do the things I want to do and enjoy it.

I am CT'ing as much as I can to help me with all of my issues and it is working for sure on some. I have been doing the LRx since July of last year. I didn't know then everything I know now about how bad things really are so the reset for me has been slow. I am still plugging along though and will continue. I know this path is the best one out there and I totally trust Dr. K.

I live in the midwest now so I won't be climbing any mountains anytime soon but I have been to Glacier Nat. Park many times and having you talk about climbing the mountain brings back great memories. I love it on top of the mountain; not so much at the bottom though. Thinking back, I remember the beautiful streams and lakes, the wild flowers that flow endlessly in the breeze and the occasionally mound of snow in August that begs to be sat in. My mom and my brother came to visit me and we all went up there in the month of September way back when and they were standing in snow up to their thighs. It was a great experience for all since they were from the midwest too.

Sounds like you have done a fair amount of travel yes??? Sorry I took so much of your space. You just got me thinkin' is all.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-26T07:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-27599>

Enjoyed your comments. While fixing adrenal fatigue etc. is certainly admirable what makes it all worthwhile is the thought of playing with the grandbabies and seeing them graduate from college, the visits to the mountains, and the visuals of the wildflowers which by the way are great where I am. They are in tiny little mounds of yellow, white and lavender sprinkled all over the mtn top. Here's to being optimal!

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-05-28T20:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-28069>

Had to miss 5-26 as wind was very high. Went up 5-27 Sunday for 13th visit up to 10,378 feet. Lots of tourists as it's memorial weekend. I'm focusing on strengthening my feet, ankles and knees so i went up and down on the stones. Saw lots of wild flowers. (Note to self, get a flower ID book). No noticible dizziness, or nausea. I don't want to concentrate on this now. Before I knew it I had been moving about for an hour and 20 minutes. That I could feel. Someone even asked if I was feeling alright. I sat down inside for the last half hour. In addition, I wore only a tee shirt and carried a white sunproof shirt. Again, I was concerned as I reached the top about the cold. The wind was at least 25 mph, temp at top was about 34 deg F. Windchill was about 25 deg. I could feel the bite of the cold and my hands immediately became icy. But I brought my hiking gloves too. I put them on and was very comfortable in my tee shirt, hands became warm, amazing. I think that's how the Sherpa kids became cold acclimatized. It doesn't seem to take long either. Total time at top this time 3 hours, some due to heavy traffic to get on the tram to come down. Didn't check 02 level as mostly felt OK. I took off my sunglasses most of this time as I went in and out of the shade and sun against greenery. I'm only wearing sunglasses during driving for the glare otherwise they're off. If I wear the blu-blockers too much I get agitated.

### caroline — 2012-06-17T09:56:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-31723>

I am enjoying reading your story so much - thankyou. I just came back from a month in Tibet and I will never be the same \[good thing\] The people are so beautiful. It was such a joyful and spiritual trip and I loved every single second. However - it was impossible to eat paleo and I am struggling to get back to where I was. Not eating paleo brought back all my aches and pains and arthritis. It was quite stunning to see how fast it returned.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-07-24T00:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-40490>

> **caroline;31829 wrote:** I am enjoying reading your story so much - thankyou. I just came back from a month in Tibet and I will never be the same \[good thing\] The people are so beautiful. It was such a joyful and spiritual trip and I loved every single second. However - it was impossible to eat paleo and I am struggling to get back to where I was. Not eating paleo brought back all my aches and pains and arthritis. It was quite stunning to see how fast it returned.

Hi Caroline, thanks for the interest. i just got back from vacation at sea level, I felt like Tarzan!. As I was climbing around on top of the mtn earlier I thought that this must be what the Sherpa kids do and they get naturally acclimated. they just play around and reap the benefits. Given you were eating their diet I wonder if the altitude they were acclimated to played a part in preventing any aches and pains on their part. How high did you go up in Tibet? Of course they don't eat any processed food I bet. Did you just eat their food? I know when i traveled in China and India I took all kinds of bars of packaged food and that sort of thing played havoc with my aches and pains. This time since I stayed in the US I took some supplemental whey and collagen protein powder. I barely used half of what I took. Since I'm fat adapted I could stop eating whenever I wanted to so could avoid eating the wrong thing. i mostly ate white fish and buffalo steaks. Eating twice a day was so helpful. If I did more activity I added a tablespoon of MCT or coconut oil in mid afternoon.

Talk about aches and pains! The first three days I thought I would die. I slept in a pup tent and crawling in and out was excruciating! Since I came by plane I had about an inch of padding off the ground. I could have bought another pad but I decided to tough it out. Amazingly by the 4th day it got a lot easier and my sore shoulder even felt better. At the end of the 10 days after all the rain, thunder and lightening (and me under the tree) I felt quite well and no aches and pains.

My mattress is due to be replaced and I'm giving it second thoughts due to cost. I had some really supportive pillows out there on the ground and even my neck pain got better. Hmmm.

Hi TerryF -I am loving hearing about what you are doing! You are a very motivated woman! When in Tibet we went to Mt Everest base camp - so -5300M approx. Everyone seems so healthy there - the children are gorgeous. We ate alot of rice and noodles. I think there is a lot of yack meat and fat there - but they didn't seem to be giving that to us. They also drink a ton of yak butter tea.

so they are getting lots of fat in there diet and no junk. I am going to Uzbekistan next. I think there will be more variety of food - but it will still be a problem for me. I do take coconut oil with me and contemplated taking cans of salmon - but I think it is impossible to take enough stuff for 26 days. suitcase Weight is a factor as well.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-07-29T21:10:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-41836>

@Caroline. thanks for the info. so that's about 15,000 feet? If I can suggest. eat as much yak butter as you can. They have salmon and tuna in soft pacs that may weigh less so you can get your protein in. If you are not fat adapted yet, make every effort to do so as you will not have as much hunger. You could ask about their meat and fat. They may think others won't eat that stuff so they don't serve it. Have fun, I'm jealous.

### caroline — 2012-07-30T11:03:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-41915>

Thanks TerryF - I did drink the yack butter tea a few times - but it is very salty and smelly. definitely something to work on! I will try to become fat adapted before I go. Uzbekistan will be quite a bit different with much more variety and fresh fruit and veg. I am going to have coffee beforehand with the woman who is leading the tour and so I can get a handle on what will be available and plan accordingly. looking forward to hearing more of your escapades! Cheers from down under

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-08-01T04:55:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-42203>

Well, I'm back home and recovering from mold exposure in Michigan and Cleveland OH. I'm not sure but I think the hallucinations came from this. I haven't checked yet. I've had periods of sneezing, coughing etc. since I've been back for last two weeks. I've also been feeling feverish but no temperature increase. I finally got the HSCRP done and it is 2.45. Yikes. I'm eating a low inflammatory diet and trying to get to sleep at a decent time. Oh yes, My BG is way up. used to be 89 FBS last few days it's been 135, 121 etc. My BP has been up too. Maybe I will have to rethink going to live in Cleveland to be near family. I have started ice packs last two nights. Today I went back up the mtn and stayed 45 minutes, I believe this is about the 14th visit. First in about a month. I sat and read a book about 45 minutes. I then felt nauseated as I walked back to the tram. heart rate was about 110 upon walking. Guess I have to readjust. I'm still having episodes of vaso vagal syncope (I think that's what it is called.) I'm getting the 23and me test then will make that appt with Dr K. I am having a hard time adjusting to the altitude. Much more difficult than I thought it was going to be. I've lived here in the high desert for 15 years too! Dr K will probably say it's hormones as I am in that menopausal age group. Sigh.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-08-04T18:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-42858>

Aah well, sad to say diabetic symptoms are still here and alive and well. I'm still at about 50 gm CHO per day. Todays FBS is 140. The trip was very stressful more so than I thought. Dr K told me to re-read CT-13 and CT-11. As I traveled around doing my errands the next day I realized my situation was similar to Kevin's although I wasn't in any sort of training situation. I unknowingly self-selected for inactivity because any time I tried a new exercise or activity I had over training symptoms such as sore throat, cough, stuffy nose, feeling of unwellness etc. This time on the trip I had no choice but to keep up activity daily. This day as I traveled about the city I realized this has been going on for at least 15 years. I recall being slightly annoyed that any physical activity did this but never thought to connect it to inflammation. I look at least 15 years younger than I am but inside I always feel like I am on the edge of getting sick.

The HSCRP was a real eye opener for me. My labs average is 1-3 and mine is 2.45 which isn't above average but it is far from what Dr K recommends below 0.03. Another thing that is helping is the excell spreadsheet I've done with my labs and the medical history I am putting together for the appointment.

I have part of the protocol in place so I'm not starting from scratch. After dark, check. Diet, check. Labs, almost check. Appt with Dr K, next check. CT, ummm, not yet but I have made several ice/alcohol packs. I re-read CT protocol and every ones suggestions, I started torso icing on 8-2 once a day. Today I want to make two more of the big ones for torso icing. Dr K said to keep ice on self as much as possible. I'm keeping track of my tolerance too as I have read many comments on this. I'm doing face dunks twice a day for the next two weeks and figure out what it takes to get water temp to 45 F so my skin temp is appropriate. Next I have to decide on how best to decrease the inside home temp and to remind myself to keep car temp low and maybe to get an ice vest. To prepare for the in water sessions I have to decide what I can use as a compression shirt being female. I have one that just compresses the torso and is meant to be worn with a bra. This may work. I want to retest HSCRP in a month because I want to focus on this even though they recommend 3 months.. In addition, I start another 6 week contract job then.

### terryfischer39@yahoo.com — 2012-08-04T19:07:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-high-desert-sw-new-mexico-usa-hcg-labs.248/post-42862>

Oh yes, magnesium. I've started to soak in it, and added to the am in addition to the regular pm dose.

# Thread: Bad hormones levels without being leptin resistant?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-hormones-levels-without-being-leptin-resistant.250/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-03-17T22:29:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-hormones-levels-without-being-leptin-resistant.250/post-2118>

Long question coming, here's a summary: leptin resistance means your hormones will be messed up, but is the reverse also true? That is, does having screwed up hormones necessarily mean you are leptin resistant and so you should follow the protocol to reset?

Background: I had a hysterectomy 18 years ago and was continuously on synthetic hormones until Feb 2011. All last spring, summer & fall, I was a mess in terms of hot flashes, lack of sleep & energy, and poor recovery from exercise. I briefly went on synthetic hormones starting in October, had labs done in December and started bioidentical hormones & supplements in January 2012.

The results from the labs were mixed - some good, some bad. The good: HDL of 78, D3 of 69, K2 of 76, homocysteine 7.3, other vitamins & minerals OK. The bad: cortisol high, many hormones low or undetectable (DHEA, aldosterone, testosterone, progesterone derivatives), estrogens a problem ("good" ones low, "bad" ones high). I also had high hsCRP (4.4) so I underwent an EBCT which came back clean, no coronary calcium detected.

I still am not sleeping well, waking every 1-1/2 to 2 hours. The hot flashes are gone except for the last couple of days, but maybe that is related to the CT I started 2 weeks ago.

I am 5'6", 120 lbs and am not looking to lose weight.

I read this in the Hormones 101 blog: So anytime cellular stress is high (high HS CRP), it also forces all the hormone backbone substrate called pregnenolone to be shunted to cortisol production. This is called pregnenolone steal syndrome. What exactly does this mean, Doc? DHEA, Androstenedione, Vitamin D, testosterone, estrogen, and aldosterone production all fall dramatically. These are all of the hormones that are made from a common precursor. Remember that chronic Leptin resistance leads to huge hypercortisolism all the time! NEVER FORGET THIS BIOLOGIC FACT!

Because I don't fit this pattern (Vit D is good, HDL is high), I suspect my problems are due to years of being on synthetics and then dropping them for 9 months and not because of leptin resistance. I'm thinking the high hsCRP is due to the hormone imbalance and not leptin resistance.

Does that make sense?

Would I benefit from following the leptin reset RX anyway?

I'll be retesting in another couple of months.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-19T23:14:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-hormones-levels-without-being-leptin-resistant.250/post-2948>

Your hsCRP shouldn't have shot up because of not being on hormones. Just getting on bioidentical hormones won't fix everything. It didn't for me. My CRP was still high and so was my homocysteine (doc likes that below 5). But doing the leptin reset sent both of those plunging and my cholesterol is just over 200 now, a normal range instead of extremely low like usual.

I think there is a thread for leptin stuff with those who don't need to lose weight. You might look over there and chat with others in the same situation.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-20T00:07:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-hormones-levels-without-being-leptin-resistant.250/post-2964>

> **colleencoble;2969 wrote:** Your hsCRP shouldn't have shot up because of not being on hormones. Just getting on bioidentical hormones won't fix everything. It didn't for me. My CRP was still high and so was my homocysteine (doc likes that below 5). But doing the leptin reset sent both of those plunging and my cholesterol is just over 200 now, a normal range instead of extremely low like usual.
>
>
>
> I think there is a thread for leptin stuff with those who don't need to lose weight. You might look over there and chat with others in the same situation.

Thanks for the support and pointer to the other thread. It's so frustrating to have conflicting lab data and not know how to interpret it.

# Thread: Leptin reset when possibly Leptin sensitive?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-17T23:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/post-2141>

Is there any harm in doing a leptin reset if one is leptin sensitive as I am not sure my level of sensitivity/resistance. I have been on a low carb Paleo Diet for 13 months , and pretty close to my goal weight. I am thinking of following the Leptin Reset protocol for 8 weeks just to make sure. Just wondering if its OK as I may be Leptin sensitive by now. Thanks!

### Lyndra — 2012-03-18T00:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/post-2151>

Great question, I've been wondering the same thing.

### Kirstin — 2012-03-18T01:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/post-2177>

Dr. K has said that it won't \*\*hurt\*\* anything, but basically you would be wasting your time (which he has said would be better spent optimizing). You would be trying to bypass a system in you that already works. The reset is designed to bypass the way Leptin is sensed in the brain by stimulating the vagus nerve and resetting that system via this bypass so the brain eventually becomes sensitive to Leptin again.

### tweety — 2012-03-18T12:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-when-possibly-leptin-sensitive.253/post-2332>

What signs make you think you need a reset?

I was paleo for almost a year and pretty close to goal weight, but flabby/chubby belly. Add to that screwed up hormones and adrenals and I definitely needed the reset. I had immediate benefits like increased energy, shifts in body composition, improved mood... I definitely was not LS at any point (brain, muscles, or liver), even though I have been slim. I can attest to that because 10 months later and I am still working out the issues. Hormones and screwed up cortisol and yeast/parasite overgrowth will stop you dead in your tracks on the way to progress. I even went backwards!

If you don't need the reset, do the post RX and add in CT and you will benefit from that!

# Thread: CT Beginning

### Kpcst — 2012-03-18T00:45:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-beginning.254/post-2162>

I started CT last week and after attending PaleoFX I am even more committed to it.

I had a DEXA Scan at the UT Fit Lab as well. I also just did a max a few weeks back at my Crossfit box.

I figure this was a great combination of things to help me track the benefits of CT as I go.

My stats are Male.32.6'.200lbs . My Dexa showed I was 23% bodyfat. My Max's were 330 Squat. 340 DL. and 180 Shoulder Press. I plan to proceed with doing as much CT as I can. I also intend to restrict my diet even more and try eat fewer carbs and only ones in season. I will also stop eating as much coconut oil until the end of May. My workouts are currently moderate crossfit 3x a week. I'm going to cut that back to once a week. and do OLY Lifts once a week.

Hoping CT improves the stats listed above and I'll be able to update this periodically with some changes etc.

Working on getting some tests done as well. Especially the Telomere one.

# Thread: Am I optimal yet?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ginastarke — 2012-03-18T01:53:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/post-2180>

today's weight 213

Didn't eat nearly enough BAB but I had a hard time throwing something together.I was planning on sleeping in until the farmer's market was open and having salmon, but my brain woke up at at 5 and my body wanted to join it. I guess when I get enough sleep almost every night, I don't need to sleep in on the weekends. So after biking around town, Dinner turned into a real feeding frenzy. ( I started skipping lunch on day 1)

Carbs, about 14 , all from unsweetened baking chocolate

Not a lot of time for ghetto cold therapy today ( a frozen bag of corn on my stomach) but going to do as long as I can before I have to get ready for church in a few hours. After that it's right to bed. I'm not doing the face in ice water for right now because i'm sticking to the places that either have too much fat or loose skin - that's my stomach, inner thighs, and "bingo wings" , that and the face dunk makes my sinuses go berserk!

### ginastarke — 2012-03-18T15:11:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/post-2359>

today's weight 214. Not worried, i know it's temporary.

bab - pork chops, tea and a little chocolate. Chocolate for breakfast, tee hee!

Last Night's sermon was "you have more power than you think!" About how faith can make anything happen- now who does that sound like?

Ordinay day suddenly sad. I got a call from my boss to tell me that my favorite coworker died suddenly from her diabetes. First my uncle, now her, and I worry about my best friend who is also type 2. Furious beyond words at cw advice. Sick of watching it kill good people.

### ginastarke — 2012-03-19T14:09:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/post-2689>

today's weight 213 - apparently my TOM decided to show up. Can't tell if it was emotional or the cold therapy that made it show up.

### ginastarke — 2012-03-20T14:19:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/post-3194>

Today's weight 212.

Hubby on Atkins is coming down with a cold. I wish he ate vegetables other than green beans so I could make him some chicken soup

I like two meals a day. I always used to feel bad about what I saw as binging at the end of the day, because I'd worked out, ridden home in the cold and was ravenous. At the same time, I hated having to pack, carry, and take the time to eat at work.

No ct today. Afraid that an ice pack on my cramping stomach will set off pain like tin foil on a filling!

### ginastarke — 2012-03-21T14:03:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/post-3828>

Today's weight:211

I'm losing weight during my TOM :-O

I can't believe what's happening to my appetite. Lamb shoulder steak for breakfast, biked to work then home in the snow, thinking I was going to eat everything in sight. Nope! some chicken wings 4? And half a chicken wings breast plus some coconut oil cocoa with cinnamon and anise seeds, couldn't finish, set aside for breakfast.

### ginastarke — 2012-03-23T02:16:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/post-4750>

211 again. I think I lost half a pound but I forgot to weigh in before BAB. On the fence about CT becauose I seem to have a nasty respiratory infection.

### ginastarke — 2012-03-23T19:01:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/post-5048>

209, just where I hoped to be before this trip to Portland.

Too bad about this crud, though. My cold meds have a little ace k and I'm taking a little emer-gen C which has one gram of fructose. Keeping it with my meals and I'll stop as soon as I'm better. I hope it doesn't cause problems.

### ginastarke — 2012-05-11T05:36:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-optimal-yet.256/post-23097>

205

Stalled for a long time until my pms made My appetite skyrocket. I ate 2 huge steaks for dinner, expecting to gain weight the next morning -Nope! 1 pound down. So for now I'm allowing myself to eat more, ignoring everything someone tried to teach me about gluttony being the cause of obesity. last night was a lot of chicken salad, and more strawberries than I intended - another pound lost. Yay! tonight : 3 flatiron steaks, an avocado 1/2 a pork steak, a few macadamias, a little coconut manna and 3 squares baking chocolate- let's see what happens...

# Thread: How soon did your CT mayhem start?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### blindpig — 2012-03-18T02:30:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2198>

Glamorama asked how long the CT mayhem lasts. Well, I want to know how soon your mayhem started after you began doing CT?

I know my hormones are really whacked and I am expecting all manner of crazy stuff. I have played around some with face dunks and spot icing, but as I have no thermometers to check skin and water temps I haven't gotten serious about doing them until yesterday. Yesterday I did two separate face dunks in much colder water than I had before, for much longer duration. I also did spot icing about twice as long as I had before, in more areas.

By last evening, I was an emotional wreck. Moods up and down, crying at the drop of a hat, craving sweet stuff, acting like a PMSing teenager. (I am about 13 years post-menopausal and haven't had any food cravings AT ALL since I started the Leptin Reset on November 2.) I have been just as crazy all day today.

Is it possible that one day of serious face dunks and spot icing could cause this reaction? Or has reading this forum planted suggestions in my head?

I was getting ready to start some cold soaks. I will have to have a long heart to heart talk with DH first if this is how it's gonna be. We can't afford a divorce attorney.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-18T04:00:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2255>

I went straight into spot-CT on my tummy, 45 min from day 1. I think the mayhem begun on day 3 or 4.

### blindpig — 2012-03-18T20:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2441>

Thanks, Glamorama, I know you have really been having a time of it and it helps to know when and how it started for you. I really don't know how those of you with young kids and jobs are getting by.

I don't think I truly and completely believed I could ever touch "optimal" because of my myriad of health issues. I didn't think it was even realistic to dream that big. Now I do. I am convinced that my hormonal upheaval was from the intensified CT that I did on Friday. I think I needed something to slap me upside the head and show me just how powerful this is, and I have been duly slapped. I am in awe of what that one day did to me (even though it was very unpleasant).

DH and I had a long talk this morning and we are making a plan so that we can make this a priority for me, no matter what challenges the CT might bring. We have set a date and we are clearing our calendars of all our on-going stuff so that nothing is going to interfere with this. I am beyond pumped to get this started. Even if it means weeks of hormone hell!

I would still be interested in hearing others' experiences regarding how quickly hormonal upheaval began if you care to share...

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-18T21:34:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2446>

I am interested in hearing more. Being a guy, I think we respond differently. I'm just not sure how.

### shah78 — 2012-03-18T22:02:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2451>

Negitive side effects happened after60 hours. Started to get a sorethroat and fuzzy head/sinus. Positive side effects started within hours. After initial ice gel seesion, a very "enriched" love session with wife. Was a little skeptical though. But right after shirtless pre dawn b'ike after thirty six hours, had "enriched" gym session. So good I injured myself doing one too many chin. I didn't belive it was true. But now a week later, I am convinced. So the bottom line. I felt the CT effects almost from the get go. Note:I'm in excellent shape, LS,and have no cravings> Just a cold wimp!

### blindpig — 2012-03-18T22:09:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2455>

Hey, Don,

I am certain that you (lucky?) guys are going to respond differently than we girls are just because of the hormonal differences, but I'm not sure how, either. I was going to tell you that if you haven't read the thread titled, "How long before the CT mayhem ends?", you might want to give it a look. But then I went back and re-read it, and now I'm thinking you might want to skip it because it might just scare the bejeezus out of you!

I suggest you start a thread asking for the guys specifically to share their experiences. I know I would like to hear how the guys are reacting, too, since I am trying to drag my DH along on this crazy ride.

Happy CTing to you!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T01:08:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2502>

I always want to highlight that the positives of CT became apparent in less than a week of spot-CT. For some reason we all tend to focus of the bad (read: detox), but once you're out of the messy stuff that CT creates, you're stronger, elaner and better for it. I'm just about to see light at the end of the tunnel, and I have done 4 weeks. I make family-life work around my CT... I do baths after the kids are in ebd at night, and spot-CT when the baby naps and the twins are in kinder.

### Honey Crisp — 2012-03-19T13:03:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2678>

Note:I'm in excellent shape, LS,and have no cravings> Just a cold wimp!

I've been dabbling with CT. Yesterday, I got a bit more serious about it. I did a cold shower in the morning and a cold bath in the evening. This morning I woke up with a sore throat, congestion and an arthritic feeling in some of my joints. I thought the cause was from sleeping with the window opened. Now, I'm wondering if it's side effects.

Thanks for sharing your experience.

### caroline — 2012-03-19T13:51:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2687>

Is anyone having bloating after CT and spot icing? This started a couple of days ago and I first thought it was something I was eating but nothing has really changed - so I am now sure it is the CT. What should I do? I have very few problems except I have a lot of fat in the middle area and pretty thin every where else. I assume this is detoxing. This fat middle area started a few years ago when I was under a lot of stress with a very sick husband so I would guess this is a hormonal thing?? I am post menopause. I would appreciate any thoughts. Thanks

### blindpig — 2012-03-19T15:48:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2722>

(I just have a couple events coming up that require that I be sane...then it will be balls to the wall!) If the CT can cause powerful negative effects, to me that means it can also cause powerful positive effects. I am happy you are getting close to the light at the end of the tunnel. I have been following your progress and you have been one of my inspirations. You are looking amazing and you deserve to feel amazing too!
>
>

> caroline;2695 wrote:** Is anyone having bloating after CT and spot icing? I don't have a bit of advice about the bloating, Caroline, but I just wanted to say that it makes sense to me that it is hormonal. That is how I am shaped, too...pretty thin everywhere but a nice layer of belly fat which I am certain is courtesy of my menopausal hormones. And if the CT shakes up the hormones the way I am now convinced that it does, I can certainly believe that it could be messing with your hormones and causing the bloating. Hopefully someone will chime in with some advice for you. I hope you will keep us all posted.

### tweety — 2012-03-19T19:52:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2845>

I have done a few sessions and not had any negative side effects. I feel wonderful these days, but I want to jump start my thyroid and adrenals and lose the weight around my middle. Not seeing any die off or other reactions makes me think I am not doing it right!

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-19T20:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-2869>

I'm a week in now, and feeling exhausted, itchy and very, very cold and achy everywhere. Yesterday felt very upbeat and energetic, but today have been feeling awful. Hope it's the start of things happening! So far I've gained 5lbs but lost 2 inches around the tummy and gained an inch around the waist ... feeling very bloated and sore today.

### Swagger — 2012-03-23T17:35:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-4997>

Started the Rx 15 days ago and recently started CT. I've been sub 25 carbs along with doing CT and I have had emotional swings as well. I am a guy, so don't think the ups and downs are exclusive to you ladies. I was a complete bas\*\*\*d last night until my CT. Ive had my ups too, but the down is just as strong. Not like "go kick a puppy" angry, but still a bit hard to manage. Dealing with the itchy skin too. I'm losing inches of my chest but the belly has stopped shrinking since starting CT. I think my brain is like Stu from "The Hangover". It's running in circles screaming "WHAT IS GOING ON!!!" like it just found a jungle cat in the bathroom.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-23T18:16:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-5030>

Love it! Stay away from day-care centers and the humane society. The idea is the "confusion" to the system/brain is what's needed for re-wiring. At least that's what I'm going on!

It's really helping to see all the itches on here. Makes me feel like it's working or at perhaps misery just loves company.

I am taking photos of my crazy skin eruptions (overnight skin tags!) so I can tell if they are changing. Perhaps I'll share once they are GONE. As it is, no dresses or revealing clothing for work - my body looks crazy diseased! I'm grateful my face is lookin good. I swear the face dunks have made me look younger.

### nate.reik@yahoo.com — 2012-03-24T00:25:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-5193>

Anyway, tried CT a couple times a couple weeks ago, cold bath in 62-63 water. Then one coldish shower last week. This week, I've started a more hardcore Leptin Rx and CT combo. So CT twice so far this week. 20-25 minutes in 55-56 degree water in the tub. (We have a deep tub) Last night, very emotional before my CT. Today, much calmer, but I've had a headache all day, which is HIGHLY unusual for me. I guess I don't have anything else to attribute it to than CT.

### kalibex — 2012-03-28T16:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-7694>

### Myree67 — 2012-03-28T19:53:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-7802>

This must be what's going on with me! A few things didn't go my way yesterday and usually I can brush it off and let go and let God..not yesterday... I was forgetful and irrtitable. I went to the dentist and had an eye appt. I couldn't follow simple directions...opthamologist told me to look up...I turned my whole head up making it kinda hard for him to see my eyes...I need new glasses and Ibecause I,m spending so dang much money on good food and supplements we can't afford them...just the lenses for my existing glasses after insurance is $262 I'm still really irritated especially since none of this has helped at all with my chronic pain and of course I can't find a doctor who believes all my symptoms are related and just want to drug me up further... THEN I found out I've over done it with the Vitamin D3 and now iit's at 117 from the 31 it started out at... NOT having a good week... I'm a believer of positive outlook and seeing the lessons I have in trials and tribulation but this week I am really having difficulty not feeling sorry for myself... then I broke down last night and ate gluten free cookies.... I was so good about my eating habits since July but when I got sick in February I resorted to eating bad and now can't seems to eat protein from good meat or fat enough to hold me over for 5 hours so I'm really frustrated because my HMO doesn't seem to want to help me be optimal... sorry just had to vent to people that know what I'm going through

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T20:04:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-7810>

I think my biggest negative was being freezing cold afterward. We've also had wild temperature swings where I live, so one week it was 80 degrees in the house and the next it was 65. But I haven't worn a coat or jacket since learning about CT, and I have not suffered, even into the 40s.

@Myree, you can get very cheap eyeglasses from eyebuydirect.com. They may not be the prettiest, but at least they could get you through until you have more $.

### penguinmom718@yahoo.com — 2012-03-29T06:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-8130>

I guess my mayhem started mildly a week or two ago, but it really kicked in last night/today. I started CT about 5 weeks ago now, but I have been taking it slowly. I am 57 and post menopause. I have thought my thermostat has been wonky since the frequent hot flashes ended several years ago, so the few times I had a very warm flash over the last couple of weeks, I put off to simply adapting to the cold. My only other weird symptom was one evening getting a zit on my right face cheek while simultaneously getting a zit on left bum cheek. Odd, and they were gone the next day. I have never been prone to zits though.

However, in the middle of last night I woke up very hot, and threw off the covers. While I never got the hot flash sweat and subsequent freezing phase, it took me a while to cool down enough to sleep again. Maybe 10-15 minutes.

And then this morning my rosacia flared up something fierce. My face is RED. I haven't had a flare up like this in years.

### Myree67 — 2012-03-29T10:42:50+0300

According to the eye center I need digital progressive lenses... I'm getting a second opinion since my glasses that I just got last February cost that much with frames AND transition lenses soooo I think someone is trying to push me into something I don't need...

I lost who it was but normal range for D3 is 30-80 according to Group Death errr I mean Health...

I'm really irritated now because I had a phone consult with their fancy pain doctor and the doctor doesn't remember what terms she used when telling me what was wrong with me... DIDN'T document my visit evidently... urgh I had to have a friend drive me up to Seattle( over 1.5 hours) causing horrible pain for me for this doctor to not "remember" and then denied telling me I have Autonomic Neuropathy in one nerve on my right leg... said neuropathy is a general term used for any kind of nerve damage... now I got poked with electric needles in 2009 and was told then that I had A Neuropathy in my right leg... I'm going to demand that they refer me to a local Neurologist AND refer me to a Homeopath or something! I'm so furious at this point!

### mycrazycartoons@gmail.com — 2012-03-29T16:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-soon-did-your-ct-mayhem-start.258/post-8256>

> **vwaggs;8131 wrote:** My mayhem didn't start until about a month into doing baths every other day for 30 min. I broke out in a rash on my arms, face, and neck and have horrible drainage(could be a virus??) and super productive cough. It all doesn't seem to want to go away even after a week. Not sure why my symptoms to so long to arrive.

I reacted similarly, but rather than getting rashes, I have small bruises (someone suggested on another thread that it might be from krill oil thinning my blood and to supplement higher with K2) but the horrible drainage and productive cough are definitely my biggest issue.. wondered, if it's a virus and whether or not I should CT... my reactions started a couple days after doing the face plunge..and the sinus/cough went away for a couple weeks then started up again after doing more intense CT with 55 deg baths.
