# Thread: RSD - does anyone have it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-03-18T04:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2267>

My daughter has had RSD for about 12 years - from an injury to her hand. She is always in pain pretty much and now it seems to morph into fibromyalgia. The only treatment that has ever worked is to go into the hospital for a week \[twice a year\] and be on a Ketamine drip. She hates it so much but it is the only thing that gives her some relief.

It seems to me that the leptin reset and CT would be very helpful.

Any thoughts anyone???

### caroline — 2012-03-18T04:19:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2272>

I should have said what RSD is - since most Docs don't even know about it. It is Reflex Sympathetic Dystrophy Syndrome. It was her physio that diagnosed her much later on. Apparently if you start treatment right away you have a chance of reversing the nerve damage. Hers was left much too long.

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-03-18T07:18:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2315>

> **caroline;2273 wrote:** My daughter has had RSD for about 12 years - from an injury to her hand. She is always in pain pretty much and now it seems to morph into fibromyalgia. The only treatment that has ever worked is to go into the hospital for a week \[twice a year\] and be on a Ketamine drip. She hates it so much but it is the only thing that gives her some relief.
>
> It seems to me that the leptin reset and CT would be very helpful.
>
>
>
> Any thoughts anyone???

I am sorry to hear about your daughter's pain....

I am not to familiar with RSD aside from having 2 friend that suffer with it. I have watched both of them struggle with pain, and doctors thinking it was all in their head, and family members misunderstanding their condition....my heart goes out to you and your daughter. I'm sorry that I'm not able to offer much advice here, aside from saying that I would imagine that the Leptin rx would help, since it seems to help with SO much! Really my thought is, it couldn't hurt to do it?!

One of the friends of mine who has RSD also has IBS/gut issues...does your daughter any gut issues?

### caroline — 2012-03-18T11:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2322>

Thankyou joyfuljoyful for your kind thoughts. I love your name! Dr K was very critical of one poster and their name. He said your name should be positive and up lifting or something along those lines. yours is wonderful!

You are exactly right - people often think the pain is in your head. I had a friend in Canada who would go to the mayo clinic for treatment. Some people would amputate the offending limb to relieve the pain - sadly - even that doesn't work. My daughter was hurt at work and went thru an agonizing five years and tried to get compensation. Even tho everyone agreed she would never work again \[she was 27 when it happened\] she was generally ridiculed and treated like she was lying - even by doctors. One specialist asked her to remove her watch and her shoes and then in his report said she was fine because she was able to do these things. The thing is - even when you have this - you still have to do things and you still have to use your hand regardless of how much pain you are in. Sorry to be so long winded.

I am not sure about the gut issues - I would suspect yes. She is carrying a lot of weight around her middle. In both pregnancies she got gestational diabetes. She eats a lot of junk but thankfully makes sure her little boys eat very healthy.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-18T17:50:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2395>

My cousin has had it for many years. She had a few surgeries long ago and thinks that the metal implants might have been the trigger. It took numerous visits to various doctor to figure out what was ailing her and like others, was accused of being a hypochondriac. Unfortunately, she follows a SAD diet, smokes heavily, and isn't apt to change. I wish you well for your daughter.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-18T17:53:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-2396>

My friend's niece has it and has undergone some type of extensive physical therapy that has allowed her to be a normal 16 yr old. I don't know the name of the therapy but if you would like it let me know as I can always find out.

Unfortunately, she follows a SAD diet, smokes heavily, and isn't apt to change. I wish you well for your daughter.

Thanks very much Lyndra - I think people with RSD get very depressed from always being in so much pain and they dig themselves into a hole which they can't get out of

Some people would amputate the offending limb to relieve the pain - sadly - even that doesn't work. My daughter was hurt at work and went thru an agonizing five years and tried to get compensation. Even tho everyone agreed she would never work again \[she was 27 when it happened\] she was generally ridiculed and treated like she was lying - even by doctors. One specialist asked her to remove her watch and her shoes and then in his report said she was fine because she was able to do these things. The thing is - even when you have this - you still have to do things and you still have to use your hand regardless of how much pain you are in. Sorry to be so long winded.
>
> I am not sure about the gut issues - I would suspect yes. She is carrying a lot of weight around her middle. In both pregnancies she got gestational diabetes. She eats a lot of junk but thankfully makes sure her little boys eat very healthy.

Thanks, caroline, I'm glad you like my name  I agree that it is nice to have something uplifting, and I am so grateful that I do have JOY!

One of my friends was injured at work, and since her accident (about 4 years ago), workmans comp has tried so hard to 'prove' that she is not really injured....it is so sad. Thank God they haven't won that battle and she is still under her doctors care. She goes in for pain treatments a couple times a month. Hers progressed from one foot to the other, and now is in her hands. The one thing I would wonder about CT with RSD patients is this: my friend said that some RSD patients cannot feel the cold in the affected body part any longer. The other friend of mine who suffers from RSD is proof of this, I've seen her in the cold of winter with no socks or shoes on (on her affected foot). So I wonder if this would be good or bad for the affected area. Sorry I don't have any real knowledge on this.

Thanks Joyful. Now that you mention that - she never wears a jacket or sweater always says she is hot. interesting

### natural_girl — 2012-03-21T01:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rsd-does-anyone-have-it.259/post-3611>

Caroline,

I was dx with RSD in my right arm in 2000. I went through medical hell with it. Many local nerve blocks, stellate ganglion blocks, spinal cord stimulators and almost a med pump. To think of the drugs I used to take. I take nothing now! NOTHING! After years of going to specialists I was tired of nothing really helping so I found natural health & a Dr. to help me. I took my life back. I did regular myofascial massage, tons of Hellerwork, and completely changed my eating & lifestyle. I have never looked back...Keep the faith it is like anything else you have to want it so bad you can taste and NEVER use the words I can't

### natural_girl — 2012-03-21T19:48:59+0200

Caroline,

I am definitely a strong person....but desperate times call for desperate measures and it was all a journey I was meant to take. I was not going to let the meds kill me or own me....I had 3 small children at that time. I am in Ohio but the lady who did my Hellerwork actually trained in Australia at the Hellerwork school there! It was expensive & excruiating painful but it was nothing short of a miracle along with a 100% strict organic diet, no grains or dairy & no nightshades. I also spent a fortune in supplements (still do) but it was better than the alternative.

I am pain free most of the time and have a bit of pain now and again but usually don't need anything more than an adjustment or massage. I do CT but that was long after my healing but I think it will only strengthen my system. If you need any further info feel free to PM me. You & your daughter are in my prayers as I know the hell RSD can bring.

# Thread: Lovelife's Journey!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-18T04:42:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-2289>

I've been wanting to track things better for awhile so might as well start this journal here.

**Stats:

Female

5'3"

28yrs

**Backstory:**

I was a vegetarian and vegan for about 5 1/2 yrs. Oops.  I learned of paleo/primal in Aug 2010. Went from veg to primal and lasted for about a month and a half. Then I tried Tim Ferris's slow carb in March 2011.. which lasted for another month or so. Realized at that point that I couldn't have a "cheat day" or I would end up going off course for an entire wknd instead. In June I reached my highest weight ever of 176 lbs. I started back on paleo Aug 2011 @ 171.8 lbs. By mid Sept I had gotten down to 159 lbs. Then I stalled. I started having to fight for every lb no matter what I did. I tried changing my macro's many times.. I even started working out in Nov.. nothing made a difference. By the beginning of Dec I was only at 154 lbs. I had also started losing my hair like I was in chemo or something which seriously started to worry me. My mother has hypothyroidism and I was concerned that I was getting it as well. Unfortunately, I had no way to get any testing done as I have no insurance or money. I haven't been to a general doctor since I was 17 I think. The last two wks of Dec and the first wk of Jan I stopped eating paleo for the most part as I was frustrated at how well I had been eating yet my weight was barely budging and seemingly getting less healthy with the hair loss. I took a break over xmas/new years time and started looking for ways to help your thyroid with nutrition. At the end of Jan 2012 I found Dr. Kruse's site in my search for thyroid help and paleo help. Leptin explained my issues so well that I had to start asap. I started the Leptin Reset on Jan 29, 2012.

Leptin Reset + CT**

I still have no money for testing and very little money for food. So, if I can accomplish this on the small amount I have then I know anyone can. I wish I could have had starting numbers, but I figure looks and feelings are what matters in the end anyways. As soon as it's possible though I will be getting every test possible.

Today is the last day of my 7th wk. I wasn't expecting to lose weight during the reset as I had read on the MDA thread that women often don't. Luckily to my enjoyment and amazement I have been losing this entire time. In fits and starts, but still a downward trend in weight and inches.

I weigh myself every morning and measure myself every monday. After the first couple wks I now eat the same thing every day. I plan to stay on the reset until I at least reach my first goal weight of 120 lbs. I haven't always been able to get the complete 50 gms of protein due to monetary problems, but I am able to for the next month at least. I obviously cannot afford most anything grassfed or many supplements at this time. I know I cannot become optimal without doing that, but I figure I will do the best I can for now and work on optimal when I have the means.

This is my food for each day.. it may vary slightly, but not by much:

Breakfast:

~2 cans tuna

~1 egg

~1-2 Tbls Coconut Oil

~Mayo

~Hot tea with whole milk (plan on changing to heavy cream in April.. milk is cheaper for now)

Lunch:

~2 beef patties made with grassfed beef liver (about 8oz total)

~Mayo

~Dijon Mustard

~A couple Tbls of Bubbies Sauerkraut

~1 Raw Brazil Nut

"Dinner":

This is most nights, but not every night.

~I make my own Chai tea that has 1 cup whole milk

~Half a square of 90% chocolate (I don't like this dark of choc, but am trying to acquire the taste and will be moving to 99% in April)

Only supplement I have is D3 which I take b/w 4,000-8,000 IU/day. I have tried to make my food have things I need that I can't afford as supplements the best I can right now. Hence the liver, tuna, brazil nut, and sauerkraut. My teeth are my main health issue from being a sugar and pop aholic my entire life and I believe from being vegan. So, I need D3, K2, and A badly. I'm sure O3 as well, but canned tuna is the best I can do for now.

I started CT 2 wks ago. Well, I technically started 3, but I had an issue with my second bath so I moved back to showers for awhile. My water temp stays b/w 48-55 degrees F. I don't try to stay in for a certain time.. I just go by how I feel. When I feel I need/should get out I do. I think the longest I've been in was 30-35 min. I start as a shower.. I have a removable shower head and use that to focus on my thyroid/chest and upper back area. I also can move the water around then. I plug my tub and let it fill while I use the shower head. I also can do face "dunks" at the same time with the shower head. When the tub is half full I move to laying down and fill it the rest of the way with the faucet. Then I lay in it until I feel I should get out. If I don't feel like taking a bath then I use ice packs on my stomach, neck, and back.

I have not worked out at all for the most part during my reset. I also sit all day so walking doesn't happen much either. I hoop dance though for about 60 min 1 day a wk. That is pretty much the extent of my exercise. I hate walking and get extremely bored so I decided to just not exercise during the reset as I prefer exercise that is not allowed during the reset.

**Results Up to Now:

Starting weight (1/29/12): 156.8

Today's weight (3/17/12): 145.2

Starting Measurements (1/31/12):

~Neck: 13.5

~Chest (over largest part of breast):38

~Right Arm: 12.5

~Left Arm: 12

~Waist: 30

~Lower Stomach (over poochie part): 33.75

~Hips (over ass): 38.25

~Right Thigh: 23

~Left Thigh: 23

~Right Calf: 14.5

~Left Calf: 14.5

Last Measurments (3/12/12):

~Neck: 13.5

~Chest (over largest part of breast): 37.5

~Right Arm: 12

~Left Arm: 12

~Waist: 29

~Lower Stomach (over poochie part): 32.5

~Hips (over ass): 37.5

~Right Thigh: 22.75

~Left Thigh: 22.75

~Right Calf: 14.5

~Left Calf: 14.25

Cool Results From CT:**

A couple wks ago I did about 10 burpees to see if my recovery rate had changed (showing LS signs) and to see if I was any stronger from CT. Right before I started the Leptin RX I had started a 100 pushup challenge.. so I knew how many I could do and how easily. At that time I could do 8 with perfect form. When I did the burpees I did 10 with perfect form and pretty easily. The next day I was slightly sore on my chest and arms. Only lasted a day though. Then 2 days ago I thought I'd check again. I did 12 pushups easily! The best part though is yesterday I realized I wasn't sore at all!! It was like I hadn't done anything differently than my normal sitting around.

I also am a beginning pole dancer and a hoop dancer. Both are helpful as I can easily tell strength gains by how long I can hold pole tricks as well as cardio by how out of breath I may get from hoop dancing. I haven't touched my pole since starting the reset. Which was hard as I love the workout. Tonight, inspired from my lack of soreness from the push-ups a couple days ago.. I decided to test my strength. I am noticeably stronger! I can hold myself up with only my hands/arms in a pull-up type position while on spin mode. Where before this I couldn't do that at all let alone for a short period of time of sustained pull-up/spinning! Second thing is that I had a weak left wrist and elbow from many yrs of waitressing. It had been causing me serious issues with learning pole dancing. Even though it isn't my dominant hand I still have to use it a lot for support. In certain moves my elbow would hurt like the bone was breaking almost.. and my wrist would also hurt. To the point where I had to stop trying certain moves. I tried a few of them tonight and not only can I do them now because of increased strength my left arm was FINE! No pain at all anywhere! I'm so excited.

One of the best things has been that my hair is almost back to normal. Since a couple wks into the Leptin Reset and now with the CT my hair has been getting better.. I think it is helping my thyroid. My hair is almost normal in the amount it falls out now.

The only negative things I have encountered from CT are mainly related to estrogen I believe. My breasts have been tender before my period which had stopped when I gave up all grains.. but has been back since using CT. No other changes with my period thankfully. I have been lucky my entire life and have always had an extremely easy period and have never had cramps or any pain. Which I definitely DON'T want to change. LOL. I also have always been on "time" for me.. though always a shorter cycle at 25 days.. though as I just looked it seems it has moved to 27 days now.. interesting. Also, I have been breaking out on my face more often.. which had almost completely disappeared since starting the reset. I also have been faintly nauseous a few times this wk. Which is odd as I never am nauseous even when I have been sick for my entire adult life. Other than that everything has been great!

Sorry so long everyone. I will be much shorter on my other entries. It's a lot more to write since I've been doing it longer than a few days.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-18T06:20:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-2311>

Great news on your progress! I am also on a super-tight budget. Things I can recommend: added canned sardines to your list of fish. Just as cheap and probably better for you since you get more oily fish as well as the bones. Make your own sauerkraut (super easy, super cheap and better for you). Look into the human grade salmon oil sold on the petfood market. Dexter (from the Monster MDA thread) has posted about where he gets it - it's cheap, fresh and keeps in the fridge. (I am in New Zealand so my source won't help you -but you can search the MDA thread or look here on the O3/O6 thread because I it's there, too.) Good luck!!!

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-18T15:35:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-2363>

Thanks for the suggestions KiwiLauren. I have never had sardines and think they sound gross, but I know they r supposed to be good. So, just the other day I got a tin to try out. Unfortunately, tuna is cheaper than sardines so I will mainly have to stick with that for now. Basically what is going on is what some people refer to as the "poor tax" LOL. Yes the salmon oil isn't expensive.. $75 for a tub that lasts a few months is cheaper than the tuna and better in O3, but since tuna is $.54 a can that is cheaper in the short run. Unfortunately $75 was almost my entire food budget for this month. So, you can see my dilemma. If I like the sardines though I will try to add in a couple a month.

**Today's Update:**

Weight: 144.4

Measurements: same as last

Mood: 71 percent (percent symbol doesn't work in this forum it seems)

Good news is started TOM yet lost weight from yesterday. Yay. I don't expect to lose anymore this wk becuz of TOM, but will still be ct-ing and eating the same of course. Slept really well last night. I think I woke up once, but I can't be sure. Other than that slept through the night.. which is nice as my sleep is still sometimes erratic. Went to bed at 10:45pm.. probably fell asleep around 15-20 min after.. woke up at 7:15am.

In my first post I didn't mention any sleep things. I have always been a night owl. I would go to bed b/w 2:30am-5:00am and get up 12pm-2pm normally. I never had a normal 24 circadian rhythm as my sleep/wake cycles seemed to always move the same.. I think I have/had a 26 hr one. But not positive. I have read that not everyone has a 24, but I don't know if that's something we are born with or mess up by not living well. Anyways, I haven't gone to bed this early since I stopped having a bed time as a child. So, to say the least it is hard. Even though I am tired now around 10-11pm I don't want to go to bed. I force myself too though. I do enjoy waking up this early.. which is strange to ever think I'd say. I always hated to get up early. My roommate thinks it's hilarious and is completely shocked and "weirded out" that I have actually kept it up these past 7 wks. I am as well though LOL.

Oh, I have also started tracking my moods every day with this cool new website. It's meant for people with depression/bipolar/ect, but I think it's cool so I'm going to list it here as well. Maybe see if I notice a correlation with mood and ct/food/sleep patterns. Anyone who's interested it's [Moodscope](http://http://www.moodscope.com/). It's free.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-23T16:52:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-4981>

Whoops, thought I missed one day and see I missed 2. Oh well.

**3/22 Update:**

Weight: 144

Mood: 80 percent

Did CT shower + bath last night for 30 min in 48 degree water. TOM is over so hopefully weigh loss will start back up again. Did a small amount of pole dancing and hoop dancing yesterday. Went well. I'm not even sore at all. My left arm is still doing great with the pole dancing.. no pain still! I believe I'm starting to see signs of being LS. My nails seem to be growing somewhat more quickly, the skin on my elbows is the best it's ever been, still need a little work on the skin on my heels, noticeable strength gains without any workouts, and my hair may be growing a little faster. Took some pictures yesterday finally.. just waiting on some starting pictures. I wish I had taken more as I'm having a harder time finding some starting ones. I think I'm still going to continue eating Reset style until I reach my first goal of 120 lbs or at least through April. I'm finally getting some K2 in April for my teeth.. I'm so excited.

Went to bed last night at 10:30pm.. fell asleep around 11pm.. got up at 8:11am. My sleep has been consistently better this wk with sleeping through the entire night. I often wake up at either 6am, 6:30am, or 7:30am... then fade in and out until I get up b/w 7:30-8:30 am. I think the issue with waking is that because I have blacked out my room I need some kind of light to wake me. As soon as I have the money I think I will get one of those automatic blue/sun lights that you can set like an alarm to wake you in a dark room.

I'm debating on whether to start working out or not. On the one hand I think it may help progress my weight loss and strength gains, but on the other working out has always made me hungrier and I don't have any extra food to up my calories at all right now. So, I'm not sure what to do. Maybe I will try it and hope that with becoming LS I won't be starving after I work out.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-25T02:03:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-5623>

**3/24 Update:**

Weight: 144

Mood: 58 percent

Well, last night I had some issues. I went to bed early at 10pm. But for dinner I had something "new".. I had eggs and two sausages. MISTAKE! The ingredients listed "natural flavors" which is code for "a bunch of shit we don't have to tell you about", but I went for it anyways as I've never had issues before with it. Well, that changed. I fell asleep around 11pm and then woke up around 1:30-2am with a slight amount of acid reflux and horrible diarrhea. Went back to sleep at 2:45am and got up late at 9:00am. Where I again had stomach issues. I will say one good thing about eating the exact same thing every day is that if you add something new you always know what messed you up.

No more sausage of that brand for me! So, sleep was a little blah and I think it showed in my mood today which is interesting as I don't feel too tired, but obviously something is amiss. Other than that everything is going well. I did CT last night with ice packs because I didn't feel like taking a bath/shower. One more day and it's measurement time again.. yay! Hopefully I've lost some inches. I wish I had a way to measure body fat percent as that would also help me feel better when I don't lose weight on the scale.

3/25 Update:**

Yay mood is up again. Went to bed last night at 10:20pm.. fell asleep around 11pm due to loud children outside my window.. woke up at 7:30am. Good sleep last night. I don't think I woke up once until 7:15am and then I took 15 min to convince myself to leave my bed. LOL. Back to eating like normal with no more evil sausage! I can't wait to his the 130's as I haven't been there since 20-21 yrs.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-26T19:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-6443>

@SQ Thanks! When I don't feel like taking a shower/bath I spot with ice packs on my stomach, upper chest/neck, and upper back/neck. I have a removable shower head so it's great for putting the water wherever you want for however long you want. I have enjoyed the showers more than baths and seem to have better success so far with them. But, I do often do both at the same time. I plug the tub and start with the shower part.. putting the water where I want it while the tub is filling.. then when it's about ankle deep I still down in the water while still using the shower head to spray water on my upper body where I want it. Then after my legs are almost covered I will turn off the shower part and let the tub fill the rest of the way from the tap and lay in it for however long.

I enjoy the showering part more as I can still get things very cold yet it doesn't seem to effect my core temp as much. Vs when I'm in a tub of water I'm fine while in it, but when I get out I'm shivering for at least an hour or more. The showering is also helping me adapt my feet without it being too bad. The water still hits them, but since it's not constant like doing it in a bath it's easier when I'm done to warm them up. They also seem to be adapting better this way.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-27T17:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-7030>

**3/27 Update:**

Weight: 143.4

Mood: 66 percent

Went to bed last night at 10:30pm... woke up once or twice during the night not sure when tho.. got up at 7:42am. Felt well rested today even with the wakings.. I think it was because I changed my room a little. I had blacked out the window and it was very dark, but there was still a faint light from my modem that I had covered, but it would still hit the wall some. I covered it more so that last night my room was COMPLETELY black with absolutely no light anywhere. Hopefully it will help my sleep.. but for now I think I'm adjusting. I didn't CT last night.. I didn't feel like it and was really tired from my bad sleep the night before.. so I deferred to my feelings on it.

I'm contemplating starting the post Leptin eating plan in April. I've been on the reset for 8 1/2 wks now. I think I have most of the signs of being LS now. But they are slightly vague so I'm not positive.

Signs I Have

~The skin on my elbows are the best they've ever been

~The skin on the top of my feet is softer than ever.. the bottoms are better.. still a little dry skin on my heels, but I am always barefoot unless I leave the house

~Just this past wk the skin on my face is softer and not dry on my t-zone even after ct.. I don't need to use coconut oil anymore either unless I want to

~My energy is about the same, but I've always slept at least 8 hours ever since I left high school.. only difference is my eyes have stopped feeling so tired when I wake up now

~My sleep is better.. I'm not waking constantly in the night like I used to

~My sweets cravings are basically gone. I still will always love sweets so things still look good, but it's not annoying and super hard anymore to go to the store and not get something sweet. (provided I'm not hungry when I go)

~I still go back and forth b/w eating 2 or 3 meals a day.. I am still hungry by lunch usually, but when I eat lunch I don't need dinner. I still usually choose to "have dinner" in the form of hot tea w/ crm.. but that is more because I want something with flavor and not out of any hunger.

~I've been losing weight and inches the entire time since I started.

I still need to check my recovery from workouts. I've done one 10 min pole dance session and I wasn't sore the next day, but I wasn't always sore before that either. So, I'm slightly confused about what constitutes a "better recovery" time?? If I over train for example.. I haven't worked out in months and then lift a lot of weights or do a bodyrock workout I could be sore for 3-4 days... but is that too long when obviously I'm out of shape. If I'm consistently working out I am "sore" for 1-2 days normally. So, what is better? I imagine that being sore at least a day is normal as your muscles were "injured" and now are repairing.. am I wrong? IDK, I guess I will do a bodyrock workout and see if I'm sore at all since I really haven't worked out in the last 2 months.. If not then I guess I'll have my answer.

Went to bed (late) at 12am.. asleep by 12:30.. got up at 7:55am. Sleep was pretty crap again last night with quite a bit of waking up. Feeling a little tired, but not too bad. Thinking about maybe trying to push the CT more by doing it twice a day instead of once. Hoping maybe I will adapt faster that way. I have the time to do it..I just haven't wanted to yet. Hoping it will also fix my sleep faster. Weirdly enough, this sleep pattern lately is what happened when I first started the LeptinRx and changed my sleep times so drastically. ### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-30T19:09:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-8843>

**3/30 Update:**

Weight: 143.8

Mood: 65 percent

Went to bed last night at 11pm.. fell asleep within 15 min.. got up at 7:50am. Last night I finally slept a little bit better.. so I'm not as tired today. Still woke up about twice I think. Just took my first shower/bath of the day at 50 degrees for 35 min. Today is the start of my two a day CT plan. Hopefully this will speed up my progress of cold adaptation and weight loss/strength gain.  Last night I had my roommates leftover steak and steamed veggies. So, that's cool.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-04-04T17:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-11275>

**4/4 Update:**

Weight: 142.4

Mood: 82 percent

Well, I've obviously been slacking on this journal. LOL. April 1st I started the Post Leptin way of eating. It's going really well. Actually better than the Reset I think. I'm now eating when I get up.. usually b/w 7-8:30 am and then I am actually not as hungry as I was on the reset even with the less protein.. so then I've actually been skipping lunch lately and going with an early dinner around 4:30-5:30pm. I was able to change the food around from last month some.. which is good for my sanity. LOL. I'm still eating almost the same things every day, but w/ a few more options.

List of food for April:

Burger patties (often made with grassfed liver and onions in them)

Tilapia

Chicken

Only about 6 cans of tuna for this entire month

1 can of Mackeral (I still need to try it and make sure I like it)

1 tin of Kippers (also need to try to make sure I like it)

Mushrooms

Broccoli

Roasted Red Peppers

Lots of butter

Mayo

Cream cheese

Some heavy cream in my tea most days

Green and black tea all day long

1-2 Brazil nuts a day

Spices of course

~I added the mushrooms for some b-vitamins and becuz I love them.

~I added broccoli for Vitamin C and because I needed something that would help fill me up more becuz I don't have enough money to buy as much meat as I'd like.

I also was able to get K2 and some B complex vitamins this month. Yay!! I also found some Vitamin C from the dollar store.. so I've been taking 1 tablet that has 500 mg in it. Not sure on how good they are considering where they're from, but I have the broccoli to help too.

I'm still eating keto even with the added veggies. I don't count always.. but I roughly added it up and I get anywhere from 5-15 carbs a day. Also, from my previous experiences going keto.. using the ketostix I've found that after I've been keto for a couple days it takes quite a bit of carbs to pull me out of it. I've eaten like 50g a carbs and I was still showing a decent amount of ketones. So, I think I'm good even with the veggies.

I have been slacking on the CT though. I haven't done any CT since Friday. I really need to get back to it. I'm going to try to force myself to do it today. I know what the issue is and it has nothing to do with the cold part. That doesn't really bother me. I just have a weird quirk that is getting in my way lately. But, I have just started losing weight again after being stuck for like 10-15 days. So, I want to push it as much as I can before I start my period again and am stuck for another 10-15 days.  Sometimes being a chick really fucking sucks. Stupid hormones! Men have it so fucking easy it's annoying as hell. LOL.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-04-05T20:56:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lovelifes-journey.260/post-11830>

**4/5 Update:**

Weight: 142

Mood: 87 percent

Went to bed last night at 10pm.. fell asleep within 30 min.. got up at 8:30am. Well, last night was a little better. I woke up twice I think during the night .. which is getting better. I did wake up wide awake at 5:30am. Which I just refuse to get up that early LOL. So I kind of laid around fading in and out until 6:30-7am.. then fell back asleep and woke up at 8:30am. Last night I did a 35 min CT shower. I also did some bodyweight exercises. I don't have weights and I'm more into functional fitness. So, I will probably be doing my own workouts or bodyrock workouts or pole dancing for my "weight lifting" days.. and then hooping and hopefully 1 day for sprints. I figure it's time to start working out again since I'm on the Post Leptin Rx now and it seems to be going well. I'm often still eating three meals, but I don't think it's a big deal. My calories are still probably around only 1300/day. Which is pretty low. I do seem to notice a trend that I lose weight on the day right after I've done some kind of workout. So, hopefully this will help me since I stalled out for quite some time there.

I don't think I've been this low of a weight since I was about 22 yrs old! I'm excited. I can't wait to hit the 130's though!

I will say that yesterday I did some planks and some squats. I did some reg squats and some plie ones. Like I used to do in ballet. I'm thinking of finding some ballet exercises online to refresh me on my dancing again. I had great legs from being a dancer for a few yrs. I really like their exercises. This must be what becoming LS means.. because I am not even sore. My legs feel a little tired.. like where I can tell I used them quite a bit, but not sore in any way. So that's awesome too.

# Thread: Anyone tracking body composition on CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BenG — 2012-03-18T10:57:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-2320>

Hi all,

Has anyone been tracking their body composition on CT? I was losing about 3lbs a week just doing Leptin reset, but since starting CT, my weight has nearly stalled (losing about .5-1lb a week), but my clothes keep getting loser. I'm figuring I'm either holding water, or increased lean/muscle weight. Anyone noticed similar findings? I just find it hard to swallow that I'm gaining lean weight on only about 1500kcal a day (and I'm full on that) from only 4 weeks of CT.

Thanks.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-18T11:45:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-2328>

Haven't tracked any body composition, but I have lost inches around my stomach (2 to be precise) and have gained 5lbs :/ Happy about the inches lost, and wondering what the gain is?

### Inger — 2012-03-18T13:12:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-2336>

I have not lost anything.. I have been doing CT 2,5 weeks now. Only showers as I have no tub.

I swear my breast have gone smaller (and they are already quite small eh) and I have lost fat on my torso, even if I have almost no fat there.. I can see my ribs now. I am waiting to loose the fat on my arms and legs though..

But really, I was sure I had lost some weight, but when I stepped on the scale today - all the same.

Strange ideed. I have been eating really much though, but only the good stuff. I guess around 1500-2000 calories/day LOT of fat and seafoods.

I am 5,4 and 119 pounds, 35 yo.

Inger

### taryn.ourso@gmail.com — 2012-03-24T01:14:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-5213>

> **BenG;2327 wrote:** Hi all,
>
>
>
> Has anyone been tracking their body composition on CT? I was losing about 3lbs a week just doing Leptin reset, but since starting CT, my weight has nearly stalled (losing about .5-1lb a week), but my clothes keep getting loser. I'm figuring I'm either holding water, or increased lean/muscle weight. Anyone noticed similar findings? I just find it hard to swallow that I'm gaining lean weight on only about 1500kcal a day (and I'm full on that) from only 4 weeks of CT.
>
>
>
> Thanks.

Yes! I have gained 3-4 pounds in about 3 weeks. I do some baths, some soaks in the pool, and spot CT with ice packs almost every night. I don't feel like I've been doing anything extreme at all, but I have lost body fat for sure. Down to about 28% from 31%. This is welcome as I have gotten to the point where I don't really need to lose weight, but I do need to increase muscle and lose fat. I don't do any cardio. I do a few sets of body weight squats, chinups, and pushups after CT every few nights. I'm pretty impressed. I'm actually seeing more definition and I feel I haven't done that much!!! I'll take it. I'll keep you posted.

### Cody — 2012-03-24T01:32:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-5230>

I've gained about 8 pounds, but that was coming off a very calorie restricted diet. My waist has gotten smaller and my muscles have gotten noticeably larger. Good stuff.

### Kpcst — 2012-03-24T03:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-5278>

I am. Had a dexa scan last week. Plan on doing CT 60-90 days then having another.... We shall see.

KP

### Glamorama — 2012-03-26T02:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-tracking-body-composition-on-ct.262/post-6154>

Yep I am. I get my BF percentage checked every 4-8 weeks. Scvales much the same, but less fat over the boobs, belly and hips, while muscle and strenght is rapidly increasing.

# Thread: The Sleep Thread FFTA

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 65
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-18T13:36:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2338>

Here's where we celebrate and comiserate our previous night's sleep.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-18T13:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2339>

I'll go first. Last night, I slept like a rock for the first time since starting CT, and I feel great!

### Phoenix — 2012-03-18T16:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2374>

I'm exactly the opposite. Since starting CT I'm up hot flashing between 2 and 4 every night. I try to get 8 hours, but CT is only letting me get 6 or less. Makes for an exhausting day. I'm staying with it though ... CT must be affecting my hormones, and I'm hoping that it will eventually level off. I'm 55 and think it may be something menpausal women are going to go through with CT.

Anybody else have this experience?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-18T17:00:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2385>

> **Phoenix;2381 wrote:** I'm exactly the opposite. Since starting CT I'm up hot flashing between 2 and 4 every night. I try to get 8 hours, but CT is only letting me get 6 or less. Makes for an exhausting day. I'm staying with it though ... CT must be affecting my hormones, and I'm hoping that it will eventually level off. I'm 55 and think it may be something menpausal women are going to go through with CT.
>
>
>
> Anybody else have this experience?

I had that within the first week of starting CT. It lasted another couple of weeks and then stopped. I was so glad because it was interfering with sleep. It's your hormones moderating but they have to go through the wonky stage first. I jsut found out I horribly estrogen dominant with estradiol at over 500 so continuing on with CT and letting the hormones do what they are going to do....hopefully leave!

### Lyndra — 2012-03-18T17:44:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2392>

I'm waking up every 1-1/2 hours feeling warm. It feels like the hot flashes I experienced when I wasn't taking hormones but a bit milder. I feel surprisingly good during the day considering how poorly I'm sleeping at night. I'm really hoping this will pass soon. I've been doing cold baths for 11 days now.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-18T19:05:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2412>

> [Lyndra said:](/goto/post?id=2399)
>
> I'm waking up every 1-1/2 hours feeling warm. It feels like the hot flashes I experienced when I wasn't taking hormones but a bit milder. I feel surprisingly good during the day considering how poorly I'm sleeping at night. I'm really hoping this will pass soon. I've been doing cold baths for 11 days now.\[/QUOTE
>
>
>
> Your experience almost mirrors mine. I am soon to be 59 and have been postmenopausal for about eleven years (not sure exactly how many years). I took bio-identical hormones for 10 yrs, but I have been off of them now for about a year. I have had only seven CT sessions so far in the tub and about seven icing session (burnt my upper arm the first time I tried it - stupid). CT has not yet improved my sleep pattern. I take my magnesium at night along with three melatonin under the tongue. It has always has, and still does, take me 1.5 - 2.0 every night to get to sleep. I think perhaps I am stressing too much about getting to sleep, and that I need to not worry so much about it. I too wake up as if I were having a small hot flash most nights now. I believe that has only begun since I started CT. I notice that in the evenings before, I retire, that my cheeks are a bit warm when I don't remember them warming up in the evenings before CT. I generally take my tub in the mid-afternoon.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-18T19:08:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-2414>

Susan M. :>)

### fitness@home — 2012-03-20T02:57:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3051>

Another postmenopausal woman here. I am still relatively early on in my CT. Have done face baths for about a week and used ice packs on abdomen & waist. Last night I did a 35 minute soak in the tub (tap water only) followed by ice packs for another 30 minutes.

Slept like a zombie  It was great...and doesn't happen very often for me.

I know there are detox and hormonal symptoms coming my way as I get more serious using colder water/ice in the baths. It helps reading about everyone else's experiences.

### mycrazycartoons@gmail.com — 2012-03-20T15:44:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3221>

> **Phoenix;2381 wrote:** I'm exactly the opposite. Since starting CT I'm up hot flashing between 2 and 4 every night. I try to get 8 hours, but CT is only letting me get 6 or less. I'm 55 and think it may be something menpausal women are going to go through with CT.
>
>
>

I'm 52 and also had major hot flashing in the middle of the night and trouble sleeping after CT, but the past two nights have been much better and I've actually slept better than I have in years--without ibuprofen (been off that for 2 weeks) and melatonin (actually sleep better without it) I'm hoping my hormones are stabilizing to optimal. I'm finding that about 30 minutes after CT in the evening, I become extremely drowsy and am in bed by 8:30.. Anyone else get really tired after CT?

### Syl — 2012-03-20T17:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3257>

I'm finding that about 30 minutes after CT in the evening, I become extremely drowsy and am in bed by 8:30.. Anyone else get really tired after CT?

sometimes i feel tired cause my muscles seem to constricting harder toward the last minutes of my 30 minute tub soak and it actually feels like a work out (i'm beginning to see six pack  ) other times it clears my head when i can study my butt off (i'm a college student  ). i guess it all depends on where everyone is on the hormone balance scale and omega 3 ratio (and propably other factors too).

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T21:49:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3464>

> **PaleoNana;3244 wrote:** Anyone else get really tired after CT?

I've noticed that I get crushed at night. Not so much being right after CT though, but more of a "since I started CT" thing. It's giving me a bit funky sleep cycles. I'm assuming things are working through, but as an example, last Sunday afternoon, I decided to take a short nap because I was feeling a bit tired and woke up 13 hours later (no CT that day)...

### chocolate — 2012-03-20T23:08:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3520>

Does anyone notice if wearing the blue blockers has a cumulative effect, just like CT? It seems to me that it does, but I am in the Mojave Desert and we have the most intense UV in the world. I wonder if anyone else has noticed, or if it is just placebo? It seems to be doing something.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-20T23:37:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3535>

In general my sleep is better with CT. CT has worked best for me when I can do it mid-afternoon. To warm up after, I throw my kettle-bell around for awhile. Then I have the rest of the day to warm up and I sleep well.

When I do it too close to bedtime, or go to bed kind of chilled...no bueno. I toss and turn trying to get warm.

A few times, I've really slept well and pop outa bed before my alarm. Like I said, a few times...most of the time it's sheer force of will to get up.

I know I need longer, higher quality sleep. That's one of my main hacks to do.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T23:40:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3538>

No sleep again last night. Zero. It was the fact that I had 85% choc at 7.30 pm. Too much caffeine for my poor brain to shut down. Not even CT helped.

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-20T23:45:34+0200

### Mikey7 — 2012-03-21T02:08:58+0200

### Glamorama — 2012-03-21T02:10:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3656>

I'm starting yellow lenses glasses soon. Sick of my unpredictable insomnia. I think it is a sideffect from CT, it's slowly easing.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-03-21T04:44:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-3767>

CT on a Friday night can knock me out within half an hour of getting out of the tub. somehow the CT allows me to let go of the accumulated stress from the week and I am hard pressed to keep my eyes open

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-22T12:08:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4294>

So far I have been sleeping much better, fewer waking episodes during the night, and no need to pee during the night either. I have been completely exhausted by about 9 in the evening and, by the time I've done the evening chores and get to bed, I'm asleep by 10 and up again feeling refreshed around 7 in the morning. But, if I do my cold soak after about 5.00 p.m., then it's no sleep for me.

### MartiD — 2012-03-22T14:01:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4306>

I agree..can't do it to late in evening. Last night for some reason I was shivering for awhile after and I haven't done that in week. The dog also woke me up at 2:00 am. I have slept really well with CT but it also improved dramatically with Leptin RX in October. Does anyone take Melatonin?

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-22T14:20:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4312>

> **Mikey7;3677 wrote:** I went on a taping spree today. I taped up all my LED lights... it gonna be so dark in my room now. CT plus complete darkness = good night of sleep.

I've done this too. I also have the tv and dvd player plugged into a socket that is turned off by a light switch on the wall, so I just flip that switch at night, and turn everything off. I'm still looking for a good "blackout curtain" hack, since there's some artificial light still getting in from my blinds.

My sleep has been unpredictable, like most of you. Last night was good, though, but the night before was rough.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-22T14:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-4317>

This was the first time I didn't wait for a period from bath to bed. I woke up hot like usuaul - but getting to sleep in the first place was a struggle. I probably won't do that again. I also had some cramps in my toes.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-26T06:39:48+0300

I function quite well, but I think deep sleep is essential for healing and further fat loss, because thats where I seem to stall!

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-26T12:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-6279>

### eva — 2012-03-26T13:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-6287>

sleep is around 8 h no problems - up once to pee... getting rid of the blanket also. one problem - i have always slept long on my days off - like 10-12 hours. now im up around 8 hours sharp. been wakeing up 7.30ish on weekends. stupid.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-26T15:25:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-6297>

Ivy

I've just recently hit the wake up btwn 1-3am I'm either HOT, have to pee, completely WIDE AWAKE, or just wondering why I can't get back to sleep...

I do my CT mid-morning (Btwn 830-10am depending on the day) When I CT At night it takes for ever for me to go to sleep...

### quelsen — 2012-03-26T15:51:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-6305>

I am taking 80mg of melatonin nightly to help my sleep.

why 80...

I found out from a vet that Melatonin is one of their first lines against hormone imbalances in estrogen dominant dogs.

then some extra research indicated to me that melatonin works to regulate estrogen before it works on sleep....

so i titrated up to a good nights sleep at 80mg

[http://www.pureencapsulations.com/itemdy00.asp?t1=me21](http://http://www.pureencapsulations.com/itemdy00.asp?t1=me21)

### differentstory — 2012-03-28T18:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-7747>

I feel for you guys!!

Just wanted to add my two cents and say that I am having a weird CT-sleep thing that I was curious if anyone else is having.

My sleep has not been screwed up. I do think I was getting a week of rough sleeping at first because my dreaming was in overdrive. And, of course, they were all bad, stressful dreams where I forgot to pay bills or failed all of my classes (haven't been in school in years, go figure).

I am hoping that CT/Leptin Rx helps my sleep problem. I am a 9-hour-er. I have to sleep almost exactly 9 hours where others around me feel great after 7 or 8 hours. I am also not much of a morning person. I have a hard time turning off the lights before midnight and don't really get going until noon. What I would give to wake up at 5am after only 7 hours of sleep!!!

Currently, I am having these "blackouts" when I sleep where I almost don't even remember going to sleep and I have no "feeling" of time passing! For instance, last night I noticed I was pretty tired. After CT that ended at 9pm, I felt like I could close my eyes and go to sleep at any second. But, of course, tv got the best of me and I stayed up watching it until 11:30. At 11:30, I finally gave in and I remember turning off the TV, rolling over, and. . .that's it! The next time I open my eyes its 3:30 in the morning and I'm hot.

It might not seem like much but it reminds me of anesthesia (if you've ever been put under) and I am experiencing this everyday now. I'm wondering if its just because I am so exhausted from the CT? I am not in good shape in the least. I've had other instances of this too where I get sleepy, close my eyes, open them again and it's 3 hours later. It's such a weird feeling (apparently hard to describe too!).

I know this is definitely a CT thing as this started a week after the crazy dreams.

I have been CT'ing (full emersion) for two weeks now. Icing one week before that.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-28T20:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-thread-ffta.264/post-7838>

Has anyone noticed they are having more/remembering more dreams since staring the leptin RX and CT?

I am sleeping mostly very well these days. I had suffered insomnia for years, and had mostly fixed it with the occasional blip before starting the leaky gut rx. But I was still tired all the time (likely from all the sugar and junk I was eating). But 2-3 days a week I would wake up at 4 a.m. and not be able to go back to sleep.

Since starting the RX and CT, my sleep has been very sound. I fall asleep quickly, which is a nice change. I am still waking up to use the restroom at night, but I fall right back asleep. I use a Phillips light that dims gradually at night and rises gradually in the morning, and I almost always wake up before the audio alarm.

But I'm having so many vivid dreams now. It's pretty wild. I can't ever remember having this many dreams.

The chickens were upset that they hadn't been fed. And I actually took a long nap Friday and Saturday evening, something I haven't done since my carb-crazed days years ago. The naps didn't keep me from zombie-like sleep at night though.

I usually sleep pretty well. When I went on the Leptin Reset, for the first time I started waking before the radio. That was amazing to me. Then I also started CT, which made me start waking up in the middle of the night, usually for just a few minutes. But I still got fairly good sleep. Now this change. Just thinking about it makes me want to take a nap!

I don't know what is in store for me. I've still got a rash on my right hand, still haven't lost weight to speak of, but my arms muscles are looking good, and the tummy fat is leaving, and even my thighs are getting squishy. I feel spectacular. No way I'm stopping CT, but I'm going to have to start buying more ice! My multitude of ice cube trays just can't keep up with the tap water temp increases.

# Thread: My Unintentional, Unknowing Experience with the Leptin Rx
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-unintentional-unknowing-experience-with-the-leptin-rx.265/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2012-03-18T14:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-unintentional-unknowing-experience-with-the-leptin-rx.265/post-2351>

I've been on the Leptin Rx for 6 weeks and have been doing CT for 2 weeks. Not much success so far. However, back in November, before I had become aware of the Dr. Jack, I think I had an unintentional run-in with the Leptin Rx and CT. I will note my observations after the narrative:

Back in November of 2011 I was vacationing with the family. During the first week, nightly temps were in the low 40s at best. Durnig the day it would hit the low to mid 60s. Our accommodation was rustic: there was not heating other than a space heater and showering was done essentially outdoors. So despite taking a warm shower, there was constant cold otherwise.

During the second 2 weeks, the temps increased a bit, but nights were still pretty cold. Throughout this time, we would just wear jackets during the day to stay comfortable and would bundle under heavy covers at night.

Interestingly, I was very inactive this entire time. Because of an autoimmune flare and a ligament pull, I was not able to really exercise much. The first week I walked for 3 hours one day. The next week I did some kettlebells and squats once. The last week I did about 8 sprints. That's it.

In terms of eating, I was downing immense amounts of fatty meat and eating salad. I added in a small amount of seasonal fruit in the last week.

So to summarize, it was very cold, with no real indoor heating. I ate ton of protein, fat and leaves. And I slept for as long as I wanted and sat around most of the day with exception of a few bouts of exercise and small amounts of light walking.

The results were amazing. Pounds were just flying off. My pants were fitting so loose it was unbelievable. In the pictures I took during that period, I look like a different person.

Since returning, and discovering Dr. Jack, things have not gone so well. Weight is up, I'm stalled out, and have even experienced cravings. So here are my observations of my unintentional Leptin Rx w/CT:

\- Being naturally exposed to cold all day for extended periods is maybe superior to trying to "dose" yourself with cold each day through CT.

\- Being fully and completely ketogenic may be a game changer. And the carbs some of us may be counting out each day on the Leptin Rx may lead us unwittingly out of ketosis.

\- By exerising once a week, I probably allowed my body to reap the benefit of full adaptation to the exercise stimulus.

\- Sleeping to as much as your body seems to want is probably a very good thing. Even though we try, many of us probably don't sleep as much as we should, and the quality of sleep during a normal night vs. a vacation night is probably not as good.

\- Stress can be a game changer. Sure, I was sick and not feeling well, but I had no responsibilities other than restnig and trying to feel better. I bet that helped me.

Anyway, just my experience. Thought it migth be helpful.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-25T02:54:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-unintentional-unknowing-experience-with-the-leptin-rx.265/post-5652>

Very interesting and so true about your quality of sleep. I know how essential it is yet in this crazy and hectic modern world we all live in it's one of the most challenging aspects of this journey to optimal.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: How to apply Crossfit properly
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 33
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bernadette — 2012-03-18T18:06:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-2398>

This morning Dr Kruse posted this comment on Facebook

> Cross fit is ok if applied properly. In my view it rarely is. So it can be dangerous.

I am well aware of the pitfalls of Crossfit and how people can push themselves too far with it... I was interested in any expanded thoughts Dr K might have on the subject, such as what it looks like properly applied

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T05:22:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-2610>

I have no idea I would imagine it means no more than twice weekly and only in short bursts?

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-19T19:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-2809>

Excellent question. And I would love the answer as well.

That said, I think CF is changing culturally now that it has the Reebok endorsement and The Games have become a focus. Crossfit is becoming - IMHO - less about functional fitness for every body and more about PR's, performance, competition. The number of people who compete in The Open is high. Yet there is a WOD with Muscle UPs? Seriously. The focus in Crossfit has moved to being the "sport of fitness" and that means competition. That is mentally light years away from joining a group of people to work out together and get into the best shape of your life.

Every box is programmed differently - as it should be - because it should be tailored to the box members. But even within a box there is going to be a variety of athlete levels and it takes a great coach to be able to program for the 40 year old newbie who needs to lose 40 lbs and the 27 year old who does butterfly pullups.

IMHO I think the principles of strength training with weights 2-3 times a week and some sort of metcon/sprinting 1-2 times a week may well be within Dr. K's guidelines. So IF, and it is a big IF you can get than done within the programming of a CF box, great. Otherwise, it is time to pony up and find a personal trainer if you need help with proper form and then start programming your own exercise.

Just my two cents. And I do hope Dr K weighs in on this.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T19:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-2831>

> **cgk;2818 wrote:** Excellent question. And I would love the answer as well.
>
> That said, I think CF is changing culturally now that it has the Reebok endorsement and The Games have become a focus. Crossfit is becoming - IMHO - less about functional fitness for every body and more about PR's, performance, competition. The number of people who compete in The Open is high. Yet there is a WOD with Muscle UPs? Seriously. The focus in Crossfit has moved to being the "sport of fitness" and that means competition. That is mentally light years away from joining a group of people to work out together and get into the best shape of your life.
>
>
>
> Every box is programmed differently - as it should be - because it should be tailored to the box members. But even within a box there is going to be a variety of athlete levels and it takes a great coach to be able to program for the 40 year old newbie who needs to lose 40 lbs and the 27 year old who does butterfly pullups.
>
>
>
> IMHO I think the principles of strength training with weights 2-3 times a week and some sort of metcon/sprinting 1-2 times a week may well be within Dr. K's guidelines. So IF, and it is a big IF you can get than done within the programming of a CF box, great. Otherwise, it is time to pony up and find a personal trainer if you need help with proper form and then start programming your own exercise.
>
>
>
> Just my two cents. And I do hope Dr K weighs in on this.

These are all excellent observations, and I feel I am in the minority with my CF gym. We are the only one I know that is NOT encouraging the entire place to participate in the Open. I want my members safe and injury-free, thank you! I do have individuals who are competition worthy, and they can workout with a rank beginner, but they are doing different workouts. I know (even if they don't) that they have different goals, abilities, injury/medical histories etc. I get pissed sometimes when CF gets this bum rap, 'cause I think, "hey! MY gym isn't like that!" but I know I am a little fish in the big sea. I program 6 weeks at a time. I use periodization in my cycles.

Bernadette, I would find out about local CF gyms, ask if they do their own programming. If they do, good. Ask if they have strength only classes. If they do, good. If they don't, ask if you can only do the strength portions of their workouts. So if it is back squat 3-3-3 followed by a metcon, can you squat and then leave? Would they be willing to write a program for you? Strength plus limited conditioning.

If you are doing CF from home, you should have bumpers, squat rack etc and do a strength program 2-3 x wk depending, with limited conditioning, less than 12 minutes 1-2 x wk

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-21T00:46:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-3593>

My gym is the mountain. There's less MRSA lurking there. Plus I can throw rocks and logs and no one stares.

Most people have this idea in their mind (I know because I used to torture myself through 5-6 triathlons a year) that you have to kill yourself in the gym or run marathons to get rid of your belly, get ripped muscles, etc. However, this exercise paradigm is not true. Body composition is mostly a diet and sleep affair, not gym hours. Mark Sisson says it 80 percent diet and 20 percent exercise. He's probably right. (And who wouldn't want to look like Mark Sisson?) Wink.

If Kruse's CT thing plays out, then exercise plays even less of a role.

Paleo and Cross-fit are kinda linked in a lot of people's minds. This is unfortunate. My sister asked me if she went Paleo, does this mean she has to "go" Cross-fit, too. Umm...hell no.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-22T14:59:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-4332>

> **Bernadette;3733 wrote:** Also, though the attitude of our trainers isn't simply 'make 'em feel like they got the crap kicked out of them', I definitely think there is an underlying expectation that people should be able to do 5 days a week, which I know my body just can't handle.

In my gym, which is small granted, most memebers do 2-3 x week. We do have a few that come 5 days or so, and we (the coaches) take care to ratchet their intenstiy down on certain workouts, so they are not going \*all out\* on every workout. Every few cycles will have them take their intensity down 50% for the entire week. They are usually unwilling to do so at first, then in retrospect, realize how beneficial that time was.

I dislike the "make 'em feel like they got the crap kicked out of them" attitude you mention, and boy, is it prevalent. Some workouts I see just make me cringe. And the YELLING, even berating, from the "coaches". ugh. My philosophy is to make every person who enters my gym feel welcome upon entering and feel about themselves when they leave.

Not like we're a bunch of lotus eating, praise-everybody-who-does-anything, everyone-gets-a-finisher's-medal kind of place LOL but I see no need to harrass and goad clients into doing more than they can/want/need to do. Basically I took what I percieved to be the big problems from the CF gyms I had attended and did the OPPOSITE of that. I was made to feel inferior for wanting to scale, ignored at times because I wasn't a competitor, and yelled at, in a way that I was supposed to see as "motivational", but had the opposite effect on me.

I wouldn't give up on CF, I'd keep looking Bernadette. Or start building a home gym

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-03-23T14:55:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-4921>

cgk, and that would be about the ONLY reason to move here! This is the most corrupt, expensive, crowded state EVER! Ugh.

.... but I do love the shore ....

And while I agree with Jack about the education, it isn't about a piece of paper from a program or institution. The head coach at the gym has no formal degree ( I have my BS in exercise science) yet has extensively "schooled" himself in physics, chemistry, biology, metabolism, nutrition. And like many other coaches, we have lots of "certifications" but so do other coaches. I guess it comes down to what the coaches goals are, personally and for their clients. I do not expect to "breed" a Games competitor, and I have zero desire to do CF competitions. I don't think that is a healthy option, as a long term goal. (short term, maybe) I'm not looking for notoriety as a "bad-ass coach" who makes up these ridiculously difficult workouts. If I'm honest, I guess I want people to say "wow, that programming is solid" or "sheesh, her people are strong" And as far as my clients, I want them to remain injury free, lead healthy low stress lives, and have a balanced perspective on their workouts: as in, not every workout is gonna be a homerun, nor does it need to be. PRs are fun, but aren't the be all/end all of training. (hmmm, maybe I need to remind myself of that LOL)

Anyway, cgk, if you did meander up to NJ for any reason; ie. passing through on a road trip 'cause why would anyone make it a destination (hee hee), certainly drop in. Love to have you.

### Leevy13 — 2015-06-28T22:52:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-169788>

I'm glad I found this thread. CrossFit changed my life and I have a real passion to continue doing it. However, I want to be smart about it. The owner at my box put it best. He said (and I'm paraphrasing), You don't have to come in here and kill yourself every day. You may go hard for two days in a row, then come in on the third day and only workout with the PVC pipe. You want to work on proper technique and mobility. We don't want to run people off by burning them out or injuring them. That's not the spirit of our box.
I'm far more focused on the functional movement aspect. I want to be as fit as possible, but not at the expense of my joints and definitely not at the expense of my nervous system. I noticed that this is an old thread, so if you guys have any updates or current advice, I would love to hear from you. Thanks!

### JoeBranca — 2015-07-20T20:59:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-172444>

Hi Leevy, not so sure I have 'expertise' but I'll throw in some comments.

I can't speak for any one particular crossfit box, but my sense is in general CF's main goal is exercise-as-competitive-sport. This is not an optimal approach for a general population trying to get themselves out of a hole. Combine that with little emphasis on being connected to nature, and I don't consider that fitness in an evolutionary direction.

Crossfit also promotes itself as preparation for the real world. but in reality IMHO the functional exercises executed for time or volume mainly prepares one for real world strength and conditioning contests, or real world boot camps (military, law enforcement). Of course there will be _carry over_ from CF strength and conditioning and metcons to real world situations (provided you don't get too injured in the process), but a properly executed "functional" movement is not the same thing as a practical movement, which I define as an innately stable movement pattern that optimally and efficiently couples our body plans with real environments, adapatable to whatever imposed demands may come, not just boxes. Many of the kinds of exercises Crossfit workouts consist of for time or volume are best appropriate for met-con addicts who do not plan to get a lot of mileage out of their bodies (examples: olympic lifts for time, barbell deadlifts performed in a pre-exhausted state?).

The real problem I think - philosophically - is approaching physical movement only in context of competition. This is of course not just CF but common in the modern fitness and athletics worlds... interestingly its very rare to non-existent among observed hunter gatherer societies. Technique and movement skill would lend itself either to play (challenging, but not a competitive structure selecting for individual winners) or practical utility, which is dictated by the "conditions of existence".

I'd imagine the rare crossfit gigs "doing it right" would be the ones that are way more serious about movement quality -- that is, focus on technique so that it is safe, energy efficient (focus on making movement as easy as possible for the given task, not feeling destroyed as a performance goal), _and_ performance is good. A true multi-functional approach to fitness, meaning the exercises are making one more _adaptive not just more conditioned._ Perhaps if a CF is bringing in things like yoga as well that would be a good sign they really want to explore movement. And the community spirit isn't built around folks commiserating about smashing their heads against the wall, so to speak, 3-4x / week.

### thomas — 2015-07-21T19:43:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-172536>

I read that some movements in crossfit carry with them a high risk of injury. aka not the way we're designed to move.

Obviously risk of injury depends mostly on the force you apply. which is dependant on mass and acceleration. So if you move really quickly or with a high weight or both it's likely you're gonna injure yourself. Lifting 10kg in half a second is more dangerous than lifting 20kg in 5 seconds.

### JoeBranca — 2015-07-25T00:08:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-172909>

A training mindset is huge and not discussed often enough... training can be a way to self benefit and look down on your fellow man, or it can be a way to self-give and lift up your fellow man. This was posted in a Movnat forum recently:

> I finished level one certification yesterday with Kellen Milad and Erwan in Santa Monica, CA. While walking to get lunch today, I found an elderly homeless man passed out on the sidewalk in the blazing sun, clearly burning deeper his already burned skin. Frankly he was covered in urine and filth, but with Erwan Le Corre's message of love resonating deeply inside my soul, he felt like my brother, my father, my uncle, and my son. I was able to wake and communicate with him just enough to get his permission to allow me to lift and carry him to the safety of shade. Deadlift to Waist Carry became something real, something tangible, something profoundly human for me. Once I set him down and arranged his belongings, he thanked me and honestly I cried in gratitude for this wondrous human body I inhabit.
>
> All of my pushing to be strong as a way to "perfect" myself from the world fell away to become a path towards humanity and kinship with creation. Thank you from the bottom of my heart to the Mov Nat community, to Erwan and to Kellen for inspiring me to be strong to be wholehearted. No one is free until we are all free.

Quite different from this:

[http://therxreview.com/the-uglier-side-of-crossfit/](http://therxreview.com/the-uglier-side-of-crossfit/)

Crossfit is a "sport", its not wellness or health.

When I'm ready to integrate more exercise into my plans, I'm going to be going with this system [http://arxfit.com/](http://arxfit.com/) it is based on Doug Mcguff's Body by Science protocols of superslow intense weight training, but it uses an adaptive resistance motor for you to fight against (potential nnEMF exposure aside) its a fantastic device that allows you to get a full body workout in only eight minutes, it tracks you to make sure you are not over training and on average you should only be doing this once a week. ### René Borg — 2015-08-20T17:33:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-175209>

For those who enjoy MovNat (Erwan's work) also have a look at Ido Portal's work. I attended his weekend workshop after doing my MovNat Trainer education and while it is quite different it is a fantastic system for learning 'Movement' as a language. He calls it 'Movement X' (Movement Culture) and he is a remarkable teacher. What his students achieve in terms of moving with grace and power is impressive.

Ido has a good axiom: 'the arms manipulate, the legs locomote, the spine organises' and this informs a lot of his approach on how to get the best out of each subsystem. Its a heuristic of course, so imperfect, but as a rule of thumb it has served me really well with clients especially in understanding what type of skill training and conditioning is most effective in movements where one of these three areas dominate (i.e. crawling patterns and climbing/pulling is generally best for the upper body/hands, sprinting/running for legs and so on).

If somehow MovNat and Movement X could mate and have a love child we'd be close to a perfect system (knowing we'll never get that...)
