### Danco3636 — 2015-08-22T02:58:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-175353>

> [My anatomy PSA for today: the arms guide, the legs move but the spine organizes all human movement. What organizes the spine? Light water and magnetism that dictates how the neocortex is organized in sensory and motor cortices. Carry on.
>
> Dr. Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse)Many believe that exercise and caffeine can shrink vessels in the subarachnoid space of the brain bathed in CSF.......but what happens to the neocortex is the key to understanding how movement can occur or cannot occur......When the redox potential of the person is optimized or good the cerebrovascular bed also shrinks.......but in most people it gets larger because their redox potential is suboptimal in our modern world (we can see this in functional MRI now)......This action steals electrons from CSF to provide them to the vessels and neural pathways controlling sensory and motor cortices when we exercise.........This is why many with poor redox potentials die, get injured easier, and move improperly when we watch them. This is an epidemic in modern young pro sports. Consider all the injuries that just happened this week in NFL camps. Today we see more young professional athletes get injured because they are all addicted to blue lit video games, carbohydrates 24/7, and excessive training. this is why cross fit now markets its own line or orthotics. It is becoming obvious too all those who are not brain dead and believe modern paradigms.
> When the neocortex struggles for its "electron fix" the result is a headache or poor neural coordination of the motor and sensory systems to generate natural movement. Cross fit build zoo movements. This is why Ido Portal and MovNat people can do things that cross fitters can not do. It has very little to do with your level of fitness and has a lot to do with the movements being congruent with your CNS and PNS design. This is why how Ido and Erwan move is rarely seen in the modern world. Some people can increase electron flux via coffee or exercise in an indoor lit crossfire box.....hence why they are drawn to use it, but this does not mean it is optimal or ideal. The underlying biophysics tells us the environment they exist in and do these things are the real problem..........they ignore and injuries result. Pills and surgery can't fix environmental deficits ever.

### René Borg — 2015-08-26T13:15:34+0300

Great summary Jack. A question related to my question on the Achilles thread: if light, water and magnetism are the main drivers of proper motor control because they setup the 'winning terrain' for the spine, where do you see motor skill relearning.

I suppose I am asking:

1\. Would you: Setup the 'winning terrain' (body in tune with the three natural laws) and then start rehabilitating the patient's motor skill through techniques (such as Ido Portal's micro exploration of the spinal movements etc.)
2\. Or would you: just Setup the winning terrain and then simply encourage natural lifestyle in the confidence the body will reorganise its own movement in the ideal way now that the 'pipe is clean'

### Dan E — 2016-04-27T02:49:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-apply-crossfit-properly.266/post-193633>

I think I can add some valuable thoughts to this thread. I had the fortunate experience of living near one of the best Crossfit boxes on Long Island and began training there in 2011. I gained some valuable knowledge that lead me from one amazing teacher to the next. I will list them so you can check them out.

It was love at first sight as I walked through the door. It was a gym with almost nothing in it. A big room of pull-up bars, gymnastic rings, climbing ropes and squat racks; the least amount of equipment of any gym I had ever seen. I had studied weight lifting for almost 20 years, and this seemed to begin my education. I learned about the proper mechanics of running, jumping, squating, rowing, gymnastics, mobility, recovery, you name it, and the coaching was excellent. Within 6 months my lungs and heart were in the best shape of my life and my physique was catching up also.

These discoveries also had a dark side. The more I trained, the more I needed to. I think this is the slippery slope of dopamine deficiency. After a while, I only felt alright when I had the rush of endorphins after a workout, and so I never wanted to miss a day. We were advised to train three times per week max but I'd often double that. My coffee consumption rose to an all time high. I did incur many injuries as the sport is often criticized for, I think it was more age related. The ideas discussed here on this site would be a godsend to the community because they are truly looking for optimal.

There are many positive aspects and there are many unintended consequences. If you can find the right coaching, there is a huge advantage as you can learn a ton without having to pay a personal trainer. These coaches can and should give you homework including books and video tutorials.

Kelly Starrett mobility wod
[http://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLB67wXqPqtMcNVA-UndNUy7bahGJzLQbT](http://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLB67wXqPqtMcNVA-UndNUy7bahGJzLQbT)

Jagroops mobility class

Brian MacKenzie
[http://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKWVooneqr-uPZleSPxUHFIpB0eljK2a\_](http://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKWVooneqr-uPZleSPxUHFIpB0eljK2a_)

Mark Rippetoe
[http://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNhFKPjedRnQ0fZAf1v\_K6uCPICapzzJc](http://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNhFKPjedRnQ0fZAf1v_K6uCPICapzzJc)

Dr Romanov pose method running
[https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLGE4JfVVA0XzotFjh3hKw1Osb33aELoy6](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLGE4JfVVA0XzotFjh3hKw1Osb33aELoy6)

I can't tell you how much I learned from their books and videos. Crossfit might go off the rails when it involves intense competition but there's no pressure to get involved. Like in any sport, recovery is paramount, and if you don't know, you learn the hard way. I also made many friends and acquaintances with a positive and healthy outlook. As an adult where can you find people like that?

I have left the gym and since I have learned so much about light, I have taken my [www.ironmaster.com](http://www.ironmaster.com)
dumbbells and kettlebell to the woods, beach or high school track to do interval training based on what I learned at Crossfit. It's not as fun as training with a bunch of people but until I drag someone out there with me, it will be just me and the sun throwing weight around. I really like the movenat idea and will be searching that out in the future also. If lifting doesn't appeal to you but you want to get rid of aches and pains, look into Kelly Starett and Jagroop, they will change your life.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Can you eat too much liver?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-18T18:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2407>

What do you think? Is it possible to eat too much liver? I seriously love the stuff and have been going through about a pound every 2-3 days, so roughly 5-7 oz a day.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-18T22:14:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2458>

I think it's possible to each too much anything, no matter how good it is for you. I suppose the questions are why are you eating so much? Do you need it? (If it's a K2 issue, can you do testing?) Do you need something else instead/with? As long as you're feeling you've got good answers to your questions, I'm guessing it's fine. If something doesn't add up, keep digging.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-18T22:19:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2460>

I'm going to have the osteocalcin test this week so I guess that will tell me if it's a K2 issue. Because it's so high in several vitamins it just made me wonder if I should be concerned about any vitamin toxicity issues with too much. I know too much of a good thing tends to be bad, but I don't know what that limit would be with liver. I'm probably overthinking it . . .

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T00:57:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2497>

You should be very careful with liver, as its high in copper and Vit A. You are eating a lot of it. I read somewhere about suggested weekly amount but I can't rememeber now... but yes, be careful!

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-19T03:21:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2549>

yes, you can eat too much liver. also depends on what animal's liver you're eating. Chicken liver is low in vitamins compared to turkey, which is sky high (vitamin A) or beef (copper issue)

maybe consider spectra cell nutrient analysis to see if there's a deficiency underlying

Our bodies can handle way more vitamin A than most people think... I was taking 50,000 a day for months to combat PMS ala WAPF style... but I was also watching for toxicity symptoms. Never got any. Just got better. Make sure you get equivalent amounts of vitamin D if you get a lot of A.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-19T15:29:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-2715>

It is beef liver that I tend to devour, so I guess I should be concerned about copper toxicity. At my last appt, my doctor suggested getting the NutraEval from Genova. I had it done a year ago too - very comprehensive info on nutrient status. It looks very similar to the Spectracell. Anyone familiar with the NutraEval? The good thing is I would only be responsible for $169 of it and my insurance covers the rest. I don't think they'll cover any of the Spectracell.

### thomasmite996 — 2012-03-28T11:09:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-eat-too-much-liver.267/post-7643>

I thing you have to show serious in eating very things . liver is very important and sensitive part of our body. It have need care and look after. eat all those items which have essential vitamins and low cholesterol level.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin.270/post-2432>

Well, this is a switch. After starting to cold adapt, I'm suddenly getting dry skin. It's very weird for me since I usually eat "enough fat to make Dr Oz cry" . Is this a passing phase on my way to optimal.?

### L8F — 2012-03-18T22:05:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin.270/post-2453>

Yes, passing. I got this too. Some of it has to do for me with being submerged in water which is drying...

> **L8F;2460 wrote:** Yes, passing. I got this too. Some of it has to do for me with being submerged in water which is drying...

Yes the cold water seems to be drying. I've been using coconut oil on my face and lips and love it !! Much better than expensive and chemical laced creams.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-19T00:44:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin.270/post-2487>

Yes, I went sticky then dry then now sticky again. Its like my skin is havinga major freak-out. I think its detox and cold adaptation happening.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-19T05:05:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin.270/post-2600>

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-19T05:21:19+0200

My skin was already amazingly soft and smooth since I was following the GAPS diet for 2 years. But it's become even more silky since starting CT. My hair is less oily, I don't have to wash it as often, but my face and my back are much more greasy. yuk.

Weird stuff this CT.

# Thread: Hot Tub While CT???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Myree67 — 2012-03-18T21:48:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2448>

Ok so I don't know if anyone has the answer but my hubby got our hot tub hooked up last night and going. I've been sitting in it because it gives me so much relief from the chronic pain. Do you think this will hurt my CT? I don't have a bath tub so can't actually take a cold bath. I've been using home made gel packs that are colder than ice packs on my low back, tummy and tricep area because my upper arms have HUGE bat wings! I'm also doing the cold face dunk in the bathroom sink.

### L8F — 2012-03-18T22:04:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2452>

Most people, me included, have been answered that the hot defeats the cold. Cold should help chronic pain, especially if coupled with diet, but it is not as INITIALLY as relaxing feeling. The goal is as cold as possible as much as possible once acclimated per the instructions and building up period. Safety first....

### Myree67 — 2012-03-19T09:34:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2665>

Thanks... I'm only going to use it when I'm hurting alot more than usual... it's still freezing at night here so I made sure that I stood naked outside afterwards for several minutes and then came into the house that was 65° I get up every hour and go outside with a light weight jacket on and stay out for 10 minutes... it's only 30° out there tonight but the cold actually felt good on my legs... this is pretty difficult for me since I'm hypothyroid and have a really hard time getting warm once I'm chilled so I'm having to start slow unfortunately...

### Myree67 — 2012-03-19T09:39:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2666>

Eek my degree symbols didn't format lol it's 65 degrees F in the house and 30 degrees outside... it was a balmy 50 degrees F today in the heat of the day and I'm running around in thin jeans and a sweatshirt on... tonight I did use a jean jacket lol... I really hate this cold stuff but I'll do it if it gets me out of pain and healthy!!!

### AKMan — 2012-03-19T13:06:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-2680>

> **Myree67;2673 wrote:** Eek my degree symbols didn't format lol it's 65 degrees F in the house and 30 degrees outside... it was a balmy 50 degrees F today in the heat of the day and I'm running around in thin jeans and a sweatshirt on... tonight I did use a jean jacket lol... I really hate this cold stuff but I'll do it if it gets me out of pain and healthy!!!

We have a hot tub. We have been keeping it at 102 deg. I was feeling guilty about using it, but it does help warm you up when you are chilled to the core. Last week, I lowered it to 90 degrees and it feels pretty good.

In theory, any temperature less than body temp is considered 'cold'. I'd say that if you have a hot tub, use it on days you don't CT or at least 6-8 hours before of after you CT. The magic of CT seems to come from activating the body's cold receptors in the skin and you don't have to be cold and miserable all the time for it to work.

Unless you die, your body temp will be around 98 degrees, you can't change that no matter how much you CT or HT (hot tub).

### Myree67 — 2012-03-21T04:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-while-ct.271/post-3752>

Thanks alot! I tried the cold shower today and actually got it down all the way brrrr we are on well water and it's pretty cold this time of year!

# Thread: From Alaska to Florida

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6

### AKMan — 2012-03-19T01:24:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-alaska-to-florida.274/post-2507>

So, I'm in Tampa for 2 weeks for some job training. It was -20 when I left Fairbanks yesterday and 82 in Tampa. Last night, I set the thermostat in the hotel room to 60, the lowest it would go and woke up nice and chilly! I took a nice, cold shower and spent the day at the beach soaking up some free vitamin D. I had 12 raw oysters for lunch and stopped at the local grocery store for sardines for meals for all week.

I'll be curious to see how this compares with playing in -20 temps without many clothes on.

I saw an ice machine down the hallway I will probably hit later for some ice-pack time!

Later

# Thread: High Blood Pressure and CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoanneA — 2012-03-19T01:39:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2511>

I asked Dr. K if CT was ok if you have high blood pressure, he said yes, but since it was on his facebook page i did not want to spam it with anymore questions, can anyone here say more about CT and high blood pressure?

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-19T02:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2523>

What kind of questions do you have? I have high blood pressure and I'm doing CT, but I also started on a small dose of blood pressure medicine. I got a home blood pressure cuff and I will be monitoring at home. One goal is to be off high blood pressure meds eventually. My understanding is that to keep eating right, sleep well, do the CT and use a few supplements, monitor a few labs and eventually the system will correct itself.

### JoanneA — 2012-03-19T03:10:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2542>

Im not on meds yet, i guess im afraid of the CT while having HB my last reading yesterday evening was 154/89 im hoping to naturally correct it before meds, my question i guess is reasurance........ Will i be ok doing CT?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-19T03:12:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2544>

> **JoanneA;2518 wrote:** I asked Dr. K if CT was ok if you have high blood pressure, he said yes, but since it was on his facebook page i did not want to spam it with anymore questions, can anyone here say more about CT and high blood pressure?

I started the Leptin Rx on Jan 10 and had been Paleo since the first. I started CT on Feb 12. My BP prior to all this was around 125/80 on a BP med. With the eating and CT I have been off all meds for six weeks. I checked my BP before the CT, 15 min into it, and immediately after and it actually lowered mine! So I stopped checking during the CT a few weeks ago and now just do my baths and monitor my BP as usual. Hopefully it will help yours as well.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-19T03:18:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2547>

CT lowers BP eventually......

### JoanneA — 2012-03-19T03:28:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2553>

Well then, it will be another testimony for the forum! Im in!!!

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-19T03:47:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2560>

Joanne, do post your progress. I am eager to get my BP down too, and I'll post my results too.

### Destiny — 2012-03-19T12:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2677>

My husband lost 30 lbs on the LR. His blood pressure (155/99) went down 3 months after that to 125/78 - the usual reading. It was not immediate so give it a few months. Now, he is doing CT and BP is normal.

### vlynnb — 2012-03-19T18:55:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-blood-pressure-and-ct.275/post-2807>

Thanks for this question and the answers and to Dr. K for starting this forum. I knew the answer was in the reading somewhere but couldn't find it. This made it easier to find.

# Thread: Firefox  font size Issue?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/firefox-font-size-issue.277/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pjms9 — 2012-03-19T02:55:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/firefox-font-size-issue.277/post-2539>

I am not sure any body used mozilla firefox to use this forum. when I tried to use firefox for the forum, the thread content font size is HUUGE. What can be done to rectify this. All the forum heading and menu items are all fine.

### admin — 2012-04-02T21:47:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/firefox-font-size-issue.277/post-10445>

That may be something specific to your browser settings. Inside of Firefox, you'll see "View" in your toolbar. Under "View" is an option to "Zoom". Your content may be zoomed in - giving the appearance of HUGE text. Try zooming out. It should fix your problem.

I will update any results from both of us in the near future. Glad to be here!

# Thread: MamaGrokkin' on!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MamaGrok — 2012-03-19T03:17:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/post-2546>

I go to Puerto Rico for 3 days and come back and my favorite thread is dead!  I'm kind of afraid of all the time I'm going to be spending on here ... must develop some self-control.

No doubt it was easier to keep up with only one thread, but no doubt that more people will be better served with an entire new optimal forum!

Howdy, y'all!

I'm MamaGrok (I'm probably going to regret that username following me around everywhere, lol) and the LR has changed my life. Last June, I was absolutely at my wit's end after many years of battling sugar addiction, several years of eating traditional foods, over a year of primal eating, and still not being able to get rid of the cravings and binges. They were much further apart, weeks apart, sometimes months apart, but still destroying my health.

Just days after posting this [thread of desperation](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread32223.html), the monster thread at MDA started. I believe it was divine intervention, frankly.

I doubted at first whether doubling my protein intake would make any difference, but once I dove in - wow. Instant death of cravings. They did come back after a long vacation, and then died back again. My binges spread out even more.

But a few things I read and experienced led me to increase my BAB to 65g of protein, and even though even after that monster breakfast, I was still hungry enough to eat it all over again, within a few days, I saw total craving death. I had one more borderline binge (I debated whether to actually call it that, but went with it) at the end of the month. That was October 30, 2011.

I have not had a binge since.

This is totally life-changing for me. I could finally stick to the LR. I could finally stick to primal, to TF, to GAPS, to all the healing protocols I had tried for so many years. I could do them, and I could finally win.

In November, not only did I start feeling full on the 65g BAB, I lost the desire to eat lunch. I have only eaten lunch about 3 times in the five months since, and those were not because I was hungry.

My calories have naturally reduced, and I am naturally IF'ing, as Dr. Kruse predicted. With the effects of crap food out of the way, I have been able to see that dairy is a problem for me, and that even occasional, small amounts of wheat are a problem for me. I've been able to have success with chronic bloating by seeing that I likely have a FODMAP problem. All these food sensitivities stem from my leaky gut, and I've gone a long way toward fixing that.

My lifelong dry skin is gone, and softer than a baby's bottom. It startles me every time I touch it!

For the first time in my life, I don't ever think about food between meals. I easily make a decision about what to eat based on what will nourish me, not my food obsessions, which are virtually eliminated.

I sleep soundly like I did years ago. I don't yet wake with vim & vigor, but I no longer have insomnia or am easily woken and stuck awake after small disruptions.

I don't find myself screaming at my children at five or six o'clock every afternoon. Praise be to God. I just cannot tell you how much of a blessing this is.

I'm sure there is more I'm unjustly forgetting right now. Brain fog is still a battle.

I've stopped gaining weight, and in fact lost 12 pounds and almost four dress sizes. I'm wearing clothes I last wore in 2004 or earlier, and actually rocked my high school cheerleading uniform a few weeks ago.

2012 is the year for the end of chronic bloating, binge eating disorder, chronic fatigue, borderline depression, hypothyroidism, and much more. This is the year I return to living fully. Thank you, Dr. Kruse.

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-19T13:04:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/post-2679>

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-19T14:26:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mamagrokkin-on.279/post-2694>

LOVED the 3 days away. It was a wonderful 15th anniversary with my love.

I should mention for those new to this that it took me at least FIVE times (really, I think it was more) trying the reset to be able to persevere through the 8 weeks. Every time I started, I'd have a binge or enough off-plan eating (little bit of this little bit of that little bit more of this...) that I'd see all the progress I'd made reverse (Dr K is absolutely right-on with the rule of thumb that a decent sized indiscretion = 10-14 days backwards). I'd lose two weeks,, then two more,, then have to restart the counter again. Over and over and over again.

I finally got it done. Jan 1 - Mar 1. 8 weeks, LR accomplished! (My first attempt had been in June!)

My point - if you have cravings that do you in, NEVER give up. Never never never never never give up. It was increasing the protein again and again (while being sure to eat plenty of fat and plenty of other nutrient-dense foods) that enabled me finally to stick to this and get the healing done. And yes, I'd tried sticking to many other dietary protocols, but none of them had been able to stop the bingeing. Finally, finally, success.

# Thread: Walked into the pond
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2012-03-19T04:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2568>

Ending week five of ct. Following the protocol. Started with face dunking for a week. Went to 20 lb ice on chest with compression shirt 1-2 weeks. Layed down in the snow a few times. Started taking 52 deg showers once a day for a week then stepped it to two times a day for a week. Tonight I walked out into a 50 deg pond under a clear starlit sky. I just walked in slowly until i was up to my chin and stood there for ten minutes. I am going back in the am. This is starting to get really amazing. I test bg before ct and after. Ten minutes of ct will drop my bg 40 points. I try to ct 2 hrs post prandial if possible. Stick with it it gets easier everyday.

### kalibex — 2012-03-19T12:19:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2674>

> **Dali Dula;2575 wrote:** Ten minutes of ct will drop my bg 40 points.

Very cool!!

### AKMan — 2012-03-19T13:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2683>

Thanks for sharing the BG info. The way brown fat was 'rediscovered' recently was by cancer researchers. They would fill people with glucose and give them a PET scan to see where the glucose was going. If they had cancer, the cancer cells would actively start heating up as they metabolized the glucose. [http://www.lipidworld.com/content/10/1/238](http://http://www.lipidworld.com/content/10/1/238)

They noticed something strange. Bands of high activity around the collar bone, chest and back in people when the temperature in the room was cool, and none when it was warm. They found they had to keep it warm to prevent these false alarms. They also found it was BAT (brown fat) causing the activity.

They did lots of studies using PET to see what activated BAT, short exposures to cold activated it and repeated exposure grew it. It doesn't even need to be cold-cold, just like 68 degrees in the room, but ice water on a foot will make it explode.

I wonder if that is the drop in BG you are seeing, caused by activation of BAT and it's metabolism of glucose.

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-19T13:29:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2686>

> **Dali Dula;2575 wrote:** Ending week five of ct. Following the protocol. Started with face dunking for a week. Went to 20 lb ice on chest with compression shirt 1-2 weeks. Layed down in the snow a few times. Started taking 52 deg showers once a day for a week then stepped it to two times a day for a week. Tonight I walked out into a 50 deg pond under a clear starlit sky. I just walked in slowly until i was up to my chin and stood there for ten minutes. I am going back in the am. This is starting to get really amazing. I test bg before ct and after. Ten minutes of ct will drop my bg 40 points. I try to ct 2 hrs post prandial if possible. Stick with it it gets easier everyday.

I've noticed the BG drop also. If I CT at night it drops but it doesn't stay that way by the time morning rolls around even though I haven't eaten anything.

### Dali Dula — 2012-03-19T14:14:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/walked-into-the-pond.281/post-2692>

> **Groketteliveson;2694 wrote:** I've noticed the BG drop also. If I CT at night it drops but it doesn't stay that way by the time morning rolls around even though I haven't eaten anything.

Yes, I have the same thing happening.

# Thread: Hi from CA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-ca.282/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### karin7771@gmail.com — 2012-03-19T04:43:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-ca.282/post-2592>

Hi: Trying to figure this all out! I did HCG in the old days when the doctor gave me one shot for the whole month. I ate a lot of meat and veggies in those days just because I like them. Now I'm much older and desire to get my body back in shape. I do like cold water, so that's a plus! Any tips would be greatly appreciated! I did read The Paleo book. I'm ready to start!!

### hellojtm — 2012-04-06T00:00:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-ca.282/post-11934>

Hi Karin,

The starting point is to read a good book on Paleo eating. I found Robb Wolf's book to be awesome and very approachable. But ultimately from a lifestyle perspective I adopted Art DeVany's approach. They eat the same diet and probably live the same way, but it comes across clearer, how to do it forever, in Art's book The New Evolution Diet. But Robb's book is clearer on why not to eat those foods! And Loren Cordain wrote a book called The Paleo Answer that is probably the clearest on the food side but zero lifestyle stuff and not a fun read. So... that's the food. Then Dr. Kruse has a whole meal timing and sleep regimen that is very important to start, but it can be really hard to wade through. It's called the Leptin Rx and there is a forum for it on here and many blog posts. I think it comes down to... eat a paleo diet... eat a high protein breakfast early in the AM... don't snack... don't eat a couple hours before bed... sleep in a completely dark room and don't look at computers/lights for about 2 hours before bed. This should reset a lot of hormonal imbalances that we all suffer from. My suggestion is to go onto the leptin forum and request a Leptin Reset for Dummies post...

best of luck,

jesse

edit: thinking about it, if you just read Robb Wolf's book, and Lights Out by T.S. Wiley you could come back in a year and start on the cold thermogenesis stuff, as long as you really take what you've read to heart.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-08T03:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-ca.282/post-12568>

Robb Wolf, plus a keto slanted diet Leptin Rx and CT is the thing you need to slowly adapt your mind too.......Welcome here.

# Thread: Overly Zealous
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-19T13:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2688>

I've been doing CTing for 2 weeks now. After re-reading yesterday on Dr. K's blog I thought I would start spot icing my stomach as my lower stomach had extra fat and isn't the prettiest after two Caesarians. Yesterday, I thought I'd strap on some ice while I was busy working and it honestly never felt cold. I should have known better as I have nerve damage from the surgeries. However, after 45 minutes I removed the ice to see a VERY red belly. After some time the spot warmed up and got much warmer than the rest of my body. This morning I woke up to find it looking bruised. Has anyone else experienced this? I think maybe it's frostnip???

### vwaggs — 2012-03-19T16:09:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2730>

Is it painful? I think I remember someone posting this same thing on the blog comments and Dr. K said it was "normal" but I am not 100% sure. I would start the ice with a thin t-shirt in between the ice and your skin until you have done it a few times to get used to it. I know a lot of people have done this and it helped.

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-19T16:49:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2741>

No, it's not painful, but I don't have normal feeling in that region of my stomach anymore. However, it is very ugly!
[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/2749=7-2012-03-19_09-14-00_133.jpg)

### BJK77 — 2012-03-19T18:46:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2799>

Yes, I had something similar happen though it wasn't quite as dark of a bruise as you have. It took several days for it to go away, but it eventually did. I'm much more careful with the ice packs now. Did you have the ice directly on your skin? You should have a compression shirt in between skin and the ice.

### ealachan — 2012-03-19T18:52:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2805>

My stomach FEELS a little bruise-y where I've been icing it, but it hasn't actually gotten bruised-looking. I've been doing the ice in a bag against whatever thin shirt I have on at the time, not against the actual skin.

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-19T20:06:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2854>

I had it over my shirt in the beginning. I had my shirt tucked into my waistband and the icepack between my shirt and pants; however, I was grooming the dog and the shirt became untucked at some point and I didn't even notice.

> **BJK77;2808 wrote:** Yes, I had something similar happen though it wasn't quite as dark of a bruise as you have. It took several days for it to go away, but it eventually did. I'm much more careful with the ice packs now. Did you have the ice directly on your skin? You should have a compression shirt in between skin and the ice.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-19T20:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2862>

I haven't had any bruises - but when I started the icepacks it felt like a bruise in random patches on my gut.

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-19T20:51:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-2880>

Go too far, risk tissue damage. Dr. K has mentioned Body Fat percentage is a variable with CT. I'd treat that bruise like a big "don't do that again" sign. PS you look great!

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-20T14:01:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3187>

Do you mean to not spot ice again, or be much more careful to ensure that the ice doesn't get in direct contact with the skin? I'm determined to get rid of the extra insulation there  BTW, thanks for the compliment.

> **Chrisgraff;2889 wrote:** Go too far, risk tissue damage. Dr. K has mentioned Body Fat percentage is a variable with CT. I'd treat that bruise like a big "don't do that again" sign. PS you look great!

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-20T15:55:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3230>

I'd lay off the spot icing until that bruise was completely healed. A frostbite scar isn't the result you're looking for! ;-)

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T16:19:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3237>

Dr. K replied similarly to someone - lay off that spot until it's totally healed, then be sure the next time to keep cloth between you and the ice.

The first time or two I iced, I started with shirt, then moved to ice directly on skin. I got a bruised feeling area, but no actual bruise. I made sure to keep the shirt there the next few times, and now I don't need to bother with the shirt, no soreness, just a nice cherry red. Fat is leaving the area rapidly!!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-20T17:05:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3254>

> **JeffieJoe;2749 wrote:** No, it's not painful, but I don't have normal feeling in that region of my stomach anymore. However, it is very ugly!
> [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/2749=7-2012-03-19_09-14-00_133.jpg)

A picture is worth a 1000 words!

Is it painful like a bruise? I have had no problem getting my skin down in temp with clothes between the ice a moi. I would totally let that heal before freezing that area again. It's an "adaptation".

I have not been able to tolerate cold showers more than 2 mins, but icing by area has been a breeze. One of the "benefits" of this is you don't feel pain in the same way with cold therapy, thus overdoing it might be pretty easy to do.

Compression clothes or even a towel between should help.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-20T23:50:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-3549>

Directly icing for too long is not the right approach in my opinion. You just need to the get the skin to 50-55F. Try 10 minutes on, 10 off or something like that. It doesn't have to hurt! As a matter of fact, if you are pushing it too hard, you're probably spiking stress hormones that can be counter-productive.

One little thing I'm trying: sucking ice cubes while I chill out in the tub. Dr. Kruse says,

> "This process is controlled by surface skin cold receptors and wiring from the mouth, gut, Peripheral Nervous System and to the Central Nervous System via the spinal cord and then to the brain. The brain gets inputs of this tract from the vagus nerve, and from a CD 36 receptor in the mouth which relays sensory inputs to the spinal cord and from the surface cold receptors.
>
>
>
> What does CD 36 do for mammals? CD36 is an oral receptor in the lingual papillae of taste buds that mediated perception of long-chain fatty acids. It involves the gustatory neural pathways in cranial nerve 9 and 10 (glossophayrngeal nerve and the vagus nerve). These inputs head to the floor of the fourth ventricle (area postrema) of the hind brain to synapse in the nucleus of the solitary tract. Here they interact with the somatic sensory cold receptor system of the face and of the body."

Sucking on a frozen sardine my just be the ticket!

### JeffieJoe — 2012-03-28T00:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-7393>

No it's not painful; however, \*if\* if was on part of my body that hasn't suffered from nerve damage from surgeries I would imagine that it would hurt. It's peeling now, just like a sunburn would.

>

>
> Compression clothes or even a towel between should help.

### Mikey7 — 2012-03-28T00:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overly-zealous.286/post-7395>

Thanks for the reminder!

# Thread: Progress... I think?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-19T14:59:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-2703>

I've been trucking along pretty well with the CT. The face dunks were very hard for me for about a week and then all of a sudden, it seemed I could keep my face in the water indefinitely. Towards the end of the face dunks I started cooling off the shower and doing a bit of spot icing. About a week and a half ago I started the baths. The first couple were around 58 but since then I've had the bath down to 55 or less. Saturday night I pulled off a 45 minute bath. My longest bath, and would have been longer had my twin 9 month olds not been so unhappy with daddy.

I've been concerned that I've not been seeing any symptoms from doing the CT - not any positive or negative. I do seem to be get used to the cold and tolerating it a lot better. I was quite disappointed I didn't get in early enough last night for my bath.

My husband has also been doing this with me and he had several days where he was VERY fatigued and I assumed it was from the CT. Do I need to be concerned I'm not seeing anything happen yet? Do the nasty die-off side effects I see people writing about come later on? Am I on the right track, or am I missing something?

### vwaggs — 2012-03-19T16:06:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-2728>

I don't have any major die off symptoms either. But I am not really "sick" at all. Some headaches and tiredness here and there but that is all. I have been doing the baths for 3 weeks every other day and biggest thing I notice is my lack of appetite which is awesome. I am also losing weight where I couldn't before (hCG was the only way I could really lose) and I am on my last 5 lbs. Those last few are always a killer to lose so I am so excited that this is working and I have no hunger what so ever. I couldn't be happier with the results! I know there is "good stuff" happening on the inside even if I can't feel it right now. So I will embrace the fact that I didn't have too much junk inside to die off. I think when people are losing lots of fat, that is when the toxins come out and can make you feel cruddy. I have had a 2-3 day migraine but I am losing fat so I will attribute it to that.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-19T17:55:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-2777>

That's the thing... I have plenty of fat to lose! I probably need to lose about 50 pounds. No pounds or inches lost since starting CT. I probably just have to let it work it's magic a little while longer. Anxious to see SOMETHING happen though!

### Phoenix — 2012-03-19T19:06:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-2812>

> **Trisha;2785 wrote:** That's the thing... I have plenty of fat to lose! I probably need to lose about 50 pounds. No pounds or inches lost since starting CT. I probably just have to let it work it's magic a little while longer. Anxious to see SOMETHING happen though!

My guess, Trisha, is that as a result of those two beautiful 9 month olds, your hormones are still very different than anyone else on this forum. Not too many of us have had twins in the last year! So I don't think you can expect anything "normal" to happen to you as a result of CT. At least not for a while.

Just for the record, today is day 12 for me of CT. I started with cold showers on the 7th, and have worked my way up to cold baths. Just took my 4th yesterday. Nothing for me either. No inches lost, no weight lost. So I'm patiently waiting for something to happen. I'm 55 though ... very different hormone situation that you I would imagine!

Phoenix

### Destiny — 2012-03-20T01:38:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-3010>

> **Phoenix;2821 wrote:** My guess, Trisha, is that as a result of those two beautiful 9 month olds, your hormones are still very different than anyone else on this forum. No inches lost, no weight lost. So I'm patiently waiting for something to happen. I'm 55 though ... very different hormone situation that you I would imagine!
>

My weight is not moving down either. I only lost 5 lbs since last October when I went low carb and then LR. After that I got a Triest cream to improve my non existent estrogen. That helped with my FBS but the weight is still the same. CT right now is positive for my well being but there is no weight loss and my appetite is increasing.

### Eve — 2013-03-07T19:40:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-79492>

I wanted to bump this to see if anyone else wanted to chime in or if any of the posters here had updates...particularly in terms of weight loss.
I started CT last Spring haphazardly and picked it up again last week after a couple months of hiatus. My CT is the coldest it's ever been as the tap water is cold 42F. Despite the initial shock of getting in the tub, it feels good. I feel energized afterwards and the next day. However, today I feel exhausted and like I need a massage. Is there such thing as delayed detox? No weight loss yet and I have like 30-40lbs to lose

### AK Man — 2013-03-07T21:03:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-79504>

You guys may remember me, I have been CT'ing a long time. Here are my long-term observations on CT:

\- It promotes subcutaneous fat growth
\- It is nearly impossible to CT with low functioning thyroid, ie. TSH high/T4 low
\- It is a healthy undertaking in that it boosts metabolism, increases calorie burn, and hopefully leads to BAT expansion
\- BAT is impossible to measure for the average person doing CT
\- Water temp doesn't have to be painfully cold, 50-70 deg F is fine, colder is not needed, warmer does no good
\- CT outdoors is fine if air temps are below 32 deg F, may need longer exposure times, dress just warm enough to prevent frostbite/hypothermia
\- If it gets cold where you live, take advantage of winter temps
\- If it doesn't get cold where you live, seek out the coldest water and swim there as much as you can
\- Consider these: keep house thermostat below 66 degrees, 60 deg at night (or colder if others will allow)
\- CT is not a magic bullet to turn around any disease state, especially involving thyroid
\- CT will not make you slim, but CT in conjunction with eating less and exercising more will
\- Spot icing doesn't work for spot fat reduction
\- You don't need a special diet, ie. keto in winter, to make CT work

### nonchalant — 2013-03-07T21:40:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-79516>

Hi AKMan, glad to hear from you again.
I think CT promotes subcutaneous fat in the presence of a large amount of carbs.

### freesia — 2013-03-07T23:35:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-79536>

Hi Eve,
I used to get what I called a CT hangover. I think this was while I was shedding some (small amount of) visceral fat, and the resulting estrogen dump. It lasted a couple of months. I eased into CT. I had to because I was pretty unwell when I started. I have been ice bathing consistently every one or two days for 11 months now. All seasons, mid 40s temps for the past 6 months. Overall, no weight loss here either (I wasn't looking for any!). I haven't _noticed_ an increase in subcutaneous fat, but it would make sense to me actually AKman if that did happen.
What did change noticibly was that my waist got smaller - and I put on some muscle.  No exercise. I gained some fitness...and the ability to tolerate change in temperature much better. My body temperature is normal for the first time since adolescence. I feel much fitter and more comfortable (less inflammation) for doing CT. I agree it's not a cure all. Nice tool though if you can manage it...especially for someone like me who had post exertional malaise on trying any kind of exercise.

ETA: Eve I have noticed other benefits from CT too (mentioned in other threads in the CT forum) but have noted observations relating especially to weight/body comp above.
Hope that helps.

### FreeRangeKiwi — 2013-03-08T03:21:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-79582>

i think AK's experiences must be highly personalised as they are quite contrary to mine.

i have found that the cooler the water, the more pronounced the effects are mentally - i really enjoy pushing the temp down and find it gets easier in cooler temps - my fat feels like a wetsuit and I feel toasty inside. Not only this, but die off becomes more and more pronounced when the boundaries are pushed. Spot icing has a major impact on both myself and my wife in spot reduction, varicose veins, pain and sleep temp. Keto is imperative for my tolerance and time to see skin blushing (highly recommend eating fish + fat prior).

things started really happening at the 3 week mark for me although i noticed changes after only few sessions. i could sprint what felt like twice as fast as before, would hardly puff, and had an incredible mental improvement (acuity and attitude).

on another note, it is the end of summer here downunder and i am just preparing myself for the change in seasons. i have been a badass and had butter/full dairy instead of ghee for the last month - something I know causes me issues. it seems it has killed my dhea-s/vit-d/cortisol rhythm/circadian clocks as my mood has plunged and my sleep is screwed. i know what to do - the rx's lay it out nicely. but here is the lesson, don't let up. dicing with the grey areas will set you back. you'll find that out as soon as you let up.

### freesia — 2013-03-08T05:17:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-79602>

FreeRange - so great to hear from someone else about the _fitness gains_! Thanks for sharing.

Progress in that department shocked me...maybe it was so noticable in my case because I was effectively disabled before? I could walk on the flat for 30 - 40 mins but with joint pain (ankles), and post exertional malaise payback. Mornings were the only time I had stamina to stand more than 30 minutes at a time. My progress stunned my husband too because he's fit, trains and plays regular sport...and less than a month of regular ice baths and I could climb a pretty steep hill with less effort than him. What's that about? Seriously.  No puffing either, like you. I'm still shaking my head over that one.
So, with all due respect to AKmans careful observations of his own experience, for _me_ discovering CT has been an extraordinary gift really. The icebaths got me fit and strong enough for the ocean too. Such a long way from where I was a year ago. Perhaps the most obvious advantages are for those with the greatest fitness deficits?? Just speculating there, and trying to make sense of AKmans experience vs mine. It's obviously a personal thing!

I have noticed lots of benefit in those warmer temps too lately. Despite having a small ice making industry going here, and deliveries twice a week too...I can't make the sea colder in summer, so 70 F it is!
Icebaths are still best for me around mid 40's. 42 F yesterday. It's a different beast than a seaswim at 70 F. Couldn't compare the two personally.

### Inger — 2013-03-08T08:24:45+0200

AKman.. 66 degree F at home? That sound pretty warm to me. I have 60 degree F and sometimes even less and it feels okay.. not only to me! I slept all winter long with the widow wide open in any temps, no heating on. But I have warm covers.
I am going to miss winter because of the cold bedroom temps. I just love it. It is so amazingly refreshing!

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-08T19:36:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-i-think.287/post-79682>

So having a Keto diet does seem to be important for some people with Cold Adaption.....I guess that means maybe I just need to wait it out. I've been going for about 4 weeks with almost nothing but only 4 days with a low carb diet.

I ate a bit above the 50 carb limit yesterday with some scalloped potatoes...love those things hard to give up...and pork chops with shake n bake....a bit hard to remove that stuff. My mom didn't make any without this time.

Anyway I don't think I got to 100 but it was still a low carb day in comparison to just a week ago when I was binging every 2-3 days.

I'm trying to be strict for a good 2 weeks and those potatoes/seasoned pork chops are my only deviation. Hopefully I'll notice something next week. I think I might be getting a bit better...maybe but its hard to tell.

I do seem to respond positively in regards to energy levels afterwards, but its very temporary...like the cold just energizes me...doesn't last long.

I'm very curious about the Thyroid issue....according to one of the CT posts...CT bypasses the Thyroid or something...is this based on faulty reasoning or is there some reason it doesn't happen in everyone? If the Thyroid hormones being screwed messes this up than if I have issues maybe I can't get the most out of it without fixing the issue.

The Thyroid bypass mechanism if it works at all like that should allow adaption even with a Thyroid problem....I would think.

"THE COLD ADAPTED HUMAN THYROID FXN: does not bother with T3 at all. Why? When you are supremely LS by cold you go straight the the source, the hypothalamus and make TRH from the brain. The brain controls all thyroid function in coldâ€¦â€¦.forget the Moose thyroid.

SKEPTIC BOMB: You bypass all hormones and TSH too. TRH drives the whole show. The brain is completely in control and it up regulates fat burning everywhere. This is how the Ancient Pathway lights your pilot light. The warm adapted human always complains about the cold and always feels coldâ€¦â€¦the cold adapted on is always pink to cherry in cold radiating heat like a furnace. You can thank TRH for this. This is does not even require a thyroid gland either. Is not life grand in the cold, folks?"

"Once leptin enters and binds to its receptors, it effects the lateral hypothalamic tracts to immediately send a second messenger signal to the thyroid to signal it to up-regulate thyroid function and efficiency. See whne we are warm adapted it require help to partially access this pathway from T3 hormone. If you are LR you never can access this pathway. This specifically is how we can raise our basal metabolic rate when we are leptin sensitive. These coupled events, matched with leptinâ€™s actions peripherally in muscles, occur at the UCP3 sitesâ€¦â€¦to burn fat as we sleep at a higher basal metabolic rate. This means electron chain transport does not make ATP as usual. When leptin allows this uncoupling to occur we make heat and not energy from normal metabolism. This means we will burn off our excess calories as pure heat."

This part leads me to believe perhaps the reason I'm not seeing many of the effects is because my Leptin level is still high because of my constant binges. I have to have enough days of rather low carb before my Leptin levels fall....perhaps 2 weeks is the amount of time it takes for the cold to reduce Leptin sufficiently?

I'm guessing this varies depending on how many carbohydrates you eat...would love some feedback on my thoughts.

Thanks

Brain isn't well fueled enough...still a slow thinker...probably because of my deficiencies up till now. The Fluoride in the drinking water and other toxins probably haven't helped.

I'm currently drinking RO water that I get periodically when it runs out in the 5 gallon jugs I fill to put on my water dispenser. Wonder how much Fluoride I get from my food since sadly I have a mostly conventional food base without much wild caught fish and not any pastured grass-fed animals. Though they are still probably dosed with water that contains Fluoride...eating their proper diet probably leads to a nutrient profile which would assist with Fluoride...perhaps they get more Iodine.

I'm somewhat curious about Iodine in cattle...they get it from bugs accidentally eaten and its in the grass to a certain extent? I wonder if our animals get enough Iodine when being pastured. I'd love to see a comparison of more than just the Omega 3/Omega 6 with pastured animals vs Feedlot. There aren't enough nutritional comparisons out there involving stuff like that...I love numbers oh well.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-08T19:57:52+0200

> [Raiken3712 said:](/goto/post?id=79682)

I'd love to see a comparison of more than just the Omega 3/Omega 6 with pastured animals vs Feedlot. I believe coastal hay is prized for livestock. Maybe it's the higher iodine content?

I'm confused also about the thyroid and CT. I'm pretty low on thyroid, though with iodine supplementation it is edging upward. But adapting to CT has never been a problem for me. I didn't hurry through the adaptation, but I seemed to adapt in an exponential curve. Especially below 50F. So "bypassing the thyroid" seems to apply to me. But there are plenty of others with low thyroid that experience considerable difficulty with CT. Perhaps it is a protein uncoupling issue?

I don't think you have to have a strict keto diet, but heavy on the animal fats and proteins, definitely.

# Thread: Chapped Lips?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chapped-lips.288/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T16:09:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chapped-lips.288/post-2729>

This isn't necessarily from the Reset or CT as I have been having problems with chapped lips before I was even Paleo. I have them year round no matter the season. I put coconut oil on them a lot, but it only helps for a moment. Does anyone know what vitamin or mineral I may be lacking that could cause persistently chapped lips?

I know mouth breathing can also be a cause and I have been trying to consciously keep my mouth closed more and breath through my nose. It doesn't seem to make much of a difference so far.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-20T15:44:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chapped-lips.288/post-3223>

No one has any ideas?

### PrimalPam — 2012-03-21T00:39:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chapped-lips.288/post-3588>

I had terrible chapped lips for a while last year. I did a ton of searches for some soothing, because normal lip balms did not seem to help. So here's what I did (and am doing now, as they are a bit chapped again): At night before bed: Apply Aquaphor around the edges of your lips, especially the areas which are cracked. You can find it in the lotions section at the drugstore. Then apply Blistex medicated (the one in the small blue tub) on the rest of your lips. Then apply Vaseline around the edges to prevent the Aquaphor from migrating. I use this formula every time my lips get chapped, and they are usually better by morning.

During the day: apply Vaseline liberally.

### Maria12345 — 2025-09-05T05:49:09+0300

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-19T17:00:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-2745>

Hi All, I've been a lurker for years, but never felt compelled to join the forum until the Dr Kruse posted the Cold Thermogenesis protocol. I started this about 3 weeks ago, and once I started adding the ice packs last week with a compression shirt, my body started feeling like it was going through this transformation. Since i"m just starting this up, I was wondering if the people who have gone through this would chime in with their experiences. I've searched the thread, but didn't see to much with experiences all in one thread.

I'm being cautiously optimistic at this point, but feel like i might be finally busting through a weight loss plateau I have been stuck on for years.

Cheers!

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T02:47:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3040>

My biggest fear with this whole CT craze that Dr. K got rolling here, is that people will get discouraged in a few weeks or months and quit.

One of the wildcards is Dr. K. will say something like, 'when you are cold adapted, you will lose weight, increase health, etc..' But, how do people in the real world know when they are cold adapted? A 30 year old lady, 30 lbs overweight, puts an icepack on her belly 3 nights a week...her husband laughs at her and she put on 5 lbs in a month. Was she cold adapted?

Same lady, does the same thing, but eats a completely keto diet--loses 5 lbs. Was she cold adapted?

All the studies I've read, and I've read a lot of studies! mostly point to BAT (brown fat) as the big winner in CT. If you can activate your BAT, it will definitely boost your metabolism. Trouble is, the only way to know if you've activated your BAT is through a PET scan under controlled conditions--doubt that's cheap!

However, all the studies I've read say that some, most, or all of us have some BAT. I tend to believe the 'all' camp. It is suggested BAT is greatly reduced in obese, elderly, and men. Young, thin, and women have the most. It's also said to be seasonal, changing with light and temp.

It has been well studied that brief exposures to cold activate BAT. One-time footbaths in 40 degree water result in BAT activity. Chronic cold exposure, as they put it, results in new BAT being formed, and old BAT being expanded.

I like to think that daily or 3X weekly icepacks, anywhere on the body, will stimulate growth of BAT. I like to think that daily ice-water immersion of the whole body will turn a person into BATMAN (pardon the pun).

I think ultimately the success of CT will hinge on whether people can do it long enough to activate and recruit new BAT. I doubt we will hear success stories other than, 'I broke a 6 mo stall', for a long time. It would be awesome to hear people saying 'I cured my diabetes' or 'my Alzheimer's went away' in a year or two, but even then there will be naysayers attributing results to something else. If someone doesn't get results after a year of doing CT, we will say, 'you musta cheated on your keto diet' or'you didn't get cold enough'.

Anyhow, CT has to be like exercising--just do it if you believe in it and don't freak if you don't get fast results. Find a way to give yourself some chronic cold exposure and see what happens.

And report back!

If you want to see some good studies, go to Google Scholar, type in 'cold thermogenics obesity BAT' or similar, then sort results for hits 2011 or newer. Awesome stuff out there!

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T03:07:53+0200

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-20T03:23:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3071>

Akman, I think you are spot on re: the wild card of how long it takes/how you know. It helps to know its not linear but I, for one, am not seeing amazing changes 3-4 weeks in. To make things worse, I am cold all the time now. I go numb in the bath so I stop shivering, but as soon as I get out it takes me a few hours to get warm again. Then I keep drinking my iced drinks and try not to wear too much clothing and I'm cold again. I realize the specifics of how long it will take are unique to each of our situations, but it's good to be reminded that it's not an easy or immediate arrival. Cause when you add in the carb cravings that have come back since stating CT with the feeling cold.... Well it takes a lot of faith and tough mindedness to keep going.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-20T14:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3196>

So I know this is really early in the game. I have been doing the ice packs for about an hour each night starting a week ago. Last night I did my second immersion bath (I tired a 10 minute one on the weekend) and I did 15 minutes.

Here's the things I've noticed - I wake up in the middle of the night, hot and sweaty. I don't notice any difference in my sleeping patterns - but I have always been a sound sleeper (been doing a paleo type diet for 12+ years so maybe that helps).

When i first started the ice packs, I notice some sore spots on my fat, but that has pretty much gone away after 4-5 days.

I have lost 3 pounds since starting the ice packs last week.

I go through periods of having amazing energy and thought processes, to being utterly tired and I need a nap in the day.

My belly fat feels less dense and more fluid. I wish I had measured my waist when I started, but it just feels like it is shrinking.

I honestly didn't expect anything to happen at this point, so that's why I was wondering about people who have been doing this longer than me - what is down the road from here?

### Destiny — 2012-03-20T14:58:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3207>

What am I doing wrong? I was 152 lbs in October and started on low carb, no wheat at that time because after always being 130-135 lbs I needed to do something.

I lost 2 lbs on it before starting LR in December on which I lost additional 3 lbs so I was down to 147 lbs. During that time my fasting blood sugar was climbing up well over 110.

I had salivary testing done in January. That one showed that my estrogen was almost nonexistent, however, my DHEAs was high for my age (50). My testosterone was in the upper range, my progesterone too was OK (have been supplementing). My cortisol was decent in the morning, a bit lower at noon and spiking at night.

I got estrogen supplementation in February. Once I started, my sugar went down to 80th and I was hoping that my weight will go down. That did not happen. I started TC at the end of February. **This morning my weight was up at 150 lbs and I have increased appetite** but I still follow the LR rules. That really crushed me. What else can I do? If I have enough DHEAs, should I take Pregnenolone or not?

I am not sure what else to do. Where is my problem? All of this has been working for my husband! He started at 229lbs and is 196lbs!! We do the same stuff and I do it more precisely. He gets results and I don't. Why? I am really frustrated this morning.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T15:01:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3208>

Because you're a woman. Yeah, it sucks, but we have much more complicated hormonal stuff going on. Do you think the weight is fat, or could it be water retention? I gained 6lb in one day two weeks ago, and it went away over 9 days. It was obvious it was water, b/c I didn't even eat 6lb worth of food, but a lesser amount can be deceiving. I also measure a lot, and the tape tells me when I'mholding on to water - my wrist and calf swell. My arms and thighs do, too, but I know it's pure water when my calf goes up half an inch in a day.

I have no idea what the answer to water retention is, but it's not at all the same thing as gaining fat, so it's an important question.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T17:55:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3292>

One thing I've notice so far is some pretty funky tiredness at night. It all started after the time change though. Guess it was a bad idea to start CT at the same time.  Essentially what happens is I get totally crushed around 7pm. Hit bed and sleep like the dead till 12-1am. Then I'm up the rest of the night and can't sleep. I used to stay up till 11pm/midnight before and sleep till 8am or so. I've been trying to push that back an hour or so to adjust it by forcing staying awake. Still working on that though.

Note, this is pretty much happening every night so far. Not doing CT every day either.

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-20T18:00:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3295>

> **JC2K;3309 wrote:** My fear is that people will not pay attention to the protocol, go way overboard early and hurt themselves, then the protocol get's blamed for it and gets bad press. I can see it in the news now "person gets frostbite following the kahrazy Dr K protocol to lose fat." add in misc non-related doctors saying how dangerous and unproven it is and boom, all without giving it a chance...

This is what I'm thinking too. People get tied up in neolithic thinking and want everything now, now, now, which leads to skipping steps in the protocol and going harder and faster than what is advisable.

Also, I think people outside the Paleo community who get wind of this might try the CT without addressing the diet, which could also be problematic, especially if they are eating SAD and have totally f-ed up O3/O6 ratios.

To share my experience thus far, it's been on a non-linear trend towards awesomeness. I have experienced gains in energy and sex drive (and enjoyment - wink); decreases in cravings/appetite; improvements in body comp/weight; increases in tolerance to cold; improved mental clarity; improved sleep (though my circadian clock is still waking me up with a lovely shot of cortisol an hour before I need to wake up due to the stupid time change). I've also experienced hot flashes (or a sort), loose stools (still experiencing this, but it's getting better), and momentary dips in energy. I've been CTing for 2 weeks. I wouldn't say I'm adapted yet, but I'm on my way!

Thank you for this post, because it is just what I needed to read today! I have been icing, as well as getting in 9 soaks in 54 degree water, for 40 minutes at a time, over a ten day period. I am doing this to improve my lower spine, and if I lose a couple of pounds in the mean time, that is just gravy. I am having a problem, however, in that the first 6 soaks were very doable, but the last three are getting increasingly more difficult. Even though the water temperature is the same day-to-day my ability to remain in that frigid water is waning. Yesterday, while I was soaking, my thighs never did get to the doable point. The goosebumps and the stinging from the cold never quit the entire time I was in the water. I am afraid it might be even worse when I do my soaking later today. I make sure to keep my feet out of the water, and I only have the water up to my waist. Has anyone else had this happen? I thought that it was going to get more tolerable, not less.

If this continues to get increasingly more painful (yes actually painful), then I guess I will have to resort to the icing. I also notice, that when I use the ice wraps on my upper arms (were the most of my adipose fat likes to reside!) that that process is probably the hardest for me to maintain. I have every intention to get cold adapted, but if spot icing will do the job then I just might move that way if soaking does not improve.

I will not know if the protocol of diet and CT is making a difference until I get a DEXA, sometime this year - seems like a long time away. Thankfully, I have this forum to help keep me going.

Susan M.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T18:52:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3331>

> **BJK77;3324 wrote:** It worries me that it's an adrenal issue, but I thought CT was supposed to correct those issues.

I'm giving it time to shake out. Doesn't seem to be an adrenal issue to me so far. I'm not too concerned with it. Yet.  I'm fine during the day, it's just a wall I'm hitting at night. I do recall something somewhere about needing less sleep on CT.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T19:07:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3342>

> **Shijin13;3336 wrote:** Ok...Why is it I no longer shiver while in a cold bath? now that its getting warm - maybe my tap water isn't cold enough? today after my CT session in the bath I got out dried off, and started a CT Spot session... my trunk is cold for the 1st time and I'm shivering again...normally my feet feel cold -but right now I feel like heat is rolling off them...
>
> maybe be I'm starting to see CT Tolerance... Whats funny its the upper body that's shivering...trunk/legs no shivers???

Check the temp of your water. Could be warmer than you think, could be you're adjusting. Hard to tell. The CT spot stuff is definitely colder than the water though.

For me, the shivering is pretty much limited to the areas that are cold (so the trunk shivering and legs not shivering would be normal for me).

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-20T19:14:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3349>

> **JC2K;3365 wrote:** Check the temp of your water. Could be warmer than you think, could be you're adjusting. Hard to tell. The CT spot stuff is definitely colder than the water though.
>
>
>
> For me, the shivering is pretty much limited to the areas that are cold (so the trunk shivering and legs not shivering would be normal for me).

I'm bright cherry red when I get out... and my body temp according to IR temp gun ranges btwn 56-62degress...wondering if the warmer temps is making it easier... yeah for me its all upper body shakes... non on the lower body at all..

### vkiernan — 2012-03-20T20:08:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3390>

I have had headaches and nausea from the CT baths for a few weeks - 30 minutes in 50 degree water for 3 weeks. That finally went away. I never shiver in the tub, only when I get out. An hour or so later I'm good but now I do small exercises to warm me up afterwards. Just started this this past week and it works fairly well. What I noticed this weekend was after working outside cleaning flower beds and gathering 5 trailer loads of wood with my dh, I was not a bit sore and I have not been working out due to adrenal fatigue. I expected something to hurt and not one thing. I worked for 3 days outside. Crazy. Got to be something to it. I might just start doing weights before dinner, then eat and sit in the tub afterwards like Dr. K would do and see if I get any soreness. In the process of getting firewood, my tendonitis flared but I felt nothing the next day. I'm a believer and not giving up. And yes, I think everyone's mileage may vary on this depending what we are all dealing with.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-20T20:34:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3405>

I love the pics on here. . . so here goes.

I've seen comments from some worried about some of the negatives. Thanks for commenting, it made this a little less scary for me!

Today was my 2nd day of icing torso exactly per the RX. I had been doing two weeks of face dunks, working up to 20 mins. I had tried several cold showers and could only do

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-20T20:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3410>

Enjoying reading everyone's experiences so far.

Susan M, for what it's worth - I took 8 baths (30-45 min each) spread out over 2.5 weeks before I had any kind of pain in extremities. Then out of nowhere, the 10th bath caused my toes and feet to ache and just plain hurt. Took longer to warm up that particular night afterward also. There were 3 days between it and the next bath, but no pain on the next bath and I used more ice to get the water a little colder. I believe it really is as Dr. Kruse has said - nonlinear. Our body may take things in stride and then one day have trouble, then back to business as usual, then trouble. Maybe this is part of the exercise in learning to listen to our bodies and trust it instead of overriding it with our neolithic thinking.

Options you can try that others have had success with is keeping toes/feet out of water or wearing socks/neoprene. If your upper arms are the hardest, keep them out of the water or icing and add them later when the full baths are easier to tolerate. I can't tolerate much cold on the backs of my arms and that's one of the places I need it the most. I'm going to get there, just maybe slower than I want. You will too.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-20T20:44:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3413>

**Cavemam;3429 wrote:** I love the pics on here. . . so here goes.

I've seen comments from some worried about some of the negatives. Thanks for commenting, it made this a little less scary for me!

Today was my 2nd day of icing torso exactly per the RX. I had been doing two weeks of face dunks, working up to 20 mins. I had tried several cold showers and could only do

### Susan M. — 2012-03-20T20:51:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3421>

Thanks Clabbergirl. I just finished my fatty bf and my cold water and am getting ready to do it again. We will see if there is more or less pain today. I do keep my piggies out of the water as much as I can using neoprene water shoes, and that does help. I don't soak from the waist up, as a matter of fact, I wear a sports bra during soak time otherwise I am just too cold to tolerate the entire 40 mins. I will be icing my arms this evening while I am reading. It's odd, but the icing of my arms is worse than the soaking, but I still can't make myself lay down in the tub to cover my arms. I was always one of those weenie kids that hated to get splashed, and now here I am torturing MYSELF with cold water. Who'd thunk!

Gotta embrace the cold ........ !

Susan M.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-20T21:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3433>

Oh good lord I hope not! If you have this rash from CTing - don't read the wiki on "cherry angiomas". I'm assuming it will go away after awhile. Anybody who's getting this have an eta on clearing up? I am with Clients all afternoon, and I'm already trying to figure out how to sneak in scratches to these itchy legs.

Nothing says "I'm a professional" like constant scratching.

### quelsen — 2012-03-20T21:38:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3458>
