Coconut oil

### Kathy — 2012-03-20T21:43:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3461>

I'm on the 12th day of face dunking & have also been keeping the house cooler, wearing fewer clothes & lowering the hot water in the shower - not cold showers, just cooler...

This morning I was feeling quite chilly so decided to "treat" myself to a warmer shower. I noticed that the water felt much warmer on my face than it did on the rest of my body. When I turnded down the hot water and it reversed: the cooler water felt colder on other parts of my body than my face! Maybe just my face is cold adapting?!?

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-20T21:45:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3462>

> **Cavemam;3458 wrote:** Oh good lord I hope not! If you have this rash from CTing - don't read the wiki on "cherry angiomas". I'm assuming it will go away after awhile. Anybody who's getting this have an eta on clearing up? I am with Clients all afternoon, and I'm already trying to figure out how to sneak in scratches to these itchy legs.
>
>
>
> Nothing says "I'm a professional" like constant scratching.

Didn't mean to alarm you, lol. I asked because I've noticed just this morning that I'm getting little red dots all over my upper thighs - places and in a concentration I'm 98 percent positive I've not had before. I've always had the odd red dot here and there (assumed they were those cherry angiomas as a doc told me once), but these are tinier and prolific - similar to what your photo shows. Noticing these on my legs this morning was enough to snap me out of my regular early morning stupor because I have not noticed these. I thought, _great, CT is going to make me have MORE of these things??_ (I don't have any itchiness, however.)

I have been submerging my legs in the CT baths so I can attribute it to cold water on them at least, unlike your experience. I seem to remember reading something Dr. Kruse wrote about cherry angiomas (if this is in fact what they are for me?) ...off to research this further.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-20T23:53:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3553>

> **Clabbergirl;3434 wrote:** \]
>
> If your upper arms are the hardest, keep them out of the water or icing and add them later when the full baths are easier to tolerate. I can't tolerate much cold on the backs of my arms and that's one of the places I need it the most. I'm going to get there, just maybe slower than I want. You will too.

Good advice. I was putting my upper arms in the bath at the 10 minute mark but that made the remaining 35 minutes just a shiver-fest, plus terrible shakes after the bath for more than an hour. The last 2 nights I've excluded my arms and the bath was far more comfortable and post-bath warm-up was easier. No need to rush through this - just let the body adapt on its own schedule.

### Destiny — 2012-03-20T23:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-your-ct-experiences.289/post-3557>

> **MamaGrok;3231 wrote:** Because you're a woman. Yeah, it sucks, but we have much more complicated hormonal stuff going on. Do you think the weight is fat, or could it be water retention? I gained 6lb in one day two weeks ago, and it went away over 9 days. It was obvious it was water, b/c I didn't even eat 6lb worth of food, but a lesser amount can be deceiving. I also measure a lot, and the tape tells me when I'mholding on to water - my wrist and calf swell. My arms and thighs do, too, but I know it's pure water when my calf goes up half an inch in a day.
>
>
>
> I have no idea what the answer to water retention is, but it's not at all the same thing as gaining fat, so it's an important question.

I understand, however, it is hard to be almost the same weight I was 6 months ago after all the effort I put in.

# Thread: Details, Details, how are you performing your CT to get skin 50-55 degrees.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 213
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-19T17:25:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2761>

I'm trying to figure out ways to spend more time in CT, like ice suit so I can clean house and cook those paleo meals all while CTing.

I just did dishes and spent lots of time adjusting falling homemade gelpacks and generally thinking "there must be a better way". Please share hints and tricks.

I did make my own gel bags with 4/1 ratio of water to alcohol. Those are good. Getting them to stay in place isn't so good.

I'd love a "CT ROBE" or something. Any ideas? Anyone in the Boulder area want to partner on manufacturing something and making an awesome product we can then sell of so we can buy a fun estate in the mountains?

ok, so now I am sandwiching gel bags between the compression garmet against my skin, and one on top. HOwever these do not lend themselves to moving around.

Ideas?!

### BJK77 — 2012-03-19T18:44:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2797>

I've been trying to create my own little homemade ice vest with duct tape . . . quite the sight! It isn't working so well though. I'd love any ideas too. I struggle to find the time to lay down or sit on the couch for 45-60 minutes a day with the ice packs on. It's difficult enough to get my bath time in as frequently as I'd like.

### ealachan — 2012-03-19T18:49:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2802>

Could you bind the gel packs to your body with an Ace bandage, maybe?

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-19T19:02:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2811>

I've been wondering about how to fit CT into my schedule also, and then I think that maybe the problem is one we've created ourselves with our neolithic brains. Maybe one of the points of this is to stop trying to multi-task and just embrace the CT for what it's supposed to be - an ancient pathway to health.

Of course that being said, we all live in a modern world where there just isn't enough time for anything. Personally, if I'm sitting down (at home, having done it at work yet), I've got an ice pack on/underneath something. I've also tried this in the car to/from work even though that's only a 15 min commute one way. I've been tucking things in shirts or wrapping with ace bandages to hold against the skin. Not perfect, but cheap and effective.

### tweety — 2012-03-19T19:38:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2835>

my husband found a clay therma-paq thing at target for $20. I haven't used it while doing activities, but am about to try. I put it on my tummy last night while reading. I try to read or do something calming before bed most nights so I am implementing it then. I would like to find a morning use for it though

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-19T19:42:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2838>

> **tweety;2844 wrote:** my husband found a clay therma-paq thing at target for $20. I haven't used it while doing activities, but am about to try. I put it on my tummy last night while reading. I try to read or do something calming before bed most nights so I am implementing it then. I would like to find a morning use for it though

That reminded me, tweety. I've got some of those freezable bubble-pack things from Walmart that I've slept on a night or two when I didn't have time for a bath. They don't have condensation and are comfortable enough to fall asleep on since I'm using them on a mattress. Since I sleep on my stomach and I need the most CTing there, it's worked out pretty well. Just an idea.

### JC2K — 2012-03-19T21:06:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2888>

I got this vest at Amazon. Substantially cheaper than Arctic Heat, and has more ice coverage, adjustable. A bit bulky though, not sure I'd want to roam around much with it either stylewise.

[http://www.amazon.com/Flexi-Freeze-Ice-Vest-Navy/dp/B001P30358/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1332183843&sr=8-1](http://http://www.amazon.com/Flexi-Freeze-Ice-Vest-Navy/dp/B001P30358/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1332183843&sr=8-1)

You can stick your own ice blocks in it too, since it is velcroed pockets. I like it so far.

### lynn@ellijay.com — 2012-03-19T21:09:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2890>

Those bubble-ice pack things from Walmart are really great. They don't get you wet and it is easy to tuck them under garments. I bought a cheap "Spanks" type under-thing that comes up high on my waist. I tuck one bubble pack around my tummy (under the Spanks) and the second one I run up my back, tucked under a sports bra (the bottom part tucks into the shorts too.) It is easy to do your housework or whatever while you are icing. And my skin gets under 50 degrees under the ice pack but they have not left me with a bruise or freezer burn. Look for some - they are $2.88 at my store and they re-freeze quickly.

### Snowyky — 2012-03-19T21:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2897>

I made one using those waist trimmer belts ([http://www.walmart.com/ip/Gold-s-Gym-Waist-Trimmer-Belt/12167842](http://http://www.walmart.com/ip/Gold-s-Gym-Waist-Trimmer-Belt/12167842)) I'm a quilter so I have mega fabric around, I bought a couple of the belts, so cheap, under $5. Cut them then sewed them to material I had sewn into a long enough 'belt' to go around my body with pockets that I then put in a couple of those ice sheets ([http://www.amazon.com/Flexi-Freeze-Refreezable-Sheets-pack/dp/B001PTGO5A](http://http://www.amazon.com/Flexi-Freeze-Refreezable-Sheets-pack/dp/B001PTGO5A)), also made small ones to go around the top part of the arms, could make some to go around the thighs but I find that the legs get enough from the ice baths, don't really need them. [Here is a link to the image](http://https://www.facebook.com/media/set/?set=a.10150650516843702.400203.726593701&type=1), I also use it in the tub to hold the bag of ice, which I let out part of the ice so it forms around my body easier, attach the velcro so it's wider at the top then the bottom and it lets me move around but keeps the ice in place. The smaller ones have pockets big enough for the pint size ice blocks, but it's easier to use if you use the ice sheets, fits better.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-03-19T23:08:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2947>

For spot CTing I have two large ziplock bags I made into ice packs. I wear a compression shirt and then put a "spanx" type shirt on over it. Then I tie two scarves around my waist. I keep wondering if my neighbors can see in the windows in the morning when I'm stuffing my shirt with ice blocks. I checked myself in the mirror and I look like a sumo wrestler.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-19T23:28:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2955>

I looked like nutter wanting-to-be pregnant lady when I did something similar. My ice bags kept slipping down into a baby bump!

### blindpig — 2012-03-19T23:44:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2959>

I have only gotten so far as to make two packs from two gallon size zip-locks, then put them end-to-end in a Food Saver bag (to prevent leaking), to make one long pack to wrap around my stomach or back. Thanks for the ideas on how to hold it in place.

I really just wanted to say that this whole thread gave me a good laugh as I pictured everyone's contraptions. I think I see some new fashion trends coming!

Thanks, everybody!

### Mom — 2012-03-19T23:53:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2961>

I just took an old all-over-control bathing suit that is too big for me now (yippee!) and cut a line in the lining just below the bust. I put the icepacks inside between the 2 layers of the suit. I put my clothes on over the bathing suit so if UPS rings the bell I am able to answer the door looking relatively normal. I think this will work for me for a while. It actually would be more comfortable if the bathing suit wasn't as baggy.

### David — 2012-03-20T00:10:47+0200

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T01:00:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-2990>

So, being in a hotel in sunny Florida, I had to do something. I raided the ice machine down the hallway, filled a trashbag with ice, and watched Nat Geo's new show on Arctic Life.

This is the first time I've done ice bags, but I think it worked pretty good. I kept moving it around when a spot got too uncomfortable. It would turn my skin a nice cherry red after about 5 minutes. I didn't bring my thermometer, but my skin got pretty cold.

From all the studies I've read, spot treatments should be a fine way to activate the brown fat. Most of the research they do involves putting people's feet in cold water (40-50 deg), so a bag of ice should definitely give your brain a signal that you are exposed to cold.

### kc_boxerman@yahoo.com — 2012-03-20T02:25:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3026>

> **AKMan;3012 wrote:** Most of the research they do involves putting people's feet in cold water (40-50 deg), so a bag of ice should definitely give your brain a signal that you are exposed to cold.

Seems like most people are skipping the ice bag portion in lieu of baths. I started with baths but decided to back up and do as the doctor prescribed (he's indicated it best to do as he described). Just got to 60 minutes with a 20lb bag of ice on a compression shirt and need to start without the shirt tonight.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-20T02:43:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3037>

Ok, the seal n save thing has piqued my interest!! I could do a LONG homemade ice blanket made of the alcohol/water mix. Shouldn't leak much unlike the baggies. perhaps even superduper long and could hang them over shoulders. . . then I could wrap a robe around tight to make em stick against my body. . . .I need a way to seal it out of the machine. Side of an iron? That way I could make it like baffles.

Love the bathing suit idea. I'll have to go find a 1 piece, I've been tank-ini-ing and all my ice keeps falling out when I'm moving around.

I so have to start taking photos of this stuff. Do you ever just kind of wonder/imagine that we might all be nuts and in the throes of a cult?

Did some major ice package today. Totally easy to do. I had been doing face dunks, and showers of a 1-1.5 min duration. Had no problem doing 45 mins of ice bags.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T05:09:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3121>

> **Cavemam;2973 wrote:** Oooh, thanks. Have you worn it for long periods (30mins - 60mins). Does it stay frozen long enough to keep skin temps down for a long haul.

I've been wearing it around 45 min with a t-shirt under it. It actually gets a bit uncomfortable by then so I take it off. It gets my skin temp down to the 50's at the end (testing with an IR thermometer). There are a bunch of small ice blocks in it, and depending on where it was touching the body, some blocks are more melted than others, so it's hard to judge. The Amazon site has people saying it lasts 2 hours, but that's not quite what we're looking for. I'm guesstimating it will last an hour easy, but not two hours for the temp we want.

It gives me full coverage front and most of the back.

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T12:50:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3178>

> **MamaGrok;3128 wrote:** Love the ideas.
>
>
>
> I was in 62 degree water and my skin got to 65 degrees, but never turned even slightly pink. Sounds like some of y'all are getting pink from this. Does that mean my 5:1 O6:3 ratio is actually serving me well, or is somethign broken in me?

I have been filling a plastic bag with ice cubes, maybe 2 quarts worth, and holding it on my belly and legs til it gets about numb--a couple minutes in each place anyway. This will turn the area under it a nice cherry red, which lasts 5-10 minutes. try that and see what happens. Cold air and cold water don't turn me red, either. I keep doing this until the ice is about melted, which can be an hour or more. It's easy to do while watching TV. It really gets cold when you get more water than ice, more skin contact.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-03-20T13:36:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3181>

Just ordered an IR thermometer to check skin temperature - I've been using my old-style mercury under-the-tongue one which is accurate in bathwater but useless for skin temperature! I've been cold showering for 5 - 10 minutes every morning, having a cold bath at about 56F for about 20 - 30 minutes a day, and using picnic ice-blocks for spot CT for a couple of hours every evening. I only do my tummy, and I move the block around as patches get cold and uncomfortable. By the end of the evening my tummy is bright red and really cold to the touch, and takes 3 - 4 hours to heat up again! I had a look at the cooling vests on Amazon UK, but they are extremely expensive, so I think I'll stick with what I'm doing for the time-being, and maybe think again once the thermometer turns up.

### SuzieQ — 2012-03-20T17:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3272>

What is everyone using to take the temp of the skin? I have an IF gun that I used with tempering chocolate. lol Will that be good to use for skin?

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T17:30:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3274>

> **MamaGrok;3258 wrote:** Yes, I know we're different people, silly.  I'm wondering specifically, biologically, what the difference is.

My take (could be wrong). Normally, non cold adapted body , when exposed to cold temperatures, it'll reduce bloodflow to the extremities/skin through vasoconstriction. Keeping the bulk of the warm blood in the core to keep the temps up/you alive. You need to have X temperature in your core to not get hypothermia. The danger here is that there's a reduction to the extremities, so you can get frostbite, etc.

In the cold adapted body, that core can run at a lower temperature, so the body doesn't vasoconstrict or doesn't do it as much. There's more blood flow to the extremities/cold spots (pink/red skin) in comparison, and the metabolism really ramps up to keep you running.

So, for you, it could be that your skin needs to be at less than 47 degress to get that reaction, and the water's not cold enough to do it otherwise. If you're taking the pink/red as the goal, it sounds like you need more adjusting.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T17:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3275>

> **SuzieQ;3295 wrote:** What is everyone using to take the temp of the skin? I have an IF gun that I used with tempering chocolate. lol Will that be good to use for skin?

How do you check the temperature of the chocolate with that? There's no probe right, so it's just the external temperature?

And yes, that's what I use (and I think most people do). I've also got a kitchen thermometer with a remote probe that I use in the shower/bath to check the water temperature. Obviously don't use that in your dinner roast right after the bath.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-20T17:33:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3276>

SuzyQ, I use the IF on skin. I like it, but I wouldn't know if it were wrong! It's also great for me to check temp of water etc and figure out "if water is X, skin becomes X" and track those changes.

### JC2K — 2012-03-20T17:38:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3279>

> **Cavemam;3299 wrote:** SuzyQ, I use the IF on skin. I like it, but I wouldn't know if it were wrong! It's also great for me to check temp of water etc and figure out "if water is X, skin becomes X" and track those changes.

I found the infrared better for skin temperature than water temperature. When I compared my kitchen probe in the water, it was lower than the infrared gun when shooting it at the water. I think that the IF actually is taking the temperature of the tub under the water, which isn't necessarily the water temperature. When I compare the kitchen probe to a different type of kitchen probe, it was consistent for water temperature. Both probes were not quite the same as the IF when touching skin (but relatively close). Of course, all three could be off, but I was happy it was all close in comparison.

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-21T00:12:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-3569>

I duct taped a bunch of the ziploc bag coldpacks together ... the large size fit 6 across the back with one taped above for between my shoulder blades (you might have to tape down the "under the arm part" corners down to fit under your arms)... and two big baggies in the front which fit nicely from belly to under-boobs  Fronts and backs were joined up with duct-tape shoulder straps  I even went so far as to make velcro side zips to hold it together. The compression vest would likely have held it in place if I'd decided to move around. Yeah, it does leak just a bit, but kept me COLD. I've got pics if you want to see

Thank you very much, Lyndra!

Unfortunate that I'm also 8.5! What brand of neoprene bootie do you use? There are so many brands and I can hardly swim let alone know about diving gear! Do these really help with tolerating the cold?

I can't help but wonder. . .20 years from now are we going to be sitting at the dinner table with our grandchildren saying "Ah, yes, those were the days. I once heard that freezing our fat would make it go away so I used to walk around the house doing chores with big bags of ice taped to my body."

### blindpig — 2012-03-22T01:26:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4122>

> **August;3924 wrote:** I'm wondering if this would work . . and make good economic sense (plus I've always kind of wanted one) . . . one of those foodsaver machines that has the ability to heat-seal the bags (and also the vacuum) . . . for making my own reusable packs . . . I'm afraid the zip locks will leak and make me unhapppy . . . what do you think . . .

August, I used my Food Saver to make a large pack to wrap around my stomach or back and it is working well. I made two one-gallon ziplocks with alcohol/water mixture and laid them end to end inside the Food Saver bag I made, then sealed it up. It does sweat so I just put it inside a pillow case. But this did eliminate the leaking problem I kept having, in spite of trying every brand of zippered bag out there. I recommend this method for homemade ice packs, especially if you are looking for an excuse to buy a Food Saver!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-22T01:37:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4128>

> **differentstory;4120 wrote:** I can't help but wonder. . .20 years from now are we going to be sitting at the dinner table with our grandchildren saying "Ah, yes, those were the days. I once heard that freezing our fat would make it go away so I used to walk around the house doing chores with big bags of ice taped to my body."

ha, I hear ya. I too wonder. It does have a bit of the "drink the koolaid" vibe eh? Today my husband was home. I have been cool with the fam in on the face dunks, but really my ice baby and homemade baggies of ice stuffed in a bathing suit were just too much.

I said something like "YOU DONT NEED IN THE BEDROOM FOR THE NEXT HOUR DO YOU" he said "why", and I was carrying the bin from freezer to my room. I said "Don't look at me, I don't want to talk about it!".

Although, I did point out all my new itchy and gross rashes from the CT along with todays batch of skin tags that popped up out of nowhere. He was clearly on the "I'm not sure that's a GOOD thing" side. I figure it has to be like "fix myself" before I can tell people of YET ANOTHER health experiment I'm on.

I remember susan powter and the FAT MAKES YOU FAT craze. I was a zealot livin on nothing but white rice and bagles for a good 6 months.

### Lyndra — 2012-03-22T01:49:42+0200

Bummer about the size. They used to fit me once upon a time. I have Okespor booties I got many (many) years ago when I used to windsurf around here in the perpetually cold water. Yes, they help immensely with tolerating the cold. I am doing 45 minutes in a 50-52 degree tub and my toes get a bit cold by the end but not bad at all.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-22T15:33:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4357>

If I have time in the next couple of days I'm going to go with the duct tape/sealer option. Seal the ziplock baggies in strips in the seal and save - and tape those to the outside of compression garmets.

### texasmom4 — 2012-03-22T15:53:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4371>

I'm cracking up while reading this thread!  Dr K in a speedo in front of his a/c vent, and all of us with our homemade ice body wraps. I've been wearing two spanx-style tank tops and sandwiching the ice packs between them, and that's been working really well. I try to do that once a day. It's gotten so hot here that I now have to use a 20 lb bag of ice to get my water temp down to where it needs to be. So there I lay in the tub, with a shower cap on and my husband's orange wool hunting hat over it. Surely this qualifies for being in Dr K's 10% right?! I'm sure 90% of the population would NOT do any of this!

### Lahrie — 2012-03-22T16:12:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4383>

I had a "change your thoughts - change your life" moment yesterday as I was sitting here in the store absolutely "bummed" that I can't walk around with ice stuffed in my pants while I'm at work. It seemed like such a huge waste of time because I spend a large part of my day sitting at the computer. Wait - _sitting at the computer _- I can sit ON the ice packs! Two plastic bags full of ice and a towel later - I am CTing my butt any time I feel like it!

Maybe it's obvious to others, but seriously - I have the worst case of brain fog from my multiple issues - this was a MAJOR sign that I am changing!

Laurie__

Ok- I need some ideas! The water in my tub is coming out at 70F. I have added 4 frozen water bottles and 6 pounds of ice and I only got it to 67F yesterday. Does anyone have any ideas?

By the time I buy a bag of ice and get it home, it is halfway melted. Today I filled up my bundt pan with water and put that in the freezer. My goal is the get the temperature down to 55F and be able to have the water deep enough to soak everything but my head.

Oh that made me laugh!!

I feel blessed to live where there are no street lights, and I have blackout blinds, shutters and blackout lined curtains over the windows in our bedroom, and I've taped over anything that emits light at night (only my telephone). My only problem now is that when my daughters are home from school, they insist on leaving a corridor light on overnight, and it seeps around the doorframe ...

For spot CT sessions when standing, I've discovered the beauty of an apron with a front pocket which I stuff with ice blocks and use when I'm cooking or ironing, or vacuuming. It seems to be effective. Otherwise I lay the iceblocks over a teeshirt on my tummy whilst I'm watching telly in the evening, or if I'm reading or on the computer. Must try that sitting on ice bags, though - that would be effective!

### trudyap — 2012-03-22T19:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4525>

I did my first CT bath today, after doing the "face thing" for a couple of weeks. I've been dreading it but after reading something on Mercola this morning about cold baths being good for sore muscles, and having over done it @ the gym I decided there is no finer time than the present. So I filled the bathtub with cold tap water and was surprised to find that it was 40 degrees! So, I climbed in and lasted 15 minutes the first time out. Even with me sitting in the water 15 minutes it only warmed up to 50 degrees. Do I need to get it colder than this? The most discomfort was my toes, almost to the point of being painful! Definitely going to have to get one of those overflow plugs as I was only in a little bit above my waist. Anyway, now that it's "behind" me I'm thinking that's not so bad. How long should I be staying in and how often should I be doing it? (61 years old with 50 lbs to lose)

### differentstory — 2012-03-22T20:44:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4594>

> **trudyap;4553 wrote:** I did my first CT bath today, after doing the "face thing" for a couple of weeks. I've been dreading it but after reading something on Mercola this morning about cold baths being good for sore muscles, and having over done it @ the gym I decided there is no finer time than the present. So I filled the bathtub with cold tap water and was surprised to find that it was 40 degrees! So, I climbed in and lasted 15 minutes the first time out. Even with me sitting in the water 15 minutes it only warmed up to 50 degrees. Do I need to get it colder than this? The most discomfort was my toes, almost to the point of being painful! Definitely going to have to get one of those overflow plugs as I was only in a little bit above my waist. Anyway, now that it's "behind" me I'm thinking that's not so bad. How long should I be staying in and how often should I be doing it? (61 years old with 50 lbs to lose)

YAY!!! That was my experience as well - it wasn't nearly as bad as I thought. And I have found that if I get an inch of warm water in the bath first and then get in and turn on the cold water, it is easier to handle as well (referred to as the "croak soak"). I just increase the depth as I get more used to it. The first few times I only got up to my legs, now I can go all the way up to my chest (and it really wasn't that much different).

I also had the cold feet problem and I have scuba socks coming tomorrow for that reason.

I think 40 sounds VERY COLD! I've been thinking I'll stop at 50 degrees but for now I am at 60 degrees. And besides my feet, I don't find it hard to stay in that long.

Make sure you have a VERY good book. I was doing 30 minutes in the beginning, then 40, and now I can easily do 60 minutes. I get so wrapped up in my book! I've been experimenting about 3 weeks overall.

### JC2K — 2012-03-22T20:51:09+0200

> **August;4512 wrote:** And just to double check . . are there any reasons not to use ice pack-like things designed to keep food cold .. on my body instead?

Nope, they work fine. The flexible bag/bubblewrap ones are more form fitting and easier to strap on vs the big blocks though. Also, as a reminder, don't stick it against your skin to start off, have a shirt in between.

### LinD — 2012-03-23T13:14:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4900>

> **LabLover;2899 wrote:** Those bubble-ice pack things from Walmart are really great. They don't get you wet and it is easy to tuck them under garments. I bought a cheap "Spanks" type under-thing that comes up high on my waist. I tuck one bubble pack around my tummy (under the Spanks) and the second one I run up my back, tucked under a sports bra (the bottom part tucks into the shorts too.) It is easy to do your housework or whatever while you are icing. And my skin gets under 50 degrees under the ice pack but they have not left me with a bruise or freezer burn. Look for some - they are $2.88 at my store and they re-freeze quickly.

How long do these stay frozen?

Sent from my Sprint Samsung Galaxy S II Epic.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-23T15:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4925>

> **LinD;4932 wrote:** How long do these stay frozen?

I'm finding these bubble packs don't stay frozen near as long as some of the 'lunchbox' ice blocks. They're essentially tiny plastic bags filled with water that freezes to ice and then quickly thaws back to water (maybe within an hour or so?). I think the reason they don't last long frozen is because there's just not much insulation surrounding the ice. I still find them very usable and you can't beat the price of $2.98.

### lynn@ellijay.com — 2012-03-23T15:09:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-4930>

> **LinD;4932 wrote:** How long do these stay frozen?
>
>
>
>
>
> Sent from my Sprint Samsung Galaxy S II Epic.

It depends on how tightly you squish them up to your body. The one I put around my lower tummy which is held in place by the "spanks shorts" melts more quickly than the one I run up my back which is just held in place with a sports bra. Still when I remove them after 45 minutes, there is still some ice in most of the "compartments." I think they are great.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-23T19:23:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-5058>

Got my face to below 55F finally this morning. The water I'm starting with is about 2 liters in a pan. It's 33F to begin with and by the end of 2 minutes of dunking it's at 39-40f (someone can figure out the calories there if they want  ) Today I made it over 2 minutes of dunking and over 25 seconds for a single dunk. That one had me at 49f. I took less "rest" between dunks. I also hyperventilated a little bit so that oxygenation wouldn't be an issue. When the forehead pain (brain freeze) came on I just imagined I was out on the tundra and had no choice but to hunt for food, or that I had dove into the water to grab some shellfish and had to stay under until I found some. It was probably a lame thought but it distracted me long enough to exceed the ~20s I had been previously limited at due to pain. _So I'd say that with ~35-40f water 25-30s is required to get below 55f skin temp._

Now I'm set to reread the CT series, including the evolution of the leptin rx post so I can see how long I'm supposed to get it under 55f. It's taken me a month to get here.... cold showers are getting really easy. The tap water here in San Francisco is ~52-53f.

I think everyone must be different. I've moved on from face dunking (as of yesterday), but the last few days I was easily able to get my skin temp under 55Ã‚Âºf, water temperature about 48-50Ã‚Âº (also in SF, I just dumped in a cup of crushed ice), by the 3 dunk, which would be ~45 seconds.

BTW, I can hold my breath for about 75-80 seconds outside of water, but 48 seconds was my max face dunk. I did see the time increase steadily over 2 weeks and decided to move on as that's how I interpreted the protocol.

I don't get headaches though... my o3:06 ratio was 2.7 Dec 2011.

> **blindpig;4148 wrote:** August, I used my Food Saver to make a large pack to wrap around my stomach or back and it is working well. I made two one-gallon ziplocks with alcohol/water mixture and laid them end to end inside the Food Saver bag I made, then sealed it up. It does sweat so I just put it inside a pillow case. But this did eliminate the leaking problem I kept having, in spite of trying every brand of zippered bag out there. I recommend this method for homemade ice packs, especially if you are looking for an excuse to buy a Food Saver!

### differentstory — 2012-03-23T23:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-5168>

This is what I do. . .I take a freezer ziploc bag and fill it and then I put another bag over the top of it (in essence, double-bagging it). I turn the ends opposite of each other (so the zipped opening of the inner bag is at the opposite end of the zippered opening of the outer bag.

### Cody — 2012-03-25T01:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-5606>

Orange mesh hunting vest from Bass Pro Shops. $5.

Duck Tape. $3.

3 Reusable ice pouches (looks like giant water filled bubble wrap) from walmart. $9

I duck taped two of the pouches to my front going up and down each side of my chest and stomach, and duck tape the other on my upper back.

I then take a hunting vest with big chest and belly pockets, and fill those up with freezer gel pouches and wear it over the vest. Looks like I'm wearing a WWII Flak Vest. This keeps me cold for 2+ hours. Once the ice melts on the orange vest, I take it off and put the hunting vest back on with fresh freezer gel pouches. Give's me another hour of cold.

### differentstory — 2012-03-25T01:47:31+0200

I have this thing and absolutely love it! Keeps me quite entertained. It will hold my iPad, iPhone or Kindle (no they are not all mine!). I once got my iPad wet and screwed it up using Ziploc freezer bags, so now I use this and haven't had any problems.

[http://www.amazon.com/Kwik-Tek-Multi-Purpose-9-Inch-12-Inch/dp/B000OF72WK/ref=sr\_1\_21?ie=UTF8&qid=1332632694&sr=8-21](http://http://www.amazon.com/Kwik-Tek-Multi-Purpose-9-Inch-12-Inch/dp/B000OF72WK/ref=sr_1_21?ie=UTF8&qid=1332632694&sr=8-21)

### Cody — 2012-03-25T02:00:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-5621>

> **healthnut;5652 wrote:** Looks like I've been doing it the hard way. Was wrapping ice packs on with ace bandages, your idea sound much better!

This is my second version. I first tried to use velcro tape on a tshirt. The cold made the adhesive not work well.

Duck tape however, wrapped over itself, seems to get stickier when it's cold. I take the vest off and put it back in the freezer. Been using it for a week now.

In hindsight, I would have made a vest out of duck tape. So many colors to choose from. LOL

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-03-27T17:34:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7027>

Had a bad time of CT last night. Now that it is cold here again, I am really struggling with the cold baths. I got off to a late start because I forgot about this project I had to finish Monday. So I didn't go up for my bath until 9:15. I keep my heat super low any way and was already chilled, so it was so hard getting the nerve up to get in. I only managed about 35 min in 55 degree water and then when I went to my bedroom it was so cold (52 degrees), brrr...I put on a heater to warm the room up and climbed into bed. Had a bad night of sleep due to being cold. SO, i have read other people say that they can't do CT before bed and I think I am going to change my time to after dinner and before bed so I have time to warm up before sleep!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-27T18:26:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7070>

I have found the best tme for CT is afternoon after a small hunk o'protein and some fat. Within an hour I jump into the tub with ice and ice packs and it settles to about 57 degrees. I can stay in an hour. If I do it at night I can only last about 30-40 min and really cold the whole time. Morning after b/fast is OK to but I think for me the benefits happen more when it is in the afternoon.

### Vicster — 2012-03-27T22:03:41+0300

### BJK77 — 2012-03-27T22:28:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7337>

The thought of getting into a cold bath when I'm already shivering is challenging to say the least!

It helped a bit - definitely made it easier to get in the tub - but, I found myself shivering so much more than I did last week. I can't wait to be adapted so this isn't so dang excruciating!

### differentstory — 2012-03-27T23:27:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7375>

> **Jennifer;7064 wrote:** Had a bad time of CT last night. Now that it is cold here again, I am really struggling with the cold baths. I got off to a late start because I forgot about this project I had to finish Monday. So I didn't go up for my bath until 9:15. I keep my heat super low any way and was already chilled, so it was so hard getting the nerve up to get in. Had a bad night of sleep due to being cold. SO, i have read other people say that they can't do CT before bed and I think I am going to change my time to after dinner and before bed so I have time to warm up before sleep!

Same here!! I was really cold. And I did my CT at 6pm (which is new for me - normally I do it at 9pm).

I have also found that it is taking me a LOOOOOOOOOOOOONNNNGGGGG time to warm up. In the past, I will CT at 9, then go to bed freezing and sometime during the night I will finally warm up and throw the blankets off. Going to sleep really helps end my misery and I often fall asleep curled up in a little ball. I worry that If I CT earlier in the evening, I am just prolonging my conscious suffering. Sucks and does not make for a very good evening at all. Last night, my 60 minutes of CT turned into 5 hours of being cold and that seemed forever. I worry that the earlier evening CT sessions will make me give it up altogether.

I am thinking tonight that I am going to CT for 60 minutes at 6pm and naturally "thaw" for 60 minutes. If I don't warm up by then, then I am going to either take a hot shower, stand in front of a stove or use an electric blanket to warm up. I feel like I have to be easier on myself.

How long is it taking you guys to warm up???

### Susan M. — 2012-03-27T23:42:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7379>

After 40 - 50 mins @ 55 degrees, it takes me about an hour to warm up. However, two toes on my right foot take about two hours to return to normal. It's always those two toes.

I don't know why, but am always a little more receptive to my CT when the sun is shining (I have a skylight in the bathroom). I don't know why it should make a difference, but it does.

### differentstory — 2012-03-27T23:43:59+0300

> **Susan M.;7417 wrote:** After 40 - 50 mins @ 55 degrees, it takes me about an hour to warm up. However, two toes on my right foot take about two hours to return to normal. It's always those two toes.
>
>
>
> I don't know why, but am always a little more receptive to my CT when the sun is shining (I have a skylight in the bathroom). I don't know why it should make a difference, but it does.

Susan! That makes me ridiculously jealous!! Are you a pretty healthy person? I am not. I am hoping as I get healthier my body will recover faster.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-28T00:05:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7384>

Compared to today's population of 58 year old post-menopausal women, I guess I might be considered pretty healthy. However, three years ago I was not a bit healthy. Since then, I have lost 45 lbs going LC then semi-paleo. I take no medicines of any type now. I was on bio-identical hormone RT for about 11 years. I had to, to save my sanity and my marriage. I do take quite a few of the recommended supplements. I would still like to lose about five more pounds, but my main concern is to reverse osteoporosis in my lower spine and my hips. I really hate cold water with a passion, but by gollie, if I can stay away from medications with horrid side-affects, then I will give it a good go! I am hoping to build up some muscle strength as well, which for sure is not going to happen if I don't get away from this blog and forum once in a while and go do something physical. Oh well, good weather is sure to get here soon, and then I will be out in the yard gardening all day; and if I understand correctly, with the CT protocol I should recover quickly without the massive amount of aching that I get every year from over doing it.

I have to say, that I have a huge rush of energy right after I get out of the tub, and it lasts for hours. If I did my CT in the evenings, I would not be in bed as early as 10:00 pm.

### differentstory — 2012-03-28T00:10:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7390>

Oh, I just had to share this story too. . .it was my revelation this morning.

I am on the heavier side (200-210 has been my weight in the past few years). I had a co-worker that had just started work after she had just had a baby and she was gorgeous. . .I would guess she weighed 125-135. . .her and her husband both are very healthy. They rarely eat out, they only eat healthy, I never see her overeat, and they both workout hardcore a few times a week. Their leisure is often biking, running, etc. I think they even did personal training as a part-time job for a few years.

Anyways, soon after she started, she was talking about how she had been working out like a maniac trying to get down to her pre-pregnancy weight (she had just had a baby 6 weeks before) and she couldn't lose the last 2 pounds. She said that she had been pushing herself so much but it would just not come off and she was so frustrated. She was running up to 4 miles a day at that point. I don't know if it ever came off for her. Honestly, I had a hard time talking about health and wellness with her.  I do know that as her job progressed, she did cut way down on the exercising though.

Fast forward 6 months. It is our busy season and we are required to work in the warehouse doing physical labor if we get too busy (we typically work in a warm office all day). This can last for a period of 3-4 weeks. The warehouse is heated somewhat so the pipes won't freeze. I would guess it is typically 50-60 degrees back there during the winter. Whenever we would start our shift, we typically started work with our jackets on because it was so cold. But, then as you really got moving (it's pretty much all walking), you start to warm up and start shedding your layers. Everyday I was always surprised at how cold it was in the warehouse when I walked in because I remembered it not even being cold from the previous day (because I would usually warm up within an hour of working and stay warm for the remaining time).

So at the end of busy season, we are sitting in the office and I happen to notice that my co-worker is looking soooooooooo SMALL and I remember saying, "Wow, have you lost weight?? You look like you've lost weight." And she says to me, "Yeah! I have! 15 pounds! This is the lowest I've ever weighed. I have no idea what I did either." I always remembered that story because 15 pounds?! Come on, that is crazy! She was so small to begin with. I know she was able to keep it off for a long time too. Whenever we would travel for business her husband was always harping on her saying, "You are going to gain that 15 pounds back by the time you get back." We used to have quite a time when we were travelling.

As of today, I am pretty convinced it was the CT that caused her to lose. I never tracked my weight then, but even I remember losing weight and having more energy during those periods. A few months after busy season, I would alway revel at how hard I had worked then. We could work up to 60 hours - so at least 30 hours was in the warehouse in the cold enviroment.

### LinD — 2012-03-28T04:47:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7537>

### hellojtm — 2012-03-28T06:23:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7589>

Kathy, Jack,

re: putting ice in the tub and living in SF. I graduated myself to the tub. Did 25 minutes yesterday toes to belly button, and today I did 15 minutes but 5 of it was with my full torso in. I think I went much longer the first day because I didn't bring a timer in the bathroom so I always assumed I was giving up early

Anyway, the water temp starts at 50f and my skin temp eventually matches the water temp after a few minutes. In that case why do I need to bother with ice? By the end of the longer session the water temp read 53f, and if I needed I could always let some drain and put fresh 50f water in the whole time.

So do I really need the ice? From what I've read on the blog I'm going for 45 minutes of 50-55f and then I will have arrived. Of course everyone probably arrives differently, but I'm pretty lean and I'm looking for the off-label benefits of CT, so once I'm officially adapted I'll reduce the sessions to something that makes more sense with my lifestyle...

So do I really need the ice? It's a logistics question, I'm fine if the answer is yes and with some reasoning.

thanks,

jesse

Congrats to you! Also, just wanted to say that my experience was that I started with my legs and butt only (and I did that for a few days). So once those started getting warm in the water, I started increasing the water depth and I was shocked that it was so easy from that point on! My theory is that once you are cold-adapted, your whole body is cold-adapted. Not just the parts you are putting in the water. (Of course, this is related to CT baths; not sure about icing). What do you think? When you started doing your torso did you feel you were starting all over again?

### LinD — 2012-03-28T19:34:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7791>

STILL cannot get this Texas water down to 55 degrees. Even with the extra ice sheets, plus reusable ice blocks. Add more? I can get it to 62 degrees, but only by constantly moving the ice blocks to mix the cold water with the water I'm sure my body is warming. I can get my abdomen to 50/55 where I directly place an ice sheet or ice block... Of course being careful not to go under 50. J stay in the water 25-30 min. Frustrated I cannot get water colder yet.! Should I go ahead and stay in longer even though the temp might not be low enough?

Thoughts?

### hellojtm — 2012-03-28T20:36:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-7842>

> **differentstory;7778 wrote:** Congrats to you! Also, just wanted to say that my experience was that I started with my legs and butt only (and I did that for a few days). So once those started getting warm in the water, I started increasing the water depth and I was shocked that it was so easy from that point on! My theory is that once you are cold-adapted, your whole body is cold-adapted. Not just the parts you are putting in the water. (Of course, this is related to CT baths; not sure about icing). What do you think? When you started doing your torso did you feel you were starting all over again?

No, I think everything is adding up to make each new step easier. In fact, even my preconceived anticipatory pain before getting in the tub is way off from the reality now... So I think you are right and the legs/butt are creating a whole body adaptation. However, I have massive shivering when I do the torso. I am experimenting with thoughts and breathing to try to eliminate it... It used to work when I was snowboarding in a blizzard. (lol my trick was to remind my body I was going to get a huge serving of chili at the lodge soon and it always helped my shivering to relax and my extremities warmed up... One thought changing my DNA? Even though I no longer eat chili I still tried this in the tub to minimal affect  )

Thanks for the response differentstory,

Also, thanks JC2K for your opinion. I think you are right although going with the ice may make the bath relatively easier in a way...

Jesse

### Cody — 2012-03-29T23:59:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-8478>

I got my blue ice vest yesterday, along with two spares for the cold packs. This thing is awesome! Getting about an hour and a half to two hours of cold. So I've got enough for 6 hours of cold on my torso. Then I can switch to my homemade getup. But, this works so much better. The vest can be tightened so that you get constant contact whereas my homemade vest doesn't give me constant contact. I feel much colder with this on.

Good stuff. Highly recommended, especially for the price!

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/8526=66-41icQlxLP3L._SL500_AA300_.jpg)

Thanks for the review on this. I have been waiting for a review. I saw this a while back but thought it may not work. I'm always a little skeptical if things will fit when something is adjustable. Often times it won't get tight enough. Does it come down long enough to cover the stomach? I'm 5'8" so I want to make sure it's long enough.

I figure at least I can wear it around while doing things in the house. Adding the ice blankets to my torso in the bath have created that new found shivering affect and I'm sure this will do the same. Yippee!

### Garfield — 2012-03-30T00:40:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-8492>

> **Patty Cakes;8539 wrote:** Thanks for the review on this. I have been waiting for a review. I saw this a while back but thought it may not work. I'm always a little skeptical if things will fit when something is adjustable. Often times it won't get tight enough. Does it come down long enough to cover the stomach? I'm 5'8" so I want to make sure it's long enough.

I haven't really used mine yet, but I plan on using it soon...

It should cover your stomach without a problem. (You can adjust how high/low it sits with straps that go over your shoulders.)

What it doesn't cover at all is the sides, so love handles don't get direct treatment. (Though you could probably stick a gel pack under the elastic side cords that hold the front and back together.)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-8575>

### Garfield — 2012-03-30T06:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-8639>

I tried the vest out tonight and I can say it keeps most of your front and back fairly cool. I would say that after about 30 minutes most of the area covered by the ice packs was between 55 and 65 degrees. (This will of course depend on a number of things like how cold your freezer is.) I'm not noticing a lot of sweating, so my shirt and the outside of the vest are staying dry, which is very nice.

The best part is that you can work around the house, or mow the grass, etc., while CTing making it actually eat into your time less.

But I think you do need to be careful about monitoring skin temperature/color especially if your freezer is really cold.

I only bought one extra set of ice packs for it, but I figure I probably wouldn't go for more than the 3-4 hours that it would last, and that it probably only takes a few hours to re-freeze a still cold pack. (But at only ~$18 the extra ice packs are fairly cheap.)

### Adriana — 2012-04-06T00:40:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-11959>

I have this 110 gallon rubber stock tank [http://www.agrisupply.com/heavy-duty-oval-tank-rubber-gal/p/39391/](http://http://www.agrisupply.com/heavy-duty-oval-tank-rubber-gal/p/39391/)

I bought 6 gallon jugs of water, removed about 10% to allow for expansion and put them in my grassfed beef freezer.

I keep 12"+ of water in the tank and add the frozen jugs at bath time. The tank is on my back deck, covered with a tarp. I can fill it up to 2' deep.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-06T01:00:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-11966>

MamaGrok, adding alcohol to the quart jars might help get them colder, if your freezer is set to 0F.

I cool my tub in two steps, putting in all the bottles of ice and sheets of ice first, then taking out the thawed sheets of cubes and adding regular ice. If the temp gets down to 50F, I also remove the bottles of ice before entering. If it is only 55F, I keep them in.

\-Sally

Like this, I can't visualize/conceptualize what 110 gallons is. Is it big enough for a fat person to sink in up to neck? I have been contemplating a summer option - thinking hot day/cold tub would be a nice way to go.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-04-06T16:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/details-details-how-are-you-performing-your-ct-to-get-skin-50-55-degrees.290/post-12112>

Ok, so I'm still not clear on the effects of doing CT in a warmer environment. I was finding I would start shivering at the first dip in cold tub (my temps are between 47-55 depending on warmth of day). So I began heating the room and having space heater on my exposed parts. It R E A L L Y helped! I could go much longer than my previous 20 mins (up to 55 max so far) without getting shivery.

As far as the production/use of BAT, I think what you want is NON shivering. So, until I talk to Jack to clarify - I'm going to continue this as. . . .

I think I've noticed a difference after my TWO longer cold baths. I'm submerged from about waist down. I was cold for a couple of hrs after, but it wasn't nearly as uncomfortable as shivering for hours after.

I think it may have effected my appetite! This is one area I've not been able to tackle. . I always feel like I have cravings and want something more to eat (except BAB which I have to choke down). I have a severed Vagus nerve, so I've really been hoping the CT will do it for me.

Last night, after CT, I realized I hadn't eaten that much yesterday - and wasn't mentally thinking about the next meal all day like I usually do. I did it again last night, so I'm hopeful to see how today goes.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-06T18:40:13+0300

I've heard people say it's b/c it's colder, but (not able to reconcile that with my limited understanding of thermodynamics) I wonder if it's a matter of the blend being more malleable, and so it can be in greater surface contact with the skin

# Thread: Women Who Chart Basal Body Temps etc.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 192
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### L8F — 2012-03-19T17:35:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/post-2763>

Hi, thought it would be nice to have a thread here for those women who have cycle questions, issues and concerns, and to not drag the poor guys along for the ride. Anyway, I have been charting (obsessively, I might add) for many years, and know a lot about it. I have noticed that two months into the LR/Post LRx that I did not have to take OTC drugs for cramps. This was HUGE--first time in 20 years. Also noted temps were very very steady for much of cycle, moving right toward textbook chart. This month I ovulated right at day 13/14, which is traditionally the "right" time to ovulate, but many don't. I was running at day 21 at the earliest previously.

For those who don't have a clue what I am talking about, taking your temp first thing in the AM after waking, before talking or moving etc, is a great way to see what is happening in your cycle. It can give you (free) info on Estrogen and Progesterone levels, as well as whether or not you are ovulating or not. All you need is to have at least one ovary and it should work just fine. No uterus needed! I find it fascinating.

Resources:

Best book, but terrible name: Taking Charge of Your Fertility

Best Site in my opinion for charting graphs and help: Fertility Friend

ACRONYMS:

BBT: Basal Body Temps

O: Ovulation

LP: Luteal Phase=phase of cycle after ovulation

Follicular phase: Part of cycle pre ovulation

E:Estrogen

P: Progesterone

CM: Cervical Mucus or as I prefer Cervical Fluid (CF) (EWCM is Egg White Cervical Mucus, a sign of impending or recent ovulation)

Soem basics, a luteal phase less than 10 days, or even less than 12 or 13 COULD indicate a progesterone deficiency (Mine has always been textbook 14, but I still have P deficiency).

So many more, but I will leave it at that for now...

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-19T18:02:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/post-2782>

I chart... I've stopped taking temps and just follow CM, O pain, and migraines

1 month after starting the LR I got my first post pregnancy period in 4yrs (after having 2kids). initially started out at 22day cycle, then extended to a 35 day cycle, and finally settled down to a 30 day cycle...

only to be thrown in to a 27 day cycle this month.

I usually get a migraine 5days before my TOTM; get one ~5days after the start of my cycle, and one mid cycle after O. Since the LR this is the first time I've actually been able to link my migraines to hormones.

I use a fertility app for my iphone called FMC - I know there are better ones out there but this thing has close to 4yrs of stuff on it! so I'll keep on using it...

One thing that freaks me out is I got sterilized in 2010 2x. the first was the essure procedure, the 2nd was a tubal b/c the essure only took on one side... so I have this fear as I become optimal my tube will re-grow!

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-20T02:02:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/post-3016>

Yeah for this thread! I'm 47 and have charted my whole married life! NFP has been my best friend as I've moved through my 40's. I have cycles upon cyles of data. Yes, I'm Catholic and went with Sympto Thermal vice Billings when we started charting. I'm a little different in that I don't track cervical os position. Instead I use CM and both a vaginal and an oral temperature - taking the former with a glass thermometer, the latter with a digital. Husband records all data in a spreadsheet that nicely converts times when I don't wake up at the usual moment. Last summer as things got more haywire with perimenopause, my obgyn who is an expert chart reader suggested going to Marquette method or at least add LH readings to our charts. So I use LH strips from the first sign of EWCM until I get a positive reading.

I can tell you that I've lived the SAD for most of my fertile years and was fine until I hit my 40's when PMS moved more to PMDD. I always had a bit a of luteal phase defect - less than 9-12 from peak to end of cycle. It just got worse as my cycle shortened. At 43 I had the menorraghia that sends you down anemia way, so it was suggested I do an endometrial ablation. Of course, given my faith, that had to be considered as a last resort. The ob/gyn I had then was NOT very supportive of NFP or me trying to avoid the procedure. But after 20+ years of people actually writing NO BIRTH CONTROL on my chart, I've pretty much given up.

I did get lucky that an obgyn in the midwest told me to consider insulin resistance as the source of my issues. But other than gettting a fasting glucose of 105 (and being told that was fine), I didn't get any real support on the lifestyle end. Primary care at the time was your standard calories in /calories out type of guy and wouldn't even consider me as metabolic X. Yeah military health care.

After the EA, I had zero tangible flow for 5 mos, and then just enough to mark TOTM. The anemia was gone, but my cycles continued to be short 23 -25 and then they stopped dead last Sept.  I freaked because my charts show all the LH surges, temp rises/falls, and mucus changes. Just no flow. I was eating Paleo and was worried that being too keto for too long was the cause of the problems - the missing flow and the now spotting at days 8-10. No cancer - thank goodness, but a fibroid may be the cause of that.

Anyway, add DEAD libido to this picture.

Then enter Leptin Rx (cue chorus of cherubim). DH was definitely supportive of any diet that was going to show it was working by improving my mood and libido. And it did. And I had a perfect 27 day cycle with a tangible marker at TOTM and I nice long 12 day luteal phase. Interest is back. I can't say where I will be this month because I have been sick as a dog. Fever was >103 for 4 straight days. LH tried to surge and failed, so I am getting one of those "second surge" attempts right now. This is why I chart. I can see what is happening and won't be having an oops baby. Love kids, but once you have an ablation you can't have a child without a HUGE risk of placental abruption.

So another charting woman.... trying to realign her hormones before they are all gone!

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-20T08:41:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/post-3165>

> **Rhoswen;3187 wrote:** Guys, I'm curious... I'm a person who NEVER EVER EVER wants to have children. Would charting my cycle help me to figure out the best times to NOT have sex?  The LadyComp seems like an awesome idea but, wow, so expensive. I'm just worried about how accurate charting would be and I definitely do no want to do hormonal birth control.

I'm sure that's what NFP was invented for! It must have been about 27 years ago, that one of my dh's aunts (devout catholic) told me that it was the best way to space your children. She had had 7 children, all spaced 2 years apart, because that's how she wanted it; every one of them planned. Then she wanted no more.

She was such a demure, softly spoken lady. She almost whispered to me that she had three silk roses, one of which she would place in a vase beside her bed. Red, when it was TOM, yellow for when she was fertile, and (with a twinkle in her eye) white, for when he could got for it! Because, of course, they never spoke of such things.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T14:26:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/post-3195>

> **Rhoswen;3187 wrote:** Guys, I'm curious... I'm a person who NEVER EVER EVER wants to have children. Would charting my cycle help me to figure out the best times to NOT have sex?  The LadyComp seems like an awesome idea but, wow, so expensive. I'm just worried about how accurate charting would be and I definitely do no want to do hormonal birth control.

I think a lot of us once felt as you do. Nevertheless, there are loads and years of studies finding that NFP/FAM is as or more effective than the Pill so long as you actually bother to follow the rules (and no, they're not that complicated or difficult - just takes a wee bit of self-control, really not much!). If your cycle is regular, you just abstain from the first sign of approaching fertility (usually cervical mucus seen on toilet tissue that is liftable or slippery) until three full days after the last sign (usually CM that is clear, stretchy, and/or slippery, also there are signs like a temp that goes up and stays up, cervix moving down, firmer, and closed, and some others, all of which can cross-check the mucus sign). If your cycle isn't regular, the info you learn will be priceless to help learn what's going on with your cycle.

You don't need any expensive software. I've been doing NFP for 15 years with just paper and pencil and the info I learned in a free class. (And the only one of my sweeties whose conception was a shock was the one conceived while using contraception.)

### L8F — 2012-03-20T15:47:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/post-3228>

> **Rhoswen;3189 wrote:** Haha, she sounds really sweet. I definitely will have to look into this more... Not too sure where to start, but I've heard good things about that book, "Taking Charge of you Fertility".

It was indeed originally for not getting pregnant I believe, but of course with people being so out of touch with their bodies in general, it has shifted into a popular way to gauge fertility for those with trouble TTC (trying to conceive). Some people were trying on only day 14 for example, and come to find out that they ovulate at day 8...

Lady Comp in my opinion is not necessary--interprets your temp data itself. I think there are other devices that can help you more. (OvaCue I have used as well as Clear Blue Easy Fertility monitor) Temping and charting is almost the same as lady comp, and very effective if you have a REGULAR cycle. Some people are super duper fertile, so having sex any day within a week of ovulation gets them pregnant...for them, I am not sure. A lot of people use condoms around the time when they are most likely to be fertile.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T15:59:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/post-3232>

It's possible (but very unlikely - but POSSIBLE!) to get pregnant up to five days pre-O, and up to 3 days post-O. There is a rule in the sympto-thermal method that takes the five days pre-O into account, but I can't remember what it is. I have fairly regular cycles, so I just start abstaining six days before expected ovulation.

NFP/FAM + condom around O time = baby, in my experience.  (LOVE that baby who is now 13!)

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-21T03:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-who-chart-basal-body-temps-etc.291/post-3730>

Yes, this is the best thread. L8F you are a God send... a psych who gets women's biology!

.

As for reasoning behind WHY someone uses a fertility awareness method, there can be many. Yes, I am Catholic, so I am going to first extol a little of Church wisdom here. Take it in more deeply than what you read in the NYT or Wash Post, because I actually am credentialed by the Holy See and have my MA in Theology. So before you ask Sibelius, Pelosi, or any other professed Catholic what the Church teaches, please ask ME. I won't steer you wrong. Everyone is free to make their choices, I just get way, way, way bent out of shape when I hear "Well the Church says... and then everything that follows is misinterpreted sound bytes"

The Church does not teach that you have to have a HUGE family. You would think that would be a no brainer, since Mary and Joseph - the model family pretty much changed the world by having one child. The point being was they had the child that God ask them to have. And we should do the same. Granted we probably won't get Gabriel knocking at the door, but who really wants to be scared to death by an angelic visit anyway?

So the Church encourages couples to have children. They are a blessing and a sign of life and love in a marriage. The marital act is for the procreation of children AND the good of the spouses. Yes, the Church does know that sex does a body good. But the Church also sees the act as a total giving of oneself to the other - modeling Christ's total sacrifice. So you give it all in that relationship - not just all the loving minus my fertility. That is why the Church is opposed to contraception. It violates the natural process of the relationship. A baby is what is supposed to happen when you have sex.

Next it is important to understand that the Church does teach that we must provide for our children. First responsibility of a parent is to raise that child to know his/her Creator. And that requires providing spiritually and of course materially (within reason) for the child. You need to teach the child to know God, you don't need to give him a car for his 16th birthday. (My daughter got a new baby sister for hers - lol). So the Church knows there are constraints to all that we can do - financial, physical and yes mental. And if any of these are indeed truly at their limit, there is a "grave and serious reason" not to have another child at that time.

So you can see where the new mom can legitimately seek to delay pregnancy. Or another couple may delay more children because of unemployment. And in these cases the Church offers the use of fertility in NFP to avoid another pregnancy.

Now, to share my own situation. I have 5 ... 21, 20, 19, 9, and nearly 6. And that is seriously the limit of my span of control! Others can do more, but this type A Marine just doesn't tolerate the chaos beyond this. Wish I could, but I can't. My 9 year old has a genetic disorder that by the textbooks would include MR, but he is not. And that has been a full time job in itself. So yes, my mental health is my "grave and serious" reason. And I had to be very long and thoughtful when I had my endometrial ablation that I had the right intention - I could not pursue it as radically reducing my fertility - but as a last resort medical treatment to restore my health. Ugh never mind that IF I had the Leptin Rx and Paleo back then I probably never would have needed the EA.  Fortunately God knows I did the best with what I had for health care.

Now to the efficacy of NFP - it is 100% effective if you don't cheat. And if you do right by what you eat... your hormones are well put together and you will have very few days where you need to abstain from relations.

br />
So that's it from a Catholic perspective. The faith isn't for everyone... well it is out there for whomever wants it. But I totally understand that being hard core Catholic these days is pretty darn counter cultural. I leave you with this one other thought. The Church teaches from what is referred to as a natural law perspective (that's why it's in my signature line). Beyond Scripture and all that we know from Jesus, the Church also teaches that God's creation is a revelation of His love for us. To that end we should always be stewards of the earth and work with the order that He set into motion. We can see it all around us... how things are meant to go. Natural law is the common ground of people of all faiths.

Okay - I'll keep the rest of the thread to nuts and bolts of charting now
